# Deferred-Anchor Registry

> The canonical source of truth for every `D-*` anchor cited in `src/` but
> deferred to a future cycle. The leading underscore sorts this file before
> the numbered plans in directory listings — call this out as the registry,
> not a plan. Per-plan §3.1 rows are still the preferred home for anchors
> whose feature area maps cleanly onto a plan; this file catches the orphans
> + cross-cutting anchors that don't belong to any single plan.
>
> **CI/pre-commit guard** (`tools/check-anchor-registry.{sh,ps1}`): every
> `D-*` identifier appearing in `src/` MUST resolve to a row in this file
> OR a citation in any `.plans/*.md`. Pre-fix this recurred TWICE
> (memory: "the same gap silent-failure caught for MULTI-PAGE-GOT").
> The guard makes the system enforce the discipline — same shape as the
> agnosticism veto + the `CallConv` static_assert.
>
> **V2-4.X / full-C ownership (2026-06-10):** the C-language-feature deferral
> CLUSTER — modulo codegen (`D-CSUBSET-MOD-OP-CODEGEN`, …), primitive widths
> (`D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH`), linkage/visibility syntax
> (`D-CSUBSET-LINKAGE-VISIBILITY-SYNTAX`, `D-DECL-PREFIX-STRIP-SHARED-HELPER`),
> rodata aggregates (`D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER-NONSTRING-ARRAY`), non-main implicit
> return (`D-LK10-ENTRY-MAIN-IMPLICIT-RETURN` — the OPEN non-main successor row,
> cross-ref `D-CSUBSET-ENTRY-NAME-SPLIT`), variadic FFI
> (`D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VARIADIC-SIGNATURE`), the apple-arm64 ABI divergence
> (`D-FF3-APPLE-ARM64-ABI-DIVERGENCE`), and peers — is now OWNED by **stepper
> V2-4.X / [`23-full-c-plan`](./23-full-c-plan%20-%20tbd.md)** (c-subset → full
> C23). Each closes inside the relevant FC phase (see the plan §3.1).
>
> **NOT in this cluster — already shipped, do NOT re-claim as new FC work**
> (caught by the 2026-06-10 plan-lock accuracy review). Two genuinely-CLOSED
> anchors: `D-CSUBSET-DIVISION-OP-CODEGEN` (✅ 2026-06-04 — signed division shipped;
> the modulo successor `D-CSUBSET-MOD-OP-CODEGEN` ✅ since closed too — FC1 cycle 1, 2026-06-10, above) and `D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH`
> (✅ 2026-06-08 — direct-plt/indirect-slot dispatch shipped; the OPEN variadic concern
> is `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VARIADIC-SIGNATURE`, above). PLUS one closed FEATURE with no
> anchor row: the `main` implicit-return-0 (shipped 2026-06-02, live in
> `c-subset.lang.json`, config-only) — the anchor `D-LK10-ENTRY-MAIN-IMPLICIT-RETURN`
> itself remains the still-OPEN non-main successor (above), NOT a closed item. The
> full-C arc supersedes each predecessor with its named successor, never by reopening it.
>
> New full-C exotic-tail exclusions land as `D-FULLC-*` rows as clusters ship.
> Individual rows are NOT rewritten here yet (the plan is the live map until a
> cycle actually closes the anchor).
>
> **Schema (one row per anchor)**:
> ```
> | Anchor | Trigger | Closing work | Cross-refs |
> ```
>
> When an anchor closes, replace its row's status with `✅ CLOSED <date>`
> and cite the commit/SymbolId. Don't delete the row — the audit trail is
> load-bearing for future regressions.
>
> **Authorization log — robustness sweep (2026-06-24, branch `feature/0-0-2-p14`):**
> the user authorized a "robustness sweep," which FIRED the trigger for three
> trigger-gated anchors and CLOSED them in one sweep (393/393 ctest, MSVC
> Release + MinGW): `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-PROTOTYPE`,
> `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-DEFINITION-MERGE`, and
> `D-SEMANTIC-ASSIGN-STMT-ASSIGNABILITY-BYPASS` (see their rows below). The
> sweep surfaced one new backlog row, `D-CSUBSET-TENTATIVE-DEFINITION-MERGE`
> (C 6.9.2 tentative definitions — still fail-loud, trigger-gated). [That backlog
> row was later ✅ CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c33) when the sqlite3.c
> re-probe fired its trigger — see its row below.]

---

## Anchor Index

| Anchor | Trigger | Closing work | Cross-refs |
|---|---|---|---|
| `D-LINK-LOCAL-FN-ADDR-STATIC-DATA-VA0` | **✅ LANDED 2026-07-22 (TF-C45, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`/PR #46) — the cross-CU MIR merge FOLDED a file-LOCAL (`static`) symbol onto a same-named EXTERNALLY-VISIBLE (Global) symbol in another CU (a C 6.2.2p3 internal-linkage violation).** ★ The pre-registered ranked-#1 "SymbolId identity mismatch at lowering" hypothesis below was REFUTED by instrumentation: the per-CU aggregate-literal symbol and the function symbol BOTH derive from `hir.functionSymbol` and AGREE, and a 202-TU synthetic reproducer did NOT trigger. REAL ROOT (instrumented DATARELOC/SYMTAB dump on the 88-TU link): sqlite os_unix.c's `static struct unix_syscall aSyscall[]` (24-byte rows → the static `posixOpen`) shares the NAME `aSyscall` with test_syscall.c's NON-static (external-linkage) `aSyscall[]` (32-byte rows → `ts_open`). `mergeCuMirs` (a) FOLDED the Local onto the Global winner's merged id in `assignSymbol` (the `canonicalForName` collapse fired for a Local symbol whose name merely matched a Global winner) AND (b) DROPPED the Local as a "shadowed loser" in `isShadowedLoser` — so os_unix's own code read test_syscall's 32-byte table through its 24-byte struct: off 8 → `ts_open`, and `ts_open` called `orig_open`=`aSyscall[0].xOrig`(off 16)=NULL → the NULL call. Invisible until BOTH TUs link (the 2-TU sqlite3.c+main build lacks the extern `aSyscall`) — the "works at 2, crashes at 88" behavior. **FIX (agnostic — a `SymbolBinding::Local` predicate, no source/target/object-format branch; fail-loud preserved):** thread `isLocalDef`/`isLocal` into `assignSymbol` + `isShadowedLoser` (mir_merge.cpp) so a Local (internal-linkage) def is NEVER name-folded onto / shadowed by a same-named externally-visible winner — it always mints its own fresh merged id and is always cloned. `link/cross_cu_resolve.hpp` already EXCLUDES Local from `winners`; the bug was purely in these two merge CONSUMERS. Post-fix the testfixture carries TWO distinct `aSyscall` (os_unix id→posixOpen, test_syscall id→ts_open); the file-db `sqlite3 db /tmp/x.db; CREATE/INSERT/SELECT` returns R=42, no SIGSEGV. Sibling `D-LINK-DATA-RELOC-TARGET-VALIDATE` closes the #2 fail-loud gap. Tests: `MirMerge.MergeKeepsLocalStaticGlobalDistinctFromSameNamedExtern` (red-on-disable: drop either guard → the Local `T` folds/drops, `size()==2` pin fails) + the `static_extern_name_collision` RUN example (exit 42; red-on-disable → osOpen reads the wrong table → 231). **_(Superseded hypothesis below, kept for the audit trail:)_** The DSS-built 88-TU testfixture SIGSEGVs on FILE-db open: sqlite's unix-VFS `aSyscall[0].pCurrent` (the `open` syscall ptr — a static-aggregate field initialized `(sqlite3_syscall_ptr)posixOpen`, i.e. the ADDRESS of a file-local static fn, cast) is NULL → `osOpen(path,flags,0644)` calls addr 0. `:memory:` works (no file VFS); a MINIMAL 2-TU build (sqlite3.c+main, ±TESTFIXTURE_FLAGS, real file I/O) works; the NULL appears ONLY at ~88-TU link scale. Ruled OUT: runtime reset (no test-init touches the syscall table), DCE (Phase-3 fixpoint keeps address-taken-in-data live), the C44 gate (only erases externImports). ROOT (code-explorer, ranked #1): a SymbolId IDENTITY MISMATCH — the SymbolId baked into aSyscall's `MirSymbolAddrValue` reloc (hir_to_mir `functionSymbols` pre-pass ~10600 / `tryClassifyAsSymbolAddrImpl` ~10994) vs posixOpen's own MirFunc/AssembledFunction.symbol (asm.hpp:65-68); `SymbolId` is a bare per-CU counter (strong_ids.hpp:70, non-arena, non-mangled), so a wrong-but-valid id is faithfully propagated by `mergeCuMirs` → resolves to a symbol at VA 0; coincidental numbering masks it in tiny builds, exposes it at scale. | INSTRUMENT to confirm the mismatch (dump aSyscall reloc target vs posixOpen def SymbolId, per-CU + merged) → FIX the identity source (agnostic, fail-loud, best-long-term) + close the #2 fail-loud gap (`linker.cpp` ~264-277 validates only fn relocations, NOT data-item relocations → a dangling data reloc must error LOUD, not NULL-at-runtime). Minimal multi-TU reproducer test (static-fn-addr-in-static-data + enough symbols to shift the id space; red-on-disable). Verify: testfixture opens a file db (no crash) + full ctest green + sqlite ×2. | plan-00 §0.1 TF · mir_merge.cpp / hir_to_mir.cpp / asm.cpp / linker.cpp / exec_data_section.hpp · [[D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-GLOBAL-SYMBOL-ADDRESS]] (c67) · TF-C45 corpus run · sibling `D-LINK-DATA-RELOC-TARGET-VALIDATE`. |
| `D-LINK-DATA-RELOC-TARGET-VALIDATE` | **✅ LANDED 2026-07-22 (TF-C45, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`/PR #46) — the linker's `resolveCrossCuSymbols` (linker.cpp) now validates DATA-item relocation targets the SAME way it validates function-relocation targets (the suspect-#2 fail-loud gap).** Pre-fix it inspected only `m.functions[].relocations`, never `m.dataItems[].relocations`, so a dangling / mis-targeted DATA reloc (an abs64 pointer slot in a symbol-address global / function-pointer table / c70 switch jump table) slipped past the resolution tier and could surface only as a silent wrong/NULL pointer at runtime (the class the sibling `D-LINK-LOCAL-FN-ADDR-STATIC-DATA-VA0` miscompile belonged to). FIX: an added `m.dataItems` loop emits `K_SymbolUndefined` ("data-item relocation in CU …") when a data reloc target is declared by no function / data-item / extern in its CU (`compoundIndex`-checked — that index already covers functions, synthetic block symbols, data items, and extern imports; symmetric with the function loop; agnostic — no format branch). Test: `Linker.DataItemUndefinedRelocationFailsLoud` (red-on-disable: remove the `dataItems` loop → the resolution-tier "data-item relocation" diagnostic never fires). | plan-00 §0.1 TF · linker.cpp `resolveCrossCuSymbols` · sibling `D-LINK-LOCAL-FN-ADDR-STATIC-DATA-VA0` (the miscompile that motivated closing this asymmetry). |
