| `D-FFI-STRERROR-NONTHREADSAFE` | **✅ LANDED 2026-07-22 (TF-C49, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`/PR #46) — the `<string.h>` `strerror` descriptor symbol for `SQLITE_THREADSAFE=0`.** ROOT: SQLite's `unixLogErrorAtLine` (os_unix.c:41902) selects its errno→string source by a 3-way `#if SQLITE_THREADSAFE && defined(HAVE_STRERROR_R) / #elif SQLITE_THREADSAFE / #else` — the DEFAULT build (threadsafe, no `HAVE_STRERROR_R`, which DSS never defines) takes the `zErr = ""` branch and references NEITHER strerror NOR strerror_r (why the default + FULL-feature corpus compiled clean without either); `--define SQLITE_THREADSAFE=0` takes the `#else` branch `zErr = strerror(iErrno)` → DSS's string.json lacked strerror → `error[S0001] got strerror` (the agent's THREADSAFE=0 fallback, user-flagged mandatory). FIX (descriptor-only): ship `strerror` (C89 7.11.6.2, `fn(i32) -> ptr<char>`) in `string.json` — libc-exported on EVERY format (glibc/msvcrt/libSystem `T`) so NO `availableObjectFormats` gate; the eager-import law (`D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT`) holds (referenced→imported→resolved). strerror_r stays UN-shipped: its branch needs `HAVE_STRERROR_R` (autoconf-only, DSS never defines) → no consumer → shipping it would be a SPECULATIVE build. VERIFIED: sqlite3.c `--define SQLITE_THREADSAFE=0` CANON (4.66MB) + FULL (7.00MB) both rc=0 (was rc=1 S0001); `examples/c-subset/strerror_call` runs 42 on pe64 + elf64-x86_64 + arm64-elf64-exec-qemu + macho-darwin (RED-ON-DISABLE: delete the strerror row → S0001 got strerror). | (closed — the string.json strerror row) | plan-00 §0.1 TF · TF-C49 full-feature+flag arc · `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT` (the eager-import law) · siblings `D-FFI-ZLIB-DESCRIPTOR`/`D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` (descriptor completeness) · the SQLITE_THREADSAFE flag-support frontier |
| `D-LINK-EXTERN-IMPORT-REFERENCE-GATE` | **✅ LANDED 2026-07-22 (TF-C44, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`/PR #46) — an extern import survives the link iff (descriptor eager-import) OR (referenced by a relocation); an UNREFERENCED non-eager extern is DROPPED (gcc's rule: an unused declaration emits no import).** ROOT (C44 probe — SUPERSEDES C43's "fail-loud gap" framing): DSS emitted an UND import for a declared-but-NEVER-REFERENCED `extern` fn. The explicit-`extern`-keyword producer A (cst_to_hir.cpp `recordExtern` ~8815) 3-field-inits `HirExternRecord` → `noLibraryBinding=false` → FFI-synth auto-binds the FORMAT-DEFAULT lib (elf `libc.so.6`) → the row is library-bound → BYPASSES the linker's only reference gate `rejectOrDropUnboundExterns` (linker.cpp:81-178, which only processed zero-library rows). Producer B (bare proto) was already c86-hardened libraryless; producer A was the completeness gap. Repro: `extern int unused(int);`+`int main(){return 0;}` → ELF fails load exit 127 (`undefined symbol: unused`); gcc emits no ref. In the 88-TU testfixture, `Sqlitetestsse_Init` (SEE ext, declared in src/test_tclsh.c, NEVER called) was the SOLE load-blocker (the other ~326 UND are referenced/eager libc/m/tcl/z, all resolvable). **MECHANISM (Design 2a — design-audit GO, NOT a §B; agnostic, no `if(format==)`):** thread a new `isEagerImport` bit producer-C-only (descriptor synth) through HirExternRecord→FfiMetadata→ExternImport (rides the LK11 merge whole-row copy like `version`); generalize the gate so drop⟺`(!isEagerImport && !referenced)` for BOTH bound+unbound rows — ★ CRITICAL: the exec reject-LOUD stays scoped to `libraryPath.empty()` (else every referenced libc import rejects); the merge dedup OR-combines the bit (order-independent eager law). KEEPS referenced auto-bound externs (the ~12 `extern int printf(...)`/`abs`/`puts` examples), KEEPS unreferenced eager descriptor imports (`D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT` untouched), DROPS unreferenced non-eager (Sqlitetestsse_Init) → the testfixture LOADS. | Tests: (a) unreferenced-extern RUN example loads exit 42 [red-on-disable: revert → 127]; (b) referenced `extern_call_elf`/`hello_printf` stay green; (c) 3-row gate unit pin (unref-eager KEPT, unref-nonEager DROPPED, ref-nonEager KEPT); (d) exec-reject-still-fires pin; (e) merge-collapse pin. ★ GATE-CAUGHT SELF-REPAIR: the reloc-based `referenced` scan missed the pe64 SEH personality `__C_specific_handler` (referenced via the UNWIND_INFO EHANDLER handler-RVA field / `SehHandlerPatch`, NOT a fn/data relocation) → 3 `seh_catch_*` examples' pe64 compile regressed; FIX = `synth_seh_funclets.cpp` mints that synthesized personality import `isEagerImport=true` (a compiler-synthesized always-needed import → exempt from the reloc-reference-gate, keeping the gate itself format-agnostic; the `seh_catch_*` examples are its red-on-disable). ★★ MILESTONE: the 88-TU testfixture now LINKS + LOADS + runs Tcl (`exit 7`, `% ` prompt; `Sqlitetestsse_Init` dropped, UND count 0). Gate Win 717/717 + WSL 717/717 (x86_64+arm64 qemu, SEH runtime witness native on Win) + anchor guard OK + independent design-audit GO + code-audit VERIFIED-CLEAN + sqlite CRUD 42 ×2 (x86_64+arm64). | plan-00 §0.1 TF · [[D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT]] (preserved) · [[D-CSUBSET-BARE-PROTO-EXTERN-SYNTHESIS]] (c86 producer B — this completes the symmetry for producer A) · supersedes-framing [[D-LINK-EXEC-UNDEFINED-SYMBOL-FAIL-LOUD]] · §B-deferred: Design 3 (also drop unref eager imports — changes the law) + residual "referenced auto-bind-to-libc defers to loader 127". |
| `D-LINK-EXEC-UNDEFINED-SYMBOL-FAIL-LOUD` | **↩ REFRAMED 2026-07-21 by TF-C44 → see [[D-LINK-EXTERN-IMPORT-REFERENCE-GATE]]. The C43 "fail-loud gap" hypothesis was WRONG: `Sqlitetestsse_Init` is an UNREFERENCED extern (declared, never called) → the correct behavior is to DROP it (no import), not fail loud. The fail-loud for a REFERENCED-undefined symbol in an exec ALREADY exists (c143/c150, `allowsUndefinedImports()` reject in `rejectOrDropUnboundExterns`).** Original (now-corrected) framing:** ⏳ TRIGGER FIRED 2026-07-21 (TF-C43 testfixture link) — priority: high (fail-loud/correctness). Investigate + close in C44.** DSS's multi-TU **exec** link emitted a truly-undefined internal symbol — `Sqlitetestsse_Init` (SEE encryption-ext test-module init, referenced in `src/test_tclsh.c`, defined in NO input TU and declared by NO descriptor) — as a runtime dynamic import → load-time `undefined symbol` (exit 127) instead of a LINK-TIME diagnostic. A real `ld` hard-errors an undefined non-weak symbol in an executable. | Make the exec-format linker FAIL LOUD when a referenced symbol is (a) not defined in the link set AND (b) not declared by any DT_NEEDED/descriptor import — a real diagnostic, not a deferred runtime crash. Must stay source/target/format-agnostic (per-format linker policy, config-driven — no `if(format==)`). Pairs with the testfixture SEE-module gating (a sqlite build-config `#ifdef`), which C44 also resolves so the corpus can run. | plan-00 §0.1 TF · [[D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT]] · [[D-FFI-TCL-LIST-VAR-DESCRIPTORS]] · TF-C43 link probe. |
| `D-LANG-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-INT-POINTEE-COMPAT` | **✅ LANDED 2026-07-21 (SQLite testfixture-link sub-arc, TF-C41, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`/PR #46) — a shipped-FFI-descriptor integer-pointee parameter accepts a same-representation real C integer pointer at the CALL-ARG boundary ONLY.** DSS emitted S0003 (S_TypeMismatch, `.actual`="got &iVal") when a real C `long long*` / `sqlite3_int64*` / `Tcl_WideInt*` / `long*`(LP64) argument was passed to a SHIPPED-DESCRIPTOR function parameter modeled as the abstract width-based `ptr<i64>` (ABI-identical on LP64 — both 8-byte, gcc accepts) — blocking the LAST 2 testfixture TUs `ext/session/test_session.c:1744` `Tcl_GetWideIntFromObj(interp,objv[4],&iVal)` + `ext/rbu/test_rbu.c:222` `Tcl_GetWideIntFromObj(interp,objv[2],&nLimit)` (both DIRECT calls to the tcl.json `fn(...,ptr<i64>)->i32` symbol). ROOT: `long` / `long long` / the descriptor `i64` are DISTINCT TypeIds BY NAME (identity vocabulary — `_Generic(long:,long long:)` must still split them) despite a byte-identical representation, so the STRICT pointer-pointee reject fired. **MECHANISM (agnostic — an attribute-driven predicate, config-gated, NO `if(type==long long)` / source / target / object-format branch; type IDENTITY is UNTOUCHED — a compat admission + a coerce bitcast, never a TypeId merge):** (1) `SymbolRecord::isShippedDescriptorFn` (semantic_model.hpp) set TRUE only in the shipped-lib descriptor injection loop for `kind==Function` (the builtin/intrinsic loop deliberately does NOT — keeps `_InterlockedCompareExchange`'s `int*`-reject pinned); (2) a TREE-KEYED node-mark `ffiIntPointeeCompatNodes` (UnitAttribute<bool>, mirroring `nullPointerConstantNodes`) + `isFfiIntPointeeCompat(NodeId)` accessor, set at the call-arg admit site so admit⟺realize by construction; (3) a config flag `PointerConversionRules::ffiDescriptorIntPointeeCompat` (default FALSE — strict; loaded in grammar_schema_json.cpp, enabled ONLY in c-subset.lang.json `pointerConversions`); (4) `type_rules.hpp::isAssignable` gains a trailing `ffiDescriptorPointeeIntCompat` param → in the Ptr↔Ptr block, AFTER the void arms, admit iff BOTH pointees are integer kinds (signedIntRank∨unsignedIntRank) AND `interner.sameRepresentation(lhsElem[0],rhsElem[0])`; (5) threaded into `checkCallAgainstSig` via a `calleeIsShippedFfi` param passed TRUE only at the DIRECT-symbol call site (`s.symbols.at(calleeSym).isShippedDescriptorFn`), FALSE at the fn-pointer/indirect sites — the strict `isAssignable(...,false)` is tried FIRST and only a strict-FAIL-then-relaxed-SUCCESS marks the node (node-mark ⟺ relaxation fired); NOT threaded into init/assign/return (Condition 3 — those keep the default false); (6) `cst_to_hir.cpp::coerce` gains a `srcNode` param (threaded from `coerceCallArg`←`lowerCallArgOrBraceInit` / the work-stack `CallCtx.argNodes` / the flat-call loop) → in the Ptr↔Ptr block, IFF `srcNode.valid() && model.isFfiIntPointeeCompat(srcNode)` emit the SAME synthetic `makeCast(child.id, target, Synthetic)` the void arm emits — a Ptr→Ptr bitcast MIR lowers as a no-op, retyping the arg node to the param type so the HIR verifier's arg==param equality holds (the verifier is the missing-cast backstop → H_VerifierFailure). **Per-target by construction (Condition 6):** `sameRepresentation` compares kind∧extensionKind∧operands∧scalars (NOT the identity NAME) → on LLP64/pe64 where `long` is I32, `long*` vs `ptr<i64>` FAILS the kind axis → STILL S0003, with NO format branch; a `_BitInt(64)*` fails the extensionKind axis; `unsigned long long*`/`int*`/`double*`/`enum E*` fail kind/base-kind → the predicate discriminates exactly (size ∧ signedness ∧ integer-base-kind). **RESULT: test_session.c + test_rbu.c 1→0 → all ~81 testfixture TUs compile.** Tests (`tests/analysis/semantic/test_type_identity_vocabulary.cpp` + a MIR/HIR realize pin + a RUN witness `examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_wideint`): positive (`long long*`/`sqlite3_int64*` + `long*`-on-LP64 admitted, NO S0003), per-target (`long*`→`ptr<i64>` on LLP64 STILL S0003), predicate negatives at the shipped boundary (`int*`/`unsigned long long*`/`double*`/`enum E*`/`_BitInt(64)*` STILL S0003), Condition-3 red-on-disable (the same sameRep-distinct mismatch at init/assign/return STILL S0003 — no leak past the call arg), identity witness (`_Generic(long,long long)` still distinguishes + `EXPECT_NE(pointee TypeIds)`), the `InterlockedCompareExchangeTakesALongPointer` must-stay-green (builtins excluded), a fn-pointer round-trip (STILL S0003 — indirect passes false), a realize/verifier-backstop MIR pin (Ptr→Ptr bitcast, no width op; neutering the coerce arm reds the HIR verifier), and the RUN witness (a wide-int pointer filled by libtcl → 42, red-on-disable = S0003). | Mechanism landed — no deferred work (row is provenance for the anchor guard). | `D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY` / LD-5 (the #47 identity-vs-representation split — the identity NAME keeps `_Generic(long,long long)` distinct while `sameRepresentation` reads the shared representation; this row's predicate rides that exact seam); `D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` (the testfixture arc + the `Tcl_GetWideIntFromObj` `ptr<i64>` param); `D-LANG-VOIDPTR-FN-CONVERT` (the sibling Option-B config-gated call-arg pointer-compat relaxation — the loader + isAssignable-admit/coerce-realize-in-lockstep precedent this mirrors); `D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-DESCRIPTOR-AGNOSTIC` (the descriptor model + the abstract width-based `ptr<i64>`); TF-C15/C16/C32 pointer-compat-policy notes (the shipped-boundary pointer-conversion-policy lineage) |
| `D-FFI-ZLIB-DESCRIPTOR` | **✅ LANDED 2026-07-21 (SQLite testfixture-link sub-arc, TF-C39, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`/PR #46) — the neutral `<zlib.h>` FFI descriptor.** SQLite `ext/misc/zipfile.c` `#include <zlib.h>` (under `SQLITE_HAVE_ZLIB`) and round-trips ZIP payloads through raw deflate — the last compile blocker in the testfixture-link sub-arc after `tclsqlite-ex.c` went clean (C38); it F001A'd `got zlib.h` (zlib.h lives only in /usr/include, ships no descriptor — the `<zlib.h>` follow-up the `D-INCLUDE-ANGLE-SOURCE-FALLBACK` row anticipated). **DATA (config-only, `src/dss-config/shippedLibs/zlib.json`, need-driven + minimal — the tcl.json growth discipline):** the `z_stream` struct (14 fields / 112 bytes on LP64, `offsetof`-verified vs /usr/include/zlib.h 1.3 — next_in@0/avail_in@8/total_in@16/next_out@24/avail_out@32/total_out@40/msg@48/state@56/zalloc@64/zfree@72/opaque@80/data_type@88/adler@96/reserved@104; `uLong→u64` `uInt→u32` `Bytef/Byte→u8`; `zalloc`/`zfree` carry the REAL `alloc_func`/`free_func` fn-ptr types, the tcl.json Tcl_ChannelType driver-proc-field precedent, so the struct is ABI-faithful not lazily-opaque) modeled in BOTH `typedefs` (a by-name TypeId for the 9 signatures + `z_streamp`, via Option C `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-TYPEDEF-NAME-RESOLUTION`) AND `structs` (the field-name scope so `str.next_in` resolves) — byte-identical bodies intern to the ONE z_stream (the Tcl_ChannelType pattern) + `Byte`/`Bytef`/`z_streamp` typedefs + 9 EXPORTED functions (`nm -D libz.so.1` `T`-verified — `deflate`/`deflateEnd`/`deflateInit2_`/`inflate`/`inflateEnd`/`inflateInit2_`/`crc32`/`zlibVersion` unversioned, `deflateBound` a DEFAULT-version export `deflateBound@@ZLIB_1.2.0` that binds an unversioned import at load exactly like glibc `printf@@GLIBC_2.2.5`) + the two `deflateInit2`/`inflateInit2` function-MACROS (NOT libz exports — a function-row would break every zlib binary's LOAD 127, the `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT` law / tcl.json Tcl_IsShared precedent) + 7 `Z_*` object-macros + `ZLIB_VERSION`="1.3". `availableObjectFormats:[elf,macho]` — a pe `<zlib.h>` fails LOUD F001D identical to sys-mman.json (the per-format-gate precedent). **DISCOVERED (probe re-baseline after the F001A cleared — the C37 masked-tier lesson):** (1) zipfile.c uses BARE `Byte` (not only `Bytef`) at :1013/:1015 — the real zconf.h `typedef unsigned char Byte; typedef Byte Bytef;` — added faithfully. (2) the arm64 `-exec` compile-verify is CLEAN of any zlib error (front-end binds the zlib symbols to libz on arm64 — descriptor is target-agnostic); the only arm64 residuals are orthogonal (the non-exec-object `L_RequiredLirOpcodeMissing` externCallDispatch, generic to any extern TU; the `-exec` `K_SymbolUndefined` for the amalgamation's own `sqlite3_*`). **RESULT: zipfile.c F001A → CLEAN (0 errors); csv.c/normalize.c/test_recover.c stay 0 (no regression; sqlite3recover.c's 13×H0003 is the pre-existing separate blocker).** Tests: `RealZlibDescriptorLayoutAndLinkSurface` decode pin (`tests/ffi/test_shipped_lib_descriptor.cpp` — SONAME + 14-field/112B `offsetof` + 9-symbol count + the deflateInit2/inflateInit2-are-macros-NOT-symbols eager-import split + Z_* value pins; red-on-disable per field/symbol) + `examples/c-subset/zlib_roundtrip` RUN witness (a real deflate→inflate round-trip + crc32 cross-check → 42 on elf x86_64; behaviorally red-on-disable — deflateInit2_ rejects a wrong `sizeof(z_stream)`, deflate/inflate corrupt on a wrong offset). | Mechanism landed — no deferred work (row is provenance for the anchor guard). **FOLLOW-UP ✅ CLOSED 2026-07-21 (TF-C41 → `D-FFI-MACHO-NONDEFAULT-DYLIB-LOAD`):** the Mach-O writer used to populate `image.loadDylibs` ONLY from the format schema (libSystem) and REJECT any non-libSystem descriptor import library (`/usr/lib/libz.1.dylib`); `macho.cpp encodeExecDynamic` now AUTO-EMITS an `LC_LOAD_DYLIB` per referenced import library — the Mach-O sibling of the ELF `DT_NEEDED`-per-referenced-library walker (`D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED`). The `zlib_roundtrip` example gained an `arm64:macho64-arm64-darwin-exec` RUN arm that links a native arm64 Mach-O against the system libz and RUNS to exit 42 under dyld (witnessed on Apple Silicon; the macos-latest CI leg re-confirms). | `D-INCLUDE-ANGLE-SOURCE-FALLBACK` (the row that anticipated this `<zlib.h>` follow-up); `D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` (the sibling testfixture descriptor arc + the modeling precedent); `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-TYPEDEF-NAME-RESOLUTION` (Option C, the by-name z_stream/z_streamp); `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT` (why the two Init2 forms ship as macros not symbols); `D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED` (the ELF DT_NEEDED walker that links libz.so.1 + the Mach-O follow-up's model); `D-SHIPPED-HEADER-PER-TARGET-AVAILABILITY` (the elf/macho gate); `D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-DESCRIPTOR-AGNOSTIC` (the descriptor model) |
| `D-FFI-MACHO-NONDEFAULT-DYLIB-LOAD` | **✅ CLOSED 2026-07-21 (TF-C41, SQLite testfixture-link sub-arc, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`) — the Mach-O non-default-dylib `LC_LOAD_DYLIB` walker: the Mach-O sibling of the ELF `DT_NEEDED`-per-referenced-library walker (`D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED`).** ROOT: `src/link/format/macho.cpp encodeExecDynamic` built `libraryOrder` from each extern's `libraryPath` but then only VALIDATED that every referenced library was already declared in the format schema's `image.loadDylibs` (which ships just `/usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib`) and REJECTED otherwise (`K_SymbolUndefined` "extern imports reference library '/usr/lib/libz.1.dylib' but it is not declared in image.loadDylibs") — it never AUTO-EMITTED an `LC_LOAD_DYLIB` for a referenced non-libSystem library. So a C program importing zlib (`zlib.json` macho library `/usr/lib/libz.1.dylib`, `D-FFI-ZLIB-DESCRIPTOR`) compiled to Mach-O FAILED LOUD (purely additive gap; no silent broken binary). **FIX (config-free; `macho.cpp` walker only — AGNOSTIC, no arch/format/symbol identity, same shape as the ELF walker):** the emitted LC_LOAD_DYLIB set = the schema's `image.loadDylibs` UNION the DISTINCT referenced import libraries (`externImports[].libraryPath`), STABLE order (schema libs FIRST at their fixed ordinals — libSystem stays ordinal 1, so `_exit`/TLV binds are unmoved — then the referenced extras sorted lexicographically + deduped, the ELF DT_NEEDED ordering discipline). `totalDylibCmdSize`/`ncmds`/`sizeofcmds`/the `dylibOrdinal` map/the emission loop all key off the fuller `emittedDylibs` set, so each import's `BIND_OPCODE_SET_DYLIB_ORDINAL` targets the dylib that actually exports it (the libz import binds to ordinal 2, NOT libSystem's 1). timestamp/current_version/compatibility_version = 0/0/0 (dyld accepts 0; a `/usr/lib/...` dylib resolves by path even from the dyld shared cache). FAIL-LOUD PRESERVED: an extern with an EMPTY `libraryPath` is rejected (Mach-O's two-level namespace has no library to search — the ELF library-less guard's Mach-O analog; no `.so`-style exemption). **WITNESSES:** (1) host-independent encoding pins `MachOExecWriter.{NonLibSystemImportAutoEmitsLcLoadDylibAndBindsToItsOrdinal, EmptyLibraryPathImportFailsLoud}` (`tests/link/test_macho_writer.cpp`) — 2 LC_LOAD_DYLIB (libSystem then libz), the bind opcode stream decoded to prove `_deflate`→ordinal 2 / `_printf`→ordinal 1, ncmds +1 / sizeofcmds + the libz command size vs a single-lib baseline; RED-ON-DISABLE EMPIRICALLY DEMONSTRATED (revert the walker to validate-or-reject → the K_SymbolUndefined reject returns → the test fails). (2) THE RUNTIME WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/zlib_roundtrip` gained an `arm64:macho64-arm64-darwin-exec` RUN arm (`runOn` darwin/macos) — a native arm64 Mach-O links against the system libz (`otool -L`: libSystem + `/usr/lib/libz.1.dylib`) and RUNS to exit 42 under dyld on Apple Silicon (a real deflate→inflate+crc32 round-trip; a byte-perfect Mach-O can still fail to load, so only the run proves the LC_LOAD_DYLIB + binds are correct end-to-end). The macos-latest (Apple Silicon) CI leg re-confirms post-push. | (closed — the `emittedDylibs` = schema ∪ referenced walker in `macho.cpp encodeExecDynamic`) | `D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED` (the ELF `DT_NEEDED`-per-referenced-library walker this mirrors); `D-FFI-ZLIB-DESCRIPTOR` (the `<zlib.h>` descriptor whose macho library `/usr/lib/libz.1.dylib` this links + the follow-up row this closes); `D-LK3-DYLIB-WEAK-EXPORT` / `D-LK3-DYLIB-CHAINED-FIXUPS-EXPORT-TRIE` (Mach-O dylib EXPORT follow-ups a classic-bind exec importing libz does NOT need — left pinned); `src/link/format/macho.cpp` + `tests/link/test_macho_writer.cpp` + `examples/c-subset/zlib_roundtrip` |
| `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-FN-DEFINITION` | **✅ LANDED 2026-07-21 (SQLite testfixture-link sub-arc, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`/PR #46) — `extern` on a FUNCTION DEFINITION.** DSS could not parse `extern <T> f(){…}` (the `extern` storage-class on a function DEFINITION — valid C 6.9.1, symmetric with `static int f(){…}` which already worked), so the testfixture MAIN harness `bld-dss/tclsqlite-ex.c` P0009'd ×14 at `EXTERN int Sqlite3_Init(Tcl_Interp*){…}` (EXTERN = a tcl.h macro → `extern`). ROOT CAUSE: `externDecl` was a DECLARATION-ONLY production (ended in `EndStatement`/the import-library string) — no function-definition (`{body}`) arm — while `static` flowed through the general `topLevelDecl`+kindByChild function path (`ExternKeyword` is deliberately NOT a `singleDeclSpecifier`, so `extern` routes to its own `externDecl` unique-production, keeping `topLevel`/`statement` non-speculative). **§B RESOLVED (2026-07-21): "Fix the front-end" (general C, not a tcl hack).** MECHANISM (agnostic — grammar-config + the language-driven semantic/HIR the engine already walks; NO source/target/format branch): (1) GRAMMAR (c-subset.lang.json) — a new `externDeclTail` = `{ block | EndStatement }` (the twin of `topLevelDeclTail`) replaces externDecl's trailing `EndStatement`; a `block` tail ⇒ a function definition, an `EndStatement` tail ⇒ the ordinary declaration. FIRST(block)={BlockOpen} vs {Semicolon} DISJOINT → non-speculative. The `{optional stringLiteralExpr}` import-library override stays a DIRECT role child BEFORE the tail (externLibraryOverride unchanged; declaration-arm parse BYTE-PRESERVED except the `;` now rides externDeclTail, exactly like topLevelDecl's `;`). (2) SEMANTICS — the externDecl row gains `kindByChild` (childPath [2,0] → the tail's block child, whenKind function) + externDecl joins `scopes` (dominates the definition's params/body; the empty scope for a plain declaration is harmless — the topLevelDecl precedent; isDefinitionAtNode defaults true, and the generic `declNodeIsFunctionDefinition` binds a definition's params into that scope while a bare-proto's params still isolate via isPrototypeParamScopeNode). Keeps `nonDefiningDeclaration:true`. (3) ENGINE (semantic_analyzer.cpp, ONE condition) — `isExtern = decl.nonDefiningDeclaration && effectiveKind != Function`: an extern on a DEFINITION (kindByChild upgraded effectiveKind to Function) is DEFINING (a body present), so it collides (not merges) with a second definition + binds `__func__`, while a proto/object extern stays non-defining. Generic (any declarator-mode row that is both nonDefiningDeclaration AND kindByChild-Function). The Pass-1.5 `isFunctionForm` "exactly one declarator" S_InvalidFunctionDeclarator check is already kindByChild-generic → applies to extern (`extern int a, f(){}` fails loud). (4) HIR (cst_to_hir.cpp) — the isFn function-lowering block was EXTRACTED from `lowerTopLevelInto` into a shared `lowerDeclaratorModeFunction` helper; `lowerExternDeclInto` detects isFn (same kindByChild discriminator) and delegates to it (a real Function with an EMITTED body + EXTERNAL linkage), instead of ExternFunction. FAIL-LOUD (two guards, no silent miscompile): a NESTED function definition (a block-scope `extern int f(){…}`, not valid C) is rejected at the block-scope ExternDecl statement handler (never a silent hoist to module scope); the pathological `extern int f() "lib" {…}` (a library override AND a body — the string shifts kindByChild off the tail) is rejected H_ExternDeclMalformed at lowerExternDeclInto (never a silent body-drop). (5) tcl.json gains `{ EXTERN → extern }` (faithful to tcl.h's `extern TCL_STORAGE_CLASS`, minus the DSS-irrelevant DLLEXPORT/visibility). **RESULT: tclsqlite-ex.c 14 P0009 → CLEAN (0 errors).** Tests: `examples/c-subset/extern_fn_definition` RUN witness (`extern int addone(int x){return x+1;}` + main → 42, x86_64-ELF + arm64/qemu + release arm; value-divergent runtime arg) + parser pins (`ExternFunctionDefinitionParses`, the `ExternFunctionPrototypeParses` golden re-pinned to the externDeclTail wrapper, multi-declarator/prototype/import-override regressions) + semantic pins (extern-fn-def mints a Function WITH a body + external linkage NOT nonDefiningDeclaration; contrast static=internal + the bare prototype); red-on-disable = P0009 (revert the grammar tail arm). | Mechanism landed — no deferred work (row is provenance for the anchor guard). | `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-MULTI-DECLARATOR` (the declarator-mode externDecl this extends — the tail arm sits after the same initDeclaratorList); `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-LIBRARY-SYNTAX` (the import-override the tail preserves as a direct role child); `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-EXTERN` (the statement-position externDecl the nested-fn guard protects); `D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` (the testfixture arc + the tcl.json EXTERN macro); `topLevelDecl`/`topLevelDeclTail` (the plain/`static` function-definition path this mirrors) |
| `D-INCLUDE-ANGLE-SOURCE-FALLBACK` | **✅ LANDED 2026-07-21 (SQLite testfixture-link sub-arc) — angle `#include <h>` real-header fallback (Option A, user-ratified).** DSS mapped every `#include <angle>` to a `<stem>.json` shipped descriptor and NEVER fell back to a real header, so SQLite ext TUs that ANGLE-include `<sqlite3.h>` / `<sqlite3ext.h>` (headers that DO exist on the `-I` path but ship no descriptor) fatal'd `F_ShippedHeaderNotFound` (displayed F001A). **MECHANISM (agnostic — a generic resolver change, NO source/target/format branch):** a SHARED funnel `resolveAngleInclude(filename, systemDirs, includeDirs) -> {Descriptor \| Source \| NotFound}` in `src/core/types/include_path_resolve.{hpp,cpp}` — descriptor FIRST (`resolveSystemDescriptor`, keys on descriptor-FILE existence; per-format availability stays the CALLER's verdict, so an existing-but-unavailable descriptor does NOT fall through to source), else a REAL source header on the `-I` `includeDirs` (`findInDirs`; the angle form does NOT search the including file's own dir, C 6.10.2p2, UNLIKE quote), else NotFound. Wired at the TWO sites that must agree byte-for-byte (FC15c parity, so `#include`/`__has_include` can't drift): (1) the PP `SynthBuilder` ANGLE arm (`src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp`) — Source → `child.build` recursive TEXTUAL splice + DROP the directive, byte-for-byte like the quote-on-disk arm (an angle `<sqlite3ext.h>` then behaves EXACTLY like the quote `"sqlite3ext.h"` the 39 clean ext TUs use — its macros + typedefs inline into THIS TU, which a post-parse CrossTreeRef could NOT carry), GATED on `includeResolvable()` (P0016 — no eager file inline in a dead/uncertain branch; a dead one is left verbatim + elided by the authoritative pass, a wrongly-skipped live one fails loud downstream) + the quote arm's `includeStack` circular guard; Descriptor / NotFound flow the UNCHANGED macro-splice-or-verbatim path (Descriptor keeps the line for the import resolver's typed-surface injection; NotFound → verbatim → hard F_ShippedHeaderNotFound). (2) `__has_include(<h>)` (same file) — Descriptor → existing per-format availability; Source → true; NotFound → false. The post-parse import resolver is UNCHANGED (with the PP enabled it only ever sees Descriptor + NotFound angle directives after the PP drops the Source ones). ★ SITING (the design-audit catch): the fix is the PP TEXTUAL splice, NOT the post-parse resolver — a CrossTreeRef links symbols, not the header's `#define`s (`SQLITE_OK`, `SQLITE_EXTENSION_INIT1` are macros), and the includer parse-fails on them (P0009 cascade) BEFORE import resolution runs; only inlining the header text at preprocess time makes the TU clean. Clears sqlite `ext/misc/csv.c` + `ext/misc/normalize.c` (F001A → CLEAN). Tests: `Preprocessor.FC15cAngleSourceFallback{Splices,HasIncludeParity,…}` + `ImportResolver.CSubsetAngleSourceFallback{Resolves,DescriptorFirst,DoesNotSearchIncludingDir,TotalMissHardError}` (all red-on-disable → F_ShippedHeaderNotFound / no splice). | Mechanism landed — no deferred work (row is provenance for the anchor guard). | FC15c (`resolveSystemDescriptor` — the `#include`/`__has_include` parity funnel this extends); `D-PP-DESCRIPTOR-MACRO-INJECT` (the Descriptor arm the Source arm coexists with); `D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-DESCRIPTOR-AGNOSTIC` (the descriptor-first model); a `<zlib.h>` follow-up (`ext/misc/zipfile.c` still F001A — zlib.h is only in /usr/include, not on any `-I`; a separate zlib.json, out of scope) |
| `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-UNION-MEMBER-INJECTION` | **✅ LANDED 2026-07-21 (C34b, SQLite testfixture arc) — the named-member shipped `unions` surface.** A shipped-library descriptor may now declare a top-level `"unions": [{ "name": …, "fields": [{name,type}…] }]` surface — the named-member SIBLING of `structs`. Motivation: the real `Tcl_GetHashKey` macro reads `h->key.oneWordValue` / `h->key.string`, members of the `key` UNION field of `Tcl_HashEntry`, but the hir-text `union "N" { T,… }` spelling carries member TYPES positionally with NO names — so, exactly like `structs`, the member names must live in a descriptor surface. **MECHANISM (agnostic — generic over any union-typed field/member, NO `if(name==)`, no source/target/format branch):** (1) `src/ffi/shipped_lib_descriptor.{hpp,cpp}` — a `ShippedUnion {name, fields, typeId}` decoded right after `typedefs` (so a member may spell an earlier typedef by name, `ptr<Tcl_Obj>`) and before `symbols`/`structs` (so a later signature/struct-field spells the union BY NAME, Option C — the name is published into `mergedNamedTypes`); reuses `decodeStructFieldList`; interns via `TypeInterner::unionType` (`TypeKind::Union`, every member overlaid at offset 0, C 6.7.2.1); a member `offset` is REJECTED fail-loud (an explicit-offset overlapping layout is the c107 `structs` channel, `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-UNION-OVERLAY`, not this). (2) `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` — a `desc->unions` injection loop MIRRORING the `structs` loop: builds the member field scope + registers `compositeScopeByType[un.typeId]` (so `unionValue.member` resolves) + injects the union TAG (shares `injectedTags` with structs — one C 6.2.3 tag namespace). FAIL-LOUD: an unknown union member misses the scope → `S_UndeclaredField`/`S_UndeclaredIdentifier` (NOT a silent access). (3) `src/ffi/shipped_type_consistency.cpp` — `add()` walks `desc.unions` (the #47 `F_ShippedTypeIdentityConflict` per-target byte-identity guard, mirroring structs). **DATA (tcl.json):** the hash cluster — Tcl_HashTable (88B, keyType@60), Tcl_HashEntry (40B, clientData@24, key@32 the union), Tcl_HashSearch (24B), the Tcl_HashKey UNION (8B, all @0) — + 6 exported hash functions (Tcl_Init/DeleteHashTable/Create/First/Next/DeleteHashEntry, all `nm -D` `T`-verified) + 3 macros (Tcl_Get/SetHashValue + the REAL Tcl_GetHashKey) + 4 key-type constants (as macros, so TCL_ONE_WORD_KEYS/TCL_CUSTOM_PTR_KEYS expand inside the Tcl_GetHashKey body). Clears sqlite `src/test_malloc.c` (S0006 Tcl_HashTable/Tcl_HashSearch + S0001 Tcl_HashEntry + the hash API) → testfixture corpus 42→43. Tests: `UnionsSurfaceParsed`/`UnionsOnlyDescriptorIsValid`/`UnionMemberOffsetFailsLoud`/`UnionMemberBadTypeFailsLoud`/`UnionReferencedByNameInStructField`/`RealTclHashClusterLayout` (`tests/ffi/test_shipped_lib_descriptor.cpp`) + `ShippedUnionFieldMemberResolves`/`ShippedUnknownUnionMemberFailsLoud` (`tests/analysis/semantic/test_semantic_analyzer_c_subset.cpp`) + `examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_hash` RUN witness (a real ONE_WORD_KEYS Tcl_HashTable, Tcl_GetHashKey→key.oneWordValue → 42; red-on-disable: remove the union injection → S_NotAComposite). | Mechanism landed — no deferred work (row is provenance for the anchor guard). | `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-TYPEDEF-NAME-RESOLUTION` (Option C, reused for the by-name union reference); `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-UNION-OVERLAY` (the c107 explicit-offset STRUCT channel — the offset-bearing overlapping layout this deliberately is NOT); `D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` (the consumer arc); `D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY` (the #47 identity guard the unions walk extends) |
| `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-TYPEDEF-NAME-RESOLUTION` | **✅ LANDED 2026-07-21 (C34a, SQLite testfixture arc) — "Option C" (define once / reference by name).** `src/ffi/shipped_lib_descriptor.cpp` now resolves a descriptor's OWN `typedefs` FIRST (relocated before the `symbols` loop) and threads each resolved `name -> TypeId` into a working `mergedNamedTypes` span used for the REST of that descriptor's signature / struct-field / constant / later-typedef `parseTypeFromText` calls — REUSING the existing c82 `NamedTypeBinding` / `va_list` alias mechanism (the ONE structural change: typedefs decoded early + merged, seeded from the caller's bindings). So a descriptor may spell `ptr<Tcl_Obj>` BY NAME instead of re-inlining `struct "Tcl_Obj" {…}` at ~45 sites — collapsing the body-change ripple. Content-blind + agnostic (NO name special-cased; no source-language / CPU / object-format branch). Fail-loud PRESERVED: a genuinely-unknown type name still rejects at the hir-text identifier fallback (`hir_text.cpp` — the LAST resort, never shadows a keyword/primitive), and the #47 `F_ShippedTypeIdentityConflict` guard (`shipped_type_consistency.cpp`) still runs. Enabled the real Tcl_Obj (48B, typePtr@24) + Tcl_ObjType (40B) + Tcl_CmdInfo.objProc fn-ptr modeling in `tcl.json` (clears test1.c S000D×2 + S0004). Tests: `OptionCDescriptorTypedefReferencedByName` + `OptionCUnknownTypeNameStillFailsLoud` + the grown `RealTclDescriptorDecodesLinkSurface` pin (`tests/ffi/test_shipped_lib_descriptor.cpp`) + `examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_objfields` RUN witness. | Mechanism landed — no deferred work (row is provenance for the anchor guard). | `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VA-LIST-TYPE` (the `NamedTypeBinding` reuse); `D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` (the consumer arc); `D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY` (the #47 identity guard that still runs) |
| `D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` | **OPEN (arc ACTIVE) — C1 LANDED 2026-07-17: the seed `<tcl.h>` FFI descriptor + libtcl-link RUN witness.** The first brick of the SQLite **testfixture** arc (user 2026-07-17: "run as many /dss-cycle as needed to full testfixture. TCL included (start by it). we need to be a complete compiler working" — this LIFTS the linker/FFI hold; PRs #41/#42/#44 landed the backend). "All sqlite units" = build SQLite's `testfixture` (the binary that runs the 1190-file `.test` suite) with DSS + run it. testfixture = ~90 dense C TUs (44 `src/test*.c` + ext/* + `tclsqlite-ex.c` + `sqlite3.c`) linked against **libtcl8.6** + libz + libm. KEY DE-RISK (probed 2026-07-17): testfixture calls TCL **DIRECTLY** — its `make -n testfixture` recipe carries NO `-DUSE_TCL_STUBS` (that flag is only on the loadable-EXTENSION targets), and tcl.h has zero `tclStubsPtr` — so `tcl.json` is an ORDINARY (large) FFI descriptor of plain extern protos, NOT the architecturally-risky stubs-macro form. API surface is bounded: 106 distinct `Tcl_*` symbols across all test TUs. **C1 SHIPPED:** `src/dss-config/shippedLibs/tcl.json` (5 syms Tcl_CreateInterp/DeleteInterp/Eval/GetObjResult/GetIntFromObj + opaque Tcl_Interp/Tcl_Obj typedefs modeled like stdio.json's FILE; elf `library`=libtcl8.6.so [TCL's unversioned SONAME — present in the runtime pkg, so it resolves without tcl8.6-dev], availableObjectFormats:[elf]) + `examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_eval` (RUN witness: DSS compiles → ELF with NEEDED libc.so.6 + NEEDED libtcl8.6.so [the `D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED` libm DT_NEEDED precedent]; real libtcl evaluates `expr 40 + 2` → exit 42 on elf-x86_64, empirically verified in WSL) + the `RealTclDescriptorDecodesLinkSurface` always-on unit pin (SONAME + signatures, red-on-disable) + a CI `libtcl8.6` install (pipeline-pr.yml x86_64 legs; native-arm64 arch-gates the run, windows/macos compile-only). **★ C2 ✅ LANDED 2026-07-17:** grew tcl.json 5→8 symbols (+ Tcl_NewIntObj/Tcl_SetObjResult/Tcl_CreateObjCommand — the last carries a FUNCTION-POINTER param `ptr<fn(...)>`, the signal.json handler precedent) + `shipped_tcl_command` witness (a C command registered via Tcl_CreateObjCommand that **libtcl CALLS BACK** — the FFI reverse-callback, the core shape of every SQLite `test*.c` command — sums its objv args → 42) + the fn-ptr-param unit pin (red-on-disable). ★ **C3 ✅ LANDED 2026-07-17: the `-I`/`--include-dir` quote-include CLI flag** (all 4 forms `-I<dir>`/`-I <dir>`/`--include-dir <dir>`/`=<dir>` → `Options.includeDirs` → the PRE-EXISTING but CLI-unwired `UnitBuilder::addIncludeDir`; a `program.cpp::applyIncludeDirs` helper resolves each to absolute [cwd-relative gcc semantics] + threads at BOTH CU-build sites; empirical pe64 `-I`→compile→run→42; pins: `program/test_include_dirs` [red-on-disable resolution — without `-I` → P0016, with → clean] + `cli_args` 4-form parse). ★ **CORRECTS the C2 probe note** (which said "-I not needed"): `-I` IS needed — test_backup.c (includes the GENERATED `sqlite3.h`, in bld-dss/ not src/) P0016'd without it, and `-I <bld-dss>` CLEARS the P0016 (34 next-layer TCL `S0006` undeclared-type errors then remain — ClientData/Tcl_CmdInfo/…). test1.c's earlier odd resolve was via its own `sqliteInt.h` self-dir chain, not the general case. The frontier now = the TCL API surface + POSIX sys/* descriptors + CONST/Tcl_Size PP-macros, driven by real test-TU compiles. **★ C4 ✅ LANDED 2026-07-17:** the TCL scalar/string/result surface — `ClientData` typedef (void*) + `TCL_OK`/`TCL_ERROR`/`TCL_STATIC`/`NULL` PP-macros (the `macros` surface, signal.json precedent) + `Tcl_GetString`/`Tcl_WrongNumArgs`/`Tcl_SetResult`/`Tcl_AppendResult` (the last VARIADIC — the stdio printf precedent). Witness `examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_strcmd` (a string-arg command → libtcl calls back → 42); test_backup.c DROPS **34 → 6** errors (remaining 6 = the `Tcl_CmdInfo` command-introspection layer = C5). Gate: Win 674/674 + Linux 2384/2384 + release→42 + the decode pin (12 syms / 3 typedefs incl ClientData=Ptr). **★ C5 ✅ LANDED 2026-07-17:** the TCL command-INTROSPECTION layer — the `Tcl_CmdInfo` struct (the FIRST field-access shipped struct here: 8 fields [`isNativeObjectProc` i32 + 7 opaque `ptr<void>`] via the `structs` surface so `info.isNativeObjectProc`/`info.clientData` RESOLVE + libtcl fills them at the ABI-correct offsets) + its BARE typedef (the `structs` surface gives tag+fields but NOT the bare-name type → S0006 without the typedef row) + `Tcl_GetCommandInfo`/`Tcl_SetCommandInfo`/`Tcl_DeleteCommand`/`Tcl_GetIndexFromObjStruct`; ALSO corrected Tcl_CreateObjCommand's 5th param (deleteProc) from `ptr<void>` → a real `ptr<fn(ptr<void>)->void>` (was S0003-rejecting a genuine fn-ptr). 16 syms total. Witness `examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_cmdinfo` (registers a command, `Tcl_GetCommandInfo` fills the struct, asserts `info.isNativeObjectProc==1` [a RUNTIME LAYOUT witness — offset 0, set by libtcl for an ObjCommand], writes `info.clientData` + `Tcl_SetCommandInfo`, evals → 42, deletes → 'tcl-cmdinfo-ok'/42 on REAL libtcl8.6). **test_backup.c DROPS 6 → 5 — and the residual 5 are a DIFFERENT LAYER**: all `K_SymbolUndefined` for `sqlite3_backup_init`/`step`/`finish`/`remaining`/`pagecount` (SQLite's OWN public API — declared in sqlite3.h, no import library binds it) — so test_backup.c is now PARSE+SEMANTIC+IR-CLEAN on the TCL surface; the sqlite3 amalgamation API is the **C6** layer (link sqlite3.c or a sqlite3.json descriptor), NOT a TCL concern. Gate: Win 675/675 + WSL full ctest 675/675 (QEMU_LD_PREFIX; #525 shipped_tcl_cmdinfo RUNS→42 incl. the release arm) + cmdinfo direct runtime→42 + the decode pin (16 syms / 4 typedefs incl Tcl_CmdInfo=Struct / the fn-ptr param / the 8-field struct). **★ C7 ✅ LANDED 2026-07-17:** the `CONST` compat macro. tcl.h's `#define CONST const` is the OLD-Tcl objProc form `Tcl_Obj *CONST objv[]` (an array of const-pointers — the real ObjCmdProc signature), used by 30 of the 44 sqlite `src/test*.c`. Before C7, DSS left CONST UNEXPANDED (tcl.json lacked it) so `Tcl_Obj *CONST` parsed as pointer + a declarator named `CONST`, then `objv` was unexpected → P0001 'expected ) got objv' — **the DOMINANT test-TU blocker AFTER the C6 extern-aggregate fix (16 of the 44 TUs hit it FIRST)**. FIX (config-only): add `{ CONST → const }` to tcl.json's `macros` (the signal.json PP-macro precedent; 5 macros now). Witness `examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_constcmd` (a command whose objProc uses `Tcl_Obj *CONST objv[]` → libtcl calls it back on `mysum 40 2` → 42) + the decode pin GROWN to assert the CONST macro present + replacement 'const' (red-on-disable EMPIRICALLY demonstrated — rename CONST in tcl.json → the decode test FAILS 'missing Tcl macro: CONST'). **Frontier advanced: 'got objv' 16→0** (test_autoext.c etc. now parse their command signatures); CLEAN test TUs 18→20. The NEW dominant blocker = 13 `S0006` (undeclared type — `time_t`/POSIX-descriptor cases; ★ `Tcl_Size` [13 TUs] is SQLite-DEFINED [tclsqlite.h:39 `#define Tcl_Size int`, tclsqlite.c:51], NOT a tcl.json concern) = C8. Gate: Win 677/677 + WSL 677/677 + constcmd runtime→42 + the decode pin (5 macros incl. CONST). **★ C8 ✅ LANDED 2026-07-17:** the 5 missing TCL TYPEDEFS — the dominant S0006 'undeclared type' blocker after C7 (13/44 src/test*.c hit S0006 first, dominantly `Tcl_ObjCmdProc` [27 uses] / `Tcl_CmdProc` [19]). Added to tcl.json (9 typedefs total): **`Tcl_ObjCmdProc`** + **`Tcl_CmdProc`** — FUNCTION-TYPE typedefs (`int(ClientData,Tcl_Interp*,int,Tcl_Obj*const*)` / `...char**`), the **FIRST fn-type typedefs in a shipped descriptor** (used mostly as `Tcl_ObjCmdProc *` = a fn-pointer; the loader accepts a bare `fn(...)->i32` typedef target — EMPIRICALLY verified, shipped_tcl_eval still loads); **`Tcl_WideInt`** = i64 (long long); **`Tcl_DString`** = a 216-byte struct (`char*+int+int+char[200]`; used as a LOCAL so the size is load-bearing → `arr<u64,27>`); **`Tcl_Channel`** = an opaque ptr. Witness `examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_typedefs` (registers a command via a `Tcl_ObjCmdProc *` fn-type-typedef pointer → libtcl calls back summing a Tcl_WideInt → 42; asserts `sizeof(Tcl_DString)==216` at RUNTIME [a layout witness] + refs Tcl_Channel) + the decode pin grown to 9 typedefs (Tcl_ObjCmdProc/Tcl_CmdProc=FnSig, Tcl_WideInt=I64, Tcl_DString=Struct, Tcl_Channel=Ptr; red-on-disable EMPIRICALLY demonstrated — rename Tcl_ObjCmdProc → the decode test FAILS). **Frontier advanced: S0006 13→1** (only `time_t`×2 remains — DEFERRED normal: sys/types.json + time.json already define time_t, but test_quota's use isn't reached — a POSIX header-mapping/injection follow-up, C9+); CLEAN test TUs 20→21. The NEW dominant = 12 S0001 (undeclared Tcl FUNCTIONS — Tcl_GetInt/Tcl_NewObj/Tcl_NewStringObj/Tcl_DecrRefCount/… + the `TCL_VOLATILE` macro + POSIX errno) = C9. Gate: Win 678/678 + WSL 678/678 + typedefs runtime→42. **★ C9 ✅ LANDED 2026-07-17:** the TCL FUNCTION surface — **39 exported `Tcl_*` functions** (the dominant S0001 'undeclared identifier' blocker after C8: 12/44 src/test*.c, dominantly Tcl_NewStringObj [26]/Tcl_SetVar2 [25]/Tcl_ListObjAppendElement [27]) + 8 flag macros (TCL_GLOBAL_ONLY/TCL_EVAL_GLOBAL/TCL_VOLATILE/TCL_APPEND_VALUE/TCL_LIST_ELEMENT/TCL_LINK_INT/TCL_LINK_READ_ONLY) + ckalloc/ckfree function-macros. tcl.json symbols 16→55. The 40-signature batch was modeled by an INDEPENDENT subagent (from tclDecls.h protos, hir-text-translated) + empirically verified (descriptor loads, S0001 drops). Witness `examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_listfuncs` (a command builds a Tcl list [Tcl_NewObj/Tcl_ListObjAppendElement/Tcl_NewStringObj], reads it [Tcl_ListObjLength/Index/GetIntFromObj], sums 40+2→42; + Tcl_SetVar/TCL_GLOBAL_ONLY/Tcl_ResetResult) + decode pin grown to 55 symbols (+ a NEGATIVE assertion that Incr/DecrRefCount stay ABSENT). **★ CRITICAL FINDING (subagent, `nm -D`-verified):** `Tcl_IncrRefCount`/`Tcl_DecrRefCount` are MACROS in real tcl.h, NOT exported by libtcl8.6.so — and DSS EAGER-IMPORTS all descriptor functions even when UNREFERENCED, so listing them broke the LOAD of EVERY tcl binary (shipped_tcl_eval→exit 127; A/B-proven 56-sym→127 vs 54-sym→42). Dropped them → all 6 existing witnesses recover to 42. Deferred: [[D-FFI-TCL-REFCOUNT-MACROS]] (model as macros; blocked on a Tcl_Obj named-field layout + the DecrRefCount free path) + [[D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT]] (DSS should import only REFERENCED descriptor symbols). **Frontier: S0001 12→7** (residual = the 2 refcount macros [39 uses, deferred] + POSIX errno/fopen/MAP_FAILED + SQLite-defined sqlite3TestTextToPtr); CLEAN test TUs 21→24. Gate: Win 679/679 + WSL 679/679 + witness→42. | **REMAINING arc cycles:** (a) grow tcl.json to the full 106-`Tcl_`-symbol surface + the real Tcl_Obj layout; (b) a multi-dir `-I` include-path CLI flag (the internal `preprocessor.includeDirs`/`import_resolver.includeDirs` machinery EXISTS but no CLI flag exposes it — the test*.c `#include "sqliteInt.h"`/`"vdbeInt.h"` + generated headers span src/+bld-dss/); (c) a `zlib.json` descriptor (libz link, `-DSQLITE_HAVE_ZLIB=1`); (d) compile the ~90 test TUs (each surfaces compiler gaps); (e) link `testfixture`; (f) run the `.test` corpus; (g) pe/macho/arm64 TCL sonames + per-runner libtcl provisioning. | plan 23 §0.1 (testfixture arc); `D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED` (the external-`.so` DT_NEEDED precedent); `D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-DESCRIPTOR-AGNOSTIC` (the descriptor model); `real-examples/c/sqlite/build-and-test.{sh,ps1}` (the pre-existing SELECT-sum→42 smoke harness) |
| `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-INCLUDES` | **LANDED (TF arc C32, 2026-07-20, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`/PR #46) — the transitive shipped-header `#include` graph.** A shipped-library descriptor may declare an optional top-level `"includes": ["stdio.h"]` string array naming the sibling headers it transitively `#include`s in the real world; when a TU includes the parent header (so DSS resolves the parent's `<stem>.json`), DSS ALSO resolves + injects the declared sibling descriptors' surfaces (symbols/typedefs/constants/macros) into that TU — modeling the real transitive graph a FLAT descriptor cannot carry. **Motivation:** the FILE §B — real `tcl.h` `#include`s `<stdio.h>`, but DSS's flat `tcl.json` didn't carry that, so sqlite `src/test_md5.c` (which reaches tcl.json via `tclsqlite.h → <tcl.h>`) S0001'd on `FILE *in;` + the fopen/fread/fseek/fclose/SEEK_SET cascade. **MECHANISM (agnostic — a generic config-declared graph walk, NO `if(name==tcl)`, no source/target/format identity branch):** a SHARED cycle-safe closure walker `ffi::forEachDescriptorInClosure` (shipped_lib_descriptor.cpp) — a single DFS keyed on the WEAKLY-CANONICAL descriptor path (the SAME key the semantic `readDescriptors` dedup + `cachedDescriptorJson` use) so it TERMINATES on cycles (A→B→A) + diamonds — expanded at the TWO tiers that have `systemDirs`: the import resolver (records a `ShippedDescriptorRef` per closure descriptor, parent-FIRST → the semantic first-wins dedup lets the named parent beat a transitive sibling) + the preprocessor macro-splice (`spliceSystemDescriptorMacros` splices each closure descriptor's macros, each independently per-format-gated). The interner-less semantic tier is NOT hooked (it lacks `systemDirs`); it is UNCHANGED, just receiving more refs. Config decoded via `decodeShippedIncludes` (shared by the interned `readShippedLibDescriptor` + the interner-free `readShippedLibIncludes`, lock-step, the `decodeShippedMacros` precedent). **FAIL-LOUD (uniform, SUB-FORK 2 Option X):** an unresolvable `includes` entry (typo `stdioo.h`) → `F_ShippedHeaderNotFound` positioned on the `#include` line (import resolver — the ONLY tier with `systemDirs` to catch it; the preprocessor defers to it, dead-branch-inert); a malformed `includes` FIELD (non-array / non-string) → `F_ShippedLibDescriptorMalformed` (readShippedLibDescriptor/readShippedLibIncludes); a needed-but-per-format-absent sibling symbol → downstream S0001 (P0016-safe). **DATA EDIT (need-driven, minimal):** `tcl.json` gains `"includes": ["stdio.h"]` (stdarg.h/limits.h are real edges DEFERRED until a consumer needs them). Witness `examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_transitive_stdio` (`#include <tcl.h>` with NO direct `<stdio.h>`, opens a file via FILE/fopen/fread/fclose reached ONLY through tcl.json→stdio.h → exit 42, elf x86_64 + arm64/qemu, release + baseline arms; red-on-disable: remove `includes` → S0001 FILE). Unit pins: `IncludesSurfaceDecodes`/`IncludesMalformedFailsLoud`/`ClosureCycleTerminates`/`ClosureDiamondVisitsOnce`/`ClosureUnresolvedIncludeReported` + the tcl.json decode pin grown to assert `includes==["stdio.h"]` + a preprocessor closure-splice pin. **test_md5.c ADVANCES (its FILE/stdio S0001 class GONE) but is NOT yet CLEAN** — a SEPARATE residual fn-ptr→`void*`(ClientData) strict-conversion S0003 (4 sites, `Md5_Init` passing a function to `Tcl_CreateCommand`'s clientData param; ISO C 6.3.2.3, POSIX/gcc-lenient) is a distinct next-cycle policy blocker `includes` does not touch (batched with the C15 char-family-pointer §B). CLEAN test TU delta from this cycle = 0 (test_md5 stays non-clean), but `includes` is the correct best-long-term transitive-include fix that also generalizes to test_syscall/test_quota. | (mechanism BUILT this cycle — the closure walker + `includes` config field + tcl.json edit; the fn-ptr→void* residual + stdarg.h/limits.h edges are the deferred follow-ons) | `D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` (parent testfixture arc); `D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-DESCRIPTOR-AGNOSTIC` (the descriptor model); `D-SHIPPED-HEADER-PER-TARGET-AVAILABILITY` (the per-format gate the transitive refs reuse); `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT` (why the injected stdio symbols are load-safe on elf); `D-PP-DESCRIPTOR-MACRO-INJECT` (the preprocessor macro-splice tier the walker extends) |
| `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-AGGREGATE-TYPE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-17 (TF arc C6, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`/PR #46).** A file-scope `extern` declaration whose type-specifier is a struct/union/enum aggregate — `extern struct Foo g;` / `extern union U u;` / `extern enum E e;` (+ the body `extern struct Foo {int x;} g;`, const `extern const struct Foo g;`, and pointer `extern struct Foo *p;` forms) — P0009'd ("expected [simple-type-specifiers / Identifier / const / typeof] — got 'struct'"). `static struct`/plain `struct Foo g;`/`typedef struct` ALL worked — the `extern` path was the SOLE gap. ROOT CAUSE: `externDecl → typeRef → typeBase`, and `typeBase` (c-subset.lang.json) omitted `structSpec`/`unionSpec`/`enumSpec` (the regular decl path uses `topLevelCompositeSpec`, which carries all three). **This is the DOMINANT sqlite-testfixture compile blocker: 19 of 44 `src/test*.c` TUs hit it FIRST** via sqliteInt.h:5505 `extern SQLITE_WSD struct Sqlite3Config sqlite3Config;` (SQLITE_WSD → empty in the default non-WSD build). The amalgamation transforms that line away (grep: no `extern .* struct Sqlite3Config` in sqlite3.c) — which is why `sqlite3.c` compiled clean while the test TUs, which `#include "sqliteInt.h"` directly, did not. FIX (config-only, agnostic — parser engine untouched): add the 3 aggregate arms + `speculative`/`lookahead` to `typeBase`, mirroring `typeBaseAllowingStruct`/`topLevelCompositeSpec`. **Zero semantic/HIR change** — the type resolver dispatches on the composite node's OWN rule (structSpec/unionSpec/enumSpec isTagReference / definition arms), not on externDecl, so an aggregate extern resolves IDENTICALLY to a `static struct Foo g;`. Witness `examples/c-subset/extern_aggregate` (extern struct + union + enum, runtime field read → 42). **★ 2026-07-18 (D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-MULTI-DECLARATOR): externDecl was re-routed off the `typeRef`→`typeBase` pair (both RETIRED) onto the shared star-free field head `typeRefAllowingStruct`→`typeBaseAllowingStruct`, which carries the IDENTICAL aggregate arms — so this C6 capability is preserved verbatim (extern_aggregate still → 42) AND the deferred 4-site type-specifier unification noted below is partly advanced (extern + field now share ONE base production).** | (closed — the extern type-base now accepts the same type-specifiers as the regular decl path; C23 re-route folded `typeBase` into `typeBaseAllowingStruct`) | plan 23 §0.1 "TF" row; `D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` (parent arc); `typeBaseAllowingStruct` (the struct-field sibling); DEFERRED (normal, no trigger): a full 4-site type-specifier unification (`typeBase`/`typeBaseAllowingStruct`/`typeSpecifierForDecl`/`typedefHead` → one shared production) — grammar hygiene, pre-existed C6 (3 sites), out of the extern-gap scope |
| `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-EXTERN` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-18 (TF arc C10, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`/PR #46).** A BLOCK-scope `extern` declaration inside a function body — a FUNCTION prototype (`extern int f(int);`) AND an OBJECT reference (`extern T *p;`) — P0009'd ("expected [expression/statement starters] — got 'extern'"). ROOT CAUSE: the `statement` alt (c-subset.lang.json) had no `extern`-led branch (and `localDeclSpecifier` omits ExternKeyword), so `extern` at statement position was rejected — `static`/`register`/`auto`/`typedef` block-scope decls ALL worked; extern was the SOLE gap. **DOMINANT single-cause test-TU blocker after C9: 6 of 44 `src/test*.c` hit it FIRST** (test_thread.c:116 `extern int Sqlitetest_mutex_Init(Tcl_Interp*);`, test_btree.c:32 `extern BtShared *sqlite3SharedCacheList;`, test2/test_func/test_multiplex/test_tclsh — 3 fn + 3 object incl. a pointer). FIX (config + one lowering arm, agnostic — no engine identity branch): (1) add `externDecl` to the `statement` alt (ExternKeyword-led, FIRST-disjoint → non-speculative unique-survivor direct descent); (2) a `cst_to_hir.cpp` `lowerStmt` `k=="ExternDecl"` arm that REUSES the file-scope `lowerExternDecl` WHOLESALE — it mints the ExternFunction/ExternGlobal + records the import row — routing that node to the module-decls accumulator (the `D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-STATIC` / `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-PROTOTYPE` pattern) and lowering the STATEMENT to a no-op (empty Block, the Skip precedent). Pass-1's rule-keyed full-tree walk mints the `nonDefiningDeclaration` symbol into `current` (BLOCK) scope; collectExterns registers it → block uses resolve via GlobalAddr, IDENTICAL to a file-scope extern; a cross-TU DEFINITION resolves at the LK11 merge (import stripped, call rewired direct). **★ DELIBERATELY does NOT re-home to file scope the way `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-PROTOTYPE` does for bare prototypes** — block-scope binding is REQUIRED so a block extern OBJECT that shadows an outer local (C 6.2.1) reads the extern, not the local (independent design-audit Finding 3; do NOT "unify" the two paths, that regresses object shadowing). Design-audit Finding 2 (same extern name block-declared in 2 functions → 2 SymbolIds → 2 import rows) verified BENIGN: the ELF symbol table dedups by name (throwaway multiplicity probe → 42). Fail-loud inherited unchanged: block-scope `extern int x=5;` → `H_ExternHasInitializer`. Witness `examples/c-subset/block_scope_extern` (cross-CU main.c+def.c, BOTH a fn proto + an object extern, release + baseline arms, → 42 on elf-x86_64 + arm64/qemu) + `HirLoweringCSubset.BlockScopeExternRehomesToModuleDecls` (CST→HIR module-decl structural pin; red-on-disable: drop the lowerStmt arm → "statement maps to unsupported HIR kind 'ExternDecl'"; the grammar red-on-disable demonstrated via a runtime-config strip → P0009). **The 6 P0009-on-extern TUs all advance past extern** (test_btree/test_multiplex → CLEAN; test2/test_func/test_tclsh/test_thread → residual S0001/S0002/S0003 descriptor/POSIX blockers). Gate: Win + WSL full ctest + example → 42 ×2 legs + anchor guard. **★ 2026-07-18 (D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-MULTI-DECLARATOR): the no-re-home shadow rule is PRESERVED under the C23 declarator-mode re-route — the isProto file-scope re-home is now explicitly suppressed for a `nonDefiningDeclaration` extern, so a block-scope `extern int c, d;` (multi-declarator) OR a block-scope extern FUNCTION binds in the CURRENT block scope, exactly as the C10 wholesale-reuse did. block_scope_extern re-verified.** | (closed — block-scope extern parses + lowers + links; the C23 re-route made externDecl declarator-mode + preserved the no-re-home rule) | plan 23 §0.1 "TF" row; `D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` (parent arc); `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-PROTOTYPE` (the bare-proto sibling — re-homes to file scope; this deliberately does NOT); `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-AGGREGATE-TYPE` (the C6 file-scope-extern sibling); `D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-STATIC` (the moduleDecls_ accumulator pattern) |
| `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-MULTI-DECLARATOR` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-18 (TF arc, `feature/c23-again-2324234324`).** A MULTI-declarator `extern` declaration — `extern int a, b;` (sqlite test1.c:9341 `extern int sqlite3_sync_count, sqlite3_fullsync_count;`) — P0009'd ("expected EndStatement — got ','") at BOTH file scope AND block scope. ROOT CAUSE: the legacy `externDecl` grammar (`externSpecifiers typeRef Identifier externTail`) was a SINGLE-declarator production — one `Identifier` bound at a fixed child index, a bespoke func/object tail (`externFuncTail | varDeclTail`), and the pointer stars baked into the shared `typeRef` head. It predated the multi-declarator machinery every other declaration position uses. FIX (config + a contained HIR N-emitter + one engine condition; agnostic — no lang/arch/format branch): (1) GRAMMAR — externDecl is now the extern twin of `topLevelDecl`: `externSpecifiers typeRefAllowingStruct initDeclaratorList EndStatement` — the SAME star-free base-type head struct fields use (pointer stars bind PER-DECLARATOR so `extern int *a, b;` types a:int*/b:int) + the PROVEN `initDeclaratorList` (each declarator owns its pointer/array/fn suffix + optional initializer). The retired single-declarator rules `typeBase`/`typeRef`/`externTail`/`externFuncTail`/`varDeclTail` were DELETED (no dead config). The DSS per-declaration import-library override (`extern void* GetStdHandle(int) "kernel32.dll";` — D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-LIBRARY-SYNTAX, witnessed by examples/c-subset/hello_writefile) is PRESERVED: re-homed from the retired tails to a TRAILING `{optional stringLiteralExpr}` on externDecl (after the declarator list, before `;`; FIRST disjoint from Comma/`=`/`;` → non-speculative, applies to every declarator in the declaration); CST→HIR's `lowerExternDeclInto` decodes it (externLibraryOverride) into each emitted extern's import-library map. D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-AGGREGATE-TYPE (`extern struct Foo g;`) preserved — typeBaseAllowingStruct carries the identical specifiers; a `volatile` head is a strict gain (advances the deferred 4-site type-specifier unification). (2) SEMANTIC — the row is DECLARATOR-MODE (head:0/declaratorList:1), routing through the EXISTING per-declarator minting that already honors `nonDefiningDeclaration` → N `isExternDeclaration` symbols, each with its own folded type; `requireNamedDeclarators` fails loud on an abstract declarator (`extern int *;`). A function declarator (`extern int f(int);`) is a prototype (isProtoDeclaration via hasFnSuffixOnName), Variable+isProto (the same model topLevelDecl uses for a bare proto — observable HIR = ExternFunction, unchanged). (3) ENGINE (one condition, semantic_analyzer.cpp) — the isProto file-scope re-home (D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-PROTOTYPE) is SUPPRESSED for a `nonDefiningDeclaration` extern, so a block-scope extern (OBJECT or FUNCTION) binds in the CURRENT block scope (preserves D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-EXTERN shadow correctness — a re-home would mis-scope an outer-local shadow). (4) HIR — `lowerExternDecl` → `lowerExternDeclInto(node, out)`, an N-emitter mirroring lowerVarLikeInto's declarator loop: per declarator, skip an absorbed extern, emit ExternFunction (isProto — FnSig carries params, {} children, the proto-synth precedent) or ExternGlobal, reject an initializer LOUD per-declarator (D-FF2-3 H_ExternHasInitializer). extern-with-initializer stays fail-loud; the single-declarator path (`extern int a;`, `extern int f(int);`, `extern struct S g;`, `extern char v[];`) is byte-preserved. Witness `examples/c-subset/extern_multi_declarator` (file-scope `extern int a, b;` + block-scope `extern int c, d;`, cross-TU def, release + baseline arms → 42 on elf-x86_64 + arm64/qemu) + parser/semantic red-on-disable pins. | (closed — externDecl is a declarator-mode declaration-list rule; N declarators mint N symbols + emit N extern nodes at both scopes) | plan 23 §0.1 "TF" row; `D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` (parent testfixture arc); `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-EXTERN` (the C10 block-scope sibling — its no-re-home shadow rule is preserved for the multi-declarator form); `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-AGGREGATE-TYPE` + `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-INCOMPLETE-ARRAY` (extern type-base/incomplete-array capabilities carried through the re-route); `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR` (the struct-field multi-declarator precedent); `typeRefAllowingStruct` + `initDeclaratorList` (the reused proven rules) |
| `D-CSUBSET-TESTTU-SILENT-EXIT1` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-18 (TF arc C12, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`/PR #46) — surfaced C10, root-caused C11 (partially wrong), fixed C12.** 7 of 44 sqlite `src/test*.c` TUs (test6, test_devsym, test_journal, test_vdbecov, test_vfs, test_window, test_wsd) compiled with **exit 1 and ZERO bytes on BOTH stdout AND stderr** — a SILENT FAILURE (the worst class: a non-zero exit with no diagnostic = a fail-loud violation). **★ C12 CORRECTION + FIX:** the C11 localization to `optimizeModule` (below) was WRONG — `buildCuMir` returns NON-null for an empty module; a Debug-build instrumentation pass pinned the real cause to **FOUR `expectedFuncCount > 0` gates in the LIR/asm/link tiers' `ok()` methods** — legalize (`lir_2addr_legalize.hpp`), callconv (`lir_callconv.hpp`), assemble (`asm.hpp`), link (`linker.hpp`) — each rejecting a 0-function module in sequence (`compile_pipeline.cpp` itself unchanged; the reject was in the `ok()` definitions it calls). FIX (agnostic — no lang/arch/format branch): relax all 4 (`ok()` = count-match) + **completion flags** (`allFunctionsLegalized` / new `allFunctionsLaidOut` / new `linkedCleanly`) set ONLY on the success/empty conclusion so a FAILURE that returns an empty result stays fail-loud (the silent-miscompile seam — independently verified: each flag set at exactly one success/empty point, never a failure early-return) + a fail-loud net (`D_CompileUnitNullNoDiagnostic` 0xD013 if `buildCuMir`/`lowerCuMirToAssembly` ever returns null WITHOUT a reported diagnostic — the D-PERF-4 contract belt). The ELF/PE/Mach-O object writers needed NO logic change (they already emit valid empty relocatable objects; only stale comments updated). Empty/decl-only TU → valid empty object + exit 0, matching gcc/clang (C89 6.9 admits decl-only TUs; testfixture-link-critical: test_wsd.o is legitimately empty in a standard build). Witness `Program_CompileFiles.EmptyDeclOnlyTuEmitsValidEmptyObject` (a decl-only TU → valid ELF `.o` + rc 0; red-on-disable: restore any `> 0` clause → the test reds) + 7 flipped tier tests (asm_substrate ×2 / lir_2addr_legalize / lir_callconv EmptyModuleIsOk / linker EmptyModuleIsOk / wasm_writer / spirv_writer). **The 7 sqlite silent TUs → rc 0 (valid empty objects); verified across x86_64-ELF/PE + arm64-ELF/Mach-O relocatable.** Residual (pinned separately): a truly-empty TU compiles on all 4 formats, but a decl-only TU that emits an EXTERN IMPORT on arm64-ELF hits a PRE-EXISTING `L_RequiredLirOpcodeMissing` (arm64-ELF-relocatable declares no `externCallDispatch` shape) — unrelated to this fix, x86_64-ELF (the testfixture target) unaffected → [[D-LK-ARM64-ELF-RELOC-EXTERN-DISPATCH]]. Gate: Win + WSL full ctest + the empty-TU witness + anchor guard. **(C11 diagnosis retained below for the audit trail — it was a reasonable but incorrect localization; the verifier/optimizeModule ACCEPT an empty module.)** DIAGNOSIS so far (gdb, C10): `main` RETURNS 1 (exit(1) reached from `__libc_start_call_main` — the normal main-return path, NOT a signal/segfault), and a `catch throw` catchpoint never fires (NO C++ exception is thrown) — so a normal control-flow path in the compile driver returns failure WITHOUT routing through the diagnostic reporter. The other ~20 failing TUs all print `error[...]` lines; only these 7 are silent. **★ ROOT-CAUSED (TF arc C11, 2026-07-18):** the 7 TUs are `#if defined(SQLITE_TEST)`-gated (test_wsd = `#if defined(SQLITE_OMIT_WSD) && defined(SQLITE_TEST)`) — with NO `-D` defines their whole body is preprocessed OUT → an **EMPTY translation unit (0 function/object DEFINITIONS)**. A 0-definition module SILENTLY exits 1 — reduced probes confirm `only_comment` / `#if 0`-excluded / `extern int x;` / `int f(int);` ALL silent-exit-1, while `int f(void){return 42;}` → exit 0. Call chain: `main`→`run`→`compileFiles`→`compileOneTarget`→`buildCuMir`→ the MIR optimize/verify stage **`optimizeModule` (compile_pipeline.cpp:615) returns FALSE for a 0-definition module WITHOUT emitting a diagnostic** (`lowerToMir` itself returns ok=true — hir_to_mir.cpp:11680 `result.ok=(errorCount==errorsBefore)`). The D-PERF-4 "front-half tier failure already reported" contract (program.cpp:401/455) is thereby VIOLATED — the empty case returns null UNREPORTED. **CORRECT behavior = an empty / declaration-only TU compiles to a valid EMPTY OBJECT + exit 0** (gcc/clang do exactly this; C89 6.9 admits decl-only TUs; and testfixture NEEDS it — `test_wsd.o` is legitimately empty in a STANDARD build [WSD not omitted] and gcc emits an empty `.o` that links). Trigger: FIRED + root-caused. Priority: **HIGH — a silent-failure is the worst fail-loud class, and empty test-TU objects are testfixture-link-critical.** ★ §B RESOLVED (2026-07-18, TF C11): presented to the user, who chose **FIX-IT (recommended)** — the NEXT cycle implements the empty-TU→empty-object fix. It is a MIR-optimizer/verifier SUBSTRATE fix → runs an independent design-audit (dss-cycle Step 3.5) before building. | Dedicated cycle (§B design-audit first): make `optimizeModule` / the MIR verifier ACCEPT a 0-function module (produce a valid empty MIR, ok=true) + CONFIRM the emit tiers (MIR→LIR + the ELF/PE/Mach-O object writers) each emit a valid empty relocatable object for a 0-function module across all 3 formats → an empty/decl-only TU compiles to an empty `.o` + exit 0. Add a behavioral pin (a decl-only TU compiles clean + emits an object; red-on-disable) + fix the D-PERF-4 `buildCuMir`-null "already reported" contract (fail loud if a null build ever escapes unreported). NOT a config-descriptor cycle. | `D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` (parent testfixture arc); `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-EXTERN` (the C10 sibling cause the probe also surfaced); plan 23 §0.1 "TF" row |
| `D-LK-ARM64-EXTERN-DATA-ADDR-PIE-GOT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-22 (TF-C52) — address-taken UNDEFINED/preemptible externs on arm64 relocatable/staticlib now materialize via a foreign-linker GOT slot, DEFAULT-PIE-safe.** New format capability `externAddrBinding: "got"` (new `ExternAddrBinding` closed-enum in object_format_kind.hpp, the byte-for-byte sibling of `dataImportBinding`/`externCallDispatch`; `std::optional`, nullopt = not-declared = the ordinary lea, unknown value = fail-loud) declared ONLY on `elf64-aarch64-linux.format.json` + `-staticlib` (NEUTRAL: exec/pie/dyn omit it → byte-identical). A new DISTINCT MnemonicSlot **`lea_extern_got`** (arm64.target.json GOT macro `adrp Xd,:got:sym` [R_AARCH64_ADR_GOT_PAGE, target kind **7**, ELF nativeId **311**] + `ldr Xd,[Xd,:got_lo12:sym]` [R_AARCH64_LD64_GOT_LO12_NC, target kind **8**, nativeId **312**]) — NOT a `lea` variant (its `[symbol]` guard would collide with the absolute ADRP+ADD lea, the design-audit's key catch). MIR→LIR `lowerGlobalAddr` gains a value-form arm keyed on `externAddrGotSymbols_` (ALL extern imports — data AND fn — under externAddrBinding==Got), ordered AFTER `globalAddrFoldsIntoDirectCall` (a bare `&extern` CALLEE still folds to a plain BL — closure gate #7) + the riprel fold suppressed for these syms; capability-derived, ZERO arch/format branch in shared substrate. 2 new `RelocFormulaKind` (Aarch64AdrGotPage/Ld64GotLo12) declared REAL non-Linear kinds (so the D-LK-DYN-TEXT-ABS-RELOC slide-safe classifier does not mis-treat them as Linear-absolute) but DSS NEVER APPLIES them (foreign-linked only): the sole exhaustive switch in `exec_reloc_apply.hpp` gains an EXPLICIT FAIL-LOUD REFUSAL arm (no silent S/A/P). **WITNESS (WSL/qemu):** `extern int abs(int); int(*a)(int)=abs;` → arm64 .o carries R_AARCH64_ADR_GOT_PAGE + R_AARCH64_LD64_GOT_LO12_NC vs `abs` (UND, NO ADR_PREL_PG_HI21/ADD_ABS_LO12_NC) → `aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc` DEFAULT-PIE link (no -no-pie) SUCCEEDS + `qemu-aarch64` → exit 42 (before the fix default-PIE REJECTED it). sqlite3.c → arm64 .o compiles CLEAN; `dlsym` (a real address-taken UND extern) → GOT relocs, and ZERO undefined externs carry the absolute page-pair. Tests: test_mir_to_lir two-directional `GotExternAddrValueEmitsGotMacroNotAbsoluteLea` pin (RED-ON-DISABLE demonstrated: revert the arm → absolute lea reappears + GOT macro vanishes, both flip red) + `NoExternAddrBinding…` neutrality pin; test_object_format_schema capability + reloc-row + exec/pie/dyn-neutrality + loader-reject-unknown pins; test_aarch64_reloc_formulas GOT formula round-trip + non-Linear-coherence + 2 fail-loud-apply pins + the shipped-target kind-7/8 pins. SCOPE NOTE (design-audit's load-bearing check, verified): this cycle covers UNDEFINED/preemptible extern IMPORTS whose address is taken as a value — `externAddrGotSymbols_` is built from extern imports only. Of the registry's original 3 named sqlite residuals: **`dlsym` is an UNDEFINED extern → NOW GOT (directly witnessed above)**; **`stdout` is a libc UNDEFINED data extern → routes through the IDENTICAL arm** (not referenced in the standalone no-SQLITE_TEST config, so not directly witnessed, but same capability-keyed path as dlsym); **`sqlite3_search_count` is DEFINED-IN-TU** (`SQLITE_API int sqlite3_search_count = 0;` at sqlite3.c:95944 under SQLITE_TEST) — within sqlite3.c it is only incremented (its ADDRESS is taken in the testfixture `test1.c`, not sqlite3.c), so it is a MODULE GLOBAL, NOT an extern import → it is OUTSIDE this cycle's scope. Materializing the address of a DEFINED-but-preemptible (default-visibility) global via the GOT under a `-shared`/PIE link is a SEPARATE surface (it would widen the predicate to defined globals + touch the DSS-linked shared path) — deferred as a distinct follow-up, NOT covered here. SQLITE_TEST also needs `tclsqlite.h` on the testfixture include path (a standalone-probe environment detail, not a DSS defect), so `dlsym` is the directly-witnessed sqlite residual. — *Original (audit trail):* ⏳ **OPEN — surfaced 2026-07-22 (TF-C50 arm64 relocatable extern-call witness).** Taking the ADDRESS of an UNDEFINED extern (a fn-pointer or data global materialized as a LIVE value — `&stdout`, `&dlsym`, `&sqlite3_search_count`) on `arm64:elf64-aarch64-linux` lowers to an ABSOLUTE `adrp`+`add` (R_AARCH64_ADR_PREL_PG_HI21 + ADD_ABS_LO12_NC) against the extern. Against a DEFINED symbol that is PIE-safe, but against an UNDEFINED/preemptible extern a foreign DEFAULT-PIE link REJECTS it ("may bind externally … making a shared object") — the SAME class the extern-CALL fix [[D-LK-ARM64-ELF-RELOC-EXTERN-DISPATCH]]/[[D-ML7-2.9]] closed, but for a LIVE address (the dead-callee suppression does NOT apply — the value is consumed). x86_64 unaffected (its address materialization is PC-relative rel32). NOT the common case (most externs are CALLED — now PIE-safe); sqlite3.c CANON+FULL → arm64 .o has exactly 3 (`dlsym`/`sqlite3_search_count`/`stdout`), so an arm64 libsqlite3.a (with SQLITE_TEST) does not yet fully link under default-PIE. Trigger: FIRED (the TF-C50 sqlite arm64 witness). Priority: normal (the dominant extern-CALL path already works). | Closing work: for an address-taken UNDEFINED/preemptible extern under a PIE-flavored ELF, materialize via the GOT (`adrp :got:sym` [R_AARCH64_ADR_GOT_PAGE] + `ldr` [R_AARCH64_LD64_GOT_LO12_NC]) instead of the absolute adrp+add — a new `lea`-GOT target variant + the 2 got relocs + (for a DSS-linked image) a GOT-slot-synthesis path; capability/config-driven (no if(arch/format==)), the sibling of the direct-plt call shape. Witness: `&extern` → an arm64 .o that links + runs under DEFAULT-PIE; the 3 sqlite residuals clear. | [[D-LK-ARM64-ELF-RELOC-EXTERN-DISPATCH]] + [[D-ML7-2.9]] (the extern-CALL PIE fix this extends to address-taken externs); [[D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT]] (the GOT-indirect extern-DATA precedent, c117); `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.cpp` lowerGlobalAddr; plan 14 linker / arm64 sqlite-library route |
