| `D-LK-ARM64-ELF-RELOC-EXTERN-DISPATCH` | **✅ CLOSED 2026-07-22 (TF-C50) — arm64 relocatable-object extern CALLS are now PIE-safe.** Two parts: (1) CONFIG — declare `"externCallDispatch": "direct-plt"` on `elf64-aarch64-linux.format.json` + `-staticlib` (mirroring x86_64's D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-CALL-RELOCATABLE c141; the extern-call substrate was ALREADY arch/format-agnostic per an independent Explore trace — MIR→LIR branches only on the field, the ELF ET_REL writer reads the reloc nativeId from the format, the fixed32 encoder emits BL+call26 format-blind); NO `pltNativeId` on R_AARCH64_CALL26 (283) — AArch64 has no distinct PLT-variant branch reloc, CALL26 against an undefined symbol is what gcc/clang emit + the foreign linker inserts the veneer. (2) CODEGEN ([[D-ML7-2.9]]) — the arm64 .o COMPILED but did NOT link default-PIE: a direct call's callee `GlobalAddr` was lowered to a DEAD `adrp`+`add` (ABSOLUTE) before lowerCall folded the symbol into the direct `bl`; those absolute relocs against an undefined extern break a foreign PIE link. FIX: `globalAddrFoldsIntoDirectCall` (mirrors `globalAddrRiprelFoldsIntoLoad`; single-use + operand[0]=callee guards) suppresses the dead lea → the direct extern call is a PLAIN `bl` (CALL26 only), PIE-safe; x86_64 sheds a dead lea (size win). Witnessed: DSS→arm64 .o has ONLY R_AARCH64_CALL26 against `abs` (dead ADR_PREL/ADD_ABS count 0); `aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc` DEFAULT-PIE link + `qemu-aarch64` run → exit 42; sqlite3.c CANON+FULL→arm64 .o CLEAN. Tests: `test_elf_writer` ObjectExternCallEmitsUndefImportNameAndCall26RelocOnAarch64 (red-on-disable: remove the field → K_FormatLacksImportSupport) + `test_mir_to_lir` DirectCallEmitsCallOpcode FLIPPED (dead lea suppressed; red-on-disable). Independent Explore trace (config-only) + dead-lea characterization + code-audit VERIFIED-CLEAN. Gate: Win 724 + WSL 724 (qemu-arm64) + anchor guard OK. **RESIDUAL (separate follow-up [[D-LK-ARM64-EXTERN-DATA-ADDR-PIE-GOT]]):** address-TAKEN externs (`&fn`/`&data` as a LIVE value) still use absolute adrp+add → a default-PIE DATA link needs GOT-indirection; sqlite3.o has exactly 3 (dlsym / sqlite3_search_count / stdout). D-ML7-2.9 correctly KEEPS those live materializations (not dead callees). — *Original (audit trail):* OPEN (trigger-gated) — surfaced 2026-07-18 (TF arc C12 4-format probe).** On `arm64:elf64-aarch64-linux`, a RELOCATABLE object (`--compile`, no link) that references an EXTERN IMPORT (e.g. a decl-only TU `extern int x;` with `x` an unresolved import) fails codegen loud with `L_RequiredLirOpcodeMissing` — the arm64-ELF-relocatable format schema declares no `externCallDispatch` shape (the extern-call-site opcode selection). A TRULY-empty TU (0 imports) compiles fine on arm64-ELF; only the extern-import case hits it. x86_64-ELF/PE + arm64-Mach-O all handle extern imports (they declare `externCallDispatch`). EXTERN-INDEPENDENT of the C12 empty-module fix — a PRE-EXISTING format-schema gap the C12 4-format probe merely surfaced. Trigger: FIRED by the probe, but NOT on the testfixture critical path (testfixture targets x86_64-ELF, unaffected). | Add the `externCallDispatch` (+ `dataImportBinding` if needed) block to the arm64 `elf64-aarch64-linux` relocatable `.format.json`, mirroring x86_64-ELF / arm64-Mach-O. Priority: **normal (trigger-gated — first arm64-ELF-relocatable-with-extern-import consumer; testfixture x86_64 path unaffected).** | `D-CSUBSET-TESTTU-SILENT-EXIT1` (the C12 sibling that surfaced it); `D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH` (the externCallDispatch mechanism); plan 23 §0.1 TF row |
| `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-GLOBAL-CODEGEN` | **OPEN (normal) — surfaced 2026-07-17 (TF arc C6 probing).** An enum-typed FILE-SCOPE GLOBAL (`enum E { V = 42 }; enum E g = V;`) fails codegen loud: `K_NoMatchingObjectFormat: lowerMirGlobalsToDataItems: global has TypeKind=24 (Enum) — non-primitive global types are anchored under D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER-AGGREGATE-GLOBAL`. The enum is NOT decayed to its underlying integer at the global-data-lowering site, so it falls BETWEEN the primitive-int-global path and the struct/union aggregate-rodata path (both of which work — struct/union globals lower fine, witnessed by `examples/c-subset/extern_aggregate` running 42). **EXTERN-INDEPENDENT:** `enum E g = V;` fails identically WITHOUT `extern` (empirically confirmed), so it is a pre-existing global-lowering gap, NOT introduced by the C6 extern-aggregate parse fix — which is why `extern_aggregate` witnesses struct/union/const/pointer at RUNTIME but the enum form only at the PARSE tier (the `ExternAggregateSpecifiersParse` pin). Trigger: a program (or a sqlite test TU) with an enum-typed file-scope global. | Decay an enum global to its already-resolved underlying integer type at `lowerMirGlobalsToDataItems` so it lowers as an int global (fail-loud stays for genuinely-unlowerable types). Priority: **normal (trigger-gated on the first enum-global consumer).** | `D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER-AGGREGATE-GLOBAL` (the aggregate-global producer this falls between); `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-INT-CONVERSION` (enum↔underlying-int, FC8 c1); `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-AGGREGATE-TYPE` (the C6 sibling that surfaced it) |
| `D-FFI-TCL-REFCOUNT-MACROS` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-18 (TF arc C13, PR #46) — the Tcl_Obj-layout prerequisite was DISSOLVED, not built.** ★ C13 finding: libtcl8.6.so **EXPORTS `Tcl_DbIncrRefCount`/`Tcl_DbDecrRefCount`** (`nm -D` = `T`) — the debug-form backers of the refcount macros, doing the same `++/--refCount (+ TclFreeObj)`. So tcl.json models `Tcl_IncrRefCount(o)→Tcl_DbIncrRefCount((o),"",0)` / `Tcl_DecrRefCount(o)→Tcl_DbDecrRefCount((o),"",0)` as function-macros over those EXPORTED functions (the ckalloc/ckfree precedent) — needing NO Tcl_Obj named-field layout (Tcl_Obj stays the opaque `arr<u64,6>` blob), so the feared ~30-signature ripple never happens. The 2 backers are exported, so DSS's eager-import-all stays safe (unlike the C9 macro-only attempt that broke every tcl binary's LOAD → exit 127). `Tcl_DStringValue(d)→((d)->string)` DID need a field, so Tcl_DString got its real named layout (`{char* string; i32 length; i32 spaceAvl; u8[200]}` = 216B, same as the old opaque `arr<u64,27>`; every Tcl_DString signature updated for interner consistency). Also +Tcl_GetSlave/Tcl_BackgroundError (residual S0001). Witness `examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_refcount` (Incr/Decr on a Tcl_Obj + Tcl_DStringValue read → 42 on real libtcl8.6) + the decode pin FLIPPED (C9's negative "refcount macros ABSENT" assert → positive macro-presence + the 4 syms + the Tcl_DString named struct; red-on-disable proven — delete a macro → pin reds). **The 5 blocked TUs advance: test_tclvar + test8 → CLEAN; test_bestindex/test_mutex/test_func → residual sqlite-internal externs (sqlite3ErrName/sqlite3TestTextToPtr) + an S0003 string-literal gap (unrelated, next cycle).** shipped_tcl_eval still → 42 (no LOAD breakage). Gate: Win + WSL full ctest + witness → 42 + anchor guard. | (closed — the Db*-exported-function path bypasses the Tcl_Obj-layout prerequisite entirely) | `D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` (parent); `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT` (why the C9 macro-only attempt failed — the Db* funcs being EXPORTED is what makes this safe) — [ORIGINAL C9 diagnosis retained below] `Tcl_IncrRefCount`/`Tcl_DecrRefCount` are MACROS in real tcl.h (`++objPtr->refCount` / `if(--objPtr->refCount<=0) TclFreeObj(objPtr)`), NOT exported functions — `nm -D libtcl8.6.so` confirms they are absent. C9 initially added them as function symbols; because DSS EAGER-IMPORTS all descriptor functions even when unreferenced (see `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT`), that broke the LOAD of EVERY tcl binary (undefined symbol at ld.so → exit 127; A/B-proven 56-sym→127 vs 54-sym→42). So they were DROPPED — leaving 39 of the test TUs' Incr/DecrRefCount uses (20+19) at S0001. Modeling them as tcl.json MACROS is blocked on: (1) a Tcl_Obj named-field layout (refCount is the i32 at offset 0, but Tcl_Obj is currently an opaque `arr<u64,6>` blob referenced by ~30 signatures — giving it named fields ripples through all of them + changes their interner TypeId); (2) DecrRefCount's `TclFreeObj` free path (an INTERNAL symbol — availability unverified). Trigger: the Tcl_Obj named-field model lands (a dedicated cycle). | Model Tcl_IncrRefCount/DecrRefCount as function-like macros over a field-accessible Tcl_Obj + resolve the free path. Priority: **normal (blocked on Tcl_Obj field layout).** | `D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR` (parent); `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT` (why they can't be plain function symbols) |
| `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-EAGER-IMPORT` | **OPEN (trigger-gated) — surfaced 2026-07-17 (TF arc C9).** DSS emits a dynamic import (DT_NEEDED-bound extern) for EVERY function symbol in a shipped descriptor that a TU `#include`s, even ones the TU never REFERENCES. So a single unexported descriptor function (e.g. the macro-only Tcl_IncrRefCount) makes ld.so refuse to load EVERY binary using that descriptor (undefined symbol → exit 127) — even binaries that don't call it. Empirically A/B-proven (tcl.json 56-sym → shipped_tcl_eval exit 127; 54-sym → 42; the eval example calls neither refcount op). This forces descriptors to list ONLY exported symbols (the correct workaround, applied in C9). A lazy import (emit an import only for REFERENCED descriptor symbols — like a real linker DCE-ing unused externs) would let a descriptor safely carry macro-modeled or optional symbols. Trigger: a descriptor needs an unexported/optional symbol, OR the import bloat matters. | Make descriptor-symbol imports LAZY (referenced-only) in the linker's extern-bind path. Priority: **normal (trigger-gated — the exported-only-descriptors workaround suffices now).** | `D-FFI-TCL-REFCOUNT-MACROS` (the case that surfaced it); `D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-DESCRIPTOR-AGNOSTIC` |
| `D-CSUBSET-SETJMP` | **OPEN (walking-skeleton FFI + carrier LANDED 2026-07-15; optimizer hardening is the follow-on) — FC17.9(c).** C11/C23 `<setjmp.h>` setjmp/longjmp bound as libc FFI (Option A; the synthesized alternative is REFUTED — DSS's IndirectBr needs a closed same-function target list, no inline asm yet). LANDED this cycle: `shippedLibs/setjmp.json` (elf/macho `setjmp fn(ptr<void>)->i32` + `longjmp fn(ptr<void>,i32)->void noreturn`; pe `_setjmp fn(ptr<void>,ptr<void>)->i32` + a pe-only `setjmp(env)->_setjmp(env,0)` macro; `jmp_buf` per-(arch,format) opaque buffer — elf-x86_64 `arr<i64,25>`=200B, elf-arm64 `arr<i64,39>`=312B, pe-x86_64 `arr<u128,16>`=256B/**16-aligned** [the u128 element self-aligns to 16 so `_setjmp`'s `movaps` xmm saves don't #GP — no alignas field, pure config data], macho-arm64 `arr<i64,28>`=224B over-sized from the researched 192B `_JBLEN`=48); `ShippedSymbol.returnsTwice` + `SymbolRecord.returnsTwice` (the noreturn-from-descriptor mirror); THE CARRIER `MirInstFlags::ReturnsTwice` set at HIR->MIR `finishCall` from a CST->HIR `HirReturnsTwiceMap` side-table (the volatile-from-isVolatile funnel mirror — the flag the optimizer's returns-twice passes will read, since noreturn is HIR-discharged into Unreachable and never reaches MIR); `env` array-decay to void* verified (c50 D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-TO-VOID-PTR). RUNS exit 42 on pe64 + elf-x86_64 + elf-arm64(qemu) [`examples/c-subset/setjmp_longjmp`, debug/baseline arm]; macho-arm64 CI-gated. FOLLOW-ON (next cycle, its OWN anchor `D-OPT-SETJMP-RETURNS-TWICE-INLINE`, NOT this row): the mem2reg whole-function no-promote + the inliner returns-twice callee-refusal + the release/fold-resistant example arm + the M1 determinate-value runtime witnesses. | (walking-skeleton done when the example runs 42 on 3 legs — DONE; the row stays OPEN until the optimizer hardening lands under its own anchor) | plan 23 §0.1 FC17.9(c); `D-CSUBSET-NORETURN` (the mirror); `D-CSUBSET-SETJMP-SIGMASK` + `D-WIN64-SEH-SCOPE` (deferrals) |

| `D-WIN64-SEH-SCOPE` | **OPEN (trigger-gated) — registered 2026-07-15 (FC17.9(c)).** On Windows the pe `setjmp(env)` macro expands to `_setjmp(env, 0)` with frame=0 — the plain, non-SEH-aware restore. The SEH-aware form passes the caller's frame pointer (msvcrt `_setjmpex` / `_setjmp(env, frame)`) so a longjmp unwinds `__try`/`__finally` scopes crossed between the setjmp and the longjmp. c-subset has NO `__try`/setjmp interaction, so frame=0 is correct now. Trigger: a Windows program mixing SEH `__try`/`__finally` with setjmp/longjmp that needs the crossed scopes unwound on longjmp. | Emit the caller's frame-pointer as the 2nd `_setjmp` arg (or route to `_setjmpex`) + thread the SEH scope-unwind on the pe longjmp path; gated on the SEH funclet infrastructure. Priority: **normal (trigger-gated — first __try/setjmp-interacting consumer).** | `D-CSUBSET-SETJMP` (parent); the SEH arc (c114-c116 .pdata/.xdata/funclets) |
| `D-OPT-SETJMP-RETURNS-TWICE-INLINE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-15 (FC17.9(c) — the setjmp/longjmp RELEASE-pipeline optimizer hardening; the `D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-MISCOMPILE` precedent — a landed frame-sensitive refusal gets its OWN anchor).** Two correctness passes for a returns-twice (`setjmp`/`_setjmp`) Call, BOTH keyed on the GENERIC `MirInstFlags::ReturnsTwice` bit (set at HIR→MIR from the callee's `SymbolRecord.returnsTwice` — no arch/format/name matching, agnostic): **(1) mem2reg WHOLE-FUNCTION no-promote** — `src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp` `analyze()` scans EVERY instruction in every block (a returns-twice Call is MID-BLOCK, unlike the SEH scan's block-TERMINATOR `SehTryBegin`) and on ANY returns-twice Call leaves `promoted_` empty → promotes NOTHING → all locals stay memory-resident. Rationale: `longjmp` re-enters the setjmp site over an edge the CFG cannot see; a PROMOTED local reassigned on that hidden path would carry its stale entry-reaching SSA value past the resume (silent miscompile), whereas a memory-resident local survives via longjmp's SP+callee-saved restore (GCC `returns_twice` parity). Function-level over-approximation like the SEH scan — only fires for the rare returns-twice-containing function, so normal code is NOT pessimized. **(2) inliner CALLEE-REFUSAL** — `inlineLegalityGate` (`src/opt/passes/inlining.cpp`) refuses any callee whose body contains a returns-twice Call (spliced into the caller, the setjmp would capture the CALLER's frame → wrong-SP corruption on resume); a per-op arm beside the va_start-leaf refusal, fail-SAFE. PINS (both red-on-disable, DEMONSTRATED this cycle): `tests/opt/test_mem2reg.cpp::ReturnsTwiceCallInFunctionBlocksPromotion` (allocasPromoted==0; delete the scan → the otherwise-textbook-promotable slot promotes → 1) + `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp::CalleeCallingReturnsTwiceFunctionIsNotInlined` (callsInlined==0; delete the arm → the callee inlines → 1). RELEASE-ARM WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/setjmp_longjmp` now carries `{"shippedPipeline":"release"}` + the M1 witnesses — a `determinate` local computed BEFORE setjmp + never modified (→ 40, the Call-barrier + longjmp-restore proof) plus a `modified` local stored before longjmp + read after the resume (→ 2, the mem2reg-no-promote runtime companion: a promoted read observes the stale entry 0 → exit 40), anti-fold via mutable globals → exit 42 on pe64 + elf-x86_64 + elf-arm64(qemu); macho CI-gated. | ✅ (CLOSED) both structural pins red-on-disable + the release example runs 42 on the 3 local legs. | `D-CSUBSET-SETJMP` (parent feature); `D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-MISCOMPILE` (the frame-sensitive-refusal precedent + the `release`-arm corpus discipline); `D-OPT7-INLINE-FRAME-SENSITIVE-INTRINSIC` (stays OPEN — the hypothetical `IntrinsicCall` path; setjmp is an FFI `Call`, NOT an intrinsic, so closing it would falsely mark the intrinsic path done); plan 23 §0.1 FC17.9(c) |

(b) `arm64.target.json` — v0..v31 VR (128-bit Q-view) registers, a `vr` registerClassOps row (load=fldur_q/store=fstur_q, NO move — trigger discipline), the `fldur_q` opcode (LDUR Qt, fixedWord 0x3CC00000), the 6 softcall rows (add→__addtf3 args[v0,v1] result v0; sub/mul/div likewise; to_i32→__fixtfsi args[v0] result x0; from_f64→__extenddftf2 args[d0] result v0), `wideFloatSoftcallLibraryByFormat {elf: libgcc_s.so.1}`; (c) `asm.cpp appendF128` — lossless binary64→binary128 widen (exp rebias 1023→16383, 52-bit frac up 60 into the 112-bit field with IMPLICIT leading 1 — distinct from F80's explicit integer bit; normal/zero/inf-nan/subnormal-renormalize) + the F128 rodata global producer; (d) `hir_to_mir.cpp` body-literal rodata-promotion widened to F128; (e) `mir_to_lir.cpp` — `lowerWideFloatSoftcall` (the generic verb: marshal operands into the config'd physical arg regs via fldur_q/fmov, BL, capture the config'd result reg to a fresh home), `lowerF128Store` (16-byte = 2 GPR-word memory→memory copy), F128 intercepted BEFORE `requireEncodedFloatWidth` at FAdd/FSub/FMul/FDiv/FPToSI(32-bit)/FPExt(from-F64)/Load(widened)/Store, and `resolveWideFloatSoftcallExtern` — the self-serve LIR-tier extern auto-injection (mints one `ExternImport` per helper on first use, bound to `libgcc_s.so.1`, UNVERSIONED, deduped within the CU, minted past the func/global/extern high-water via `mintJumpTableSymbol`; re-asserts the D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH gate; `run()` moves `newWideFloatExterns_` into the result, `lowerToLir` appends the caller's after — the pre-wired chain to the linker); (f) the 4-hop `wideFloatSoftcallLibrary` threading (lowerToLir → Lowerer ctor; lowerMirModuleToAssembly; lowerCuMirToAssembly resolves inline from fmtKind; lowerMergedToAssembly + program.cpp pre-resolve near **formatR — the externCallDispatch precedent). **End-to-end:** `c_long_double` main.c compiles for arm64:elf64-aarch64-linux-exec (debug + release) → an ELF with `__addtf3` (g_a+g_b), `__fixtfsi` ((int)sum), and DT_NEEDED `libgcc_s.so.1`; example expected.json extended with the qemu-aarch64 leg. **12 tests** (all green on the Windows gate): the schema parse/resolve pin, the fldur_q byte pin (LDUR Q0,[X1] = 20 00 c0 3c), the Add/Sub/Mul/Div softcall-sequence pin, fixtfsi + extenddftf2 shape pins, once-only extern injection, the config-row-absence RED-ON-DISABLE, the 3 F128 boundary walls, the F128 phi wall, the GPR-pair store shape, and the regalloc v0/d0-aliasing safety net (a double live across the softcall never lands in d0/d1). Still walled (deferred sub-anchors, trigger-gated), all fail-loud via `requireEncodedFloatWidth`: F128 phi (`-CONTROL-MERGE`), call boundaries (`-AGGREGATE-ABI`/LD-4), F128→i64 (no __fixtfdi row), FPTrunc F128→narrower (no __trunctfdf2 row), F32→F128 widen (only __extenddftf2/from-F64 configured — no __extendsftf2 row; a `float`→`long double` FPExt whose source is F32 falls through to the width gate). | (closed — the config'd softcall verb IS the realization; the remaining long-double value paths are the named sub-anchors) | `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE` (parent); `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-X87-ARITH` (LD-1 memory-resident precedent); `D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH` + the setjmp/threads extern-import rows |
| `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-CONSTFOLD-PRECISION` | **✅ CLOSED 2026-07-18 — LD-3: TARGET-PRECISION constant folding for `long double` (F80 x87 64-bit / F128 IEEE binary128 113-bit) via a minimal, self-contained, PORTABLE soft-float LANDED (Windows MSVC-Debug gate green 675/675; the gcc-native differential + WSL/qemu runtime legs run by the parent).** THE KERNEL: NEW header-only `src/core/types/wide_float_value.hpp` (`WideFloatValue`, the `BitIntValue` precedent) — a pure-host value type carrying an F80/F128 value at its TARGET precision, keyed ONLY on `TypeKind::F80`/`F128` (closed enums) + `WideFloatValue::isSupportedKind`, NEVER an arch/format/target.name branch. ONE unified engine parameterized by `significandBits()` (64/113): significand in a uniform left-justified 128-bit register (integer bit at bit 63 of sigHi_), arithmetic in a 256-bit working register (≥143 guard bits) so alignment/normalization never lose a round-determining bit; ONE shared `roundNormal` round-to-nearest-even (guard/round/sticky, tie-to-even, carry-out renormalization — NOTHING free from the host FPU); portable 64×64→128 `mul64` via 32-bit halves (NO `__int128`/`_umul128`, the `BitIntValue::mul64` dup); bit-at-a-time 256/128 division; add/sub/mul/div/neg/6-compares/toInt64(trunc)/toDouble(RNE)/fromDouble+fromInt64(EXACT)/pack↔fromPacked. THE CARRIER: a NEW `std::variant` arm `WideFloatValue` on `HirLiteralValue`/`MirLiteralValue` (NOT a side field — the fail-loud "new arm crashes an un-updated consumer" discipline); the pre-existing `double` arm STAYS valid for an UNFOLDED F80/F128 leaf (the widen double→WideFloatValue is always EXACT, 53-bit ⊆ 64/113-bit); only a FOLDED result / explicit widen promotes to the new arm. FAIL-LOUD (no silent mis-fold): `asDouble` returns nullopt for the new arm (a forgetful caller crashes loud, NEVER silently narrows); a subnormal RESULT / cross-kind mismatch → nullopt → `ConstEvalFailure::UnsupportedTypeKind`; overflow → inf; float div-by-zero → signed inf/NaN (IEEE, NOT DivisionByZero). THE GATE RELAX: `refusesHostFloatFold` gains `&& !WideFloatValue::isSupportedKind(k)` (dead for F80/F128 now; live for a future unrealized kind) — `floatKindInfo`/`hostBacked` + F16 folding + `appendF80Extended`/`appendF128` all UNTOUCHED. Kernel dispatch in `applyUnaryFloat`(Neg)/`applyBinaryFloat`(Add/Sub/Mul/Div + 6 compares) BEFORE the asDouble path (result core = the kernel kind, not F64); the 4 float cast quadrants (`const_eval.cpp combineCast`) become kernel-aware (Float→Bool via isZero, Float→Float widen/narrow, Int→Float via EXACT fromInt64, Float→Int via toInt64 at operand precision). ASM: NEW additive `appendWideFloatBits` + a `get_if<WideFloatValue>` branch checked FIRST in the F80/F128 global arms (pack() yields the identical 16-byte x87/binary128 layout). ORACLE (INEXACT round-to-nearest-even proofs, bit-exact vs gcc-native `long double`/`__float128`): F80 `20/22`→`2f ba e8 a2 8b 2e ba e8 fe 3f`, F80 `1/3`→`ab aa aa aa aa aa aa aa fd 3f` (rounds UP), F128 `20/22`→`17 5d 74 d1 45 17 5d 74 d1 45 17 5d 74 d1 fe 3f`, F128 `1/3`→`55…55 fd 3f`. Tests: `test_wide_float_value.cpp` (kernel oracle pins + the `__GNUC__&&__x86_64__&&!_MSC_VER`-guarded gcc-native differential, inert on MSVC); the 3 const-eval refusal tests INVERTED to fold + div-by-zero→inf + cross-kind-refuse + F16-stays-on-double regression; the cast refusal INVERTED + widen/narrow/int-cast pins; `test_asm_substrate` folded-F80/F128-byte pins + unfolded-double regression; the semantic `LongDoubleConstexprFoldSucceedsOnWalledAxis` (x87-80 + ieee128 + f64) INVERTED. Example `examples/c-subset/c_long_double_constfold` (a folded `constexpr long double` — REQUIRED-constant, so a green build proves the fold — + a folded `const long double` global's rodata bytes; NO anti-fold trick) → exit 42 on all 4 axes. | (closed — the soft-float kernel + the variant-arm carrier + the gate relax IS the realization) | `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE` (parent); `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-X87-ARITH` (LD-1) / `-IEEE128-ARITH` (LD-2) runtime precedents; `floatKindInfo.hostBacked`; `applyBinaryFloat`/`applyUnaryFloat`; the `BitIntValue` host-value-type precedent; spun-off `-CONSTFOLD-SUBNORMAL-RESULT` / `-AGGREGATE-GLOBAL` / `-LITERAL-DECODE-PRECISION` |

Astronomically unlikely (a source constant expression whose long-double result is denormal — magnitude ~10^-4932), so deferred rather than implemented. | Implement gradual-underflow (denormal) rounding in `WideFloatValue::roundNormal` + a subnormal pack path, then drop the nullopt guard. Trigger: a real subnormal-result constant expression. Priority: low (trigger-gated). | `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-CONSTFOLD-PRECISION` (parent, LD-3); `WideFloatValue::roundNormal` |
| `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-AGGREGATE-GLOBAL` | **OPEN — DEFERRED (named 2026-07-18, LD-3; supersedes the stale `asm.cpp` "F80 struct members are LD-3" mislabel this cycle fixed).** A struct/array/union global with a `long double` (F80/F128) MEMBER — e.g. `struct S { long double x; } g = {42.0L};` at file scope — cannot be byte-emitted to rodata: the aggregate-leaf recursion (`encodeAggregateValue`) routes every scalar leaf through the u64 `decodeScalarLiteralBits` chokepoint, which returns nullopt for a 16-byte F80/F128 leaf (a WideFloatValue / wide-float leaf has no u64 representation) → the aggregate global fails loud. DISTINCT from LD-3's SCALAR arithmetic const-fold (CLOSED): a folded F80/F128 SCALAR global IS emitted (the dedicated `appendWideFloatBits` arm, checked BEFORE the chokepoint). The gap is the 16-byte-leaf-through-a-u64-chokepoint structural limit. | Give `encodeAggregateValue` a 16-byte-leaf special case (call `appendWideFloatBits` for an F80/F128 leaf, mirroring the scalar arm) — the aggregate recursion currently assumes ≤8-byte scalar leaves. Trigger: a consumer needs a long-double aggregate global. Priority: normal (trigger-gated). | `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-CONSTFOLD-PRECISION` (LD-3, the scalar sibling); `decodeScalarLiteralBits` u64 chokepoint; `appendWideFloatBits` |
| `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-LITERAL-DECODE-PRECISION` | **OPEN — DEFERRED (named 2026-07-18, LD-3; PRE-EXISTING, inherited UNWORSENED).** An l-suffixed long-double LITERAL whose value needs >53 mantissa bits — e.g. `0.1L` — is DECODED via `strtod`→host `double` in `number_decode.hpp`, so the literal LEAF already carries a binary64-rounded value BEFORE any fold. LD-3 folds ARITHMETIC at true 80/128-bit precision, but the input literals are only binary64-precise: `0.1L` widens the rounded `double` 0.1 to F80/F128 (an EXACT widen of an already-rounded value), NOT the true decimal 0.1 at 64/113-bit. A whole-number or power-of-two-denominator literal (20.0L, 0.5L, the LD-3 corpus/tests) is EXACT; only a literal with a >53-bit binary expansion is affected. NOT an LD-3 regression (the literal decoder predates it). | Decode long-double literals at target precision (a decimal→F80/F128 `strtold`-equivalent / arbitrary-precision decimal parse) into the wide-float arm at the literal leaf. Trigger: a consumer needs an exact >53-bit long-double literal. Priority: normal (trigger-gated). | `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-CONSTFOLD-PRECISION` (LD-3); `number_decode.hpp` strtod path |
| `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-AGGREGATE-ABI` | **✅ CLOSED 2026-07-18 — LD-4: `long double` (F80/F128) across USER CALL BOUNDARIES + by-value aggregates LANDED (Windows MSVC-Debug gate green; WSL x86_64 SysV-x87 + qemu-aarch64 AAPCS64-F128 runtime witnesses run by the parent).** THE MODEL extends LD-1's F80 / LD-2's F128 memory-resident design to user calls with ZERO register-allocation model added — an F80/F128 SSA value stays the GPR-held ADDRESS of its 16-byte home; the physical boundary registers (SysV x87 st0; AAPCS64 v0..v7 binary128, read from config) are transient scratch marshalled around the call. Dispatch keys on `TypeKind::F80`/`TypeKind::F128` (closed enums — each forms only on its own format axis) + config, NEVER an arch/format identity branch. **As-built:** (a) `aggregate_abi.cpp` — SysV: a long-double (F80\|F128) leaf forces the x87 MEMORY class → the WHOLE aggregate goes BY REFERENCE (inheriting D-FC7's hidden-pointer path — §2.6-1, pre-existing, NOT a new gap); AAPCS64: narrowed the reject to F80-only (`hasF80Leaf`), so a binary128 leaf falls through to the ALREADY-CORRECT HFA math (elem=16, count=size/16 → 1..4 Q-register pieces; a 2-binary128 aggregate = a 2-piece HFA); (b) `hir_to_mir.cpp pieceType` ★ the width-16→F128 arm (else a 16-byte Fpr HFA piece silently types F64 = an 8-of-16-byte ABI miscompile); the SysV F80 CALLER arg wraps in `ByValueStackArg` (payload 16, NO gpr/fpr ordinal stolen); the SysV F80 CALLEE param receives via `RecvByValueStackParam` + a private local-slot copy EXACTLY like the straddled-aggregate arm (the byte offset is a full payload — an Arg-op ordinal can't hold it, the verifier bounds Arg.argIndex; ★ bumps `currentFnFixedStackBytes_` + the sawStackedAggregate/sawFixedStackParam guards so a variadic fn's va_start overflow base skips it); (c) `target_schema{.hpp,.cpp,_json.cpp}` + `arm64.target.json` — NEW `argVrs`/`returnVrs` cc lists (v0..v7 / v0..v3 on BOTH aapcs64 + apple_arm64 [apple inert but REQUIRED for field-uniformity so the `callingConvention(0)`-at-MIR→LIR convention stays valid], parsed + VR-class-validated + name→ordinal-resolved like argFprs); (d) `mir_to_lir.cpp` — the 3 boundary walls INVERTED before `requireEncodedFloatWidth`: `lowerArg` (F80→RecvByValueStackParam; F128→entry v-reg spill fstur_q [home]<-v{k}), `lowerCall` (F80 result→fstp_m80 after call; F128 result→fstur_q v0; F128 args→fldur_q v{argVrs[k]} burst TIGHT before the call — a mixed F128+non-F128-FPR call fails loud, the NSRN interleaving unhandled), `lowerReturn` (F80→fld_m80 st0; F128→fldur_q v{returnVrs[k]}, both push NO ret operand → the value is already in st0/v0); a 2+-elem F128-HFA caller RetPiece capture fails loud (`lowerReturnPiece`; the 1-elem witness + the callee side lower it, the caller multi-piece is beyond the witness); (e) `mir_verifier.cpp` — the by-value struct-return piece-type set gains F128 (a legitimate binary128 HFA return piece) + F80. **Witness:** `c_long_double` main.c extended with a scalar `long double add(long double,long double)` called `(int)add(20.0L,22.0L)==42` + a by-value `struct S{long double x;}` variant (pass + return) → 42 on ALL FOUR axes (pe64/macho-arm64 f64-collapse; elf-x86_64 SysV-x87 stack-args+st0 / struct-ByReference; elf-arm64 AAPCS64 v-regs / 1-elem binary128 HFA). **Tests:** the 6 boundary walls INVERTED to positive lowering pins asserting INSTRUCTION ADJACENCY (recv_by_value_stack_param / fstur_q entry-spill / fstp_m80-after-call / fld_m80-before-bare-ret / fstur_q-after-call / fldur_q-before-bare-ret); the aggregate-classification pins (SysV ByReference, AAPCS64 1-elem + 2-elem binary128 HFA, F80-refuse); the argVrs/returnVrs schema parse+resolve+VR-class pin; a caller F128-arg-marshal pin (v0/v1 before the call); the cross-class regalloc pin (a `double` sharing a signature with an F128 arg does NOT land in a d-register aliasing the F128's v-register at entry — the novel entry-side aliasing case, real regalloc); the compose pin (a user F128 Call feeding an F128 softcall FAdd). The F80/F128 PHI merge STAYS fail-loud (`F80/F128PhiControlMergeWallsFailLoud`, `-CONTROL-MERGE`); F16 stays walled. STILL DEFERRED: the outgoing-stack-arg 16-byte alignment (`-STACK-ARG-ALIGNMENT`, DSS-to-DSS correct, silent-for-foreign-FFI, not reachable today). **★ REVIEW RESIDUALS (independent code-review, sub-80 confidence, non-blocking — pinned for future cycles):** (1) the AAPCS64 entry-spill aliasing safety is EMERGENT, not explicitly guarded — it rests on VR being non-allocatable + the NSRN-skip (an F128 at NSRN k consumes that ordinal so `d{k}` is never an arg home) + no entry FPR pressure; ROBUST for the current regalloc, but the future SIMD cycle that makes VR allocatable (the `subOf`/aliasing work the `arm64.target.json` VR comment already anticipates) MUST add `v{k}`'s aliased d-ordinal to the entry arg-occupied exclusion, else a reorder/coalescing pass could silently reintroduce the d{k}/v{k} clobber. (2) `mir_to_lir.cpp lowerF80Arg` is SYNTHETIC-ONLY — real SysV F80 params are intercepted in `hir_to_mir.cpp` (`RecvByValueStackParam`, byte-offset payload) BEFORE the scalar `Arg` path, so no F80 `Arg` op forms in real compilation; `lowerF80Arg` reads `argIndex` as a byte offset (its test passes coincidentally since argIndex 0 == byteOff 0) — a latent trap ONLY if that hir_to_mir special path is ever removed (then it would misread a per-class-ordinal argIndex). | (closed — the aggregate classifier + the scalar/aggregate call-boundary lowering IS the realization) | `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE` (parent); `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-X87-ARITH` (LD-1) / `-IEEE128-ARITH` (LD-2) memory-resident precedents; the D-FC7 struct-by-value hidden-pointer + straddled-aggregate arms; spun-off `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-STACK-ARG-ALIGNMENT` |
| `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-STACK-ARG-ALIGNMENT` | **OPEN — DEFERRED (named 2026-07-18, LD-4).** The outgoing-stack-arg layout (`lir_callconv`, flat 8-byte stride) does NOT 16-byte-align a stacked `long double` that follows an ODD number of 8-byte stack args (the SysV x87 ABI requires a `long double` stack slot 16-aligned). DSS-to-DSS is CORRECT (both caller + callee use the SAME flat layout, so the byte offsets agree — the LD-4 witness passes). A FOREIGN FFI call (DSS→gcc libm with such a layout — e.g. `sinl`/`powl`) would be a SILENT byte-offset mismatch — but NOT reachable today: there is no long-double libm FFI surface (sinl/powl are themselves deferred), and the LD-4 boundary is DSS-to-DSS only. ★ HONEST: this edge is SILENT-for-foreign-FFI (NOT fail-loud), so a future long-double-FFI consumer MUST fix the alignment FIRST. | Add the 16-byte-alignment pad to the outgoing-stack-arg layout when a real foreign long-double FFI call lands. Trigger: a DSS→foreign call passing a `long double` (or a long-double-leaf aggregate) on the stack after an odd 8-byte-arg count. Priority: normal (trigger-gated on a foreign long-double FFI surface). | `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-AGGREGATE-ABI` (parent, LD-4); `lir_callconv` outgoing-stack-arg layout |

VERIFIED: `_Generic((x), float:, double:, long double:)` now compiles clean on pe64 AND macho64-arm64 AND elf64 (same answer every format). Componentwise F64 arithmetic passes the LIR width gate; F80/F128 components (long-double-complex) WALL LOUD via the existing requireEncodedFloatWidth. **As-built (design-audit CRITICAL folds):** the 3-part by-address contract — (a) the `request` value→address flip extended to `isComplex||isWideBitInt` (hir_to_mir.cpp:3394/3399), (b) `materializeComplexBinaryOp`/`UnaryOp`/`Cast` routed from `lowerLvalueAddressNode` (NOT combineBinaryOp; the `materializeWideBinaryOp` precedent), (c) the caller consumes a memory-resident RHS via isByValueClass→lowerAggregateCopy, (d) a combineBinaryOp fail-loud misroute guard; `__builtin_complex(re,im)` = the FIRST aggregate-returning builtin (a BuiltinCall arm in lowerLvalueAddressNode → a ComplexMake slot); the coerce real→complex(im=0) promotion arm so mixed real/complex reduces to uniform complex ops; `_Complex` grammar + `complex:true` typeSpecifiers rows resolving `interner.complex(element)` at semantic_analyzer.cpp:1023 (honoring the LD axis for `long double _Complex`); `<complex.h>` (complex.json) ships `complex`/`I`/`_Complex_I` MACROS (the stdbit macros precedent) + creal/cimag/conj builtins; the M1 codec `complex<elem>`; the collectLeaves 2-float-leaves arm (ABI: SysV 2-SSE / AAPCS64 2-double-HFA — a silent-ABI-miscompile the runtime CAN'T catch, only test_aggregate_abi did — the implementer forgot it first pass); complex fn-return via the Call arm widened to isByValueClass (IMPLEMENTED, not deferred). Probe `c99_complex_arith` (40.0+2.0*I → creal+cimag = 42) RUNS ×3 legs; ×-cross-term + ÷ + conj runtime-witnessed; 4 red-on-disable demos (request-flip→segfault, materialize→H0009, coerce→H0009, regClass pin). | (closed — the type + by-address arithmetic + `<complex.h>` IS the (f) deliverable) | plan-23 §0.1 FC17.9(f); the wide-BitInt memory-resident precedent (`D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME`); sub-deferrals `-TRANSCENDENTAL`/`-ANNEX-G`/`-MONOMORPH-F64`/`-IMAGINARY-TYPE` |
| `D-CSUBSET-TGMATH` | **✅ CLOSED 2026-07-16 — FC17.9(g): C99/C23 `<tgmath.h>` type-generic math LANDED (CONFIG-ONLY — zero src/ changes; every mechanism was landed + empirically proven pre-build).** NEW `shippedLibs/tgmath.json`: 17 function-like `_Generic` macros (the PROVEN shape `_Generic((x), float: sqrtf((float)(x)), default: sqrt((x)))` — ★ THE CAST DISCIPLINE is load-bearing BOTH ways: the float arm carries an explicit `(float)` cast [unselected arms are FULLY type-checked — a naked arm rejects S0003 on a long-double arg via unselected-arm narrowing] and the default arm is BARE [a `(double)(x)` cast LAUNDERS a complex arg — legal C 6.3.1.7 drops imag → silently ran exit 2, a conformance miscompile — and launders F80 narrowing; bare = complex/F80 args fail S0003 LOUD]); pow/atan2/fmod = nested `_Generic` (both-float → f-variant, else bare f64; C11 7.25p3 int→double); ldexp dispatches on arg 1; fabs/ldexp = per-format `variants` (the setjmp.json precedent) — the pe float arm bridges through the f64 fn (`(float)fabs((double)(x))`, EXACT: |x| and 2^n·x of any F32 are exactly representable in F64) since msvcrt does NOT export fabsf/ldexpf (loader-verified 0xC0000139). +34 symbol rows (17 f64 + 15 msvcrt-exported f32 + fabsf/ldexpf gated `availableObjectFormats:["elf","macho"]`); math.json gains the same 17 f-variant rows. ★ `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-GENERIC-DISTINCT` STRUCTURALLY ROUTED AROUND (no double:/long double: arm pair is ever spelled — on f64 formats a long-double arg IS F64 and rides `default:` correctly-by-identity; on walled formats it fails S0003/the value walls LOUD): the blocker dissolved for this arc, stays deferred. Blue-paint PP self-reference (`default: sqrt((x))` inside `#define sqrt(x)`) proven end-to-end (Prosser hide-set). Probe `c99_tgmath_generic` → 42 pe64+elf; example `c99_tgmath` (sizeof-witnessed dispatch — 1764=42² is precision-identical both paths, so `sizeof(sqrt(floatArg))==4/==8` is the observable) → 42 ×3 legs; the 17×3 matrix pins dispatch via narrowing-assignability (wrong arm = loud); the complex-arg hazard pin demonstrated red-on-disable against the SHIPPED file. | (closed — the macros + f-variant FFI surface IS the (g) deliverable) | plan-23 §0.1 FC17.9(g); math.json + `D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED`; sub-deferrals `-COMPLEX`/`-SURFACE`/`D-CSUBSET-MATH-FLOAT-VARIANTS-PE`; the routed-around `D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-GENERIC-DISTINCT` |

| `D-PP-EMBED` | **✅ CLOSED 2026-07-16 — FC17.9(h): C23 `#embed` + `__has_embed` LANDED (a preprocessor-substrate cycle).** `#embed "resource"` splices the resource's bytes as a comma-separated decimal integer-literal token list (the C23 phase-4 token-sequence model, valid anywhere a comma-separated integer-constant list is — NOT an AST array node, which would break the PP↔parser separation + agnosticism). As-built: CONFIG-DRIVEN vocabulary (`embedDirective`/`hasEmbedOperator` OPTIONAL config words — the `elifdef`/`hasIncludeOperator` opt-in pattern; NO hardcoded "embed" in the engine [the agnosticism bar; a config-driven pin rebinds "embed"→"embad"]) + a `handleEmbed` arm in the AUTHORITATIVE MacroExpander (behind `stackActive()`; the SynthBuilder pre-scan copies the `#embed` line VERBATIM → ZERO pre-scan change) + `resolveIncludePath` with the includer's dir (the `__FILE__` line-map-origin precedent → a header-resident `#embed` resolves relative to the header) + a BINARY read → the bytes via `materializeSignificant` (★ design-audit P2 CONFIRMED EMPIRICALLY: product Comma/IntLiteral tokens ARE accepted in brace-init position, 3 PE64 run-probes). `__has_embed` = a `PpHasEmbed` callback threaded into BOTH `evaluateIfExpression` callers (the `hasIncludeCb` twin-lambda precedent) minting the trichotomy NOT_FOUND(0)/FOUND(1)/EMPTY(2); `__STDC_EMBED_*` = predefinedMacros "0"/"1"/"2". ★ FIX-1 (design-audit IMPORTANT): a LOUD 16 MiB resource size cap (`embedResourceSizeError`, a PURE count→optional-diagnostic helper → `P_PreprocessorEmbed`=0x001D naming `D-PP-EMBED-STREAMING`) so a multi-MB embed FAILS LOUD, never OOM-crashes (~2 tokens/byte × several copies would exhaust memory before the 4 GiB text wall). ★ THE .gitattributes TRAP (design-audit P7): `examples/** text eol=lf` + no *.bin override would CRLF-corrupt a committed binary resource on clone/CI (INVISIBLE locally) → appended `examples/**/*.bin binary`. Probe `c23_embed` → 42; example `c23_embed` (answer.bin {42,13,10,26,0}, self-witnessing via blob[1]==13) → 42; 15 unit pins (byte-exact product-token stream [the P2 closure] + binary-hostility + resolution-relative-to-includer + params/angle/macro-arg/missing all loud + the trichotomy + the size-cap + T12 pre-scan-parity; 2 red-on-disable demonstrated). | (closed — the directive + `__has_embed` IS the (h) deliverable) | plan-23 §0.1 FC17.9(h); the quote-include / `__has_include` / `materializeSignificant` precedents; sub-deferrals `-PARAMS`/`-ANGLE`/`-MACRO-ARG`/`-STREAMING` |

| `D-CSUBSET-INLINE-ASM` | **✅ CLOSED 2026-07-16 — FC17.9(i, the LAST FC17.9 item): the empty-template `__asm__ volatile ("")` optimizer barrier LANDED.** A SMALL cycle — REUSES the existing `MirOpcode::CompilerBarrier` (c113 `_ReadWriteBarrier`), NO new MIR/LIR op. As-built: CONFIG grammar (`__asm__`=`AsmKeyword`; an `asmStmt` rule cloned from `staticAssertDecl` — `__asm__ [volatile] ( string-literal ) ;`, non-speculative; a `hirLowering` row → `HirKind::InlineAsm`) + a config-gated pass2Post semantic gate requiring a STRICTLY-EMPTY decoded template (`decodeAdjacentStringBodies`) → else `S_InlineAsmNonEmptyTemplate`=0xE057 (UNSUPPRESSABLE — so `asm("hlt")` can NEVER be silently lowered to a no-op barrier, a miscompile) + a NEW core `HirKind::InlineAsm` 0-child leaf + a `lowerStmtNode` case → `mir.addInst(CompilerBarrier)`. CompilerBarrier already joins hasSideEffects+opcodeClobbersMemory (the 4 optimizer gates block motion across it — the CSE `LoadNotCsedAcrossCompilerBarrier` pin) + lowers to ZERO LIR target-agnostically. ★ THE SILENT-MISCOMPILE RISK + its guard: an empty barrier is RESULT-NEUTRAL, so mis-mapping asmStmt→Skip (the staticAssertDecl twin, which lowers to nothing) would PASS the exit-42 probe while SILENTLY DELETING the barrier — invisible to any exit-code test; the T2 MIR-shape pin (`InlineAsmEmptyTemplateLowersToCompilerBarrier`, EXACTLY one CompilerBarrier) is the mandatory guard, red-on-disable DEMONSTRATED. Probe `c_inline_asm` → 42; `__asm__("hlt")` → S0057 loud; operand `:` → P0001 loud. | (closed — the empty-template barrier IS the (i) deliverable; ★ the pre-FC18 config gate was ALREADY closed [both `__STDC_NO_VLA__`/`__STDC_NO_THREADS__` removed in earlier cycles] → **FC17.9 100%**) | plan-23 §0.1 FC17.9(i); `MirOpcode::CompilerBarrier` (`D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-BARRIER`, c113); sub-deferrals `-TEXT`/`-OPERANDS`/`-GOTO`/`-SPELLING` |

Strict narrow-only enforcement rides this deferral. | Parse + encode per-target asm text. Trigger: a consumer needs real inline asm. Priority: normal (trigger-gated; a large arc). | `D-CSUBSET-INLINE-ASM` (parent); the src/asm per-target encoders |

Cycle-1 ships only atomic `load`/`store` (neither dss-state probe needs an RMW); an RMW needs a real `AtomicRmw`/`AtomicCas` MIR family lowering to the target's LL/SC retry loop (arm64 `ldaxr`/`stlxr`) or a lock-prefixed x86 op (`lock xadd`/`cmpxchg`) — NOT expressible via the cycle-1 load/store fence matrix. Cited in `stdatomic.json`. | Implement the `AtomicRmw` op family + the LL/SC retry loop when a consumer needs an RMW (the `D-LIR-LLSC-SPILL-EXCLUSION` exclusive-pair spill concern applies on arm64). Trigger: trigger-gated. Priority: normal. | `D-CSUBSET-ATOMIC` (parent); the `AtomicCas`/`lowerAtomicCas` substrate; `D-LIR-LLSC-SPILL-EXCLUSION` |
| `D-PARSE-EXPR-DEPTH-GUARD` | ✅ **GUARD DELIVERED 2026-06-20 (FC13 hardening cycle); CAP RAISED 16→256 on 2026-06-20 (FC13 cycle 2) once both cap-raise blockers (`D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK` + `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC`) closed — the cap is now a real SEMANTIC limit, not a host-stack artifact (parse is O(N) + the frontend's deep recursion [CU build + `analyze`] runs on a 64 MiB worker stack via `core/substrate/large_stack_call`). The "lowered to 16" note below is HISTORICAL.** The Pratt-walker expression-depth guard (`ParserConfig::maxExpressionDepth`, enforced at the single chokepoint `parseExpressionAt` in `src/analysis/syntactic/parser.cpp`) was a `fatal()`/`std::abort()` on overflow, and its cap (256) was ~10x too high for the host C++ stack: the heaviest path (the paren atom re-entry `parsePrimary->parseUntilFrameDepth->stepOnce->walkExpression->parseExpressionAt`) overflows a Windows ~1 MB Debug stack at depth ~25 — BEFORE the guard could fire — so deeply-nested input RAW-crashed (rc 127) or hung. **DELIVERED:** (1) the `fatal()` is now a POSITIONED `P_ExpressionTooDeep` (0x0018) at the offending token + graceful RECOVERY (`recoverExpressionTooDeep_`: emit-once + Error leaf via `panicRecover` + unwind), never an abort/overflow; (2) the default cap lowered 256→16 so the guard fires with comfortable margin below the Debug paren overflow (~25) while still admitting realistic hand-written C nesting (≤15 deep). STEP-1 empirical finding: (B) on-path (NOT a bypass) — `expressionDepth` DOES grow per nesting level; the cap was simply unreachable before the stack blew. **CAP-RAISE follow-up → `D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK`:** EXPLORED this cycle and found INSUFFICIENT — a parser-only large-stack worker thread does NOT enable a higher cap, because stage markers localized the ~25-level overflow DOWNSTREAM of the parser (semantic `analyze` / HIR-MIR lowering recurse over the expr tree on the host's MAIN ~1 MB stack); raising the cap needs the downstream stages deep-safe (`D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK`) AND the speculative cost bounded (`D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC`). **SECOND follow-up (distinct, pre-existing) — c-subset speculative-operand O(N²):** the depth guard eliminates the CRASH, but on PATHOLOGICALLY deep parens the c-subset full parse is SLOW (CLI @ cap=16: depth 100 ~2s, 150-200 ~12s, 250+ >25s) because the `operand` rule is `speculative:true` (compoundLiteral/cast/paren disambiguation, lookahead 64 = 1024-token probe budget): each of the cap-many paren levels re-probes the deep tail, and nested speculation (`maxSpeculationDepth=8`) rollback-re-parses it. This is INDEPENDENT of the cap (cap=8 still times out @ depth 1000) and of THIS fix (pre-fix the deep parens crashed in Pratt recursion before the speculation finished; the fix EXPOSES the speculation cost, it does not cause it). A NON-speculative grammar (`kParenSchema`) parses 1000 parens in ~10ms — the headline unit test pins exactly that (the GUARD mechanism, isolated from c-subset speculation). The fix is a speculation-engine / grammar concern (memoize probe results, or bound speculation by expression depth), NOT the depth guard. Note: only manifests on ALREADY-INVALID input (depth > cap); valid C nests ≤4 in the entire corpus. Tests: `PrattWalker.{ExceedingMaxExpressionDepthDiagnosesAndRecovers (lowered-cap structural pin), DeeplyNestedParensDiagnoseAtDefaultCapWithoutCrashing (1000 parens @ default cap → positioned diag, no rc-127), DeepPrefixChainDiagnosesAndRecovers, DeepTernaryChainDiagnosesAndRecovers}` (test_pratt_walker.cpp) PLUS the c-subset end-to-end golden pin `tests/corpus/diagnostics/c-subset/expression_too_deeply_nested.c` (real frontend → `P_ExpressionTooDeep 1:41-1:42` golden, the exact code+position at the 17th paren where cap=16 trips). | (guard delivered; cap-raise → the 2 dedicated anchors) | plan 05 (PA2 walker); `D-PP-FATAL-HALTS-PARSE` (sibling); `D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK` + `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC` |
| `D-PP-FATAL-HALTS-PARSE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-20 (FC13 hardening cycle).** The compilation-unit PP branch (`compilation_unit.cpp` `parseAndAdd_`) parsed UNCONDITIONALLY even when a FATAL preprocessor backstop had TRUNCATED the synthesized stream (the >256 macro-expansion-nesting guard or the include-nesting guard), feeding the truncated tokens to the parser — producing an inscrutable secondary cascade / hang on top of the real PP cause. **CLOSED:** added `PreprocessResult::fatal` (set ONLY by the two stream-TRUNCATING backstops: `MacroExpander::truncated_` at the >256 guard, `SynthBuilder::fatal` ref-threaded through the recursive include builders at the include-depth guard); the CU gate now keys on `pp.fatal` and, when true, feeds the parser an EOF-ONLY stream — yielding a minimal well-formed tree that STILL carries the (remapped) PP diagnostics, halting the parse before the cascade. Crucially the gate is NARROW: a RECOVERABLE PP error (missing `#include` file, malformed directive, redefinition) or a folded LEXER error leaves the stream INTACT and the parser STILL runs (an earlier `diagnostics->hasErrors()` gate was too broad — it swallowed the rest of a file on an unresolved `#include`; caught by `HirLoweringCSubset.IncludeDirectiveIsSkippedNotFailed` + the FFI ingest tests). Tests: `CompilationUnitPPGate.{FatalMacroNestingTruncationGatesParser, OrdinaryMacroCompileIsNotGated, UnresolvedIncludeStillParsesRestOfFile}` (test_compilation_unit.cpp). | (closed) | `D-PARSE-EXPR-DEPTH-GUARD` (sibling); plan 05; FC13 |
| `D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-20 (FC13 cycle 2 — deep-expression-nesting end-to-end).** Built the standard compiler facility for deep recursion: `src/core/substrate/large_stack_call.{hpp,cpp}` — `runOnLargeStack(stackBytes, fn)` runs `fn` on a dedicated worker thread with a `stackBytes`-RESERVE stack, JOINs (synchronous — no concurrency), and re-throws any exception `fn` threw (captured into `std::exception_ptr` on the worker, rethrown on the joiner; never escapes the entry → no `std::terminate`); value-returning convenience `callOnLargeStack`; `kDeepRecursionStackBytes = 64 MiB` (cap 256 × ~40 KB/level ≈ 10 MB, lazily-committed reserve costs ~nothing). FAIL-LOUD (`std::abort` + stderr) on every thread-API failure AND caller bug (empty `fn`, `stackBytes==0`, Windows `stackBytes > UINT_MAX` — reject, never truncate the 32-bit `_beginthreadex` arg). Windows arm = `_beginthreadex` + `STACK_SIZE_PARAM_IS_A_RESERVATION` + `WaitForSingleObject`/`CloseHandle`; POSIX arm = `pthread_attr_setstacksize`/`pthread_create`/`pthread_join` (CI-verified — no `find_package(Threads)`, raw symbols resolve as `std::thread`'s do). AGNOSTIC: pure host/OS utility, names no language/CPU/format; the `_WIN32`/POSIX `#ifdef` is host-portability. Self-test `tests/core/substrate/test_large_stack_call.cpp` (RunsTheCallable; RethrowsEscapedException value-preserving; ProvidesStackForDeepRecursion = ~1000 frames × `volatile char[8192]` ≈ 8 MB that the default stack can't hold). **KEY EMPIRICAL CORRECTION of the localization below:** wrapping JUST `analyze` was INSUFFICIENT — stage markers in the full c-subset→PE-exec pipeline showed the analyze-wrap lifted the DOWNSTREAM ceiling well past 25 (n=100..180 compile + run clean), but at the raised cap (256) a NEW overflow appeared UPSTREAM: the **CU build (parse / preprocess / type-name oracle)** in `Program::compileFiles`/`compileUnits` (`UnitBuilder::addFile`→`finish`) recurses building the expression tree and overflows the host main stack at ~n=185 (Debug-MSVC) — the low cap (16) had MASKED it (the guard fired at 16, no deep tree was ever built). So the CU build, `analyze`, AND the BUILD-half `buildCuMir` (HIR + MIR) are ALL wrapped on the 64 MiB stack: `analyze` via a file-static `analyzeImpl` + a thin public `analyze` (`callOnLargeStack(kDeepRecursionStackBytes, …)`); the two CU-build sites in `program.cpp` via `callOnLargeStack` returning the `CompilationUnit`. The BUILD half `buildCuMir` (= analyze + CST->HIR + HIR->MIR) is wrapped via a file-static `buildCuMirImpl` + a thin `buildCuMir` (`compile_pipeline.cpp`). **THIRD incompleteness, independent-audit-caught (this cycle's dss-audit):** wrapping JUST parse + analyze was STILL insufficient — CST->HIR (`cst_to_hir.cpp` `lowerExpr`/`lowerOperand`) and HIR->MIR (`hir_to_mir.cpp` `lowerExpr`) ALSO recurse one host-stack frame per nesting level, running inline on the MAIN thread inside `buildCuMir` AFTER `analyze`'s worker has joined; so a valid 41..256-deep expression that parsed + analyzed could still overflow downstream (it HAPPENED to fit at n<=250 on Debug-MSVC, but that is host/frame-size luck, NOT a guarantee — exactly the uncontrolled-margin the wrap removes). FIXED by the `buildCuMir` wrap (analyze nests as a benign inner worker; peak live-deep stack ~10 MB << 64 MiB). Only the LOWER half (MIR->LIR->codegen->link, `lowerMirModuleToAssembly`) genuinely needs no wrap — it iterates a FLAT SSA arena (`mir.hpp`), NOT a tree, so it is non-recursive in nesting depth (audit-confirmed). Cap raised 16→**256** (`parser.hpp`); now a real SEMANTIC limit (parse O(N) via `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC`'s LL(k) prune + analyze/parse on the big stack), so deep-but-legal C compiles end-to-end and only pathological nesting past 256 trips the positioned `P_ExpressionTooDeep` guard. **Verified:** n=100/200/250 c-subset compile (rc 0, ~0.2s) + run (exit 0); n=300 fails LOUD fast (`P0018`, no hang/crash). Test harnesses that build deep CUs unwrapped were also routed onto the facility (the corpus harness `test_diagnostic_corpus.cpp` for the >256-paren fixture; the headline `PrattWalker.DeeplyNestedParensDiagnoseAtDefaultCapWithoutCrashing` 1000-paren pin at the raised default cap) and the duplicate local `runOnLargeStack` in `test_parser_speculation.cpp` was CONSOLIDATED onto the substrate facility (Win32 `CreateThread` trampoline deleted). Re-added + DEEPENED the runnable witness `examples/c-subset/deeply_nested_expression` (200 nested parens → exit 7; PE-windows + ELF-linux) — a VALID deep expression that compiles AND runs through the WHOLE BUILD+LOWER pipeline at 200 nesting levels (closes the audit's M2: the prior 40-paren example + the guard-tripping fixtures never lowered a valid deep tree past HIR). Corpus fixture `expression_too_deeply_nested.c` regenerated to 300 parens (golden `P_ExpressionTooDeep 1:281-1:282` + `P_UnexpectedToken 1:582-1:583`). 335 ctest green. — *Original localization (audit trail):* Frontend stages DOWNSTREAM of the parser (semantic `analyze`, HIR/MIR lowering) recurse over the expression tree on the host's MAIN ~1 MB stack (Windows) and OVERFLOW at ~25 nested levels (rc-127, NO diagnostic), independent of the parser (which handles deeper). This is what caps `ParserConfig::maxExpressionDepth` at 16. Localized empirically via stage markers in `program.cpp` `runCusToTargets` (parse completes; the crash is inside `compileOneTarget`→`analyze`). A parser-only large-stack worker thread was EXPLORED this cycle and does NOT help (the crash is downstream). [SUPERSEDED: the crash was ALSO upstream in the CU build once the cap allowed a deep tree — see the close note above.] | (closed — `large_stack_call` substrate; CU build + `analyze` + BUILD-half `buildCuMir` [HIR/MIR] on a 64 MiB worker stack; cap 16→256) | `D-PARSE-EXPR-DEPTH-GUARD`; `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC`; `parser.hpp` maxExpressionDepth; `core/substrate/large_stack_call.hpp` |
| `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-20 — fixed by a config-driven LL(k) predictive prune (the `Correct direction (b)` below, IMPLEMENTED): `computePredictivePrefixes` (grammar_schema_json.cpp) precomputes a per-rule FIRST_k `predictivePrefix` at schema-load (the leading single-token run + the first variable element's FIRST set, STOPPING before a NULLABLE stop element so each recorded offset is an EXACT admissible-token set ⇒ a SOUND over-approximation); `predictivePrefixPrunes` (parser.cpp) prunes a speculative candidate when an OBSERVED next-token is outside its prefix; a lone unguarded survivor DESCENDS DIRECTLY (no probe). `((` → castExpr/compoundLiteral pruned (token[+1]=`(` ∉ FIRST(castTypeRef)) ⇒ O(N); n=300 154s→0.17s (~1100×); the lone-`(Identifier)` ambiguity still speculates (binder triage unchanged). Agnostic (walks FIRST sets + the position table, names NO token) + config-driven (FIRST + the rule's `lookahead`); independent review-folded — nullable-stop soundness guard + contextual-keyword precondition → `D-PARSE-PREDICTIVE-PRUNE-CONTEXTUAL-KEYWORD` + synthetic RED-on-disable tests (`ParserSpeculation.{DeepNestCastVsParenIsLinear, PrunesFirstBranchAtOffsetOneAndDirectDescends, NullableStopElementDoesNotOverPrune}`); 333 ctest. A RESIDUAL mild wall-clock super-linearity in the deep parse itself was CHARACTERIZED 2026-06-22 and promoted to its own row `D-PARSE-DEEP-NEST-RECURSION-MEMORY` — a controlled flat-chain-vs-nested experiment PROVED the parse WORK is O(N) (the "TreeBuilder/arena bookkeeping" guess first sketched here is REFUTED: a flat `0+0+…+0` chain of equal node count parses with FLAT per-element cost), so the residual [measured ~exp 1.7, not the ~exp 1.2 first estimated] is a constant-factor cost of the N-deep HOST recursion (inferred BY ELIMINATION to be memory-hierarchy behavior — not directly cache-instrumented), bounded/moot under the 256 depth cap — LOW-priority / trigger-gated. — Original characterization (audit trail):** The c-subset `operand` rule is `speculative:true` (compoundLiteral/cast/paren cast-vs-paren disambiguation; lookahead 64 = 1024-token probe budget). Deeply-nested input parses SUPER-LINEARLY (O(N²)+) in depth — measured @ cap=16: depth 20 ~0s, 100 ~3s, 300 TIMES OUT (>40s) — so deep input HANGS before the depth guard can fail loud, even at a LOW cap (the speculation descends the deep tail at the early levels, before the guard depth is reached). INDEPENDENT of the cap (repro @ 16/64/256) and of the guard (a non-speculative `kParenSchema` parses 1000 parens in ~10ms). FIX ATTEMPT 1 FAILED 2026-06-20: a code-trace MIS-diagnosed the cost as O(depth) `SpeculationProbe` state-snapshots (walker `cursorStack_`/`wrapFrameFlags_`, builder `open_`/`scopes_`); the O(1) size-truncation fix had ZERO timing effect AND broke the general non-append-only `SchemaWalker::snapshot/restore` API (`core/test_schema_walker`) — REVERTED. **MEASURED root cause 2026-06-20 (instrumented counters, NOT a code-trace):** the O(N²) is PANIC-MODE RECOVERY running INSIDE doomed speculative `castExpr` probes — `panicInSpec == panicRecovers` (100% of panics are in-probe) and `panicScanTokens ≈ tokensAdvanced` (the parser advances tokens almost solely to panic-scan inside failing cast probes, then rolls them back). For `((((0))))` the `castExpr` probe (tried BEFORE `parenExpr` in the `operand` alt) fails at each inner `(` (castTypeRef can't start with `(`) and `panicRecover` scans forward to the end-of-expression sync point = O(min(N,64)) tokens × N levels. **FIX ATTEMPT 2 FAILED 2026-06-20:** suppressing the panic-scan inside probes (`panicRecover` early-return when `speculationDepth > 0`) was correctness-safe (ctest 333-green) but COUNTERPRODUCTIVE — deep input HUNG WORSE (n=100: ~3 s → timeout, NO diagnostic emitted): the panic-scan is LOAD-BEARING for the failing probe to TERMINATE / make forward progress, NOT pure waste. REVERTED. **Correct direction (UNTRIED — a real engine rework, not a one-liner):** a CHEAP fail-fast for the cast-probe mismatch that PRESERVES termination — either (a) dispatch-level: fail the probe immediately on a required-alt no-candidate while `speculationDepth > 0` (instead of routing through panic recovery), or (b) a k-token candidate prune at the `operand` AltChoice (don't ENTER the cast/compound-literal probe when the token after `(` cannot start `castTypeBase`); both must stay agnostic (FIRST-set / generic dispatch, no language identity) and must not break cast / compound-literal / sizeof / the binder-sketch commit triage. Only manifests on deeply-nested input (valid C nests ≤4 in the corpus). | (closed — LL(k) predictive prune, direction (b)) | `D-PARSE-EXPR-DEPTH-GUARD`; `D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK`; `D-PARSE-PREDICTIVE-PRUNE-CONTEXTUAL-KEYWORD`; `c-subset.lang.json` operand rule |
| `D-PARSE-PREDICTIVE-PRUNE-CONTEXTUAL-KEYWORD` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-23 (FC-deferral close-out cycle 13, branch p14).** Closing the end-to-end "a contextual keyword in a speculative candidate's fixed prefix parses correctly" needed BOTH a PRUNE half (the registry's prescribed fix) AND a deeper PROBE half (the build DISCOVERED it; the anchor did not scope it → §B-surfaced, user chose build-deep). **(1) PRUNE:** a loader-derived token-id-keyed `contextualKinds` set (`computeContextualKinds` in `grammar_schema_json.cpp`, from the per-`LexemeMeaning.contextual` flags) + an O(1) `GrammarSchema::isContextualKind(SchemaTokenId)` query; `predictivePrefixPrunes` (`parser.cpp`) now `continue`s at any offset whose OBSERVED token is contextual → the candidate survives the prune. **(2) PROBE (the crux the build exposed):** even surviving the prune, the candidate's speculative probe `recoverAt(P_UnexpectedToken)`'d — the TokenLeaf MATCH GATE in `stepOnce` tests the UN-demoted keyword kind (`effectiveKind` by design does not model demotion), so the token never reached the builder's `pushToken` contextual-demotion. FIX: the match gate now MIRRORS the builder's demotion (a contextual `tokKind` ∉ expected → treat as a match when `identifierKind` IS expected) and advances the PARSER's walker with the demoted `identifierKind`, keeping it in lockstep with the builder's walker (no spurious desync-latch trip → the probe commits). Both halves AGNOSTIC (config-derived `isContextualKind`; the demotion target REUSES the parser's EXISTING `identifierKind` — the schema's resolved `Identifier` token kind, IDENTICAL to the builder's demotion this mirrors → walker lockstep) — EMPTY `contextualKinds` for a non-contextual grammar ⇒ ZERO behavior change, the deep-nest O(N) prune win preserved. (Review-noted pre-existing residual `D-PARSE-IDENTIFIER-KIND-HARDCODED-VS-CONFIG`: the parser+builder resolve the demotion target via the literal name `"Identifier"` rather than the live `semantics.identifierToken`; latent — all shipped grammars declare `identifierToken: "Identifier"` so they coincide; trigger-gated, NOT introduced here.) 374 ctest green, ZERO regression incl. **tsql** (the `reservedWordPolicy: contextual` grammar where the gate CAN fire). Pinned RED-on-disable with BOTH halves toggled INDEPENDENTLY (prune-skip off → caseId pruned; match-gate off → caseId survives prune but probe rejected → replays wrong candidate) by the synthetic contextual-keyword speculative-alt schema in `test_parser_speculation.cpp`: `ParserSpeculation.{ContextualKeywordInSpeculativePrefixNotMispruned` (soft keyword `kw` in caseId's `[A,Identifier,;]` prefix demotes + commits + asserts the `P_ContextualKeywordResolution` demotion diagnostic fired)`, HardKeywordInSpeculativePrefixStillPruned` (negative control — a HARD keyword stays pruned; the fix doesn't over-broaden)`}`. RESIDUAL: none for this anchor. — *Original OPEN characterization (audit trail):* **registered 2026-06-20 (FC13 predictive-prune soundness fold).** The LL(k) speculative-alt PREDICTIVE PRUNE (`Parser::predictivePrefixPrunes`, the O(N) deep-nest lever that landed alongside `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC`) compares a candidate's exact per-offset FIRST_k prefix against the observed token's `effectiveKind`. `effectiveKind` DELIBERATELY does NOT model contextual-keyword demotion (`parser.cpp` ~L63 — a soft keyword keeps its KEYWORD kind here; demotion to Identifier happens later in the builder against the live cursor expectedSet + scope stack). So for a grammar with contextual keywords (`contextual: true` per entry, or grammar-wide `reservedWordPolicy: "contextual"` — e.g. tsql), at an offset whose admissible set holds the demotion target (Identifier) the prune could see the keyword kind, find it absent, and WRONGLY drop a candidate the demoted token would in fact match — a silent mis-parse. NOT live today: c-subset's speculative prefixes are all hard keywords / punctuation, and tsql's lone `nameOrCall` speculative alt discriminates at offset 1 on `(` / qualified-name punctuation, not on a soft-keyword Identifier slot — so no shipped speculative candidate puts a contextual keyword in its FIXED prefix. The precondition ("sound only for grammars with no contextual / scope-resolvable kind in a speculative candidate's fixed prefix") is DOCUMENTED prominently at both `Parser::predictivePrefixPrunes` and `computePredictivePrefixes`. A clean conservative fix is deferred because no token-id-keyed "is this kind contextual" query exists today — the `contextual` flag is per-`LexemeMeaning` (lexeme-string-keyed); only the grammar-wide `reservedWordPolicy()` lever is exposed, which would NOT cover a per-keyword `contextual: true` under a Strict policy. | Add an agnostic token-id-keyed "is this SchemaTokenId a contextual/scope-resolvable kind" query (derive a `contextualKinds` set in the loader from the `contextual` LexemeMeaning flags + `reservedWordPolicy`), then make `predictivePrefixPrunes` CONSERVATIVE — skip the prune at any offset whose OBSERVED token has a contextual/scope-resolvable kind (do not constrain that offset). Must stay agnostic (no token/language naming) and preserve the deep-nest O(N) win for non-contextual grammars. Pin with a synthetic contextual-keyword speculative-alt schema whose candidate prefix admits Identifier at offset ≥1 + a soft-keyword input (RED-on-disable: the un-skipped prune drops the valid candidate). Priority: **normal (trigger-gated — first contextual-keyword speculative-alt consumer with a soft keyword in a candidate's fixed prefix).** | `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC` (the prune this guards); `parser.cpp` `effectiveKind` / `peekSignificantKind`; `grammar_schema.hpp` `reservedWordPolicy` / `LexemeMeaning::contextual`; tsql `nameOrCall` |
| `D-PARSE-IDENTIFIER-KIND-HARDCODED-VS-CONFIG` | **OPEN (trigger-gated) — registered 2026-06-23 (cycle-13 pr-review LOW).** The parser (`parser.cpp:247` `identifierKind(schema->schemaTokens().find("Identifier"))`) and the tree-builder (`tree_builder.cpp` `identifierKind_`) resolve the identifier / contextual-demotion target via the LITERAL token name `"Identifier"`, NOT the live `semantics.identifierToken` config field — which the SEMANTIC + HIR layers DO read (`semantic_analyzer.cpp` `cfg.identifierToken`, `cst_to_hir.cpp` `sem.identifierToken`, 20+ sites). LATENT: all three shipped grammars (toy / c-subset / tsql) declare `"identifierToken": "Identifier"`, so the hardcoded string and the configured kind COINCIDE — no test diverges. | `D-PARSE-DEEP-NEST-RECURSION-MEMORY` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (branch feature/0-0-2-p14, HEAD 941d75f, 388/388 ctest MSVC Release + MinGW).** **FOLLOW-UP 2026-06-24 (feature/0-0-2-p16): MISSED SITE `prescanLabels` flattened.** The arc flattened lowerExpr / lowerStmt / Assign / Switch but MISSED a SEVENTH host-recursion site — the FC5 label prescan (`cst_to_hir.cpp` `prescanLabels`, run in the `lowerFunction` prologue BEFORE the flat `lowerStmt` driver). LATENT because the close gate was MSVC **Release** + MinGW (smaller / 8 MiB frames fit) — only MSVC **Debug** (~1 MiB, larger frames) overflowed, and NO CI leg runs MSVC Debug (caught locally during the p16 deferral loop). FIX: converted to an explicit heap work-stack (children pushed reversed → popped left-to-right ⇒ byte-identical pre-order ⇒ identical label ordinals + S_DuplicateLabel order); `HirLoweringCSubset.DeepRightAssocAssignChainLowersFlatOnNormalStack` (already main's witness) now passes on MSVC Debug too; 394/394 Debug ctest. The stale lowerExpr comment claiming "Assign in lowerBinary DELEGATES to recursive helper UNCHANGED" is now also incorrect (the arc flattened Assign) — a separate doc residual, not load-bearing. *Original close note:* The full iterative expr+stmt+const-eval traversal LANDED across all six recursion families — the parser's 4 deepening expression arms (now the `exprWorkStack`/`driveExprWorkStack` driver; the paren/postfix arm is the §B-deferred Stage 5b residual, worker-bounded), ALL of cst_to_hir (expr+stmt+Assign+Switch), ALL of hir_to_mir (value+address+CFG+Call+stmt), semantic `subtreeType`+pass1/2, and BOTH const-evaluators are now explicit heap work-stacks → deep nesting is flat O(1) host-stack. `maxExpressionDepth` lifted 256→1024 CONFIG-DRIVEN (`P_ExpressionTooDeep` backstop KEPT — BC-1). See [plan 24](24-iterative-expression-traversal%20-%20tbd.md) (CLOSED). **SUPERSEDED below:** the "Convert the recursive-descent EXPRESSION parser… `parseExpressionAt`/`parsePrimary`/`parseUntilFrameDepth`" fix-direction + the `parser.cpp` `parseExpressionAt` cross-ref are now that iterative driver (stale symbol names, kept as the historical fix-sketch). — *Original (audit trail) — was 🟦 IN PROGRESS, staged build (cycle 14+, 2026-06-23). USER FIRED THE TRIGGER (4× sign-off, "execute as many cycles as needed"):* full iterative expr+stmt+const-eval traversal across SIX recursion families (parser / cst_to_hir expr+stmt / hir_to_mir expr+stmt / semantic / const-eval ×2), replacing host recursion with explicit heap work-stacks so deep nesting is flat O(N) host-stack. Plan-locked (REWORK→reworked) design at [plan 24](24-iterative-expression-traversal%20-%20tbd.md); backup branch `feature/0-0-2-p14-before-rework`. Acknowledged: NOT a crash fix (already mitigated by the 64 MiB worker), NO production consumer — a robustness goal. Cap is RAISED not removed (`P_ExpressionTooDeep` backstop kept). — *Original characterization (audit trail):* **OPEN (trigger-gated) — registered 2026-06-22 (residual deep-expr parse super-linearity characterization cycle).** A deeply-nested expression like `((((0))))` parses with a residual MILD WALL-CLOCK super-linearity (measured ~exp 1.7, Debug, cap=4000 on a large-stack thread: depth 250 ≈23 ms → 500 ≈71 ms → 1000 ≈240 ms → 2000 ≈873 ms → 3000 ≈1897 ms; ns/level rises 93k→632k). DISTINCT from `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC` (the now-FIXED O(N²) cast-vs-paren panic-scan blowup — that made n=300 take 154 s; GONE, n=3000 is now 1.9 s); it is the SAME residual a sibling session flagged (matching ~exp-1.7). **ROOT CAUSE — decisively localized by a CONTROLLED experiment, not a code-trace:** deep `((((0))))` (host-recursion depth = N) vs a flat `0+0+…+0` left-assoc chain (recursion depth ~2, SAME O(N) node/token count, built ITERATIVELY by the Pratt climb). The FLAT chain is perfectly O(N) — ns/level FLAT at ~20.5 µs across 250→3000, node count exactly linear — while only the NESTED case grows. Both exercise the IDENTICAL shared machinery (TreeBuilder frame open/close, the node-arena `std::vector` GEOMETRIC growth, schema-walker advance), so the "TreeBuilder/arena bookkeeping" hypothesis from the QUADRATIC row is **REFUTED**. Exhaustive code reading confirms every per-level op on the direct-descent paren path is O(1) (operand AltChoice dispatch, `candidateBranches` over the fixed 6-branch set, the LL(k) `predictivePrefixPrunes` over the fixed prefix, `peekSignificantKind`, frame open/close, ALL `SchemaWalker` ops, `finish()` once) and the lone-survivor `parenExpr` DIRECT-DESCENDS with NO `SpeculationProbe`/checkpoint/snapshot (so the O(N²)-snapshot path is not taken). The residual is therefore NOT algorithmic (PROVEN: the flat control isolates the shared WORK as O(N)) — it is a constant-factor cost of the N-deep HOST recursion, attributed BY ELIMINATION (not directly cache-instrumented) to that recursion's memory-hierarchy behavior: the live C++ call stack ≈ N × {parseExpressionAt, parsePrimary, parseUntilFrameDepth, stepOnce, walkExpression} frames PLUS the strided unwind re-access of the depth-indexed `frames`/`frameRules`/`cursorStack`/`pendingChildren` vectors as the working set outgrows cache (the rising-not-constant local exponent ~1.6→1.9 is consistent with cache-tier transitions, not a clean algorithmic O(N²)). **BOUNDED / MOOT:** `ParserConfig::maxExpressionDepth` caps depth at 256 (`D-PARSE-EXPR-DEPTH-GUARD`; valid C nests ≤4 in the whole corpus; >256 FAILS LOUD via `P_ExpressionTooDeep`, never parses) AND the whole downstream frontend (semantic/HIR/MIR) is itself recursion-bound to the same 256 cap (`D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK`) — so the residual only appears in a synthetic cap-raised micro-benchmark, never in production. Pinned by `ParserSpeculation.FlatChainParseWorkIsLinear` (the O(N)-WORK regression guard) + the existing `DeepNestCastVsParenIsLinear` (the deep-path O(N²)-regression guard, whose generous 8× bound is explained by this residual). | Convert the recursive-descent EXPRESSION parser (`parseExpressionAt`/`parsePrimary`/`parseUntilFrameDepth` + the `walkExpression` re-entry) to an explicit heap worklist/stack so deep nesting carries no host-recursion memory cost — AND, for any END-TO-END benefit, do the same for the recursion-bound downstream passes (`D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK`'s semantic/HIR/MIR), since the 256 cap is set by THEM. Stay agnostic (no token/rule/language identity). Pin with a deep-nest wall-clock ratio the iterative form keeps ~linear. Priority: **normal (trigger-gated — fires only if a real consumer needs expressions nested beyond the 256 cap AND the cap is first lifted by an iterative frontend; NO in-cap benefit otherwise).** | `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC` (the O(N²) sibling this is the residual OF); `D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK` (the downstream recursion bound that sets the 256 cap); `D-PARSE-EXPR-DEPTH-GUARD` (the cap); `parser.cpp` `parseExpressionAt`; tests `FlatChainParseWorkIsLinear` + `DeepNestCastVsParenIsLinear` |
| `D-PARSE-FLAT-CHAIN-WORK-LINEAR` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-04 (p19 Cluster G c108) — the flat-chain O(N)-work pin is now DETERMINISTIC (a token-access work counter), replacing a wall-clock ratio that flaked on shared gcc-release CI.** ROOT (USER-reported CI red, gcc-linux): `ParserSpeculation.FlatChainParseWorkIsLinear` @ test_parser_speculation.cpp:956 — the timing ratio t2000/t500 = 8.71 vs the <8.0 bound (t500=0.287ms t2000=2.498ms). REAL-vs-FLAKY diagnosis (the c97 precedent): reproduced under WSL gcc **Release** — 30/30 local PASS, and a 500/2000/8000 asymptote probe showed the parse is CLEANLY LINEAR at every scale (2000/500≈4.3, **8000/2000≈4.1** — no drift toward 16×), so the CI red was PURE shared-runner noise: a contention spike inflated t2000 on the sub-ms baseline that even min-of-9 couldn't escape (CI t2000=2.5ms was 5× my local 0.49ms). FIX (the strongest-provable-property upgrade, NOT a bound-widen): TokenStream gains a `mutable accessCount_` monotonic counter (++ in peek + advance, NEVER reset by rewind/restore — it counts re-scans) exposed via `accessCount()` → threaded onto `ParseResult.tokenAccessCount` → the test pins its FIRST DIFFERENCE CONSTANT over 500/1000/1500 (exactly 6000 = 12 accesses/`+0` element; a8000=96135 confirms at scale) alongside the pre-existing node-count first-difference pin. This is DETERMINISTIC (zero-flake) AND STRICTLY STRONGER: the token-access count is the exact quantity the wall-clock ratio was a noisy proxy for — a speculative-backtracking O(N²) ([[D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC]] class) re-consumes a growing prefix → super-linear accesses → the first difference GROWS. The flaky wall-clock ratio + its min-of-9 timing helper were REMOVED (the two deterministic first-difference pins fully cover the O(N²) intent). AGNOSTIC (the counter names no token/rule/language; the pin uses equal-spacing first-differences, no magic constant). 3-leg gate 556³. The DEEP-NEST residual (an N-deep host-recursion memory-hierarchy constant factor, moot under the depth cap) stays the separate still-open [[D-PARSE-DEEP-NEST-RECURSION-MEMORY]]. | (closed — deterministic token-access work metric) | p19 Cluster G c108 (USER-ordered gcc-CI fix); `token_stream.{hpp,cpp}` accessCount + `parser.{hpp,cpp}` ParseResult.tokenAccessCount + `test_parser_speculation.cpp`; sibling [[D-PARSE-DEEP-NEST-RECURSION-MEMORY]] + [[D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC]] |

| `D-FC12A-VARIADIC-AL-GATED-SPILL` | The variadic-callee prologue currently spills all `fpSaveCount` SSE arg registers UNCONDITIONALLY into the register-save-area (byte-correct — va_arg only reads fp slots the source actually consumed, so caller-garbage in unused slots is never observed).
