Resolved AGNOSTICALLY via a NEW config flag `aggregateStackExhaustsRegisters` (`TargetCallingConvention` + loader + x86_64=false / arm64=true), read by BOTH caller + callee cursor handling + flowing into va_start's `__gr_offs`/`__vr_offs` clamp. **Tests:** the 2 fail-loud pins INVERTED to `...Succeeds` (SysV payload==16 + gp_offset==40 backfill witness; AAPCS64 payload==24 + __vr_offs==0 exhaust + no -32) + a CALLER placement pin (`CallerNonVariadicStraddlingAggregateUsesStackCarrier`: ByValueStackArg + RecvByValueStackParam, red-on-disable revert Phase A) + the inlining gate REFUSES a callee with a `RecvByValueStackParam` (frame-sensitive, like `ReadIndirectResult`). **Runtime corpus:** `examples/c-subset/varargs_struct_straddle_fixed` (SysV native, exit 131) + `..._aapcs64` (qemu, exit 161), both under the shipped release pipeline (Inlining ON). **2 named fail-loud residuals (NO silent miscompile, rare multi-overflow shapes outside scope, cite the same anchor):** a stacked fixed scalar AFTER a stacked aggregate (the post-clamp/post-stack per-class ordinal can't site it), and a stacked aggregate preceded by another stacked fixed param (the per-class-ordinal scalar siting desyncs from the byte cursor). | (closed) | `D-FC12A-VARIADIC-OVERFLOW-FIXED-STACK-ARGS`; `D-FC12C-AAPCS64-VARIADIC-OVERFLOW-FIXED-STACK-ARGS`; `D-FC12A-VARIADIC-MEMORY-CLASS-STRUCT`; plan 23 FC12 |
| `D-FC12A-VARIADIC-MEMORY-CLASS-STRUCT` | ✅ **SHIPPED 2026-06-20 (FC12a-struct-memory).** SysV x86_64 struct-by-value at the variadic boundary for the MEMORY-class (>16B) + register-exhaustion-split shapes. **§B (user 2026-06-20): Option C — a first-class BY-VALUE-AGGREGATE STACK-ARG carrier** (a prior "push eightbytes as ordinary operands" design was REJECTED — an audit proved it a SILENT MISCOMPILE: the operands land in FREE registers, not the stack). SysV §3.2.3/§3.5.7 requires a MEMORY-class aggregate AND a register-exhaustion-split InRegisters aggregate to be passed ENTIRELY in the outgoing overflow (stack) area UNCONDITIONALLY (even when arg registers are free), never split. The greedy register-then-overflow callconv had no per-arg force-to-stack lever, so the carrier IS the lever: a NEW MIR op `ByValueStackArg` (operand 0 = the `freshAggregateTemp` copy address via `lowerByteWiseCopy`, same as the by-ref arg; payload = aggregate byte size; result = pointer, threaded through) sits as ONE Call operand at the aggregate's left-to-right position. `mir_to_lir` lowers it to a `Reg` (the address — kept live by liveness/regalloc) immediately followed by a NEW `LirOperandKind::ByValueStackAgg` size marker (mirroring the leading-Reg + trailing-descriptor memory-operand convention). `lir_callconv` (BOTH `computeMaxOutgoingStackArgs` pre-scan AND the materialize placement loop, via the shared `byValueStackAggSlots` helper) places it at the next overflow offset(s) — reserving ceil(size/outgoingSlot) slots + byte-copying the temp into `[sp + shadowSpaceBytes + overflowIdx*outgoingSlotSize]` (caller-saved scratch GPR avoiding every carrier addr reg + the indirect callee + the count reg + every pending arg-move / FP-dup SOURCE register — the silent-arg-clobber avoid-set, the review-caught BLOCKER-3 fold: the byte-copies run BEFORE the register arg-moves read their sources, so the scratch must not be a live arg source; a blanket argGpr exclusion was REJECTED as over-broad — a dest-only argGpr is written by its own arg-move after the copy, so its transient clobber is dead). It consumes NO arg register (runGpr/runFpr NOT advanced; class counters NOT advanced) and does NOT inflate the SSE/AL count (the GPR-class address Reg fails the FPR test; the marker is non-Reg). CC-NEUTRAL by construction (the byte size is the only datum; the overflow geometry is config-driven) so the Win64 + AAPCS64 struct-vararg cycles REUSE this carrier. Caller routing (`hir_to_mir.cpp` Call arg loop): MEMORY-class ByReference → carrier; register-exhaustion split (`runGpr+numGp > gpSaveCount || runFpr+numFp > fpSaveCount`) → WHOLE struct to carrier. Callee `va_arg(>16B)` (`lowerVaArgAggregate`): an EARLY ByReference dispatch → the overflow-ONLY arm (addr = overflow_arg_area, bump roundUp(size, gpSlotBytes)), never the register-gather diamond (numGp=numFp=0 would make the atomic-fit trivially-true → gather ZERO pieces). Wall-3 (fixed >16B struct param in a variadic fn): the fail-loud removed — `receiveByValueParam`'s ByReference arm receives the hidden pointer as 1 GPR arg (counted in `currentFnFixedGpr_`), so the common case (1 GPR + ≤5 scalars ≤ 6) lowers cleanly. The 4 fail-loud pins inverted to SUCCESS + structural pins (carrier present; va_arg overflow dispatch = no ICmpUle pair; the BLOCKER-1 force-to-stack + BLOCKER-2 outgoing-area sizing LIR pins in `test_lir_callconv.cpp`, both red-on-disable verified). Run-witnessed ELF x86_64 (WSL native): `examples/c-subset/varargs_struct_memory_class` (24B MEMORY-class, exit 60), `varargs_struct_split` (register-exhaustion split, exit 60), `varargs_struct_oddsize` (20B → roundUp(20,8)=24 tail bump, exit 66). REMAINING (own cycles, REUSE this carrier): Win64 `D-FC12B-WIN64-STRUCT-VARARG`, AAPCS64 `D-FC12C-AAPCS64-HFA-STRUCT-VARARG`; the SEPARATE `D-FC12A-VARIADIC-OVERFLOW-FIXED-STACK-ARGS` (fixed params overflowing to the stack) stays OPEN. | (shipped) | `D-FC12A-VARIADIC-OVERFLOW-FIXED-STACK-ARGS`; `D-FC12B-WIN64-STRUCT-VARARG`; `D-FC12C-AAPCS64-HFA-STRUCT-VARARG`; `D-FC7-STRUCT-BY-VALUE-ARG-RETURN`; `D-FC12A-VARIADIC-CALLEE` |
| `D-FC12B-WIN64-VARIADIC-CALLEE` | ✅ **SHIPPED 2026-06-19 (FC12b).** **§B (user 2026-06-19): Option A — a closed `VaListStrategy` enum with the va_list TYPE DERIVED from the strategy, chosen over Option D (an explicit per-CC `vaListType` config field); derived avoids an unused-flexibility knob that must be kept consistent with the strategy. (Rejected extremes: a va_arg recipe-DSL, and a unified ptr-to-opaque-blob va_list — both break the bar.)** Microsoft x64 (ms_x64) variadic CALLEE support — `va_start`/`va_arg`/`va_end` for SCALAR + FP types, config-driven + source/target/linker-AGNOSTIC. The per-CC va_list TYPE + lowering STRATEGY is a NEW closed enum `VaListStrategy` (`aggregate_layout.hpp`, beside `AggregateClassKind`/`BitFieldStrategy` so the SEMANTIC va_list-type injection reads it without pulling the link/target substrate): `SysVRegisterSave` (FC12a, unchanged-but-now-strategy-tagged), `HomogeneousPointer` (Win64, this cycle), `Aapcs64DualCursor` (declared, fail-loud-until-FC12c). `VaListLayout` gains `strategy` (first field) + `namedArgSlotBytes`; `x86_64.target.json` ms_x64 declares `{"strategy":"homogeneous_pointer","namedArgSlotBytes":8}`. **BLOCKER-2 (va_list TYPE per ABI):** `analyze()` gains an `optional<VaListStrategy>` param threaded from the resolved CC (pipeline reads it where it reads `aggregateLayout`); the sema injection switches on it — SysV → `__va_list_tag[1]` (24B), Win64 → `char*` (8B), AAPCS64 → loud `S_VariadicCalleeUnsupported`. A wrong sizeof(va_list) would mis-size the `ap` local + corrupt the stack — pinned `sizeof(va_list)`==8 ms_x64 / 24 sysv. **BLOCKER-1 (no-shadow contiguous base):** Win64's named-arg HOME space (rcx/rdx/r8/r9 slots = the caller's shadow space) and the 5th+ stack args are CONTIGUOUS from `totalFrameSize + callPushBytes` (NO shadowSpaceBytes — adding it would skip the home and read past every named arg). A NEW MIR leaf `VaHomeArgAreaAddr` (payload = the slot-aligned named-arg count) + LIR `va_home_arg_area` op materializes to `lea [sp + totalFrameSize + callPushBytes + payload*outgoingSlotSize]`; the Win64 prologue spills the named int regs into the SAME base (spill-target == va_arg-read-target). HIR→MIR lowers va_start (Win64) to `Store(VaHomeArgAreaAddr, ap)`, va_arg to a LINEAR pointer bump (NO diamond: Load ap → Load T → Gep +namedArgSlotBytes → Store ap), va_end to nothing. **FP-dup (PART 5):** Win64 passes each FP vararg in BOTH the xmm AND the matching integer (home) reg; the callee reads va_arg(double) from the home GPR slot, so the CALLER duplicates each register-resident FP VARARG (operand past `fixedOperandCount`) into argGprs[slot] via a NEW dedicated opcode `movq_xmm_to_gpr` (`66 REX.W 0F 7E /r`; byte-pin `movq rdx, xmm1` = `66 48 0F 7E CA`). A dedicated opcode (not `movq_xclass`) because the encoding-variant guard keys only on (operandKinds, width) and cannot disambiguate the two MOVQ directions, and `movq_xclass` is consumed bidirectionally by Bitcast — overloading would force a silent miscompile on GPR→FPR Bitcast. Every va seam dispatches on `VaListStrategy` with a fail-loud default (no half-migrated site silently runs the SysV path on a Win64 layout). Run-witnessed on PE x86_64 (this Windows host): `examples/c-subset/varargs_win64_sum` (int+double varargs, anti-folded via a mutable-global seed). FAIL-LOUD: Win64 struct-by-value varargs (`D-FC12B-WIN64-STRUCT-VARARG`); Aapcs64 at every seam (FC12c). | (shipped) | `D-FC12B-WIN64-STRUCT-VARARG`; `D-FC12A-VARIADIC-CALLEE`; `D-FC7-STRUCT-BY-VALUE-ARG-RETURN`; `D-LANG-VARIADIC`; plan 23 FC12 |
| `D-FC12C-AAPCS64-VARIADIC-CALLEE` | ✅ **SHIPPED 2026-06-19 (FC12c).** AAPCS64 (ARM64-ELF) variadic CALLEE support — `va_start`/`va_arg`/`va_end` for SCALAR int+fp, config-driven + source/target/linker-AGNOSTIC, the THIRD `VaListStrategy` (`Aapcs64DualCursor`, beside SysVRegisterSave + HomogeneousPointer). `va_list = __va_list`, a typedef to the 5-field STRUCT DIRECTLY (NOT an array like SysV, NOT a pointer like Win64): `{void* __stack; void* __gr_top; void* __vr_top; int __gr_offs; int __vr_offs;}` (32B; sizeof pinned). Config = a `vaListLayout` block on `aapcs64` with the 5 field locators + GR/VR save geometry (gpSaveCount8/gpSlotBytes8/fpSaveCount8/fpSlotBytes16, regSaveAreaBytes()=192); the semantic injection + isVaList accept the struct shape for the strategy. **BLOCKER-1:** a NEW `fstur_q` opcode (STUR Qt,[Xn,#simm9], 128-bit, fixedWord `0x3C800000` — NOT `0x3C000000`=STURB which would spill 1 byte/FP slot → va_arg(double) garbage; byte-pin STUR Q0,[X1,#0]=`0x3C800020` + a disasm Imm9/MemBaseNoScale round-trip arm). The AAPCS64 prologue spills x0..x7 (GR block, 8B via the class GPR store) then v0..v7 (VR block, 16B via `fstur_q`) into the callee-local register-save-area at `vaRegSaveAreaOffset()`, ascending order. va_start inits the 5 fields: __gr_top=rsa+64, __vr_top=rsa+192, __stack=overflow base, __gr_offs=-(8-fixedGpr)*8, __vr_offs=-(8-fixedFpr)*16 (NEGATIVE i32 cursors). **BLOCKER-2:** va_arg's per-class diamond — Load the NEGATIVE class cursor, `ICmpSlt(offs,0)` (a reg slot remains while <0), reg arm: **SIGN-EXTEND the i32 cursor to i64 (SExt) BEFORE the byte Gep** (a 32-bit -40 zero-extended → 0xFFFFFFD8 ≈ +4 GiB wild addr → segfault), addr = `<gr|vr>_top + SExt(offs)`, bump by the slot stride; stack arm: addr = __stack, bump by gpSlotBytes=8 (the NSAA round-up quantum, even for a double); Phi the two → Load T. va_end = no-op. Every va seam dispatches on `VaListStrategy` with a fail-loud default. The callee reg-save-area zone is gated on strategy ∈ {SysVRegisterSave, Aapcs64DualCursor}. Run-witnessed on ELF aarch64 (qemu): `examples/c-subset/varargs_aapcs64_sum` / `_double` / `_overflow` / `_double_overflow` (the FP `va_arg(double)` __stack overflow arm — 9 doubles, the 9th from __stack, exit 60) / `_sret` (the variadic + >16B-struct-return COMPOSITION, exit 126). **Adversarial-review FOLD (2026-06-19):** the prologue save-area scratch-base picker (`add <scratch>, sp, #off`) excluded only the arg GPRs and so landed on **x8** — but on AAPCS64 x8 is `indirectResultRegister` (the incoming sret pointer); a variadic fn ALSO returning a >16B struct had its x8 sret pointer clobbered BEFORE `read_indirect_result` reads it (silent miscompile — the struct written to the callee's own frame). FIX: add `cc.indirectResultRegister`'s ordinal to the scratch avoid-set (→ x9, genuinely free). Structural pin `Aapcs64VariadicStructReturnPrologueScratchAvoidsX8` (red-on-disable verified: revert → scratch=x8) + the `_sret` qemu witness (red-on-disable: scratch=x8 → exit 0, not 126). **Also fixed a latent SHARED-regalloc bug surfaced by the FP-overflow witness:** `pickScratchRegs` (`lir_rewrite.cpp`) keyed its register-in-use mask by the GLOBAL register-table ordinal in a uint64 bitmask, but arm64's 33 GPR table slots push d31 to global ordinal 64 — past the mask → a fail-loud `out of 64-bit bound` on any function spilling an FPR up to d31 (the 9-double case). Re-keyed to an unbounded per-class `unordered_set` of global ordinals, mirroring `buildFreeLists` (lir_regalloc.cpp). Pin `LirRewrite.Arm64HighFprSpillScratchPoolHandlesOrdinalsBeyond64`. FAIL-LOUD: struct/union va_arg + struct-vararg-to-AAPCS64-variadic (`D-FC12C-AAPCS64-HFA-STRUCT-VARARG`). | (shipped) | `D-FC12C-AAPCS64-HFA-STRUCT-VARARG`; `D-FC12C-APPLE-ARM64-VARIADIC-CALLEE`; `D-FC12A-VARIADIC-CALLEE`; `D-FC12B-WIN64-VARIADIC-CALLEE`; `D-FF3-APPLE-ARM64-ABI-DIVERGENCE`; plan 23 FC12 |
| `D-FC12C-APPLE-ARM64-VARIADIC-CALLEE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-21 (FC12c) — runtime WITNESSED GREEN on the macos-15-arm64 leg.** Apple arm64 variadic CALLEE — realizes the variadic half of `D-FF3-APPLE-ARM64-ABI-DIVERGENCE`. Apple passes ALL variadic args on the stack (vs AAPCS64's register-then-stack), so `va_list` is a PLAIN POINTER (the existing `HomogeneousPointer` strategy, like Win64) that linearly bumps over the contiguous overflow area — NO register-save-area, NO dual cursor. Two NEW config flags: `variadicArgsAlwaysStack` (on the CC) forces the CALLER to stack every vararg past `fixedOperandCount` regardless of free arg registers (named args stay register-placed; gated on the flag + isVariadic so AAPCS64 + x86 CCs are unaffected — the outgoing-arg-area pre-scan mirrors the forcing so the area is sized right); `variadicUsesOverflowBase` (on `vaListLayout`) makes va_start anchor `ap` at the OVERFLOW base (`VaOverflowArgAreaAddr`) rather than the home base (Apple has no home area — named args stay in their registers, read via SSA). `apple_arm64` CC declares `{strategy:homogeneous_pointer, namedArgSlotBytes:8, variadicUsesOverflowBase:true}` + `variadicArgsAlwaysStack:true`. The prologue home-spill is gated `!variadicUsesOverflowBase` (Apple spills nothing); the callee reg-save-area zone stays 0 (HomogeneousPointer). `functionUsesVaStart` gained a THIRD op handle (`va_overflow_arg_area`, REUSED — not a new `va_stack_arg_area` op) so Apple's va_start is detected. Congruence: the va_start overflow-base lea (== totalFrameSize, no shadow/callPush on arm64) == the caller's first-vararg stack-store offset — pinned. Compiles on Windows (structural witness `examples/c-subset/varargs_apple_sum`, Mach-O `arm64:macho64-arm64-darwin-exec`); **RAN GREEN on the macos-15-arm64 CI leg at HEAD — `varargs_apple_sum` + `varargs_apple_struct` executed NATIVELY with their asserted exits (run 27891928277, 2026-06-21) → §A.5 cross-target runtime closure satisfied.** | (✅ closed — runtime witnessed, macos leg run 27891928277) | `D-FC12C-AAPCS64-VARIADIC-CALLEE`; `D-FF3-APPLE-ARM64-ABI-DIVERGENCE`; `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VARIADIC-SIGNATURE`; plan 23 FC12 |

| `D-ASM-AARCH64-LARGE-FRAME-IMM12` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-21 (imm12 cycle).** Shipped the AArch64 imm12-scaled unsigned-offset LDR/STR form: a NEW `EncodingSlotKind::Imm12Scaled` (encodes byteOffset/accessSize at bits 10..21) + new `load_u`/`store_u` opcodes in `arm64.target.json` (fixed words 0xF9400000/0xB9400000/0x79400000/0x39400000 + store peers) + the fixed32 encoder scaling (fail-loud on negative / non-access-size-aligned / >4095) + the disasm windows (Imm12 + Imm12Scaled) + a LOWERING-TIME selection in `lir_callconv.cpp` (offset≥0 && %accessBytes==0 && /accessBytes≤4095 && >imm9 → `load_u`, else imm9 `load`, else fail-loud) — AGNOSTIC: pure arithmetic + the `loadOpcodeHandles` optional-handle table (the `h.loadU==0` sentinel no-ops on x86_64; no arch identity branch). The prologue VR spill ALREADY works around this with a scratch-base register (`add scratch, sp, #vaRegSaveAreaOffset()` then small imm9 offsets — `lir_callconv.cpp` emitVariadicPrologueSpill), but ARBITRARY body loads from a large frame do not. | `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12` | ✅ **ADD/SUB/LEA-IMMEDIATE half CLOSED 2026-06-22 (FC12-deferral 2 / Item 3②)** — the PRACTICAL large-frame blocker. A function with a frame > 4095 bytes (`int big[9000]` = 36000B) failed to encode at THREE sites, all an AArch64 `add`/`sub`/`lea` IMMEDIATE > 4095 (the imm12 slot): prologue `sub sp,#36032`, epilogue `add sp,#36032`, and the high-element FRAME-ADDRESS `lea` (`&big[8999]` = 35996). FIXED scratch-free at the ENCODER (zero `lir_callconv.cpp`/`mir_to_lir.cpp` change): a new `EncodingSlotKind::Imm12HiLo24` slot + a 2-word shifted-imm12 macro variant per `add`/`sub`/`lea` — `op Xd,Xn,#(V&0xFFF)` (sh=0) then `op Xd,Xd,#(V>>12),LSL #12` (sh=1, fixedWord base|0x400000), magnitude-routed by a new optional `immMin`/`immMax` variant guard (1-word ≤4095, 2-word 4096..0xFFFFFF). Reaches 16 MiB; >16 MiB → `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-16MIB` (row below). AGNOSTIC (x86_64 has no imm12 slot → never reached; only slot-kind `==` + value-magnitude arithmetic). Byte-pinned (sub sp #36032 → 0xD13303FF/0xD14023FF; add → 0x913303FF/0x914023FF; lea → split) + boundary pins (4095/4096/16777215/16777216) + a callconv two-inst prologue/epilogue count pin; **run-witnessed `examples/c-subset/large_frame_arm64` exit 42 on qemu-aarch64 + x86_64-linux** (release pipeline). NON-leaf (saved-reg spill + call) AND register-pressure-spill large frames also confirmed correct at runtime (exit 42 / 104) — the frame layout keeps saved-regs/spills LOW (in imm12), so only the high array-element address goes beyond imm12 and routes through the now-fixed lea. Independent plan-lock + independent pr-review both CLEAN. **DIRECT frame LOAD/STORE displacement (the encodable scaled-imm12 reach) ✅ CLOSED 2026-06-24 (branch feature/0-0-2-p14, HEAD 15c728a, 389 ctest MSVC Release + MinGW + corpus `large_spill_frame_arm64` exit 42 qemu-aarch64 baseline+release).** The scaled-imm12 selection (`load`→`load_u`, `store`→`store_u` when the frame offset exceeds the unscaled imm9 ±256 reach and is encodable: non-negative, access-size-aligned, scaled ≤4095) is now applied at the SINGLE frame load/store CHOKEPOINT `selectFrameMemOp` inside `emitFrameLoad`/`emitFrameStore` (`lir_callconv.cpp`) — so EVERY frame access (spill reload/store, saved-reg save/restore, incoming + outgoing stack args, by-value copy, va spills) is covered BY CONSTRUCTION (the prior incoming-stack-arg-only selection was REMOVED and re-routed through the chokepoint; the encoder `Imm12Scaled` handler + the `load_u`/`store_u` JSON opcodes from `D-ASM-AARCH64-LARGE-FRAME-IMM12` needed NO change). AGNOSTIC: the swap is gated on `baseOp == h.store/h.load` (the universal GPR form — an FPR `fldur`/`fstur`/`fstur_q` keeps its class encoding) AND `h.storeU/h.loadU != 0` (0 on x86_64 → inert, byte-identical goldens). **The earlier "UNREACHABLE in shipped codegen / no producer emits one" claim was FALSIFIED 2026-06-24: the plan-24 `deep_stmt_mir` corpus (600-deep nested-if + the full-release optimizer spills ~590 vregs → frame > 256 B) hit this DIRECT frame `ldr/str [sp, #off]` residual on the macos-clang-release CI leg** — a real producer (heavy register-pressure spill under optimization) DOES emit a direct frame load/store beyond imm9. Closed + RUN-witnessed by a NEW deterministic corpus `examples/c-subset/large_spill_frame_arm64` (a 30-fixed-param AAPCS64 callee `g` called with 30 args → the 9th+ INCOMING stack-arg loads in `g` + the OUTGOING stack-arg stores + caller spill reload/store all exceed imm9 at offsets 256/264 → scaled `load_u`/`store_u`; returns p28+p29+p08 = 1+40+1 = 42, miscompile-sensitive; arm64-only because the un-optimized x86_64 caller exhausts its 16-GPR scratch pool materializing 30 args — a SEPARATE pre-existing regalloc limit, not this fix; qemu-aarch64 exit 42 VERIFIED + macho arm64 leg; RED-on-disable: revert the selector → the arm64 compile fails loud `A_ImmediateOperandOutOfRange` at offsets 256 AND 264 (store AND load) → cannot build) + the host-independent structural callconv pin `Aarch64HighStackParamUsesScaledImm12FrameLoad` (a 40-param callee → asserts `load_u` present in the materialized module + clean assembly, red-on-disable, runs on EVERY CI leg) + the agnostic counter-pin `X8664HighStackParamHasNoScaledImm12FrameOps` (x86_64 declares no `load_u`/`store_u`) + imm9/scaled BOUNDARY byte-pins (offset 255→imm9 STUR, 256→scaled STR, 32760→scaled imm12-max, 256 on the unscaled `store`→fail-loud). The genuinely-UNENCODABLE tail (offset NEGATIVE outside imm9, aligned >4095*accessSize=32760, or non-access-aligned outside imm9 — needs a register-offset / address-materialization form) STAYS fail-loud → the narrower residual `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-SCALED-IMM12` (row below). Priority: **CLOSED.** | (load/store displacement CLOSED 2026-06-24; the unencodable register-offset tail → `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-SCALED-IMM12`) | `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-SCALED-IMM12`; `D-ASM-AARCH64-LARGE-FRAME-IMM12`; `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-16MIB`; `D-FC12C-AAPCS64-VARIADIC-OVERFLOW-FIXED-STACK-ARGS`; plan 13 (asm encoding) |
| `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-16MIB` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-23 (FC-deferral close-out c12, branch feature/0-0-2-p14, 374 ctest + corpus `large_frame_beyond_16mib` exit 42 qemu-aarch64 baseline+release).** AS-BUILT (§B Option A — encoder macro, x16 baked): a NEW `EncodingSlotKind::Imm32MovzMovk` (value-bearing fixed32 slot; the encoder splits a 32-bit value lo16→word0 / hi16→word1 into the MOVZ/MOVK imm16 windows, mirroring `materializeViaMovkLadder`'s chunk split) + a NEW 3-word `fixedWords` variant per `add`/`sub`/`lea` in `arm64.target.json`, magnitude-routed by the variant `immMin:16777216`/`immMax:2147483647` guard. The macro is `MOVZ Xs,#(V & 0xFFFF)` + `MOVK Xs,#((V>>16) & 0xFFFF),LSL #16` + an EXTENDED-register op: for the sp-adjust `sub sp,sp,x16` (0xCB3063FF) / `add sp,sp,x16` (0x8B3063FF) with **x16 (AAPCS64 IP0 scratch) BAKED** into all 3 base words (NO resultSlot — sp=31 baked into the op word, validate's rule-G `imm32.movzmovk` baked-dest exemption); for the lea `add Xd,sp,Xd` (0x8B2063E0 base) SCRATCH-FREE — the displacement materializes into the lea's OWN dest reg Xd, threaded through all 3 words via resultSlot+extraResultSlots (the operand-0→rn wire sets Rn from the ACTUAL frame-base operand — sp here, so Rn=31=sp, byte-pinned; the override is general so a non-sp base would get its own Rn). The EXTENDED-register form (bit 21, option=011 UXTX) is the make-or-break: the shifted-register form (0xCB000000) would write XZR not sp → corrupt frame. AGNOSTIC (x86_64 has no imm16/movzmovk slot → never reached; slot-kind `==` + value-magnitude arithmetic only; the x16 identity is config-baked like XZR=31/sp=31). Byte-pinned (sub sp #0x1234000 → MOVZ/MOVK + 0xCB3063FF full 32-bit word; lea → MOVZ/MOVK + 0x8B2263E2; boundary pins 0xFFFFFF→2-word / 0x1000000→3-word / 0x7FFFFFFF→3-word / 0x80000000→fail-loud) + a callconv F4 structural pin (prologue's first word = `MOVZ x16` + x16 not value-allocated, durable scratch-safety) + **objdump-confirmed + run-witnessed `examples/c-subset/large_frame_beyond_16mib` exit 42 under qemu-aarch64 (baseline+release, Inlining ON) + x86_64-linux** (RED-ON-DISABLE: removing the variant's reachable range → A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant on the sub/add sp-adjust → the arm64 leg cannot produce a binary). Genuinely-unencodable residual → `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-2GIB` (row below; a >2 GiB frame goes negative through the int32 frame size → no variant matches → fail-loud). _Original deferral below._ NEW RESIDUAL of `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12` (FC12-deferral 2, 2026-06-22): the SCRATCH-FREE shifted-imm12 word-pair (`EncodingSlotKind::Imm12HiLo24` + the `add`/`sub`/`lea` 2-word variants `op Xd,Xn,#lo` then `op Xd,Xd,#hi,LSL #12` in `arm64.target.json`, magnitude-routed by the variant `immMin`/`immMax` guard) now lands an `add`/`sub`/`lea` IMMEDIATE in (4095, 0xFFFFFF] — a 16 MiB frame reach, covering every realistic frame (e.g. `int big[9000]` = 36000B prologue + `&big[8999]` lea). A value > 0xFFFFFF (16 MiB) STILL fails loud: the add/sub/lea variant selector rejects it (`A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant`, since `immMax:16777215` excludes it), and the encoder's `writeHiLo24` 24-bit gate (`A_ImmediateOperandOutOfRange`) is the defense-in-depth backstop. No shipped corpus has a > 16 MiB single frame; this row keeps the ledger honest about the genuinely-unencodable tail. Priority: **low** (only a single function with > 16 MiB of locals). | Add a THIRD word to the macro (`#hi2,LSL #24` reaching 36 bits) OR materialize the offset into a scratch register via MOVZ/MOVK + a register-form `add/sub Xd,Xn,Xm` and select it when the value exceeds 0xFFFFFF. | `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12`; `D-ASM-AARCH64-LARGE-FRAME-IMM12`; plan 13 (asm encoding) |
| `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-SCALED-IMM12` | **OPEN (trigger-gated) — registered 2026-06-24 (scaled-imm12 frame load/store close-out, branch feature/0-0-2-p14).** NEW narrower residual of `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12` (row above): now that the ENCODABLE scaled unsigned-offset frame load/store (`load_u`/`store_u`, LDR/STR `[Xn,#imm12]`, reach 0..4095×accessSize = 0..32760 for an 8-byte slot) is selected at the `selectFrameMemOp` chokepoint, a frame `ldr/str [sp, #off]` whose `off` is GENUINELY UNENCODABLE by BOTH the unscaled imm9 (−256..255) AND the scaled unsigned imm12 STAYS fail-loud `A_ImmediateOperandOutOfRange` at the fixed32 encoder (`src/asm/format/fixed32.cpp` Imm12Scaled handler). Three sub-cases: (a) a NEGATIVE offset outside imm9 (the scaled form has no sign bit), (b) an aligned offset > 32760 (scaled field > 4095), (c) a non-access-size-aligned offset outside imm9 (the scaled field is byteOffset/accessSize — an unaligned LDR is architecturally undefined). The chokepoint keeps `baseOp` (the unscaled form) in each case → the encoder fail-louds. NOT reachable by any shipped corpus: case (a) needs a base-relative negative beyond −256 (frame offsets are non-negative sp-relative), (b) needs a single frame spill/saved-reg/stack-arg offset > 32760 B (a ~32 KB spill area — far past the deepest corpus pressure), (c) needs an unaligned frame slot (all slots are slotSize-aligned). This row keeps the ledger honest about the genuinely-unencodable tail. Priority: **low** (the closing form is the register-OFFSET LDR/STR `ldr Xt,[Xn,Xm]` or an address-materialization into a scratch — only needed for a >32 KB single-access offset or a future unaligned/negative frame access, neither of which a producer emits today). | Emit the register-offset frame form (`ldr/str Xt,[sp,Xm]` after MOVZ/MOVK-materializing the offset into a scratch `Xm`), OR materialize the address `add Xd,sp,#off` (via the shipped shifted-imm12 / MOVZ-MOVK macro) then `ldr/str [Xd,#0]` — selected when the offset fits NEITHER imm9 nor the scaled imm12; stay agnostic (slot-kind `==` + value-magnitude arithmetic, no arch/cc identity). Pin with a synthetic >32760 frame-offset case (fail-loud red-on-disable) + a runtime corpus only if a producer ever emits a >32 KB single-access frame offset. | `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12` (the parent, load/store-displacement half closed 2026-06-24); `D-ASM-AARCH64-LARGE-FRAME-IMM12` (the scaled-imm12 encoder + opcodes); `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-16MIB` (the add/sub/lea-immediate sibling); plan 13 (asm encoding) |
| `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-2GIB` | NEW RESIDUAL of `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-16MIB` (FC-deferral close-out c12, 2026-06-23): the MOVZ/MOVK + EXTENDED-register 3-word form (`EncodingSlotKind::Imm32MovzMovk` + the `add`/`sub`/`lea` 3-word variants in `arm64.target.json`, magnitude-routed by `immMin:16777216`/`immMax:2147483647`) now lands an `add`/`sub`/`lea` IMMEDIATE in (0xFFFFFF, 0x7FFFFFFF] — a ~2 GiB frame reach, far past any realistic frame. A value > 0x7FFFFFFF (2 GiB) STILL fails loud: the frame size flows through `emitSpAdjust`'s `static_cast<int32_t>(bytes)` and `variantImmMagnitude`'s `immInt32` (int32) — a >2 GiB magnitude goes NEGATIVE, so `variantImmMagnitude` returns nullopt → NO variant matches → `A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant`; the encoder's `writeMovzMovk` 0..0x7FFFFFFF gate (`A_ImmediateOperandOutOfRange`) is the defense-in-depth backstop. No shipped corpus has a > 2 GiB single stack frame (it would itself exceed most process stack limits); this row keeps the ledger honest about the genuinely-unencodable tail. Priority: **low** (a single function with > 2 GiB of locals — absurd; fail-loud is correct). | Widen the frame-size carrier from int32 to int64 (`emitSpAdjust`, the magnitude key, the LIR ImmInt payload) AND extend the macro to a 4-word MOVZ + 3×MOVK (LSL #16/#32/#48) materialization, selected when the value exceeds 0x7FFFFFFF — only if a producer ever emits a > 2 GiB frame. | `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-16MIB`; `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12`; plan 13 (asm encoding) |
| `D-WIN64-LARGE-FRAME-STACK-PROBE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-23 (FC-deferral close-out c11, branch p14, 372 ctest + corpus `large_frame_win64` exit 42 native Windows baseline+release).** AS-BUILT: an INLINE page-walking stack probe emitted by the x86_64 encoder from a NEW `stack_probe` LIR op (chosen over `__chkstk` because dss PE exes are FREESTANDING — import only kernel32, not msvcrt where __chkstk lives; verified). Config-driven `stackProbePageBytes` on TargetCallingConvention (ms_x64=4096, all others 0/absent → no probe; validate() requires a power of two). `emitPrologue` (threaded `cc`) emits `StackProbe(sp, frameBytes, pageBytes)` when `cc.stackProbePageBytes>0 && totalFrameSize>pageBytes`, else the plain `sub rsp,F` (byte-identical small-frame/non-Windows path). The encoder hand-emits the loop `mov r11d,frame / .L: sub rsp,page / or qword[rsp],0 / sub r11d,page / cmp r11d,page / ja .L (0x77, compile-time rel8, no reloc) / sub rsp,r11` — r11 is ms_x64 caller-saved, not arg/return, probe runs before arg-home. AGNOSTIC (no `if arch/format==`; page step from the op operand, not hardcoded; arm64 OMITS the op → A_NoEncodingDeclared if ever emitted). RED-ON-DISABLE (the strongest kind): the corpus CRASHES (SIGSEGV exit 139) without the probe, exit 42 with — native on the Windows box. Byte-pins (frame 36000 non-multiple + 8192 exact-page, exact 0x77 ja) + prologue structural pins (ms_x64>4096→probe; ≤4096 + sysv>4096→plain). **★ the unforgeable native witness caught a real bug in the build: a first `sub rsp,r11` mis-encoded as `49 29 DB` (=`sub r11,rbx`) segfaulted EVEN WITH the probe → fixed to `4C 29 DC`, byte-pinned.** ORIGINALLY: **OPEN — a SILENT RUNTIME CRASH** (surfaced 2026-06-22 while adding the `large_frame_arm64` corpus, but ENTIRELY ORTHOGONAL to the arm64 fix — a pre-existing x86_64-Windows gap). DSS did NOT emit a stack-probe (`__chkstk` / page-walk) for `ms_x64` / `pe64` functions whose frame exceeds one guard page (4096B). A function like `int main(){ int big[9000]; ... }` for `x86_64:pe64-x86_64-windows-exec` emits a bare prologue `sub rsp, ~36016` that jumps ~9 pages PAST the stack guard page WITHOUT touching the intermediate pages → the first deep-slot access raises `STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION` (0xC0000005) at runtime. The Windows x64 ABI REQUIRES a function allocating >1 page to probe each page (`__chkstk`, or an inline `sub rsp,0x1000`/`test [rsp],eax` loop) so the guard page walks down one page at a time and the kernel commits each. Empirically verified: a 400B + a 4400B frame exit 42, a 36000B frame segfaults. Linux x86_64 + AArch64-ELF do NOT need it (kernel stack-growth guard-gap absorbs the multi-page jump — which is why `large_frame_arm64` exits 42 on x86_64-linux + qemu-aarch64). This is WHY the `x86_64:pe64` target was excluded from `examples/c-subset/large_frame_arm64/expected.json` (see its `$comment`). Priority: **normal** (a real silent-crash class on >1-page Windows frames — large local buffers; converting it to FAIL-LOUD is a cheap interim). | Implement Windows large-frame stack probing in the prologue SP-adjust (`lir_callconv.cpp` emitPrologue/emitSpAdjust), gated AGNOSTICALLY on a CC/format-declared stack-probe-threshold field (mirroring `redZoneBytes`/`shadowSpaceBytes`, NOT `if(arch=="x86_64")`): when the frame exceeds the page size, emit the page-walk probe (or a `call __chkstk`) before/in place of the single `sub rsp,N`; add a runtime corpus witness (re-add the `x86_64:pe64` target to `large_frame_arm64`) + a structural pin. INTERIM (cheaper, kills the silent crash now): a fail-loud guard refusing a >1-page ms_x64 frame until probing lands. | plan 13 (asm encoding); plan 14 (linker); `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12` (the arm64 sibling, closed) |
| `D-WIN64-PDATA-XDATA-UNWIND` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-06 (p19 Cluster G c114) — the pe64 leg emits Windows x64 SEH/unwind tables (`.pdata` RUNTIME_FUNCTION[] + `.xdata` UNWIND_INFO) per function and points `IMAGE_DATA_DIRECTORY[3]` (EXCEPTION) at `.pdata`; sqlite3.exe compiles SEH-only (no `SQLITE_DISABLE_INTRINSIC`) + RUNS on Windows (`--version`→3.54.0, canonical smoke `SELECT sum`→42, full CRUD 52, index-filter 40).** WHY: the x64 ABI needs each function's PROLOGUE described so `RtlVirtualUnwind` can reconstruct the caller's context; without `.pdata` a fault (or, later, a `__try`) cannot unwind. AS-BUILT (agnostic, format-gated): (1) a NEW format-neutral `FrameUnwindInfo`/`FrameSavedReg` (asm.hpp) carries each function's `{totalFrameSize, usesStackProbe, stackProbePageBytes, savedRegs[]}`; (2) `compile_pipeline.cpp` PROJECTS it from the callconv pass's per-function `FrameLayout` (`cc.perFunc[fi]`) onto `assembled.functions[fi].unwind` post-`assemble()`, mapping the DSS physical register ORDINAL → its x64 hardware encoding via `target.registerInfo(ordinal)->hwEncoding` (GPR ordinal==hwEncoding was coincidental; FPR ordinal 16..31 must map to xmm 0..15) — pure frame data, ONLY the pe writer reads it (ELF/Mach-O populate a dead optional, no `if(format==)`); (3) `pe.cpp buildFunctionUnwindInfo` RECOMPUTES the prologue byte-offsets deterministically (allocLen 7 for `sub rsp,imm32` / `9+28*pages+3` for the inline stack-probe; +8 per GPR save, +8/9 per FPR `movsd`), emits `UWOP_ALLOC_{SMALL,LARGE}` + `UWOP_SAVE_NONVOL` per GPR sorted DESCENDING by CodeOffset, DWORD-aligns each blob, and a first-byte prologue-shape guard (0x48 sub / 0x41 probe) fail-louds if the emitted bytes desync; Edits A–F chain `.xdata`+`.pdata` sections, headers, raw-size/pointers, `SizeOfImage`, and data-dir[3]. ★ THE FPR DECISION: DSS spills xmm callee-saves as low-64 `movsd` (not the 16-byte `movaps` that `UWOP_SAVE_XMM128` assumes), so the xmm save is OMITTED from the unwind codes (no matching UWOP; an xmm save does NOT move RSP → the stack/return-address walk stays exact) while its store STILL advances the CodeOffset cursor so following GPR saves stay right — the handler-case RESTORE deferred to [[D-WIN64-XMM-UNWIND-RESTORE]] (c116). WITNESSES: host-independent byte pin `PeExecWriter.FunctionUnwindInfoEmitsPdataXdataAndExceptionDataDir` (frame 0x20 + saves [xmm6@0, rbx@8, rbp@16] → CountOfCodes=5 [xmm6 omitted, else 7], rbx CodeOffset 23 [proves xmm width 8 via hwEncoding 6, not 9 via ordinal 30], EXCEPTION dir→.pdata; RED-on-disable=revert FPR-omit→fail-loud, or drop emission→no .pdata) + runtime corpus `examples/c-subset/pe_unwind_selftest` (main takes `&target_fn`, calls kernel32 `RtlLookupFunctionEntry`, asserts the OS-found entry's BeginAddress==the fn RVA → exit 42; RED-on-disable→NULL→91; pe64-only, `release`-pipeline arm). objdump on sqlite3.exe: `Entry 3 … Exception Directory [.pdata]`, 3200 RUNTIME_FUNCTIONs + 86 KB `.xdata`. **Trigger:** FIRED — sqlite's os_win.c saves xmm callee-saves, so the compile hit the FPR path. | (closed — `.pdata`/`.xdata` emission + the EXCEPTION data dir + the FrameUnwindInfo projection) | [[D-WIN64-XMM-UNWIND-RESTORE]] (c116 handler restore) · [[D-WIN64-CHKSTK-LARGE-PROLOGUE]] + [[D-WIN64-HUGE-FRAME-ALLOC]] (fail-loud siblings) · `D-WIN64-LARGE-FRAME-STACK-PROBE` (the probe whose length allocLen recomputes) · the SEH arc c115 (`__try`/`__except` parse) + c116 (funclets); plan 14 linker / plan 23 SQLite-green Windows leg |
| `D-WIN64-SEH-FUNCLETS` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-06 (c116) — the x64 SEH catch WORKS: sqlite compiles with SEH ON (NO defines) + runs (`--version` 3.54.0 / `SELECT sum` 42 / full CRUD 62), and `SQLITE_OMIT_SEH` is DROPPED from the harness.** c116 AS-BUILT: the `__except` FILTER is EXTRACTED into a synthesized `ms_x64` FUNCLET (arg0/rcx=EXCEPTION_POINTERS, arg1/rdx=establisher frame — NO new CC, ms_x64 already maps them; `SehExceptionInfo`→arg0, `SehExceptionCode`→`*(u32*)*(void**)arg0`, `SehFilterReturn`→Return); the parent's c114 UNWIND_INFO gains `UNW_FLAG_EHANDLER` (byte0 0x01→0x09) + the `__C_specific_handler` THUNK RVA + a SCOPE_TABLE `{Begin, End, Handler(=filter funclet RVA), JumpTarget(=__except body RVA)}` in `.xdata` (all link-time RVAs; the personality import is SEH-GATED — synthesized on demand, NOT in windows.json symbols, the c101 0xC0000139 law); parent locals (sqlite `pWal`) are recovered in the funclet via a NEW GENERIC `RecoverParentFrameSlot(base, slotIdx)` MIR op → `[establisherArg + parentLayout.localAreaOffset() + slot*slotSize]` (base = the funclet's establisher SSA param — NEVER a hardcoded rdx; offset from the parent's FINALIZED FrameLayout via sibling-layout threading into `materializeOneFunc` — the audit-Claim-3 pipeline change; NO frame pointer [establisher==parent post-prologue RSP for FrameRegister=0]; NO arch/format identity in the substrate — AGNOSTICISM VERIFIED by the code-audit); the c115 `SehFilterReturn→handlerBB` edge is KEPT as a prune/verifier FICTION that emits no runtime branch (the OS resumes at the scope-table JumpTarget); multi-block guarded bodies are laid out CONTIGUOUSLY (`SehParentPolicy::selectBlocks` relayout, enforced-not-assumed) so `[Begin,End)` is ONE PC range; the by-value-param gap (sqlite `pWal` is a bare `Arg`, not an alloca) fixed by the localescape model (force filter-referenced symbols memory-backed, `collectSehFilterReferencedSymbols`). H5 [[D-WIN64-XMM-UNWIND-RESTORE]] RESOLVED (see its row). WITNESSES (runOn windows): `examples/c-subset/seh_catch_av` (a real AV→42), `seh_catch_parent_local` (H1 — the filter reads a parent local→42, negative control `marker=7`→segfault, unforgeable), `seh_catch_multiblock` (a loop-in-`__try` that faults→42) + byte-exact `PeExecWriter.SehScopeTableEmitsEhandlerFlagAndScopeRecord` + `.SehGuardingFunctionSavingNonVolatileXmmFailsLoud` + 4 `SynthSehFunclets.*` MIR pins. Independent DESIGN-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (6 folded) + pre-commit CODE-audit GO (0 CRITICAL/MAJOR). 3-leg gate 563/563 (Win Debug + WSL x86_64 + arm64-qemu); both linux legs a proven no-op off the SEH path. c115 SHIPPED the FRONTEND (as-built): `__try compound __except ( expr ) compound` is a c-subset keyword+grammar (`sehTryStmt`, config-only), a core `HirKind::SehTryExcept` {tryBody, filterExpr, handlerBody}, and 5 neutral MIR ops — `SehTryBegin` (terminator, succs [tryEntry, filterEntry], region-id payload), `SehFilterReturn` (terminator, [i32 filterValue]→[handlerEntry]), `SehTryEnd` (single fall-through marker), `SehExceptionCode`/`SehExceptionInfo` (0-op value ops = `_exception_code()`/`_exception_info()`, MSVC excpt.h intrinsics, exposed as builtins + windows.json `GetException*` macros). Optimizer soundness kit (each pinned): `opcodeClobbersMemory += SehTryBegin/SehTryEnd/SehFilterReturn` (CSE/LICM never cross a region boundary — the FilterReturn membership is the audit-F1 filter→handler fix), mem2reg SKIPS any SEH-containing function (fault-time locals stay memory-true), SimplifyCfg never elides/merges a SEH-successor block, the inliner refuses SEH callees + routes SEH hosts through the single-block rebuild (MSVC parity). `MirVerifier::checkSehStructure` (I_SehStructure) pairs Begin/FilterReturn/End on the region id + single-pred filter/handler. HIR-verifier `checkSehContext`: `_exception_code`/`_exception_info` legal only in filter/handler (H_SehBuiltinContext); no jump INTO a region (H_SehJumpIntoRegion); option-(C) early-exit ban [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-EARLY-EXIT]]; no `&&label` into a region [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-LABEL-ADDR]]. `mir_to_lir` FAILS LOUD on all 5 ops (L_UnsupportedLoweringForOpcode citing THIS anchor, every target). c115 WITNESS: sqlite compiles WITHOUT `SQLITE_OMIT_SEH` — the 87 P0001/P0009 parse errors collapse to exactly 35 SEH-funclet-wall diagnostics (frontend fully handles the ~13 wal.c SEH sites). **CLOSING WORK (c116):** emit the x64 filter + handler FUNCLETS (separate mini-fns sharing the parent frame via the establisher-frame pointer / RBP-based parent-local access), the `__C_specific_handler` scope table + set `UNW_FLAG_EHANDLER` + the handler RVA on the c114 UNWIND_INFO, wire the dispatcher, resolve [[D-WIN64-XMM-UNWIND-RESTORE]] (handler-resume xmm restore), refine mem2reg to region-precise (perf), and DROP `SQLITE_OMIT_SEH` from build-and-test.sh. Witness (c116): a corpus `__try` catching a REAL access violation (PAGE_NOACCESS VirtualAlloc read) → 42, native Windows; sqlite compiles NO-defines + runs CRUD. | (closed c116 — funclets + `__C_specific_handler` scope tables + the generic `RecoverParentFrameSlot` op + OMIT_SEH dropped; commit at cross-plan) | [[D-WIN64-PDATA-XDATA-UNWIND]] (c114, the UNWIND_INFO this sets EHANDLER on) · [[D-WIN64-XMM-UNWIND-RESTORE]] (handler-resume restore) · [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-FINALLY]]/[[D-CSUBSET-SEH-LEAVE]]/[[D-CSUBSET-SEH-EARLY-EXIT]]/[[D-CSUBSET-SEH-LABEL-ADDR]] (the c115 trigger-gated fail-louds) · plan 23 SQLite-green Windows leg |
| `D-CSUBSET-SEH-FINALLY` | ⏳ **TRIGGER-GATED / FAIL-LOUD-IN-PLACE (opened c115 2026-07-06; trigger = a shipped consumer of `__try { } __finally { }`).** c115 PARSES the `__finally` termination-handler arm (`sehFinallyArm` grammar rule) so a use gets a POSITIONED fail-loud at CST→HIR (`H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind`, "SEH __finally … not supported (D-CSUBSET-SEH-FINALLY)") rather than a raw P0001 parse cascade — but there is NO HirKind/lowering for it. sqlite ground-truth (amalgamation-swept): ZERO `__finally` uses (only `__except`). CLOSING WORK (when a consumer appears): `__finally` unwinds via a TERMINATION funclet that runs on BOTH normal fall-through AND exceptional unwind — a genuinely different lowering from `__except`'s exception funclet (it needs `__try`/`__leave` exit interception + a scope-table termination entry). | ⏳ trigger = a shipped `__finally` consumer | [[D-WIN64-SEH-FUNCLETS]] (parent, c116) · [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-LEAVE]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-SEH-LEAVE` | ⏳ **TRIGGER-GATED / FAIL-LOUD-IN-PLACE (opened c115 2026-07-06; trigger = a shipped consumer of `__leave`).** c115 PARSES `__leave ;` (`sehLeaveStmt`) so a use gets a positioned CST→HIR fail-loud (D-CSUBSET-SEH-LEAVE) not a parse cascade; NO HirKind/lowering. sqlite: ZERO uses. `__leave` jumps to the end of the innermost `__try` body (structured early-exit within a guarded region — interacts with the [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-EARLY-EXIT]] single-exit invariant, so it needs the SehTryEnd-target machinery when it lands). | ⏳ trigger = a shipped `__leave` consumer | [[D-WIN64-SEH-FUNCLETS]] (parent) · [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-EARLY-EXIT]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-SEH-EARLY-EXIT` | ⏳ **TRIGGER-GATED / FAIL-LOUD-IN-PLACE (opened c115 2026-07-06, the c115 design-audit's option (C); trigger = a shipped consumer needing return/goto-out/break-out of a `__try` guarded body).** c115 REJECTS (HirVerifier `H_SehEarlyExit`) any `return` / goto-out / break-out / continue-out / computed-goto from inside a `__try` guarded body, so the guarded body has EXACTLY ONE exit (the fall-through `SehTryEnd`). WHY: this keeps c116's scope-table region membership CFG-DERIVABLE (reachable-from-tryEntry-without-crossing-the-join) and makes the SimplifyCfg boundary-absorb hazard STRUCTURALLY impossible. The audit ground-truth-swept sqlite: ZERO early exits in any of the ~13 wal.c SEH bodies, so this narrows NO real consumer. CLOSING WORK (option (A), when a consumer needs MSVC-complete early exit): emit a `SehTryEnd(regionId)` on EVERY region-leaving edge (fall-through + each goto-out/return/break/continue-out), and c116 emits instruction-precise (possibly split) scope ranges keyed on the markers (`.xdata` natively supports multiple scope entries per region). | ⏳ trigger = a shipped early-exit-from-`__try` consumer | [[D-WIN64-SEH-FUNCLETS]] (parent) · [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-LEAVE]] |

| `D-WIN64-XMM-UNWIND-RESTORE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-06 (c116) via option (b) PROVE-NO-CONSUMER + an ENFORCED fail-loud invariant.** The trigger fired (the c116 handler funclets landed). RESOLUTION: sqlite's 7 `__try`-guarding WAL-recovery functions spill ZERO non-volatile xmm (empirically — `.pdata`/`.xdata`-extracted; WAL recovery is pure integer/pointer code), so no `__except` handler resumes into a frame reading an unrestored xmm → the omitted `SAVE_XMM128` is harmless for the shipped corpus. This proof is now an ENFORCED INVARIANT, not a hope: `pe.cpp buildFunctionUnwindInfo` FAILS LOUD (`K_NoMatchingObjectFormat`) if a scope-table-bearing (SEH-guarding) function saves a NON-volatile xmm — so a future SEH-fn-with-xmm cannot silently ship a broken unwind table; it forces the option-(a) work (spill xmm6-15 with `movaps` + emit `UWOP_SAVE_XMM128`) to land first. PINNED red-on-disable: `PeExecWriter.SehGuardingFunctionSavingNonVolatileXmmFailsLoud` (a scope-bearing fn + an xmm6 save → the encoder errors; drop the `guardsSeh` arm → it silently omits the restore) + the paired non-SEH negative (`FunctionUnwindInfoEmitsPdata...` — the same xmm6 save on a NON-SEH fn encodes fine). [SUPERSEDED OPEN TEXT:] c114 OMITS FPR callee-saves (MS-x64 xmm6..15) from the `.xdata` unwind codes because DSS spills them low-64 via `movsd`, for which there is NO matching x64 UWOP (`UWOP_SAVE_XMM128` restores a full 16-byte `movaps` slot at an offset/16 node). This is CORRECT for c114's scope — an xmm save does not affect RSP/return-address reconstruction, so `RtlVirtualUnwind`'s STACK walk stays exact — but a language-level exception HANDLER that RESUMES after catching (SEH `__try/__except`, c116) needs the non-volatile xmm6..15 RESTORED to their entry values, which the unwinder can only do from a described `SAVE_XMM128` slot. CLOSING WORK (at c116): either (a) spill xmm callee-saves with a 16-byte `movaps` to a 16-aligned slot + emit `UWOP_SAVE_XMM128` (widen the DSS FPR save form on ms_x64), or (b) prove no `__C_specific_handler` scope resumes into a frame reading a clobbered xmm. Until then a handler that reads a non-volatile xmm after unwinding gets a stale value — a LATENT gap with NO shipped consumer (c114 has no exception handlers). | (closed c116 — prove-no-consumer + the fail-loud invariant) | [[D-WIN64-PDATA-XDATA-UNWIND]] (parent, c114) · [[D-WIN64-SEH-FUNCLETS]] (c116) |
| `D-WIN64-CHKSTK-LARGE-PROLOGUE` | ⏳ **TRIGGER-GATED / FAIL-LOUD-IN-PLACE (opened c114 2026-07-06; trigger = a shipped pe64 function whose PROLOGUE exceeds 255 bytes).** x64 `UNWIND_INFO` CodeOffsets are SINGLE BYTES, so a prologue longer than 255 B is undescribable by one `UNWIND_INFO`. c114's `buildFunctionUnwindInfo` FAIL-LOUDS (`K_NoMatchingObjectFormat`, "prologue exceeds 255 bytes … switch the large-frame probe to __chkstk") rather than emit a truncated/wrong table. The inline stack-probe ([[D-WIN64-LARGE-FRAME-STACK-PROBE]], `9+28*pages+3` bytes) crosses 255 B at ~8 guard pages (~32 KiB frame). CLOSING WORK (when a corpus reaches it): switch the large-frame path to `call __chkstk` (a fixed ~5-byte prologue needing only a small `UWOP_ALLOC`) — but msvcrt `__chkstk` reintroduces the freestanding-import question the inline probe was chosen to avoid — OR emit a chained-info (`UNW_FLAG_CHAININFO`) split. No shipped corpus reaches it today. | ⏳ trigger = a pe64 fn prologue > 255 B in a shipped/corpus build | [[D-WIN64-PDATA-XDATA-UNWIND]] (parent) · `D-WIN64-LARGE-FRAME-STACK-PROBE` (the probe length that would cross 255 B) |

CLOSING WORK: add the op-info-1 (u32-node) `UWOP_ALLOC_LARGE` arm when a corpus needs it. | `D-FC12B-WIN64-STRUCT-VARARG` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-20 (FC12b-struct).** Win64 (ms_x64, `VaListStrategy::HomogeneousPointer`) struct-by-value at the variadic boundary + `va_arg(ap, struct)`. **NO new MIR op / LIR kind / carrier, NO lir_callconv / aggregate_abi change** — the design REUSED proven mechanisms: the Win64 ABI passes a by-value struct in EXACTLY ONE arg slot (pow2-≤8B → `classifyAggregate(Win64BySize)` InRegisters[1 GPR piece] = the bytes BY VALUE; every other size → ByReference = a hidden caller-copy POINTER), placed like a scalar by lir_callconv's existing `cc.slotAligned` walk (Win64 has NO SysV MEMORY-class-to-stack rule — one struct = one slot, register-or-stack BY POSITION). **Caller (`hir_to_mir.cpp` Call arg loop, both walls restructured into a clean VaListStrategy branch):** the two fail-loud HomogeneousPointer guards (ByReference wall + InRegisters wall) → `appendByValueArg(operands, kids[i], argTy, *abi)` (the SAME helper the non-variadic path uses: InRegisters → copy-to-temp + load 1 I64 → 1 value operand; ByReference → copy-to-temp + push the temp POINTER → 1 operand) + `runGpr += numGp` (==1) so a subsequent vararg's slot index is right; the SysV atomic-fit/`gpSaveCount` split + `appendByValueStackArg` carrier + the AAPCS64 guard are SKIPPED for HomogeneousPointer and PRESERVED byte-unchanged for SysVRegisterSave/Aapcs64DualCursor. **Callee (`lowerVaArgAggregate` HomogeneousPointer arm):** classify via `byValueClassify(T)` — InRegisters (≤8B) → the slot IS the storage, return apVal (the slot addr), NO extra Load (mirrors the SysV overflow-arm `return ovfArea`); ByReference (>8B) → Load the pointer from `*apVal` → the caller-copy addr; both bump `ap` by `namedArgSlotBytes` (one slot, value or pointer — NOT roundUp(size,8)), LINEAR (no diamond), mirroring the scalar HomogeneousPointer va_arg. **Pins (red-on-disable verified):** the 2 fail-loud pins INVERTED to success + 2 new (`Win64VaArgStructLeq8ReturnsSlotAddr` / `Gt8DerefsPointer` — no ICmpUle/carrier, the >8B deref adds a Load; `Win64StructByValueVarargCallLeq8OneSlot` / `Gt8OnePointer` — the Call has exactly 1 value/pointer operand, no carrier). THE §B Mem2Reg/va_list HYPOTHESIS WAS REFUTED by investigation — the name is a MISNOMER kept for traceability to the §B that spawned it.** ACTUAL ROOT CAUSE: a PRE-EXISTING use-after-`pop_back` in `TreeBuilder::closeFrame_` (`src/core/types/tree_builder.cpp`) — `Frame& fr = open_.back()` → `open_.pop_back()` → then read `fr.openerSpan`/`fr.rule` from the dead back slot. UB: MSVC-release reads the stale-but-intact bytes by luck (passed windows-msvc + the Windows-only local gate), gcc/clang-release + ASan abort (the 4 non-Windows legs). NOT optimizer- or variadic-related — `varargs_win64_struct` merely drove the parser into the speculative-branch cascade-close shape (`trySpeculativeBranch` → `closeFrameOnce` → `closeFrame_`); the crash fires during PARSING, before MIR/the optimizer. FIX: snapshot `openerSpan`+`rule` into locals BEFORE `open_.pop_back()`, then `leaveRule` uses the copies — a generic, source/target/format-AGNOSTIC parser-lifetime fix (no identity branch). VERIFIED: 345/345 ctest (no regression) + MSVC-ASan red-on-disable on BOTH the corpus AND `dss_core_test_tree_builder` (revert → `container-overflow` READ at `closeFrame_` tree_builder.cpp:500 → restore → clean). REGRESSION GUARD: the existing TreeBuilder cascade-close tests (`OutOfOrderCloseCascadesAndDoesNotDoubleDiagnose` + every close-path test) are host-independent red-on-disable under any ASan leg; the corpus is a hard-crash witness on gcc-release too. COVERAGE LESSON: the Windows-only local gate STRUCTURALLY cannot catch sanitizer-class UB — the CI ASan/release legs are that gate, so confirm each commit's CI green BEFORE stacking the next cycle (the MSVC-ASan recipe is now a known local tool: `build-asan/` + `/fsanitize=address` RelWithDebInfo + global `/bigobj`, PCH on). **SECOND BUG (HIR→MIR lowering, UNMASKED by the 7f321fd parser fix, fixed in this commit):** a dangling-`std::span`-into-`TypeInterner::operandPool_` heap-use-after-free in `lowerAggregateCopy` (`hir_to_mir.cpp:2490`) — it held `interner.operands(aggTy)` (a span into operandPool_) across the field-copy loop's `interner.pointer(...)` interns, which REALLOCATE the pool → dangling read. ASan legs = heap-use-after-free; release legs misread the freed bytes → an invalid TypeId → `MirBuilder fatal: addInst: 'load' result type invalid`. Host-dependent (MSVC vector growth 1.5× usually has spare capacity → Windows + local gate pass; libstdc++/libc++ 2× realloc → the 4 non-Windows legs abort). The TWIN of the already-fixed FC7-C1c hazard at `hir_to_mir.cpp:4660`. FIX: copy the span into an owning `std::vector<TypeId>` before the loop (agnostic; matches the 4660 precedent). VERIFIED: 345/345 ctest + MSVC-ASan forced-realloc red-on-disable (revert→heap-use-after-free at lowerAggregateCopy→restore→clean; NO third bug). The investigation audited every `operands()`/`fnParams()`/`scalars()` span site — 2490 was the SOLE remaining unfixed instance. Recurring class (4660 + 2490) → preventive hardening pinned `D-TYPEINTERNER-OPERAND-SPAN-LIFETIME-GUARD`. _Original (refuted-hypothesis) blocker text below._ 🔴 OPEN — ACTIVE BLOCKER (immediate next cycle), discovered 2026-06-20 by the FC12c-struct Step-8.5 self-audit + CI verification. `examples/c-subset/varargs_win64_struct` (FC12b ②, the ONLY variadic corpus with an `optimizedPipelines` arm) crashes the dss COMPILER ("Subprocess aborted") on ALL 4 non-Windows CI legs (linux-clang-asan, macos-clang-release, linux-gcc-release, linux-arm64-gcc-release) while compiling its optimized pipeline; **windows-msvc-release + the Windows-only local gate PASS** — which masked it through both the ② and ③ cycles. DETERMINISTIC (41a64d7's single CI run aborted on all 4 legs together — the earlier "flaky/same-sha-flip" read was a sha misattribution; the 15:08 pass was on `c74aeae` ① which predates the corpus). §A.6 (all CI legs green) is VIOLATED on the branch tip. Leading hypothesis (self-audit forensic, **UNCONFIRMED — needs a linux/asan repro**): Mem2Reg (`src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp`) promotes the `va_list`/`ap` backing alloca to SSA — unsound, because a va_list aliases ABI-defined memory (the prologue-spilled register-save / home area), so the post-promotion access OOBs. Trigger HAS fired. | ✅ DONE — the real fix DIFFERS from this hypothesis-based plan (see the description column for the actual TreeBuilder root cause + the copy-before-pop fix). SUPERSEDED plan was: Reproduce on linux (WSL build or the linux-clang-asan config) → confirm/refute the Mem2Reg↔va_list root cause → fix AGNOSTICALLY: disqualify any alloca whose address feeds `VaStart`/`VaArg`/`VaEnd` from Mem2Reg promotion (the rule "a va_list slot is ABI-aliased memory", uniform across every `VaListStrategy`), OR mark the `ap` alloca non-promotable at lowering — NO target/CC/format identity branch. Add a HOST-INDEPENDENT red-on-disable opt-tier pin (the va_list alloca SURVIVES Mem2Reg under the optimized pipeline; red if the exclusion is reverted). Close the coverage gap that hid it: give ≥1 variadic corpus PER ABI (SysV / Win64 / AAPCS64 / Apple) an `optimizedPipelines` arm. | `D-TYPEINTERNER-OPERAND-SPAN-LIFETIME-GUARD` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-22** — shipped the Option-1 DEBUG generation-guard: `operands()`/`scalars()`/`fnParams()` now return a `GuardedSpan<T>` that captures a `poolGen_` counter (bumped once per pool-mutating intern at the sole `internContent` chokepoint, AFTER the dedup early-return) and ABORTS DETERMINISTICALLY on a read after a later intern — on EVERY host, no ASan / realloc-luck needed; under `NDEBUG` it is a zero-overhead `using` alias to `std::span<T const>`, so the public contract + codegen + all ~234 call sites are byte-identical (built clean with ZERO call-site edits). Pinned by `StaleOperandSpanAfterInternAborts` (debug death test — RED-ON-DISABLE DEMONSTRATED: guard off → "failed to die"; `GTEST_SKIP` in release where the guard is intentionally absent) + `FreshSpanAndOwningCopySurviveIntern` (no false-fire on immediate read + the own-before-mutate fix pattern survives later interns). The conservative guard found NO 3rd latent instance across the suite (347/347 ctest). Independent code-review CLEAN. `name()` (a `string_view` into the SEPARATE `names_` interner) is deliberately OUT of scope (no incident; extend the same pattern if a names_-realloc hazard ever surfaces). _Original OPEN text:_ PREVENTIVE hardening for a RECURRING bug class; all current instances fixed. `TypeInterner::operands()`/`fnParams()`/`scalars()` return a `std::span` INTO `operandPool_`'s heap buffer; any caller that retains such a span across a subsequent intern (`pointer`/`array`/`internContent` → `operandPool_.insert` may REALLOCATE) gets a dangling-span heap-use-after-free. This class has bitten TWICE, both fixed reactively via copy-to-owning-vector — `hir_to_mir.cpp:4660` (FC7-C1c, function params) + `hir_to_mir.cpp:2490` (lowerAggregateCopy, the `D-OPT-VALIST-ALLOCA-MEM2REG` cycle) — both host-dependent (MSVC vector growth masks it; libstdc++/libc++ realloc → non-Windows CI aborts) and INVISIBLE to the Windows-only non-ASan local gate. Pin with a unit test that retains a span, forces an `operandPool_` realloc, then asserts the guard fires (red-on-disable on EVERY leg). Source/target/format-agnostic. | `D-OPT-VALIST-ALLOCA-MEM2REG`; `D-FC7-STRUCT-BY-VALUE-ARG-RETURN`; plan 12 (MIR/interner) |

| `D-CSUBSET-LINKAGE-UNKNOWN-SPECIFIER-DIAGNOSTIC` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-04 (cycle 14).** Built as decided (agnostic specifier-identity + fail-loud ERROR). **Mechanism**: new per-decl facet `linkageSpecifierIgnoredKinds` (the prefix's STRUCTURAL token KINDS to skip — c-subset: AttributeKeyword/ParenOpen/ParenClose); loader resolves names→SchemaTokenId (unknown→C_UnknownToken, mirrors variadicMarker; non-array/non-string→C_InvalidSemantics); `linkageFrom` skips ignored-kind tokens and emits `H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier=0xF00C` on any non-ignored prefix token missing from `linkageSpecifiers` (skip-list DEFAULT = fail-loud). All THREE decl-lowering arms (lowerTopLevel func+var, lowerFunctionDecl, lowerExternDecl) route through the `linkageFrom` chokepoint — coverage by-construction; the **Step-3.5 design-audit caught + closed the lowerExternDecl bypass (Gate 1)**. 2 tests (func+var `__attribute__((bogus))` → H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier + hir->ok false), REGRESSION-PROVEN (disable emit → RED). Agnostic (SchemaTokenId + text compare, no hardcode), 193/193. Step-8.5 self-audit VERIFIED-CLEAN. **Latent follow-ups (deferred — no current consumer)**: (i) `linkageFrom`'s `linkageSpecifiers.empty()` early-return means a future lang with prefix + ignoredKinds + EMPTY effects-map would silent-skip — add a loader reject for that contradictory config when a real consumer appears; (ii) `lowerVarLike` (the non-topLevelDecl local-var path) uses raw `visible` — needs `declVisible` IF a future VarDecl-dispatch rule ever gains a `specifierPrefix`. Both fold into `D-DECL-PREFIX-STRIP-SHARED-HELPER`'s shared-chokepoint extraction. — ORIGINAL §B + decision retained below. The §B is resolved — do NOT re-litigate. **Decided design**: a per-language config distinguishes specifier-IDENTITY tokens (lookup-able in `linkageSpecifiers`) from STRUCTURAL syntax (`__attribute__`, parens) — likely a small facet listing the prefix's structural token KINDS to skip (e.g. `AttributeKeyword`/`ParenOpen`/`ParenClose`), so any OTHER prefix token (`StaticKeyword`, the attr `Identifier`) NOT found in `linkageSpecifiers` emits a fail-loud Error + diagnostic; the exact facet shape to be refined via feature-dev + dss-audit next cycle (keep it agnostic — the language declares the structural set, the engine just walks it). Behavior = Error (NOT GCC warn-and-ignore), per the original design-lock + §A.4. Priority: **high (next cycle).** — Cycle-13 review finding (silent-failure-hunter, MEDIUM): an UNRECOGNIZED specifier identifier inside `__attribute__((...))` — a typo (`((wek))`) OR a valid-C attribute c-subset doesn't model (`((noinline))`, `((packed))`) — is SILENTLY ignored by `linkageFrom` (facet miss → no effect, no diagnostic); the decl silently keeps default (Global) linkage. NOT a valid-code miscompile (static/weak are correct + proven) and GCC-compatible leniency (GCC ignores unknown attributes with a `-Wattributes` warning), but misses the project's fail-loud preference on unrecognized input. **§B DECISION (needs user input before building)**: (a) the AGNOSTIC MECHANISM — `linkageFrom`'s flat token-walk cannot distinguish an unknown SPECIFIER from structural SYNTAX (`__attribute__`, parens) without language config; the reviewer's "Identifier-directly-under-attrSpec" distinguisher is c-subset-grammar-specific (would break agnosticism if hardcoded), so a clean fix needs the language to DECLARE which prefix tokens/positions are specifier-IDENTITIES vs pure-syntax (a new facet or a per-rule marker), then the engine emits on an identity-token that misses the facet while still skipping syntax; (b) the BEHAVIOR — Error (strict) vs Warning (GCC `-Wattributes`) vs silent-ignore (current). Priority: **normal** (missing-diagnostic on unrecognized input, not a correctness bug for valid input). | Resolve the §B (mechanism + behavior) with the user, then implement the agnostic specifier-identity recognition + emit the chosen diagnostic; pin with `__attribute__((bogus))` → diagnostic. Revisit the earlier "unknown-attribute fork = fail-loud error" design note in light of GCC-compat + the agnostic-mechanism cost. | `D-CSUBSET-LINKAGE-SPECIFIERS`; plan 22 pre-OPT7 P1 |

| `D-AS4-ARM64-BASE-INDEX-LEA` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-16 (arm64 indexed-lea cycle, branch p8).** `lowerGep` (target-blind) emits a 4-op `lea` `[base, index, MemBase(1), MemOffset(0)]` for an indexed pointer access `p[n]`; x86 had the 4-op SIB lea variant but arm64's `lea` had only symbol (ADRP+ADD, D-AS4-3) + base+disp (ADD-imm12) — NO base+index, so EVERY `p[n]` failed loud on arm64 for int AND char alike (exposed by the char value-codegen cycle's `p[n]` witnesses — NOT a char limitation). Closed by: a NEW universal width-0 `EncodingSlotKind::MemOffsetZero` ("memoffset.zero") validator slot — the displacement twin of `MemBaseNoScale` (asserts the GEP's vestigial MemOffset IS zero, writes no bits; an Imm12 reuse was UNSAFE — bits 10..21 overlap Rm in the register-add encoding) — plus an arm64 `lea` 4-op variant `ADD Xd,Xn,Xm` (fixedWord 0x8B000000), wiring base→rn / index→rm / membase.noscale / memoffset.zero. Scale=1 + disp=0 (the GEP's only emitted shape today) IS the complete realization. Pins: `Arm64Encoder.BaseIndexLeaEncodesAddRegReg` (0x8B020020 byte-pin) + `BaseIndexLeaNonZeroDispFailsLoud` (memoffset.zero red-on-disable) + `examples/c-subset/char_ptr_indexed` GAINED arm64 targets (was x86-only because of THIS gap; runtime-witnessed char `q[n]`/`p[1]` + int `p[n]` on arm64-ELF qemu → 131/99/77, byte-identical exits to x86). | (closed) | plan 13 §2.4; commit 69dbf9f (#25); `D-AS4-3` (arm64 symbol lea) + `D-AS-ENCODINGSLOT-EXHAUSTIVE-WARN` (the slot-switch gate the new enumerator passed) + successor `D-AS4-ARM64-INDEXED-LEA-SCALE` |
| `D-AS4-ARM64-INDEXED-LEA-SCALE` | **⏸ TRIGGER-NOT-FIRED — RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-23 (FC-deferral close-out c8, branch p14): §B-surfaced as NOT a real bug, deliberately NOT built (a speculative build would violate trigger-discipline).** Empirically re-confirmed the reframe still holds: `scaleIndexToBytes` (hir_to_mir.cpp:1790/2052) pre-scales every array index to a BYTE offset at HIR→MIR, so the LIR `lea` always carries scale=1; a runtime-indexed WIDE-element array (`int a[N]; a[i]`, 4-byte stride, `i` a runtime var) RUNS on arm64-ELF under qemu TODAY — `examples/c-subset/array_storage_index` exit 42 (re-run 2026-06-23 on WSL qemu) — with NO hardware scaled-lea; the `MemBaseNoScale` slot (fixed32.cpp:895 → `A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant`) fails any scale≠1 LOUD, so a scaled stride can never silently miscompile. The hardware scaled-lea remains a pure OPT with NO consumer. **REFRAMED 2026-06-17 (array-storage cluster, Option A — `D-MIR-STORAGE-ARRAY-INDEX-GEP`): now a pure CODEGEN OPTIMIZATION, no longer a correctness gap.** The original trigger below ("a scale≠1 indexed GEP exists") now NEVER fires: Option A pre-scales every array index to a BYTE offset at HIR→MIR, so the LIR `lea` always carries scale=1 (the `membase.noscale` guard still fails any scale≠1 LOUD — never a silent wrong stride). Element scaling needs NO hardware scale for CORRECTNESS (and non-pow2 strides could never be hardware-scaled anyway — exactly why MIR pre-multiply is the complete solution). **New trigger:** a future address-mode-synthesis peephole that folds a `Mul-by-pow2 + Gep` into a hardware-scaled `lea` / `ADD Xd,Xn,Xm,LSL #k` is built (an OPT, not speculative). Priority: **opt, trigger-gated.** **Original trigger:** First consumer emits an indexed GEP with a NON-unit element scale (a typed-array `lea [base + index*sizeof(T)]`, scale ∈ {2,4,8}) — the ML6 address-mode synthesis co-design (`lowerGep` emits scale=1 today, so this is unfired). | Add the shifted-register arm64 lea form `ADD Xd, Xn, Xm, LSL #log2(scale)` (the ADD-shifted `imm6` shift-amount field, bits 10..15) + a `membase.scale → shift-amount` encoder slot; the `membase.noscale` guard already fails scale!=1 LOUD meanwhile (never a silent wrong stride). x86's 4-op lea already encodes the SIB scale. Priority: **opt (trigger-gated)** (build only when a scale-folding peephole consumer exists). | plan 13 §2.4; `D-AS4-ARM64-BASE-INDEX-LEA` (the scale=1 form it extends); `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-WIDE-IMMEDIATE` (sibling shifted-immediate precedent) |
| `D-AS-ENCODINGSLOT-EXHAUSTIVE-WARN` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-09** (hygiene/lockstep cycle) — the four `src/asm/format/` walkers' `switch (EncodingSlotKind)` sites (`fixed32::windowFor`, `fixed32_disasm::windowFor`, `x86_variable::wireSlot`, `x86_variable_disasm::valueForSlot`) had drifted non-exhaustive (missing the slot kinds added since they were written) → emitted `-Wswitch` on the macOS / linux-clang CI legs but INVISIBLE on local MSVC. Made every switch EXHAUSTIVE (one explicit case per enumerator, BEHAVIOR-PRESERVING — each previously-drifted slot maps to the SAME result the trailing fallback returned: `nullopt` for the three optional-returning functions, fail-loud-`false`/`A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant` for `wireSlot`) with NO `default:` arm; kept the post-switch fallback as the out-of-range-ordinal backstop (also satisfies constexpr / GCC-Clang `-Wreturn-type` / MSVC C4715 — an enum switch is not treated as exhaustive for control flow). Added per-file `-Werror=switch` (GCC/Clang) / `/we4062` (MSVC) on the four files in `src/asm/CMakeLists.txt` (mirrors `D-OPT-RUNPASS-EXHAUSTIVE-WARN`), so a future `EncodingSlotKind` enumerator added without updating a walker is now a BUILD ERROR on local MSVC too. SCOPE: the gate covers the four format-walker TUs (the `-Wswitch` sources). Other `EncodingSlotKind` consumers compiled elsewhere — notably the header-inline `slotShapeFor` classifier in `target_schema.hpp` (a fifth no-`default` switch) — are NOT under this per-file flag; a new enumerator there falls to `slotShapeFor`'s trailing `None`, caught LOUDLY at load by `checkSlotShape`'s shape-`None` rejection (an opcode declaring the new slot is rejected — never a silent miscompile) rather than at compile time. Extending the gate to those consumers (or making `slotShapeFor` exhaustiveness `static_assert`-backed) is a future completeness item, NOT a correctness gap. | (closed) | `D-OPT-RUNPASS-EXHAUSTIVE-WARN` (the optimizer-runPass precedent it mirrors); `target_schema.hpp` `EncodingSlotKind` / `slotShapeFor`; plan 13 §2.4 (encoding shapes); `D-AS-DISASM-FIXED32-SLOT-FAILLOUD` (deferral surfaced by this cycle) |
| `D-AS-DISASM-FIXED32-SLOT-FAILLOUD` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (deferral loop, branch feature/0-0-2-p16).** The 10 fixed32/ARM64 slots (Imm16/Imm9/MemBaseNoScale/MemOffsetZero/Imm12/Imm12Scaled/Imm12HiLo24/Imm32MovzMovk/SymbolPatchMarker/Imm19) in `x86_variable_disasm.cpp` `valueForSlot` now converge onto the EXISTING `A_RoundTripMismatch` fail-loud arm (alongside Rd/Rn/Rm/Ra/Imm26); the genuinely-undecoded x86 slots (ModRmRmMem/MemBaseScale/Disp32Mem/SibIndex/RipRelDisp32/CondCodeNibble/BlockRel32) STAY nullopt (the disasm-completeness gap D-AS5-MULTIWORD-DISASM — valid x86 slots, "value undefined here", not cross-shape). `fixed32_disasm::windowFor`'s mirror site needs NO parallel loud arm: there a no-window slot sets shapeOk=false → the variant is SKIPPED → the final no-match fail-loud fires, so a cross-shape slot can never masquerade as a VALUE; only valueForSlot, which EXTRACTS a value, carries that hazard (decision documented in-code). Pinned RED-on-disable by `DisasmDefensive.Fixed32SlotInX86VariableWalkerFailsLoud` (test_asm_roundtrip.cpp) — a synthetic schema built via `detail::TargetSchemaData` DIRECTLY (bypassing validate(), the only way to reach this otherwise-unreachable arm) feeds an Imm16-wired x86-variable opcode to `disassembleInst` and asserts A_RoundTripMismatch fires (pre-fix the slot returned silent nullopt → no diagnostic → test fails; verified by toggle). 394 ctest green. — *Original row (audit trail):* 2026-06-09 — surfaced by the `D-AS-ENCODINGSLOT-EXHAUSTIVE-WARN` cycle: `x86_variable_disasm::valueForSlot` returns SILENT `nullopt` for the fixed32 slots Imm16/Imm9/Imm12/Imm19/MemBaseNoScale/SymbolPatchMarker, whereas their siblings Rd/Rn/Rm/Imm26 FAIL LOUD (`A_RoundTripMismatch`) on the same "fixed32 slot reached the x86-variable walker" condition (the documented silent-failure-hunter CRITICAL-2 convergence). Harmless today — every such arm is unreachable under a valid schema (validate() rejects cross-shape variants) — so the exhaustiveness cycle PRESERVED the silent-`nullopt` rather than change behavior (a behavior-preserving hygiene cycle must not also fix a latent defense-in-depth gap). Priority: **normal** (defense-in-depth consistency; not a valid-program miscompile). | Route the six fixed32 slots onto the existing Rd/Rn/Rm/Imm26 fail-loud arm in `valueForSlot` (and decide `fixed32_disasm::windowFor`'s parallel treatment); pin with a synthetic cross-shape variant that reaches the arm → asserts `A_RoundTripMismatch` (RED-on-disable: the silent-`nullopt` path lets a bogus round-trip masquerade as a SymbolRef slot). | `D-AS-ENCODINGSLOT-EXHAUSTIVE-WARN` (the cycle that surfaced + deferred it); `D-AS5-MULTIWORD-DISASM` (the sibling disasm-completeness gap); silent-failure-hunter CRITICAL-2 |
