| `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MEMBER-DECLARATOR` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-26 (p18 Cluster G c10).** struct/union FIELDS used a LEGACY positional schema (`name:1, type:0`) so a function-pointer member `int (*f)(int);` (the SQLite-pervasive shape that derailed MOST structs → the cascade of `};`/scope-unbalance errors) was rejected at the member declarator. FIX = switch `structField`/`unionField` to declarator mode (`head:0, declarator:1`, the SHARED `declarator` rule every other position uses) so the parenDeclarator group + fnSuffix derive the fn-ptr field type for free. AGNOSTIC (config rows + the generic declarator engine). | ✅ The struct-field path was deeply legacy-positional (plan-locked COMPLETE inventory of Pass-1/1.5 — the cascade-avoider): grammar→`declarator` + `bitfieldDeclSuffix` as an OUTER sibling; rows→declarator-mode; FAM via threading `allowFlexibleArray` through `declaratorDeclaredType`/`applyDeclaratorSuffix` (the group recursion passes FALSE — a nested fn-ptr param's array is not the field's FAM); NAMED bit-field width by porting `resolveBitfieldSuffix` into the declarator-mode Pass-1.5 loop (★ searched from the structField `node`, NOT the declarator — the sibling-suffix gotcha that breaks every bitfield if missed); ANONYMOUS fields (`int :3;`/`int ;`) by minting a `<anon:…>` symbol in Pass-1 (after the declarator loop, when no named symbol bound) + a Pass-1.5 anon block (bit-field width + `S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing`). Everything downstream (composition/intern, fieldIndex densify, union-variant, designated-init, member-access, brace-init anon-skip) is MODE-INDEPENDENT (reads the symbol record) → no cascade. WITNESS: corpus `struct_fnptr_member` (member assign + CALL through → exit 42; pe-native + elf/macho/arm64) + 416/416 ctest. | p18 Cluster G c10; cited in `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (structField/unionField) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (FAM/bitfield/anon) + plan-23 §3.1 F6; pairs with the FC7 struct mechanism + `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-FNPTR-BRACE-INIT` (the brace-init follow-on). |
| `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-FNPTR-BRACE-INIT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-26 (p18 Cluster G c12, LOCAL case)** — discovered p18 c10. A struct fn-ptr member resolves + member-assigns + CALLS (c10), but LOCAL BRACE-INIT (`struct Ops a = { dbl, 5 };`) FAILED LOUD at the HIR verifier: `H_VerifierFailure: ConstructAggregate child[0] type N ≠ field type M`. ★ ROOT (NOT two distinct FnSigs — both the declarator-`fnSuffix` and the function-decl path intern the SAME `FnSig` via the shared `collectParamTypes`+`normalizeSoleVoidParams` chokepoints + `CcSysV`): a function-to-pointer DECAY GAP — the field is `Ptr<FnSig>` but the bare function name `dbl` stayed an undecayed `FnSig` DESIGNATOR (`operands(fieldPtr)[0]` IS that FnSig). Member ASSIGNMENT passes via `isAssignable`'s decay arm (which emits no node — a bare fn Ref inherently lowers to its ADDRESS at MIR), but a brace-init element must carry the field's `Ptr<FnSig>` TYPE for `ConstructAggregate`'s strict child==field equality. | ✅ Fix = option (a) TIGHT: CST→HIR `coerce` gained an `FnSig → Ptr<FnSig>` decay arm (a synthetic `Cast` lowered as a representation-free MIR `Bitcast` over the function's GlobalAddr, `mapCast(FnSig,Ptr)→Bitcast`), gated on the SAME same-signature predicate `isAssignable` uses (`tk==Ptr && operands(target)[0]==child.type`) — a SIGNATURE MISMATCH stays a loud `ConstructAggregate` verifier failure (proven: field `int(*)(int)` init with `int(int,int)` → still red). WITNESS: corpus `struct_fnptr_brace_init` (two distinct fn-ptr members + scalar field + call-through → exit 42; pe-native + elf/macho/arm64 + release arm). ★ RESIDUAL (separate anchor `D-CSUBSET-GLOBAL-AGGREGATE-RELOC-MEMBER`): a FILE-SCOPE/GLOBAL aggregate whose member is a RELOCATION (a fn-ptr `{dbl}` OR a data-ptr `{&x}`) is a PRE-EXISTING limitation (NOT a fn-ptr-specific issue, NOT this cycle's regression). | p18 Cluster G c12; cited in `examples/c-subset/struct_fnptr_brace_init/` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`coerce` FnSig→Ptr arm) + `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` (`mapCast` FnSig→Ptr Bitcast); follows on from `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MEMBER-DECLARATOR` (c10). |
| `D-CSUBSET-GLOBAL-AGGREGATE-RELOC-MEMBER` | **OPEN — pre-existing limitation surfaced p18 c12, 2026-06-26; fail-LOUD, normal.** A FILE-SCOPE/global aggregate (`struct`/array) whose initializer contains a RELOCATION-bearing member — a function pointer (`struct Ops g = { dbl };`, the SQLite VFS/method-table vtable shape) OR a data pointer (`struct P g = { &x };`) — fails LOUD: `H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind ... aggregate compound-literal/initializer must be lowered into a slot by ADDRESS ... (D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME)`. The global-init path (`hir_to_mir.cpp` `tryClassifyAsSymbolAddr`) recognizes only SCALAR symbol-address inits (`int* p = &x;` AddressOf(Ref), `char* g = "...";` Cast(string)) — an aggregate `ConstructAggregate` initializer returns nullopt, const-eval can't fold a reloc, and the runtime-init path can't store an aggregate RVALUE. NOT fn-ptr-specific (a `{&x}` data-ptr member fails identically); NOT a silent miscompile (fails loud); the LOCAL case works (runtime store-into-slot). The aggregate analog of F5's scalar symbol-address globals. | Closing work = extend the global-aggregate emitter to lay out per-FIELD data items with per-member abs64 relocations (mixed const + symbol-address members at their byte offsets — `lowerMirGlobalsToDataItems` per-field, the F5 scalar path generalized to aggregate members). Pin: `int x; struct P g = { &x }; … *g.p` → exit 42 AND `struct Ops g = { dbl }; … g.op(21)` → exit 42, both red-on-disable. | Surfaced p18 Cluster G c12 (`struct_fnptr_brace_init` deferral note); cited in `examples/c-subset/struct_fnptr_brace_init/expected.json`; `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` (`tryClassifyAsSymbolAddr`/`emitGlobals_`); the aggregate analog of `D-CSUBSET-SYMBOL-ADDRESS-GLOBAL` (F5). |
| `D-CSUBSET-PER-TARGET-STRUCT-LAYOUT` | ✅ **MECHANISM DONE 2026-06-26 (p18 Cluster G c13, plan 25); ALL 6 linux POSIX headers DONE (c14a-e); the per-target `variants` axis EXTENDED to constants/typedefs/macros + macOS errno PROVEN (c15a 187a54a / c15b 73c5763); remaining 5 macOS headers OPEN (c15c).** §B decision (user, 2026-06-26): build the FULL per-target struct-layout mechanism (not linux-x86-64-only) so macOS/arm64 SQLite are reachable without reworking descriptors. THE GAP: shipped-library `structs` were single-layout, but a field-accessed struct's byte layout DIVERGES per target — glibc `struct stat` is 144B on x86-64-linux (st_nlink 64-bit, st_mode@24 after __pad0) but 128B on arm64-linux (st_mode@16, st_nlink 32-bit) and different again on macOS (even different field NAMES: st_mtimespec vs st_mtim). libc writes the REAL struct + we read fields at OUR offsets → one layout SILENTLY corrupts data on the other targets. ★ CRUX (plan-lock-verified vs the code): x86_64/arm64 target.json `AggregateLayoutParams` BYTE-IDENTICAL (Natural+16) + `computeLayout` param-driven (no arch branch) → per-target offsets come ENTIRELY from the FIELD LIST → per-target layout = per-target field-LIST SELECTION in the decoder; injection + layout engine UNCHANGED; selection at semantic time does NOT multiply the c9 once-per-format-kind build. | ✅ MECHANISM: `ShippedStruct` gains optional `variants:[{when:{arch?,format?},fields}]` (XOR flat `fields`); `analyze()` gains `optional<string_view> activeTarget`=`target.name()` (nullopt=back-compat → flat decode, variants-only not injected); decoder EAGER-decodes all variants (anti-lurking, mirrors `signatureByDataModel`), matches `when` by GENERIC string-equality (AGNOSTIC), >1 match → `F_ShippedStructVariantAmbiguous` (0x501E, **kUnsuppressableCodes**), 0 match → not injected (reference → S_UnknownType); a variants-only descriptor DECLARES a struct surface. FIRST consumer = `sys/stat.json` `struct stat` x86_64-elf 144B + arm64-elf 128B (gated elf). ✅ DONE c14a-e: the full os_unix surface authored behind the mechanism — errno (E*+errno macro), fcntl (O_/F_ + struct flock + variadic open/fcntl via the c14c variadic-externs FFI extension), sys/stat (S_ + fstat/lstat/mkdir + S_IS* + st_*time macros), unistd (~28 fns + SEEK_/access), sys/mman (PROT_/MAP_ + mmap/munmap/mremap), pthread (fns + pthread_mutex_t shipped as a flat max-size 48B opaque typedef sidestepping the per-arch size). ✅ DONE c15a/c15b: the `variants` axis EXTENDED from structs to constants/typedefs/macros (shared `matchVariantWhen`; macros are format-only — arch isn't threaded into preprocess; 3 ambiguous-fail-loud diagnostics 0x501F-0x5021 + kUnsuppressableCodes 64→67) + `errno.json` proven on macOS (the `errno` macro → `(*__error())` macho vs `(*__errno_location())` elf; the divergent E* per-format; corpus `shipped_errno_macho` runs on the macos-latest leg). ✅ DONE c15c (2026-06-26, 433/433): the remaining 5 macOS headers (sys.mman/unistd/fcntl/sys.stat/pthread macho variants) — value-verified vs apple-oss-distributions/xnu+libpthread; the macho `struct stat` 144B Darwin (SAME SIZE as x86-64-elf, reordered → corpus pins st_size@96 FIELD OFFSET not sizeof — the c13 same-size-swap) + `struct flock` 24B (reordered) + pthread_mutex_t 64B/mutexattr 16B typedef variants (FIRST real consumer of c15a typedef-variants) + the O_/F_ divergences + PTHREAD_MUTEX_INITIALIZER __sig magic 0x32AAABA7; mremap/MREMAP_MAYMOVE/O_TMPFILE elf-only fail-loud; fdatasync/fallocate unref-externs. 4 corpus (macos-latest leg). pr-review caught + folded a __sig hex→dec typo. **ALL 6 POSIX headers serve BOTH formats; macOS POSIX = linux completeness.** This anchor's mechanism (c13) + the full os_unix authoring (linux c14 + macOS c15) are COMPLETE; the sqlite3.c re-probe is the user's downstream step. Pin: per-arch sizeof/offset (gate 6 — `shipped_struct_stat_{x86,arm64}` corpus CI + `RealSysStatPerArchLayout` unit) red-on-disable. | p18 Cluster G c13; plan 25; `src/ffi/shipped_lib_descriptor.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp` + `src/dss-config/shippedLibs/sys/stat.json` + `examples/c-subset/shipped_struct_stat_{x86,arm64}` + `tests/ffi/test_shipped_lib_descriptor.cpp`; builds on FC7 struct mechanism + c8/c9 availability; macOS constants/typedef variance extends the (scalar-closed) `D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH` theme onto this axis. |
| `D-CSUBSET-EAST-CONST-CAST` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-26 (p18 Cluster G c11).** A cast/sizeof type-name `(char const *)` — EAST-const (const BETWEEN the base type and the stars) — was rejected (P0009) while west-const `(const char *)` + the DECLARATION head `char const *p` already worked: the `castTypeRef` grammar had const slots only PRE-base + POST-stars, not POST-base-pre-stars (a decl puts the `*` in the declarator so its head `char const` parses; a cast/sizeof bundles base+stars in `castTypeRef`). SQLite casts pointers to const constantly (`(unsigned char const *)` — 50+ in the sqlite3.c compile.log). | ✅ Added an optional POST-base `ConstKeyword` to `castTypeRef` (`[const?, base, const?, star*]`); DROPPED the prior POST-stars const (`char * const` cast — a useless form: const on an rvalue pointer ≡ no const) to keep it UNAMBIGUOUS (two const optionals straddling the REQUIRED base token; two adjacent across the zero-star case is `C_AmbiguousAlternatives`). The const resolver IGNORES const for type identity (counts StarOp tokens + the base — line 813-831), so all forms intern the SAME pointer type → GRAMMAR-ONLY, no semantic change. WITNESS: corpus `east_const_cast` (`(unsigned char const *)` cast + deref → exit 42; pe-native + elf/macho/arm64). ★ The "DROPPED the prior POST-stars const … to keep it UNAMBIGUOUS" decision is SUPERSEDED by `D-CSUBSET-POST-STAR-CAST-QUALIFIER` (c29): the post-star qualifier is now ADMITTED by riding inside `pointerLayer` (present only WITH a star → no zero-star adjacency), so the c11 ambiguity argument no longer forces dropping it. | p18 Cluster G c11; cited in `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (castTypeRef); a sqlite3.c parse gap from the user's compile.log probe; pairs with `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MEMBER-DECLARATOR` (c10). |
| `D-CSUBSET-POST-STAR-CAST-QUALIFIER` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-27 (p19 Cluster G c29).** ROOT (sqlite3.c:106644, the `sqlite3MemCompare`/`vdbeRecordCompareInt` pattern): `const u8 * const p1 = (const u8 * const)pKey1;` failed P0009 "expected expression — got 'const'" at the POST-STAR `const` (the pointer's own east-const). `castTypeRef` had `{repeat StarOp}` — BARE stars, no post-star qualifier slot — and c11 had DELIBERATELY dropped a post-stars-const optional citing `C_AmbiguousAlternatives` (a 2nd post-stars optional adjacent to the post-base one across the zero-star case). ★ THE AMBIGUITY WAS THE KEY RISK — VERIFIED EMPIRICALLY BEFORE the impl (milestone-1): the c-subset schema LOADS CLEAN (no `C_AmbiguousAlternatives`) with the fix, because the qualifier rides INSIDE the pointer layer, present ONLY with a star — there is NO zero-star adjacency (unlike a separate post-star optional). FIX = change `castTypeRef`'s `{repeat StarOp}` → `{repeat pointerLayer}` (`pointerLayer` = `StarOp {repeat ptrQualifier}`, the SAME generic layer every declarator position uses; `ptrQualifier` = const/volatile/restrict). The resolver's co-located cast/typeRef arm counts each `pointerLayer` child as ONE pointer level for `ptrDepth` (alongside the still-supported bare-`StarOp` token form `typeRef`/`sizeofType` keep) and STRIPS the layer's ptrQualifiers — a cast yields an RVALUE with NO top-level cv (C 6.5.4): `(int * const)p` and `(int *)p` intern the SAME `Ptr<int>`, and a post-star VOLATILE is dropped (NO `VolatileQual` on the cast POINTER). ★ KEY CORRECTNESS POINT: the c27 PRE-stars-volatile POINTEE path (`volatile u32 *`→`Ptr<VolatileQual(u32)>`, the head-volatile-before-the-first-star arm) is SEPARATE and UNBROKEN — the star-run boundary for the `baseIsVolatile` scan is now the first `pointerLayer` child, so a post-star volatile inside a layer can NEVER reach the base-volatile wrap (the declaration path's east `* volatile`→`VolatileQual(Ptr<…>)` object-volatile is a DIFFERENT engine, `declaratorDeclaredType`, and stays). AGNOSTIC: reuses the generic `pointerLayer`/`ptrQualifier` rules + `cfg.pointerToken`/`DeclaratorConfig.pointerLayerRule` (no lang/arch/format/keyword hardcode). WITNESS: 4 parser pins (exact `pointerLayer`-nested shape for `(const u8 * const)`/`(u32 * volatile)`, the 5-form swap-keeps-parsing battery incl. `(volatile u32 **)p`, updated `(int*)`/`(char const *)` shape pins) + 4 semantic TypeId pins (`(int * const)p`≡`(int *)p` SAME id; `(u32 * volatile)p` no pointer VolatileQual; `(volatile u32 *)p` keeps pointee VolatileQual c27-unbroken; `(volatile u32 **)p`→`Ptr<Ptr<VolatileQual(u32)>>`); frontier repro `(const u8 * const)pKey1` compiles clean via the CLI → elf64 x86_64-linux. NO new runtime corpus — a cast top-level-cv strip is a no-op at runtime (the TypeId pins are the strict witness; a vacuous corpus is not shipped). 451 ctest UNCHANGED (the 8 new TEST() pins land in existing gtest binaries; no new corpus → no new ctest entry). | (closed — the `pointerLayer` reuse + the resolver strip IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c29; cited in `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`castTypeRef` `{repeat pointerLayer}`) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the co-located cast/typeRef arm — `isPointerStar` lambda counting pointerLayer + bare StarOp, the strip); SUPERSEDES the c11 `D-CSUBSET-EAST-CONST-CAST` "dropped post-stars const" decision; builds on c27 `D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-POINTEE` (the pre-stars pointee path) + c26 `D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-DECLARATOR-TYPE-NAME` (the abstract tail after the layers); a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green. |
| `D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-TYPEDEF` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-27 (p19 Cluster G c30).** sqlite3.c:187603 (the `sqlite3_config` / `SQLITE_CONFIG_LOG` path) — a BLOCK-SCOPED typedef as a STATEMENT inside a function: `typedef void(*LOGFUNC_t)(void*,int,const char*); LOGFUNC_t xLog = va_arg(ap, LOGFUNC_t);` failed P0009 "got 'typedef'" — `typedef` parsed ONLY at top level (`typedefDecl` in `topLevelDecl`); the block-item `statement` rule did not admit it (2nd instance 191331 `typedef int(*sqlite3FaultFuncType)(int);`). | ✅ FIX = a grammar line + a one-line HIR→MIR no-op. (1) GRAMMAR: add `typedefDecl` to the `statement` alt (FIRST = `TypedefKeyword`, DISJOINT from every other statement alt → unique-production direct descent, NO speculation; schema loads clean — no `C_AmbiguousAlternatives`). The ENTIRE typedef-name machinery was ALREADY scope-keyed → CLEAN EXTENSION, NO architectural change (no §B): Pass-1 binds the alias into `current` (= the enclosing block scope) in the Ordinary namespace; the type resolver (`resolveTypeNodeImpl` SE5 arm via `s.scopes.lookup`) AND the parse-time oracle (`BinderSketch`, which already opens/closes `block` scopes) both walk the scope chain → a block typedef resolves IN the block, SHADOWS an outer same-name typedef, and does NOT leak out (CLI-PROVEN: an outer use of the block-local name fails S_UnknownType, IDENTICAL to never-defined). (2) HIR→MIR: CST→HIR's `lowerTypeDecl` already MINTS the TypeDecl node, but `lowerStmtNode` (the leaf-statement dispatch) had NO statement-position TypeDecl case → added `case HirKind::TypeDecl: return true;` (a typedef emits no code; mirrors the TOP-LEVEL TypeDecl no-op). ★ The architecture explorer's "0 downstream lines" claim was WRONG here — the milestone-1 end-to-end CORPUS caught the missing lowering (H0009 H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind at the typedef); the analyze-only semantic pins did not (the c10 lesson: an exploration's "no downstream effects" is a CLAIM). An independent adversarial audit then confirmed clean lowering in EVERY statement context (if/for/while/switch arm, anon-struct/tag typedefs, fn-ptr call-through) + the non-leak + the release pipeline. Mirrors c28 (block-scoped composite definition). WITNESS: 4 semantic pins (node-shape statement-nested `typedefDecl` + alias→`Ptr<Fn>`; the sqlite `LOGFUNC_t` frontier shape; ★ BLOCK-SCOPE NON-LEAK — an outer use of the block-local typedef-name is a PARSE error, because a leak would corrupt the `BinderSketch` oracle into parsing the name as a type; SHADOW — an inner typedef resolves DISTINCT from an outer same-name one) + runtime corpus `local_typedef_fnptr` (a block-scoped fn-ptr typedef + call-through → exit 42; 4 targets + release). 451→452 ctest; anchor guard OK. | p19 Cluster G c30; cited in `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`statement` alt); a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; mirrors c28 `D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-TYPE-DEFINITION`; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green. |
| `D-CSUBSET-LABEL-BUDGET-CLIFF` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c31).** sqlite3.c:213922 — a C label before a LARGE statement: `json_parse_restart:` preceding a ~900-case JSON `switch` failed P0001 "got ':'". ROOT: `declOrExprStmt` (`alt:[labelStmt,identVarDecl,exprStmt]`, speculative lookahead 256 ⇒ a `lookahead*16` = 4096-token probe budget) speculatively parses `labelStmt = Identifier Colon statement` to COMPLETION; a labeled statement >4096 tokens exhausts the probe budget → rollback → exprStmt → P0001 at the `:` (confirmed empirically: a small labeled switch parses, a ~900-case one does not). | ✅ FIX = a NEW general parser facet `commitAfterPrefix` (a PEG "cut"), AGNOSTIC (names no token/rule/keyword/language). `labelStmt` KEEPS its `Identifier Colon statement` shape (so a label stays correct in EVERY context, incl. a braceless control-flow body — `if(x) L: s;` ⇒ `s` IS the if's body). The facet: once a candidate's FIXED leading token-prefix (`predictivePrefixLen` — here `Identifier Colon` + the first `statement` token) is consumed in a speculative probe WITHOUT failure, the parser COMMITS (`probe.commit()`) and hands the STILL-OPEN frame to the non-speculative dispatch loop — the rest of the rule parses with NO budget (exactly the unique-production direct-descent's "open frame, let the outer loop drive it"). SOUND because after `Identifier Colon` no other `declOrExprStmt` alt can match (neither identVarDecl nor exprStmt has Colon in 2nd position), so committing discards nothing; a non-label `foo + bar;` fails at the `Colon` slot BEFORE the prefix is consumed, so the cut never fires (falls through to exprStmt). Wiring: `CompiledRule::commitAfterPrefix` (bool) ← loader parses an optional boolean `commitAfterPrefix` on the shape body (fail-loud if non-boolean; mutually-exclusive with `commitRequiresTypeName`); `GrammarSchema::commitAfterPrefix(RuleId)` accessor; the cut block inside `trySpeculativeBranch`'s probe loop after the post-`stepOnce` diag/desync rechecks; `SpeculationProbe::startPos()` accessor. Only `labelStmt` carries the flag (`"commitAfterPrefix": true`). NO HIR/MIR/verifier/lowering change (`git diff --stat`: 6 files — parser.cpp, compiled_shape.hpp, grammar_schema.{hpp,cpp}, grammar_schema_json.cpp, c-subset.lang.json). ★ BRACELESS-BODY CORRECTNESS (the case that doomed any flatten-labelStmt alternative) VERIFIED at runtime: `if(x) L: g=42;` exits 5 for x=0 (the labeled `g=42` is the if's body and is NOT executed) and 42 for x=1. WITNESS: 2 HIR pins (`LabelBeforeOversizeStatementParsesPastProbeBudget` — a label before a ~5500-token block parses clean, red-on-disable ⇒ P0001; `LabelAsBracelessIfBodyStaysInsideTheIf` — the silent-miscompile guard: the if's then-branch IS the LabelStmt with the assignment UNDER it) + runtime corpus `label_before_switch_goto` (a `goto` INTO a label before a multi-case switch, fold-resistant via feed() + 3 goto passes → exit 42; 4 targets pe/elf/qemu-arm64/macho + release arm). 452→453 ctest; anchor guard OK. | (closed — the `commitAfterPrefix` PEG-cut facet IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c31; cited in `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`labelStmt` `commitAfterPrefix`), `src/core/types/compiled_shape.hpp` + `src/core/types/grammar_schema.hpp` + `src/core/types/grammar_schema_json.cpp` (the facet + loader), `src/analysis/syntactic/parser.cpp` (the cut); a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; sibling facet to the FC2 `commitRequiresTypeName` type-name commit guard; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green. |
| `D-CSUBSET-FNPTR-PARAM-SCOPE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c32).** sqlite3.c:1175-1176 (`struct sqlite3_io_methods`) — the PARAMETER NAMES of a function-POINTER declarator collided across sibling declarators AND leaked into the enclosing scope: `int (*xRead)(... int iAmt ...); int (*xWrite)(... int iAmt ...);` → `error[S0002] S_RedeclaredSymbol: got iAmt`. Confirmed forms: fn-ptr struct members / fn-ptr typedefs / fn-ptr params with a shared param name all collided; `typedef int (*A)(int gv); int gv;` collided AND `…; int main(){return gv;}` resolved `gv` to the PARAM (a LEAK). ROOT: param names bind in Pass-1 `pass1Node`'s declarator-mode mint loop into `bindScope=current`; `pass1Node` opened a child scope ONLY for a `semantics.scopes` rule node — `["block","topLevelDecl","structSpec","unionSpec","enumSpec"]`. A plain prototype is a `topLevelDecl` (a scope rule) so its params isolate per-prototype, but a fn-POINTER declarator's params live under `fnSuffix`→`parenDeclarator`/`structField`/`typedefDecl`/`param` — none a scope rule — so they bound into the enclosing struct/file/block scope and collided/leaked. C 6.2.1p4: a param in a NON-definition declarator has function-prototype scope (per-declarator, terminating at the declarator end); a DEFINITION's params reach the body purely via the parent chain (`S_body → S_tld(topLevelDecl)`) with NO param re-binding, so they MUST keep binding into the topLevelDecl scope. | ✅ FIX (Option B — a per-declarator function-prototype scope; config-driven, agnostic). (1) CONFIG: a NEW OPTIONAL declarator role `prototypeParamScopeRule: "paramList"` (beside `fnSuffixParamsRule`; a language OMITTING it = prior behavior — toy/tsql declare no `declarators` block). (2) STRUCT+LOADER: `DeclaratorConfig::prototypeParamScopeRule` (+ name field) loaded via the same `readOptionalRuleRole` discipline + added to the closed-keys set (15→16). (3) SCOPE-OPEN: Pass-1 opens a THROWAWAY scope for any node of that rule UNLESS it is a function DEFINITION's OWN param list — detected by the SHARED config-driven shape tests (`directDeclaratorIsNameBearing` — the suffix sits on a NAME-bearing direct declarator, NOT a fn-pointer whose base is a `parenDeclarator`) AND `declNodeIsFunctionDefinition` (the enclosing decl's kindByChild resolves to Function — a body present). So: a fn-ptr member/typedef/param ⇒ base is a parenDeclarator ⇒ ISOLATE; a bare prototype ⇒ name-bearing but no body ⇒ ISOLATE (its name re-homes to file scope via the separate `isProto` path); a definition ⇒ name-bearing + body ⇒ do NOT open (params bind into the topLevelDecl scope, body-visible). (4) ★ 3-PASS CONSISTENCY: the whole "does this node open a child scope (and which)" decision is FACTORED into ONE shared predicate `nodeOpensChildScope` (folding the c25 `scopeByRule`+`isDefinitionAtNode` gate AND the c32 prototype case) used by ALL THREE passes — `pass1Node` (pushes), `resolveDeclTypes` + the `pass2` driver (re-derive by anchor lookup) — so a param can never bind in one scope yet be looked up in another. The fn-ptr's TYPE (FnSig param types via `collectParamTypes`, which walks the tree not the name symbols) is UNCHANGED — only NAME scoping changes. | (closed — the per-declarator prototype scope + the shared `nodeOpensChildScope` predicate IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c32; cited in `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`declarators.prototypeParamScopeRule`) + `src/core/types/semantic_config.hpp` (`DeclaratorConfig::prototypeParamScopeRule`) + `src/core/types/grammar_schema_json.cpp` (loader) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`nodeOpensChildScope` / `isPrototypeParamScopeNode` / `directDeclaratorIsNameBearing` / `declNodeIsFunctionDefinition`); witness = 9 semantic pins (`FnPtrStructMembersSharedParamNameNoRedecl`, `FnPtrTypedefsSharedParamNameNoRedecl`, `FnPtrParamsSharedParamNameNoRedecl`, `FnPtrParamNameDoesNotLeakToEnclosingScope` (collision-form leak) + `FnPtrParamNameDoesNotResolveOutsideDeclarator` (the DECISIVE resolve-form leak guard — an outer use of a fn-ptr-typedef param name is S_UndeclaredIdentifier; distinguishes a leaked-but-non-colliding param the type-only collision pin cannot), `NestedFnPtrParamsSharedNamesNoRedecl`, `DefinitionParamsRemainBodyVisible`, `PlainPrototypesSharedParamNameClean`, `DuplicateParamNameInSingleFnPtrStillCollides` — 7 red-on-disable, the 2 PRESERVE pins green both ways) + runtime corpus `fnptr_struct_dispatch` (a struct of fn-ptr members with SHARED param names, assigned + called through → exit 42; 4 targets pe/elf/qemu-arm64/macho + release). 453→454 ctest; reuses the c10 `hasFnSuffixOnName` / `isDefinitionAtNode` machinery; a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green. |
| `D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-SIZE-INFERENCE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c34).** sqlite3.c:506 `SQLITE_API const char sqlite3_version[] = SQLITE_VERSION;` (i.e. `const char x[] = "3.x.y";`) — an array declared with an EMPTY bound `[]` AND an initializer was rejected `error[S000B] S_NonConstantArrayLength` (the resolver returned an incomplete array ONLY in a flexible-array-member context; a `[]` VARIABLE with a missing length always erred). C 6.7.9p22: such an array infers its length from the initializer — `char x[] = "abc"` → `char[4]` ("abc"+NUL), `int a[] = {1,2,3}` → `int[3]`. | ✅ FIX (config-driven, REUSE; one completion site so all downstream agree). (1) The array-suffix `[]` (absent length) already yields an INCOMPLETE array when "missing-length-is-OK" (`allowFlexibleArray`); REUSE that path for a VARIABLE by RELAXING the flag at the Pass-1.5 var/global declared-type site (`resolveDeclTypes`, semantic_analyzer.cpp) to `decl.allowFlexibleArray || <this declarator has an initializer>` — so a `[]`-with-init resolves to `T[]` (incomplete) instead of erroring, while a `[]` with NO init keeps the original flag (false for a var/global rule) ⇒ `S_NonConstantArrayLength` still fires (never a silently-sized array). The group recursion into a nested declarator still passes false (a nested fn-ptr param's `[]` errors). (2) A NEW helper `completeIncompleteArrayFromInit` (Pass-1.5, the SINGLE completion site → sizeof/layout/element-access/BOTH HIR paths observe the SAME sized array) completes the incomplete array: a STRING-literal init → N = decoded-bytes + 1 (the NUL), counted via the SHARED `decodeAdjacentStringBodies` chokepoint (escapes count once — `"\x41\x42"` → 2+NUL=3; adjacent concat `"ab" "cd"` → 5); a BRACE init → N = the count of TOP-LEVEL `initElement` children of the brace list. The element type is recovered from the incomplete array's `operands()[0]`, so a malformed escape leaves it incomplete (no guessed size; the HIR tier fails loud on the bad literal). NO HIR/MIR change — the existing explicit-size lowering (`char x[4]="abc"`, `int a[3]={…}`) applies once the type is completed to the same sized array (the HIR var/global path reads the symbol's resolved `.type`). AGNOSTIC: two new by-NAME index rules (`braceInitList`/`initElement`, resolved like `stringLiteralExprRule`) + the existing `stringLiteralExprRule`/body-token; no lang/arch/format/keyword hardcode. PRESERVE-verified: explicit `[N]` unchanged (completion is a no-op on an already-sized array — `char x[8]="abc"` stays `[8]`, not re-derived to 4); over-long explicit `char x[2]="abc"` keeps its current S0003; a bare `[]` no-init stays incomplete/error. ★ AUDIT-CAUGHT REGRESSION (fixed before commit): an EMPTY brace `int a[] = {}` (top-level count 0) returned the incomplete array with NO diagnostic — and the unguarded HIR/MIR tier LOOPED on the -1 sentinel length (a compiler HANG; a fail-loud VIOLATION the relaxation introduced — pre-c34 it was a clean S_NonConstantArrayLength). FIX: `completeIncompleteArrayFromInit`'s un-sizable returns (empty brace; OR no string/brace initializer found at all) now call `failUnsized()` → emit S_ArrayLengthOutOfRange (an inferred 0/undeterminable length = the non-positive `int a[0]` case) + return InvalidType, matching the no-init clean-error path (no hang). WITNESS: 6 semantic pins (`ArraySizeInferredFromStringInit` Array len 4 + NOT incomplete; `ArraySizeInferredFromBraceInit` Array<I32,3>; `ArraySizeInferredFromInitLocal`; `ExplicitArraySizeUnchangedByInference`; `EmptyArrayNoInitNotSized` — no-init S_NonConstantArrayLength; ★ `ArraySizeInferenceEmptyBraceFailsLoud` — empty-brace `{}` → S_ArrayLengthOutOfRange, NOT a hang) + runtime corpus `array_size_inference` (an inferred `char s[]="DSS"`, `int a[]={2,7,11,22}`, and the sqlite frontier shape `const char ver[]="9"` read across a non-inlined call → exit 42; 4 targets pe/elf/qemu-arm64/macho + release arm). 455→456 ctest; anchor guard OK. ★ NOTE (pre-existing, NOT c34): `sizeof(a[0])` mis-resolves to `sizeof(a)` for ANY array (reproduces with explicit `int a[5]`) — independent of inference; size correctness was proven via an out-of-the-explicit-bound read (`int a[]={0,0,0,0,42}; a[4]`→42) + `sizeof(x)`→4 for the string. | (closed — the relaxed-flag REUSE + the single `completeIncompleteArrayFromInit` site IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c34; cited in `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the relaxed `allowIncomplete` at the Pass-1.5 var site + `completeIncompleteArrayFromInit` + the `braceInitList`/`initElement` index rules); a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier (sqlite3_version line 506); reuses the FAM incomplete-array path (`D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MEMBER-DECLARATOR` c10) + the adjacent-string decoder (`D-CSUBSET-ADJACENT-STRING-CONCAT` c20); plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green. |
| `D-CSUBSET-UNARY-PLUS` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-26 (p18 Cluster G c12).** The unary `+` operator (`int x = +1;`, C 6.5.3.3) was rejected (P0009) — only unary `-` was in the prefix op-group; SQLite uses `+` as a no-op sign/grouping marker. | ✅ Grammar = add `"+"` to the prefix operator-group + a `PlusOp → Pos` `unaryOps` row. CST→HIR's `combineUnaryOp` lowers `+x` to its OPERAND DIRECTLY (identity — there is no `Pos`/`Identity` HirOpKind; the integer promotion is already realized by the lazy-consumer model, sub-int values live promoted in 32-bit regs), gated on an ARITHMETIC (or Enum) operand — a pointer/struct operand fails LOUD (`H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind` "operand of unary '+' must have arithmetic type"; STRICTER than `-`, which has no such gate). The semantic typer's `combineUnary` makes `Pos` operand-preserving (explicit, mirroring PreInc). `#if +1` works for free (`pp_if_eval` already special-cased a `Pos` target as identity). GRAMMAR + thin HIR, AGNOSTIC (config target name `Pos`, no hardcoded `+`). NOTE: making `+` a prefix op made `(a) + b` record a type-name candidate (the cast-vs-binary ambiguity) exactly like the pre-existing `(q) - x` — the `ParserCastExpr` pin was updated to match. WITNESS: corpus `unary_plus` (`+1`, `+(x+40)`, `+ +x` → exit 42; pe-native + elf/macho/arm64 + release arm). | p18 Cluster G c12; cited in `examples/c-subset/unary_plus/` + `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (prefix group + `Pos` unaryOps row) + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`combineUnaryOp`). |

| `D-CSUBSET-DIVISION-CORPUS-MATERIALIZE-BUG` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-04** (cycle 10r BUG FIX). Root cause identified + fixed: NOT a materialize-pass bug. The cycle-10q compound encoding packed `[REX.W 0x99, REX.W 0xF7 /7]` as a single opcode's `opcode` array. The x86-variable encoder auto-emits ONE REX prefix at instruction start computed from operand high bits (e.g., 0x41 = REX.B for R14). Per x86 decode the LAST REX before the opcode wins — the embedded second literal 0x48 (REX.W only) superseded the auto-REX, losing REX.B. IDIV operand decoded as RSI (without REX.B) instead of R14 → divide-by-zero trap. Cycle 10r SPLIT the compound into separate `cqo`/`idiv_op`/`xor_rdx_zero`/`div_op` opcodes (each a single instruction with its own auto-REX). | (closed) | plan 12 ML5 (asm encoder) + ML6 (regalloc); paired with D-CSUBSET-DIVISION-OP-CODEGEN closure |
| `D-LIR-REGALLOC-PRESSURED-IMPLICIT-CLOBBER-PIN` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-11** — the pressured DIV pin landed: `LirRegAlloc.PressuredDivCoveringVregsExcludeImplicitInputAndClobberSet` (tests/lir/test_lir_regalloc.cpp) sweeps nLive 10..16 live-across-division c-subset locals through the real pipeline; EVERY vreg range COVERING a declared-implicit-op position (the cqo+idiv pair — agnostic discovery over `implicitRegisters`, no mnemonic list) must avoid (inputs ∪ clobbered); non-vacuity guards = shape-found + ≥1 phys-assigned covering range. RED-on-disable DEMONSTRATED + restored: commenting out the `implicitClobbersCrossedBy` consultation lands covering vregs on rdx(ord 2)/rax(ord 0) at EVERY sweep point. The likely closure: switch `excludedStorage` to `boost::container::small_vector` or a hand-rolled fixed-capacity vector that grows-with-fail-loud. C semantics: counts ≥ width are UB (C23 6.5.7p3) — nothing emitted, hardware masks (x86 &63/&31 incl. imm8; arm64 mod 64/32); negative `>>` defined as sign-fill (SAR/ASRV) on every target. Width axis keys on the RESULT type (C's promoted LEFT operand). CONFORMING for I64/U64/Ptr (native) and for I32 (signed overflow is UB, so 64-wide implements every DEFINED I32 program) but WRONG for types with DEFINED narrower wraparound/comparison semantics: U32 (`unsigned int` — modulo-2³² is C-defined), U16/U8/I16/I8/I128/U128/Char/Byte (+Bool in ALU position). Such values still FLOW freely (load/store/mov/phi/args — only arithmetic semantics break at 64-wide). Trigger fired via D-CSUBSET-INT-SAME-SIGN-NARROW (implicit narrowing made sub-native conversions source-reachable). WITNESSED: `int_narrowing` corpus exit 42 on x86 native + arm64 qemu; LIR red-on-disable pins `SExtFromI16AndI32SourcesBothRealized` / `ZExtFromBoolKeepsDefaultWidthAndNarrowSourcesNowRealized` / `TruncToSixteenAndEightNowRealizedViaPromotedMov`. **RESIDUE STILL OPEN: sub-native ALU COMPUTE forms (8/16/128-bit add/sub etc. — unreachable under C promotion, no consumer) + Bool-ALU; the xor/not walls were closed at F2.** ORIGINAL OPEN row (created 2026-06-11, audit-residue sweep c2, `D-AUDIT-BOOKKEEPING-TRUTH` (d)) — carried the residue of the ✅-closed `D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS` (above) so it greps as open work. Scope, per target, all fail-loud today: (1) sub-native INTEGER widths — 8/16-bit ALU forms (and 128-bit) have no encoded variants; C's UAC keeps them source-UNREACHABLE in expressions (everything promotes to int), so no consumer can exist until packed struct fields / atomics / `_BitInt`-class work makes an 8/16-bit COMPUTE source-reachable; the I8/I16 sext variants (x86 0F BE/BF; arm64 SXTB/SXTH) belong here too (cross-ref `D-TARGET-ENCODING-WIDTH-GUARD` — the guard.width axis makes each a declaration-only addition). (2) Bool-ALU (`(a==b)+(a!=b)` walls at S_ReturnTypeMismatch — the semantic tier types Bool arithmetic by the left operand). (3) The xor/not walls, per the sweep-c1 verification: **x86 xor + x86 not** declared WITHOUT encodings (assembler `A_NoEncodingDeclared`); **arm64 `not` mnemonic UNDECLARED** (lowering `L_RequiredLirOpcodeMissing`); arm64 xor=EOR IS encoded — `^` walls on x86 only, `~` on both at different tiers. | Trigger: packed struct fields / atomics make 8/16-bit compute source-reachable, OR the first `^`/`~` consumer lands in a corpus row. Priority trigger-gated. | `D-CSUBSET-SYMBOL-ADDRESS-GLOBAL` | **✅ CLOSED 2026-06-25 (Cluster F F5 — 8 stage commits; 405/405 ctest Release; PE+ELF runtime exit 143; Mach-O rebase byte-verified + macOS-CI-leg runtime CONFIRMED GREEN — run 28150522650 (headSha 191b756) macos-clang-release ✓).** A global initialized to the LINK-TIME-CONSTANT address of another symbol — `char* g = "...";` (string→rodata), `int* p = &x;` (&global), or a function-pointer table. F5 realizes it as a new mechanism: a `MirSymbolAddrValue` literal-pool variant (classifyGlobals recognizes the `AddressOf(Ref(global/fn))` + `Cast(Literal(string), Ptr)` shapes BEFORE const-eval, routing to the variant instead of `__module_init__`) → the assembler emits a pointer-width slot + an ABSOLUTE-64 relocation (`abs64`, found by FORMULA `widthBytes==8 && !pcRelative`, NOT by name → agnostic) against the target symbol → the linker resolves it to the target's VA, across PE (DIR64 base-reloc) / ELF (ET_EXEC in-place) / Mach-O (in-place patch **+ a dyld REBASE opcode stream** so the PIE slide is applied). **A symbol-address pointer is INHERENTLY load-writable** (the loader writes the resolved/slid address into it), so the asm arm forces it to `.data` on every format regardless of source-const — never read-only rodata (which Mach-O PIE cannot rebase). Two pr-review folds: (1) the whole-program **merge** now remaps the `MirSymbolAddrValue.symbol` embedded id through `mergedSymbolOf` (was copied verbatim → stale abs64 target in any multi-CU build → `K_SymbolUndefined`/silent-wrong-VA); (2) the DCE liveness BFS propagates through a global's symbol-address init (release-arm corpus exposed it dropping the reloc target). Host-independent pin `MachOExecWriter.SymbolAddressDataGlobalEmitsDyldRebaseStream` (red-on-disable: rebase_size→0). The `char buf[] = "...";` char-ARRAY-into-global form is SEPARATE (fail-loud `S000B`, `D-CSUBSET-GLOBAL-CHAR-ARRAY-INIT`). | (closed) | mir_literal_pool.hpp + hir_to_mir.cpp + mir_merge.cpp + opt const_fold/dce + asm.cpp + link/format/{pe,elf,macho}.cpp + exec_data_section.hpp + plan 23 Cluster F |

So a typedef'd struct can be named in expressions but never declared as a variable's type — the declaration grammar is the gap, the binder sketch machinery (FC2) already resolves typedef names. | (closed at the declaration-statement/param/typedef-head scope; field/extern remainder = `D-CSUBSET-TYPEDEF-FIELD-EXTERN-POSITION`) | promoted from `D-CSUBSET-COMPOUND-LITERAL-TYPEDEF` residue (c); plan-23 §0.1 FC4 row; `D-CSUBSET-TYPEDEF-FIELD-EXTERN-POSITION` |
| `D-CSUBSET-FN-PROTOTYPE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-22 (FC-deferral close-out loop cycle 3, branch p14).** A C function PROTOTYPE (`int f(int);`) is now a function DECLARATION that MERGES with a later definition into ONE symbol — forward/mutual recursion works (the registry trigger). **As-built (plan-locked GO-WITH-FIXES, 4 MF folded; delegated build + 3 independent verification passes):** the 3-pass pre-order already pre-binds all symbols (forward CALLS already worked); the merge hooks the Pass-1 bind-collision site with a 4-CASE direction table keyed on proto-vs-def (`SymbolRecord.isProtoDeclaration` set SYNTACTICALLY at Pass 1 via a shared `hasFnSuffixOnName` — a fn-suffix on the name's direct declarator, distinguishing a proto from a fn-pointer): **proto→def** = the DEFINITION wins the binding (`injectBinding`), proto absorbed; **def→proto / proto→proto / extern→proto** = redundant decl, keep prior, absorb the new (its name binds to its OWN record so the HIR skip + compat compare both work — a deliberate, documented deviation); **def→def / extern→def** = the existing `S_RedeclaredSymbol` (two bodies / scope-out). Pass 1.5 UPGRADES a proto's kind Variable→Function (callable) instead of `S_InvalidFunctionDeclarator`. A post-Pass-1.5 sweep compares each merged pair's resolved FnSig TypeId (interner equality = STRUCTURAL: return type + params + count + cc + variadic) → NEW `S_IncompatibleRedeclaration` (0xE022) on mismatch. The HIR lowerer emits NO node for an `isProtoDeclaration || isAbsorbedProto` declarator (no spurious FnSig-typed Global — the pre-change silent path). **Fail-loud (all verified):** incompatible sig → S0022; a proto declared-but-called-but-never-defined → loud `H0009` unbound Ref at HIR→MIR (NOT a silent FnSig-global read — the make-or-break the plan-lock caught); two real defs / extern+def → S0002. AGNOSTIC (merge reads DeclarationKind/effectiveKind/directRule/fnSuffixRule from config, no identity branch). **★ Found+fixed a LATENT pre-existing issue:** 10 MIR/LIR variadic tests declared the callee as a BARE prototype `int sink(int n, ...);` and leaned on the now-removed spurious-FnSig-global behavior (the call resolved through a data global despite an unchecked S_InvalidFunctionDeclarator) — corrected to DEFINE the callee (assertions unchanged). **Witnesses:** corpus `fn_prototype` (mutual recursion `is_even`/`is_odd` via forward proto, value-divergent → exit 42) on x86_64-PE (ctest) + x86_64-ELF (WSL) + arm64-ELF (qemu) [macho macos leg]; `fn_prototype_incompat_error` (expectDiagnostics S_IncompatibleRedeclaration 6:5); 5 semantic unit pins (proto→def merge / idempotence / def→proto / incompatible-loud / forward-call) DEMONSTRATED red-on-disable (merge off → 3 tests + corpus fail). 364/364 ctest; independent plan-lock + pr-review (empirical, runtime-witnessed the use-resolution ordering) + my own diff/gate/CLI review — all CLEAN. **RESIDUALS (fail-loud, named exclusions — NOT silent):** (1) `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-PROTOTYPE` (below) — a block-scope proto (C 6.7.6.3, `int g(int);` inside a function) SHADOWS the file-scope function rather than referring to it, so a block-proto + file-def fails loud (H0009) instead of resolving to the file def; (2) `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-DEFINITION-MERGE` (below) — `extern int f(int); ...def` stays S_RedeclaredSymbol (the extern→ExternFunction machine differs; bare-proto+def is the C-idiomatic forward decl and the deliverable). — ORIGINAL DEFERRAL (created 2026-06-11, FC4 c1 stage 2a): the FC4 declarator grammar makes a C function PROTOTYPE (`int f(int);` — declaration-without-definition, at file or block scope) PARSE for the first time (head + declarator-with-fnSuffix + `;`), but declaration-merging (a later definition completing the prototype) and prototype-only emission are NOT implemented — `extern` declarations carry the cross-unit-declaration role today. The semantic tier fails LOUD: a Variable-kind declarator whose folded type is a bare `FnSig` emits `S_InvalidFunctionDeclarator` ("function prototype declarations are not supported here; use 'extern'") rather than silently minting an FnSig-typed data global (which would miscompile at MIR). Fn-POINTER objects (`int (*fp)(int)`) are unaffected (type = Ptr<FnSig>, fully supported). | (closed) | (closed) | FC4 stage 2b registry sweep; `semantic_analyzer.cpp` Pass-1 4-case merge + Pass-1.5 proto upgrade + compat sweep; `cst_to_hir.cpp` proto-node skip; `S_IncompatibleRedeclaration` 0xE022; plan 23 FC4 |
| `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-PROTOTYPE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (robustness sweep, user-authorized — the user's authorization fired the "robustness sweep" trigger; branch feature/0-0-2-p14, 393/393 ctest MSVC Release + MinGW).** As-built: a block-scope function prototype now RE-HOMES onto the file scope (new `fileScopeOf()` helper in `semantic_analyzer.cpp`; Pass-1 `if(isProto) bindScope=fileScopeOf(...)`) so the existing FC fn-prototype 4-case merge (`D-CSUBSET-FN-PROTOTYPE` ✅) resolves it against the file definition (C 6.2.2p4 — a no-linkage block decl of a function refers to the external); `+ warnIfUnused = decl.warnIfUnused && !isProto` (a re-homed proto is never an unused-variable). Corpus `block_scope_prototype` (forward mutual call → exit 42) + 4 red-on-disable pins. An undefined re-homed proto still fails loud H0009; an incompatible signature → S_IncompatibleRedeclaration. — *Original OPEN row (audit trail):* **OPEN — normal (created 2026-06-22, FC-deferral close-out cycle 3 self-audit, branch p14).** A block-scope function prototype (C 6.7.6.3 — `int g(int);` inside a function body) is treated as a DISTINCT symbol that SHADOWS the file-scope function rather than referring to it (same external linkage). Consequence: `int main(void){ int g(int); return g(20); } int g(int n){...}` FAILS LOUD — the call resolves to the bodyless block-scope proto (upgraded to Function kind, callable, but no body in its scope) → `H0009` HIR Ref to unbound symbol at HIR→MIR. NOT a silent miscompile; a fail-loud limitation with a poor (internal-shaped) diagnostic. The C-idiomatic + common forward declaration is FILE-scope (`D-CSUBSET-FN-PROTOTYPE` ✅, the deliverable + trigger); a block-scope prototype is the rarer C 6.7.6.3 form. | Trigger: a user/corpus need for block-scope prototypes, or the robustness sweep. | **Closing work:** a block-scope function-typed proto must resolve to (or merge with) the file-scope function of the same name with external linkage (C 6.2.2p4 — no-linkage block decl of a fn refers to the external) rather than minting a distinct shadowing symbol; OR, at minimum, a precise diagnostic ("block-scope function prototypes are not supported; declare at file scope") instead of the internal H0009. | `D-CSUBSET-FN-PROTOTYPE` (✅, the file-scope merge); `semantic_analyzer.cpp` Pass-1 bind scope (block vs file) |
| `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-DEFINITION-MERGE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (robustness sweep, user-authorized — the user's authorization fired the "robustness sweep" trigger; branch feature/0-0-2-p14, 393/393 ctest MSVC Release + MinGW).** As-built: a new config flag `nonDefiningDeclaration` (on `DeclarationRule`, set on c-subset's `externDecl`) + `SymbolRecord.isExternDeclaration`; the proto-merge was GENERALIZED into a shared `mergeOrCollideRedeclaration()` keyed on non-defining (proto OR extern) + a function/object category guard, called from BOTH Pass-1 mint paths (the declarator-mode path AND the legacy positional path); HIR `lowerExternDecl` suppresses the absorbed extern node. `extern int g; int g=5;` (objects AND functions, both orders, idempotent) now MERGES → the definition wins. Corpus `extern_definition_merge` (+ `extern_definition_merge_incompat_error` negative) + 8 pins, red-on-disable. An incompatible extern+def → S_IncompatibleRedeclaration; two real/tentative (non-extern) defs still collide S_RedeclaredSymbol (→ the new backlog row `D-CSUBSET-TENTATIVE-DEFINITION-MERGE`). — *Original OPEN row (audit trail):* **OPEN — normal (created 2026-06-22, FC-deferral close-out cycle 3, branch p14).** `extern int f(int);` followed by a same-TU definition `int f(int x){…}` collides `S_RedeclaredSymbol` (S0002) — the prototype/definition merge (`D-CSUBSET-FN-PROTOTYPE` ✅) does NOT extend to an explicit `extern` declaration, because an `extern` routes through a DIFFERENT machine (the `externDecl` rule → `ExternFunction` HIR → `collectExterns` import row, which requires FFI metadata) than a bare proto (Variable-kind → the new merge path). The C-idiomatic forward declaration is the BARE prototype `int f(int);` (which merges); `extern` on a function is redundant + rarer. Fail-loud (S0002), NOT silent. | Trigger: a user/corpus need for extern-decl + same-TU-definition merge. | **Closing work:** when an in-TU definition follows an `extern` function declaration of the same name, the definition WINS and the extern's `collectExterns` import row is suppressed (absorbed), mirroring the bare-proto merge but across the ExternFunction↔Function boundary. | `D-CSUBSET-FN-PROTOTYPE` (✅, bare-proto merge); `cst_to_hir.cpp` lowerExternDecl / `hir_to_mir.cpp` collectExterns |
| `D-CSUBSET-TENTATIVE-DEFINITION-MERGE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c33).** sqlite3.c:16250 then 24217 — `SQLITE_PRIVATE u32 sqlite3WhereTrace;` (a file-scope TENTATIVE definition, no initializer) then `SQLITE_PRIVATE u32 sqlite3WhereTrace = 0;` (the real definition) collided `error[S0002] S_RedeclaredSymbol` (also `sqlite3_temp_directory`/`sqlite3_data_directory`). C 6.9.2: a file-scope object declared with NO initializer and NO `extern` is a TENTATIVE definition — any number of tentatives + at most ONE real (initialized) definition of the same name MERGE into ONE object. ROOT: only an `extern`/proto NON-DEFINING declaration was treated as mergeable (`D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-DEFINITION-MERGE` ✅, via `nonDefiningDeclaration` + `mergeOrCollideRedeclaration`); a bare tentative was minted as a full DEFINITION, so the merge's `bothDefinitions` arm fired → S0002. ✅ FIX (the non-defining axis EXTENDED, AGNOSTIC — config roles only, no lang/arch/format/keyword hardcode): the Pass-1 variable bind computes `isTentativeDefinition` = (effectiveKind Variable) ∧ ¬isProto ∧ ¬`nonDefiningDeclaration` ∧ a declarator-LIST row (`declaratorListChild` — EXCLUDES single-`declaratorChild` PARAMETER rows, so a prototype param sharing a name never merges) ∧ bindScope == `fileScopeOf(current)` (FILE-scope only — a block-scope `int x; int x;` and a struct/union MEMBER dup, bound in a body scope, STAY S0002) ∧ the init-declarator carries NO initializer (a NEW structural `declaratorHasInitializer` helper — the init-declarator's lone non-declarator visible-Internal child, mirroring the HIR global-init lowering so the two can't drift). It folds into the merge's non-defining flag (`newNonDef = isProto || isExtern || isTentativeDefinition`) AND is RECORDED on a NEW `SymbolRecord.isTentativeDefinition` (so a LATER redeclaration sees THIS symbol as `priorNonDef` and merges). The 4-case `mergeOrCollideRedeclaration` table then handles every shape WITHOUT a new state: tentative→def = the def wins the binding + its init; tentative↔tentative / def→tentative = keep the survivor (one surviving object); def→def (BOTH initialized) = S0002 STAYS. A TYPE CONFLICT (`int g; int* g = 0;`) fails loud `S_IncompatibleRedeclaration` via the EXISTING post-1.5 merged-decl type-compat sweep (interner-TypeId identity → target-aware: `int g; long g = 5;` MERGES under LLP64 where `long`≡`int` but conflicts under LP64/ELF where `long` is 64-bit — VERIFIED both targets). NO HIR/MIR change — the surviving (non-absorbed) symbol lowers to ONE global with the right init via the existing `isAbsorbedProto` skip + the `.bss` tentative-zero-init path. ★ Two existing tests UPDATED to match conformant C (real GCC accepts both): `test_declarator_engine` `SameNameTwiceInOneListRedeclares`→`…TentativeDefinitionsMerge` (`base x, x;` — two same-list tentatives — now MERGES, was asserted-collide); the FFI `DuplicateExternRedeclarationRejectedByFrontend` STAYS green (the param-exclusion preserves its prototype-param collision). WITNESS: 7 semantic pins (tentative+def / two-tentatives / static-tentative+def MERGE; ★ PRESERVE two-real-defs collide + block-scope dup collide + type-conflict fails-loud; extern+def still merges) + runtime corpus `tentative_definition` (tentative+def + def+tentative + two-tentatives, each read across a NON-INLINED call → exit 42; 4 targets pe/elf/qemu-arm64/macho + release). 454→455 ctest. — *Original OPEN row (audit trail):* **OPEN — normal (created 2026-06-24, robustness sweep, branch feature/0-0-2-p14).** Two NON-extern OBJECT declarations of the same name at file scope — `int g; int g;` or `int g; int g = 5;` (C 6.9.2 TENTATIVE definitions: a file-scope object declaration with no initializer and no `extern` is a tentative definition that coalesces with a later real or tentative definition into a single object) — still collide LOUD `S_RedeclaredSymbol`. Only an `extern` NON-DEFINING declaration merges with a definition (`D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-DEFINITION-MERGE` ✅, via the `nonDefiningDeclaration` flag); a bare tentative definition is NOT marked non-defining, so the shared `mergeOrCollideRedeclaration()` collide-arm fires. Rare, FAIL-LOUD (S0002), NOT a silent miscompile. | (closed) | (closed) | p19 Cluster G c33; cited in `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`isTentativeDefinition` Pass-1 fold + `declaratorHasInitializer` + `mergeOrCollideRedeclaration` `priorNonDef`) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_model.hpp` (`SymbolRecord.isTentativeDefinition`) + `examples/c-subset/tentative_definition/`; GENERALIZES `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-DEFINITION-MERGE` (the non-defining/extern axis) + `D-CSUBSET-FN-PROTOTYPE` (the 4-case merge table); a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green. |
| `D-CSUBSET-FN-TYPEDEF-PROTOTYPE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-21 (TF-C34c — the FINAL sqlite testfixture cycle, 43→44/44 = ALL sqlite units green).** A declaration `[static] T x;` where T is a FUNCTION-TYPE typedef (`typedef int Fn(int); static Fn foo;`) declares `foo` a function PROTOTYPE (C 6.7 / 6.9.1p2 — a declaration whose declared type is a function type declares a function), MERGING with a later definition exactly like a syntactic `int foo(int);` proto. This is SQLite `test_thread.c`'s `static Tcl_ObjCmdProc sqlthread_proc;` shape (a function-TYPE typedef `typedef int (Tcl_ObjCmdProc)(…)`, NOT a pointer). Pre-fix DSS minted the bare declarator an OBJECT: Pass 1.5 emitted `S_InvalidFunctionDeclarator` (S0018 "function prototype declarations are not supported here") and, because the Variable-category clashed with the later Function definition, Pass 1 emitted `S_RedeclaredSymbol` (S0002). ROOT: the function-ness of `T x;` is only known AFTER Pass 1.5 resolves the typedef's type to a FnSig — and a SHIPPED-descriptor typedef (`Tcl_ObjCmdProc`) is not even injected into scope until after Pass 1 — but the redeclaration category (proto-vs-object) was decided in Pass 1 (SYNTACTICALLY, `hasFnSuffixOnName` — a `()` on the name). ✅ FIX (AGNOSTIC — general C 6.7, no lang/arch/format identity branch; reads declarator/head grammar ROLES from config): (1) **Pass 1** flags a bare UNDECORATED-name object declaration whose head is a type-NAME identifier (a potential typedef) as a CANDIDATE (`SymbolRecord.maybeFnTypedefProto`) — gated to object-declaration rows (a declarator LIST, no bit-field suffix) so a struct FIELD / PARAMETER is never a candidate; a `*`/`[]`/`()`-DECORATED declarator (`Fn *fp;` — a function POINTER OBJECT, type Ptr<FnSig>) is EXCLUDED via a new structural `declaratorIsUndecoratedName` (no pointer layer, no suffix, no group). (2) **`mergeOrCollideRedeclaration`** routes a candidate meeting a GENUINE same-name function through the SAME proto/def MERGE path (the definition wins the binding, the candidate absorbed) — but ONLY genuine-function↔candidate, NEVER candidate↔candidate (two typedef OBJECTS keep the ordinary tentative-merge path; `category()` is left unchanged). (3) **Pass 1.5** upgrades a FnSig-typed candidate (or a syntactic proto) Variable→Function + marks it a proto (`isFnSig` = a bare function SIGNATURE, never a Ptr<FnSig> fn-pointer — so this is unambiguously a function declaration); a FnSig-typed Variable that is NEITHER (a function-typed struct FIELD) STILL fails loud S0018. (4) **Post-1.5** the merged-decl sweep VERIFIES: a candidate absorbed into a genuine function whose resolved type is NOT a FnSig (`MyInt foo; int foo(){}`) is a real object-vs-function clash → the DEFERRED `S_RedeclaredSymbol` fires (gated on the survivor being a real Function, so two legal tentative typedef-OBJECTS `typedef int Fn; Fn foo; Fn foo;` are untouched). FAIL-LOUD preserved on every malformed shape (object-vs-fn S0002, fn-typed field S0018, fnptr-object collision S0002); a genuine signature mismatch still → `S_IncompatibleRedeclaration`. WITNESSES: runnable `examples/c-subset/fn_typedef_prototype` (bare `static Fn apply; static Fn twice;` protos forward-called then defined → exit 42; ×4 targets pe/elf/qemu-arm64/macho + release) RED-ON-DISABLE (revert the candidate/upgrade → S0018+S0002, no compile); 5 semantic pins (typedef-proto+def merge / forward-call / ★ object-vs-fn fails-loud S0002 / ★ fn-typed field fails-loud S0018 / ★ fnptr-object is a collision). Independent pr-review + dss-audit both VERIFIED-CLEAN (audit also swept shadowing/read-before-assign/init-refs-sibling/uncalled-fn/const/array/struct/address-taken — all work or fail-loud; ZERO silent miscompile). 362/362 ctest. **RESIDUALS (all fail-loud, pre-existing, NOT this anchor's scope):** (1) a precise C 6.7.9p4 "static initializer must be constant" diagnostic — today the generic D-LK4 asm-tier error fires (loud but cryptic) — POLISH, tied to the `D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER-RUNTIME-INIT` work; (2) a static (or file-scope) pointer/string init the global classify path can't const-fold to static data (`static int *p=0;` ≡ file-scope `int *gp=0;` — both fail loud identically, pre-existing global-emission limitation under D-LK4). — ORIGINAL DEFERRAL (created 2026-06-11, FC4 c1): Block-scope `static` (`static int counter = 0;` inside a function — C static-storage-duration locals) is a PARSE error today: `localDeclSpecifier` deliberately admits only `register`/`auto`/attributes (its grammar `$comment` pins the exclusion), and `static` at file scope is the LINKAGE specifier (`declSpecifiers`/Local binding), a different role. | `D-CSUBSET-INT-CROSS-SIGNEDNESS-CONVERT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-22 (FC-deferral close-out loop cycle 1, branch p14).** New `intCrossSignednessConverts` gate (`semantic_config.hpp`, c-subset ON; default FALSE keeps non-C schemas strict) + an `isAssignable` cross-signedness arm (`type_rules.hpp` — admit when one side is a signed-int rank and the other unsigned-int rank, BOTH directions, ALL widths incl. cross-signedness NARROWING U64→I32; RANK-keyed, ZERO target/lang identity) threaded to the 4 callers (`semantic_analyzer.cpp` 2916/2964 init, 3285 call-arg, 4112 return) + HIR `coerce()`'s arithmetic-core arm materializing the width-exact Cast (int↔unsigned both `isArithmeticCore` → reinterpret/truncate; runtime-proven). **SCOPE GUARD:** signed↔unsigned ONLY — SAME-signedness narrowing stays strict, int↔float stays rejected. **Witnesses:** corpus `cross_signedness_convert` exit 42 (init/call-arg/return cross-sign + high-bit 0xFFFFFFFF→-1 + U64→I32 narrow→42, mutable-global anti-fold, baseline + full-release-like + shipped-release) on x86_64-PE (ctest) + x86_64-ELF (native WSL) + arm64-ELF (qemu) [arm64-Mach-O macos-latest leg]; unit `IsAssignableAdmitsCrossSignednessWhenGated` (gate-ON admits — RED-on-disable: revert the arm → 4 EXPECT_TRUE flip) + the unchanged `IsAssignableRejectsCrossSignedness` (gate-OFF rejects — conservative-default guard); the fc3 `expectLiteralTypes` helper was REDESIGNED to assert the literal's EXACT stamped type via `typeAt` (the old cross-sign-rejection proxy became invalid once the gate admits it — the direct assertion is STRICTLY STRONGER). The 4 GATED sites are init(×2)/call-arg/return; this surfaced a SEPARATE pre-existing strictness asymmetry → new `D-SEMANTIC-ASSIGN-STMT-ASSIGNABILITY-BYPASS` (below). — ORIGINAL DEFERRAL (created 2026-06-18, FC8 cycle-5 investigation, branch p10): A cross-signedness implicit conversion in an ASSIGNMENT CONTEXT — `int x = u;`, `x = u;`, `return i+u;` (int-returning fn), `f(u)` (int param) where one side is signed and the other unsigned — is REJECTED `S_TypeMismatch` (S0003); a few return forms (unsigned literal, int→unsigned) surface `S_ReturnTypeMismatch` (S0008). **This is INTENTIONAL, UNIFORM, and already DOCUMENTED:** `isAssignable` (`type_rules.hpp` lines 83-86) deliberately makes cross-signedness (and int↔float) NOT assignable — *"languages requiring them widen their config rather than getting silent C-style implicit conversions."* It is NOT a return-specific bug: init / assignment / call-arg / return ALL reject identically through the one `isAssignable` rule (the originating report's "return inconsistent with assignment" premise was DISPROVEN — `int x = u;` rejects too, same S0003). **But** C 6.3.1.3 / 6.5.16.1 MANDATE an implicit signed↔unsigned assignment conversion (value-preserving in-range, modular out-of-range), so for the full-C arc + SQLite (which mixes `int`/`unsigned`/`size_t` freely) c-subset MUST admit it — via the SAME config-gated opt-in as Bool/Char/Enum, default-strict for non-C schemas. | (closed) | **Closing work (mirrors `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-INT-CONVERSION` / `D-CSUBSET-CHAR-INT-WIDENING` EXACTLY):** a new `intCrossSignednessConverts` gate in `semantic_config.hpp` (c-subset ON; default false) + an `isAssignable` cross-signedness arm (admit signed↔unsigned within the int ranks, both widening AND narrowing per C) threaded to the 4 semantic callers (`semantic_analyzer.cpp` 2792/2840 init, 3161 call-arg, 3988 return) + HIR `coerce()` materializing the int↔unsigned Cast (a width-exact reinterpret/mov — no value change at equal width) + strict red-on-disable tests + a runtime corpus (`return i+u` exit-code witness). | plan 23 FC; `type_rules.hpp` isAssignable (lines 83-86 document the current deliberate rejection); `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-INT-CONVERSION` + `D-CSUBSET-CHAR-INT-WIDENING` (exact templates); `semantic_config.hpp` conversion gates |
| `D-SEMANTIC-ASSIGN-STMT-ASSIGNABILITY-BYPASS` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (robustness sweep, user-authorized — the user's authorization fired the "robustness sweep" trigger; branch feature/0-0-2-p14, 393/393 ctest MSVC Release + MinGW).** As-built: the assign-STATEMENT arm (`assignByRule`) now routes through the SAME gated `isAssignable()` chokepoint the 4 init/call/return sites use (same conversion gates + null-pointer admission, S_TypeMismatch positioned at the RHS), gated to PLAIN assign (`target=="Assign" && compoundBase.empty()`, so compound `+=` is EXCLUDED as the arithmetic-conversion path). So `x = f;` now rejects like `int x = f;`, and cross-sign at an assignment statement is consistently GATED. Corpus `assign_stmt_assignability_error` + 4 pins, red-on-disable; blast-radius probe = zero over-rejection. **REGRESSION FIX 2026-06-24 (this cycle, branch feature/0-0-2-p14, 394/394 MSVC Release + MinGW + Debug):** routing the assign-stmt RHS through `isAssignable` exposed a PRE-EXISTING GAP in that chokepoint — it had NO function-to-pointer decay arm, so a bare function NAME assigned / initialized / passed to a matching function pointer (`fp = add` — the most common fn-ptr idiom: vtables / callbacks / dispatch tables) was wrongly rejected S0003, while `fp = &add` (already a pointer) passed (the asymmetry that isolated it; dss-state 90→88, `fn_fnptr_call` + `fn_fnptr_deref_call` regressed). FIXED by adding the C 6.3.2.1p4 function-to-pointer decay arm to the SHARED `isAssignable` chokepoint (`type_rules.hpp`, mirroring the array-to-pointer decay; ungated structural-decay family per `D-LANG-STRUCTURAL-DECAY-OPT-OUT`; pinned to the SAME interned FnSig so an incompatible-signature assignment stays a loud mismatch) — one arm clears assignment + initializer + call-argument at once (also clears `fn_fnptr_callback`, the related call-arg gap), with NO HIR `coerce()` change (a bare function Ref already lowers to its ADDRESS at MIR time, which is why `fp = add` compiled AND ran pre-sweep — only the new SEMANTIC check rejected it). Corpus `fnptr_bare_decay` (exit 42; bare assign + init + callback; fold-resistant self-recursive `apply`; baseline + full-release-like + shipped-release) + semantic pin `BareFunctionNameDecaysToPointerInEveryPosition` (3 positions + the incompatible-signature negative), BOTH red-on-disable DEMONSTRATED (arm disabled → the pin fails on all 3 positions AND the example fails to build). Closes the bare-decay coverage gap the suite lacked (the whole prior fnptr corpus used `&fn`). — *Original OPEN row (audit trail):* **OPEN — normal (created 2026-06-22, FC-deferral close-out cycle 1 self-audit, branch p14).** The bare assignment STATEMENT (`x = e;`) runs NO semantic assignability check — `subtreeType(Assign)` returns the LHS type without comparing operands, and the pass-2 assign arm checks only `S_ConstViolation`. Type compatibility is enforced only DOWNSTREAM: HIR `coerce()` materializes a width-exact Cast for any arith-core↔arith-core pair, and the HIR verifier catches truly-incompatible (non-arith) shapes. CONSEQUENCE — a strictness ASYMMETRY between the assignment statement and the `isAssignable`-checked init/call-arg/return sites: `int x = f;` (init, int←float) REJECTS S0003, but `x = f;` (assignment stmt) is SILENTLY ACCEPTED (coerce truncates float→int — a correct C VALUE, but the c-subset "no silent C-style implicit conversion" strictness is bypassed); likewise a non-C schema with `intCrossSignednessConverts` OFF rejects cross-sign at init/call/return yet accepts it at an assignment statement. NOT a miscompile (coerce's conversions are correct; non-arith mismatches still fail loud via the verifier as `H_VerifierFailure`) — a DIAGNOSTIC-QUALITY + consistency gap. PRE-EXISTING (independent of the cross-sign gate); empirically confirmed via CLI (`int x=f;`→S0003 exit 1; `x=f;`→exit 0). | Trigger: the robustness sweep / first user-visible inconsistency (assignment-stmt vs init for the same incompatible pair). | **Closing work:** route the assignment-statement RHS through the SAME gated `isAssignable` check as the 4 init/call/return sites (emit `S_TypeMismatch` on failure), so `x = f;` rejects like `int x = f;` AND cross-sign at assignment-stmt becomes consistently GATED (not gate-independent coerce); blast-radius check for existing assignment-stmt tests (int↔float forms currently passing to the verifier); a red-on-disable pin per direction. | `semantic_analyzer.cpp` assign pass-2 arm (~2992-3042) + `subtreeType` Assign (~3843); `type_rules.hpp` isAssignable; `cst_to_hir.cpp` coerce |
| `D-PARSE-SCHEMA-NESTED-NULLABLE-FOLLOW` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-17 (struct-head cycle — the schema-compiler root fix for `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION`).** AltChoice `expectedSet` was computed EAGERLY at build time, but `repeat` uses a tie-the-knot: the loop-entry slot is reserved as a placeholder `End` (EMPTY expectedSet), the loop BODY is built against it, THEN it is overwritten with the real AltChoice — so an `optional`/`alt` inside the body whose fall-through `cont` is that loop entry captured the placeholder's EMPTY set, permanently missing the loop's exit FOLLOW. Concretely the enum `enumerator (Comma enumerator?)*` before `}`: the trailing `{optional enumerator}` never learned `}` could follow → under a SPECULATIVE probe (`topLevelCompositeSpec`) the body parse hit `P_NoAlternativeMatched` (nullableTail=false, `}` not in the optional's expectedSet) → rolled back to the ref form → `{…}` mis-parsed. FIX: new monotone fixed-point pass `recomputeAltExpectedSets` re-unions every AltChoice position's `expectedSet = ∪ branches[i].expectedSet` until stable, sequenced AFTER `detectAmbiguousAlternatives` + BEFORE `computeFollowSets` (+ `Position::setExpectedSet`). Blast radius = EVERY grammar's expectedSets, but it only WIDENS to the correct union — 318/318 across toy/tsql/c-subset. RED-on-disable pinned by `HirLoweringCSubset.D5_5_EnumTrailingCommaParses` (top-level `enum E {…,};` — VERIFIED red when the orchestration call is toggled off, restored). **SCOPE:** fixes the TOP-LEVEL speculative surface (the NAMED `topLevelCompositeSpec`); the typedef-position surface (INLINE alts in `typedefHead`) stays a SEPARATE pre-existing gap → `D-CSUBSET-TYPEDEF-ENUM-TRAILING-COMMA`. | (closed) | `grammar_schema_json.cpp recomputeAltExpectedSets` + `compiled_shape.hpp setExpectedSet`; parent `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION` |
