EMPTY scalars when no bitfield → every pre-bitfield composite interns bit-identically) — the width is part of type identity, so `computeLayout` (TypeId-only) packs without a semantic-model dep. **Layout:** `BitFieldStrategy` (`.target.json` `aggregateLayout.bitFieldStrategy`, default None=fail-loud; `gnu_packed` on both targets) + a GNU/SysV little-endian LSB-first packing algorithm in `type_layout.cpp` (a field that would straddle its type's unit boundary starts the next aligned unit; zero-width forces a unit break; `StructLayout.bitFields[]` carries per-field {unitBytes,bitOffset,bitWidth}). **Codegen:** `hir_to_mir.cpp` member-access READ = Load unit → shift+mask (signed: sign-extend via Shl+AShr); WRITE = read-modify-write (Load → clear → OR shifted value → Store) — reuses existing MIR Shl/LShr/AShr/And/Or, NO new substrate. **Fail-loud:** non-integer base (`S_BitFieldNonIntegerType`), width negative/>type-bits/zero-on-named (`S_BitFieldWidthOutOfRange`), anonymous-non-bitfield `int;` (`S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing`). **Witnesses:** corpus `bitfields` (round-trip + packing + cross-unit + signed sign-extension + neighbour-preservation + overflow-truncation) x86_64-PE (ctest) + x86_64-ELF (native WSL) + arm64-ELF (qemu) [arm64-Mach-O runtime witness CONFIRMED on the macos-latest leg — green continuously since 2026-06-18, latest run 28150522650]; layout bit-offset pins + semantic fail-loud pins + a host-independent LIR structural pin, all red-on-disable. **Scope (all fail-loud, never silent):** the ABI-EXACT per-platform layout (MS straddling, Apple specifics) is trigger-gated → `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT` (the §B decision, user-approved 2026-06-18); enum-typed bit-fields → `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-BITFIELD`. **REVIEW-CAUGHT (independent review) & folded to fail-loud the same cycle:** (a) aggregate INITIALIZATION of a bit-field struct (`struct S f={1,2};`/designated/global) would write each field full-width → clobber co-resident bit-fields → now rejected at MIR + the asm static-data backstop → `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-INIT` (field-by-field assignment is the supported path); (b) a >32-bit-base (`long long`) bit-field needs 64-bit unit codegen (incomplete `mov`/ALU encodings) → rejected at semantic for a clean diagnostic (the `constInt`→`constIntOfType` fix already made it fail-loud not silent) → `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-WIDE-UNIT` (✅ CLOSED 2026-06-18 — the real gap was wide-const materialization, not 64-bit ALU). So the supported set is 32-bit-and-narrower integer bit-fields, ACCESSED (read/write/compound-assign/`++`), initialized field-by-field. | (closed) | plan 23 FC8; `type_lattice.cpp` structType/unionType; `type_layout.cpp`; `hir_to_mir.cpp`; `semantic_analyzer.cpp`; `.target.json` aggregateLayout |
| `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-18 (FC8 deferral 3/3, branch p10; user-authorized §B — build now + all 3 ABIs, despite the FFI trigger not naturally firing: a SUPERVISED build, with a structural conformance witness standing in for the absent runtime/FFI consumer — the user was informed it is consumer-less until FFI and chose to build).** Bit-field layout is now BYTE-ABI-EXACT per platform, config-selected at the FORMAT/ABI level, verified against the native compilers. **As-built:** new `BitFieldStrategy::MsvcStraddle` (`aggregate_layout.hpp` + name converters); `type_layout.cpp` Struct/Union bit-field arms `switch` on the strategy enum (GnuPacked = LSB-first declared-type-unit packing; MsvcStraddle = the MS rule: a unit is reused ONLY when the next bit-field's declared-type SIZE matches AND fits — any type-size change / intervening ordinary field / zero-width / straddle opens a fresh type-aligned unit at the high-water mark, and struct size covers the last unit's full declared-type width; None/unrealized = fail-loud) — NO target/format identity, switch on the config enum only. **Config moved to the FORMAT/ABI level** (the rule is format-determined: x86_64 serves both ELF-SysV and PE-MS): `bitFieldStrategy` added to the object-format schema (`object_format_schema.{hpp,cpp}` + loader, fail-loud on unknown spelling), resolved by `effectiveBitFieldStrategy(target, format)` (format-wins, target-fallback for back-compat), overlaid at the 3 layout consumers (semantic sizeof, HIR→MIR, asm globals) so a single program packs consistently write/read/ABI; 9 `.format.json` declare it (PE×2 `msvc_straddle`; ELF×4 + Mach-O×3 `gnu_packed`; wasm/spirv omit — ILP32 declared-only). **Per-ABI findings:** MS-x64 = `msvc_straddle` (goldens DERIVED from `cl.exe 14.51`: `{int a:1;char b:1;}`/`{char a:7;int b:25;}`/`{char a:1;int b:1;}` sizeof 8 [new unit on type-size change] vs SysV 4; `{char a:1;int b:1;char c:1;}`=12 [never reopens a closed unit]; same-type fields agree); Linux-arm64 (AAPCS64) = `gnu_packed` (identical to SysV for LE bit-fields); **Apple-arm64 = `gnu_packed`** (Apple's documented AAPCS64 divergences — char signedness, long double, va_list, red zone, stack args, empty structs — do NOT include bit-field packing; the macos-latest clang leg is the runtime witness). **Conformance witness (hybrid):** hermetic goldens in `test_type_layout.cpp` (computeLayout per strategy pinned to cl.exe/gcc-derived sizeof + bit-offsets) + an asm byte-exact MS pin (`{char a:7;int b:25;}`→`7F 00 00 00 FF FF FF 01`) + a NEW `test_bitfield_abi_conformance.cpp` that SHELLS OUT to the host native compiler (cl.exe/gcc/clang), reads its real sizeof + bit positions, and asserts dss's computeLayout (host-ABI strategy) is byte-identical (GTEST_SKIP when no toolchain) — ran locally via cl.exe (msvc_straddle == cl.exe byte-for-byte) and is the ONLY Apple witness (macOS clang leg). The existing bit-field corpora stay GREEN on the PE leg under `msvc_straddle` (layout-rule-agnostic value-computers → exit 42 regardless). Red-on-disable DEMONSTRATED (revert MsvcStraddle→GnuPacked → goldens + conformance witness + asm MS pin all RED; same-type control + union stay green; restored). Independent scoping + implementation + adversarial review (cl.exe byte-compare of the full battery + ~12 adversarial extensions + tamper-revert + all-consumer-sites consistency trace — SHIP) + own gate (ctest 326/326, conformance ran via cl.exe, corpora green on PE, anchor guard OK). **326/326 ctest.** Optional future test-depth ratchets (NON-blocking, on verified-correct code): a unit pin for the format-loader unknown-spelling fail-loud (empirically verified, matches the pre-existing target-loader untested pattern) + 64-bit-type/FAM rows in the committed conformance battery (dss verified-matching cl.exe). | (closed) | `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD` (parent); `aggregate_layout.hpp` BitFieldStrategy; `object_format_schema.{hpp,cpp}` (format-level bitFieldStrategy); `type_layout.cpp` strategy switch; `compile_pipeline.cpp` effectiveBitFieldStrategy; `test_type_layout.cpp` + `test_bitfield_abi_conformance.cpp` |
| `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-INIT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-18 (FC8 cycle 4 "bit-field aggregate init", branch p10).** Aggregate INITIALIZATION of a bit-field struct/union now PACKS per allocation unit at all three sites (replacing the cycle-2 fail-louds): (1) LOCAL `struct S s = {…};` → `lowerBitfieldAggregateInitIntoSlot` (`hir_to_mir.cpp`) zeroes every bit-field allocation unit in a PRE-PASS (before any field value is written) then RMWs each field in via the SAME `emitBitfieldInsert` the field-by-field write uses (single packing chokepoint); ordinary fields among the bit-fields lower normally. (2) GLOBAL const init → the const-fold yields raw per-field values that the static-data byte encoder `encodeAggregateValue` (`asm.cpp`) now packs into each unit (OR `((val&mask)<<bitOffset)` into the pre-zeroed buffer). (3) `lowerConstructAggregate`'s fold path is unchanged (raw values → encoder packs); a NON-foldable bit-field RVALUE literal fails loud cleanly (→ `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME`). **Also fixed (C 6.7.9):** a POSITIONAL initializer now SKIPS an anonymous bit-field (`lowerBraceInit` in `cst_to_hir.cpp` — `skipAnon`), so `{a,b,c}` on a struct with an interior `unsigned : 0;` lands `c` on the real field, not the anon slot (was a silent miscompile the moment init worked). **REVIEW-CAUGHT (independent review) & folded the same cycle:** the LOCAL per-unit zeroing was initially LAZY (zero a unit on first touch, interleaved with field stores in declaration order) → an ordinary field declared BEFORE a unit-sharing bit-field (`struct { char x; int a:3; }` — `x` at byte 0, `a`'s int unit at bytes [0,4)) was CLOBBERED by that bit-field's blanket unit-zero, AND diverged from the asm encoder which pre-zeroes the whole buffer once (global `gt.x`==7 but local `lt.x`==0 — the F1 silent miscompile + global/local divergence). Fixed via the TWO-PASS above (zero ALL units, then write ALL fields — order-independent; the RMW preserves the zero in bits it does not set, ordinary fields occupy disjoint bytes; dedup on `(offset,unitBytes)` covers mixed-width units sharing an offset). Source/target/linker-agnostic: keys only on the config-driven layout's `bitFields[]`/`fieldOffsets[]` (LE, as the rest of the bit-field code). Tests (red-on-disable): the MIR pin flipped (`…FailsLoudAtLowering` → `BitFieldStructInitializerPacksPerUnit`, asserts the And/Or/Shl + unit-zero packing) + the F1 ordering pin `BitFieldUnitZeroPrecedesOrdinaryFieldStoreInSharedUnit` (MIR — the FIRST store must be the unit-zero, not the ordinary field's value), an asm-tier byte-exact pin (`BitFieldStructInitPacksIntoUnitByteExact` — `{5,20}`→`0xA5`) + the F1 asm pin `BitFieldUnitSharingOrdinaryFieldByteExact` (`{char x=7; u32 a:3=5}`→`{7,5,0,0}`), corpus `examples/c-subset/bitfield_init` (global+local, positional+designated, anon-skip, signed sign-ext, ordinary-field-shares-a-bit-field-unit via `struct T` `gt`/`lt`, exit 42 / 35-if-clobbered) on x86_64-PE (ctest) + x86_64-ELF (native WSL) + arm64-ELF (qemu); macho on the macos-latest leg. | (closed) | `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD` (parent); `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME` (residual rvalue case); `hir_to_mir.cpp` lowerBitfieldAggregateInitIntoSlot (note: `lowerConstructAggregate` was removed in the RVALUE-RUNTIME cycle 2026-06-18 — globals now fold via `evaluateConstant`+`encodeAggregateValue`); `asm.cpp` encodeAggregateValue; `cst_to_hir.cpp` lowerBraceInit |
| `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-18 (FC8 SUBSTRATE cycle "aggregate compound-literal rvalue carrier", branch p10; design (B) — synthesized slot, the established memory-based aggregate model).** A by-value aggregate-rvalue COMPOUND LITERAL now has a runtime carrier — and the fix is GENERAL, not bit-field-specific (scoping refuted the bit-field framing: the gap was that an aggregate `ConstructAggregate` rvalue had no lvalue ADDRESS at all). **Root cause:** every by-value aggregate-rvalue consumer (`appendByValueArg`, `lowerStructReturn`, assign-from-rvalue, member-of-literal) takes the aggregate's ADDRESS via `lowerLvalueAddress` (`hir_to_mir.cpp`), which had no arm for `ConstructAggregate` (HirKind ordinal 32) → fail-loud `H0009` "lvalue kind ordinal 32 not supported" for EVERY by-value compound literal (bit-field AND non-bit-field, arg/return/assign) — reached BEFORE `lowerConstructAggregate`'s bit-field-specific fail-loud, so that diagnostic was on a path the consumer never took. **As-built (one new arm):** `lowerLvalueAddress` gains a `ConstructAggregate` arm (C11 6.5.2.5p4 — a compound literal IS an lvalue): for a Struct/Union/Array type it materializes a slot (`freshAggregateTemp`, fail-loud on un-sizeable type), inits IN PLACE (`lowerArrayInitIntoSlot` for Array; `lowerAggregateInitIntoSlot` for Struct/Union — which ALREADY routes a bit-field type to `lowerBitfieldAggregateInitIntoSlot`, the cycle-4 per-unit packing chokepoint), and returns the slot address. The unchanged FC7 by-value arg/return/assign path then consumes the address exactly as it does a named local — so struct/union/array + bit-field/non-bit-field are covered BY CONSTRUCTION. Keys ONLY on HirKind + type-kind (Struct/Union/Array) + the config-driven layout — no target/arch/format/lang identity. **Dead-path cleanup (anti-resurrection):** the former SSA-aggregate path (`lowerConstructAggregate`'s `Const(zero)` seed + `InsertValue` chain) + its recursive `zeroLiteralOf` seed-builder are now UNREACHABLE — a stderr-probe across the full ctest suite AND the entire `examples/c-subset/` corpus showed ZERO hits (every aggregate ConstructAggregate flows through VarDecl slot-init, the global static-data encoder, or the new arm). Both helpers DELETED (~232 lines); the `lowerExpr` ConstructAggregate dispatch arm is converted to a fail-loud INVARIANT ("must be lowered into a slot by address, never a bare SSA rvalue") so the chain can't silently resurrect. `evaluateConstant`/`toMirLiteral` survive (globals still fold through them); `hasBitfieldMember` preserved. **Witnesses:** corpus `examples/c-subset/aggregate_rvalue_byvalue` (bit-field + non-bit-field aggregate rvalues in ARG + RETURN + ASSIGN-from-literal positions, runtime operands via a mutable global seed so ConstFold/Mem2Reg can't fold the literal away; optimized arm survives) exit 42 on x86_64-PE (ctest) + x86_64-ELF (WSL) + arm64-ELF (qemu) [macho on the macos-latest leg]; structural MIR pins `ByValueCompoundLiteralArgMaterializesSlotElementWise` (InsertValue==0 + Alloca + per-field Stores at byte offsets) + `ByValueBitfieldCompoundLiteralArgPacksPerUnit` (And/Or/Shl packing). Red-on-disable: revert the arm → `H0009` ordinal 32 (corpus RED, MIR pins fail-loud); restore. **DISCARD POSITION (independent-review fold F2/F3):** an aggregate compound literal used as a DISCARDED statement (`(struct P){h(),2};`) now routes through the SAME slot carrier at the `ExprStmt` site (materialize into a throwaway slot so operand side effects run, then drop the address) — this removed a regression (a discarded aggregate literal had briefly fail-louded) and completes the carrier across the discard position while keeping the `lowerExpr` anti-resurrection invariant intact (now truly internal-only). Pinned `DiscardedAggregateCompoundLiteralStatementRunsSideEffects` (the discard lowers with the h() side-effect Call + a slot Alloca, no SSA-aggregate chain), red-on-disable. Independent adversarial review SHIP-WITH-FOLDS (F1 stale file-ref + F2/F3 discard-statement, all folded; deletion-deadness independently re-verified across full ctest + corpus in debug & release). | (closed) | `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-INIT` (parent); `D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-VALUED-CONTROL-EXPR` (sibling — the aggregate-typed ternary/comma carrier, ✅ CLOSED 2026-06-18); `hir_to_mir.cpp` lowerLvalueAddress (new arm) + lowerExpr ConstructAggregate invariant (lowerConstructAggregate/zeroLiteralOf removed) |
| `D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-VALUED-CONTROL-EXPR` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-18 (FC8 SUBSTRATE cycle "aggregate-valued ternary/comma rvalue carrier", branch p10).** A by-value aggregate-typed `Ternary` (`cond ? A : B`) or comma/`SeqExpr` rvalue now has a runtime carrier. **As-built:** `lowerLvalueAddress` gains a Ternary arm (alloc ONE common slot via `freshAggregateTemp`, lower the cond, build a CFG diamond [IfThen/IfElse/IfJoin + `addCondBr`], materialize EACH arm INTO the common slot, return the slot — NO Phi; the memory-based aggregate model has no aggregate-width SSA value, so the "merge" is shared memory, not an SSA Phi) + a SeqExpr/comma arm (run the side-effect stmts, then recurse `lowerLvalueAddress` on the result) + helper `materializeAggregateArmIntoSlot` (a compound-literal arm inits in place via `lowerAggregateInitIntoSlot`/`lowerArrayInitIntoSlot` — so a bit-field literal arm packs per allocation unit through the slot; a named-lvalue/call/nested-ternary arm resolves to an address and is COPIED in via `lowerAggregateCopy`/`lowerByteWiseCopy`). Keys ONLY on HirKind + type-kind (Struct/Union/Array) + the config-driven layout — no target/arch/format identity. **GATE-DISCOVERED SEAL (independent review SHIP-CLEAN, but found a pre-existing perimeter hole):** a DISCARDED aggregate `Ternary`/`SeqExpr` with NAMED-LVALUE arms (`(cond ? s1 : s2);` as a bare statement) fell through `ExprStmt`'s ConstructAggregate-only special-case into `lowerExpr`'s Ternary arm → silently synthesized a FORBIDDEN Phi-of-aggregate + aggregate-width arm Loads (the MIR verifier does not reject an aggregate Phi → it flowed to LIR as a latent miscompile). SEALED this cycle: (a) a shared `lowerDiscardedExpr` chokepoint routes ANY aggregate-typed discard through `lowerLvalueAddress` from BOTH discard positions — the `ExprStmt` site AND the bare for-init/for-update clause (`lowerForClauseNode`, which delivers its clause un-wrapped, not via `ExprStmt`); a FOLLOW-UP seal review caught the for-clause as a SECOND discard position the initial `ExprStmt`-only generalization missed (silent→loud, not a regression), so coverage is now by construction with NO per-kind AND NO per-position miss; (b)+(c) `lowerExpr`'s Ternary/SeqExpr arms gain aggregate-type anti-resurrection fail-loud guards (mirroring the existing ConstructAggregate guard) so any future misroute fails loud instead of resurrecting a Phi-of-aggregate. Pins: `DiscardedAggregateTernaryNamedArmsRoutesThroughSlotNoPhi` (ExprStmt site) + `ForUpdateAggregateTernaryRoutesThroughSlotNoPhi` (for-clause site), both red-on-disable. **Witnesses:** corpus `examples/c-subset/aggregate_ternary_rvalue` (non-bf ternary in ARG both-arms-witnessed via runtime pick_t/pick_f + ASSIGN; bit-field ternary in RETURN with signed sign-extension; comma/SeqExpr with a LOCAL side effect; named-lvalue copy-path arm; anti-fold via a runtime-opaque global load) exit 42 on x86_64-PE (ctest) + x86_64-ELF (WSL) + arm64-ELF (qemu) [macho on the macos-latest leg]; 5 MIR pins (`ByValueAggregateTernaryMaterializesCommonSlotUnderDiamond`, `ByValueBitfieldAggregateTernaryPacksPerUnitUnderDiamond`, `AggregateSeqExprByValueLowersWithSideEffectAndSlot`, `ByValueAggregateTernaryNamedLvalueArmsCopyIntoCommonSlot`, `DiscardedAggregateTernaryNamedArmsRoutesThroughSlotNoPhi`) using helpers `countOpcodeAllBlocks`/`funcWithCall`/`hasAggregatePhi`. Red-on-disable: revert the carrier → `H0009` ordinal 33/39; revert the seal (a)+(b) → the discard pin catches the Phi-of-aggregate (`hasAggregatePhi` true) — demonstrated empirically (probe build → 1 FAILED at the `hasAggregatePhi` assertion), restored + re-greened. **327/327 ctest.** **ORIGINAL DEFERRAL CONTEXT (created 2026-06-18, trigger-gated, sibling of D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME):** An AGGREGATE-typed `Ternary` (HirKind ordinal 33 — `cond ? (struct S){…} : (struct S){…}`) or comma/`SeqExpr` rvalue used BY VALUE (arg/return/assign/member) is a DIFFERENT carrier than the compound-literal slot: each control-flow arm must be materialized into a COMMON result slot under the branch (control-flow-dependent), not initialized once in place. The compound-literal arm just added to `lowerLvalueAddress` (D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME) does NOT handle it — it stays FAIL-LOUD at the `lowerLvalueAddress` ordinal-N catch-all (`H0009` ordinal 33), cited there in a code comment so this guard resolves. A scalar ternary/comma is unaffected (it lowers as an ordinary scalar rvalue). | (closed) | **Closing work (as-built — see status cell):** give `lowerLvalueAddress` (or a sibling) an aggregate-typed Ternary/SeqExpr arm that allocates ONE result slot, lowers each arm's aggregate INTO that slot under the control-flow diamond (CondBr → arm-then-init-slot → join), and returns the slot address — reusing `lowerAggregateInitIntoSlot`/`lowerArrayInitIntoSlot`/`lowerAggregateCopy` per arm shape (a compound-literal arm inits in place; an lvalue arm copies). Keep it source/target/linker-agnostic (HirKind + type-kind only). `hir_to_mir.cpp` lowerLvalueAddress (the ordinal-N fail-loud comment cites this anchor); `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME` (sibling — the compound-literal carrier) |
| `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-WIDE-UNIT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-18 (FC8 deferral cycle, branch p10; user-authorized §B — `mov r64,imm64` strategy chosen).** 64-bit-base bit-fields (`unsigned long long x : 40;`) work end-to-end. **The anchor's premise was REFUTED by scoping:** general 64-bit `mov`/ALU was NOT the blocker (add/sub/mul/and/or/shifts/load/store all already had REX.W variants; a small-mask 64-bit bit-field already compiled) — the ONLY gap was materializing a 64-bit integer constant > int32 (the wide bit-field masks hit a literal-pool dead-end with no encoder). **As-built:** x86 encoder gained an `imm64` operand-kind + `Imm64`/`OpcodePlusReg` slots (`target_schema.hpp`, `x86_variable.cpp`+disasm) + one `mov r64,imm64` variant (REX.W B8+rd io) in `x86_64.target.json`; shared lowering `mir_to_lir.cpp` `lowerConst` routes a wide INTEGER const by CAPABILITY PROBE (`targetHasMovImm64` → mov-imm64 [x86]; else `targetHasMovkLadder` → MOVZ+MOVK [arm64, already declared]; else fail-loud) — ZERO arch identity; the float literal-pool fail-loud is unchanged; semantic guard `typeBits > 32` → `> 64` (I128/U128 still rejected). **Also fixes a broader latent fail-loud** — any wide >int32 64-bit integer constant (`unsigned long long x = 0xFFFFFFFFFF;`) previously hit the same dead-end. **Latent-bug CAUGHT + fixed:** the 3 LIR rebuild passes (`lir_2addr_legalize`/`lir_callconv`/`lir_rewrite`) copied `LiteralIndex` operands by index but never carried the literal pool (`LirLiteralPool::at` out-of-range — invisible until the first real value rode `LiteralIndex` to the encoder) → new `lir_pass_util::copyLiteralPool`, verified complete across all rebuild sites. **Witnesses:** corpus `bitfield_wide_unit` (40+20-bit pack + signed 36-bit sign-extension + a non-bit-field wide-const arm, anti-fold via a mutable `g_seed` so masks materialize at RUNTIME in baseline AND optimized) exit 42 on x86_64-PE (ctest) + x86_64-ELF (WSL) + arm64-ELF (qemu) [macho on the macos-latest leg]; x86 byte-pins (`mov rax,0xFFFFFFFFFF = 48 B8 FF FF FF FF FF 00 00 00`, r8-r15 REX.B) + arm64 MOVK-ladder pin + a semantic-flip pin, all red-on-disable (revert-probe verified ×3). ADJUDICATION: mir_to_lir LOCAL FOLD chosen over a LIR peephole pass** — (1) the SymbolId is directly readable at MIR tier (`mir.globalAddrSymbol`; a LIR pass would re-derive it from the lea's operand via the heavyweight rewrite machinery), (2) single-use analysis is well-defined on MIR SSA (post-regalloc LIR reuses registers), (3) the direct-call SymbolRef fold in `lowerCall` is the established in-file precedent. CAPABILITY-DRIVEN: fires for x86 movsd_load/movss_load (the FC2/FC3.5-c2 riprel variants' FIRST pipeline consumers — F2/F3 0F 10 05 rel32); does NOT fire for x86 GPR `load` (no [symbol] variant) or arm64 `fldur` (arm64's lea is the 2-word ADRP+ADD macro — a symbol-relative LDR fold is a DIFFERENT encoding shape, honestly out of scope, pinned by `Arm64GlobalAddrLoadKeepsLeaPlusBaseFormLoad`). | `D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-10** (FC3 c1, V2-4.X — the prescribed `dataModel` axis LANDED, the "SemanticConfig axis" arm of the closing-work fork, plan-lock-refined to put the FIELD on the FORMAT schema [the OS lives in the format: pe→LLP64, elf/macho→LP64, wasm/spirv→ILP32 declared-only/fail-loud-if-selected; x86_64 serves BOTH Windows and Linux so a target-level field was unrepresentable]): `ObjectFormatSchema.dataModel` (REQUIRED, closed enum, loader+validate reject missing/unknown) → resolved at `buildCuMir` → threaded into `analyze()` (defaulted-LP64 param = the identity model; the driver always passes `format.dataModel()`) and carried on `SemanticModel` to HIR. `builtinTypes`/`typeSpecifiers` rows carry `coreByDataModel` overrides (`long`→I64, LLP64→I32; `unsigned long`→U64/LLP64→U32); the literal ladder resolves type NAMES through the same table (LLP64 long=I32 falls out free); **the descriptor surface closed too**: `signatureByDataModel` per-symbol overrides (Model-3 library-map shape) on the 6 width-divergent symbols (fseek/ftell/atol/strtol/strtoul/labs — LLP64 i32/u32 forms; every declared override must parse, unknown keys reject). WITNESS: `datamodel_long_width` (LP64 runtime, 2³⁰+2³⁰=2³¹ proves 64-bit long, WSL+qemu exit 42) + `datamodel_long_width_llp64` (the SAME `long` vocabulary `expectDiagnostics`-REJECTS S_TypeMismatch@21:14 under LLP64 on the PE arm — a DEFINED-behavior runtime-divergent witness is impossible-by-construction until c2's 32-bit ALU [signed overflow=UB, unsigned-32=gated]; the runtime divergent witness rides c2). Red-on-disable: pe64 dataModel flipped LLP64→LP64 → the llp64 example RED. ORIGINAL text below. — First time a c-subset corpus row needs `long` to bind to I32 (LLP64 / Win64 ABI) instead of I64 (LP64 / SysV ABI). Today `c-subset.lang.json` binds `long → I64` unconditionally — that's correct for LP64 Linux/macOS, silently wrong for Win64 LLP64 (Windows `long`=I32). Today's corpus runs Win64 only because `pe64-x86_64-windows-exec` is the sole `targets[*].spec`, but the `long`→I64 binding bakes a data-model decision into the source-language config in a way that violates the agnosticism rule (lang config should not assume ABI). **SCOPE EXTENSION (2026-06-09, the `#include puts` 3-OS string-FFI capstone — STILL OPEN):** this anchor now ALSO covers the **shipped-lib descriptor surface**. When the 15 per-platform descriptors were collapsed into 5 neutral `shippedLibs/<h>.json` (Model 3 — see `D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-PLATFORM-SELECT` ✅ dissolved), the **6 ABI-WIDTH-DIVERGENT symbols** — stdio `fseek`/`ftell` + stdlib `atol`/`strtol`/`strtoul`/`labs` (the ones whose signatures involve `long`/`size_t`) — were MERGED to the **LP64 (i64/u64)** form in the neutral signatures. That is CORRECT for the runnable linux/macos targets (LP64), but the **Windows LLP64 form for these 6 (where `long` = i32) is now LATENTLY DEFERRED** — confirmed UNEXERCISED by any corpus (no shipped-`fseek`/`strtol`/etc. call compiles to a Windows target today; the running `puts`/`abs` corpora touch no width-divergent symbol). The proper fix is the SAME as the `long`-keyword case: an abstract `long`/`size_t` resolved per-target (the descriptor signatures would spell `long`/`size_t` abstractly, the data-model axis below would bind them per active target). Do NOT file a duplicate anchor — this is one and the same "config baked an ABI/width decision" gap, now spanning both the c-subset `long` keyword AND the neutral shipped descriptors. | Extend the `SemanticConfig` schema with a `dataModel` axis (e.g., `"ILP32"` / `"LP64"` / `"LLP64"`) that `builtinTypes[*].core` can vary on; OR add an ABI-conditioning layer in the HIR→MIR lowering that overrides core types per active target. Closes the silent-mismatch when the same c-subset source compiles to two different ABIs. Pairs with the multi-target capstone (D-CAP-MARKER-MULTI-TARGET-E2E-PIN). | plan 22 (alias arc); pairs with D-CSUBSET-LONG-PRIMITIVE (which made this decision provisional) |
| `D-OPT-IDENTITYREBUILD-CALLER-WRAP-REQUIRED` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-04** (cycle 10h post-fold) — `identityRebuild` test helper signature refactored from `void identityRebuild(Mir const& src, Mir& out)` to `[[nodiscard]] bool identityRebuild(...)`. The void+ASSERT_NO_FATAL_FAILURE shape required caller discipline; a caller writing `identityRebuild(src, dst);` without the wrap would silently allow tests to proceed with a half-built `dst`. `[[nodiscard]]` triggers an unused-result warning at the missing-check call site, converting the silent-failure trap into a compile-time gate. | Refactor + ADD_FAILURE on the carve-out arm (vs the prior ASSERT_EQ which required void return); both existing test bodies updated to `ASSERT_TRUE(identityRebuild(...))`. | tests/opt/test_mir_rebuild_helper.cpp; same compile-time-enforcement pattern as `[[nodiscard]] Result<T>` patterns elsewhere in the codebase |
| `D-TARGET-ENCODING-WIDTH-GUARD` | **REWRITTEN to post-FC3.5 truth 2026-06-11 (audit-residue sweep c2, `D-AUDIT-BOOKKEEPING-TRUTH` (b) — the original text below had become pre-FC3 fiction cited by LIVE diagnostics).** The mechanism the original row asked for SHIPPED: FC3 c2 added the variant-guard WIDTH key (`guard.width`∈{32,64}, loader-validated, + the ONE shared `variantMatchesInst` matcher + the `kLirInstFlagWidth32` carrier) and `trunc`'s FIRST encoding (mov r32,r32); FC3.5 c2 put F32 on the SAME width key (F3-prefix SSE / arm64 S-forms at width 32 vs F2/D-forms at 64) with SOURCE-width keying for conversions (the fpcvt/fp_to_si widthOverride). What the anchor now MARKS — and what its live citations mean — is the residual width WALLS: any width with NO encoded variant fails LOUD instead of first-match-winning a wrong form. Citations: `requireEncodedFloatWidth` (mir_to_lir.cpp ~:648) walls F16/F128 scalar floats; the FPR literal-promotion guard (~:1350) cites it for non-F64 inline-literal widths (F32 literals ride the rodata path); sub-native INTEGER widths (8/16/128-bit ALU; the I8/I16 sext variants) wall at `requireNativeIntWidth` / the SExt source gate — that successor work lives at `D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS`. | Encode the missing width variants WHEN a consumer arrives (F16/F128: no C producer today; I8/I16: the `D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS` triggers) — the guard.width axis makes each a declaration-only addition. **ORIGINAL pre-FC3 text** (history): "First time a target needs distinct encodings for different operand WIDTHS (not just kinds like reg/imm/mem) — e.g., sext I8→I64 vs I32→I64 are different x86 opcodes (`0F BE` vs `63`). Today's variant-guard only supports `operandKinds` discrimination; same trap exists for the existing zext (BYTE→r64 single-variant) and trunc (no encoding) mnemonics. / Extend the variant `guard` schema with an `operandWidths` array discriminator + teach the encoder to dispatch on the source operand's width-of-record. Then add the missing sext variants (I8→I16, I8→I32, I8→I64, I16→I32, I16→I64) + the trunc encoding (mov r32, r32 zeroes upper 32). Pre-fold the same trap masks any I8/I16 widening / narrowing as silent miscompiles." | plan 13 §2.4 (encoding shapes); pairs with D-CSUBSET-LONG-PRIMITIVE (which closed only the I32→I64 variant); integer-width successor: `D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS` |

| `D-CSUBSET-CHAR-STRING-VALUE-CODEGEN` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-16 (char value codegen cycle, branch p8).** Full `char` VALUE codegen on BOTH targets via a sub-native BYTE width axis (`kLirInstFlagWidth8`): byte LOAD (x86 movzx r/m8 / arm64 LDURB — zero-extending, byte-exact), byte STORE (x86 mov r/m8,r8 + forceRexPrefix / arm64 STURB), byte SEXT (x86 movsx r/m8 / arm64 SXTB — char→int widening). The width-8 flag is CONFINED to {load, store, sext}: `memAccessWidthFlags(Char)=8` at every memory site (load/store/folded-rip-load+predicate/Extract/InsertValue); `registerOpWidthFlags` collapses char's byte width→32 for PLUMBING (const/copy/Trunc — no 8-bit register form; value consumed low-bits-only); char COMPUTE never occurs (integer promotion → I32) and would fail LOUD (no width-8 ALU variant). String-value rodata (`char* p="abc"; *p`) loads via the existing rodata producer + byte load. Indexed char access (`p[n]`, x86 SIB byte forms) works on x86; arm64 indexed is blocked by a PRE-EXISTING arm64 `lea` (symbol-only) gap affecting int+char alike — NOT a char limitation. Pins: 5 LIR width flags (red-on-disable demonstrated) + 2 asm byte-word pins (exact bytes, both targets) + `char_value` (cross-target, exit 132, sign-correct) + `char_ptr_indexed` (x86 indexed, exit 131). | `D-FC7-STRUCT-BY-VALUE-ARG-RETURN` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-16 (FC7 C3, branch p9, commits d25696d + 03ba02d) — ALL 4 ABIs done.** C3 = AAPCS64 + Apple arm64 via the **IRR-reroute design** (§B Option A — explicit indirect-result register, refined at build time from the plan-lock's `WriteIndirectResult` op, which was UNSOUND because x8 is allocatable: emitting `mov x8,R` before arg-setup could clobber an arg parked in x8). As-built: the caller reuses the existing sret-pointer PREPEND (operands[1]) + a new `call_payload::kIndirectResultBit` (bit 30) that lir_callconv reads to ROUTE that operand to `cc.indirectResultRegister` (x8) via the SAME `argMoves` parallel-move set (inherits the hazard detection; R is a real Call operand ⇒ regalloc keeps it live; an arg-in-x8 is read by its own earlier arg-move before the appended IRR move writes x8 — no clobber, genuine cycle fails loud like D-ML7-2.3). Callee reads x8 via a new `ReadIndirectResult` op (mirror of `Arg`) + returns VOID (`WriteIndirectResult` was DESIGNED but proved dead → removed). HFA classifier (`aapcs64_hfa`: 1–4 same-FP-type leaves → that many FPR pieces, width-honoring `pieceType` F32/F64; non-HFA ≤16B → ceil(size/8) GPR; >16B → x8-sret) + config (both arm64 CC rows: strategy/maxRegBytes=16/indirectResultRegister=x8/returnFprs=d0..d3). SF-1 ✅ (the `emitParallelRegMoves` >2-cycle guard raised — the scratch-break linearizes any cycle). **★ FOUND + FIXED a HIGH PRE-EXISTING silent miscompile (the crux of this cycle):** every MIR `Return`-CLONE site (`mir_merge.cpp` cross-CU + `mir_rebuild_helper.cpp` shared rebuild [the baseline `runPruneUnreachableBlocks` path] + `inlining.cpp` caller-clone) mapped ONLY `oldOps[0]`, TRUNCATING every multi-piece struct return to piece 0 — and `Return.maxOperands` was `1` (the verifier's upper guard of the same bug). C1c's multi-piece return therefore NEVER worked end-to-end; it was masked on x86_64 because the dropped piece's value usually still aliased its arg register at the return register (a 3rd field passed in rdx == returnGprs[1]) — AAPCS64's distinct arg/return mapping exposed it (`mkTri` read field 3 as field 2). Fix: all clone sites map ALL operands via `addReturnMulti` + `Return.maxOperands`→N + a fail-loud guard for inlining a multi-piece return (`D-FC7-INLINE-MULTI-PIECE-RETURN`). RUNTIME-WITNESSED: `struct_byval_aapcs64` arm64-ELF qemu exit 42 (HFA double-pair d0:d1 + float-HFA s0:s2 + 2-GPR x0:x1 + >16B x8-sret with a real arg in x0 [N-2] + HFA arg + >16B by-ref arg) — and ALL 133 c-subset examples pass baseline+optimized (x86 native + arm64 qemu), proving the multi-piece fix end-to-end; macho-arm64 + x86-ELF on CI. Pins: 7 AAPCS64 engine pins + `IdentityRoundTripPreservesMultiPieceReturnAllOperands` (the clone-truncation guard, host-independent) + `ThreeFprHfaReturnCycleBreaksViaScratch` (SF-1) + `Aapcs64StructSretUsesReadIndirectResultAndFlagsCall` (N-3 MIR: callee ReadIndirectResult+void return + caller IRR-flag) + the CallPayload IRR-bit roundtrip + corpus. Closes the struct-return half of `D-FF3-APPLE-ARM64-ABI-DIVERGENCE` (variadic half stays open). **NOTE (FC7 Phase 2 — the user REQUESTED struct by-value args/returns).** | Pass/return a struct/union BY VALUE — needs per-ABI classification: SysV-x86_64 eightbyte (INTEGER/SSE register pairs + MEMORY spill), AAPCS64 (HFA + ≤16-byte register pass + sret hidden-pointer), Win64 (by-reference over 8 bytes). Today FAIL-LOUD at the call site AND the function definition (`hir_to_mir.cpp` lowerCall arg/return guard + lowerFunction param/result guard) — a struct arg/return emits a positioned `unsupported` diagnostic, never a silent pointer-pass (which would diverge from by-value-copy semantics and miscompile). Priority: **high — FC7 closeout Cycle C (IN PROGRESS).** **§B RESOLVED 2026-06-16 (user):** lowering boundary = **HIR→MIR** (classify + synthesize register-pieces / sret-pointer / by-ref-copy at HIR→MIR, reusing `lowerByteWiseCopy`/alloca/Gep/Load/Store; the MIR/LIR arg machinery + register pools stay UNCHANGED; thread the resolved CC into HIR→MIR like FC3 threaded `dataModel`) — over the MIR→LIR alternative. AGNOSTIC design: extend `TargetCallingConvention` with by-value params + a CLOSED `aggregateClassification` STRATEGY enum (sysv_eightbyte / aapcs64_hfa / win64_by_size); a bounded per-strategy classify algorithm in the realization tier switches on the enum (the `cc.slotAligned` precedent), NEVER target/cc.name identity. PHASED: C1 substrate + SysV (x86_64 ELF/MachO, runtime-closed ELF+qemu), C2 Win64 (by-ref>8 + sret + shadow), C3 AAPCS64+Apple (HFA + ≤16-in-regs + x8-sret); the fail-loud guard becomes strategy-availability-keyed so un-built CCs stay loud; anchor CLOSES at C3 (all 4). Array-storage gaps (`D-MIR-STORAGE-ARRAY-INDEX-GEP`, `D-MIR-ARRAY-FIELD-AGGREGATE-INIT`) DEFERRED to a dedicated post-FC7 cycle (user). **C1a ✅ (commit b0a140c)**: config substrate + the `aggregate_abi` SysV eightbyte classifier (13 engine pins) + caller/callee synthesis for SINGLE-register (≤8B, 1 eightbyte) + BY-REFERENCE (>16B) struct ARGS — runtime-witnessed x86_64-ELF exit 42. The 2-eightbyte (9–16B) in-register case + struct RETURNS are fail-loud, pinned below (the multi-register MIR-Arg extension = §B-resolved Option A, the C1b step). **C1b ✅ (commit b7f547d): 2-eightbyte structs pass/receive IN REGISTERS — `D-FC7-SYSV-STRUCT-ARG-MULTIREG` ✅ + the latent `D-ML7-2.10` ✅. C1c ✅ (commit 1016d47): SysV struct RETURNS by value — reg-pair via the new `ReturnPiece` opcode + sret + mixed-class — `D-FC7-SYSV-STRUCT-RETURN-IN-REGS` ✅. FULL x86_64 SysV struct-by-value ABI (args + returns) DONE. C2 Win64 + C3 AAPCS64/Apple remain; THIS parent anchor closes at C3.** **C2 ✅ (commit a72d249): MS x64 (Win64) struct by-value — a bounded `win64_by_size` classifier arm (power-of-2 ≤8 → 1 GPR; every other size incl. 3/5/6/7 + >8 → by-reference; NO SSE/HFA — a small aggregate is an integer of its size) + the `ms_x64` CC's `aggregateClassification`/`aggregateMaxRegBytes=8`; REUSED the C1a+C1c arg/return synthesis (single-reg / by-ref / sret) verbatim — no new mechanism. Runtime-witnessed x86_64-PE NATIVELY on Windows: exit 42 (8B in-GPR + 12B by-ref/sret + 3B non-pow2 by-ref/sret). Engine pins (1/2/4/8→1 GPR, 3/12→by-ref, float-struct→GPR-not-SSE) + `struct_byval_win64` corpus. C3 AAPCS64+Apple (HFA + ≤16B in regs + x8-sret) is the LAST step; THIS parent anchor closes there.** **C3 §B RESOLVED + DESIGN PLAN-LOCKED (2026-06-16; ready to BUILD next cycle, resume = `.temp/c3-aapcs64-plan.md`).** §B (user): the x8 sret = **Option A — explicit indirect-result-register path** (a dedicated IRR role, over B sentinel-arg-ordinal / C hidden-arg-rejected-as-ABI-non-conformant). Closes `D-FC7-STRUCT-BY-VALUE-ARG-RETURN` (all 4 ABIs) + the struct-return half of `D-FF3-APPLE-ARM64-ABI-DIVERGENCE`.** | plan 23 §3.1 FC7; `hir_to_mir.cpp` lowerCall + lowerFunction guards; `target_schema.hpp` `TargetCallingConvention`; `abi_catalog.cpp` CC selection; built on `D-FC7-MEMBER-ACCESS` + `D-FC7-AGGREGATE-COPY-MEMCPY` |
| `D-FC7-SYSV-STRUCT-ARG-MULTIREG` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-16 (FC7 C1b, §B Option A, commit b7f547d).** A 2-eightbyte (9–16 byte) struct now passes/receives IN REGISTERS. **As-built (simpler than the registered plan):** (1) HIR→MIR emits each param + struct-piece `Arg` with a MONOTONIC PER-CLASS counter (`ArgOrdinalCounter` — GPR/FPR separate for an independent CC, flat for a slot-aligned one; `argSlotAligned` threaded from the CC like `dataModel`), so the payload is the per-class physical register ordinal and N pieces land in consecutive regs. (2) The `mir_verifier` bound changed from `argIndex < FnSig paramCount` to `argIndex < (number of physical `Arg` instructions in the function)` — sound because a per-class payload < its class count <= the total, and a flat payload < the total — so NO classifier/ABI threading into the verifier was needed (simpler than the planned "classifier threaded into mir_verifier"). The C1a caller + callee multi-register fail-loud guards were removed. **ALSO CLOSED `D-ML7-2.10`** (the per-class change fixes the latent mixed-class scalar-arg payload — a scalar param's payload is now its per-class index, not the param index). Runtime-witnessed x86_64-ELF: `{int,y,z}` 12B in rdi:rsi (exit 42, debug+release) + `{long,long}` 16B + the D-ML7-2.10 mixed int/double/int call (the double correctly in xmm0, exit 42). Pins: `SysVTwoEightbyteStructParamReceivesTwoRegisterPieces` (2 I64 Args, per-class payloads 0,1) + the `struct_byval_sysv` corpus (now single+multi+by-ref) + `mixed_class_args` corpus (D-ML7-2.10). | plan 23 §3.1 FC7; commit b7f547d; `mir_verifier.cpp` physical-arg-count bound + `hir_to_mir.cpp` `ArgOrdinalCounter`; `D-ML7-2.10` ✅ (closed with it); `D-FC7-STRUCT-BY-VALUE-ARG-RETURN` (still open — returns + C2/C3) |
| `D-FC7-SYSV-STRUCT-RETURN-IN-REGS` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-16 (FC7 C1c, §B Option A, commit 1016d47).** A struct/union RETURNED by value now works across ALL SysV return classes. **§B (user) = Option A: a new MIR `ReturnPiece` opcode** (the caller-side mirror of `Arg`) for the multi-register capture — over (B) a side-table + post-regalloc memory synthesis or (C) sret-everything (ABI-non-conformant for ≤16B, rejected). **As-built:** (1) **callee** loads the eightbyte pieces and emits a MULTI-operand `Return` (maxOp bumped to N); the lir_callconv materialization moves each piece → `returnReg[class][per-class cursor]`. (2) **caller** (`emitStructReturningCall`): the struct Call's result IS piece 0 (returnReg ordinal 0 of its class) and each further eightbyte is a `ReturnPiece(call, ordinal)` read; all pieces store into the result slot `R` (a frame Alloca), and `R`'s address is the call's value (consumed by every aggregate-by-address consumer via the new `lowerLvalueAddress(Call)` arm). (3) **sret** (>16B MEMORY): a hidden result-pointer `Arg` (GPR ordinal 0, shifting the real params via the shared `ArgOrdinalCounter`) the callee copies the result through (`lowerByteWiseCopy`) + returns; no new opcode. (4) **MF-1 cycle-break** (BUILT — the arg path only REJECTS cycles, D-ML7-2.3): `emitParallelRegMoves` breaks a return-register 2-swap with a caller-saved scratch (≥3-cycle = AAPCS64 HFA, fails loud → C3). **Latent-crash fix:** `lowerFunction` copied the `interner.fnParams` SPAN into an owning vector — the sret/by-value `interner.pointer(…)` interning can REALLOCATE the pool and dangle a retained span (an interner-state-dependent "TypeId out of range"; also hardened the C1a/C1b struct-arg path). Verifier relaxed: a struct-returning func's Return may carry the first-class aggregate VALUE, N register pieces (I64/F64), or an sret Ptr. Runtime-witnessed x86_64-ELF exit 42 (reg-pair rax:rdx + sret + mixed {double;long} xmm0:rax, debug AND release). Pins: MF-2 callconv swap unit pin (pre-colored {rdx,rax}, value-correct sim) + 4 MIR pins (multi-Return shape, sret hidden-ptr, mixed-class split, call-once SF-2) + `struct_return_sysv` corpus. Reachable via the typedef-name return form (`struct Tag` top-level return type = the pre-FC4 grammar residue `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION`). | plan 23 §3.1 FC7; commit 1016d47; `mir_opcode.hpp` ReturnPiece + `hir_to_mir.cpp` emitStructReturningCall/lowerStructReturn + `lir_callconv.cpp` emitParallelRegMoves; `D-FC7-SYSV-STRUCT-ARG-MULTIREG` (shared substrate); parent `D-FC7-STRUCT-BY-VALUE-ARG-RETURN` (C2/C3 remain) |
| `D-FC7-NESTED-STRUCT-FIELD` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-16 (FC7 closeout Cycle A, branch p9).** A struct/union FIELD whose type is a user struct/union (typedef-name) now PARSES. The entire gap was a SINGLE agnostic chokepoint: the c-subset grammar's `typeBaseAllowingStruct` (consumed ONLY by `structField`/`unionField`) was missing the bare-`Identifier` alt that `typeBase`/`declHeadForParam`/`castTypeBase` already carry — added it (symmetric; UNAMBIGUOUS because a struct/union body has no expression-statement production, so no binder-sketch guard, and a non-type name fails loud at semantic scope-lookup — verified `S0006`). Everything downstream was ALREADY ready (VERIFIED against code, not assumed): recursive `computeLayout`, `lowerLvalueAddress` nested `a.b.c` recursion, SE5 typedef lookup, post-order composition. ALSO closed the nested-INITIALIZER gap in the SAME cycle: `lowerAggregateInitIntoSlot` now RECURSES into a struct/union-typed field whose initializer is a brace-init (a nested `ConstructAggregate`), element-wise into the sub-slot (an aggregate VALUE into an aggregate field stays fail-loud → `D-FC7-AGGREGATE-COPY-MEMCPY`, not a NEW anchor). Reconciled the now-stale FC2 `$fc2CastExprComment` that had claimed this alt was deliberately omitted (its premise — that `typeRefAllowingStruct` feeds varDecl/param — was inaccurate; those use their own heads). Pins: `NestedMemberAccessComposesChainedGepOffsets` (chained-GEP offset composition 0→4, base-chain identity) + `UnionWithStructTypedFieldParsesAndAccesses` (union half of the multi-form contract) + `StructLocalNestedInitRecursesIntoSubSlot` (3 leaf stores, offset multiset {0,0,4,8}, 0 InsertValue) + `struct_nested_field` (exit 42, member access) + `struct_nested_init` (exit 42, nested brace init, fold-resistant via non-inlined `pick()`). RED-ON-DISABLE DEMONSTRATED: disabling the recursion → `struct_nested_init` fails LOUD at LIR (no aggregate InsertValue realization), `struct_nested_field` stays 42 (lever isolated). RUNTIME-WITNESSED x86 PE + arm64-ELF qemu (42/42); macho-arm64 + x86-ELF on CI. Independent review CLEAN (no HIGH/MED/LOW). | (closed) | plan 23 §3.1 FC7; commit 45ccc25; `c-subset.lang.json` `typeBaseAllowingStruct` Identifier alt + `hir_to_mir.cpp` `lowerAggregateInitIntoSlot` recursion; `D-FC7-MEMBER-ACCESS` (the codegen it unblocked) |
| `D-FC7-AGGREGATE-COPY-MEMCPY` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-16 (FC7 closeout Cycle B, branch p9).** A new `lowerByteWiseCopy(src,dst,size)` primitive copies an aggregate's whole object representation via the widest available integer chunks — I64 (8B) → one I32 (4B) → `Char` (1B) for the 0–3 byte tail (NO width-16 form exists; `I8` would silently widen to a 64-bit access — `Char` is the only safe 1-byte vehicle; memAccessWidthFlags: Char→8, I32→32, else→64). `lowerAggregateCopy` now dispatches: a UNION (variants overlap → field-wise of one misses the rest) or a struct with ANY aggregate field (struct/union/ARRAY) → byte-wise whole; a flat scalar struct keeps the existing width-exact field-wise path. ALSO closed the path Cycle A had deferred here: an aggregate field INITIALIZED from an aggregate VALUE (`struct Outer o = { existingInner, … }`) now copies via `lowerAggregateCopy` (was fail-loud). Greedy chunking proven gap-free + overrun-free for all sizes (independent review traced 0,1,2,3,4,5,7,8,12,16,20). Pins: `UnionCopyIsByteWiseNotFieldWise` (I64 chunk, NOT the 4-byte `n` variant — the discriminator) + `StructWithStructFieldCopyIsByteWise` (I64@0+I32@8, far field z@8 survives) + `StructWithArrayFieldCopyIsByteWise` (MIR-tier — array READ-back blocked by the separate `D-MIR-STORAGE-ARRAY-INDEX-GEP`) + corpus `struct_union_copy` / `struct_nested_copy` / `struct_init_from_value` (exit 42, fold-resistant). RED-ON-DISABLE DEMONSTRATED: disable the byte-wise arms → all three corpus fail loud. RUNTIME-WITNESSED x86 PE + arm64-ELF qemu (42); macho + x86-ELF on CI. Independent review CLEAN (no HIGH/MED). | (closed) | plan 23 §3.1 FC7; commit 0107a2c; `hir_to_mir.cpp` `lowerByteWiseCopy`+`lowerAggregateCopy`; `D-FC7-MEMBER-ACCESS`; surfaced `D-MIR-STORAGE-ARRAY-INDEX-GEP` |
| `D-FC7-INLINE-MULTI-PIECE-RETURN` | The OPT7 inliner is now SAFE for by-value-struct-returning callees (the silent-miscompile half ✅ CLOSED 2026-06-16, FC7 C3 follow-up); the *optimization* of actually inlining such a callee stays deferred (first need to inline a multi-piece-`Return` / x8-sret callee profitably). DISCOVERED 2026-06-16 (FC7 C3) while fixing the multi-piece-`Return`-clone truncation cluster; the inlining HALF was caught by the post-commit independent review (two single-block splice paths + the `ReadIndirectResult` frame-splice were initially unguarded). | **Silent-miscompile ✅ CLOSED via a chokepoint REFUSAL:** `inlineLegalityGate` (`inlining.cpp` ~:277) now REFUSES (`return std::nullopt` — fail-SAFE, the call survives, the program compiles) any callee whose body contains a multi-piece `Return` (N>1 operands) OR a `ReadIndirectResult` (x8-sret entry read). This covers EVERY splice path at once — the original review found the single-block `spliceCallee`/`spliceSingleBlock` Return clones (~:518/:892) took only operand 0 (truncating the struct to field 0) AND a spliced `ReadIndirectResult` would read the CALLER's x8 (garbage) — both REACHABLE under the shipped `release.pipeline.json` (which DOES run `Inlining`, threshold 50; the earlier "unreachable today" note was WRONG and is corrected here). The multi-block continuation-Phi `cops.size()>1` abort (~:1115) is now a defensive backstop. **Deferred (optimization):** actually inlining a struct-returning callee (reconstruct the aggregate / thread N piece-Phis / splice the sret pointer to the caller's allocated storage). Priority: **normal** (OPT7 territory — not autonomously openable). Pin: `InliningRefusesMultiPieceStructReturningCallee` (red-on-disable: remove the gate refusal → the callee inlines + truncates). | `inlining.cpp` `inlineLegalityGate` refusal; the multi-piece-`Return`-clone fixes landed this cycle (`mir_merge.cpp` + `mir_rebuild_helper.cpp` + `inlining.cpp` caller-clone, all map ALL operands via `addReturnMulti`; `mir_text.cpp` round-trip too); `D-FC7-SYSV-STRUCT-RETURN-IN-REGS` (the multi-piece machinery) |
| `D-FC7-INDIRECT-X8-SRET-CALLEE-EXCLUSION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-23 (FC-deferral close-out c7, branch p14).** The regalloc indirect-callee exclusion set (`lir_regalloc.cpp`, the FC4 c2 R2 rule in `allocateOneFunc`) now ALSO excludes the cc's `indirectResultRegister` (x8 on AAPCS64) for an indirect callee whose call carries the indirect-result payload bit — a 4-part MIRROR of the existing variadic-vector-count-reg exclusion: `IndirectCalleeAt.indirectResult` (set from `call_payload::hasIndirectResult`), the resolved `ccIndirectResultRegOrdinal`, and the per-callee `if (ic.indirectResult && ...)` application (added to `excludedScratch` BEFORE the span is formed, so it propagates to BOTH `tryAllocateExcluding` AND `findSpillCandidate` — the spill fallback can't recreate the clobber). AGNOSTIC: reads `cc->indirectResultRegister` (config), NO hardcoded x8; the `.has_value()` guard makes it a no-op for SysV/Win64 (hidden-arg sret, no indirectResultRegister) and for any function without an indirect struct-returning call. Closes the fail-loud edge: an indirect (fn-ptr) >16B-struct-returning call could park the callee in x8 → the FC7-C3 `mov x8, sretPtr` reroute clobbered it → the loud L_IndirectCalleeClobberedByArgSetup backstop (a VALID program failed to COMPILE, not a miscompile). **Validation:** host-independent RED-ON-DISABLE structural pin `LirRegAlloc.Aapcs64PressuredIndirectStructReturnCalleeExcludesX8` — a register-pressure sweep (k=5..11) lands the callee ON x8 at k=10 the moment the exclusion is removed (empirically demonstrated callee=13,12,11,10,9,**8**,20 + restored) + the END-TO-END runtime corpus `examples/c-subset/struct_byval_indirect_aapcs64` (`Big (*fp)(void)=&makeBig; b=fp();` 24B x8-sret fn-ptr call → qemu-aarch64 exit 42). The simple corpus does NOT force x8 (unpressured, fp parks on callee-saved x29) — it is the path-WORKS witness, the structural pin is the red-on-disable guard. 368/368 Windows + WSL qemu; independent review VERIFIED-CLEAN (it rebuilt + toggled the fix red/green + ran qemu + disassembled, and caught an over-claim in the corpus prose — folded). | (closed — c7 branch p14) | `lir_regalloc.cpp` allocateOneFunc indirect-callee consumer; `D-FC7-STRUCT-BY-VALUE-ARG-RETURN` (the IRR move it pairs with) |
| `D-MIR-STORAGE-ARRAY-INDEX-GEP` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-17 (array-storage cluster, branch p9, Option A — user §B).** The index is PRE-SCALED to a BYTE offset at HIR→MIR (`scaleIndexToBytes`, element stride via the FC6 `computeLayout` engine — agnostic, all element sizes incl. non-pow2), so the storage-array Index arm COLLAPSES the vestigial leading 0 to the SAME 2-op byte-offset GEP the pointer path uses (the 3-op form is no longer produced; the LIR `lea` stays scale=1 — NO hardware scale). Also FIXED the latent non-`char` `p[i]` scale-1 silent miscompile + array-LOCAL alloca sizing (8-byte slot → full byte size) + a `stride==0` fail-loud guard. Runtime-witnessed: `array_storage_index` exit 42 (storage `a[i]` runtime index + `int*` `p[2]` + array-local brace-init) on x86-PE + x86-ELF + arm64-ELF qemu under the REAL release pipeline. Pins: `IndexIntoIntArrayCollapsesToTwoOpGepAndScales`, `IndexOverIntPointerScalesIndexToByteOffset` (miscompile-fix), `IndexIntoCharArrayDoesNotScale`, `ArrayLocalAllocaReservesFullByteSize`, `IndexIntoZeroSizeElementArrayFailsLoud`. **Was:** First need to index a LOCAL or struct-FIELD storage array's elements at runtime (`int a[3]; a[i]` or `s.arr[i]` as an r/l-value). DISCOVERED 2026-06-16 during FC7 closeout Cycle B — NOT an FC7 anchor (pre-existing, general array-indexing gap, unrelated to aggregate copy). | Indexing a STORAGE array (an array LOCAL or struct field, baseKind≠Ptr) lowers in `hir_to_mir.cpp` `lowerLvalueAddress` Index arm to a **3-op GEP `[basePtr, 0, idx]`** (the leading 0 steps through the pointer-to-array), but `mir_to_lir.cpp` `lowerGep` does NOT lower the 3-op shape → fail-loud `L_UnsupportedLoweringForOpcode` (deferred opcode) on BOTH targets. POINTER indexing (`p[i]`, 2-op `[ptr,idx]`) works; storage-array indexing never did. Closing work: lower the 3-op array-index GEP — the vestigial `0` makes it equivalent to base+index, so collapse to the 2-op base+index form + apply the ELEMENT SCALE (x86 SIB scale / arm64 LSL), coordinating with `D-AS4-ARM64-INDEXED-LEA-SCALE` for scale≠1. Until then a struct with an array field COPIES correctly (byte-wise, `D-FC7-AGGREGATE-COPY-MEMCPY`) but its elements can't be read (MIR-tier pin only: `StructWithArrayFieldCopyIsByteWise`). Priority: **normal** (a general codegen gap; likely an early post-FC7 / FC8 cycle). | `mir_to_lir.cpp` `lowerGep` (3-op arm) + `hir_to_mir.cpp` `lowerLvalueAddress` Index arm; `D-AS4-ARM64-INDEXED-LEA-SCALE` (the scale half) |
| `D-MIR-ARRAY-FIELD-AGGREGATE-INIT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-17 (array-storage cluster, branch p9).** New `lowerArrayInitIntoSlot` lowers an array ConstructAggregate ELEMENT-WISE (one Store/recurse per element at the CONSTANT byte offset `j·stride`), mutually recursive with `lowerAggregateInitIntoSlot` for struct elements + itself for multi-dim. | `D-LIR-INT-MEMORY-WIDTH-EXACT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-17 (array-storage cluster, branch p9, user §B "all widths, one cycle").** DISCOVERED by the array work's self-audit: integer memory access was 8-byte-wide for every int except `char` (`memAccessWidthFlags` returned width-default; the x86/arm64 schemas had only width-64 + width-8 mem variants), so a 64-bit load/store of a PACKED sub-8-byte element (array element or struct field) overran — `array_storage_index` CRASHED (ACCESS_VIOLATION) under the REAL release pipeline; FC7 struct-field writes were latently affected too. FIX (width-exact integer memory access): a new `kLirInstFlagWidth16` LIR flag; `memAccessWidthFlags` now returns the EXACT width per integer byte size (8: Char/I8/U8/Bool/Byte; 16: I16/U16; 32: I32/U32; default-64: I64/U64/Ptr — incl. `long`→I32 under LLP64); NEW width-32 + width-16 memory load/store encoder variants on BOTH targets (x86 `mov r32` 0x8B / `mov r16` 0x66 0x89 / `movzx r32,word` 0F B7; arm64 LDUR/STUR Wt + LDURH/STURH — no-index only, the index folds into the `lea`); the schema-loader accepts `width:16`. Also fixed the latent `lowerByteWiseCopy` I32-chunk overrun (it was silently a width-64 access). Witness tiers: int(32) RUNTIME-witnessed (`array_storage_index` exit 42, x86-PE/ELF + arm64 qemu, under the REAL release pipeline) + char(8) by the pre-existing `char_ptr_indexed`; I8/U8/Bool(8) + I16/U16(16) are BYTE-PINNED (`WidthAxisX86/Arm64.Int32And16MemFormsEmit*`) + fail-loud-safe — their VALUE codegen (Trunc/SExt/ZExt to/from sub-32) is the SEPARATE `D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS` gap, so they cannot be runtime-exercised yet (no miscompile — they fail loud at value codegen). Loader pin: `WidthAxisLoader.GuardWidthSixteenAcceptedOutOfVocabularyRejected`. Full corpus (314 examples) re-validated x86-native + arm64-qemu (every int load/store changed bytes). | (closed) | `mir_to_lir.cpp` `memAccessWidthFlags`; `lir_node.hpp` `kLirInstFlagWidth16`; `x86_64.target.json`/`arm64.target.json` load/store; `target_schema_json.cpp` width loader; surfaced by the array-storage cluster; pairs with `D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS` (sub-32 VALUE codegen) |
| `D-CSUBSET-INDEX-INTEGER-PROMOTION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-22 (FC-deferral close-out loop cycle 4, branch p14).** A `char` subscript of a WIDE-element array/pointer (`int a[N]; char c; a[c]`) now integer-PROMOTES to `int` (C 6.3.1.1 / 6.5.2.1) BEFORE the index arithmetic. **As-built (small, front-end, the BEST-long-term placement — not a MIR band-aid):** the CST→HIR `Index` lowering (`cst_to_hir.cpp` ~1862) coerces a sub-int index via the SAME `integerPromotedType`+`coerce` the binary-op path uses (config-driven C 6.3.1; gated on `arith_.has_value()` so block-less toy/tsql are byte-identical) — so the HIR index node is already `int` and `scaleIndexToBytes` sees a promoted index. **`builder.makeIndex` has EXACTLY ONE call site** in all of `src/` (independently verified), so EVERY subscript — rvalue load, lvalue store, compound-assign, `&a[i]`, `a[i]++` — funnels through the one promoted chokepoint (no §A.5 multi-site miss). The crux is OVERFLOW: an un-promoted `(char)100 * 4` = 400 wraps mod 256 → reads/writes the WRONG element; promotion computes 400 at int width. Signedness preserved (signed `char`→SExt, unsigned→ZExt; a negative index sign-extends). int/long index untouched (`integerPromotedType` returns the same TypeId → no coerce). **Witnesses:** corpus `index_integer_promotion` exit 42 — char load AND char-indexed STORE both overflow-sensitive (`c=100`/`d=120`, value-divergent) + signed-char — on x86_64-PE (ctest) + x86_64-ELF (WSL) + arm64-ELF (qemu) [macho macos leg]; MIR pin `CharIndexIntegerPromotesToI32BeforeStrideMul` (the stride `Mul` is I32-typed, its lhs is the SExt/ZExt promotion not the raw char Arg) DEMONSTRATED red-on-disable (promotion off → inst-count 8→7, Mul Char-typed, example walls/overflows). Independent review VERIFIED-CLEAN. 365/365 ctest. **SHORT residual (orthogonal, fail-loud, NOT this anchor):** a `short` index promotes CORRECTLY (the cycle emits the SExt) but the I16 SExt/Trunc ENCODING is unrealized at the backend → loud `L_UnsupportedLoweringForOpcode` on x86 + arm64 — the separate sub-native I16-codegen gap `D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS`/`D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS`, NOT the promotion. — ORIGINAL: First need a `char`/`short`-typed subscript of a WIDE-element array/pointer (`int* a; a[c]` where `c` is `char`) to compile. DISCOVERED 2026-06-17 (array-storage cluster re-audit, NIT). Today FAIL-LOUD (NOT a miscompile — and strictly better than the pre-cycle SILENT scale-1 form): `scaleIndexToBytes` types the scaling `Mul(idx, stride)` at the index's OWN type (`hir.typeId(kids[1])`), so a `char` subscript makes a `Char`-typed Mul that walls at the Char-ALU gap (`D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS`). Per C 6.3.1.1 the subscript should integer-PROMOTE to `int` before the index arithmetic — a pre-existing FRONT-END promotion gap the scaling faithfully reflects. `char`-index-of-`char`-array (stride 1, no Mul) and `int`-index both work. | (closed) | `cst_to_hir.cpp` Index lowering (integerPromotedType+coerce); `D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS`/`D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS` (the orthogonal I16-codegen gap blocking the short end-to-end); `D-MIR-STORAGE-ARRAY-INDEX-GEP` (the cycle that surfaced it) |
| `D-CSUBSET-WIDE-HEX-OCTAL-ESCAPE-VALUE` | ⏳ **trigger-gated** — first corpus that needs a `\x` hex / `\ooo` octal escape to name a raw CODE-UNIT VALUE inside a wide/UTF literal — e.g. `u"\xFFFF"` → one 0xFFFF `char16_t` unit, `u8"\xFF"` → one 0xFF `char8_t` unit, or `U"\x1F600"` → one 0x1F600 `char32_t` unit. | **Behavior is now FAIL-LOUD (Cycle C, `\u`/`\U` + surrogate arc), NO LONGER a silent miscompile.** Cycle C found that `\x`/octal in a wide/UTF literal SILENTLY collapsed the escape bytes through the UTF-8 re-decoder — `u"\xC3\xA9"` became ONE wrong 0x00E9 unit instead of two intended units. So the wide path now REJECTS any consumed `\x`/octal escape: strings → `H_WideByteEscapeUnsupported` (`cst_to_hir.cpp lowerStringLiteral`, keyed on `EscapeDecodeOutcome::usedByteEscape` + a wide opener), chars → `WideCharError::ByteEscapeInWide` → the same code (`decodeWideCharCodepoint`). The DEFERRED FEATURE is assembling the escape VALUE directly as one code unit of the element (C 6.4.4.4 / 6.4.5: the byte/hex value IS the unit — unbounded hex digits for `\x`, must fit the element width), bypassing the UTF-8 round-trip. Closing work: thread the escape VALUES (not the decoded bytes) so a wide element consumes them as code-unit values; the NARROW `"…"`/`'…'` path stays byte-producing (correct, UNCHANGED). Use `\u`/`\U` for a code POINT until then. | `char_decode.hpp` (`EscapeDecodeOutcome.usedByteEscape`); `wide_string_encode.hpp` (`decodeWideCharCodepoint` `ByteEscapeInWide`); `cst_to_hir.cpp` (`lowerStringLiteral` FF3 guard); `semantic_analyzer.cpp` (leave-untyped mirror); `H_WideByteEscapeUnsupported`; examples `wide_string_hex_escape_error`; plan 23 (full-C wide/UTF literal arc, Cycle C) |

| `D-FF1-MACHO-READER` | Roadmap B2 (ACTIVE): bind externs against a REAL `.dylib` (libSystem) -- the Mach-O twin of `D-FF1-PE-READER`. **c160 HARDENING (2026-07-16): the EXPORT-reader half is now PRODUCTION-GRADE** -- the names-only nlist v1 became a full EXPORT-TRIE walk (`LC_DYLD_INFO`/`LC_DYLD_INFO_ONLY` export_off, or `LC_DYLD_EXPORTS_TRIE`) -- the exact dlsym surface + the inverse of the c153 export-trie writer -- with fn/data KIND classification by SECTION membership (a section carrying `S_ATTR_PURE_INSTRUCTIONS`/`S_ATTR_SOME_INSTRUCTIONS` is Function; `__DATA`/`__DATA_CONST`/`__TEXT,__const` is Object -- the Mach-O analog of the c159 PE section-executability heuristic), and REEXPORT detection (`EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_REEXPORT` -> `SymbolKind::Forwarder` + `forwardTarget` "<dylib-leaf>.<import-name>", the PE-forwarder analog). The nlist walk survives as the FALLBACK for images with no trie (a relocatable `.o`, an ancient dylib, a minimal fixture), now also classifying by `n_sect`. Fail-loud vs skip+warn split mirrors c159: structural trie corruption (a ULEB/edge-string/child-offset past the trie region, a child offset >= the trie size, or a back-edge cycle -- the dyld visited-offset guard) fails loud `CorruptedBinary`; per-entry corruption (an out-of-section address, an out-of-range `n_sect`, an empty resolved name) is skipped + summarized via `F_BinaryReaderPartialCorruption`. STRONGEST oracle landed: an in-process WRITER->READER round-trip (`test_binary_reader_macho_roundtrip.cpp`) encodes a real DSS MH_DYLIB via `dss::macho::encode` (fn `_dss_add` + int `_dss_global=7` + a LOCAL `_hidden_helper`) and reads its export trie back -> `_dss_add`=Function, `_dss_global`=Object, `_hidden_helper` absent (red-on-disable RE-VERIFIED: forcing the classifier to Function reddens the Object asserts on the trie path, the nlist path, AND the round-trip). Plus 14 synthetic export-trie/section fixtures (classification, same-name reexport, region-past-EOF, child-offset-past-trie, unterminated edge, cycle, truncated ULEB, unterminated reexport name, out-of-section skip, nlist n_sect classification + out-of-range skip). **Memory-safety review folds (post-implementation, adversarially fuzzed CLEAN -- cmdsize=0/4GiB, over-long/shift-overflow ULEB, diamond all fail loud):** (HIGH) a name-explosion DoS guard -- a structurally-VALID "caterpillar" trie (a spine whose every node terminates) drives Theta(size^2) name materialization (a ~1MB dylib -> ~GBs -> OOM); the walk now tracks TOTAL materialized name bytes and FAILS LOUD past 32x the trie size (a legal-trie invariant; real dylibs run ~1-3x), plus a per-name length cap at the trie size -- red-on-disable-verified (cap off -> the 2000-node caterpillar surfaces 2000 rows); (LOW) trie empty-name terminals now skip+count for parity with the nlist path; the reexport 1-based dylib-ordinal space now spans ALL five dylib-load commands (added LC_LAZY_LOAD_DYLIB + LC_LOAD_UPWARD_DYLIB) so a reexport past a lazy/upward dep names the right dylib. STILL OPEN (the roadmap-B2 remainder): wiring the resulting `ImportSurface` into the linker's extern-bind path + the real-libSystem otool/nm oracle-match on the macOS CI leg (structural byte-parse + the DSS-dylib round-trip are the local oracles; no macOS host here). | Parse the export trie (✅ c160) / `LC_SYMTAB` fallback (✅ c160) into the shared `ImportSurface` the linker binds externs against; mirror the PE reader (✅); fail-loud on truncation (✅ trie region + ULEB/edge/child-offset bounds). | `D-FF1-AR-READER` | Roadmap B3: pull members from a REAL `ar` static archive (`libc.a`/`libm.a`/`.lib`). **c161 (2026-07-16): the READER half is DONE.** New `src/ffi/binary_readers/ar_reader.{hpp,cpp}` parses a GNU/System V archive into (a) the member list -- each member's resolved name + header/data offset + size, with GNU "//" long-name tables + "/N" refs resolved -- and (b) the armap symbol index (the "/" member: a BIG-endian u32 count + u32 member-header offsets + a NUL-terminated name blob), mapping each exported symbol -> its defining member. Wired into the format-blind `readImports` dispatch via a new `!<arch>\n` magic arm (`FormatGuess::Ar` -> `readAr`), which PROJECTS the armap to `ImportSurface` rows -- the honest linker-facing export surface (the UNION of the members' exports; each row's `libraryPath` = "<archive>(<member>)" nm/linker notation naming the defining member, `kind` = NoType since the armap carries no fn/data kind, `visibility`/`linkage` = Default/External since an armap indexes defined externals). Fail-loud `CorruptedBinary` on every malformed shape (bad magic, member header/data past EOF, non-numeric size field, armap count/offset out of range, a "/N" offset past the "//" table, an armap offset matching no member); a degenerate empty armap name is skip+counted via `F_BinaryReaderPartialCorruption` -- the c159/c160 structural-hard-fail vs per-entry-skip+warn split. Every field bounds-checked against untrusted bytes (`rangeExceedsBuffer`). **Memory-safety review fold (c161):** the GNU `//` long-name table is tokenized ONCE into an offset->name map (a single linear pass) -- the prior per-member rescan was O(members x table-bytes) on a crafted unterminated `//` + many `/N` members (a TIME-hang DoS on the untrusted format-blind path -- memory-safe but unbounded work, the c160-Mach-O-trie DoS class); the map is O(1) per member AND fails loud on a mid-entry / out-of-range `/N` offset (the old rescan silently returned a shifted substring) + on an unterminated final entry. Real libc.a stresses it: 526 of 2211 members are long-named `/N` refs, all resolved exact. **Witnessed vs REAL GNU `ar` 2.42:** W1 `ar rcs libtest.a a.o b.o` -> members=[a.o,b.o] (sizes exact) + armap foo/bar_data->a.o, baz->b.o EXACT vs `ar t`+`nm -s`; W2 a 33-char member name resolves via the "//" table (vs `ar t`); W3 the REAL `/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.a` -> members=2211 (== `ar t \| wc -l`) + 4839 armap symbols, printf->printf.o / malloc+free->malloc.o / fopen->iofopen.o / strlen->strlen.o / __libc_start_main->libc-start.o all EXACT vs `nm -s`; W4 three truncation points (mid-header / mid-armap / mid-data) each fail loud with a distinct named diagnostic. 18 hermetic tests (`tests/ffi/test_binary_reader_ar.cpp`; byte-exact synthetic fixtures + red pins, incl. the DoS/mid-entry/multi-long-name pins). **STILL OPEN (roadmap-B3 remainder):** the LAZY-PULL linker wiring -- on an unresolved extern, extract the defining member via `bytes.subspan(dataOffset,size)` + hand its `.o` to the per-format reader + bind (plan 11 sec 4 Q5); the reader exposes the member offsets/sizes that make this a lookup. Variant follow-ups: `D-FF1-AR-BSD-VARIANT` (BSD `__.SYMDEF` armap + `#1/N` inline names; today detected + fail-loud-clean) + `D-FF1-AR-COFF-IMPORT` (Windows COFF `.lib` short-import members; the first "/" linker member is SysV-shaped so it already parses). | (reader closed c161; lazy-pull wiring + variants open) | plan 11 FF1; `src/ffi/binary_readers/ar_reader.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/ffi/binary_reader.cpp` (dispatch) + `tests/ffi/test_binary_reader_ar.cpp`; roadmap B3 |
| `D-FF1-AR-BSD-VARIANT` | ⏳ **trigger-gated (c161).** The c161 ar reader is GNU/System V (the primary form: what `ar rcs` writes on Linux + the first linker member of a Windows COFF `.lib`). The BSD variant differs in two spots: (1) the symbol table is a member named `__.SYMDEF` (or `__.SYMDEF SORTED`) carrying a LITTLE-endian ranlib array, not the SysV big-endian "/" armap; (2) long member names are inline `#1/N` (the name field is `#1/N` and the first N bytes of the member DATA are the real name), not a "//" table + "/N" ref. The GNU 64-bit `/SYM64/` armap (>4 GiB archives, u64 BE offsets) is a third gated shape. Today all three are DETECTED and fail loud cleanly (`CorruptedBinary`, message names the variant + this anchor) rather than silently misparsed -- cited in `ar_reader.cpp` (the `__.SYMDEF`/`#1/`/`/SYM64/` guards). Trigger: first corpus needing a real BSD `.a` (a macOS-produced static lib) OR a >4 GiB archive. Closing work: parse the BSD ranlib armap (LE `(strx, offset)` pairs + a trailing string table) + `#1/N` inline names; add a `/SYM64/` arm reading u64 BE offsets. No witness on this box (WSL = GNU); reserved for a macOS host / a real BSD fixture. | Add a BSD-armap parse arm + `#1/N` inline-name resolution + a `/SYM64/` u64-offset arm to `readArArchive`. | plan 11 FF1; `src/ffi/binary_readers/ar_reader.cpp` (the fail-loud guards cite this); pairs `D-FF1-AR-READER` (the GNU reader) |
| `D-FF1-AR-COFF-IMPORT` | ⏳ **trigger-gated (c161).** A Windows COFF import library (`.lib`) is an `ar` archive whose FIRST linker member is a SysV-shaped "/" armap (so the c161 reader already parses its framing + export surface), plus TWO COFF-specific extras the reader does not yet interpret: (1) a SECOND "/" linker member (an alternative sorted symbol->member index Microsoft's linker prefers) and (2) "short import" members (a tiny `IMPORT_OBJECT_HEADER` record naming the imported symbol + its owning DLL + ordinal/name-type, instead of a full COFF object) -- these are how a `.lib` records "symbol X is imported from Y.dll". The armap-union export surface is correct today; what is deferred is (a) reading the short-import member to recover the owning-DLL + import-type per symbol, and (b) recognizing the 2nd linker member. Trigger: first corpus that binds externs against a real Windows `.lib` (e.g. `kernel32.lib`) and needs the per-symbol owning-DLL, not just the symbol->member map. | Parse the COFF short-import member (`IMPORT_OBJECT_HEADER`) + the 2nd linker member; surface the owning-DLL + import-type on the `ImportSurface` row. | plan 11 FF1; pairs `D-FF1-AR-READER` + `D-FF1-PE-READER` (the `.idata`/import half) |
| `D-FF1-READER-CONSUMER` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-17 (c162, roadmap Job A) -- the reader-consumer is LIVE.** The FF1 readers (ELF/PE/Mach-O export + ar) produced a format-blind `ImportSurface`, but `ingest()` -- the consumer that calls them -- had NO production caller (externs resolved only via inline `extern` + shipped JSON descriptors). c162 makes it live behind the `--resolve-library <path>` driver surface (`CompileOptions.resolveLibraries` / `Program::setResolveLibraries` / the CLI flag): compile_pipeline step 2.5 PARTITIONS source-declared externs -- those with no explicit per-symbol override AND not no-library-binding are BINARY-GOVERNED and routed to the live `ingest()` against the named binaries; the rest keep the trusting `synthesizeFfiFromSourceDecls` path. PRECEDENCE (highest first): noLibraryBinding > explicit libraryOverride (source `extern "lib"` / shipped descriptor) > `--resolve-library` binary match (validated present) > format-default `externLibraryByFormat`. The resolving insight: a DSS-BUILT library has NO shipped JSON descriptor, so reading its real export table is the ONLY way to link against it -- a genuine, NON-DUPLICATIVE capability. The read provides exactly (a) VALIDATION and (b) LIBRARY BINDING (the true soname/DLL-name -> DT_NEEDED / PE import descriptor). **VALIDATION policy (silent-failure-review folds, c162): DESCRIPTOR-AWARE, not blanket.** `ingest()` binds every governed extern the named binaries export + SILENTLY SKIPS the rest (it is a bind mechanism); compile_pipeline then splits the unmatched by the shipped-descriptor oracle (`collectShippedExternSymbolNames`): a KNOWN system symbol (a bare `extern int puts;` the user did not #include) FALLS THROUGH to its format-default library (gcc implicit-libc -- NOT fail-loud), a GENUINE typo (in NEITHER a named binary NOR any shipped descriptor, e.g. `dss_lib_answr`) FAILS LOUD `F_FfiResolveLibrarySymbolAbsent` [0x5022, unsuppressable]. This keeps meaningful compile-time typo-catch WITHOUT wrongly rejecting a legitimate `bare extern puts + --resolve-library ownlib` mixed program (the HIGH the review caught). PLUS an EAGER open-probe validates every `--resolve-library` path UNCONDITIONALLY (fail-loud `F_FileOpenFailed` on a missing/unreadable path even when no extern routes to ingest -- the MEDIUM). `collectShippedExternSymbolNames`==nullopt (config discovery failed) ⇒ fall through all (never a false-positive). The extern TYPE still comes from the inline declaration -- the reader supplies existence + binding only, never a signature. WITNESSED end-to-end PER-PLATFORM NATIVE: DSS builds `dsslib.so`/`dsslib.dll`, DSS builds `main` resolving `dss_lib_answer` against it, RUN -> exit 42 (ELF under WSL + ubuntu CI: `readelf -d main` shows DT_NEEDED=dsslib.so + `dss_lib_answer` GLOBAL FUNC in the .so's `.dynsym`; PE native: import descriptor -> dsslib.dll, CreateProcess -> 42). Red-on-disable: (a) validation (revert the step-(4) miss-arm to `continue` -> silent dangling import) + (b) resolution (WITHOUT `--resolve-library`, dss_lib_answer mis-binds to the format-default msvcrt.dll/libc.so.6 -> exit != 42). Integration test `tests/program/test_ffi_resolve_library.cpp` + unit pins in `tests/ffi/test_ingest.cpp` (the `UnmatchedExternIsSilentlySkipped` pin FLIPPED to `UnmatchedExternFailsLoudNamingSymbolAndLibrary`). This closes the consumer/extern-bind half that `D-FF1-PE-READER` + `D-FF1-MACHO-READER` (+ elf) left OPEN, for the DYNAMIC round-trip; the macOS `.a` STATIC path -> `D-FF1-AR-WRITER-STATIC-LINK` (c163). | (closed -- consumer LIVE, PE .dll + ELF .so dynamic round-trips RUN) | plan 11 FF1 (roadmap Job A); closes the extern-bind half of `D-FF1-PE-READER`/`D-FF1-MACHO-READER` (dynamic); `D-FF1-READER-SONAME` (basename stand-in); `D-FF1-AR-WRITER-STATIC-LINK` (macOS c163); `D-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-MULTI-ARTIFACT`; `src/ffi/ingest.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp` |

All three extractions are BOUNDS-SAFE (reuse the readers' `readNulTerminated` bounding + range checks -- a DT_SONAME d_val past `.dynstr` yields EMPTY, not a crash; witnessed). WITNESSED: 8 strict tests across the 4 reader/ingest suites (synthesize a `.so` w/ DT_SONAME "libwidget.so.2" / a `.dylib` w/ LC_ID_DYLIB "@rpath/libwidget.dylib" verbatim / a `.dll` w/ DllName "widget.dll" -> exact `row.soname`; the ingest prefer beating the importName override + the basename fallback + `meta.soname`), all RED-ON-DISABLE (EMPIRICALLY verified -- fabricating `soname=basename` in each reader or removing the ingest prefer turns the pins red, then reverted byte-exact). Closing work: extract the recorded identity in each per-format reader (ELF `DT_SONAME` from `.dynamic`, PE `Name` RVA in `IMAGE_EXPORT_DIRECTORY`, Mach-O `LC_ID_DYLIB`) into a dedicated `ImportSurface` field; the consumer prefers it over the basename. | (closed c171 -- 3 per-reader extractions + ingest prefer; 8 red-on-disable tests) | plan 11 FF1; `D-FF1-READER-CONSUMER` (the basename stand-in it refines); `src/ffi/binary_readers/*` + `src/ffi/import_surface.hpp` + `src/ffi/ingest.{hpp,cpp}` |

The arm64 CI leg keeps BUILD (cross-compile) structural coverage today. | (closed c171 — qemu-aarch64 run → exit 42 via the corpus arm64 arm in staticlib_resolve_roundtrip) | `examples/c-subset/staticlib_resolve_roundtrip` (the arm64 arm) + `tests/examples/examples_runner.cpp` + `integrated_tests/runner.cpp`; pairs `D-FF1-AR-STATICLIB-DRIVER-WIRING` (the CLI `.a` surface that fired the trigger) |
