| `D-LK-STATIC-ARCHIVE-WRITER` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-17 (c163) -- the `ar` static-archive WRITER (the writer half of `D-FF1-AR-WRITER-STATIC-LINK`; roadmap "write STATIC libs for all platforms").** DSS could WRITE dynamic libs on every platform (`.so` c150 / `.dll` c152 / `.dylib` c153) + READ archives (c161 ar reader) but could not WRITE a static archive. New `src/link/format/ar.{hpp,cpp}` (`dss::link::format::writeArArchive`) is the EXACT INVERSE of the c161 reader: it takes N relocatable object members (file name + opaque `.o` bytes + exported-symbol name list) and emits the `!<arch>\n` container + the SysV/GNU armap ("/" member: BIG-endian u32 count + u32 member-header offsets + NUL-terminated name blob) + the GNU "//" long-name table + "/N" refs when a member name > 15 bytes. **FORMAT-BLIND**: the framing is identical for ELF/Mach-O/COFF members (no source/target/object-format branch); the armap is built purely from the members' exported symbols (`AssembledModule.symbols` filtered by `isExternallyVisible`). Byte-audited against GNU `ar` 2.42 (mtime/uid/gid=0, regular-member mode 644, "/"/"//" mode 0; external '\n' 2-byte member alignment). Fail-loud belts: empty/framing-reserved ('/'/newline/NUL) member name or empty exported-symbol name -> `K_ArchiveMemberNameInvalid` [0x8017]; payload past the 10-digit ASCII size field or a member-header offset past the SysV armap's 32-bit range (the `/SYM64/` >4 GiB case) -> `K_ArchiveFieldOverflow` [0x8018]. The pipeline composition `linkAndWriteStaticArchive` (`src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp`) links each module INDEPENDENTLY to its `.o` bytes (never the cross-CU merge -- an archive PACKAGES, does not merge) + bundles them; `link::writeBytes` (a refactor of `writeImage`'s byte-commit core) commits the `.a`. **Witnessed**: W1 writer<->c161-reader round-trip (armap lists dss_lib_answer + dss_data -> member lib.o, member list + sizes exact); W2 REAL GNU `ar t`/`nm -s`/`ar tv` accept + index a DSS-written `.a` (mode 644 -> `rw-r--r--`) AND real `gcc main.c -l:libdsslib.a` PULLED the member from the DSS armap -> RUN exit 42; W3 2-member + a >15-char name (forces the "//" table) resolve via both the reader + real `ar t`. 11 hermetic tests (`tests/link/test_ar_writer.cpp`; byte-pins + round-trip + wrong-endian-count red-on-disable + fail-loud belts + the real-`.o` pipeline composition). SCOPED: SysV/GNU `.a` for Linux + macOS is the MUST-SHIP (both use this form); the Windows COFF `.lib` 2nd-linker-member is the follow-up `D-FF1-AR-COFF-WRITER`; the DRIVER/CLI request surface is `D-FF1-AR-STATICLIB-DRIVER-WIRING`. | (closed -- writer shipped + real-toolchain witnessed) | plan 14 (linker/format writers) + plan 11 FF1; `src/link/format/ar.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/link/writer.{hpp,cpp}` (`writeBytes`) + `src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp` (`linkAndWriteStaticArchive`); `tests/link/test_ar_writer.cpp`; parent `D-FF1-AR-WRITER-STATIC-LINK` |
| `D-FF1-AR-STATICLIB-DRIVER-WIRING` | ✅ **CLOSED c171 (2026-07-17) -- the DRIVER/CLI staticlib surface, via the §B FORMAT-CONTAINER decision (user Option 1), NOT the profile-branch the original closing-work (b) assumed.** A relocatable format declares `container: "archive"` + `artifactProfiles: ["staticlib"]`; the driver dispatches on the FORMAT's `isStaticArchive()` -- a NEW `ObjectFormatContainer {Single, Archive}` schema axis (loader OPTIONAL `container`, closed-enum fail-loud; validate() rejects `archive` on any image flavor / wasm / spirv -- a foreign linker cannot pull a member from a pre-linked image) -- NEVER the artifactProfile NAME (the `artifact_profile.hpp` veto): output SHAPE stays 100% format-driven, exactly as `elf.type`/`pe.type`/`macho.filetype` select the single-file shapes (cli->exec, lib->shared, staticlib->archive). PIECES: (1) `compileOneTarget` routes `isStaticArchive()` -> lowers each CU to its OWN relocatable member (NO cross-CU merge -- an archive PACKAGES) -> `linkAndWriteStaticArchive`; (2) `outputExtension` archive arm -- `.a` (elf/macho) / `.lib` (pe); (3) `linkAndWriteStaticArchive` threads `ArArchiveFlavor` from `kind()` (COFF for pe -> the LE 2nd linker member; SysV else -- fixes the c163 SysV default); (4) 5 shipped `*-staticlib` formats (elf x86_64/aarch64, macho x86_64/arm64, pe x86_64) generated from the relocatable siblings + `container`/`artifactProfiles`; (5) an INPUT static archive on a staticlib build is rejected FAIL-LOUD (`D_StaticLibFatArchiveUnsupported` 0xD013 -- a merged "fat" archive is the follow-up `D-FF1-STATICLIB-FAT-ARCHIVE`; dynamic `--resolve-library` libs still accepted). WITNESSED: 10 strict tests (schema load/validate + **red-on-disable** container guard + `.a`/`.lib` extension arm + the DRIVER end-to-end: exactly 1 member, exact armap `{dss_add, dss_sub}`, member re-parses as valid ET_REL, the PE `.lib` carries exactly 2 `/` linker members, **red-on-disable** fat-archive guard) + a REAL CLI witness (`dss-code-prime --compile --target x86_64:elf64-x86_64-linux-staticlib` emits `mylib.a` (964 B) + the pe target emits `mylib.lib` (466 B), both `!<arch>` magic) + **MSVC `dumpbin /ARCHIVEMEMBERS` + `/LINKERMEMBER` ACCEPT the DSS `.lib`** (member `mylib.obj`; `dss_add`+`dss_sub` present in BOTH linker members -- the COFF flavor proven by MS's own tool). — ORIGINAL (c163 registration): ⏳ trigger-gated. The c163 `ar` writer (`D-LK-STATIC-ARCHIVE-WRITER`) ships the WRITER + the `linkAndWriteStaticArchive` pipeline composition, but NOT the DRIVER/CLI request surface that ROUTES a build to it. Today a `.a` is produced only by calling `linkAndWriteStaticArchive` programmatically (the witness path). The `staticlib` artifact profile is REGISTERED (`core/types/artifact_profile.hpp`) but NO shipped format declares it in `artifactProfiles[]` -- deliberately: declaring it without the driver honoring it would let a `artifactProfile: "staticlib"` project silently emit a bare `.o` (a half-wired silent-wrong-output). Trigger: first need to build a `.a` from the CLI (`dss-code-prime build` a `staticlib` project, or an `--emit staticlib` flag). Closing work: (a) a relocatable format declares `"artifactProfiles": ["staticlib"]`; (b) `program.cpp::compileOneTarget` routes an artifactProfile=="staticlib" build to `linkAndWriteStaticArchive` (each CU -> a member) instead of `linkAndWrite`; (c) a `.a`/`.lib` extension policy (the `outputExtension` staticlib arm) + member-naming convention. | (closed c171 -- format-container driver surface + 5 `*-staticlib` formats; 10 strict tests + real CLI `.a`/`.lib` + MSVC `dumpbin` witnessed) | plan 11 FF1 + plan 06 (artifact profiles); `D-LK-STATIC-ARCHIVE-WRITER` (the composition it routes to); the §B format-container decision (user Option 1); follow-up `D-FF1-STATICLIB-FAT-ARCHIVE`; `src/link/object_format_schema*.{hpp,cpp}` (`ObjectFormatContainer`) + `src/program/program.cpp` + `src/program/target_spec.cpp` + `src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp` |
| `D-FF1-AR-COFF-WRITER` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-17 (c169) -- the Windows COFF `.lib` 2nd linker member.** `writeArArchive` (ar.cpp) gains an `ArArchiveFlavor` param: `SysV` (the c163 GNU `.a`, the byte-identical default) or `Coff` -- which emits, AFTER the 1st "/" armap (the BE SysV first linker member, unchanged) and BEFORE the "//" table, a SECOND "/" linker member = Microsoft's LITTLE-endian SORTED symbol->member index (u32 memberCount + u32 memberOffsets[M] + u32 symbolCount + u16 1-based member indices[S] in SORTED name order + the sorted NUL-terminated name blob) that `link.exe` PREFERS. The 2nd member shifts every member offset; BOTH linker members' offset arrays are back-filled with the shifted positions in one layout pass. Fail-loud belts: > 65535 members (the u16 index cannot name them, `kMaxCoffMembers`) + the 10-digit ar_hdr size-field overflow. The c161 reader (ar_reader.cpp) is FIRST-"/"-WINS: it keeps the 1st BE armap and SKIPS the 2nd LE member (else it would mis-read the LE payload as BE) -- a plain GNU `.a` (one "/") is unaffected. **WITNESSED**: byte-exact pins of the 284-byte `.lib` (`w1Member`: LE memberCount/offsets/symbolCount, u16 sorted indices, the SORTED name blob distinct from the 1st armap's appearance-order blob) + the c161 round-trip (1 object member, both symbols, 2nd member skipped) + **the NATIVE toolchain end-to-end: real `cl.exe` builds a COFF `.obj`, DSS bundles it into a `.lib`, `dumpbin /ARCHIVEMEMBERS` accepts it, and `link.exe main.obj dsslib.lib` -> `main.exe` RUNS to exit 42** (DISABLED `WriteCoffLibForNativeWitness` + the scratchpad driver). 13 hermetic ar-writer tests + the native witness. Independent code-review VERIFIED-CLEAN. STILL OPEN: the COFF short-import member (an IMPORT lib for a `.dll`) is a separate follow-up (`D-FF1-AR-COFF-IMPORT`, reader-side pair); the `staticlib`/`.lib` driver request surface is `D-FF1-AR-STATICLIB-DRIVER-WIRING`. | (closed c169 -- COFF 2nd linker member; native link.exe exit 42) | plan 11 FF1; `D-LK-STATIC-ARCHIVE-WRITER` (the SysV `.a` writer extended, c163); pairs the reader-side `D-FF1-AR-COFF-IMPORT`; `src/link/format/ar.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/ffi/binary_readers/ar_reader.cpp`; unblocks `D-LK-RELOCATABLE-OBJECT-READER-COFF` (c170) |
| `D-FF1-STATICLIB-FAT-ARCHIVE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-22 (TF-C51) -- the `isStaticArchive()` arm now MERGES input static-archive members into the output library (a fat/merged static library).** When a `container: archive` build is ALSO handed INPUT `--resolve-library` static archives, `compileOneTarget` (program.cpp) calls the NEW `extractStaticArchiveMembers` (compile_pipeline.cpp) and appends its modules+names to the CU-derived members before `linkAndWriteStaticArchive`. `extractStaticArchiveMembers` reads EVERY member of each input archive (WHOLE-archive: a static library is a PACKAGE, so all members are carried -- UNLIKE the lazy referenced-subset `pullStaticArchiveMembers` pulls for an exe/final LINK; dropping an unreferenced member would silently ship an incomplete library) via a SHARED per-format member-reader chokepoint `readArchiveMemberModule` (extracted from the pre-existing lazy pull -- one switch on the closed `ObjectFormatKind` [Elf/MachO/Pe], the blessed agnostic axis, fresh cuId minted per member; both callers funnel through it so a new object format lights up for both by construction). FAIL-LOUD on any open (`F_FileOpenFailed`) / archive-parse (`readArArchive` `CorruptedBinary`) / member-read / unsupported-kind (`F_UnsupportedBinaryFormat`) error -- never a silent member omission; a cross-format input member (COFF `.lib` → ELF staticlib) fails loud via the reader magic mismatch. The output armap is the UNION of all members' exported symbols (duplicate symbols across members = legal `ar`, first-wins on both DSS's reader + a foreign linker; duplicate/empty member names handled -- `ar` indexes by position, empty synthesized `member_<n>.o`). WITNESSED: `StaticLibDriverMergesInputStaticArchive` (staticlib build + input `.a` → output `.a` has EXACTLY 2 members + the UNION armap {dss_extra, dss_lib_answer}, both members re-parse as ET_REL each defining its symbol) + the RUNTIME witness `StaticLibFatArchiveExecRunsFortyTwo` (build a fat lib MERGING an input archive's `dss_lib_answer`=42 member → static-link `main` against the FAT lib → run → exit 42, proving the merged member survives the input.a→module→fat.a→relink DOUBLE round-trip and is linkable+runnable; Linux-x86_64-gated). RED-ON-DISABLE: restoring the `D_StaticLibFatArchiveUnsupported` fail-loud makes the staticlib-with-input-archive build return rc≠0 → the `ASSERT_EQ(rc,0)` in BOTH tests flips red; member-count==2 is reachable ONLY via the merge (a silent drop → 1 member). Independent code-audit **VERIFIED-CLEAN**: the per-archive `bytes` local's lifetime is DISPOSITIVELY safe (`AssembledModule` + every nested type hold ONLY owning containers -- `std::vector`/`std::string`, ZERO `span`/`string_view` -- so `readRelocatableObject` deep-copies; corroborated by the pre-existing `linkAndWriteWithStaticArchives` return-then-use pattern that already relies on the same deep-copy). Gate: Win build-dbg ctest (721/723; the 2 example fails are examples-runner-contention FLAKES that PASS on isolated re-run; `tests/lir/test_mir_to_lir` is COFF-section-size-deferred to the WSL leg, NOT my change) + WSL x86_64 full ctest + qemu-arm64 example legs + sqlite CRUD smoke + anchor guard. **The now-orphaned `D_StaticLibFatArchiveUnsupported` (0xD013) is RETAINED inert** (stable-value diagnostic registry; no longer emitted -- the corpus harness is discovery-based, no "every code emitted" guard). Agnostic (closed-enum dispatch, no if(arch/format-name==)); fail-loud preserved. — *Original (audit trail):* ⏳ **OPEN -- DEFERRED (named 2026-07-17, c171).** Building a static library (`container: archive`) while passing INPUT `--resolve-library` STATIC archives should MERGE those archives' members INTO the output (a "fat"/merged static library, à la `libtool -static` / an `ar` merge script). Today `compileOneTarget`'s `isStaticArchive()` arm FAILS LOUD (`D_StaticLibFatArchiveUnsupported`, 0xD013) on that combo rather than silently DROPPING the input archive -- the honest v1 stance (a silent drop would ship a library missing the members the user asked for). Dynamic `--resolve-library` libraries (for a member's extern FFI surface) ARE accepted (they ride the per-CU FFI path; a member's externs resolve at the FINAL link). Trigger: a real need to bundle prebuilt `.a`/`.lib` members into a DSS-built static library. Closing work: in the `isStaticArchive()` arm, pull each input archive's members (`readRelocatableObject` per member -- the c164 static-LINK machinery) and append them to the `members` span before `linkAndWriteStaticArchive`. | Merge input static-archive members into the output static library (fat archive). | c171 `D-FF1-AR-STATICLIB-DRIVER-WIRING` (the driver arm that fails loud today); `D-LK-STATIC-LINK` / `D-LK-RELOCATABLE-OBJECT-READER-*` (the member-pull machinery to reuse); `src/program/program.cpp` `compileOneTarget` |
| `D-LK-RELOCATABLE-OBJECT-READER` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-17 (c164) -- the ELF ET_REL relocatable-object MEMBER READER (ELF slice); the read-half that unblocks the c165 static-LINK + the object-file-INPUT-to-linker path half of `D-FF1-AR-WRITER-STATIC-LINK` capability (2).** New `src/link/format/elf_object_reader.{hpp,cpp}` (`dss::elf::readRelocatableObject`) is the EXACT INVERSE of elf.cpp's ET_REL writer: it reconstructs a relocatable object's FULL LINKABLE BODY back into an `AssembledModule` (the structure the c154 cross-CU `mergeModules` consumes), NOT just the export surface (c159/c160) or the armap index (c161). Reconstruction: `.text` sliced into per-function byte ranges by the STT_FUNC symbols' (st_value,st_size); `.data`/`.rodata`/`.bss`/`.data.rel.ro` defined OBJECT symbols -> `AssembledData` items (section kind recovered from the section NAME via the format schema's rows -- AGNOSTIC, no hardcoded `.rodata`); SHN_UNDEF symbols -> `externImports` (isData inferred from the reloc that references it -- a PLT-variant reloc marks a function, plain PC32 marks data, mirroring exactly what the writer emitted); each RELA entry -> a `Relocation{offset,target,kind,addend}` with the ELF r_type mapped back to the universal `RelocationKind` via the format schema's `nativeId`/`pltNativeId` rows and the target schema's `addendBias` UN-BAKED (subtracted) so re-emission re-adds it exactly once; defined function/data/section symbols -> `ModuleSymbol` rows (name+binding+visibility) for the merge's cross-CU name-match. FORMAT-STRUCTURAL constants only (the record layout the writer also hardcodes); every reloc TYPE number + section name comes from the schema (target-agnostic -- witnessed on x86_64 AND arm64). Fail-loud (F_* + nullopt) on every bounds/structure violation (header/section-table/symtab/rela past EOF, wrong ELF class, non-ET_REL, unknown reloc type, a reloc site in no reconstructed item) -- the c159-c161 `rangeExceedsBuffer` discipline; truncation-at-every-length fuzz + 5 targeted red-pins. **Witnessed**: W1 DSS-writer<->reader FULL-object round-trip (functions + rodata/data/relro data + extern fn + extern data + `.rela.text`/`.rela.data.rel.ro` relocations, every field class asserted); W2 REAL `gcc -c lib.c` .o (embedded golden, byte-for-byte identical to fresh live gcc output) -> recovered functions/extern/data/relocations; W3 arm64 agnosticism round-trip (R_AARCH64_ABS64=257 mapped via the arm64 schema). 10 hermetic tests (`tests/link/test_relocatable_object_reader.cpp` + `gcc_lib_c164_object.inc`). SCOPED: ELF ET_REL is the reference format (Linux `.a` is the primary static witness, WSL-runnable); Mach-O MH_OBJECT + COFF are the named follow-ups below. Non-producer metadata sections (`.eh_frame`/`.comment`/`.note.*`) + interior block-symbol (computed-goto) VA bindings are NOT reconstructed (the AssembledModule model has no representation; DSS's own writer emits none) -- a documented boundary, not a silent drop; section-relative relocs (gcc's `.rodata`-symbol references) are represented faithfully (target = the section's LOCAL ModuleSymbol) with their merge-time resolution to the specific item deferred to the c165 static-link. | (closed -- the ELF reader IS the read-half deliverable; the static-LINK that consumes it is c165) | plan 14 (linker/format) + plan 11 FF1; `src/link/format/elf_object_reader.{hpp,cpp}`; `tests/link/test_relocatable_object_reader.cpp`; the INVERSE of elf.cpp's ET_REL writer (`D-LK-OBJECT-DATA-SECTION-RELOCATABLE`/`-EXTERN-CALL-RELOCATABLE`/`D-LK-RELRO-CONST-DATA-RELOCATABLE`); consumer = c154 `mergeModules`; parent `D-FF1-AR-WRITER-STATIC-LINK` capability (2); follow-ups `D-LK-RELOCATABLE-OBJECT-READER-MACHO`/`-COFF` |
| `D-LK-RELOCATABLE-OBJECT-READER-MACHO` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-17 (c168) -- the Mach-O MH_OBJECT relocatable-object MEMBER READER, the Mach-O sibling of the c164 ELF reader.** New `src/link/format/macho_object_reader.{hpp,cpp}` (`dss::macho::readRelocatableObject`) reconstructs a Mach-O `.o`'s full linkable body into an `AssembledModule` -- the exact structure the c154 merge consumes -- mirroring elf_object_reader.cpp's pipeline + `rangeExceedsBuffer` fail-loud discipline (constants re-declared locally; the `link`->`ffi` cycle rule). SIX Mach-O-vs-ELF inversions handled: (1) `__text`/data sliced by SORTED `n_value` (nlist_64 has NO size field -- atom [off_k, off_{k+1}), last -> section.size); (2) `n_value` is a FLAT `.o`-space address (section-relative = `n_value - section.addr`); (3) SectionKind from the (SEGMENT,SECTION) NAME PAIR (the two `__const` rows differ by segment); (4) reverse reloc map keys on the packed `r_info & 0xF7000000` nativeId, no pltNativeId (BRANCH26 formula is the "is a function" signal); (5) the DATA-reloc addend read from the in-place slot bytes (sign-extended, `addendBias` un-baked -- Mach-O has no RELA addend column); (6) DSS always writes r_extern=1, so an r_extern=0 SECTION-INDEX reloc (foreign clang) FAILS LOUD. WIRED into the static-link pull: `compile_pipeline.cpp::pullStaticArchiveMembers` now `switch (format.kind())` (Elf->elf reader, MachO->macho reader, else fail loud). **WITNESSED**: DSS writer<->reader round-trip (arm64: 2 functions sliced by n_value + rodata/data/relro + externs + relocs incl. the in-slot data addend + isData inference; x86_64 leaf agnosticism; a 2-item-per-section value-correctness pin) + the STATIC-LINK STRUCTURAL witness (`test_static_link.cpp`: DSS Mach-O `.a` -> pull dispatches to the macho reader -> merge binds `dss_lib_answer` + STRIPS the import, red-on-disable the alone case) + the DRIVER build (a self-contained Mach-O exec, RUN `__APPLE__`-gated on the macos-latest leg). 11 hermetic reader tests + 2 static-link tests; truncation-at-every-length fuzz + 4 corruption red-pins (bad magic / MH_EXECUTE / unknown nativeId / r_extern=0). Independent silent-failure review: items 2/3/5/6 VERIFIED-SOUND, 3 MEDIUM fail-loud-gap folds landed (Findings A/C fail-loud + B doc/test, below). | (closed c168 -- reader + dispatch + static-link structural + driver build) | `D-LK-RELOCATABLE-OBJECT-READER` (the ELF sibling mirrored); the macho.cpp MH_OBJECT writer; `D-LK-STATIC-LINK` (c165, the merge it feeds); `src/link/format/macho_object_reader.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp`; follow-ups `D-LK-MACHO-STATIC-SECTION-RELATIVE-RELOC` / `-ISDATA-NO-CALL-SIGNAL` / `-MULTI-ITEM-SECTION-PADDING` / `-STATIC-LINK-RUNTIME` |
| `D-LK-MACHO-STATIC-SECTION-RELATIVE-RELOC` | ⏳ **OPEN -- DEFERRED (named 2026-07-17, c168).** DSS always writes r_extern=1 (symbol-relative) Mach-O relocs; a FOREIGN clang `.o` uses r_extern=0 SECTION-INDEX relocs (r_symbolnum is a 1-based section number, and anonymous content -- string literals / jump tables -- is reached via a section symbol). The c168 reader FAILS LOUD on r_extern=0 (never mis-binds a section index as a symbol index). Closing this is the Mach-O analog of the c167 ELF section-relative work (`D-LK-STATIC-LINK-SECTION-RELATIVE-RELOC`): gap-atom reconstruction of anonymous `__cstring`/`__const` content + a section-relative redirect keyed on r_symbolnum-as-section. Trigger: static-linking a real Apple/clang `.a` member (no local clang to produce the golden -- the witness rides a Mac / the macos-latest leg). | Reconstruct anonymous Mach-O section content as gap atoms + redirect r_extern=0 section-index relocs to the owning atom. | `D-LK-RELOCATABLE-OBJECT-READER-MACHO` (c168); `D-LK-STATIC-LINK-SECTION-RELATIVE-RELOC` (the ELF sibling, c167); `src/link/format/macho_object_reader.cpp` |
| `D-LK-MACHO-ISDATA-NO-CALL-SIGNAL` | ⏳ **OPEN -- DEFERRED (named 2026-07-17, c168).** The Mach-O reader infers an extern's function-vs-data class from a CALL/BRANCH-formula reloc (arm64 `ARM64_RELOC_BRANCH26` = `aarch64_call26`). The shipped `macho64-x86_64-darwin` schema's `X86_64_RELOC_BRANCH` is a plain `linear`/pcRelative `rel32` -- INDISTINGUISHABLE from a PC-relative data reference by formula -- so on x86_64 Mach-O the call/data class cannot be inferred. The c168 reader FAILS LOUD (silent-failure-review Finding A) when an extern is reached by a reloc on a target that declares no call-branch-formula reloc, rather than silently defaulting to data. Unreachable today (the x86_64 Mach-O schema is leaf-only, no extern-call dispatch, so no extern is ever reached). Trigger: an x86_64 Mach-O runtime/call leg. | `D-LK-RELOCATABLE-OBJECT-READER-COFF` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-17 (c170) -- the COFF `.obj` relocatable-object MEMBER READER + native Windows static-link, the THIRD reader sibling (ELF c164 / Mach-O c168 / COFF c170).** New `src/link/format/coff_object_reader.{hpp,cpp}` (`dss::pe::readRelocatableObject`) reconstructs a COFF `.obj` into an `AssembledModule` -- the inverse of pe.cpp's COFF Obj writer, mirroring the c168 Mach-O reader's shape (constants re-declared locally; the link->ffi cycle rule). COFF-vs-Mach-O/ELF inversions: IMAGE_SYMBOL.Value is ALREADY section-relative (no subtraction, unlike Mach-O's flat n_value); no symbol size -> slice atoms by SORTED Value per section (like Mach-O); SectionNumber is a 1-based ordinal (0=extern, -1/-2=absolute/debug); per-section IMAGE_RELOCATION tables, addend IN the slot bytes (no RELA column, .text REL32 addend 0); name mangling is IDENTITY (verbatim); the derived-type hint IMAGE_SYM_DTYPE_FUNCTION gives isData (canonical `(Type&0x30)==0x20`); rodata-vs-relro disambiguated by RELOC-PRESENCE (the two header-identical `.rdata` rows: reloc-bearing const = relro, reloc-free = rodata -- the semantic essence of relro, agnostic). WIRED into the static-link pull (`compile_pipeline.cpp` switch: added `case ObjectFormatKind::Pe`). **WITNESSED**: DSS writer<->reader round-trip (arm... x86_64: functions sliced by sorted Value + rodata/data/relro + externs + relocs incl. in-slot addend, isData LOSSLESS; a multi-item value-correctness pin) + the STATIC-LINK STRUCTURAL witness (pull dispatches to the COFF reader -> merge strips the import) + **the DRIVER end-to-end NATIVE on Windows: DSS static-links `main` against a DSS COFF `.a` -> a self-contained PE exec -> RUN -> exit 42** (`CoffDriverStaticLinkExitsFortyTwo`, `_WIN32`-gated -- executes on the MSVC gate + windows-msvc CI leg, UNLIKE the WSL/macOS legs). 9 reader tests + 2 static-link tests; truncation fuzz + 4 corruption red-pins. | `D-LK-COFF-COMDAT-UNSUPPORTED-SELECTION` | ⏳ **OPEN -- trigger-gated (named 2026-07-22, TF-C53).** The TF-C53 COFF foreign-object reader (`src/link/format/coff_object_reader.cpp`, Gate 3) maps a kind-RESOLVED (code/data) COMDAT section's IMAGE_COMDAT_SELECT_* to the universal SymbolBinding: NODUPLICATES(1) -> Global (a duplicate fires K_SymbolRedefinedAcrossUnits -- the NODUPLICATES contract); ANY(2)/SAME_SIZE(3)/EXACT_MATCH(4) -> Weak (the existing all-weak merge dedup keeps one body + drops the shadow, ZERO merge change). LARGEST(6)/ASSOCIATIVE(5)/0/unknown on a code/data COMDAT FAIL LOUD (F_CorruptedBinary): lifting LARGEST to Weak would be a SILENT WRONG-SIZE MISCOMPILE (cuId is minted in PULL order, NOT size order -- compile_pipeline.cpp readArchiveMemberModule -- so weak lowest-key-wins can keep the SMALLER copy); ASSOCIATIVE ties a section's liveness to another section, which the symbol-atomic model does not represent. Both are C++ selectany/RTTI/vtable constructs essentially absent from C/SQLite. NOTE: an ASSOCIATIVE COMDAT on a kind-UNRESOLVED metadata section (a `/Gy` object's `.pdata`/`.xdata`, cl.exe-witnessed selection 5) is NOT this case -- it is skipped whole by Gate 2 (`D-LK-COFF-FOREIGN-UNWIND-DROP`), never reconstructs a body, so its selection is never read (Gate 3 is gated on `sec.kind.has_value()`). Trigger: static-linking a real C++ `.obj`/`.lib` whose vtable/RTTI/selectany data lands in a LARGEST or ASSOCIATIVE code/data COMDAT. Closing work: size-aware selection resolution that stays format-BLIND -- a reader-side pre-resolution (pick the largest / associated body before the merge module is minted) or a size tiebreak that never touches the pure cross_cu_resolve kernel. | Size-aware LARGEST/ASSOCIATIVE COMDAT selection (reader-side pre-resolution or a format-blind size tiebreak). | `D-LK-COFF-READER-FOREIGN-OBJECT` (TF-C53, the parent); `src/link/format/coff_object_reader.cpp` (Gate 3) |

A real cl.exe/clang-cl `.obj` can carry ANONYMOUS content owned by no symbol -- string literals / switch jump tables packed into a kind-RESOLVED `.rdata`/`.data` section with no defining symbol. There is NO COFF gap-atom analog to the ELF c167 reconstruction, so such a section that carries relocations but reconstructs NO atom FAILS LOUD (F_CorruptedBinary) in Gate 2's kind-RESOLVED arm -- never a silent drop. Trigger: static-linking a real `.obj` whose `.rdata`/`.data` holds anonymous reloc-bearing content (string-literal tables, `-O2` switch tables). | `D-LK-COFF-FOREIGN-UNWIND-DROP` | ⏳ **OPEN -- follow-up (named 2026-07-22, TF-C53).** The TF-C53 COFF reader SKIPS every kind-UNRESOLVED metadata section (`.pdata`/`.xdata`/`.debug$S`/`.debug$T`/`.drectve`/`.chks64`) AND its relocations (Gate 2) -- DSS's AssembledModule has no representation for SEH / x64 unwind, CodeView, or linker directives. So a foreign function's `.pdata`/`.xdata` unwind info is DROPPED: fine for a leaf / exit-42 link + for functions never unwound through, but a real correctness limitation for a foreign function that throws / is unwound (a stack-walking runtime cannot find its unwind info). The skip is gated on kind-UNRESOLVED, NOT the DISCARDABLE bit (`.pdata`/`.xdata` are 0x40000040, NOT discardable -- cl.exe-witnessed), so it fires whether or not the section is an ASSOCIATIVE COMDAT (a `/Gy` object marks them associative). Trigger: static-linking a real `.obj` whose functions require unwind (C++ EH, SEH, or a stack-walking runtime). Closing work: reconstruct `.pdata`/`.xdata` into a modeled unwind representation the PE EXEC writer re-emits (the RUNTIME_FUNCTION + UNWIND_INFO round-trip). | Reconstruct + re-emit foreign `.pdata`/`.xdata` SEH/unwind (currently skipped). | `D-LK-COFF-READER-FOREIGN-OBJECT` (TF-C53, Gate 2); `src/link/format/coff_object_reader.cpp` |
| `D-LK-STATIC-LINK` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-17 (c165) -- the STATIC-LINK, the FINALE of the static-lib arc + capability (2) of `D-FF1-AR-WRITER-STATIC-LINK` (ELF slice).** DSS links DYNAMICALLY today (import tables); c165 adds the STATIC path: on an unresolved extern, pull the defining member out of a `.a` and MERGE its code INTO the output image (a self-contained exec -- the definition is IN the exe, not a runtime DT_NEEDED). **THE DESIGN: static-linking IS the c154 cross-CU `mergeModules`, fed from archive members instead of sibling CUs** -- `main`'s `extern int dss_lib_answer` binds to the pulled `lib.o`'s definition EXACTLY as it binds a sibling TU. The flow (`pullStaticArchiveMembers` + `linkAndWriteWithStaticArchives`, `src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp`): (1) read each `.a` via the c161 `readArArchive` (armap symbol->member); (2) TWO-PASS LAZY-pull -- collect the client's unresolved extern names, resolve each through the global armap to its defining member, `bytes.subspan` the member + parse via the c164 `readRelocatableObject` -> an `AssembledModule` (a FRESH process-unique `CompilationUnitId` per member -- the merge keys by `(cuId,SymbolId)`), repeat until fixpoint (a pulled member's own externs feed the next pass); only REFERENCED members are pulled (never whole-archive); (3) `linker::link([client, members...])` -> the N>1 `mergeModules` binds each archive reference to its pulled definition + STRIPS the import. **DRIVER SURFACE: the c162 `--resolve-library` flag EXTENDED** -- `compileOneTarget` PARTITIONS the named binaries by MAGIC BYTES (`isArArchiveFile` = the `!<arch>\n` global magic, AGNOSTIC -- never a `.a`/`.lib` extension): an `ar` archive routes to the static pull+merge, a `.so`/`.dll`/`.dylib` stays on the c162 dynamic export-reader path (an unreadable path stays dynamic so its eager open-probe fails loud -- never a silent drop). The reader's `ok()` is a tautology for reader output (+ false for a data-only member), so the pull consumes the reader's `optional`, NOT `ok()`. Non-ELF `format` makes `readRelocatableObject` fail loud (the Mach-O/COFF slices are named follow-ups). **WITNESSED**: W1 DSS-written `libdsslib.a` (real compiled `lib.c`) -> static-link `main` via `--resolve-library` -> self-contained ELF exec (no dsslib DT_NEEDED; `dss_lib_answer` resolved not UND) -> RUN under WSL exit 42; W2 a 2-member `.a` where `main` references only member a.o -> ONLY a.o pulled (member b.o's `unused_symbol` absent from the pulled set -- the lazy pin); W3 a REAL gcc `libfoo.a` (`gcc -c` + `ar rcs`) -> DSS consumes the gcc `.o` member -> RUN exit 42. Red-on-disable: WITHOUT the pull, `dss_lib_answer` is left an unresolved import (elf-exec defers it as UND -> BUILDS but RUNS "symbol lookup error"; the run-42 comes ONLY from the pull+merge). 4 hermetic pins (`tests/program/test_static_link.cpp`: ar-magic dispatch by bytes-not-extension; pull resolves + merge strips the import [+ the alone red pin]; lazy-pull skips the unreferenced member; the driver end-to-end build + the __linux__ RUN=42 + red) + the DISABLED `RealLibcWitnessArtifactDrop` for the WSL cross-check. | (ELF closed c165; Mach-O/COFF ride the deferred relocatable-object readers) | plan 14 (linker) + plan 11 FF1; `src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp` (`pullStaticArchiveMembers`/`linkAndWriteWithStaticArchives`/`isArArchiveFile`) + `src/program/program.cpp` (`compileOneTarget` dispatch); `tests/program/test_static_link.cpp`; consumes `D-FF1-AR-READER` (c161) + `D-LK-RELOCATABLE-OBJECT-READER` (c164) + `D-LK-STATIC-ARCHIVE-WRITER` (c163); parent `D-FF1-AR-WRITER-STATIC-LINK` capability (2); follow-ups `D-LK-STATIC-LINK-SECTION-RELATIVE-RELOC` + `D-LK-RELOCATABLE-OBJECT-READER-{MACHO,COFF}` |
| `D-LK-STATIC-LINK-SECTION-RELATIVE-RELOC` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-17 (c167) -- real-distro `.a` static-link: SECTION-RELATIVE relocs resolved.** A REAL gcc member references string literals / jump tables / local objects via a SECTION symbol + addend (`R_X86_64_PC32 .rodata-4`) and packs ANONYMOUS content (no symbol) into `.rodata`; c165's leaf-`return 42` witness had none, so this was deferred. **§B DECISION (user, 2026-07-17): Option B -- symbol-atomic + synthetic gap atoms, resolved in the READER** (over Option A = section-atomic reconstruction, which would rewrite DSS's per-function pipeline). Resolved in `elf_object_reader.cpp` (NOT `mergeModules` as the original note guessed -- the reader is where the section->interval map lives, so the merge contract stays clean; the merge is UNCHANGED). TWO mechanisms: **(1) gap atoms (§6.5)** -- every maximal uncovered `[start,end)` region of a DECLARED data section (exact/prefix schema match, never the SHF_ALLOC flags fallback, so `.eh_frame` is NOT reconstructed) becomes a synthetic anonymous `AssembledData` (fresh SymbolId >= `numSyms`, collision-free, no ModuleSymbol -> module-private, never cross-CU folded); **(2) section-relative redirect (§7)** -- a reloc whose target is NOT a reconstructed atom but IS section-defined (a SECTION symbol / size-0 `.LC` marker) is redirected to the atom owning `sym.value + addend`. The RESIDUAL addend `bindBase - atom.start` is invariant under the redirect (preserves the apply formula `value = S + A + (pcRel?-P) + addendBias`). The interval-SEARCH offset differs: a code/absolute reference lands at `tsym.value + nativeAddend`; a DATA-section PC-relative SELF-reference (a `.rodata` jump-table entry, displacement relative to the TABLE base not RIP) lands at `+ addendBias - relInAtom` -- getting L right SELECTS the correct function in a multi-function `.text`. **PLUS a WRITER extension (`exec_data_section.hpp`, shared ELF/PE/Mach-O): `applyDataItemRelocations` now applies LINEAR pc-relative data fixups** (a jump-table `rel32` self-offset table), not just absolute pointers -- `value = S + A + (pcRel?-P) + addendBias`; a pc-relative data fixup needs NO base-reloc (invariant under rebase); the absolute path is byte-identical (addendBias=0 there). A signed-fit tripwire (silent-failure-review fold, matching the instruction kernel) fails loud on a narrow fixup outside range instead of truncating. **WITNESSED**: reader units (a `const char*` string -> anonymous `.rodata` gap atom, reference redirected residual 0; a 2-function `.text` [`lib_pad`@0 + `lib_answer`@0x19] jump table -> 24-byte gap atom + 6 interior entries SELECTING lib_answer not lib_pad + 2 lea-of-table refs -> the gap atom) + END-TO-END: a REAL gcc 2-function jump-table `.o` bundled into a DSS-written `.a`, static-linked via `--resolve-library`, RUN on native Linux x86_64 -> **exit 42** (the whole chain: gcc `.o` -> ar-writer -> ar-reader -> section-relative reader resolution -> merge -> exec emit w/ pc-rel data fixup -> run). Red-on-disable: revert the redirect and the reader fails loud on the jump table -> the static link never builds. Independent design-audit (§B) + 2 independent code-reviews (correctness + silent-failure) VERIFIED-CLEAN, 2 folds landed (the signed-fit guard + the 2-function selection fixture). Boundaries named below. | (closed c167 -- section-relative resolved in the reader + pc-rel data writer) | `D-LK-STATIC-LINK` (the c165 link this extends); `D-LK-RELOCATABLE-OBJECT-READER` (c164); `src/link/format/elf_object_reader.cpp` (gap atoms + redirect) + `src/link/format/exec_data_section.hpp` (pc-rel data fixup); follow-ups `D-LK-STATIC-LINK-INTRASECTION-BAKED-CALL` + `D-LK-STATIC-LINK-PCREL-MULTIATOM-DATA` |
| `D-LK-STATIC-LINK-INTRASECTION-BAKED-CALL` | ⏳ **OPEN -- DEFERRED (named 2026-07-17, c167).** A gcc `.o` built WITHOUT `-ffunction-sections` puts multiple functions in ONE `.text` and BAKES an intra-`.text` call as a reloc-LESS `E8 rel32` (the distance is resolved at assembly time -- see the c167 probe `callee.c`: `compute` calls `helper` with NO `.rela.text` entry). DSS reconstructs each STT_FUNC as a SEPARATE atom (symbol-atomic model) and re-lays them out; if the inter-function distance changes, the baked rel32 is silently WRONG (no reloc to fix it). This is a PRE-EXISTING symbol-atomic boundary (since c165), NOT a c167 regression -- c167's section-relative work is orthogonal (relocs, not baked calls). It cannot be cheaply fail-loud-detected: DSS's OWN `.o` also has multiple functions per `.text` but emits RELOCS for cross-function calls (safe), so function-count alone can't distinguish safe-DSS from unsafe-gcc-no-funcsections without disassembly. **Real distro `.a` is built WITH `-ffunction-sections`** (each fn in `.text.<fn>`, so cross-fn calls ALWAYS carry relocs) -- the realistic path, which c167 handles. The c167/c165 witnesses use single-jump-table + independent (non-calling) 2-function objects, so no baked intra-call is exercised. Closing work: either (a) preserve original inter-function layout for a shared-`.text` member (a section-atomic sub-model for `.text`), or (b) a disassembly-based baked-rel32 detector that fails loud. | Handle (or fail loud on) baked reloc-less intra-`.text` calls in a non-function-sections multi-function member. | `D-LK-STATIC-LINK-SECTION-RELATIVE-RELOC` (c167); the symbol-atomic reconstruction in `src/link/format/elf_object_reader.cpp` |
| `D-LK-STATIC-LINK-PCREL-MULTIATOM-DATA` | ⏳ **OPEN -- DEFERRED (named 2026-07-17, c167).** The c167 data-section PC-relative search-offset (`searchOff = bindBase + addendBias - relInAtom`, elf_object_reader.cpp §7) assumes the reference base is the containing DATA ATOM's base (true for a gcc `switch` jump table -- one contiguous `.rodata` table = one gap atom). A PC-relative data reference whose base is a DIFFERENT slot than the atom it lives in (a hypothetical multi-atom PIC data section where entries relocate against a base not equal to their own atom's start) would mis-compute the search offset. gcc does NOT emit this for the string/switch constructs in c167's scope; a mis-land FAILS LOUD (`F_CorruptedBinary` -- lands in no reconstructed atom) rather than silently corrupting. Trigger: a real member with a multi-atom PC-relative data section whose reference base != atom base. Closing work: carry the reference base explicitly (or detect the multi-atom case) instead of assuming atom-base. | Handle a PC-relative data reference whose base != its containing atom's base (currently fails loud). | `D-LK-STATIC-LINK-SECTION-RELATIVE-RELOC` (c167); `src/link/format/elf_object_reader.cpp` (the §7 searchOff) |
| `D-LK-INTERNAL-LINKAGE-FN-EMITTED-GLOBAL-FOREIGN-COLLISION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-23 (TF-C54) -- internal-linkage (`static`/synthesized) DEFINED functions + data now emit LOCAL binding in EVERY relocatable object, byte-coupled to the name carve-out.** THE FIX: a shared, format-neutral `ObjectSymbolNames::definedBinding(id)` (`object_symbol_names.hpp`) runs the IDENTICAL lookup + predicate as `definedName` (`!name.empty() && isExternallyVisible(binding,visibility)`) -> the real binding (Global/Weak) for an externally-visible named def, `SymbolBinding::Local` otherwise (static, synthesized rodata/thunk, interior block, nameless-substrate) -- so a `sym_<id>`-named symbol is ALWAYS Local and can never drift from its name. Each writer maps that ONE decision to its own vocabulary -- **ELF** `stbForBinding` (STB_LOCAL/WEAK/GLOBAL) + a LOCAL-then-GLOBAL two-pass with `.symtab.sh_info`=first-non-local (ELF ABI); **COFF** IMAGE_SYM_CLASS_STATIC vs EXTERNAL; **Mach-O** bare N_SECT vs N_SECT\|N_EXT -- no `if(format)` in the shared helper. ET_EXEC is BYTE-IDENTICAL (`definedFuncBinding` forces Global when `isExec`; ET_EXEC emits no data symbols here) -> the DSS-linked sqlite pe64/x86_64 execs are trivially preserved. WEAK defined symbols: ELF emits STB_WEAK natively; the PE/COFF + Mach-O RELOCATABLE writers FAIL LOUD (`K_NoMatchingObjectFormat`, [[D-LK-OBJECT-WEAK-DEF-RELOCATABLE]]) rather than silently degrade to strong (weak-external / N_WEAK_DEF unwired -- the [[D-LK3-DYLIB-WEAK-EXPORT]] precedent). WITNESSED: the hermetic cross-platform 2-TU writer->reader->merge pin `TfC54TwoTusCollidingStaticsReadBackLocalMergeClean` reproduces the foreign multi-TU collision fix (colliding Local `sym_<id>` kept module-private -> no `K_SymbolRedefinedAcrossUnits`); per-format writer pins -- ELF all-4-forms `ObjectSymtabCouplesNameAndBindingLocalStaticBeforeGlobalWeak` + PE/Mach-O func AND data static-flip + weak-fail-loud -- all red-on-disable (the PE static-DATA flip demonstrated empirically by reverting the ternary). The real x86_64 sqlite FOREIGN multi-TU link (sqlite3.o + shell.o via gcc) LINKS (was `ld: multiple definition` xthousands) + runs -> 3.54.0 + CRUD 42 (witnessed the prior iteration; `elf.cpp` byte-identical since -- the code-audit fold touched only pe/macho). Gate: 3-leg GREEN -- Win build-dbg 727/727 (4 examples-runner contention flakes, all pass isolated -- [[D-TEST-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-PARALLEL-CONTENTION-FLAKE]]) + WSL native `build` 727/727 (x86_64 + arm64-qemu) + independent code-audit VERIFIED-CLEAN (2 folds: PE/Mach-O DATA-site pins F1; corrected a Global+Hidden "unfired" rationale F2) + anchor guard 691. FOLLOW-UPS opened: [[D-LK-OBJECT-WEAK-DEF-RELOCATABLE]], [[D-LK-OBJECT-GLOBAL-HIDDEN-VISIBILITY-EMITTED-LOCAL]], [[D-LK-OBJECT-STV-PROTECTED-DROPPED]], [[D-LK-MACHO-STB-LOCAL-LD64-RUNTIME-WITNESS]], and [[D-LK-ARM64-SQLITE-FOREIGN-LINKED-RUNTIME-FAULT]] (the arm64 link now CLEARS but the linked binary RUNTIME-FAULTS under qemu -- the "MORE blockers surface" outcome this row anticipated; x86_64 is clean end-to-end). -- *Original (audit trail):* ⏳ **OPEN -- named 2026-07-22 (arm64 sqlite validation, prompted by the user's "did you run all sqlite units on arm64" check).** DSS emits INTERNAL-LINKAGE (`static`) functions into the relocatable `.o` with **STB_GLOBAL** binding + a synthetic **per-TU** name `sym_<counter>`. This is INVISIBLE to DSS's OWN linker (it resolves by `(cuId, SymbolId)`, not by name — the sqlite-green arc was DSS-internally-linked), but a FOREIGN linker resolves by NAME: two separately-compiled DSS TUs each restart the counter, so `sqlite3.o` and `shell.o` BOTH define `sym_3774` (etc.) as GLOBAL → `ld: multiple definition of sym_3774` ×thousands (sqlite3.o has **2298 GLOBAL `sym_NNNN` FUNC** + 271 real-named GLOBAL = the `sqlite3_*` public API). EMPIRICAL: DSS → sqlite3.c + shell.c → arm64 `.o` (clean, rc=0) → `aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc` DEFAULT-PIE link → **rc=1, multiple-definition**. ★ NOT the [[D-LK-ARM64-EXTERN-DATA-ADDR-PIE-GOT]] reloc issue — that change is CORRECT/orthogonal (the 2406 `ADD_ABS` relocs target LOCAL section symbols [fine under PIE]; the 1 GOT reloc fired for the address-taken-extern-value). So the GOT change was NECESSARY-BUT-NOT-SUFFICIENT for the arm64 (or any) FOREIGN multi-TU sqlite link. NOT arm64-specific (breaks x86_64 foreign multi-TU link identically). Distinct from [[D-LINK-LOCAL-FN-ADDR-STATIC-DATA-VA0]] (that was DSS's INTERNAL merge folding Local↔Global; this is the `.o` EMISSION binding). Trigger FIRED (arm64 sqlite foreign-link). Closing work: emit internal-linkage functions/data as **STB_LOCAL** in the relocatable-object symtab (`src/link/format/elf.cpp` ET_REL path — the binding map has a `Weak?STB_WEAK:STB_GLOBAL` shape with no `Local→STB_LOCAL` arm; + segregate locals before globals + set `sh_info` = first-global index; the front-end already carries `SymbolBinding::Local` for statics per the TF-C45 merge work) — agnostic (a `SymbolBinding::Local` predicate, no source/target/format branch), then RE-RUN the arm64 sqlite foreign-link to see if the link clears or MORE blockers surface. Verify DSS-INTERNAL linking (cuId+symId keyed) is byte-unaffected. | Emit internal-linkage (static) fns/data STB_LOCAL in the relocatable `.o` (currently STB_GLOBAL → foreign multi-TU `sym_NNNN` collision). | [[D-LK-ARM64-EXTERN-DATA-ADDR-PIE-GOT]] (the sibling foreign-link enabler this UNBLOCKS-FURTHER); [[D-LINK-LOCAL-FN-ADDR-STATIC-DATA-VA0]] (the internal-merge Local sibling); `src/link/format/elf.cpp` (ET_REL symtab binding) + `src/mir/merge/mir_merge.cpp` (Local handling) |
| `D-LK-OBJECT-GLOBAL-HIDDEN-VISIBILITY-EMITTED-LOCAL` | ⏳ **OPEN -- named 2026-07-23 (TF-C54 design-audit).** `ObjectSymbolNames::definedBinding` (TF-C54) reuses the shared `isExternallyVisible` predicate, so a Global-binding + **Hidden**-visibility defined symbol resolves to Local -> the ET_REL / COFF / Mach-O writers emit it **STB_LOCAL** (bare N_SECT / IMAGE_SYM_CLASS_STATIC). gcc keeps it STB_GLOBAL + STV_HIDDEN (still resolvable during a STATIC link by name; only hidden from the dynamic export), so a cross-TU reference to a hidden-but-global symbol would fail to bind under DSS. UNFIRED AT THE RELOCATABLE WRITER: the c-subset CAN form a Global+Hidden symbol (`__attribute__((visibility("hidden")))` on a NON-static fn -- e.g. `examples/c-subset/attributes_syntax`'s `dead_helper`), but none is routed to a relocatable writer LIVE (it is DCE-eliminated as an unused hidden helper, or reaches an EXEC writer where `isExec` forces Global). `definedName` ALREADY carved such a symbol's NAME to `sym_<id>` pre-TF-C54 (this cycle only makes the BINDING match the already-hidden name -- consistent, not a regression). [design-audit F2 2026-07-23: corrected the earlier "c-subset never forms Global+Hidden" rationale, which was factually wrong.] Closing work: a coupled name+binding STV_HIDDEN emission (real name + STB_GLOBAL + st_other=STV_HIDDEN) when binding==Global && visibility==Hidden. | Emit a Global+Hidden def as STB_GLOBAL+STV_HIDDEN (real name) instead of STB_LOCAL. | [[D-LK-INTERNAL-LINKAGE-FN-EMITTED-GLOBAL-FOREIGN-COLLISION]] (the TF-C54 fix that surfaced it); `src/link/format/object_symbol_names.hpp` (`definedBinding`, reuses `isExternallyVisible`) |
| `D-LK-OBJECT-STV-PROTECTED-DROPPED` | ⏳ **OPEN -- named 2026-07-23 (TF-C54 design-audit), PRE-EXISTING (NOT introduced by TF-C54).** The ET_REL `.symtab` emitter passes `st_other`=STV_DEFAULT (0) unconditionally (every `appendSym(..., other=0, ...)`), so a Global + **Protected**-visibility defined symbol loses its STV_PROTECTED marking (which would pin references within the defining component to the local definition even under interposition). `isExternallyVisible` treats Global+Protected as visible, so its NAME + Global binding DO round-trip post-TF-C54, but the st_other byte is dropped. UNFIRED in the c-subset (no protected-visibility construct). Out of scope for the TF-C54 binding fix; named as a known symtab-attribute boundary. Closing work: thread `SymbolVisibility` -> st_other (STV_HIDDEN / STV_PROTECTED) at the symtab emit sites, coupled to the same `ObjectSymbolNames` decision. | Emit st_other from the symbol's `SymbolVisibility` (currently hardcoded STV_DEFAULT). | [[D-LK-INTERNAL-LINKAGE-FN-EMITTED-GLOBAL-FOREIGN-COLLISION]] (sibling symtab-attribute gap surfaced alongside); `src/link/format/elf.cpp` (ET_REL `appendSym` st_other) |
| `D-LK-OBJECT-WEAK-DEF-RELOCATABLE` | ⏳ **OPEN -- named 2026-07-23 (TF-C54 code-audit fold).** The PE + Mach-O RELOCATABLE writers cannot faithfully emit a WEAK DEFINED symbol. `ObjectSymbolNames::definedBinding` (TF-C54) returns `SymbolBinding::Weak` for an externally-visible `__attribute__((weak))` def (`c-subset.lang.json:1483,1492` maps `weak` -> Weak; witnesses `examples/c-subset/weak_inline_crosscu` + `attributes_syntax`'s `weak_helper`), but neither relocatable writer wires the weak machinery: **COFF** needs `IMAGE_SYM_CLASS_WEAK_EXTERNAL` + its weak-external aux record (tagging the default resolution); **Mach-O** needs `N_WEAK_DEF` (n_desc) + `MH_WEAK_DEFINES`. Pre-fold both SILENTLY degraded a weak def to strong (PE `IMAGE_SYM_CLASS_EXTERNAL` at pe.cpp ~:1220; Mach-O `N_SECT|N_EXT` at macho.cpp ~:1357), losing the weak-override semantics with no diagnostic. TF-C54 code-audit fold: both relocatable writers now **FAIL LOUD** (`K_NoMatchingObjectFormat`, citing this anchor) at the defined-symbol emit site (function + data) when `definedBinding == Weak` -- the best-long-term interim, mirroring the project's own Mach-O MH_DYLIB export arm (`macho.cpp:2980`, `D-LK3-DYLIB-WEAK-EXPORT`), rather than half-implement weak-external. RELOCATABLE-ONLY: the EXEC/DLL arms (PE `encodeExec`; Mach-O `encodeExec`/`encodeExecDynamic`) are UNAFFECTED -- `encode` dispatches to them before the emit loops, and a weak def is merge-resolved strong-over-weak / forced Global before an image writer sees it (verified: `weak_inline_crosscu` + `attributes_syntax` pe64/macho EXEC arms still build+run 42; the fail-loud does NOT fire). ELF is already CORRECT (Weak -> STB_WEAK via `stbForBinding`; pinned `test_elf_writer` `weak_fn` -> 0x22). UNFIRED in the c-subset relocatable path today (weak defs only reach EXEC). Trigger: a weak DEFINED symbol routed to a PE/Mach-O relocatable `.o` (a foreign multi-TU link input). Closing work = the FULL machinery so a weak def round-trips a relocatable object with its weak semantics intact: **COFF** `IMAGE_SYM_CLASS_WEAK_EXTERNAL` + the weak-external aux record; **Mach-O** `N_WEAK_DEF` + `MH_WEAK_DEFINES`. Red-on-disable per format: `test_pe_writer` `ObjectWeakDefinedFunctionFailsLoud` + `test_macho_writer` `ObjectWeakDefinedFunctionFailsLoud` (revert the fail-loud arm -> the writer silently emits EXTERNAL/N_EXT -> the pin goes red). | Emit a weak DEFINED symbol faithfully to a PE/Mach-O relocatable object (COFF weak-external / Mach-O N_WEAK_DEF); TODAY it fails loud. | [[D-LK-INTERNAL-LINKAGE-FN-EMITTED-GLOBAL-FOREIGN-COLLISION]] (the TF-C54 `definedBinding` this rides on); the ELF sibling (already STB_WEAK, `src/link/format/elf.cpp` `stbForBinding`); the DYLIB precedent (`src/link/format/macho.cpp:2980`, `D-LK3-DYLIB-WEAK-EXPORT`); `src/link/format/pe.cpp` + `src/link/format/macho.cpp` (relocatable func/data emit sites) |
| `D-TEST-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-PARALLEL-CONTENTION-FLAKE` | ⏳ **OPEN, trigger-gated -- named 2026-07-23 (TF-C54 gate; recurring since >=TF-C51).** Under a PARALLEL full `ctest -j4` on the Windows leg, examples-runner entries (each spawns a compiled example subprocess with a 5s timeout) intermittently FAIL on load-timing -- distinct entries each occurrence (TF-C51: 2; the TF-C54 iterations: `deep_expr_lower`, then `bare_int_condition`, then 4-at-once right after a WSL-gate teardown), EVERY one passing on an ISOLATED re-run, and the same tree 727/727 on the WSL leg. NOT a product defect (the examples are EXEC-path front-end/codegen witnesses; the flaking set is uncorrelated with the diff under test) -- a HARNESS timing fragility: subprocess spawn + 5s wall-clock timeout under heavy parallel load. Operational protocol (memory + skill): run the Win ctest ALONE (never concurrent with a WSL gate) + re-run any examples fail ISOLATED to discriminate flake-vs-regression. This row makes the recurring pattern REGISTRY-VISIBLE (§A.7) instead of per-cycle folklore. Trigger: a CI leg (which runs examples serially today) starts hitting it, OR the local flake rate materially disrupts gates. Closing work: make the runner robust under parallel load -- e.g. a load-scaled/generous spawn timeout, ctest RESOURCE_LOCK/serialization for examples entries, or a retry-once-with-loud-tag arm (design choice at closure; a silent auto-retry that could mask a REAL intermittent regression is NOT acceptable per §A.4). | Make examples-runner ctest entries robust under parallel load (today: run alone + isolated re-run protocol). | `tests/examples/examples_runner.cpp` + `integrated_tests/runner.cpp` (the two runners); [[D-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-CLI-SUBPROCESS-NESTED-DEPENDSON]] (where the 5s-timeout masking bit before); the dss-cycle/memory operational protocol |
