| `D-LK-ARM64-SQLITE-FOREIGN-LINKED-RUNTIME-FAULT` | ⏳ **OPEN -- named 2026-07-23 (TF-C54 witness), the "MORE blockers surface" outcome the TF-C54 anchor anticipated.** With TF-C54 the arm64 sqlite FOREIGN multi-TU link now CLEARS (rc=0, no `multiple definition`), but the resulting `aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc`-linked, default-PIE `sqlite3` **faults at RUNTIME under `qemu-aarch64`**: `sqlite3 --version` / `sqlite3 db "SQL"` (arg mode) exit rc=0 with EMPTY stdout; `echo "SELECT 40+2;" | sqlite3` (interactive/stdin) SEGFAULTS (signal 11). ORTHOGONAL to TF-C54 (which changes ONLY the `.symtab` st_info binding byte, never machine code / relocations): the **x86_64** build from the IDENTICAL DSS + defines + the IDENTICAL 1491 now-LOCAL `sym_` funcs RUNS PERFECTLY NATIVELY (`--version` -> `3.54.0`, CRUD -> `42`), and a trivial DSS-arm64 `hello.c` runs correctly under the same qemu (`hello-42`, rc=7) -- so the fault is arm64-sqlite-specific codegen/relocation/runtime, NOT the symbol-binding fix and NOT a general DSS-arm64 stdio gap. Trigger FIRED (TF-C54 unblocked the link, exposing this). Closing work: root-cause the arm64 runtime fault from the CODE (candidates: an arm64 PIE relocation / CALL26 range-thunk edge on the full-scale `.text`, or an arm64 codegen construct sqlite exercises that x86_64 does not) -- a SEPARATE cycle. | Root-cause + fix the arm64 (qemu) sqlite runtime segfault now that the foreign link clears (x86_64 is clean). | [[D-LK-INTERNAL-LINKAGE-FN-EMITTED-GLOBAL-FOREIGN-COLLISION]] (the TF-C54 link fix that surfaced it -- its row explicitly anticipated "MORE blockers surface"); [[D-LK-ARM64-EXTERN-DATA-ADDR-PIE-GOT]] (the sibling arm64 PIE reloc enabler) |
| `D-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-MULTI-ARTIFACT` | ✅ **CLOSED c171 (2026-07-17).** The examples_runner now expresses a two-artifact DEPENDENT build via a PER-TARGET `dependsOn` manifest schema (`DependsOnArtifact{sources, spec, artifact, multiCu}` on `ExampleTarget`): `compileAndRunArm` builds each prerequisite LIBRARY artifact FIRST (into the arm's fresh scratch out dir, with the SAME strict compile-side checks + fail-loud belts), then threads their output paths into the dependent build's `--resolve-library` (`Program::setResolveLibraries`). PER-TARGET (not per-example) so ONE example expresses the round-trip on each platform with a matching library format (a PE `.lib` for the pe64 target, an ELF `.a` for the elf64 target). FIRST corpus example `examples/c-subset/staticlib_resolve_roundtrip`: `dsslib.c` (exporting `dss_lib_answer`) built as a STATIC library (a `.lib` on pe64 / a `.a` on elf64 via the c171-D `-staticlib` format), `main.c` (extern `dss_lib_answer`) RESOLVED against it -> the c169/c170 COFF (or c165 ELF) static-link pulls the member into a SELF-CONTAINED exec -> exit 42. The PE arm RUNS NATIVELY on the Windows gate (witnessed exit 42); the ELF arm builds (structural cross-compile) here + RUNS on the ubuntu leg / under WSL (SkippedCrossHost on Windows). Triple-witnesses, at the corpus tier + end-to-end: the c171 dependsOn runner capability, the c171-D staticlib driver surface, and the static-link pull/merge. ADDITIVE: `dependsOn` defaults empty -> every pre-c171 example is byte-identical. — ORIGINAL (c162): ⏳ trigger-gated. The `examples_runner` (`tests/examples/examples_runner.cpp`) is SINGLE-ARTIFACT-per-target: one example dir -> one artifact per target, then run. It cannot express a two-artifact DEPENDENT build (build artifact 1 = the library, then build artifact 2 = main RESOLVING against artifact 1) -- exactly the shape the c162 reader-consumer round-trip needs. c162 therefore ships the witness as an INTEGRATION TEST (`tests/program/test_ffi_resolve_library.cpp`, drives `Program::compileFiles` twice) rather than a corpus entry. Trigger: 2nd dependent-build witness wanting corpus expression, OR a decision to make the round-trip a first-class corpus example for the cross-target CI matrix. Closing work: a manifest `dependsOn`/multi-artifact schema (build the named prerequisite artifact first, thread its output path into the dependent build's `--resolve-library`). | Extend the examples manifest with a dependent-artifact (`dependsOn` + `resolveLibrary`) schema + runner support. | `D-FF1-READER-CONSUMER` (the witness that needs it); `tests/examples/examples_runner.cpp` |
| `D-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-NESTED-DEPENDSON` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-22 (corpus backfill) — the examples-runner `dependsOn` is now RECURSIVE, so a prerequisite LIBRARY build can itself resolve another library.** ★ **SCOPE-CORRECTED 2026-07-22 (fat_archive_merge fix, see [[D-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-CLI-SUBPROCESS-NESTED-DEPENDSON]]):** this closure landed the recursion in the **IN-PROCESS** runner (`tests/examples/examples_runner.cpp`) ONLY; the **CLI-subprocess** sibling `integrated_tests/runner.cpp` (the `integrated_tests` ctest entry) was MISSED, so it silently dropped the nested `input.lib` dep and built `fatlib.lib` WITHOUT the merge → `fat_archive_merge` pe64 was RED in-harness (exit `0xC0000139` = merged `dss_input_answer` unresolved at load; the FULL suite masked it as a 5s spawn timeout). The "pe64-native fat-archive → 42" gate evidence below came from the in-process runner / a manual CLI run, NOT `integrated_tests`. Parity added + this gap closed by the fat_archive_merge fix cycle. Extends [[D-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-MULTI-ARTIFACT]] (c171, single-level): `DependsOnArtifact` gains a recursive `std::vector<DependsOnArtifact> dependsOn`; a factored `parseDependsOnEntry` recursively parses it + `buildDependencyArtifact` builds nested deps FIRST into the same scratch out dir, threads their resolved PATHS into THAT dep's own `--resolve-library` (`setResolveLibraries`), THEN builds the dep. EMPTY nested `dependsOn` = byte-identical to the single-level path (every pre-existing example unchanged); fully generic (no example/arch/format special-casing). NEEDED to witness the FAT-ARCHIVE MERGE at the corpus tier: `examples/c-subset/fat_archive_merge` builds `input.a` → `fatlib.a` RESOLVING `input.a` (THE MERGE — fatlib.a now carries both `dss_fat_extra` + the merged `dss_input_answer`) → `main` resolves ONLY `fatlib.a` → RUN → exit 42 (the merged member is reachable ONLY through the merge). RED-ON-DISABLE on BOTH gates: (BUILD) revert the merge arm → `D_StaticLibFatArchiveUnsupported` fail-loud → `fatlib.a` rc≠0 → red; (RUN) a silent no-op merge → `dss_input_answer` undefined → exit 127≠42 → red (the discriminator is the RUNTIME exit, because [[D-LINK-EXEC-UNDEFINED-SYMBOL-FAIL-LOUD]] is OPEN — DSS does not fail-loud on an undefined EXEC symbol). Also backfilled `examples/c-subset/extern_fn_direct_call` (the TF-C50 direct-extern-call witness). Gate: WSL full examples 520/520 (×2) + both new examples green (x86_64 native + arm64-qemu + pe64-native fat-archive → 42) + independent code-audit VERIFIED-CLEAN (backward-compat / build-order / no-masking / witness-real / recursion-safe / agnostic-portable). | (closed — recursive dependsOn + 2 corpus backfills) | [[D-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-MULTI-ARTIFACT]] (the c171 single-level parent); [[D-FF1-STATICLIB-FAT-ARCHIVE]] (TF-C51, the fat-archive merge it witnesses); [[D-LK-ARM64-ELF-RELOC-EXTERN-DISPATCH]] (TF-C50, extern_fn_direct_call); `tests/examples/examples_runner.cpp` |
| `D-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-CLI-SUBPROCESS-NESTED-DEPENDSON` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-22 (fat_archive_merge fix) — the CLI-subprocess runner `integrated_tests/runner.cpp` now MIRRORS the in-process runner's recursive nested `dependsOn`, closing the [[D-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-NESTED-DEPENDSON]] scope gap.** THE BUG (pre-existing, NOT a compiler miscompile — root-caused by a git-stash test proving the TF-C53 COFF reader is exonerated): commit ac758736 added recursive `dependsOn` to the IN-PROCESS runner (`tests/examples/examples_runner.cpp`) but MISSED the CLI-subprocess sibling `integrated_tests/runner.cpp` — whose `DependsOnArtifact` had no nested `dependsOn` field. So the `integrated_tests` ctest entry (#2) SILENTLY DROPPED `fat_archive_merge`'s nested `input.lib` dependency, built `fatlib.lib` WITHOUT the fat-archive MERGE, and left `main.exe` with `dss_input_answer` unresolved → runtime `STATUS_ENTRYPOINT_NOT_FOUND` (0xC0000139), masked as a 5s spawn TIMEOUT in the full suite. The shipped compiler + the fat-archive merge feature were CORRECT the whole time (the CLI user-path `--resolve-library` → `main.exe` → 42, `dumpbin` shows only `kernel32!ExitProcess`); ONLY the test harness dropped the merge input. THE FIX: a recursive `std::vector<DependsOnArtifact> dependsOn` on `DependsOnArtifact` + a factored `parseDependsOnEntry` (recursive parse) + `buildDependsOnArtifactCli` (builds nested prerequisites FIRST into the same out dir, threads their paths into THIS dep's `--resolve-library`, recurses to arbitrary depth) — a faithful mirror of the in-process `buildDependencyArtifact`. EMPTY nested `dependsOn` = byte-identical to the pre-fix single-level command (every existing example unchanged; the CLI accumulates `--resolve-library`, not last-wins). Fully generic (no example/arch/format special-casing). WITNESS: `fat_archive_merge` pe64 in `integrated_tests` → **exit 42** (12 passed / 0 failed; two `dependsOn library … built` lines — input.lib THEN fatlib.lib — vs one before). PLUS a white-box regression pin `PeFatArchiveSequentialInProcessMergeExecRunsFortyTwo` (`tests/program/test_static_link.cpp`): three sequential in-process `Program` builds, asserts the intermediate `fatlib.lib` armap is the UNION `{dss_fat_extra, dss_input_answer}` (the decisive merge witness the corpus example only asserts transitively via exit code) + runs main → 42 (_WIN32). RED-ON-DISABLE: reverting the runner fix → `fat_archive_merge` red in-harness (0xC0000139); restoring the `D_StaticLibFatArchiveUnsupported` fail-loud → the pin's step-2 build rc≠0. Independent code-audit VERIFIED-CLEAN (faithful-mirror / byte-exact backward-compat / recursion-safe / build-command / agnostic / pin-strict-and-new). | (closed — CLI-subprocess runner parity + white-box PE fat-merge pin) | [[D-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-NESTED-DEPENDSON]] (the in-process sibling this achieves parity with); [[D-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-MULTI-ARTIFACT]] (c171); [[D-FF1-STATICLIB-FAT-ARCHIVE]] (the merge it witnesses); [[D-LINK-EXEC-UNDEFINED-SYMBOL-FAIL-LOUD]] (OPEN — why the dropped-merge surfaced at runtime not link time); `integrated_tests/runner.cpp` + `tests/program/test_static_link.cpp` |
| `D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-PLATFORM-SELECT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-09 — DISSOLVED by Model 3** (the `#include puts("hello")` 3-OS string-FFI capstone cycle). The per-platform-dir SELECTION problem this anchor tracked no longer exists: the 15 per-platform `shippedLibs/<os-arch>/<h>.json` descriptors were COLLAPSED into 5 **NEUTRAL** `shippedLibs/<h>.json`, each carrying platform-neutral signatures + a per-format **`library` MAP** `{pe,elf,macho}`; `shippedLibDirs` became the single neutral root `["shippedLibs"]`. The map is threaded target-AGNOSTICALLY through the front-end (descriptor→`ShippedExternSymbol`→`HirExternRecord`) and resolved to ONE library string per-target LATE, at `compile_pipeline.cpp` (via `objectFormatKindName(format.kind())`; missing key → `externLibraryByFormat` fallback; unknown key → fail loud) — so there is no longer a *directory* to pick by active target, and the front-end stays target-blind. This is **dissolution, NOT the registry's hinted per-dir platform-predicate remediation** (that fix is no longer needed — the platform axis moved INTO the neutral descriptor as the format-keyed library map). Cite: the Model-3 §B decision (plan-00 §0.2). — ORIGINAL trigger/context retained below. FF11 (2026-06-05, cycle 18+; was STILL OPEN after v0.0.2 V2-2 2026-06-06): the angle-form `#include <h>` system search path (`SemanticConfig.shippedLibDirs`) lists its platform EXPLICITLY (c-subset ships `["shippedLibs/windows-x86_64"]`, the only dir). Trigger = the FIRST time a SECOND platform's shipped-lib descriptors land (e.g. a `shippedLibs/linux-x86_64/` with a libc-flavored `stdio.json` descriptor) AND a single c-subset source must compile to BOTH — the resolver would then need to PICK the dir matching the active target (`ObjectFormatKind` / OS / arch) instead of searching every declared dir. Today: `applySystemDirs` (program.cpp) resolves EVERY `shippedLibDirs` entry and the resolver searches all of them, so a Windows + Linux shipped pair would both be on the path (first-match wins → wrong-platform descriptor could shadow). Priority: **normal** (the single-dir path is correct for the Win64-only corpus). UPDATE (2026-06-08, shippedLibs build-out): the `linux-x86_64` + `macos-arm64` descriptor sets NOW EXIST (stdio/stdlib/string/ctype/math each, all decode-validated by `AllShippedDescriptorsDecode`) — so the trigger's "a SECOND platform's descriptors land" half HAS fired. But they are STAGED data only: c-subset `shippedLibDirs` still lists ONLY `["shippedLibs/windows-x86_64"]`, so nothing is on the path to shadow and no source compiles to a non-Windows target yet. The REMAINING (unfired) trigger half is purely the resolver needing to SELECT the dir by active target. NOTE: V2-2 (2026-06-06) replaced the c-subset-source `stdio.h` with the neutral JSON descriptor (`D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-DESCRIPTOR-AGNOSTIC` ✅); the platform-select mechanism is unchanged. | Add a per-`shippedLibDirs`-entry platform predicate (e.g. `{ "dir": "shippedLibs/linux-x86_64", "format": "elf", "os": "linux" }`) OR derive the platform subdir from the active target spec at `applySystemDirs` time; the resolver then searches only the matching dir(s). Keep it config-driven (the language declares the platform axis; the engine selects) — NO `if (format == pe)` in the resolver. Pin with two shipped dirs (win + linux) each holding a same-named descriptor with a DISCRIMINATING extern, compiled to each target, asserting the right one resolved. | plan 11 FFI (FF11 angle-include); pairs with D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH (both are "config baked an ABI/platform decision" anchors); D-CAP-MARKER-MULTI-TARGET-E2E-PIN; D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-DESCRIPTOR-AGNOSTIC (the descriptor that replaced the `.h`) |
| `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VARIADIC-SIGNATURE` | A shipped-lib descriptor (`shippedLibs/<platform>/*.json`, V2-2 2026-06-06) needs a VARIADIC function (e.g. `printf`) OR a signature form the shared `parseTypeFromText` codec does not yet accept. Today the hir-text `fn(...) -> ...` type grammar parses the param list + a trailing `cc` but has NO variadic marker, while the `FnSig` `isVariadic` scalar EXISTS in the IR but is not spellable in type-text — so a descriptor cannot express `printf`'s `(ptr<char>, ...)`. Priority: **normal** (the shipped surface [stdio/stdlib/string/ctype/math, broadened 2026-06-08] is ALL non-variadic BY DESIGN — `printf`/`fprintf`/`scanf`/`sscanf`/`snprintf` and the rest of the variadic family are deliberately EXCLUDED from the descriptors and documented as such in `shippedLibs/README.md`; a variadic-needing descriptor signature fails LOUD via `F_ShippedLibUnsupportedType` [0x501C] — never silent). | Extend the hir-text `fn` type production (`parseType` in `hir_text.cpp`) + the shared `parseTypeFromText` to parse a variadic marker (e.g. `fn(ptr<char>, ...) -> i32`) into the `FnSig` `isVariadic` scalar; then a descriptor can express `printf`. Pin: a `stdio.json` `printf` row round-trips through `parseTypeFromText` with `isVariadic` set, and the symbol injects + resolves end-to-end. | `D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-DESCRIPTOR-AGNOSTIC` (the consumer); `docs/ir-type-text.md` (the `fn`-type grammar); `D-LANG-VARIADIC`; do NOT cite in `src/` (future-work pin only) |
| `D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-08** — format-driven extern-call dispatch (the fix for a CONFIRMED latent x86_64-ELF FFI miscompile). The plan-lock proved byte-level that the ELF linker points an extern's `symbolVa` at its PLT STUB (code: `jmp [GOT]`), yet MIR→LIR unconditionally emitted `call_indirect_via_extern` (`FF 15` = `call [rip+disp32]`), dereferencing the stub's bytes as a function pointer → SIGSEGV. The extern-call shape is a property of the OBJECT FORMAT, not the CPU target (x86_64-PE vs x86_64-ELF need OPPOSITE opcodes). Fix: new closed enum `ExternCallDispatch` (`core/types/object_format_kind.hpp`) + a per-format `externCallDispatch` field on `ObjectFormatSchema` (`indirect-slot` = PE IAT / Mach-O __got, deref a pointer slot via `call_indirect_via_extern`; `direct-plt` = ELF, plain direct `call`/BL to the linker-synthesized PLT stub). `lowerToLir`/`lowerCall` selects the opcode from the active format's value as DATA — ZERO `if (arch/format == …)`; ELF needs NO new codegen opcode (reuses the existing `call`/BL→PLT-stub). The 4 live exec formats declare it (pe64 + macho64-x86_64 = `indirect-slot`; elf64-x86_64 + elf64-aarch64 = `direct-plt`). NOT validate-required (an exec format built for a non-FFI purpose may omit it); the real fail-loud is at LOWERING — a module with extern imports under a nullopt-dispatch format → `L_RequiredLirOpcodeMissing`; an unknown value → load fail-loud. Pins: `MirToLir.{ExternCallEmitsCallIndirectViaExternOpcode, ExternCallOnDirectPltFormatEmitsPlainCall, ExternImportsWithNoDispatchFailLoud, IndirectSlotWithoutOpcodeFailsLoud}` + the `extern_call_elf` runtime corpus (x86_64-ELF `abs` FFI → exit 42; RED-on-disable by flipping the ELF format to `indirect-slot`). | (closed) | plan 14 linker + plan 11 FFI |
| `D-FFI-PE-IMPORT-THUNK` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-05 (c112) — the pe64 leg's LAST run-green blocker: sqlite3.exe RUNS on Windows (`--version` 3.54.0 + full CRUD `SELECT sum`→62), so all 3 legs (win pe64 + linux x86_64 + arm64-qemu) now run real SQL.** The follow-up to `D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH`: PE was the sole `indirect-slot` format, so `symbolVa[extern]` named the raw `.idata` IAT *data* slot. A DIRECT extern call worked (`call [IATslot]` derefs the slot), but an ADDRESS-TAKEN import (sqlite os_win.c `aSyscall[]` = a static-init array of `(SYSCALL)Win32Func` called indirectly) got the slot ADDRESS as a value → `call *reg` jumped INTO `.idata` and executed the pointer bytes as code → 0xC0000005 (no output). ELF/Mach-O ran the identical construct green because a `direct-plt` format points the extern's VA at a callable STUB (ELF PLT / Mach-O `__stubs`). FIX (user-chosen A; §B GO-WITH-FIXES design-audit, all 7 fixes folded): the PE walker synthesizes one `FF 25 disp32` import THUNK per extern at the tail of `.text` (`jmp *[IAT slot]` — the PE analog of Mach-O `__stubs`; `peThunkSizeFor`/`emitX86_64PeThunk`/`emitPeThunk` MIRROR `emitMachoStub`, machine-dispatched on 0x8664 with a fail-loud default), sets `symbolVa[extern]` = the THUNK VA (a `.text` code address, not the IAT slot), and flips `pe64-x86_64-windows-exec` `externCallDispatch` `indirect-slot`→`direct-plt` so every extern reference (call OR address-taken) is a callable address — PE now matches ELF/Mach-O (NO `if(format==)`; thunk block reserved before `.text` is sized, emitted after IAT VAs known; shared `applyExecRelocations` UNCHANGED). Pins: `PeExecFormatJson.ShippedPeExecIsDirectPlt` (host-independent format pin, mirrors `MachOArm64Exit.ShippedX86DarwinExecIsDirectPlt`) + `PeExecWriter.AddressTakenImportResolvesToTextThunkNotIdataSlot` (host-independent byte-scan: one `FF 25` thunk in `.text`, the extern call resolves to it, the thunk jumps into `.idata`; RED-on-disable=revert→resolves to `.idata`) + `examples/c-subset/addr_import` 3-leg runtime corpus (volatile-load fold-proof; PE 0xC0000005→exit 42; elf/arm64 already green). | (closed — sqlite3.exe run-green on Windows) | `D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH` (parent dispatch enum); `D-LK6-8` (ELF PLT) + macho `__stubs` (mirror precedents); `D-RUNTIME-PE-MAIN-ARGS` (c111, prior pe64 blocker); plan 14 linker |
| `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-FFI-ISEL` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-08** — the END-TO-END ARM64-ELF runnable FFI call-WITH-RESULT path is complete. **The gap was the `lea`/GlobalAddr lowering, NOT a "reg-move-class op"** (the D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH cycle's first guess) — empirically re-confirmed this cycle: with the arm64 `lea` opcode disabled, BOTH the MIR→LIR fixture AND the `extern_call_elf` arm64 compile fail with the EXACT diagnostic `L_RequiredLirOpcodeMissing: target 'arm64' declares no 'lea' opcode (required for lowering MIR GlobalAddr)` — the raw `lowerToLir` lowers a `Call`'s callee `GlobalAddr` via `lowerGlobalAddr`→`lea` (the peephole that folds the symbol into the call's SymbolRef is an OPTIMIZED-path thing, absent in the raw lowering). The D-AS4-3 ARM64 `lea` (ADRP+ADD, commit 92386d3) supplied the missing opcode; this cycle PROVES the FFI path: (1) the MIR→LIR pin `MirToLir.Arm64ExternCallWithResultLowersUnderDirectPlt` (the dropped pin, reconstructed — lowers clean, 2 direct calls, 0 indirect; RED-on-disable demonstrated above), (2) the runnable corpus `examples/c-subset/extern_call_elf` gained an `arm64:elf64-aarch64-linux-exec` arm (compiles host-independently → valid AArch64 ELF; RUNS on the native `ubuntu-24.04-arm` CI leg + qemu via the cross-arch gate → exit 42). The ELF-aarch64 PLT/GOT (`emitArm64PltStub`, D-LK6-8 ✅) + the format-driven `direct-plt` dispatch (D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH ✅) complete the chain. **⚠️ CORRECTION (2026-06-08, same-day next cycle): the "→ exit 42" RUNTIME claim above was an OVER-CLAIM** (exactly the runtime risk that cycle's own self-audit flagged). The compile-to-valid-AArch64-ELF half was real, but the binary SIGSEGV'd at runtime (CI exit 139; locally reproduced under qemu once the file's execute bit was set). Root cause = a SEPARATE codegen bug, NOT the FFI path: non-leaf ARM64 functions did not spill the link register x30, so `main`'s epilogue `ret` returned to a clobbered LR (an infinite self-loop overrunning the stack). The FFI dispatch itself was always correct — `abs(7-49)` returned 42 in x0. The corpus GENUINELY runs to exit 42 only after `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-NONLEAF-LINK-REGISTER` (below) landed the x30 spill. | (closed — runtime corrected by the NONLEAF-LINK-REGISTER fix) | plan 14 (D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64 family); D-AS4-3 (the lea that closed the compile path); D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH + D-LK6-8 (the PLT/dispatch chain); D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-NONLEAF-LINK-REGISTER (the x30 fix that made it actually run) |
| `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-NONLEAF-LINK-REGISTER` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-08** — non-leaf ARM64 functions now spill + reload the link register x30. **This is the ACTUAL root cause of the arm64-FFI SIGSEGV (CI exit 139)** that `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-FFI-ISEL`'s "→ exit 42" over-claimed away (the prior cycle's self-audit had flagged exactly this runtime risk). A calling convention that carries the return address in a LINK REGISTER (AAPCS64 x30) rather than pushing it on the stack (x86_64 `call`; callPushBytes=8) makes x30 live across the whole body of a non-leaf function — every `bl`/`call` clobbers it. The frame logic spilled the `calleeSaved` set (x19–x29) but NOT x30 (deliberately the `linkRegister`, NOT in `calleeSaved`, so it stays un-allocatable), so `main`'s epilogue `ret` returned to the clobbered LR (the address after the last call) → an infinite self-loop whose repeated epilogue `add sp` walked SP off the stack into a guard page → SIGSEGV. **The FFI dispatch was always correct** — `abs(7-49)` returned 42 in x0; the PLT stub (ADRP x16/LDR x17/BR x17) + the GLOB_DAT relocation both worked. **Fix** (`src/lir/lir_callconv.cpp::materializeOneFunc`, agnostic + config-driven): when `hasCalls && cc.linkRegister.has_value()`, fold the link register into `savedRegs` so the existing prologue/epilogue machinery spills it at entry + reloads it before `ret`. ZERO `if(arch/format==…)` — x86_64 (no `linkRegister`) takes the no-op path; the schema's long-declared `linkRegister` field (whose `target_schema.hpp` comment PROMISED "ML7 lowering checks linkRegister.has_value()") is finally consumed. **Pins**: `LirCallconv.NonLeafAarch64FunctionSpillsLinkRegister` (non-leaf SAVES x30 + leaf does NOT; RED-on-disable: revert the fold → x30 absent → fail) + `LirCallconv.NonLeafX8664FunctionHasNoLinkRegisterToSpill` (agnosticism control: x86_64 cc has no linkRegister → no-op path) + the `extern_call_elf` arm64 runtime corpus (now genuinely exit 42 under qemu + native-arm64; was SIGSEGV → CI red — so red-on-disable by construction). **+ HOST-INDEPENDENT BYTE-PIN (2026-06-08, same-day hardening)**: `LirCallconv.NonLeafAarch64FramePrologueEpilogueByteExact` drives the real production tail (legalizeTwoAddress → materializeCallingConvention → assemble) and byte-pins the ASSEMBLED non-leaf prologue+epilogue EXACT — incl. `stur x30,[sp,#16]` (f80103fe) at prologue offset 12 + `ldur x30,[sp,#16]` (f84103fe) reloaded before `ret` — against hand-verified ARM-ARM literals, plus a leaf-control (g spills x28/x29 but no `stur x30`). RED-on-disable DEMONSTRATED (disable the fold → frame 0x20→0x10 → both byte windows diverge). Closes the gap where the COMPOSED frame's byte-correctness was witnessed ONLY by the native-arm64 runtime leg (the per-instruction arm64 byte-pins prove instructions in isolation, never the composed frame). The THREE layers — structural (savedRegs, every leg) + this byte-pin (assembled bytes, every leg incl. Windows MSVC) + runtime corpus (exit 42, native-arm64 leg) — are complementary; none replaces another. 207/207 (no frame-size regression — x86_64 has no linkRegister so its frames are byte-identical; no arm64 non-leaf frame-size pin existed pre-fix). | (closed) | plan 14 (D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64 family); `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-FFI-ISEL` (the over-claim this corrected); `D-LK10-ENTRY-ML7-FRAME-BIAS-UNIFY` (the frame substrate it extends); `target_schema.hpp` linkRegister field |
| `D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-08** — AArch64 intra-function branch displacement resolution. The cycle-13.5 substrate (`BlockRelPatchKind { X86Rel32, Arm64Imm19, Arm64Imm26 }` on `BlockRelPatch`) left the two ARM64 arms as a fail-loud stub (`A_NoEncodingShapeWalker`); this cycle implements the real resolver in `src/asm/asm.cpp`. The displacement is PC-relative TO THE INSTRUCTION ITSELF (`disp = (target - patchOffset) >> 2`, NO +4 bias — unlike x86 rel32's relative-to-after-the-disp) and SCALED by 4 (branch targets are word-aligned); Imm19 (B.cond) occupies bits 5..23, Imm26 (B) bits 0..25. The resolver READ-MODIFY-WRITES only that bit-field (a `writeU32LEAt` would clobber the opcode/cond bits), masks the two's-complement displacement to the field width BEFORE the shift (negative-disp sign bits cannot bleed past the field), asserts 4-byte alignment (`A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant` on a non-multiple delta — never a silent low-bit drop), and signed-range-checks against the field width (`A_ImmediateOperandOutOfRange`; >reach → `D-CSUBSET-LONG-BRANCH` thunk is the future generalization). The fixed32 encoder selects `Arm64Imm19`/`Arm64Imm26` purely from `wire.slotKind` (config-driven; the new `EncodingSlotKind::Imm19={5,19}` added in lockstep) and queues a `BlockRelPatch` into `blockPatches`. ZERO `if(arch/format==…)` — the dispatch is on config-selected patch-kind DATA, symmetric with the x86 `X86Rel32` arm. **Pins**: `LirCallconv.Arm64ControlFlowSkeletonEncodesCmpBcondBWithResolvedOffsets` — a 6-word byte-exact pin through the real `assemble()` path covering forward Imm19 (+2), forward Imm26 (+3), and the **negative Imm26 back-edge** (−4 → `0x17FFFFFC`, two's-complement); RED-on-disable DEMONSTRATED (remove the `>>2` scaling → `0x14000003`→`0x1400000C` + `0x17FFFFFC`→`0x17FFFFF0`). **Runtime**: the `arm64_control_flow` while-loop corpus (exit 42) executes the resolved branches end-to-end — PROVEN LOCALLY under qemu-aarch64 (exit 42 ×3); the native `ubuntu-24.04-arm` CI leg (real arm64 hardware) CONFIRMED it GREEN — run 27170873148, 2026-06-08 (§A.5 cross-target runtime closure SATISFIED). | (closed — runtime CONFIRMED green on the native ubuntu-24.04-arm CI leg, run 27170873148) | plan 13 §AS3 (block-rel substrate, cycle-13.5); plan 14 (D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-CONTROL-FLOW); `D-CSUBSET-LONG-BRANCH` (the >reach thunk, still open) |
| `D-AS3-COND-CODE-ARM64` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-08** — AArch64's condition-code table + the generic cond-placement knobs. `arm64.target.json` now declares a `condCodeEncoding` table (10 entries: eq:0 ne:1 slt:11 sle:13 sgt:12 sge:10 ult:3 ule:9 ugt:8 uge:2) consumed by BOTH `jcc` (B.cond, the cond nibble at bits 0..3) AND `setcc` (CSET). The cycle generalized `condCodeFromPayload` with two NEW template fields — `condBitPos` (uint8, default 0) + `condInvert` (bool, default false) — so the SAME generic encoder line (`words[0] |= (condNib & 0x0F) << condBitPos`) serves x86 (the defaults → byte-identical to pre-knob) AND ARM64 B.cond (condBitPos=0) AND ARM64 CSET (condBitPos=12, condInvert=true: `CSET Xd,cond` = `CSINC Xd,XZR,XZR,invert(cond)`, the AArch64 cond-inversion `cond ^ 1`). ZERO `if(arch==…)` — the bit position + inversion are config DATA. Fail-loud: a variant declaring `condCodeFromPayload` on a schema with no `condCodeEncoding` table errors. **Pins**: `Arm64Encoder.CsetEncodesInvertedCondAtBits12` (cset x0,gt=`0x9A9FD7E0` GT 0xC→0xD; cset x0,eq=`0x9A9F17E0` EQ 0x0→0x1 — two conditions defeat a "constant nibble" bug; RED-on-disable DEMONSTRATED: disable `condNib ^= 1u` → 0xD7→0xC7) + `Arm64Encoder.ZextEncodesOrrW` (zext x0,w1=`0x2A0103E0`) + the B.cond cond-at-bit-0 path pinned via the skeleton's `b.sgt`=`0x5400004C`. **Runtime**: the `arm64_control_flow` corpus exercises cmp+jcc end-to-end (PROVEN LOCALLY under qemu, exit 42; native-arm64 CI CONFIRMED green, run 27170873148). Note: lowerCondBr FUSES the loop's ICmp into cmp+B.cond, so the separately-lowered setcc(CSET)/zext are emitted-but-dead in that corpus — their CORRECTNESS is pinned host-independently by the byte-tests, not the corpus. | (closed — runtime CONFIRMED green on the native ubuntu-24.04-arm CI leg, run 27170873148) | plan 13 §AS3 (cond-code table, cycle-13.5); pairs with `D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26` |
| `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-CONTROL-FLOW` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-08** — the CONTROL-FLOW half of plan-14's combined `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-CONTROL-FLOW + NON-LEAF` row (the NON-LEAF/x30 half closed 2026-06-08 by `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-NONLEAF-LINK-REGISTER`). A c-subset function with `if`/`while`/loops now compiles + runs on ARM64: `cmp` (SUBS XZR,Rn,Rm — flags), `jcc` (B.cond imm19 → ifTrue + B imm26 → ifFalse, a target-blind 2-WORD MACRO via `fixedWords`+`wordIndex`), `jmp` (B imm26), `setcc` (CSET), `zext` (ORR Wd,WZR,Wm), and `alloca` (virtual → callconv materializes a frame-relative `lea` = ADD Xd,sp,#imm12) all land. The block-relative branch offsets are filled by the `D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26` resolver; the cond codes by `D-AS3-COND-CODE-ARM64`. **Proof**: the `examples/c-subset/arm64_control_flow/` runtime corpus (`while (n<42) n=n+1; return n;` → exit 42) — a genuine cross-target differential (x86_64 + arm64 arms, both must exit 42), PROVEN LOCALLY under qemu-aarch64 (exit 42 ×3 for both the while-loop AND a fused-if `max(a,b)`), plus the host-independent byte-pins (skeleton 6-word + cset/zext + frame-lea). §A.5 closure SATISFIED: the native `ubuntu-24.04-arm` CI leg CONFIRMED green — run 27170873148, 2026-06-08. | (closed — runtime CONFIRMED green on the native ubuntu-24.04-arm CI leg, run 27170873148) | plan 14 line 324 (the combined row); `D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26` + `D-AS3-COND-CODE-ARM64` (the encoding substrates it consumes); `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-NONLEAF-LINK-REGISTER` (the NON-LEAF sibling half) |
| `D-OUTPUT-EXEC-BIT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (deferral loop iteration 1, branch feature/0-0-2-p16).** `writeImage` now sets the POSIX execute bits (owner/group/others, `std::filesystem::permissions(..., perm_options::add)`) on an executable-flavor output; `compile_pipeline::linkAndWrite` decides executability CONFIG-DRIVENLY via `ObjectFormatSchema::isImageFlavor()` (no arch/format identity branch — `writeImage` switches on a `bool executable = false`, staying format-blind). A rare chmod failure is a fail-loud WARNING `K_ImageExecBitFailed` (0x8014), NOT a write failure: the bytes are valid, the exec bit a best-effort convenience. No-op on Windows (PE ignores Unix modes). Pins: `LinkWriter.ExecutableOutputCarriesExecuteBitPosix` (#ifndef _WIN32 — red-on-disable BOTH ways: `true`→`+x` present, `false`→absent; the POSIX-CI-leg witness) + `LinkWriter.ExecutableWriteSucceedsEveryPlatform` (host-independent smoke, runs on the MSVC gate). `run_binary.hpp`'s test chmod kept as a now-no-op safety net (by its own design). — *Original row (audit trail):* The compiler writes the output executable (`src/link/writer.cpp`, the `--output` path) at the host umask default — mode 0644 on POSIX, NO execute bit. A user running the produced binary directly (`dss-code-prime --output foo; ./foo`) hits "Permission denied"/ENOEXEC (qemu's `prepare_binprm` rejects a file lacking `mode & 0111`; the kernel's execve too). NOT currently blocking: the examples/integrated test runners `chmod +x` the spawned binary before `posix_spawn` (`tests/test_support/run_binary.hpp`), so the corpus runs green on every CI leg. Discovered 2026-06-08 while root-causing the arm64-FFI failure — the 0644 mode masked the real x30 SIGSEGV under a *manual* `qemu-aarch64 <file>` invocation (ENOEXEC fired before any guest code ran). Trigger = the first non-test consumer running a `--output` binary directly, OR a decision to ship runnable-out-of-the-box output. Priority: **normal**. | When the output object-format is EXECUTABLE (`format.type == "exec"` — config-driven, read from the active format, NOT an arch/format identity branch), set the execute bits on the written file via `std::filesystem::permissions(path, perms::owner_exec \| perms::group_exec \| perms::others_exec, perm_options::add)` (portable; effectively a no-op on Windows where PE ignores Unix modes). Pin: write an exec-format output, assert the file carries the execute bit on POSIX. | plan 14 linker (`writer.cpp`); pairs with `run_binary.hpp` (which chmods for tests today) |
| `D-FF1-PARTIAL-CORRUPTION-LOUD` | First time a partially-truncated input `.o` (network read interrupted) reaches the reader. | Pre-flight size + checksum check; fail-loud `F_FfiInputTruncated` before any per-section walker runs. | plan 11 FF1 |
| `D-FF1-PARTIAL-CORRUPTION-MACHO` | Same shape but Mach-O specific (a truncated `LC_*` command). | Per-command bounds check inside the Mach-O walker. | plan 11 FF1 |
| `D-FF1-PE-OBJECT-EXPORTS` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-16 (c159, roadmap B1 hardening).** The PE export reader now reads the full `IMAGE_EXPORT_DIRECTORY` (not names-only) and CLASSIFIES each export by its EAT-RVA section membership: an EAT RVA in an executable section (IMAGE_SCN_MEM_EXECUTE) → `SymbolKind::Function`; in a non-executable data section (`.rdata`/`.data`/`.bss`) → `SymbolKind::Object` (a PE DATA export — the standard dumpbin/llvm-readobj heuristic). No `SymbolKind::Data` enumerator added — the shared enum already spells the data kind `Object` (ELF STT_OBJECT / Mach-O `__data` precedent). Section membership uses the in-memory VIRTUAL footprint (max(VirtualSize, SizeOfRawData)) so a zero-fill `.bss` data export resolves. Witnessed: msvcrt.dll → 1284 Function + 46 Object (`_iob`/`_fmode`/`_environ`/`__argc`/`_acmdln`/… — dumpbin-confirmed data RVAs in the 0x9Dxxx-0x9Fxxx `.data` range); DSS's own c152-emitted `.dll` round-trips (dss_add/dss_magic @ .text RVAs → Function, dss_global @ .data RVA 0x2000 → Object, dumpbin-exact). Red-on-disable: reverting the EAT walk (default all-Function) fails the Object assertion in `ClassifiesFunctionDataAndForwarder`. (The original `.lib`-archive-member framing is subsumed — the DLL export reader is the same `IMAGE_EXPORT_DIRECTORY` walk; a `.lib`/`.obj` COFF archive reader is the separate `D-FF1-AR-READER`, roadmap B3.) | (closed — c159) | plan 11 FF1; `src/ffi/binary_readers/pe_reader.cpp` (EAT walk + `sectionCharsForRva` classifier); `src/ffi/import_surface.hpp` (`SymbolKind::Object` = PE data export); `tests/ffi/test_binary_reader_pe.cpp`; parent `D-FF1-PE-READER` |
| `D-FF2-MSG-JARGON` | First time a user-facing FF2 diagnostic's jargon is flagged by a non-DSS-fluent dev. | Sweep FF2 diagnostic strings; replace internal-implementation jargon with end-user terms. | plan 11 FF2 |
| `D-FF2-UNSUPP-FULL-SWEEP` | First time a deferred FF2 declaration shape (e.g. nested type, opaque enum) lands in real corpus. | Full SWEEP across the FF2 declaration-shape switch; promote every deferred arm to a real handler OR a per-arm fail-loud code. | plan 11 FF2 |

| `D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER` / `D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER-MUTABLE-GLOBAL` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-19 — writable data sections cycle (commit 38b5cef, pushed; CI macos+arm64 legs green).** The store-into-a-mutable-global crash is fixed: `asm.cpp` `lowerMirGlobalsToDataItems` now routes an INITIALIZED global to read-only `.rodata` (const) vs writable `.data` (mutable) keyed on `MirGlobal.isConst`, and a TENTATIVE zero-init global to `.bss` (zero-fill, empty bytes + `AssembledData.reservedSize`) — this CLOSES the former fail-loud `D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER-BSS-EMIT`. Const-ness is threaded source→codegen via a dedicated `MutabilityAttr` HIR side-table (`src/hir/attributes/mutability_attr.hpp` → `HirMutabilityMap` populated in `cst_to_hir` from `SymbolRecord.isConst` → read at `hir_to_mir` → `MirGlobal.isConst` + `globalIsConst()` accessor; preserved across DCE / rebuild / merge). The shared `exec_data_section.hpp` helper was generalized `buildExecRodata`→`buildExecDataSection(kind,…)` (kind-parameterized: Rodata/Data/Bss; Bss = empty bytes + spanSize). All THREE exec writers emit `.data` (writable, on-disk bytes) + `.bss` (NOBITS / zero-fill): ELF (`.data`+`.bss` in a SEPARATE R+W PT_LOAD #2; bss extends p_memsz not p_filesz — both static + dynamic arms), PE (new `.data`/`.bss` section headers; bss SizeOfRawData=0; Characteristics incl. MEM_WRITE read from the schema row), Mach-O (new `__DATA` segment rw- after `__DATA_CONST`; `__data` S_REGULAR + `__bss` S_ZEROFILL last; flags read from schema). Section flags/types come from the format JSON rows (zero identity branches). All FOUR exec formats declare `data`+`bss` rows + `supportedDataSections`. RUNTIME-PROVEN locally: corpus `examples/c-subset/global_write` (mutable→.data + tentative→.bss, both written-then-read) → **exit 42** on PE (Windows host, was SIGSEGV 139 before), ELF x86_64 (WSL), arm64-ELF (qemu-aarch64); macho arm64 structurally verified (CI-only — Apple Silicon leg runs it). Pins: `tests/asm/test_asm_substrate.cpp` `AsmDataSection.*` (section selection) + `tests/link/test_{pe,elf_exec}_writer.cpp` writable-bit writer pins (the former Rejects* tests flipped to Accepts*). | (closed) | plan 14 LK4 / LK1; supersedes the writable-`.data`/`.bss` "STILL DEFERRED" notes on `D-LK1-ELF-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS` + `D-LK1-MACHO-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS`; closes `D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER-BSS-EMIT`; runtime-init globals stay fail-loud (`D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER-RUNTIME-INIT`, out of scope) |
| `D-MIR-TEXT-CONST-ROUNDTRIP` | A pipeline inserts a `mir_text` serialize→parse round-trip on the source→object COMPILE path (today `mir_text` is debug/test-only, NOT on any compile path — no `src/program/` reference). | `mir_text` writer + parser carry `MirGlobal.isConst` (the writable-data-section bit). Without it, a global serialized→parsed through `.dsstext` reverts to mutable → a const global would silently land in writable `.data`. Priority: **trigger-gated** (no compile-path consumer yet; fail-safe default is mutable). | trigger-gated; discovered by the writable-data-sections cycle review (2026-06-19); `src/mir/mir_text.cpp`; pairs with `D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER-MUTABLE-GLOBAL` |
| `D-LK4-RODATA-WALKER-RELOC-BASE` | First non-PE format needs the rodata-walker reloc-base extension. | Per-format `rodataRelocBase` lookup driven by format schema. | plan 14 LK4 |
| `D-LK1-ELF-RODATA-DATAITEM-RELOC` | First ELF ET_EXEC module whose `AssembledData` rodata item carries its OWN relocations (a data→data reference — e.g. a vtable / const pointer table / 8-byte rodata pointer slot). The ELF ET_EXEC writer (`src/link/format/elf.cpp`) currently fails loud on a non-empty `dataItem.relocations` (it patches FUNCTION relocations only, via `applyExecRelocations`); `int g=42;` carries none. Priority: **normal** (fail-loud today; no producer emits data→data rodata relocations yet — the PE walker's analogous `D-LK4-RODATA-WALKER-RELOC-BASE-OFFSET` path is the precedent to mirror). | Patch each rodata-item relocation into `rodataBytes` at the item's section-relative offset + the reloc's intra-item offset (the def's VA already lives in `symbolVa`), mirroring the PE walker's data-item-relocation arm (`pe.cpp` "Apply DATA-ITEM relocations into the .rdata bytes"). | plan 14 LK1 (ELF exec data sections); mirrors PE `D-LK4-RODATA-WALKER-RELOC-BASE-OFFSET` |
| `D-LK1-ELF-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS` | 🟡 **RODATA PATH CLOSED (rodata-only scope)** — the ELF ET_EXEC writer (`src/link/format/elf.cpp`) now emits a loadable `.rodata` section from `module.dataItems` (SHF_ALLOC, folded into the single R+X `.text` PT_LOAD via `shFlagsToPFlags`; data-symbol VAs added to the static `symbolVa` map so code→rodata relocations resolve through `applyExecRelocations`). Both `elf64-x86_64-linux-exec` + `elf64-aarch64-linux-exec` declare `supportedDataSections: ["rodata"]` + a `.rodata` row (one shared elf.cpp code path; arch differs only via config). Byte-pinned host-independently (`ElfExecWriter.Rodata*` in `test_elf_exec_writer.cpp`, incl. the PT_LOAD-extension RED-on-disable). **RUNTIME-PROVEN LOCALLY** (2026-06-09): the `global_int` ELF arms (`int answer=42; return answer;`) compile + RUN → **exit 42 on BOTH x86_64-ELF (WSL native) AND arm64-ELF (qemu-aarch64)**; `strace` confirms `exit_group(42)`, and inspection confirms the global's 42 is read from `.rodata` (`lea [rip+disp]` → 0x401048 → load) under a single R+X PT_LOAD whose `p_filesz` covers the rodata extent. **CI RE-CONFIRMED 2026-06-09** (commit `8040410`, run **27187309300**): the `linux-gcc-release` (x86_64) + **`linux-arm64-gcc-release` (native `ubuntu-24.04-arm` hardware)** + `linux-clang-asan` + `macos-clang-release` legs all GREEN — `global_int` RAN → exit 42 on real arm64 + x86_64. (The `windows-msvc-release` leg failed at the **Setup-CMake** step = a CI-infra flake, NOT a code failure — redundant with the local Win-MSVC 212/212; re-triggered.) The rodata-path runtime is now witnessed BOTH locally (WSL+qemu) AND on the authoritative native CI legs. **DYNAMIC-PATH ARM ALSO CLOSED (2026-06-09, the `#include puts` string-FFI capstone):** the `.rodata` emission was originally wired into the STATIC `encodeElfExec` writer ONLY; the capstone extended it to `encodeElfExecDynamic` (the dynamic-linked ELF exec path, which every libc-importing program now takes — string-literal args reach a DYNAMIC extern, and exit routes through libc) via the SAME shared `buildExecRodata`/`addDataSymbolVas` helper — byte-IDENTICAL when no rodata. So a string-literal pointer arg to a dynamic FFI call (`puts("hello")`) now lands its data in `.rodata` on the dynamic path too; runtime-proven by the `shipped_include_puts` corpus → stdout `"hello\n"` + exit 42 on linux-x86_64 (WSL) AND linux-arm64 (qemu). **WRITABLE PATH CLOSED 2026-06-19** (by `D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER-MUTABLE-GLOBAL`): writable `.data` + zero-fill `.bss` now emit into a SEPARATE 2nd R+W PT_LOAD (W^X; bss extends p_memsz not p_filesz), on BOTH the static + dynamic ELF arms — runtime-proven (`global_write` exit 42 x86_64-ELF WSL + arm64-ELF qemu). **STILL DEFERRED:** the data→data dataItem-reloc path (`D-LK1-ELF-RODATA-DATAITEM-RELOC`). Historical context — the original blocker: the ELF ET_EXEC writer emitted only `.text`/`.symtab`/`.strtab`/`.shstrtab` — NO data section. So a module-level data global (`int g = 42;`) could not reach an ELF executable. This is the blocker for a RUNNABLE ARM64 `lea` corpus: the D-AS4-3 ARM64 `lea` (ADRP+ADD) encoder is built + proven by byte-pins (`Arm64Encoder.Lea*`) + the linker reloc-formula tests (`test_aarch64_reloc_formulas.cpp`), but an end-to-end `static int g=42; int *p=&g; return *p;` corpus that exercises lea→load→exit-42 needs `g` in a loadable data section (and a 2nd R+W PT_LOAD), which the static ET_EXEC path lacks. Same gap affects x86_64-ELF data globals (the format-dispatch cycle's `extern_call_elf` corpus avoided strings for exactly this reason). NOTE (2026-06-08): the FFI direct-call path that REQUIRES the lea to COMPILE now runs end-to-end (`extern_call_elf` arm64 arm → `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-FFI-ISEL` ✅ CLOSED) — but that exercises the lea on the COMPILE path only (the lea materializes `&abs` yet the direct BL targets the symbol via the PLT, so the lea's runtime RESULT is dead there). OBSERVING the lea's runtime VALUE still needs either this data-global deref path OR an indirect call through a function POINTER (`D-ML7-2.4`, a separate isel gap). **UPDATE 2026-06-12 (FC4 c2): the fn-pointer option LANDED — `D-ML7-2.4` closed; the `fnptr_call`/`fnptr_callback` arm64-ELF arms BLR through an ADRP+ADD-materialized `&helper` at RUNTIME (qemu exit 42), so the arm64 lea's runtime VALUE is now observed end-to-end.** Priority: **normal** (the lea ENCODER, the user-directed deliverable, is complete + strongly pinned; only the lea's runtime-VALUE observation is gated). | Extend the ELF ET_EXEC writer to lay out a `.rodata`/`.data` section with the module's `AssembledData` bytes, assign each data symbol a VA, add a 2nd page-aligned PT_LOAD (R[+W]), and let the existing relocation applier patch code→data references. Then reuse `global_int`-style sources (`int g=42; return g;`) with an `arm64:elf64-aarch64-linux-exec` target arm under the qemu/native cross-arch gate → exit 42 proves lea→load end-to-end. | plan 14 LK1 (ELF exec data sections); pairs with `D-AS4-3` (the lea encoder it would make runnable) + the `D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER-*` family (the constant-encoding walker) |
| `D-LK1-MACHO-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-09 — RUNTIME-PROVEN on Apple Silicon** (run **27192415923**, the `macos-clang-release` leg ran `global_int` macos-arm64 → ctest **#184 Passed** → exit 42 on real Apple Silicon: the `__TEXT,__const` data global was READ at runtime). The macOS analogue of `D-LK1-ELF-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS`. The Mach-O exec writer (`src/link/format/macho.cpp` `encodeExecDynamic`) emits a loadable read-only `__TEXT,__const` section from `module.dataItems` (placed inside `__TEXT` after `__stubs`, gated on `hasConst`; the no-dataItems output is byte-identical to before), built via the shared `src/link/format/exec_data_section.hpp` helper (`buildExecRodata` + `addDataSymbolVas` — the SAME H1 max-align + M1 anonymous-skip + non-Rodata/data-item-reloc fail-loud as the ELF arm); `macho.cpp` cites the ELF precedent `D-LK1-ELF-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS` as the mirror. A c-subset global `int answer=42;` COMPILES to an arm64-darwin binary whose `__TEXT,__const` holds `2a 00 00 00` (byte-pinned host-independently by the `MachOArm64Exit.MachoArm64Const*` cases in `tests/link/test_macho_arm64_exit.cpp`) AND, per run 27192415923, RUNS on Apple Silicon → exit 42. **WRITABLE PATH CLOSED 2026-06-19** (by `D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER-MUTABLE-GLOBAL`): a writable `__DATA` segment (rw-, after `__DATA_CONST`) now carries `__data` (S_REGULAR) + `__bss` (S_ZEROFILL) — structurally built + CI-gated on the Apple-Silicon `macos-latest` leg (`global_write`). **STILL DEFERRED** (mirrors ELF): the ET_REL `.o` rodata arm, the data→data dataItem-reloc path (`D-LK1-MACHO-RODATA-DATAITEM-RELOC`), and the macOS-x86_64 form (`D-LK1-MACHO-X8664-DARWIN-DATA-SECTION`). | (closed) — the `macos-clang-release` leg of run 27192415923 ran `global_int` macos-arm64 → exit 42 (ctest #184). The §A.5 RUNTIME witness is shared with `D-LK6-AARCH64-ADDABSLO12-HIGH-VA`. | plan 14 LK1 / LK3-RODATA (Mach-O exec data sections); mirrors `D-LK1-ELF-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS`; shares `exec_data_section.hpp` with the ELF arm; prerequisite `D-LK6-AARCH64-ADDABSLO12-HIGH-VA` (the reloc-guard relaxation that let the high-VA `__const` access compile) |
| `D-LK1-MACHO-RODATA-DATAITEM-RELOC` | First Mach-O `__const` rodata item carrying its OWN relocation (a data→data reference — e.g. a const pointer table / 8-byte rodata pointer slot). **F5 UPDATE (2026-06-25):** `exec_data_section.hpp` now PATCHES such relocs in-place (`applyDataItemRelocations`, `allowItemRelocations=true`), but `macho.cpp` then fails loud (`K_NoMatchingObjectFormat`) on any abs64 site collected from `__TEXT,__const` — because that segment is read-execute + code-signed and dyld CANNOT PIE-rebase it (a relocated pointer is inherently load-writable). F5's own symbol-address pointers DODGE this: the asm arm forces them to `.data` (writable). So this trigger now fires only for a FUTURE const-with-relocs item the front-end places in rodata. Priority: **normal** (fail-loud today; no producer emits rodata data→data relocations). | Route reloc-bearing rodata to a WRITABLE-AT-LOAD `__DATA_CONST` segment (Apple's const-reloc idiom — writable during dyld fixup, read-only after) + emit its rebase entries (the F5 `dyldRebaseBlob` machinery, segment index `__DATA_CONST`), instead of the sealed `__TEXT,__const`. | plan 14 LK1 / LK3-RODATA (Mach-O exec data sections); shares the `exec_data_section.hpp` guard with `D-LK1-ELF-RODATA-DATAITEM-RELOC`; cited by `macho.cpp` F5 const-site guard; mirrors PE `D-LK4-RODATA-WALKER-RELOC-BASE-OFFSET` |
| `D-LK1-MACHO-X8664-DARWIN-DATA-SECTION` | The `macho64-x86_64-darwin-exec` format was NOT given a `__const` row / `supportedDataSections` this cycle (only `macho64-arm64-darwin-exec` was). Rationale: there is NO macOS-x86_64 CI leg (`macos-latest` is Apple Silicon), so enabling it would ship an UNTESTED §A.5 form (a data section that no leg ever runs). The `macho.cpp` walker code is format-blind (it WOULD work — the gate is the `linker.cpp` capability consult, never a branch), but the x86_64-darwin JSON declares no data section. Priority: **normal** (no consumer; declaring it would be untested gold-plating). | When a macOS-x86_64 runner OR a Rosetta-run gate appears: add the `__const` row + `supportedDataSections: ["rodata"]` to `macho64-x86_64-darwin-exec.format.json` + a corpus arm under that gate → exit 42. | plan 14 LK1 / LK3-RODATA; mirrors the §A.5 "do not ship an untested form" discipline; pairs with `D-LK1-MACHO-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS` (the arm64-darwin arm that DID land) |
| `D-LK-MACHO-X8664-DYLIB-RUNTIME` | ⏳ **OPEN -- DEFERRED (named 2026-07-17, c171 conformance).** The `macho64-x86_64-darwin-dylib` format (the x86_64 sibling of `macho64-arm64-darwin-dylib`, c171 variant-parity Gap 3) ships with STRUCTURAL byte-pins ONLY (`MachoDylibWriterX86_64.HeaderPinsDylibShape`: MH_DYLIB, cputype 0x01000007 (x86_64), LC_ID_DYLIB `@rpath/libdss.dylib`, ad-hoc code signature present, `__text` at 0x1000 -- the writer emits the variant correctly, proven at the byte level). Its exit-code RUN witness is macOS-x86_64/Rosetta-GATED: there is NO macOS-x86_64 CI leg (`macos-latest` is Apple Silicon), so a run arm would be an UNTESTED §A.5 form -- the SAME "do not ship an untested form" discipline as the sibling `D-LK1-MACHO-X8664-DARWIN-DATA-SECTION`. Priority: **normal** (no consumer; the arm64 dylib is the served shared-lib arm). | When a macOS-x86_64 runner OR a Rosetta-run gate appears: build a client against the DSS-emitted x86_64 `.dylib` + link + run → exit 42 on that leg. | plan 14 conformance (c171); pairs with `D-LK1-MACHO-X8664-DARWIN-DATA-SECTION` (same untested-form discipline); `src/dss-config/object-formats/macho64-x86_64-darwin-dylib.format.json` |
| `D-LK-RELRO-CONST-DATA-RELOCATABLE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-15 — c145 (relro const-data cycle, branch feature/lk-object-symbol-names).** A CONST global carrying LOAD-TIME relocations (a const function-pointer table / `int *const p=&x;` — sqlite's VFS method tables + `aSyscall[]`) now compiles to a foreign-linkable object AND runs in an executable across all shipped formats. `asm.cpp` `lowerMirGlobalsToDataItems` routes such a global (const + non-empty relocations) to the new `DataSectionKind::RelRoConst` (on-disk word `relro`, `core/types/section_kind.hpp`); each format maps it to its native relocated-read-only placement + emits the item's OWN relocations. **RELOCATABLE `.o` (the sqlite goal, x86_64 ELF):** the ELF ET_REL writer (`elf.cpp` `encode`) emits a distinct `.data.rel.ro` (SHT_PROGBITS + SHF_ALLOC\|SHF_WRITE — gcc's contract, NOT read-only `.rodata`) + `.rela.data.rel.ro` (R_X86_64_64 abs64, psABI addend via `addendBias`), GENERALIZED so any reloc-bearing data section gets a `.rela.<section>` (a reloc-bearing MUTABLE pointer global → `.data` + `.rela.data`); the `.rela.<section>` name is DERIVED from the `.rela.text` row's prefix (schema-driven, no hardcoded `.rela` literal); the undefined-extern `.symtab` scan now covers data-item relocations. aarch64 ELF `.o` brought to full data-section parity (`R_AARCH64_ABS64`=257 proves machine-agnosticism — the SAME machine-neutral writer). **EXECUTABLE (all exec formats):** a relro item is FOLDED into the format's existing relocated-writable data section ("treat relro like data" — the read-only-after hardening of a separate GNU_RELRO/`__DATA_CONST` segment is a linker-segment nicety not required for correctness) via the shared `exec_data_section.hpp` `mergeFileBackedDataSection` helper: ELF → `.data` (patched in place by `applyDataItemRelocations`, static + dynamic arms), PE → `.rdata` (base-relocated through the existing `.reloc` DIR64 table), Mach-O arm64 → `__DATA,__data` (dyld-rebased through the existing rebase stream). AGNOSTIC (section names/flags/reloc-ids from the format JSON; the walker keys on `SectionKind`/`DataSectionKind`) + every prior FAIL-LOUD preserved. Pins: `tests/link/test_elf_writer.cpp` (`RelRoConstDataItemEmitsDataRelRoSectionAndRelaDataRelRo` — strict r_offset/r_info/r_addend/st_shndx; `RelRoConstDataRelocIsMachineAgnosticOnAarch64`; `MutablePointerDataItemEmitsRelaData`), `test_elf_exec_writer.cpp` + `test_pe_writer.cpp` + `test_macho_writer.cpp` (exec fold-in + in-place patch / DIR64 / dyld-rebase) + runtime corpus `examples/c-subset/relro_const_fnptr_table` (a const fn-ptr table + `int *const` deref → exit 42, all 4 legs). ★ **MILESTONE end-to-end (WSL gcc 13.3):** the REAL 269k-line `sqlite3.c` amalgamation now compiles to `sqlite3.o` (4.5 MB, ET_REL x86-64, 2605 FUNC symbols, `.data.rel.ro` + `.rela.data.rel.ro` present + POPULATED, exit 0, ZERO errors); `gcc sqlitever.c sqlite3.o -lpthread -lm -ldl` LINKS + the binary RUNS → prints `version 3.54.0 (3054000)` via `sqlite3_libversion()`. A synthetic `.o` (`int *const gp=&gx; const fnptr ctab[]={fa};`) gcc-links → exit 0 with `.data.rel.ro` placed in the linked exe's GNU_RELRO segment (gcc's hardened contract). This was the LAST blocker for compiling the SQLite amalgamation to a foreign-linkable object — the c139-c145 cross-toolchain arc is complete for the `.o`. | **FOLLOW-UP ✅ CLOSED (Mach-O c147 2026-07-16 + PE c148 2026-07-16): BOTH `.o`/`.obj` writers now carry full data sections incl. relro with per-section relocation emission — Mach-O `__DATA,__const` + relocation_info (c147); PE a second `.rdata`-named COFF section + IMAGE_RELOCATION ADDR64 with in-slot addends (c148, END-TO-END: MSVC link.exe 14.51 linked the DSS `.obj` and the programs RAN — a fn-ptr-table + relro-pointer + dense-switch witness returned its exact expected exit). — ORIGINAL: prerequisite gap, the PE `.obj` + Mach-O `.o` MH_OBJECT writers have NO data-section support at all today (text-only; a data item hits the linker's `acceptsDataSection` gate + fails loud cleanly), so emitting relro relocations in *their* `.o` path first requires porting the ELF c141–c144 `.o` data-section machinery (raw bytes + section header + object data symbols + section-number math) to `pe.cpp`/`macho.cpp`. Supersedes the REAL-WORLD const-pointer-table trigger of `D-LK1-ELF-RODATA-DATAITEM-RELOC` + `D-LK1-MACHO-RODATA-DATAITEM-RELOC` (a hand-built RODATA item with relocs still fails loud there — correctly; a reloc-bearing item is const→relro or mutable→data, never read-only rodata). | plan 14 LK1/LK3 (relro const-data across ELF/PE/Mach-O); §B decision memory `relro-const-data-section-decision`; supersedes the const-table trigger of `D-LK1-ELF-RODATA-DATAITEM-RELOC` + `D-LK1-MACHO-RODATA-DATAITEM-RELOC` |
| `D-LK-IMAGE-DATA-SLOT-EXTERN-ADDR` | ✅ **CLOSED c153 (2026-07-16, the D-LK3-3 dylib cycle) — Mach-O half closed via the REAL symbol-based fix (both halves now done: PE = fail-loud c152, Mach-O = dyld BIND opcode c153).** **WITNESSES LANDED c158 (2026-07-16, roadmap C2 — verification-only, ZERO src change): the fix is now proven end-to-end on the EXEC arms (c150/c152/c153 had structural hand-built pins only).** (PE) `examples/c-subset/extern_data_addr_reject_pe` — a source-level DIAGNOSTIC test (`extern int _fmode; int *p = &_fmode;` → pe64 exec) proving the fail-loud reject FIRES (K_RelocationKindMismatch naming the extern + this anchor + the C2099 parity, exit != 0, NO binary emitted) through BOTH the in-process runner AND the real CLI subprocess, natively on Windows; the message-text pins stay on `PeExecWriterExternSlot.DataItemRelocTargetingExternDataFailsLoudOnExecToo`. (Mach-O EXEC) `MachoExecSourceExternSlot.StdoutAddrStaticInitEmitsSymbolBasedBindThroughShippedStdio` (`tests/link/test_macho_dylib_writer.cpp`) — compiles `FILE **pp = &stdout;` via the FULL pipeline through the SHIPPED stdio.json (macho macro `stdout`→`___stdoutp`), decodes the emitted bind stream, asserts the symbol-based `___stdoutp` bind at the `pp` __data slot + zeroed slot + NOT-rebased (host-independent byte inspection — the source→reloc chain the hand-built exec pin assumed; red-on-disable RE-VERIFIED by neutering the fold → both this + the hand-built pin RED). (ELF/Mach-O positive) `examples/c-subset/extern_data_addr_static_init` — a runnable corpus (`FILE **pp = &stdout;` → exit 42 via `pp == &stdout` + `*pp == stdout`) on elf-x86_64 (WSL-witnessed exit 42 here) / elf-arm64 (qemu CI) / macho-arm64 (macOS CI): ELF EXEC copy-relocation (the copy slot IS the object) + Mach-O EXEC symbol-based bind. The PE FUNCTION-extern-address positive stays `addr_import` (already runs on pe+elf, c112). C2 fully CLOSED (fix + witnesses); 664/664 ctest. The Mach-O dynamic walker (`macho.cpp` `encodeExecDynamic`, exec AND dylib arms) now converts EVERY extern-targeted `__DATA` data-slot relocation — DATA extern (`FILE **pp = &stdout;`) AND FUNCTION extern (`int (*fp)() = puts;`) — into a SYMBOL-BASED dyld BIND opcode targeting the slot (`externAddrBindSites` fold, right after `applyDataItemRelocations`): the baked image-local indirection-cell VA (got slot / stub) is ZEROED out of the slot bytes, the site is REMOVED from the rebase stream (bind owns the slot), and the bind stream appends SET_DYLIB_ORDINAL / SET_SYMBOL / SET_TYPE_POINTER / [SET_ADDEND_SLEB when the reloc addend is non-zero — new opcode + SLEB encoder] / SET_SEGMENT_AND_OFFSET(__DATA) / DO_BIND — dyld resolves the NAMED symbol across the two-level scope and stores `resolved + addend`, the exact gcc/ld64 shape and the semantic mirror of the ELF c150 `externAddrBySlotVa` fold (symbol-based `R_X86_64_64 <dynsym>` with a zeroed slot). Cross-image pointer identity (C11 6.5.9) therefore HOLDS on Mach-O for function externs — richer than the PE half, whose format has no symbol-based image reloc (its fail-loud stands, correctly, as MSVC-parity). Red pins: `MachoDylibWriter.ExternDataAddrSlotEmitsSymbolBasedBindNotBake` + `ExternFunctionAddrSlotEmitsSymbolBasedBindNotBake` (`tests/link/test_macho_dylib_writer.cpp` — decode the bind stream, assert the slot is zeroed + bound + NOT rebased). Runtime witness deferred to the user's Apple-Silicon validation (no macOS host here; the structural pins decode the exact opcodes dyld executes). — PE-HALF HISTORY (c152): The PE image walker (`pe.cpp` data-item reloc loop, exec AND dll arms) FAILS LOUD (`K_RelocationKindMismatch`, naming the extern + this anchor) on a data-item relocation targeting an extern DATA import — the registered fix direction verbatim (MSVC parity: C2099 rejects `&dllimport_obj` as a static initializer). Red pins: `PeDllWriter.DataItemRelocTargetingExternDataFailsLoud` + the exec-arm sibling in `tests/link/test_pe_dll_writer.cpp`. A FUNCTION-extern-targeted slot stays LEGAL by design: symbolVa[funcExtern] is the FF 25 import THUNK — a callable code address — so the bake is call-correct (the c112 shipped `addr_import` run witness, sqlite aSyscall[] shape) and its DIR64 row rebases correctly in a dll; cross-image fn-pointer identity is not a Windows guarantee (MSVC's own `&puts` binds the local thunk). — ORIGINAL (c150 registration): 🔴 **OPEN (registered c150, 2026-07-16; LATENT since c117/c149 — surfaced by the c150 ET_DYN review, which found + FIXED the same shape on the ELF dyn arm).** A static initializer taking an EXTERN's address (`FILE **pp = &stdout;` / `int (*fp)() = puts;`) on the PE EXEC and Mach-O EXEC arms bakes the INDIRECTION-SLOT address into the data slot: `symbolVa[dataExtern]` is the IAT slot (pe.cpp, c149) / `__got` slot (macho.cpp, c117), and `applyDataItemRelocations` writes it into the pointer slot — one indirection off at runtime (the pointer points at the slot, not the object); a function extern binds to the local thunk/stub (call-correct but pointer-identity breaks, C11 6.5.9). The ELF DYN arm emits gcc's symbol-based `R_X86_64_64 <dynsym> + addend` with a zeroed slot (c150 fold, runtime-proven `pp == &stdout` across ASLR runs); the ELF EXEC arm is correct via copy-relocation (the copy slot IS the object). FIX DIRECTIONS: PE exec — MSVC itself REJECTS `&dllimport_obj` as a non-constant C initializer, so the honest arm is FAIL LOUD on an extern-targeted data-item reloc in a PE image (plus, later, a CRT-style dynamic initializer if ever wanted); Mach-O exec — a dyld BIND opcode targeting the slot (the bind-stream machinery exists) is the correct symbol-based analog. Trigger: a real consumer takes an extern's address in a static initializer on pe/macho (today's corpus does not — the shape was constructed by the review). | PE: DONE c152 (pre-flight reject in the image data-reloc path naming the extern, exec + dll arms, red-pinned). Mach-O (remaining): emit a dyld bind opcode for the slot, plus its red pin mirroring `ElfDynWriter.ExternAddressDataSlotEmitsSymbolBasedAbs64NotRelative`. | discovered by the c150 silent-failure review (chip also filed); `src/link/format/pe.cpp` (~2324 symbolVa[dataExtern]) + `src/link/format/macho.cpp` (~2408) + `exec_data_section.hpp` `applyDataItemRelocations`; the ELF-dyn fold (elf.cpp externAddrBySlotVa) is the reference fix |
| `D-MIR-ELEMENT-CONST-ARRAY-GLOBAL-CLASSIFICATION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-22 (Tier-2 correctness cluster; branch feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456) — the element-const array now classifies CONST at the semantic tier, closed TOGETHER with `D-CSUBSET-GROUPED-DECLARATOR-CONST` via the SAME group-descent fix.** ROOT: `declaratorObjectIsConst` (semantic_analyzer.cpp) only inspected pointer layers that were DIRECT children of the declaratorRule, so an object-forming `* const` hidden inside a parenthesized GROUP was invisible — `int (*const ops[N])(int)` (an array whose ELEMENT is a const pointer) fell to the head scan, classified MUTABLE, and its file-scope global mis-routed to writable `.data`. FIX (single file, agnostic, NOT §B): the pointer-layer scan now DESCENDS the group (the same by-ROLE recursion `directDeclaredType`/`declaratorNameNode` use — group → inner declaratorRule → outermost source-order pointer layer), skipping the direct's own array/fn suffixes (outer type-structure, never the object's const), depth-capped by `kMaxDeclaratorDepth`. So the direct-declarator spelling classifies like its `int *const arr[N]` (top-level layer) + typedef'd `const op tab[N]` (head const) siblings, and a grouped `const char (*p)` correctly becomes MUTABLE (the former over-approximation is gone). WITNESS: `ElementConstFnPtrArrayGlobalIsConst` (tests/analysis/semantic — `int (*const ops[2])(int)` + inferred `static … exts[]` → `isConst` TRUE; mutable `muts[2]` FALSE; typedef `tab[2]` TRUE) + the grouped-scalar violation pins `GroupedConstPointerParamEmitsConstViolation`/`GroupedPointerToConstParamIsClean` (all THREE red-on-disable VERIFIED — neuter the descent → RED) + runnable `examples/c-subset/const_array_global_fnptr` (→ `.data.rel.ro`+`.rela.data.rel.ro` on ELF x86_64 AND aarch64 [R_AARCH64_ABS64, machine-agnostic]; MUTABLE contrast → `.data`; pe64 native run exit 42 debug + release; the mis-routing is runtime-NEUTRAL so the classification is red-on-disable at the semantic tier, not the exit code). sqlite NEUTRAL: `sqlite3.c` (which carries the exact shape at line 187083 `static int (*const sqlite3BuiltinExtensions[])(sqlite3*)`) + a CRUD driver compile CLEAN (0 errors) → pe64 CRUD exit 42; the fix is pre-arch front-end, so aarch64 is neutral by construction (arm64 `.o` relro proven on the example). 487/487 c-subset semantic suite green. — ORIGINAL (audit trail): 🔴 OPEN (registered c148, 2026-07-16; witnessed TWICE — the c145 ELF relro witness + the c148 PE link.exe witness). An ARRAY whose ELEMENTS are const-qualified pointers — `int (*const ops[2])(int)` (the direct-declarator form) — classifies `mir.globalIsConst` = FALSE, so the global lands in writable `.data` instead of relro/rodata, on EVERY format (verified identical on ELF: no `.data.rel.ro` for that shape). The typedef'd form `const op tab[2]` (typedef int(*op)(int)) classifies CORRECTLY (→ relro), as does a const scalar pointer `int *const p`. C semantics: an array of const elements is itself a const object (C11 6.3.2.1p1 — not modifiable-lvalue), so gcc/clang park both spellings in `.data.rel.ro`. IMPACT: correctness-neutral TODAY (a writable const table still links + runs — both witnesses passed; it merely drops the relro hardening + const-ness), but the classification lives in the SEMANTIC/HIR tier (`SymbolRecord.isConst` → `MirGlobal.isConst` via the MutabilityAttr side-table), i.e. the **C23-CONFLICT ZONE** — do NOT work it during the C23-conformance arc (PR #43+); it is a frontend declarator-analysis fix, not a backend one. | (closed — `declaratorObjectIsConst` now descends the parenthesized group to the true outermost object-forming pointer layer; the c145 `relocBearingGlobalSection` chokepoint was the correct consumer all along, now fed the correct `SymbolRecord.isConst`). | discovered by the c148 PE witness (agent observation) + the c145 relro witness (fntab→.data); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`declaratorObjectIsConst` group descent → `SymbolRecord.isConst`) + `src/hir/attributes/mutability_attr.hpp`; the c145 `relocBearingGlobalSection` chokepoint (correct consumer, wrong input); fixed in the 2026-07-22 Tier-2 correctness cluster — the classification lives in the SEMANTIC tier and const is a symbol flag (NOT interned in the type), so it did NOT collide with the C23 arc after all |
| `D-LK-RODATA-HELPER-PE-PARITY` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (deferral loop, branch feature/0-0-2-p16) — ALREADY-SATISFIED (stale-open row), verified this cycle.** PE was retrofitted onto the shared helper by the **D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER** cycle: `pe.cpp` calls `buildExecDataSection` (660-677) + `addDataSymbolVas` (921-938) for ALL three section kinds — NO inline rodata→symbolVa loop remains (the premise of this row is stale). **M1** (anonymous-`SymbolId{}` skip) is handled by `addDataSymbolVas` and chokepoint-tested via `test_elf_exec_writer.cpp:2107` (PE inherits it by-construction through the shared chokepoint). **H1** (over-aligned item) is **STRUCTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE** for PE, not merely masked: the `Alignment` type CAPS at 256 bytes (`alignment.hpp` `fromBytes`/`of<N>` reject > 256) while PE `sectionAlignment` is ≥ page (4096), so a section's `maxAlign` can NEVER exceed its alignment — a `checkMaxAlign` fail-loud guard would be untestable dead code (no constructible over-aligned item). Verified the live code state directly; no honest code or test to add. — *Original row (audit trail):* PE (`src/link/format/pe.cpp`) is NOT yet converted to the shared `src/link/format/exec_data_section.hpp` helper (`buildExecRodata` + `addDataSymbolVas`) — elf.cpp + macho.cpp are. Plan-lock chose to DEFER PE (Option b): PE is the Windows-critical-path runtime and its latent H1 (max-item-align) is MASKED by page-aligned `.rdata`, and its M1 (anonymous-symbol-skip) is dormant — so retrofitting it carries Windows-runtime risk for no current correctness gain. Priority: **normal** (DRY/maintainability + closes PE's latent H1/M1; not a correctness bug today). | Retrofit `pe.cpp`'s inline rodata→symbolVa loop onto `buildExecRodata`/`addDataSymbolVas` (this ALSO fixes PE's latent H1 over-aligned-item + M1 anonymous-item gaps). Trigger: the next PE-touching cycle, OR a producer ever emits an anonymous / over-aligned data item (which would surface PE's H1/M1 as a real bug). Supersedes the residual-consumer half of `D-LK4-RODATA-WALKER-RELOC-EXTENSION-HOIST`. | plan 14 LK1/LK2-RODATA; residual of `D-LK4-RODATA-WALKER-RELOC-EXTENSION-HOIST` (the hoist's 3rd consumer); shares `exec_data_section.hpp` with the ELF + Mach-O arms |

| `D-LK6-14-NAME-OFFSET-OVERFLOW` | First Mach-O chained-fixups module whose import-name table exceeds u32 range. | Promote `nameOffset` to u64; or paginate the name table. | plan 14 LK6 cycle 14 |
| `D-LK6-AARCH64-ADDABSLO12-HIGH-VA` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-09 — RUNTIME-PROVEN on Apple Silicon** (run **27192415923**, same witness as `D-LK1-MACHO-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS`: the `macos-clang-release` leg ran `global_int` macos-arm64 → ctest **#184 Passed** → exit 42, which means the high-VA ADD_ABS_LO12_NC reloc RESOLVED at runtime — else exit ≠ 42). Cited at `exec_reloc_apply.hpp` `Aarch64AddAbsLo12`. The ADD_ABS_LO12_NC ("No Check") reloc applier carried an ELF-centric `S+A ≤ UINT32_MAX` upper bound that WRONGLY rejected valid Mach-O PIE VAs (which sit above the 4 GiB `__PAGEZERO`, e.g. 0x1'0000'4040) — it blocked the macOS-arm64 data-global access path (`global_int` → `__const`). Root cause: `(S+A) & 0xFFF` is a magnitude-INDEPENDENT low-12 extraction (the uint32 cast cannot perturb the low 12 bits), and the address magnitude is range-checked by the PAIRED ADRP companion (`adr_prel_pg_hi21`, signed-21-bit page delta) — so the ADD itself needs no upper bound. A code-correctness relaxation, byte-pinned host-independently by `Aarch64AddAbsLo12.ResolvesSplusAAboveUint32Max` (`test_aarch64_reloc_formulas.cpp`) + `MachOArm64Exit.MachoArm64ConstAdrpAddLeaHighVaResolvesLowTwelve` (`test_macho_arm64_exit.cpp`), with RED-on-disable demonstrated (restore the `> UINT32_MAX` clause → the high-VA pins fail). The relaxation is confirmed NO-OP for ELF/PE (their VAs are < UINT32_MAX) by the `linux-gcc-release` + `linux-clang-asan` + `linux-arm64-gcc-release` + `windows-msvc-release` legs of run 27192415923 all staying GREEN. | (closed) — dropped the `S+A > UINT32_MAX` clause; kept `S+A ≥ 0` (a negative VA is nonsensical). RUNTIME witness = run 27192415923 `macos-clang-release` #184 (`global_int` macos-arm64 → exit 42). | plan 14 LK6; macOS data-section cycle; runtime witness shared with `D-LK1-MACHO-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS` |
| `D-MERGE-CONTEXT-PREFIX-CLOBBER` | First time `mergeWithTargetContext` clobbers a prefix from a sibling import. | Per-prefix-source side-table (the `D-MERGE-CONTEXT-PREFIX-SIDE-TABLE` companion). | plan 11 FF / merge policy |

| `D-MERGE-SCRATCH-FRESH` | First time the merge's scratch context is silently reused across calls. | Reset / fresh-construct the scratch on each merge entry. | plan 11 FF / merge |
| `D-FFI-ANGLE-INCLUDE-LINE-SCOPED-HEADER` | A UX pass on include diagnostics, OR a header name legitimately needing multi-token content. Priority: **normal** (fail-loud today — a malformed `#include <foo` is reported, see `tests/tokenizer/test_tokenizer.cpp::FF11MalformedAngleIncludeNoCloserFailsLoud`; only the diagnostic flavor + consumption scope are deferred polish). | A malformed `#include <foo` (no closing `>`) currently runs the coalescing `header-body` mode to EOF and reports the generic `P_UnterminatedString` (matching `string`-body semantics — `header-body` mirrors the `string` body: coalesced `defaultToken`, `endsAt: ">"`, no `popAtNewline`). The long-term polish is a header-specific unterminated flavor (a new `UnterminatedFlavor::Header` → a dedicated `P_*` code) PLUS line-scoped termination (stop the header body at the newline, like `popAtNewline`) so the error is header-specific AND the rest of the file is not consumed past the directive line. Do NOT re-engineer this cycle (scope creep) — the current behavior is consistent established fail-loud, NOT a regression. | FF11 angle-include (`c-subset.lang.json` `include-directive`/`header-body` modes); pairs with the general `popAtNewline` capability; do NOT cite in `src/` (future-work pin only) |
| `D-FFI-CROSS-HEADER-ODR-FIDELITY` | Full C ODR fidelity becomes a goal, OR a corpus surfaces two included headers declaring the same name as DISTINCT symbols. Priority: **normal** (rare — the common case of two headers declaring the SAME `extern` is correctly idempotent). | FF11 goal-2 flags a PROGRAM `extern` conflicting with an INCLUDED header's decl (`S_RedeclaredSymbol`), but NOT the header-vs-header case: two included headers each declaring the same name as DISTINCT symbols currently last-writer-wins silently (idempotent cross-tree `injectBinding` visibility — deliberately not flagged, correct for FF11's program-vs-header scope). Closing work = extend the cross-tree redeclaration check to also flag header-vs-header same-name conflicts (distinct symbols), leaving the program-vs-header path + the same-symbol idempotent case unchanged. | FF11 goal-2 (`semantic_analyzer.cpp` cross-tree redeclaration loop); surfaced by the cycle-21 self-audit; do NOT cite in `src/` (future-work pin only) |
| `D-FFI-STDINT-PTR-WIDTH-ILP32` | The first ILP32 (32-bit-pointer) target lands (e.g. i386 / armv7 / wasm32). Today EVERY DSS exec target is 64-bit (x86_64-PE/ELF, arm64-ELF/Mach-O) and LP64 + LLP64 agree on pointer width (8 bytes), so the pointer-width + greatest-width typedefs in the neutral `stdint.json`/`stddef.json` (`intptr_t`/`uintptr_t`/`intmax_t`/`uintmax_t`/`size_t`/`ptrdiff_t`) are correctly i64/u64. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated — the 64-bit-only values are correct for every current target; first surfaced by the p18 SQLite-readiness Cluster G headers cycle as the FIRST consumer of the descriptor typedef path). | When an ILP32 target is added, the pointer-width typedefs (`intptr_t`/`uintptr_t`/`size_t`/`ptrdiff_t`) must become 4-byte for THAT target while staying 8-byte on the 64-bit ones. A single literal hir-text typedef can't express "pointer-width" across both ABIs, so the close is a per-target width resolution: the descriptor declares the typedef as a width-CLASS the semantic phase resolves against the active target's `dataModel`/pointer width (NOT an `if (target == i386)` in the engine — the descriptor/loader declares the width axis, the engine reads it). Pin: an ILP32 target compiles a program asserting `sizeof(intptr_t)==4` while a 64-bit target sees 8. | cited in `src/dss-config/shippedLibs/{stdint,stddef}.json` ($comment); pairs with `D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH` + `D-FFI-WCHAR-WIDTH` (both "a descriptor baked an ABI/platform width") + `D-FFI-STDINT-LIMIT-MACROS` (the width-dependent *_MAX inherit this); SQLite-readiness Cluster G (p18) |
| `D-FFI-STDINT-LIMIT-MACROS` | A program references a `<stdint.h>` limit macro — `INT8_MAX`/`INT64_MIN`/`UINT32_MAX`/`SIZE_MAX`/`INTPTR_MAX`/etc. Today the neutral `stdint.json` ships ONLY the typedefs (no `constants` surface), so a direct limit-macro reference fails loud as an undefined identifier (never silent). Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated — SQLite's amalgamation mostly self-defines these via `#if !defined(...)`, but a direct reference would need them; first noted p18 Cluster G). | Add a `constants` surface to `stdint.json` carrying the *_MAX/*_MIN integer macros (INT8_MIN=-128 … INT64_MAX=9223372036854775807, the UINT*_MAX, plus SIZE_MAX/PTRDIFF_MIN/PTRDIFF_MAX/INTPTR_*/INTMAX_*), each integer-typed (rides as i64/u64 via the descriptor `constants` mechanism). The pointer-width + greatest-width limits (SIZE_MAX/INTPTR_MAX/INTMAX_MAX) are 64-bit-target values that inherit the ILP32 deferral [[D-FFI-STDINT-PTR-WIDTH-ILP32]]. Pin: a program using `INT32_MAX` compiles and the value is byte-exact. | cited in `src/dss-config/shippedLibs/stdint.json` ($comment); pairs with `D-FFI-STDINT-PTR-WIDTH-ILP32` (width-dependent limits) + the descriptor `constants` mechanism (FC14 item-1, cf. `limits.json`); SQLite-readiness Cluster G (p18) |
| `D-FFI-WCHAR-WIDTH` | A program needs `wchar_t` at its platform-correct width — 16-bit on Windows (PE), 32-bit on POSIX/Linux+macOS (ELF/Mach-O). The neutral `stddef.json` ships `wchar_t = i32` (the Linux+macOS width); on a PE/Windows target the conforming C width is 16-bit. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated — SQLite is UTF-8/`char` internally and uses no `wchar_t`, so the i32 default is correct for the Linux+macOS deliverables and no current corpus exercises it; first noted p18 Cluster G). | Make `wchar_t`'s width per-target (2 bytes on PE/Windows, 4 on ELF/Mach-O) via the SAME per-target width resolution as [[D-FFI-STDINT-PTR-WIDTH-ILP32]] (a `dataModel`/format-keyed typedef value the semantic phase resolves against the active target — NOT an `if (format == pe)` in the engine). Pin: a Windows-target program sees `sizeof(wchar_t)==2`, a Linux-target one sees `4`. | cited in `src/dss-config/shippedLibs/stddef.json` ($comment); pairs with `D-FFI-STDINT-PTR-WIDTH-ILP32` + `D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH`; SQLite-readiness Cluster G (p18) |
| `D-FFI-OFFSETOF-MACRO` | A program references `offsetof(type, member)` from `<stddef.h>`. Today `stddef.json` ships `size_t`/`ptrdiff_t`/`wchar_t`/`NULL` but NOT `offsetof` (a function-like macro — no descriptor surface for it yet), so an `offsetof` reference fails loud. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated — SQLite uses `offsetof` in a few spots, so this may fire during the SQLite compile; first noted p18 Cluster G). | `offsetof` is the function-like macro `((size_t)&((type*)0)->member)`. The cleanest agnostic home is a `__builtin_offsetof`-style intrinsic the semantic phase recognizes and const-folds to the member's layout byte-offset via the EXISTING FC6 aggregate-layout machinery (exact, no UB null-pointer arithmetic) — preferred over (b) shipping a textual function-like macro in the descriptor (which needs a function-like-macro surface the descriptor schema lacks today). Pin: `offsetof(struct S, b)` const-folds to the correct byte offset for a known-layout struct. | cited in `src/dss-config/shippedLibs/stddef.json` ($comment); pairs with the FC6 aggregate-layout offset computation + the descriptor `typedefs`/`constants` mechanism; SQLite-readiness Cluster G (p18) |
| `D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-MISCOMPILE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 Cluster G c2 — ROOT CAUSE = INLINING a variadic callee).** A variadic function SILENTLY MISCOMPILED under the RELEASE optimizer pipeline.
