Minimal repro (`examples`-shaped): `static int vsum(int n, ...){ va_list ap; int acc=0,i; va_start(ap,n); for(i=0;i<n;i=i+1) acc=acc+va_arg(ap,int); va_end(ap); return acc; } int main(void){ return vsum(3,10,13,19); }` → **baseline (debug pipeline) exit 42 (CORRECT); release (`--config=release`) exit 0 (WRONG)** on x86_64-PE (reproduced HEAD 0e3a550, `build/bin/dss/Release`). **Priority: HIGH — correctness-critical, silent miscompile.** BLOCKS SQLite-green RELEASE: SQLite is variadic-heavy (`sqlite3_mprintf`/`sqlite3_snprintf`/`sqlite3VXPrintf`/its test harnesses). ROOT of the blind spot: FC12 shipped the variadic feature BASELINE-ONLY (its corpus carried no `{"shippedPipeline":"release"}` arm), so the optimizer×`va_list` composition was NEVER witnessed — exactly the masked-effectiveness trap the dss-cycle `release`-arm corpus rule exists to prevent. | ✅ (CLOSED) ROOT CAUSE = the INLINER inlined the static single-call variadic callee into `main`; the spliced `va_start` leaves (`VaRegSaveAreaAddr`/`VaOverflowArgAreaAddr`/`VaHomeArgAreaAddr`) bound to MAIN's frame (which never spilled the varargs) → `ap` read garbage. FIX = `inlineLegalityGate` (`src/opt/passes/inlining.cpp`) refuses any callee whose body contains one of those three va_start leaves — the universal "a variadic fn that calls va_start is not inlinable" rule; agnostic (MIR opcodes only), sibling of the `RecvByValueStackParam` refusal. PINS (both red-on-disable): `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp::VariadicCalleeCallingVaStartIsNotInlined` (callsInlined==0) + corpus `examples/c-subset/varargs_inline_release` (release arm exit 42, PE-runnable). ★ Closing this surfaced 2 MORE variadic-release optimizer bugs the FC12 BASELINE-ONLY corpus masked (a CLUSTER, NOT a single bug) → [[D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-MEM2REG-PHI]] + [[D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-ARGINDEX]]; the broad "add a `release` arm to EVERY variadic example" closure follows each fix (this cycle adds only `varargs_inline_release` — the other arms stay off until their bug lands). | CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 c2); cited in `src/opt/passes/inlining.cpp` (gate refusal) + `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp` + `examples/c-subset/varargs_inline_release/`; pairs with `D-OPT7-INLINE-FRAME-SENSITIVE-INTRINSIC` (the hypothetical-intrinsic sibling — va_start in DSS is these dedicated MIR leaves, not an IntrinsicCall) + `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VARIADIC-SIGNATURE` + the FC12 variadic cycle + the dss-cycle `release`-arm corpus discipline |
| `D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-MEM2REG-PHI` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 Cluster G c3) — was the variadic SYMPTOM of a GENERAL Mem2Reg bug; fixed together as [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-LOOP-BODY-LOCAL-DEAD-PHI]].** Compiling `examples/c-subset/varargs_win64_sum` under RELEASE aborted FAIL-LOUD `Mem2Reg fatal: phi ... has no reaching value from predecessor`. ★ The c3 PROBE OVERTURNED the scope: it is NOT Win64/variadic-specific — the trigger is the `int iv = va_arg(ap,int); double dv = va_arg(ap,double);` LOOP-BODY-LOCALS, and a minimal NON-variadic `int f(int n){int t=0,i=0;while(i<n){int iv=i*2;t=t+iv;i=i+1;}return t;}` reproduces the IDENTICAL fatal. The variadic example was just the FIRST to exercise it under release (FC12 shipped the variadic corpus baseline-only). | ✅ (CLOSED) FIXED by [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-LOOP-BODY-LOCAL-DEAD-PHI]] (semi-pruned SSA — see that row for the mechanism). `varargs_win64_sum` gained a `{"shippedPipeline":"release"}` arm (exit 39, was Mem2Reg-fatal); the general witness is `examples/c-subset/loop_body_local`. | CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 c3); subsumed by [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-LOOP-BODY-LOCAL-DEAD-PHI]]; pairs with [[D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-MISCOMPILE]] (the inlining sibling) + [[D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-ARGINDEX]] (the still-open 3rd cluster member) |
| `D-OPT-MEM2REG-DEAD-PHI-PRUNE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 Cluster G c3 — the COMPLETE fix; HIGH, GENERAL Mem2Reg correctness bug).** Under the RELEASE pipeline Mem2Reg ABORTED FAIL-LOUD (`phi at block ... has no reaching value from predecessor`) whenever a promoted alloca got a DEAD phi at an IDF block where it was not LIVE but its incoming was undefined. TWO classes: (1) a loop-BODY-LOCAL (`while(){ int x=expr; use(x); }`, dead at the header) — the first-found symptom [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-LOOP-BODY-LOCAL-DEAD-PHI]]; (2) the SUBTLER NESTED-LOOP case — an inner counter `j` re-initialized each OUTER iteration is LIVE across the inner back-edge yet DEAD at the OUTER header. Class (2) (and a single loop whose body contains an `if`-merge) crashes with ZERO body-locals → an ENORMOUS class of ordinary C (nested loops saturate SQLite). The c3 pr-review's RUNTIME battery caught (2) after the initial semi-pruned commit (ecf40c3) only handled (1) — the green-but-incomplete trap; upgraded to the complete fix the same cycle. | ✅ (CLOSED) FIX = FULLY-PRUNED SSA (Choi/Cytron/Sarkar) in `src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp`: place a phi for alloca A at IDF block S ONLY IF A is LIVE-IN at S (a backward live-in dataflow: `live_in[B]=use[B] ∪ (live_out[B]\def[B])`, use=upward-exposed Load, def=Store, alloca-site NOT a def). Strictly stronger than semi-pruned (upward-exposure): a name DEAD at one IDF block but live elsewhere (the nested counter) is pruned THERE specifically. PROVABLY SAFE: every KEPT phi is at a block where A is live → a needed merge is never pruned (no miscompile, verified by a base==release battery: diamond/induction/nested/triple-nested/body-local-live-across all correct on both pipelines). PRESERVES fail-loud: a genuine uninitialized read IS live-in at its use → phi kept → still aborts (verified). PINS (red-on-disable): `tests/opt/test_mem2reg.cpp::{LoopBodyLocalAllocaNeedsNoPhi, NestedLoopInnerCounterNoOuterHeaderPhi}` + corpus `examples/c-subset/{loop_body_local (exit 20), nested_loop_release (exit 4)}` release arms. Agnostic (MIR/SSA only). | CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 c3); cited in `src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp` (the live-in gate) + `tests/opt/test_mem2reg.cpp` + `examples/c-subset/nested_loop_release/`; subsumes [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-LOOP-BODY-LOCAL-DEAD-PHI]] (class 1); pairs with the Choi/Cytron/Sarkar pruned-SSA literature |
| `D-OPT-MEM2REG-CONDITIONAL-INIT-UNDEF` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (post-sqlite C23+perf arc, cycle 1) — the RELEASE-pipeline Mem2Reg abort on benign CONDITIONAL-INIT; the blocker to `--config=release` sqlite.** Root: Mem2Reg's rename walk `std::abort()`'d whenever a promoted alloca had NO reaching value at a phi-incoming edge (`mem2reg.cpp:627`) or a Load (`:599`) — a LIVE-but-conditionally-defined local (`int x; if(c) x=1; return x+c;`) or a dead-path uninit read. Both are VALID C (gcc compiles sqlite, full of them); p18 [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-DEAD-PHI-PRUNE]] deliberately "preserved the fatal" (its comment), which was WRONG — a compiler must compile valid C. DISCOVERED by the T1.0 perf-profiling probe: sqlite-green was only ever verified in the DEFAULT (debug) config, which runs no Mem2Reg; `--config=release` aborted at the first sqlite conditional-init (block 1492). FIX (agnostic, MIR/SSA only): materialize undef-as-zero for the missing incoming — a new `ReachingValue::Kind::ZeroConst` (value = element TypeId.v), ONE `Const 0` per element type emitted in the ENTRY block (dominates every edge → verifier-legal incoming everywhere; entry is `selectBlocks[0]` so it precedes any Load resolve). GPR-Const-zeroable element types stay PROMOTED (the common case, no perf loss); FPR (float — no float-immediate) + aggregates have no scalar zero → the alloca is DE-PROMOTED (left as memory = what debug does; detected PRE-walk via `liveIn[entry]` = read-before-write), a narrow perf loss pinned [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-FPR-CONDITIONAL-INIT-RODATA-ZERO]]. Uninit-read DIAGNOSTIC responsibility moves to a frontend warning [[D-SEMANTIC-MAYBE-UNINITIALIZED-WARN]]. Independent design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (caught the float-`Const`-poison blocker → the de-promotion; resetPerFunction clears + fail-loud-on-miss — all folded). WITNESSES: 3 red-on-disable units (`ConditionallyInitializedLiveAllocaZeroFillsUndefEdge` [phi zero-fill + MirVerifier dominance] / `...LoadZeroFillsUninitPath` / `...FloatAllocaIsDepromoted`) + 2 release-arm corpus (`examples/c-subset/cond_init_release`→2, `cond_init_float_release`→1) + the 2-TU sqlite `--config=release` compile clears the Mem2Reg wall + the minimal repro `int f(int c){int x;if(c)x=1;return x+c;}`+`main(argc)`→exit 2. | ✅ (CLOSED) | post-sqlite C23+perf arc cycle 1 (2026-07-07); `src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp` + `tests/opt/test_mem2reg.cpp` + `examples/c-subset/{cond_init_release,cond_init_float_release}`; discovered by T1.0 profiling; pairs with [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-DEAD-PHI-PRUNE]] (complements the p18 dead-phi prune) |
| `D-OPT-MEM2REG-FPR-CONDITIONAL-INIT-RODATA-ZERO` | ⏳ **GATED (perf; opened 2026-07-07 by [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-CONDITIONAL-INIT-UNDEF]]).** A CONDITIONALLY-INITIALIZED FLOAT/aggregate local is DE-PROMOTED (left as memory) at the missing-incoming site because a float has no directly-lowerable zero `Const` (register machines have no float-immediate; DSS routes float constants through rodata `GlobalAddr+Load`) and aggregates have no scalar zero. CORRECT but a minor perf loss (those allocas stay in memory under release; rare — most floats are init-before-use). Trigger: a profile shows conditionally-initialized float promotion matters. Resolution: mirror the HIR→MIR float-literal promotion (`hir_to_mir.cpp:559-578`) — mint an anonymous rodata `MirGlobal` holding the zero bit-pattern + `GlobalAddr`+`Load` in the entry block — so FPR conditional-init allocas promote too; fail-loud-in-place for F16/F128 (no encodings). | (gated — perf refinement; de-promotion is correct today) | post-sqlite C23+perf arc; `src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp` (the de-promotion loop) + the `hir_to_mir.cpp` float-promotion precedent; plan 12 MIR/LIR optimizer |
| `D-SEMANTIC-MAYBE-UNINITIALIZED-WARN` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-CONDITIONAL-INIT-UNDEF]]).** Removing the Mem2Reg abort on conditional-init (a compiler must compile valid C) dropped the incidental "genuine uninitialized read" signal it provided. Its correct home is a FRONTEND/semantic WARNING (gcc/Clang's `-Wmaybe-uninitialized`), not a hard optimizer abort — a promoted automatic scalar Load not dominated by any Store, no address taken. Trigger: a maybe-uninitialized diagnostic is prioritized (or a corpus wants it). Resolution: a semantic-tier maybe-uninitialized warning. NOT a blocker — silently materializing undef-as-zero is conforming (LLVM's mem2reg does the same; the diagnostic is a frontend concern). | (gated — a diagnostic-quality improvement, non-blocking) | post-sqlite C23+perf arc; `src/semantic/` (frontend); the mem2reg.cpp comment records the move |
| `D-OPT1-VERIFY-FREQUENCY-CONFIG` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (post-sqlite perf arc) — config-driven optimizer verify frequency (the LLVM/GCC posture).** `optimize()` ran a full-module `MirVerifier` after EVERY pass under ALL build modes (`D-OPT1-VERIFY-AFTER-EVERY-PASS`) — on sqlite that is ~11s × (9 passes × up-to-4 iterations × 3 CU-runs) ≈ 10–20 min of pure re-verification (surfaced by the T1.0/perf profiling of the >30-min release compile). FIX: a config-driven `OptPipeline::verifyEveryPass` (default TRUE = the DEVELOPER posture, LLVM `-verify-each` / GCC `--enable-checking=yes`, which pinpoints the offending pass); `release.pipeline.json` sets FALSE → verify ONCE after the whole pipeline before codegen (the PRODUCTION posture — trust tested passes, verify at the boundary). Agnostic (a pipeline-config bool, NOT a build-mode branch in the engine); overridable per-pipeline (flip release→verify-each on demand to hunt a release-only miscompile). Also split `opt-verify` out as its own `PhaseTimers` phase + added env-gated `DSS_OPT_TRACE` per-pass / per-function optimizer instrumentation (kept — zero-cost when off). Windows ctest 397/397 (opt+examples+pipeline); the `ShippedReleasePipelineCompilesBothProbeShapes` test's explicit final-verify still green (result.ok now implies FINAL-module cleanliness). The RELEASE-optimize perf ROOT (every pass rebuilds the module + recomputes all analyses from scratch — no caching, no change-tracking) is the [[project_v0_0_2_post_sqlite_c23_perf_roadmap_2026_07_07]] LLVM-style arc (analysis caching + in-place passes). ★ **EXTENDED 2026-07-07 to MARKER MAINTENANCE:** `rederiveStructCfMarkers(mir)` (whole-module preds + per-fn RPO + dom + derivation, ~13s/call on sqlite) ran after EVERY SimplifyCfg + EVERY Inlining (8×/compile = 127s measured — ~91% of Inlining's and ~78% of SimplifyCfg's cost). Markers feed ONLY the verifier (grep-verified: no optimization decision and no codegen reads them; pass rebuilds copy them through) → `runSimplifyCfg`/`runInlining` gained `bool maintainMarkers = true` (dev/test posture byte-identical), optimize() threads `verifyEveryPass` into it and, under the release posture, re-stamps ONCE from the FINAL CFG before the final verify. Inlining 62→7.3s (−88%), SimplifyCfg 64→14.4s (−78%); compile 5m54→4m24. WITNESS: `test_optimizer.cpp::ReleasePostureRederivesMarkersOnceAfterPipeline` (canonicality-by-idempotence over a fold shape whose stale IfThen marker provably differs — dropping the final re-stamp reds it); 3-leg 566/566. | ✅ (CLOSED) | post-sqlite perf arc (2026-07-07); `src/opt/optimizer.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/opt/optimizer_json.cpp` + `src/dss-config/pipelines/release.pipeline.json` + `src/core/substrate/phase_timers.{hpp,cpp}`; plan 22 optimizer / the LLVM-style optimizer perf arc |
| `D-OPT-CSE-ANALYSIS-HOIST` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (post-sqlite perf arc, LLVM-style Phase 1) — CSE was the single biggest release-optimizer pass on sqlite (118s of a 7m39s optimize); now ~56s.** Root: `mirBuildPredecessors(mir)` (WHOLE-MODULE) was recomputed (a) per function in `CsePolicy::analyze` (cse.cpp:158; runCse calls it per function → O(numFunctions × moduleSize)) AND (b) per Load-CSE query inside `mirRegionBetween` (mir_alias.hpp), whose intersection loop ALSO scanned all module blocks per query. FIX (behavior-preserving, agnostic MIR/CFG plumbing): compute `mirBuildPredecessors(mir)` ONCE per `runCse` call (invariant while `mir` is read-only — the rebuild writes a separate builder, finalized only after the loop) + thread it (`const&`) into `analyze()` + `mirRegionBetween` (CSE-only caller, zero other blast radius); scope `mirRegionBetween`'s block-intersection to the function-local `fwd` reachable set + `std::sort` (byte-identical result). Independent design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES: PROVED byte-identical CSE decisions — cse.cpp:158 ALREADY passed whole-module preds to `computeMirDomTree`, and `runChkCore` gates preds by the function's RPO (mir_dom.cpp:92) → identical dom tree by IDENTITY (the hoist changes call FREQUENCY, not the argument); the consumer `mirAnyMayAliasingStoreInRegion` is a pure order/dup-invariant existential. Folded: intersection invariants+sort, `const&`+contract docs, a `preds.size()==mir.blockCount()` fail-loud assert (catches a stale/foreign preds), a decision-identity test. WITNESSES: 3-leg gate 565/565 (Win Debug + WSL x86_64 + qemu arm64; the new test is a gtest TEST inside the existing test_cse binary — no new ctest entry); `test_cse.cpp::CrossBlockLoadCseDecidedPerFunctionInMultiFunctionModule` (cross-block Load-CSE decided per-function in a 2-fn module — a broken scope mis-counts); `strict_alias_cse_capstone` + `release_pipeline_{loop,recursion}` corpus unchanged (exit codes identical = decisions unchanged); before/after `DSS_OPT_TRACE` Cse 118s→56s. ★ RESIDUAL: the remaining ~56s = the inherent O(K² Load-pairs × O(function-region)) region-walk COUNT — the [[D-OPT-MEMORYSSA-CLOBBER-WALK]] charter (a real MemorySSA), still trigger-gated. ★ APPLIED ×3 (the same argument-identity hoist): Cse (`69e3281`) · Licm (`141f0b1`, folded into the MemorySSA cycle) · **SimplifyCfg (2026-07-07 — the per-function whole-module `mirBuildPredecessors` was ALLOCATION-bound: ~12000 whole-module vector-of-vectors allocs → 4; 120→64s −47%; `test_simplify_cfg.cpp::BlockMergeDecidedPerFunctionInMultiFunctionModule` decision-identity pin)**. The per-pass hoists are now DONE; the remaining ~4×60s floor is the analysis RECOMPUTED per function per iteration → Phase 2/3 (dirty-set + cross-iteration SymbolId cache) is the next lever. | ✅ (CLOSED) | post-sqlite perf arc, LLVM-style Phase 1 (2026-07-07); `src/opt/passes/{cse,licm,simplify_cfg}.cpp` + `src/opt/analysis/mir_alias.hpp` + `tests/opt/`; pairs with [[D-OPT-MEMORYSSA-CLOBBER-WALK]] (the residual's gated charter — ✅ CLOSED 2026-07-07 via `MirMemoryClobbers`, plan-22 §3.1); part of [[project_v0_0_2_llvm_style_optimizer_arc_2026_07_07]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-ANON-MEMBER-PROMOTION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (T3 full-C23 / FC16) — anonymous struct/union member SEMANTICS (C11/C23 §6.7.2.1 ¶13), clearing the S0017 battery gap.** The grammar half was already done (an anonymous no-declarator struct/union member parses); this closes the SEMANTIC half: an anonymous struct/union's members are PROMOTED into the enclosing composite's namespace (`s.i` where `i` lives in an anon `union{}` inside `struct S`). Layout needed ZERO changes (`computeLayout` already lays out a Union/Struct-typed field — union members at offset 0, struct members sequential). Implemented as: two `SymbolRecord` fields (`isAnonymousMember` + `anonAncestorPath`); a Pass-1.5 THREE-way branch (promote-anon-composite / bit-field / declares-nothing — replacing the bit-field-only gate) with a recursive `promoteAnonMembers` (records the ancestor chain, collision-checks against the enclosing composite's OWN field scope — NOT the parent chain, since struct members are a separate name space per C 6.2.1); recursive `findPromotedField` in `resolveMemberAccess` (sibling-ambiguity → `MemberResolution::AmbiguousField`); and HIR chain synthesis in `combineMember` (one synthetic `MemberAccess` hop per `anonAncestorPath` level, each hop's fieldIndex validated against THAT level's composite by the HIR verifier). Collisions + ambiguity fail loud (`S_RedeclaredSymbol`). Built by a specialized agent under the blueprint; INDEPENDENT design-audit (GO) + INDEPENDENT post-build code-review (Feature B "CORRECT, could not break it" — verified aliasing/nesting/arrow/collision/ambiguity/bitfield-in-anon/global-coexistence end-to-end with discriminating exit codes). WITNESSES: `examples/c-subset/anon_union_member` (aliased) + `anon_struct_member` (sequential), both release-arm all legs; 8 semantic-tier tests; the S0017 probe now compiles+runs on all 3 targets. 2 bugs found+fixed during verification (bit-field-in-anon-composite false error; collision walking the parent scope chain). | ✅ (CLOSED) | T3/FC16 (2026-07-07); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_model.hpp` + `semantic_analyzer.cpp` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` + tests + examples; plan-23 FC16 |
| `D-CSUBSET-GENERIC-SELECTION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (T3 full-C23 / FC16) — `_Generic` generic selection (C11/C23 §6.5.1.1), clearing the last P0009 battery gap.** A primary EXPRESSION `_Generic(controlling-expr, type: e, …, default: e)` whose result type+value is the association whose type-name is compatible with the controlling expression's (lvalue-converted) type; exactly one match or the default, else fail loud. Config-driven vocabulary (`GenericKeyword` + `genericExpr`/`genericAssoc`/`genericTypedAssoc`/`genericDefaultAssoc` rules; typed assoc uses `castTypeRef` + `commitRequiresTypeName` so a value-in-type-position fails loud). THE design choice (mirroring va_arg/static_assert): SEMANTIC-tier selection at Pass-2 (types only resolved there) — resolve the controlling type (lvalue-converted, top-level VolatileQual stripped), match each assoc's `castTypeRef` via `sameType` (interned TypeId equality on the canonicalized lattice), enforce exactly-one-or-default, STAMP the `_Generic` node's result type + record the WINNING association's result-expr NodeId in a new `nodeToSelectedExpr` side-table (recording the NODE, not just the type — two assocs can share a type but differ in value); CST→HIR `lowerGeneric` lowers ONLY the selected sub-expression (its type+value), discarding the rest (the "non-selected branch NOT lowered" HIR pin proves §6.5.1.1p3). New `S_GenericSelectionNoMatch=0xE02A` + `S_GenericSelectionAmbiguous=0xE02B`, both UNSUPPRESSABLE (constraint-violation-with-silent-failure-risk, the static_assert precedent). Agnostic (config-keyed RuleIds; toy/tsql never see it). Independent blueprint+implement + MY verify: `tc(i)*10+tc(d)`→13 on pe64+x86_64+arm64, default-only→7, no-match→S002A (no binary), ambiguous→S002B, value-in-type→S0006, typedef assoc matches, pointer discrimination correct. WITNESSES: `examples/c-subset/generic_selection` + `generic_default` (release-arm all legs); diagnostic-corpus `generic_no_match`; 6 semantic + 2 HIR unit tests (incl. non-selected-NOT-lowered); the operand-alt-order parser pin updated. ⏳ DEFERRED (pinned): [[D-CSUBSET-GENERIC-NONSELECTED-BRANCH-UNCHECKED]]. | ✅ (CLOSED) | T3/FC16 (2026-07-07); `src/analysis/semantic/{semantic_analyzer.cpp,semantic_config.hpp,semantic_model.hpp}` + `src/core/types/{grammar_schema_json.cpp,hir_lowering_config.hpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp}` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` + `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` + tests + examples; plan-23 FC16 |
| `D-CSUBSET-GENERIC-NONSELECTED-BRANCH-UNCHECKED` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by [[D-CSUBSET-GENERIC-SELECTION]]).** C11/C23 §6.5.1.1p3: the NON-selected `_Generic` association expressions are unevaluated + impose no constraints beyond syntactic validity. The DSS lowering honors this exactly (a non-selected branch is NEVER lowered), BUT the semantic Pass-2 whole-tree walk type-resolves EVERY node, so a non-selected branch referencing an undeclared identifier (`double: nope`) wrongly errors S0001. FAIL-LOUD, never a miscompile — and arguably safer (catches typos); real `_Generic` usage dispatches over VALID expressions by type, so unreached in practice. Fully honoring the standard needs suppressing diagnostics under non-selected assoc subtrees (a larger whole-tree-walk change). TRIGGER: a real consumer relies on a non-selected branch being ill-formed-but-accepted (rare — the classic type-safe-macro idiom uses valid branches). | (gated — the standard idiom uses valid branches; no shipped consumer needs the relaxation) | opened by the FC16 _Generic cycle; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the Pass-2 walk) |
| `D-CSUBSET-STATIC-ASSERT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (T3 full-C23 / FC16) — `_Static_assert`/`static_assert` (C11/C23 §6.7.10).** A compile-time-assertion DECLARATION at file + block scope, both keyword spellings + the C23 1-arg (no-message) form. Config-driven vocabulary (2 keywords + a `staticAssertDecl` grammar rule with an optional-message trailer) + generic-engine mechanism. THE KEY design choice: evaluated at SEMANTIC-analysis time via the existing `constIntExpr` (pass2Post, post-order → the condition is fully type-stamped), so `sizeof(T)`, enum constants, and arithmetic/comparisons all fold — a prior lowering-tier attempt via `evalCstConstInt` (which lacks a `resolveSizeof` closure) falsely rejected the dominant `sizeof(T)==N` idiom. `SemanticConfig::staticAssertRule` (new) keys the check; the decl lowers to NOTHING (the `hirKind:Skip` mapping → also fixes the 1-arg peelToCore descent: `Skip` puts the rule in `ruleMap_` so peelToCore stops on it). New `S_StaticAssertFailed=0xE029` (Error; message discriminates false-vs-non-constant), made UNSUPPRESSABLE (a suppressed constraint violation would fail the build with zero diagnostics — the S_TypeNameDeclaratorNotAbstract precedent). Fail-loud: a false assert halts (no binary); a true one compiles to nothing. Independent design blueprints + INDEPENDENT post-build code-review ("fundamentally sound, no critical bugs; both prior-failure areas — sizeof-folding + 1-arg — correctly handled + empirically confirmed"). WITNESSES: `examples/c-subset/static_assert_true` (sizeof condition, release-arm all legs) + `static_assert_c23_1arg`; diagnostic-corpus goldens (`static_assert_failed`, `static_assert_nonconstant`); semantic + HIR unit tests; my own 11-case WSL probe (sizeof/enum/1-arg/spelling/top-level/float all correct). ⏳ DEFERRED (pinned): [[D-CSUBSET-STATIC-ASSERT-STRUCT-MEMBER]] — §6.7.10 also permits a static_assert as a STRUCT-DECLARATION (member position); currently only topLevel+statement scopes admit it (a struct-body `_Static_assert` fails loud P0009). Not a blocker (sqlite uses zero static_assert); trigger = a real consumer needs it. | ✅ (CLOSED) | T3/FC16 (2026-07-07); `src/analysis/semantic/{semantic_analyzer.cpp,semantic_config.hpp}` + `src/core/types/{grammar_schema_json.cpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp}` + `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` + tests + examples; plan-23 FC16 |
| `D-CSUBSET-STATIC-ASSERT-STRUCT-MEMBER` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by [[D-CSUBSET-STATIC-ASSERT]]).** C11/C23 §6.7.10 permits a `static_assert-declaration` as a `struct-declaration` (`struct S { int x; _Static_assert(sizeof(int)==4, "x"); };`). The FC16 static_assert cycle admitted `staticAssertDecl` only in the `topLevel` + `statement` alts, NOT `structField`/`structMemberDeclaratorList`, so a struct-body static_assert fails loud (P0009). FAIL-LOUD, never silent. TRIGGER: a real consumer (a shipped header or the C23 corpus) uses a struct-member static_assert. Resolution: admit `staticAssertDecl` in the struct-member alt + route its semantic check the same way (it mints no field). | (gated — sqlite + the current corpus use zero struct-member static_assert) | opened by the FC16 static_assert cycle; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (structField alt) + the existing semantic check |
| `D-CSUBSET-MEMBER-ALIGNAS` | ✅ **CARRIER CLOSED 2026-07-07 (T3 full-C23 groundwork) — the type-system CARRIER for C11/C23 `alignas` on a struct/union MEMBER; a PRECISE MIRROR of the c107 `fieldOffsets` channel ([[D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-UNION-OVERLAY]]).** A struct may carry a per-field EXPLICIT alignment override span `CompositeFields.fieldAligns` (bytes, power of two; `0` = no override → natural alignment). ALL-fields-or-EMPTY (a partial set is a caller bug, fail loud at `completeComposite`); enters `contentDeclSiteKey` GUARDED on non-empty so an align-free struct hashes BYTE-IDENTICALLY to today (ZERO TypeId churn — every existing composite keeps its exact declSiteKey) while `struct{alignas(16) int x;}` is a DISTINCT TypeId from `struct{int x;}`. `computeLayout`'s ordinary (non-bitfield) struct arm folds the override into each field's natural alignment with MAX semantics (a member alignas RAISES, never LOWERS) — applied to both the ordinary field path AND the flexible-array-member sub-case (`alignas(16) int fam[];`); `Alignment::fromBytes` fail-louds (nullopt→no layout) on a stored non-pow2/>256 value (an upstream bug). Threaded end-to-end exactly like offsets: the new 5-arg `structType` overload + `completeComposite` param + `hasExplicitAligns`/`explicitFieldAlign` accessors (type_interner.hpp/type_lattice.cpp) → reintern carry (type_reintern.cpp, so a reinterned member-aligned struct keeps its TypeId) → HIR type-text round-trip via a NEW per-field `~<align>` marker (non-colliding with the offset `@<off>`; the two channels are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE — `completeComposite` fail-louds if BOTH aligns and offsets are set, since explicit offsets override alignment wholesale). MIR text was NOT touched (mirrors c107 EXACTLY — MIR text never round-tripped offsets either; a member-aligned struct is not MIR-text-reachable in the carrier tier). WITNESSES: `TypeInterner.MemberAlignsEnterContentIdentityZeroChurn` (empty-span == 2-arg TypeId; aligns={16} distinct) + `TypeLayout.{MemberAlignasRaisesStructAlignAndSize, MemberAlignasNeverLowersBelowNatural, MemberAlignasRaisesFollowingFieldOffset}` + `TypeReintern.MemberAlignsSurviveReintern` + `HirText.MemberAlignsRoundTrip` + `ParseTypeFromText.MemberAlignsParseAndForkIdentity` (all red-on-disable). Full existing type-interner/layout/reintern/hir-text suites + the 573-case Debug ctest stay green (the zero-churn proof). ✅ FOLLOW-UP LANDED 2026-07-07 by [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS]] (the `alignas`/`_Alignas` PARSING + SEMANTICS frontend that POPULATES `fieldAligns` from a declared member `alignas(N)`/`alignas(T)`). The frontend cycle ALSO extended the carrier's `computeLayout` UNION arm to fold `explicitFieldAlign` (the struct arm already did; the union arm was a carrier gap — a `union { alignas(16) char c; int i; }` now correctly aligns to 16). | ✅ (CARRIER CLOSED; frontend LANDED via [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS]]) | T3 full-C23 groundwork (2026-07-07); `src/core/types/type_lattice/{type_interner.hpp,type_lattice.cpp,type_layout.cpp,type_reintern.cpp}` + `src/hir/hir_text.cpp` + `tests/core/type_lattice/{test_type_interner,test_type_reintern}.cpp` + `tests/core/test_type_layout.cpp` + `tests/hir/test_hir_text.cpp`; a precise mirror of [[D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-UNION-OVERLAY]] (c107); plan-23 full-C23 alignas |
| `D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (T3 full-C23) — the `_Alignas`/`alignas` alignment-specifier FRONTEND + SEMANTICS (C11/C23 §6.7.5), targeting the [[D-CSUBSET-MEMBER-ALIGNAS]] carrier.** Config-driven vocabulary (2 keywords `_Alignas`/`alignas` + `alignasSpec` [`kw ( alignasArg )`] + `alignasArg` [SPECULATIVE `castTypeRef` (type form) \| `assignmentExpr` (value form), with the shared `commitRequiresTypeName` binder triage sizeof/va_arg use]) admitted in the variable specifier alts (`singleDeclSpecifier`/`localDeclSpecifier`) + a NEW `structMemberDeclSpecifiers` prefix on `structField`/`unionField` (declared as their `specifierPrefix` so decl_prefix_strip drops it before the head:0/declaratorList:1 count — zero index churn) + `semantics.alignas {specRule, argChild, typeFormRule}`. SEMANTIC path (`semantic_analyzer.cpp`, Pass-1 declarator-mode, mirroring the bit-field-width plumbing): `evalOneAlignasSpec` reads the operand — TYPE form → `_Alignof(T)` via `computeLayout(...)->align`; VALUE form → `constIntExpr` (the SAME evaluator static_assert/array-dims use, so `alignas(16)`/`alignas(2*8)`/`alignas(ENUM_VAL)` fold) — validates (power-of-two → S_AlignasNotPowerOfTwo; >256 → S_AlignasExceedsMax [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-EXCEEDS-MAX]]; < the declared type's natural align → S_AlignasWeakerThanNatural, 6.7.5p4; non-constant → S_AlignasNonConstant; `alignas(0)` = NO-OP per 6.7.5p3), and STORES: `SymbolRecord.explicitAlignment` (a NEW field) for a VARIABLE (deliberately UNCONSUMED by variable codegen — a SEPARATE deferred task), OR the composite's `fieldAligns` span (gathered at Pass-1 completion exactly like `fieldBitWidths`, all-fields-or-EMPTY for zero churn) for a MEMBER → `computeLayout` raises the layout END-TO-END. CONTEXT checks (6.7.5p2, S_AlignasInvalidContext): a FUNCTION (definition/prototype) or a TYPEDEF (kind Type) declaration, or a BIT-FIELD member. All 5 codes UNSUPPRESSABLE (constraint-violation-with-silent-failure-risk, the static_assert precedent). AUDIT-caught fix folded: the TYPE-form branch resolves the type BEFORE the layout-params check so a value optimistically parsed as a type-name (`alignas(nc)`) fails loud S_AlignasNonConstant even in a layout-free analysis, NEVER silent. Agnostic (config-keyed RuleIds; toy/tsql never see it). WITNESSES: 16 semantic unit tests (`SemanticAnalyzerCSubset.Alignas*` — parse both spellings/forms, variable store, member+union layout end-to-end via `_Alignof`/`sizeof`, each constraint red-on-disable) + 5 diagnostic-corpus goldens (`alignas_{not_power_of_two,exceeds_max,on_function,on_bitfield,nonconstant}`) + a pe64 e2e probe (`struct S{alignas(16) char c;}` → `_Alignof`==16, `sizeof`==16; `struct T{char c; alignas(8) int i;}` → offsetof(i)==8). ⏳ DEFERRED (pinned): [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-VARIABLE-CODEGEN]] (thread `explicitAlignment` to globals/locals emit) + [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-TYPEDEF-PARAM-PARSE]] (a `typedef alignas(…)` / `f(alignas(…) int)` is a fail-loud PARSE error, not the S_AlignasInvalidContext semantic code — the dedicated `typedefDecl`/`param` rules don't admit a strippable mid/prefix alignas without extra grammar surface). | ✅ (CLOSED) | T3 full-C23 (2026-07-07); `src/analysis/semantic/{semantic_analyzer.cpp,semantic_model.hpp}` + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp,type_lattice/type_layout.cpp}` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (linkage-ignore) + `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` + tests + corpus; targets the [[D-CSUBSET-MEMBER-ALIGNAS]] carrier; plan-23 full-C23 |
| `D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-EXCEEDS-MAX` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (folded into [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS]]).** The `alignas(N)` OVER-LARGE constraint: a value that exceeds the maximum representable alignment (256 bytes — the `Alignment` newtype cap, `alignment.hpp`; no producer in the pipeline emits > 256) fails loud `S_AlignasExceedsMax=0xE02D` — a code DISTINCT from `S_AlignasNotPowerOfTwo` so an over-large-but-pow2 value (`alignas(512)`) reports the precise reason. Unsuppressable. WITNESS: `SemanticAnalyzerCSubset.AlignasExceedsMaxFailsLoud` + corpus `alignas_exceeds_max` (both assert S002D AND zero S002C). | ✅ (CLOSED) | folded into [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS]] (2026-07-07); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` + `src/core/types/{parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp}` + `src/core/types/alignment.hpp` (the 256 cap) |
| `D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-VARIABLE-CODEGEN` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (T3 full-C23) — thread the stored `SymbolRecord.explicitAlignment` into GLOBAL + LOCAL variable codegen.** GLOBALS: a NEW declaration-keyed HIR side-map `HirAlignmentMap`/`AlignmentAttr` (a PRECISE mirror of the mutability side-table) is populated in CST→HIR (`recordAlignment`, sparse — only a real >0 override) from the bound symbol's `explicitAlignment` at every Global + local VarDecl lowering site; HIR→MIR stamps it onto a NEW `MirGlobal.alignment` (via a new `addGlobal(..., alignmentBytes)` param + `Mir::globalAlignmentBytes`), and the assembler's `lowerMirGlobalsToDataItems` raises the emitted data item's alignment to `max(natural, explicit)` at ALL SIX `.alignment=` arms (a plain `alignas(32) int g;` is a SCALAR/zero-init → the primitive+scalar arms, NOT just the aggregate `lay->align` ones). LOCALS: the effective alignment `max(naturalAlign(type), alignas)` rides a NEW MIR `MirInst.payload2` channel set at `allocaForLocal` (the primary `payload` is the aggregate byte size); MIR→LIR aggregates the per-function MAX into `MirToLirResult.funcLocalAlignments` (SymbolId-keyed → survives the LIR rebuilds, mirrors `SehFuncletParent`), threaded through the pipeline to `materializeCallingConvention` → `computeFrameLayout`. THE FRAME FIX (2 mechanisms, both gated on `maxLocalAlign>0` so every other frame is BYTE-IDENTICAL): (1) `localAreaAlignPad` — pads the local-area base up to the local's alignment when the raw base (outgoing+saved+spill) doesn't already satisfy it (an ODD outgoing-arg count leaves the x86 base ≡8 mod 16); folded into `localAreaOffset()` + `rawPreShadow`; (2) the LEAF-frame RSP bias — a leaf function with an over-aligned local takes the SAME `alignedSizeWithBias(callPushBytes)` path a non-leaf uses, so its post-prologue RSP is stack-aligned (a plain `alignUp` leaves RSP ≡callPushBytes on an x86 return-address-push ABI → the local `callPushBytes` bytes off; on an LR ABI callPushBytes==0 so the forms coincide — no arm64 change). The alignment ceiling is `cc.stackAlignment` (16 every ABI), NOT slotWidth (=8 on arm64, which would wrongly reject a 16-aligned local there). ★ AUDIT-CAUGHT during my own runtime verification: the effective-alignment channel + `MirGlobal.alignment` are DROPPED by every MIR rebuild that copies insts/globals — the fix required preserving `payload2` in `MirFunctionRebuilder::emitInstruction` + `mir_merge` + BOTH inliner clone arms, AND `globalAlignmentBytes` in `cloneGlobalsOrCarveOut`/`cloneGlobalsVerbatim`/`dce`/`mir_merge` (a partial thread silently under-aligned in the release/inlined path — the PE global witness was a COINCIDENTAL pass until the rebuild-preservation was complete; ELF `.bss` readelf proved 0x…b8 [8-aligned] → 0x…c0 [32-aligned] after the fix). WITNESSES (all UNFORGEABLE runtime + red-on-disable): `examples/c-subset/alignas_local_aligned` (a NON-LEAF/leaf `alignas(16)` local with an ODD 9-arg stack-overflow callee → `&buf&15==0` → 42, release arm all 4 targets; RED-on-disable → 1 confirmed by disabling the pad+bias) + `alignas_global_over16` (`alignas(32) int g;` → `&g&31==0` → 42; ELF `.bss` VA readelf-verified 32-aligned; strace `exit_group(42)` on x86_64+arm64; native 42 on PE) + `alignas_local_overaligned_error` (`alignas(32)` local → the gate). Verified byte-identical existing frames (the zero-blast-radius proof = full ctest green). ★ FOLLOW-UP CORRECTION 2026-07-07 (independent audit — PER-ALLOCA arm64 gap): the base-only `localAreaAlignPad` above aligns the local-area BASE but each alloca then sat at `base + Σ allocaSlotCount*slotSize` with NO per-alloca re-alignment. On arm64 `slotWidth = max(GPR,FPR) = 8` while `stackAlignment = 16`, so a NON-FIRST `alignas(16)` local (e.g. `char pad; alignas(16) T x;`) landed at `base + 8` → only 8-aligned (x86_64 is safe: slotWidth=16). FIX (the full per-alloca channel, gated so ≤slotWidth allocas are a no-op ⇒ x86_64 + arm64-≤8 frames BYTE-IDENTICAL): the per-alloca effective alignment (already on `MirInst.payload2`) is harvested at MIR→LIR into `FuncLocalAlignment.perAllocaAlignBytes` (scan order, one entry per alloca, alongside the per-function MAX) → threaded SymbolId-keyed through `LirFuncLocalAlignment` → `materializeCallingConvention` → `materializeOneFunc` → `computeFrameLayout`; each alloca's offset is `alignUp(off, itsOwnAlign)` at BOTH the `allocaSlotOffsets` precompute AND the materialize placement (identically, so the c69 `lea_frame_slot` consistency assert holds), and `computeFrameLayout.localAreaSize` walks the SAME per-alloca-aligned progression so the reserved area never under-sizes. A length guard (per-alloca list vs the function's LIR alloca count) fails loud on any MIR-harvest/LIR-scan-order divergence. The B3 `maxLocalAlign > stackAlignment` fail-loud gate stays. WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/alignas_two_locals_over16` (`char pad; alignas(16) T x,y;` both address-taken → `(&x|&y)&15==0` → 42, release-arm ALL 4 targets incl. arm64/qemu — where #2 manifests) + `MirVerifier` now rejects a non-pow2 Alloca payload2 ([[I_AllocaAlignmentNotPowerOfTwo]]). ⏳ DEFERRED (pinned): [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-OVERALIGNED-STACK-LOCAL]] (a >stack-alignment local fails loud — no dynamic RSP realignment). | ✅ (CLOSED) | T3 full-C23 (2026-07-07); `src/hir/attributes/alignment_attr.hpp` (new) + `src/hir/hir_attrs.hpp` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/mir/{mir.{hpp,cpp},mir_node.hpp}` + `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/mir/merge/{mir_merge,synth_seh_funclets}.cpp` + `src/opt/passes/{mir_rebuild_helper,dce,inlining}.cpp` + `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/lir/lir_callconv.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/asm/asm.cpp` + `src/core/types/parse_diagnostic.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp` + 3 examples; mirrors the mutability side-table + `SehFuncletParent` SymbolId-keyed metadata |
| `D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-OVERALIGNED-STACK-LOCAL` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-VARIABLE-CODEGEN]]).** A stack LOCAL whose effective alignment EXCEEDS the CC's stack alignment (`cc.stackAlignment`, 16 on every shipped ABI) — an `alignas(32)`/`alignas(64)` local, OR a member-alignas struct with align>16 used as a local — needs a DYNAMICALLY realigned stack pointer (an AND-mask of RSP + a frame pointer to still find spills at fixed offsets), which the static frame layout does not build. `computeFrameLayout` FAILS LOUD with `L_OverAlignedStackLocal` (0xB00C, new), the message reporting the COMPUTED bound (agnostic — never an arch name). Never a silent under-align (the very miscompile the parent cycle fixes). A ≤ stack-alignment local (e.g. `alignas(16)`) is HONORED via the local-area pad + the leaf RSP bias — only past that bound does this gate fire. TRIGGER: a real consumer needs an over-stack-aligned local (a cache-line-aligned / AVX-512 stack buffer). Resolution: dynamic SP realignment (`and rsp, -N`) + a frame-pointer-relative addressing mode for spills/locals in the affected function. WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/alignas_local_overaligned_error` (`alignas(32) int loc;` → the gate; the callconv-tier diagnostic has no source span so it resolves to 1:1). | (gated — no shipped consumer needs an over-stack-aligned local; sqlite uses zero) | opened by the alignas-variable-codegen cycle; `src/lir/lir_callconv.cpp` (`computeFrameLayout`) + `src/core/types/parse_diagnostic.{hpp,cpp}` |
| `D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-REGISTER-CONTEXT` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS]]).** C11/C23 §6.7.5p2 makes an `alignas` on an object declared with the `register` storage-class specifier a constraint violation (`register alignas(16) int x;` → should be `S_AlignasInvalidContext`). The frontend's context check (`semantic_analyzer.cpp` ~3644-3673) rejects a FUNCTION/TYPEDEF/bit-field alignas but does NOT reject a `register` one, so the alignment is currently HONORED. This is HARMLESS — `register` is an IGNORED storage specifier in c-subset (it carries no linkage; `c-subset.lang.json` lists it in the ignored-kinds), so `register alignas(16) int x;` VALIDLY applies the 16-byte alignment; it is a conformance NIT (an accepted constraint violation), NEVER a miscompile or a silent under-align. Rejecting it needs a NET-NEW mechanism: `register` is not tracked as a first-class storage flag on `SymbolRecord`/`DeclarationKind` (only `static`'s linkage/storage-duration axis is folded from the specifier prefix). Resolution: add a config-driven `register`-storage detection (a `RegisterKeyword` token scan of the specifier prefix, mirroring `firstAlignasSpecInPrefix`, likely surfaced as a `SymbolRecord` storage-class bit) + gate the alignas context check on it. TRIGGER: a real consumer / the C23 conformance corpus requires the `register alignas` rejection (rather than the standards-lenient acceptance). | (gated — `register` is ignored, so the alignment is validly applied; no shipped consumer needs the reject, and it is a conformance nit not a miscompile) | opened by the alignas frontend cycle; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the context check) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_model.hpp` (a storage-class bit) + `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (RegisterKeyword) |
| `D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-TYPEDEF-PARAM-PARSE` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS]]).** C11/C23 §6.7.5p2 makes an `alignas` on a TYPEDEF or a function PARAMETER a constraint violation. The frontend emits `S_AlignasInvalidContext` for the contexts the shared declaration-specifier prefix reaches (function/prototype declaration, bit-field member) — but `typedef alignas(16) int T;` and `void f(alignas(16) int x)` are dedicated `typedefDecl`/`param` grammar rules that do NOT admit the alignas prefix, so they are rejected as FAIL-LOUD PARSE errors (P0009), not the specific semantic code. Still a LOUD rejection (never silently accepted — both are constraint violations no real code writes). The semantic typedef-context check IS wired (a Type-kind declaration carrying the prefix → S_AlignasInvalidContext) and would fire the moment the grammar admits the prefix. Cleanly admitting it needs either strip support for a MID-sequence prefix (typedef: the alignas follows `typedef`, not at child-0) or a new per-rule context flag + a position-0 optional on `param` (a variable-vs-parameter discriminator the shared declarator-mode path lacks — keying on the rule NAME would break source-agnosticism). TRIGGER: a real consumer / the C23 corpus writes a typedef-or-parameter alignas and needs the exact `S_AlignasInvalidContext` (rather than a parse error). Resolution: add the alignas admission + a config-driven context discriminator; the semantic reject is already in place. | (gated — no shipped consumer writes a typedef/parameter alignas; the parse-error rejection is loud) | opened by the alignas frontend cycle; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`typedefDecl`/`param` rules) + the existing semantic context check in `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` |
| `D-CSUBSET-PACKED` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-08 (T3 full-C23 / FC16) — C/C23 struct/union `packed` attribute SEMANTICS (`__attribute__((packed))` / `[[gnu::packed]]` / `[[packed]]` / `__packed__`), a PRECISE MIRROR of the just-landed [[D-CSUBSET-MEMBER-ALIGNAS]] channel.** `packed` removes ALL inter-field padding and sets the aggregate's natural alignment to 1; a member `alignas` still RAISES per-field via the unchanged MAX-fold (alignas wins per-field — `alignas(1)` INSIDE a packed struct is legal via a `naturalBaseline=1` thread into `resolveAlignasOverride`, while `alignas(1) double` OUTSIDE still fails 6.7.5p4). CODEGEN needed ZERO changes (arm64 uses only unaligned-tolerant LDUR/STUR, x86 plain mov — an independent audit confirmed). A per-COMPOSITE `CompositeFields.packed` bool + `isPacked` accessor (type_interner.hpp/type_lattice.cpp); `completeComposite` gains a DELIBERATELY NON-DEFAULTED `bool packed` param (F2 hardening: a forgotten packed FAILS TO COMPILE — the reintern channel is the ABI-critical one, type_reintern.cpp carries `src.isPacked` else a cross-CU round-trip silently unpacks); enters `contentDeclSiteKey` GUARDED-ON-TRUE (an unpacked composite hashes byte-identically → ZERO TypeId churn) so a packed `S` is a DISTINCT TypeId from a padded `S`; `computeLayout`'s struct AND union arms feed `Alignment::of<1>()` as the per-field baseline (F5 belt: `packed && anyBitfield → nullopt` at BOTH arms, the backstop behind the semantic front door). Grammar: a plain trailing `{ "optional": "compositeAttrList" }` on structSpec/unionSpec (the feared ambiguity — `stdAttr`'s `[[` colliding at 1 token with a following array `[` — never materialized, so the plain optional shipped and works: green + `PackedStructFollowedByDeclaratorParses` passes) — TRAILING so it never shifts the `name:1` tag index (the after-keyword position is deferred, see below). Config-driven: `semantics.packed {listRule, attributeNames, strictAttrRule}` (nothing hardcodes "packed"). Semantic scan (`scanCompositePacked`) extracts each attr identifier (dunder-normalized via the shared `stripDunder`, promoted to `core/types/attribute_naming.hpp` so the preprocessor + this scan share ONE normalizer), threads packed to BOTH completion sites. New `S_UnknownTypeAttribute=0xE031` (a GNU `__attribute__((pakced))` typo fails loud — H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier precedent; an unknown C23 `[[…]]` is standard-ignorable — [[deprecated]] precedent) + `S_PackedBitfieldUnsupported=0xE032` (UNSUPPRESSABLE — a packed bit-field ships wrong padded bytes if silenced). hir_text round-trips a `packed` marker (emit + parse via forwardComposite+completeComposite). WITNESSES: `tests/core/test_type_layout.cpp` (7 hermetic pins — plain packed, packed+alignas-member-still-raises, packed union, array-element stride, nested packed, packed+bitfield nullopt belt) + NEW `tests/core/test_packed_abi_conformance.cpp` (native gcc/clang/cl cross-check — 6-struct battery, sizeof+offsetof byte-identical) + `TypeReintern.PackedFlagSurvivesReintern` (F2) + `ParseTypeFromText.PackedParseAndForkIdentity` + `HirText.PackedFlagRoundTrip` + 11 semantic tests (GNU+C23 spellings end-to-end via `_Static_assert(sizeof/_Alignof)`, alignas-wins, alignas(1)-legal-in-packed, packed union, following-declarator, packed+bitfield fail-loud, typo fail-loud, unknown-[[…]] ignored) + `examples/c-subset/packed_struct` (a MISALIGNED multi-byte field read+written at runtime → 42, release arm all 4 targets). | ✅ (CLOSED) | T3/FC16 (2026-07-08); `src/core/types/type_lattice/{type_interner.hpp,type_lattice.cpp,type_layout.cpp,type_reintern.cpp}` + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp,attribute_naming.hpp[new]}` + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` + `src/analysis/preprocess/pp_if_eval.cpp` + `src/hir/hir_text.cpp` + `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` + tests + example; a precise mirror of [[D-CSUBSET-MEMBER-ALIGNAS]]; plan-23 FC16 |
| `D-CSUBSET-PACKED-BITFIELD-INTERACTION` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-08 by [[D-CSUBSET-PACKED]]).** A `packed` struct/union that ALSO contains a bit-field member — bit-granular packed packing (each bit-field packed to bit-precision, no unit-alignment padding) is a DISTINCT algorithm from the byte-level packed layout. Combining the two is UNSUPPORTED and fails loud TWO ways: the SEMANTIC front door `S_PackedBitfieldUnsupported` (0xE032, UNSUPPRESSABLE — emitted at completion, and the composite is completed UNPACKED so only the one error fires, no cascade) + the layout `computeLayout` nullopt BELT at both the struct and union bit-field arms (`packed && anyBitfield → nullopt`, the reliable backstop for a packed+bitfield type constructed DIRECTLY via the interner / reinterned, bypassing the semantic scan). NEVER a silent NON-packed (padded) layout. TRIGGER: a real consumer needs a packed bit-field struct with GNU bit-granular semantics. Resolution: a bit-granular packed bit-field packer arm in `type_layout.cpp` (a new strategy or a packed-aware GnuPacked variant). WITNESS: `TypeLayout.PackedPlusBitfieldFailsLoud` (belt nullopt) + `SemanticAnalyzerCSubset.PackedBitfieldFailsLoud` (front door). | (gated — no shipped consumer needs a packed bit-field; sqlite uses zero) | opened by the packed cycle; `src/core/types/type_lattice/type_layout.cpp` (the F5 belt) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the front door) + `src/core/types/parse_diagnostic.hpp` (S_PackedBitfieldUnsupported) |
| `D-CSUBSET-PACKED-AFTER-KEYWORD-POSITION` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-08 by [[D-CSUBSET-PACKED]] — the SUFFIX-ONLY fallback).** GCC honors `packed` in TWO positions: the SUFFIX `struct S {…} __attribute__((packed))` (LANDED) and the AFTER-KEYWORD `struct __attribute__((packed)) S {…}` (this gap). The suffix form keeps the tag at the positional `name:1` index; the after-keyword form shifts the Identifier to index 2, so honoring it requires locating the composite tag BY KIND (a direct-child Identifier scan) instead of `name:1` — a NET-NEW `NameMatchMode` threaded through MULTIPLE positional readers (the semantic analyzer's dual definition-name sites AND the parser's binder-sketch `resolveNameNode_` mirror + the schema decode + validation), a genuine multi-cycle refactor with silent-miscompile risk (a mis-scoped scan would treat the tagged struct as anonymous, silently dropping the tag). The after-keyword form currently parses as an ANONYMOUS struct specifier (the grammar admits `compositeAttrList` only as a TRAILING element, so the attribute is consumed as its trailing list) and the tag `S {…}` is then mis-read as a malformed function definition → it stays a LOUD SEMANTIC rejection (S_InvalidFunctionDeclarator; the anonymous variant → S_UnknownType), fail-loud, never a silent drop. TRIGGER: a real consumer / the C23 corpus writes the after-keyword form and needs it honored. Resolution: a `firstDirectIdentifier` NameMatchMode (or equivalent) applied uniformly to the composite tag lookup + an after-keyword `compositeAttrList` slot. | (gated — the suffix form is the dominant real-world idiom; sqlite uses zero packed attributes; the after-keyword form is a loud rejection) | opened by the packed cycle; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (structSpec/unionSpec after-keyword slot) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` + `src/analysis/syntactic/parser.cpp` (the tag-lookup readers) + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp}` (the new NameMatchMode) |
| `D-CSUBSET-PER-MEMBER-PACKED` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-08 by [[D-CSUBSET-PACKED]]).** GCC also honors `packed` on an INDIVIDUAL member (`struct S { int a; int z __attribute__((packed)); };` — pack only `z`, not the whole struct), a DIFFERENT grammar slot (a member-declarator suffix / the member specifier prefix) + a DIFFERENT layout channel (a per-FIELD packed bool, like `fieldAligns`, feeding the field's baseline to 1 without packing its siblings). The whole-composite packed cycle deliberately scoped this out (the trailing composite-attribute list is a whole-composite property). Currently a per-member `__attribute__((packed))` on a struct field does NOT parse (the member grammar admits only the alignas prefix) → a loud parse error, never a silent drop. TRIGGER: a real consumer writes a per-member packed. Resolution: a per-field packed channel (`CompositeFields.fieldPacked`, mirroring `fieldAligns`) + a member-attribute grammar slot + the semantic wiring. | (gated — no shipped consumer needs per-member packed; the whole-composite form covers the dominant idiom) | opened by the packed cycle; `src/core/types/type_lattice/{type_interner.hpp,type_lattice.cpp,type_layout.cpp}` + `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (member-attribute slot) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` |
| `D-CSUBSET-PACKED-ATOMIC-MEMBER` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-08 by [[D-CSUBSET-PACKED]]).** An `_Atomic`-qualified member inside a `packed` struct is a HAZARD: packing may leave the atomic member misaligned, and arm64's atomic instructions (LDAXR/STLXR/LDADD) TRAP (SIGBUS) on an unaligned address — a runtime crash, not a silent miscompile, but one that should be REJECTED at compile time (`_Atomic` in a packed aggregate is ill-advised; GCC warns/errors). Currently MOOT: c-subset does not yet model `_Atomic` as a type qualifier (only `volatile` is materialized, as VolatileQual), so an `_Atomic` member is not expressible in a packed (or any) struct — the hazard cannot arise. TRIGGER: `_Atomic` support lands (a real atomic type qualifier). Resolution: at composite completion, reject a packed struct carrying an `_Atomic` member whose packed offset is not naturally aligned (fail-loud, a new diagnostic). | (gated — MOOT until `_Atomic` is modeled; today no atomic member is expressible) | opened by the packed cycle; awaits `_Atomic` support; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the composite completion check) |
| `D-CSUBSET-NORETURN` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-08 (T3 full-C23 / FC16) — C11/C23 `noreturn` FUNCTION-attribute SEMANTICS (`_Noreturn` [C11 6.7.4] / `[[noreturn]]` [C23 6.7.12.7] / `__attribute__((noreturn))` / `__attribute__((__noreturn__))` / `[[gnu::noreturn]]`), a DIRECT structural mirror of the proven `wrapIfProvablyInfinite`.** MECHANISM (Approach A): a function symbol declared noreturn carries `SymbolRecord.isNoreturn`; at HIR lowering a DIRECT call to such a function is wrapped `Block{ ExprStmt(call), Unreachable }` (Block + terminator leaf both `Synthetic`) EXACTLY like a provably-infinite loop — so a noreturn-terminated path structurally terminates. ZERO changes to hir_verifier / hir_to_mir / prune_unreachable (all already generic: `pathTerminates` recurses to a Block's last child; the following statement lowers into a dead pruned block via the existing open-block-has-terminator guard). Config-driven vocabulary `semantics.noreturn {keywordToken:"NoreturnKeyword", attributeNames:["noreturn"]}` + a bare `_Noreturn` keyword joined into `singleDeclSpecifier`/`localDeclSpecifier` (FIRST disjoint → no speculation). DETECTION `specifierPrefixNamesNoreturn` (a bounded DFS over the STRIPPED specifier prefix) matches BOTH forms — the `_Noreturn` KEYWORD TOKEN **and** an attribute IDENTIFIER (dunder-normalized via the shared `stripDunder`, so `__noreturn__`/`[[gnu::noreturn]]` match); a scan of identifiers ONLY (the `scanCompositePacked` precedent) would MISS the bare keyword. Stored gated on `isFnSig` (per-declarator, the `explicitAlignment` pattern), OR-MERGED across a proto/def pair in the post-1.5 `mergedFnDecls` sweep (a call resolves to the SURVIVOR=definition; OR-ing both covers either merge order). SHIPPED externs (no user prototype) carry it on the descriptor: `stdlib.json` marks `abort`/`exit` `noreturn:true` → `ShippedSymbol.noreturn` → the injected record. ⚠️ F1 (THE miscompile guard): `isDirectNoreturnCall` inspects the lowered Call's CALLEE CHILD directly (`children().front()` must be `HirKind::Ref` whose record is noreturn), NEVER `firstNameToken` — a noreturn function is ADDRESS-TAKEABLE, so `(cond ? die : other)(1)` is legal C whose ternary callee lowers to a NON-Ref node → NOT wrapped (else `other`'s return path is elided = MISCOMPILE); a fn-POINTER object `fp(1)` → Ref(fp), isNoreturn==false → NOT wrapped. LINKAGE seam (audit fix, DEVIATION from the design's `{binding:global}`): the GNU `__attribute__((noreturn))` shares the `attrSpec` rule with HONORED linkage attrs (weak/visibility) so its subtree can't be ignored wholesale on `topLevelDecl`; a `{binding:global}` no-op linkageSpecifiers entry was REJECTED because it CLOBBERS a co-present `static`/`weak` last-wins (`static __attribute__((noreturn))` — legal, common — silently loses internal linkage = an order-dependent miscompile). Instead a NEW identifier-granularity `DeclarationRule.linkageSpecifierIgnoredNames` (completing the ignoredKinds/ignoredRules trio) skips `noreturn` by NAME (dunder-normalized, covers `__noreturn__`) WITHOUT a linkage effect; any OTHER unknown identifier still fails loud H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier. The bare `_Noreturn` keyword rides `linkageSpecifierIgnoredKinds` (both varDecl + topLevelDecl); `[[noreturn]]` rides `stdAttr` ∈ ignoredRules (unchanged). ZERO NEW DIAGNOSTIC CODES (reuse H_VerifierFailure / H_UnreachableCode / H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier). WITNESSES: `tests/hir/test_hir_lowering_c_subset.cpp` — `NoreturnCallTailWrapsAndVerifies` (red-on-disable: revert → H_VerifierFailure=1), `NoreturnAllFourSpellingsCompileClean` (all 4 spellings clean + zero H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier), `NoreturnIndirectCalleeIsNotWrapped` (F1: BOTH the ternary AND fn-ptr callee still fire H_VerifierFailure=1 — the un-elided-return-path witness); `tests/analysis/semantic/test_semantic_analyzer_c_subset.cpp` `NoreturnProtoMergesIntoDefinition` (proto-only `_Noreturn`, def doesn't → survivor.isNoreturn==true, red-on-disable for the OR-merge); `examples/c-subset/noreturn_function` (proto/def OR-merge + `die` tail-calls the shipped noreturn `exit`; a non-void `compute` whose only non-return path is `die(42)` → main returns compute(0) → exit 42, **release arm** [`shippedPipeline:release`] all 4 targets — the FIRST optimizer-visible reachable Unreachable NOT behind an infinite loop). ⏳ DEFERRED (all fail-loud/SAFE, NOT miscompiles): [[D-CSUBSET-NORETURN-VALUE-POSITION]] (noreturn call in value / for-init-update / cast/comma-wrapped position — only the `wrapBare` statement chokepoint wraps, so such a rare form keeps a spurious H_VerifierFailure, un-relaxed) + [[D-CSUBSET-NORETURN-NON-FUNCTION-OBJECT]] (`_Noreturn` on a non-function object — 6.7.4 constraint; today INERT via the `isFnSig` store-gate, a fail-loud reject deferred). NAMED inline (no src citation, safe): noreturn on a function-POINTER TYPE (`_Noreturn`-qualified fn-ptr — not detected, safe miss); `_Noreturn` on `main` (inert — main already terminates); a noreturn function that RETURNS (UB, undiagnosed — C 6.7.4p2); the `extern _Noreturn` storage-mixed form (extern is a distinct keyword — the prefix scan still finds `_Noreturn`, so this actually works, but a dedicated witness is deferred); `<stdnoreturn.h>`'s bare `noreturn`/`_Noreturn` macro surface (not shipped as a header). | ✅ (CLOSED) | T3/FC16 (2026-07-08); `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp}` + `src/analysis/semantic/{semantic_analyzer.cpp,semantic_model.hpp}` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` + `src/ffi/shipped_lib_descriptor.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/dss-config/{sources/c-subset.lang.json,shippedLibs/stdlib.json}` + tests + `examples/c-subset/noreturn_function`; a direct mirror of `wrapIfProvablyInfinite` (D-HIR-INFINITE-LOOP-NOT-TERMINATING); plan-23 FC16 |
| `D-CSUBSET-NORETURN-VALUE-POSITION` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-08 by [[D-CSUBSET-NORETURN]]).** The noreturn-call HIR wrap fires ONLY at the `lowerStmtExprCore(wrapBare=true)` STATEMENT chokepoint (block-item / bare if/while/for/label arm). A noreturn call appearing in a NON-statement position — a for-INIT/UPDATE clause (`wrapBare=false`, deliberately untouched), or nested inside a value expression / cast / comma operator (`(void)die(1)`, `x, die(1)`) — is NOT wrapped, so a non-void function whose ONLY terminating path routes through such a form keeps a SPURIOUS H_VerifierFailure. FAIL-LOUD (a loud over-rejection), NEVER a silent miscompile — and vanishingly rare (a noreturn call is idiomatically a bare statement). TRIGGER: a real consumer terminates a function via a noreturn call in value/for-clause position. Resolution: extend the wrap to the value-expression lowering (an ExprStmt-free Unreachable emission) + the for-clause caller, with the SAME F1 direct-Ref-callee guard. | (gated — no shipped consumer needs a value-position noreturn call; the bare-statement form covers the idiom) | opened by the noreturn cycle; `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`lowerStmtExprCore` / `lowerForClause`) |
| `D-CSUBSET-NORETURN-NON-FUNCTION-OBJECT` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-08 by [[D-CSUBSET-NORETURN]]).** C11 6.7.4p2 constrains `_Noreturn` to FUNCTION declarations; `_Noreturn int x;` (a non-function object) is a constraint violation. The noreturn detection STORE is gated on `isFnSig`, so today such a spelling is silently INERT (the flag is simply not stored — the object is unaffected, no miscompile). A fail-loud reject (a dedicated diagnostic at the `_Noreturn`-on-non-function site) is deferred: it needs the detection scan to run UNCONDITIONALLY (not only when a FnSig is present) + a context check, mirroring the alignas `S_AlignasInvalidContext` context gate. NEVER a miscompile (an inert attribute). TRIGGER: a real consumer / the C23 conformance corpus requires the rejection. Resolution: emit the reject when `declHasNoreturn && !isFnSig` (a new unsuppressable code, the alignas-context precedent). | (gated — `_Noreturn` on a non-function is inert, not a miscompile; no shipped consumer writes it) | opened by the noreturn cycle; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the per-declarator store gate) |
| `D-CSUBSET-ANON-TYPEDECL-TYPE-FALLBACK` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (user-queued, orthogonal to alignas).** A file-scope ANONYMOUS composite specifier (tagless `enum {…}` / `struct {…}` / `union {…}`) whose enumerator/member is referenced in a file-scope constant-expression (`enum { V = 16 }; int arr[V];`) failed to compile with `error[H0001]` (`H_TypeUnresolved`) on the anon composite's HIR `TypeDecl` — the NAMED form (`enum E {…}`) was always clean. ROOT CAUSE: `lowerTypeDecl` (`cst_to_hir.cpp`) resolved the TypeDecl's type off the descriptor's `nameChild`, but for an ANON composite `vis[nameChild]` is the BODY node (which carries no symbol — the tag symbol + type are bound on the SPECIFIER node itself by the Pass-1.5 stamp `nodeToType[specNode]=compositeTy`). The name-child probe left the type Invalid → the verifier fired. FIX: after the name-child probe, fall back to `model.typeAt(node)` (the type the analyzer already stamped on this specifier node) when the type is still invalid — a HIR-lowering stamp, NOT a semantic gap (the side-table was already correct) and NOT dropping the TypeDecl; keeps named+anon symmetric and also covers anonymous struct/union top-level defs. WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/anon_enum_file_scope_arraydim` (`enum{V=16}; int arr[V]; return sizeof(arr)` → 64 on all 4 targets; red-on-disable: revert the fallback → H0001). 3-leg gate + sqlite re-probe green. | ✅ (CLOSED) | user-queued cycle (2026-07-07); `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`lowerTypeDecl`) |
| `D-OPT-DOMTREE-SCRATCH-REUSE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (post-sqlite perf arc) — the reusable dominator-computation scratch: THE biggest single win of the arc (compile 4m24→2m00; the whole arc >30min→2m00).** Sub-profiling (env `DSS_OPT_TRACE` Cse/Licm sub-timers) proved `computeMirDomTree` was ~95% of BOTH passes' analyze (~94s/compile): per CALL (per function!) it re-copied the ENTIRE whole-module predecessor map into `predSlots` (~90k inner-vector allocs + all module edges) + allocated 5 more whole-module-sized arrays + ran an O(module) conversion — for ONE function's CHK walk (the fixpoint itself is function-local-linear). FIX (independent design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES, F1–F6 folded): (1) the CHK core templated over the pred/order element (`chkSlotOf`) reads the caller's `preds` DIRECTLY — the predSlots copy is GONE from the fresh path too (Mem2Reg iter-0 27.8→16.4s for free); (2) `MirDomScratch` (mir_dom.hpp) owns the whole-module buffers ONCE per pass call, reset-at-ENTRY from a SELF-RECORDED touched list (= the previous call's `order ∪ {entry}` — the audit-verified complete write set; the caller's order may dangle → never re-derived), restoring exactly the fresh-allocation defaults; (3) F2 OOB-entry bound; (4) F4 legacy size-tolerance preserved + the scratch overload fail-louds `preds.size()!=blockCount`; (5) F5 arrays STAY module-sized (the intersect step-cap derives from `idom.size()` — comment-pinned); (6) F6 `mirDomTreeChildren(scratch)` iterates the touched set SORTED-ASCENDING + dedup'd (the CSE dom-DFS depends on children order; order[0]==entry dupes would double-fill) + once-per-compute idempotence; stale-module `{id, blockCount}` abort (the MirMemoryClobbers pattern); `&dom==&scratch.tree` identity abort in children. Threaded: runCse + runLicm + rederiveStructCfMarkers (one scratch per pass call; `auto const&` bindings). MEASURED: Cse dom 14.9s→**26ms**/iter (pass 18.3→2.98s), Licm dom 14.3s→**8ms** (pass 18.9→4.3s), rederive 12→2.1s/call, optimize 3m41→1m35. WITNESSES: `test_mir_dom.cpp` `MirDomScratch.{AdversarialSequenceMatchesFreshOnEveryCall, OutOfRangeEntrySlotIsBoundedAndIdentical, RandomizedCfgSweepMatchesFresh}` — FULL module-sized fresh-vs-scratch comparison (v AND arenaTag explicitly — `MirBlockId::operator==` compares v only, a stale-tagged zero would pass it) after EVERY call of adversarial sequences (big→small→big bleed, unreachable-into-reachable preds-outside-order, same-fn-twice, OOB entry) + a 25-module seeded randomized sweep; ALL existing CSE/LICM/optimizer decision pins unchanged; 3-leg gate. | ✅ (CLOSED) | post-sqlite perf arc (2026-07-07); `src/mir/mir_dom.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/opt/passes/{cse,licm}.cpp` + `src/mir/mir_struct_markers.cpp` + `tests/mir/test_mir_dom.cpp`; the 4th member of the [[D-OPT-CSE-ANALYSIS-HOIST]] family (a NEW mechanism class though: cross-call mutable scratch + touched-slot reset, not an argument-identity hoist); sibling [[D-OPT-POSTDOM-SCRATCH-REUSE]] gated |
| `D-OPT-POSTDOM-SCRATCH-REUSE` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by the [[D-OPT-DOMTREE-SCRATCH-REUSE]] closure).** `computeMirPostDomTree` (mir_dom.cpp) still allocates per call: two (n+1)-sized nested vector graphs (revPreds/revSuccs), 3 whole-module core arrays, a full conversion, + its own `mirReversePostOrder` — the same shape the dom scratch fixed. It sits ONLY on the `deriveStructCfMarkers` path (rederiveStructCfMarkers = 3 calls/compile post-marker-posture; measured 2.1s/call TOTAL incl. post-dom — small). Fixing it requires a SECOND complete write-set proof + a `deriveStructCfMarkers` signature extension — deferred until it matters. TRIGGER: `DSS_OPT_TRACE` shows `rederiveStructCfMarkers` > ~10s/compile after the dom scratch (i.e. post-dom becomes a real term again — e.g. many more rederive call sites or much bigger modules). Resolution: the same MirDomScratch treatment over the reverse graph (write-set = reverse-order ∪ {virtual exit}). | (gated — measured 2026-07-07 at ~2.1s/call × 3 calls; far below the trigger) | post-sqlite perf arc; `src/mir/mir_dom.cpp` (`computeMirPostDomTree`) + [[D-OPT-DOMTREE-SCRATCH-REUSE]] |
| `D-OPT-MEMSSA-WALK-PAST-PRECISION` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by the [[D-OPT-MEMORYSSA-CLOBBER-WALK]] closure).** The `MirMemoryClobbers` index (mir_memory_clobbers.hpp) enumerates EVERY clobber in a region and alias-tests each — it does NOT structurally skip provably-non-aliasing defs the way a full LLVM MemorySSA walker (MemoryPhi + walk-past, with a (def,location)-keyed clobber cache) would. That was deliberately REJECTED at close: (a) a per-block walk-stop cache UNDER-REPORTS (the audit's constructed miscompile, pinned as `FrontierStopsAtNonAliasingStoreMustStillRefuse`); (b) at `mirMayAlias`'s current precision (mostly Maybe — Rules 3/4/7) the skip almost never fires, so the machinery cannot pay for itself. TRIGGER: `mirMayAlias` gains real precision (points-to / full TBAA / escape analysis) such that walk-past would fire often, OR a profile shows the index's per-query alias-TEST count (not enumeration) dominating a pass. Resolution: the (def,location)-keyed walk-past layer OVER the existing index — never a per-block stop cache. | (gated — the index is complete + byte-identical today; this is the precision-scaling successor) | post-sqlite perf arc; `src/opt/analysis/mir_memory_clobbers.hpp` (the docblock cites this) + plan 22 §3.1 [[D-OPT-MEMORYSSA-CLOBBER-WALK]] |
| `D-OPT-MEM2REG-LOOP-BODY-LOCAL-DEAD-PHI` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 c3) — ★ SUPERSEDED by [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-DEAD-PHI-PRUNE]] (the COMPLETE fix).** The SEMI-PRUNED fix this row first described (commit ecf40c3, gate phi placement on UPWARD-EXPOSURE) was INSUFFICIENT: the c3 pr-review's runtime battery found NESTED loops still crashed (a name DEAD at one IDF block but live elsewhere — upward-exposure cannot prune that); upgraded to FULLY-PRUNED (live-in) the SAME cycle. The loop-body-local class IS fixed by the general fix. Original c3 record (the first-found symptom):** GENERAL Mem2Reg correctness bug — HIGH, was masked. Under the RELEASE pipeline, Mem2Reg ABORTED FAIL-LOUD (`phi at block ... has no reaching value from predecessor ... uninit/unreachable input`) on ANY function with a local declared AND used entirely inside a LOOP BODY (`while (...) { int x = expr; use(x); }`) — an enormous, ordinary class of C (it would have blocked vast swaths of SQLite). ROOT: minimal (un-pruned) SSA placed a DEAD phi for the block-local at the loop HEADER (the body-Store's iterated dominance frontier reaches the header via the back-edge); that phi's entry-predecessor incoming was genuinely undefined (the slot was never stored before the loop) → the rename walk's empty-stack guard aborted. Surfaced (re-scoped) from the variadic symptom [[D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-MEM2REG-PHI]] by the c3 probe (a non-variadic `while(){int iv=...}` reproduces it). Was masked because release-arm corpus examples never declared a loop-body-local (the dss-cycle masked-effectiveness trap, again). | ✅ (CLOSED) FIX = SEMI-PRUNED SSA (Briggs/Cooper) in `src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp`: gate phi PLACEMENT on UPWARD-EXPOSURE — only place a phi for an alloca read-before-written in SOME block (a "global" name). A block-local (Store-before-Load in its block) is not upward-exposed → no dead phi. PROVABLY SAFE: a global alloca's placement is BYTE-IDENTICAL to before (induction vars / diamond joins unchanged), so the fix ONLY removes the spurious dead phis. CRUCIALLY PRESERVES fail-loud: a genuine uninitialized read (`int x; if(c) x=1; use(x)`) IS upward-exposed at the use → stays global → still gets its phi → still aborts on the undefined path (verified: that case still fatals). PINS: `tests/opt/test_mem2reg.cpp::LoopBodyLocalAllocaNeedsNoPhi` (induction var=1 phi, body-local=0 phi; red-on-disable = the pass aborts) + corpus `examples/c-subset/loop_body_local` (release arm exit 20, PE-runnable) + the `varargs_win64_sum` release arm (exit 39). Agnostic (MIR-only, no identity branch). | CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 c3); cited in `src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp` (the semi-pruned gate) + `tests/opt/test_mem2reg.cpp` + `examples/c-subset/loop_body_local/`; pairs with [[D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-MEM2REG-PHI]] (the variadic symptom it subsumes) + the Cytron-Ferrante / Briggs-Cooper SSA literature |
| `D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-ARGINDEX` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 Cluster G c4) — ROOT CAUSE = DCE; GENERAL (not variadic), the LAST cluster member.** Compiling `examples/c-subset/varargs_overflow_fixed_stack` under RELEASE aborted FAIL-LOUD `I_ArgIndexOutOfRange: argIndex 6 >= physical-arg count 2`. ★ The c4 probe BISECTED it to **Dce** (without `Dce` it compiles) and re-scoped it GENERAL: `pick(a,b,c,d,e,f,g,...)` uses only `a` + `g`, so DCE elided the 5 unused param `Arg`s → only 2 `Arg`s survived, but the still-live `Arg(6)` (g, the 7th int → SysV stack) kept its physical ordinal 6 → the MirVerifier's FC7 count-based bound (`argIndex < count(Arg)`, valid only while every Arg survives) spuriously rejected it. A NON-variadic `int f(int a..g){return a+g;}` reproduces it identically (any fn with an unused param before a used high-ordinal one). The codegen was always correct (ordinals preserved); only the verifier over-rejected. | ✅ (CLOSED) FIX = treat `Arg` as an ABI-parameter ROOT in DCE (`isSideEffectRoot` in `src/opt/passes/dce.cpp` returns true for `MirOpcode::Arg`): a parameter is part of the fixed ABI signature, never dead code (LLVM models params as non-instruction `Argument` roots) — so DCE never elides an unused param's `Arg`, holding `count(Arg)` == the physical-arg count the verifier bounds against. No codegen cost (an unused param's value is dropped at the LIR tier). Agnostic (MIR-opcode only). PINS (red-on-disable): `tests/opt/test_dce.cpp::UnusedParameterArgIsPreservedAsAbiRoot` (3 Args, middle unused → all 3 survive) + the `varargs_overflow_fixed_stack` `{"shippedPipeline":"release"}` arm (exit 108, was the verifier fatal). | CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 c4); cited in `src/opt/passes/dce.cpp` (the Arg root) + `tests/opt/test_dce.cpp` + `examples/c-subset/varargs_overflow_fixed_stack/`; pairs with [[D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-MISCOMPILE]] + [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-DEAD-PHI-PRUNE]] (the other 2 cluster members) + the FC7 `D-FC7-SYSV-STRUCT-ARG-MULTIREG` verifier bound it interacts with |
| `D-CSUBSET-DESCRIPTOR-TYPEDEF-POINTER-DECL` | ★ **FIRED — discovered 2026-06-25 (p18 SQLite-readiness Cluster G c1, while strengthening the `shipped_int_headers` corpus with a NULL witness).** A DESCRIPTOR-injected typedef name (from a shipped-header JSON descriptor — `int32_t`/`uint8_t`/`size_t`/…) used in POINTER-DECLARATOR position (`int32_t* p` / `size_t *p`) is NOT recognized as a type-name by the PARSER, so `int32_t * p` parses as a MULTIPLICATION expression (`int32_t` × `p`) → fails loud `S_UndeclaredIdentifier` (p) + `S_TypeMismatch` (initializer). ROOT: descriptor typedefs are injected SEMANTICALLY (post-parse, into cuRoot by `semantic_analyzer`), so the parser's `T * x` declaration-vs-multiply disambiguation never learns them. CONTRAST (all verified this cycle): a USER `typedef int myint; myint* p` WORKS (the parser sees the typedef decl → type-name visible); a BUILTIN `int* p` WORKS (keyword); and descriptor typedefs WORK in the UNAMBIGUOUS positions — scalar decl (`int32_t r`) + cast (`(int32_t)n`) — which need no parser type-name table. Only the pointer-declarator ambiguity exposes it. **FAILS LOUD (never a silent miscompile).** Priority: **normal — SQLite blast-radius UNMEASURED** (SQLite mostly uses its OWN amalgamation typedefs [`u8`/`u32`/`sqlite3_int64`, parser-visible → fine], but a direct `size_t *`/`uint8_t *` on a stdint/stddef typedef would mis-parse; re-probe sqlite3.c for descriptor-typedef pointer usage to set the real priority). | Make descriptor typedef-names parser-visible BEFORE the `T * x` disambiguation. Two designs (likely a §B fork): **(a)** pre-scan the source's `#include <...>` set → resolve descriptors → SEED the parser's type-name table before parsing (today includes resolve POST-parse in `import_resolver`, so this needs include-set-at-parse-time — the classic C lexer-hack ordering); **(b)** make the declaration-vs-multiply disambiguation SEMANTIC — speculatively parse both, resolve by whether `T` names a type in the semantic scope (incl. descriptor injections). Pin: `#include <stdint.h>\nint32_t g=42; int main(){ int32_t* p=&g; return (int)*p; }` compiles + exits 42, RED-on-disable. | discovered by p18 Cluster G c1 (`examples/c-subset/shipped_int_headers/main.c` references it in a comment — examples/ is NOT anchor-guard-scanned, so informational); pairs with the FC14 descriptor typedef-injection path (`semantic_analyzer.cpp` inject) + the c-subset declaration-vs-expression speculative parse + `D-PARSE-IDENTIFIER-KIND-HARDCODED-VS-CONFIG`; SQLite-readiness Cluster G (p18); do NOT cite in `src/` yet (discovered-gap pin) |
