| `D-OPT7-INLINE-LEGALITY-GATE` | OPT7 cycle 1 (2026-06-05): the §2.9 inline-legality gate is LIVE in `src/opt/passes/inlining.cpp` (`inlineLegalityGate` — refuses Weak/recursion/address-escape/IntrinsicCall-non-leaf/out-of-range-arity; the Weak refusal is the correctness rule the `D-OPT7-WEAK-INLINE-NEGATIVE-PIN` corpus proves). **Cycle 27 (2026-06-05) CLOSED the conservative recursion arm + the regular-`Call` non-leaf arm:** (1) the gate now LIFTS the non-leaf refusal for a callee whose body contains a regular `Call` — admitted, since the splice's generic arm clones an inner Call correctly (operands remapped via the `local` map; the inner Call's `GlobalAddr` re-emits the SAME symbol → same callee), pinned GREEN by `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp::NonLeafCalleeIsInlined` + `MultiBlockNonLeafCalleeIsInlined` (both RED-on-disable when the line-209 `Call`-leaf refusal is re-added) + the runtime corpus `examples/c-subset/non_leaf_inline/` (`twice`/`helper`/`main`, `["Inlining"]` arm == baseline 41). (2) The recursion refusal is now SCC-based: a NEW agnostic substrate `src/opt/analysis/call_graph_scc.{hpp,cpp}` (`computeCallGraphSccs(Mir const&)` → `funcId.v → sccId`, iterative Tarjan over the self-contained direct call graph) lets the gate refuse ALL recursive-cycle inlining via `funcToScc.at(caller.v) == funcToScc.at(callee.v)` — which SUBSUMES self-recursion (a self-call is the singleton-SCC case, caller==callee → same id) AND generalizes to MUTUAL recursion (`f→g→f` share a multi-member SCC). Pinned by `tests/opt/test_call_graph_scc.cpp` (3-cycle / acyclic-chain / self-edge / mixed, RED-on-disable) + `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp::MutualRecursiveCallIsNotInlined` (mutual f↔g refused + an ACYCLIC non-leaf A→B→C control that DOES inline; RED-on-disable: disabling the SCC refusal → `callsInlined` jumps to 2) + the retained `SelfRecursiveCallIsNotInlined` (now ALSO RED-on-disable via the SCC path, proving subsumption). The anchor stays OPEN for the STILL-DEFERRED relaxations: **(a) depth-bounded RECURSIVE inlining** — allow BOUNDED recursion (a depth/unroll policy), ADDITIVE on top of the SCC refusal; each bounded form needs its OWN miscompile pin (a wrongly-unrolled recursion = miscompile). **(b) the SOPHISTICATED cost model — PARTIALLY CLOSED cycle 28:** the conservative SIZE/cost bloat bound LANDED — `OptPipeline.inlineThreshold` (default 50, max `kMaxInlineThreshold`, config-driven via the pipeline JSON's optional `inlineThreshold`, loader-validated `[1, kMaxInlineThreshold]` → `X_PipelineMalformed`, threaded `runInlining(...,inlineThreshold)` → `inlineLegalityGate`) so the gate now REFUSES a callee whose instruction-count (summed across all blocks during the body scan) EXCEEDS the threshold (`if (instCount > inlineThreshold) return std::nullopt;` — `>` not `>=`, so an at-threshold callee inlines), and **`Inlining` now SHIPS in `release.pipeline.json`** (at index 1, after Identity, before ConstFold, with `inlineThreshold: 50`) bounded by that size cap. Pinned GREEN by `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp::{InlineCostModelRefusesLargeCallee, InlineCostModelBoundaryIsExclusiveUpper, InlineCostModelZeroThresholdRefusesAll}` (a real >threshold Add-chain callee refused while a small one inlines; the EXACT-threshold/over-by-one boundary; the threshold-0 fail-safe refusing all — all RED-on-disable when the cost gate is removed, and the boundary arm RED if `>` is flipped to `>=`) + `tests/opt/test_pipeline_loader.cpp::{InlineThresholdZeroRejects, InlineThresholdOverflowRejects, InlineThresholdNonIntegerRejects, InlineThresholdDefaultsWhenAbsent, InlineThresholdInRange, ShippedReleaseLoadsAllPasses (9-pass exact list + index pin), ShippedReleaseContainsInlining}`. What STAYS deferred under (b): the SOPHISTICATED cost model (call-site hotness / inlining-savings estimation / growth-vs-benefit), NOT the conservative size heuristic. **BLOAT BOUND for the shipped inlining (today):** bounded by (i) the size threshold `inlineThreshold` (default 50) — no callee over the cap inlines, AND (ii) the optimizer's `maxIterations` fixed-point cap — the inliner is one-level-per-run (reads the immutable `mir`, writes a fresh builder), so a deep acyclic chain flattens at most `maxIterations` levels per `optimize` call and the loop terminates. (The pre-cycle-28 bound "(ii) `Inlining` is NOT in release.pipeline.json" is NO LONGER TRUE — inlining ships, and the SIZE threshold is now the load-bearing bloat bound in its place.) **(c)** tightening the conservative address-escape refusal once an escape-analysis substrate can prove a taken address never reaches an indirect call. **(d) IntrinsicCall inlining — ✅ CLOSED cycle 29 (2026-06-05):** the gate's `IntrinsicCall` refusal was REMOVED — a callee whose body contains an `IntrinsicCall` (single-block OR multi-block) is now ADMITTED. It clones SSA-correctly via the splice's generic arm: the intrinsic id lives in the inst PAYLOAD (a module-stable integer the clone copies verbatim) and its operands (the args) remap through the `local` map — no IntrinsicCall-specific field the generic arm drops. Pinned GREEN by `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp::{IntrinsicCalleeIsInlined (value-threaded single-block — main's Return reads the spliced intrinsic), MultiBlockIntrinsicCalleeIsInlined (intrinsic in a non-entry block, multi-block clone path), VoidIntrinsicCalleeIsInlined (result-unused / void splice path)}` — all RED-on-disable (re-adding the `IntrinsicCall` refusal → `callsInlined`→0, the Call survives; demonstrated). The FRAME-SENSITIVITY hazard (a `va_start`/`frameaddress`/`setjmp`-class intrinsic must NOT be inlined — it would bind to the caller's frame) is split out to the trigger-gated `D-OPT7-INLINE-FRAME-SENSITIVE-INTRINSIC`: no shipped frontend emits ANY intrinsic today (the intrinsic registry has no inline-safety attribute and is empty through every real compile — pinned by the c-subset lowering tripwire `tests/hir/test_hir_lowering_c_subset.cpp::NoShippedConstructLowersToIntrinsic`), so blanket admission is correct for the current intrinsic model. Priority: **normal** (the shipped gate is sound — every relaxation only ADMITS more inlines behind a proof; nothing miscompiles today). | The conservative SIZE/cost bound (b) is CLOSED (cycle 28 — `inlineThreshold`, shipped in release); the IntrinsicCall arm (d) is CLOSED (cycle 29 — blanket admission + the trigger-gated frame-sensitivity anchor). REMAINING: build the SOPHISTICATED cost model on top of the size heuristic (call-site hotness / inlining-savings / growth-vs-benefit) (b'); extend the recursion arm from "refuse all cycles" to a depth-bounded policy (a) — each bounded form gets its own negative pin; thread an escape-analysis result so a non-escaping-but-address-taken callee can still inline its direct calls (c). Each relaxation needs its own negative pin (a wrongly-admitted inline = miscompile). | plan 22 §2.9 OPT7; cited in `src/opt/passes/inlining.cpp` (gate) + `src/opt/optimizer.hpp` (`inlineThreshold` / `kDefaultInlineThreshold` / `kMaxInlineThreshold`, cycle 28) + `src/dss-config/pipelines/release.pipeline.json` (shipped, cycle 28) + `src/opt/analysis/call_graph_scc.{hpp,cpp}` (SCC recursion substrate, cycle 27) + `parse_diagnostic.hpp` (`X_InlineMalformedCallSite`); pairs with `D-OPT7-MULTIBLOCK-SPLICE` + `D-OPT7-MULTIBLOCK-SPLICE-PHI` + `D-OPT7-INLINE-FRAME-SENSITIVE-INTRINSIC` (the intrinsic frame-sensitivity follow-on) + `D-OPT7-1` (cross-CU) |
| `D-OPT7-INLINE-FRAME-SENSITIVE-INTRINSIC` | The first FRAME-SENSITIVE intrinsic is registered/emitted by a frontend — a `va_start`/`va_arg`, `frameaddress`/`returnaddress`, `stacksave`/`stackrestore`, or `setjmp`-class intrinsic whose semantics depend on the ENCLOSING function's stack frame. Detected LOUDLY by the c-subset lowering tripwire `tests/hir/test_hir_lowering_c_subset.cpp::NoShippedConstructLowersToIntrinsic`, which goes RED the moment ANY frontend lowers to an intrinsic (the HIR intrinsic registry becomes non-empty through a real compile). Priority: **trigger-gated** (do NOT build until a frame-sensitive intrinsic exists — today NO shipped frontend emits ANY intrinsic, and the only known intrinsics [`sqrt`, `textureSample`] are pure/frame-insensitive, so the MIR inliner's cycle-29 blanket IntrinsicCall admission is correct; building an inline-safety attribute now would be a consumer-less SPECULATIVE mechanism — the §E#5 over-engineering violation). | OPT7 cycle 29 (2026-06-05) made `inlineLegalityGate` blanket-ADMIT IntrinsicCall-bearing callees (`D-OPT7-INLINE-LEGALITY-GATE` arm d). That is SSA-correct for every frame-INSENSITIVE intrinsic but would MISCOMPILE a frame-sensitive one: an inlined `va_start`/`frameaddress` binds to the CALLER's frame, not the callee's. The MIR inliner sees only the bare intrinsic id (no semantics), so it cannot distinguish safe from unsafe. Closing work = add a per-intrinsic INLINE-SAFETY attribute to `HirIntrinsicDescriptor` (`src/hir/hir_intrinsic_registry.hpp` — e.g. `frameSensitive`/`inlineSafe`, registration-driven, default inline-safe), thread it into the MIR `IntrinsicCall` (a side-table or inst flag carried from `hir_to_mir` lowering since the MIR id is bare), and gate the inliner to REFUSE a callee containing a frame-sensitive IntrinsicCall. Pin: a frame-sensitive intrinsic in a callee → NOT inlined (`callsInlined==0`), RED-on-disable; the tripwire updated to register the new intrinsic + assert the gate refuses it. | plan 22 §2.9 OPT7; cited in `src/opt/passes/inlining.cpp` (gate comments at the IntrinsicCall admission + rule 5) + `src/opt/passes/inlining.hpp` (header doc rule 5); the fail-loud tripwire is `tests/hir/test_hir_lowering_c_subset.cpp::NoShippedConstructLowersToIntrinsic`; follows on from `D-OPT7-INLINE-LEGALITY-GATE` arm (d) |
| `D-MERGE-CROSSCU-INTRINSIC-ID-NAMESPACE` | A frontend emits `IntrinsicCall` AND a multi-CU program is merged (`mergeCuMirs`) where two CUs have INDEPENDENT HIR intrinsic registries that minted the SAME bare id for DIFFERENT intrinsics. Detected by the same `NoShippedConstructLowersToIntrinsic` tripwire (no frontend emits intrinsics today → unreachable). Priority: **trigger-gated** (a PRE-EXISTING latent in the c25 merge, NOT introduced by cycle 29's inliner relaxation; unreachable today — surfaced during the cycle-29 design analysis). | `mergeCuMirs` (`src/mir/merge/mir_merge.cpp`) clones an `IntrinsicCall` payload (the bare intrinsic id) VERBATIM across CUs — it remaps TypeIds (`reinternType`) + SymbolIds but NOT intrinsic ids, which are per-HIR-registry indices. Two CUs whose registries independently minted id=1 for different intrinsics (e.g. CU0 `sqrt`, CU1 `textureSample`) would COLLIDE in the merged module. Closing work = unify the intrinsic id space at merge (re-key each CU's `IntrinsicCall` payloads through a merged intrinsic registry, analogous to `reinternType` for TypeIds) OR carry intrinsics by name through the merge. Pin: a 2-CU merge where each CU mints a distinct intrinsic at the same id → the merged `IntrinsicCall`s keep distinct resolved identities. | plan 22 §2.9 OPT7 cross-CU; `src/mir/merge/mir_merge.cpp` (IntrinsicCall clone); future-work pin (do NOT cite in src); pairs with `D-OPT7-CROSSCU-MIR-MERGE` + `D-OPT7-INLINE-FRAME-SENSITIVE-INTRINSIC` |
| `D-OPT7-MULTIBLOCK-SPLICE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-05** (OPT7 cycle 2). The inliner now splices a GENERAL MULTI-BLOCK LEAF callee (cycle 1 was single-block-only). `inlineLegalityGate` LIFTED the `funcBlockCount(callee) != 1` refusal but keeps the leaf scan across ALL callee blocks (no `Call`/`IntrinsicCall` anywhere). Because the `tryRewrite` hook (1 Call → 1 value, mid-block-fill) structurally cannot create blocks, a function with any multi-block target is rebuilt by a new `MultiBlockInliner` (`src/opt/passes/inlining.cpp`): per original caller block kept as a branch target (`blockMap_`); the call-site block SPLIT at the Call (a fresh CONTINUATION block holds the after-Call insts); the callee CFG CLONED in RPO (fresh block+inst ids, shared callee→caller `local` map); each cloned `Return` rewritten to `Br(continuation)` collecting (value, cloned-pred) edges; a RETURN-MERGE PHI in the continuation joins them (elided to the single value for a 1-return callee; void callee → no Phi). StructCfMarker RE-DERIVED to `Linear` for every cloned + continuation block (SimplifyCfg cycle-9 precedent) — a clone of the callee's `EntryBlock`/`ExitBlock` would violate the verifier's entry/exit rules, and transplanting If/Loop marker COUNTS would break caller marker-parity; `Linear` is parity-neutral. Caller `Phi` incomings redirect `pred` through `blockExitMap_` (the last continuation of a split chain). **Proven**: runtime corpus `examples/c-subset/multiblock_inline/` (`pick(x){if(x>0)return 7;return 9;}`; `main` = `pick(1)+pick(0)` = 16) with an `optimizedPipelines:["Inlining"]` arm == baseline 16; **REGRESSION-PROVEN RED** — mis-wiring the merge to always pick the first return edge makes the optimized arm fail to compile (the then-clone value doesn't dominate the else→continuation path → MirVerifier rejects via verify-after-every-pass → differential ASSERT fires). MIR-tier pins in `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp`: `MultiBlockLeafCalleeIsInlined` (Call gone + caller block count +4 [3 clones + 1 cont] + exactly 1 merge Phi + merge-Phi incomings CORRECTLY value↔pred paired [`instBlock(value)==pred` for the 7-from-then + 9-from-else edges; a swapped incoming → RED, catching a mis-merge the MIR tier alone would miss] + verifier-clean, RED-on-disable) + (at cycle-2 time) `MultiBlockNonLeafCalleeIsNotInlined` (scope boundary — **SUPERSEDED in cycle 27**: that scope boundary was the multi-block leaf restriction, which `D-OPT7-INLINE-LEGALITY-GATE` cycle 27 LIFTED; the test was flipped to `MultiBlockNonLeafCalleeIsInlined`, and a multi-block non-leaf callee now inlines). **Robustness gate (review fold 2026-06-05):** `inlineLegalityGate` ALSO refuses a leaf callee with NO RETURNING PATH (no `Return` in any block; every path ends in `Unreachable` — e.g. `int f(){ while(1){} }`). Without the refusal the splice routes each callee `Return` to a continuation block; a never-returning callee leaves that continuation predecessor-less → the MirVerifier (run after every pass) rejects the module → an otherwise-valid program becomes a build error UNDER inlining. Refusing keeps the Call out-of-line so the program compiles (conservative, never a miscompile). Pinned by `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp::NonReturningLeafCalleeIsNotInlined` (multi-block entry→`Unreachable` leaf → callsInlined==0, Call survives, module still verifies; RED-on-disable: removing `if (!hasReturn) return nullopt;` admits it → the splice's `mapCallerValue` fail-loud aborts the build). | (closed) | plan 22 §2.9 OPT7; cited in `src/opt/passes/inlining.{hpp,cpp}` + `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp` + `examples/c-subset/multiblock_inline/`; pairs with `D-OPT7-MULTIBLOCK-SPLICE-PHI` (callee-Phi deferral) + `D-OPT7-INLINE-LEGALITY-GATE` |
| `D-OPT7-MULTIBLOCK-SPLICE-PHI` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-13** (OPT7 callee-Phi cycle — §B design brief signed off, then built to the bar). `inlineLegalityGate`'s callee-`Phi` refusal is DROPPED (TOTAL — no shape gate replaces it); `MultiBlockInliner` now CLONES callee Phis via a DEFERRED-incoming-flush, a sibling of the return-merge `returnEdges` with a DISJOINT map (they compose without interference): a placeholder `dst_.addPhi(instType)` is emitted in the RPO clone loop (recorded into `deferredCalleePhis` + `local`, so later insts referencing the phi resolve), and AFTER the whole clone loop each incoming is flushed — the VALUE remapped via the shared `local` map (`mapCalleeOperand`), the PRED via the `calleeBlockMap` (callee-block→caller-block clone), with a fail-loud `std::abort` on a pred-not-in-clone-map (structurally unreachable — every callee block is pre-created in phase 1). The deferral is LOAD-BEARING for a LOOP-phi: the back-edge incoming VALUE is defined later in RPO (in the latch), so an inline flush would hit `mapCalleeOperand`'s def-before-use abort — flushing after the loop (when `local` is complete) is what makes it resolve. The `emitCalleeInst` Phi arm is KEPT as a defensive fail-loud guard (a Phi is handled in the clone loop and never reaches it; rationale comment corrected). Correctness comes from the remap, NOT from restricting shape — handles any incoming count / multiple phis / Arg-or-instruction incomings / loop back-edges uniformly. AGNOSTIC: purely opcode/map-structural; no language/CPU/format branch (routing keys only on `funcBlockCount != 1`). **Pins** (`tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp`, all RED-on-disable): `CalleePhiJoinAndReturnMergeCoexist` (a callee join-phi AND the inserted return-merge phi coexist + compose via disjoint maps), `LoopPhiCalleeInlinesBackEdgeRemapped` (the back-edge deferral — the ONLY shape that needs it; verifier-clean incl. the `I_LayoutUseBeforeDef` layout rule), `ArgKeyedTernaryPhiCalleeInlinesPaired` (the corpus wiring mirror — `pick(k){return k?7:9;}`), `CalleePhiValueTranspositionIsCaughtOnlyStructurally` (the MirVerifier provably CANNOT catch a value↔pred transposition when both values dominate from a common dominator — both modules verify GREEN; the structural pairing is the only signal), `CalleePhiPredMisremapGoesVerifierRed` (the complementary catchable case — a pred mis-remap DOES go verifier-red via `I_PhiPredNotInCfg`/`I_NotDominated`), and `CalleePhiCommonDominatorArmsPairedThroughInliner` (the design-audit's headline blind-spot, closed THROUGH the real inliner: an Arg-valued ternary `f(int k,int a,int b){return k?a:b;}` called `f(0,7,9)` — after the splice the cloned phi's values are main's consts {7,9} in a COMMON DOMINATOR, so a transposition is verifier-GREEN, making the source-intent pairing [then→7/else→9, navigated via the spliced CondBr since `instBlock(value)==pred` cannot apply] the SOLE red-on-disable guard; mutation-proven RED-on-transposition while `verify()` stayed GREEN). Runtime witness: `examples/c-subset/phi_inline/` (`pick(0)` → exit 9, the `["Inlining"]`-arm corpus). ctest 281→282. Independent review = CLEARS-THE-BAR (no FOLD-NOW); the verifier-blind sole-guard test was folded from the review's NIT-1 (also subsuming NIT-2's Arg-incoming coverage). *(Original status, preserved:)* **🔓 USER-AUTHORIZED 2026-06-13 — the OPT7 hard-stop (`G-406`) is OPENED for this anchor (the user explicitly authorized addressing it: best-long-term + no-workaround + 100% source/target/linker agnostic). It REMAINS a SUPERVISED cycle: per the OPT7 discipline, the closing cycle must open with a §B DESIGN BRIEF for the user's sign-off BEFORE building (the Phi-clone-with-deferred-incoming-flush design needs review). This is a CONSERVATIVE refusal (sound — nothing miscompiles, just a missed inline), but the gap is **FRONTEND-REACHABLE**, NOT a post-optimization edge case: the c-subset frontend emits a Phi-bearing multi-block LEAF callee DIRECTLY, because `hir_to_mir` lowers the ternary `?:` AND the short-circuit `&&` / `||` operators to a MIR `Phi` at the merge — independent of Mem2Reg. Closing work = clone the callee Phi with its incomings REMAPPED through the callee-block→caller-block clone map, deferring the incoming flush to a phase-3 pass (the `MirFunctionRebuilder` phase-3 / `Mem2Reg::finalizePhiIncomings` deferral discipline) so an incoming whose value/pred is emitted later in the clone resolves correctly. | `D-MERGE-UNREACHABLE-BLOCK` | Cycle 25 Stage B (2026-06-05): `mergeCuMirs` (`src/mir/merge/mir_merge.cpp`, `FunctionCloner::clone`) clones each surviving function's REACHABLE CFG — it pre-creates every natural block then fills in RPO from the entry (a valid def-before-use order for SSA). A pre-created block NOT reached by RPO (genuinely unreachable from the entry) is left unfilled, which the `MirBuilder`'s every-block-must-be-filled invariant would abort on at `finish()`; the cloner instead FAILS LOUD first (named abort) with attribution. This is correct for the merge's real inputs because the **c-subset frontend does not emit entry-unreachable blocks** (every block it lowers is reachable from the function entry; DCE, when the active pipeline runs it, additionally removes any dead block), and the hand-built tests are likewise all-reachable — so the unfilled-block case never arises today and the limitation is unobservable. The reachable-only assumption is NOT load-bearing for soundness, though: the **fail-loud abort is the actual backstop** — if a future input ever carried an unreachable block, the merge aborts with attribution rather than emitting a malformed function, so it is never a silent miscompile. Trigger = the first merge input carrying an unreachable block (e.g. a future frontend that emits an entry-unreachable block, or a debug/identity pipeline that runs no DCE on input that happens to contain one). Priority: **normal** (fail-loud today; never a silent miscompile). | Either (a) run DCE / dead-block elimination on each CU's MIR BEFORE the merge so only reachable blocks remain, OR (b) extend `FunctionCloner` to clone unreachable blocks too (fill them after the RPO pass in natural order; their intra-block SSA is self-contained, and cross-block operands into a dead block can't be live, so a second natural-order fill pass over the unfilled set suffices). Pin with a hand-built callee carrying a provably-unreachable block → the merged function preserves it (option b) or the merge runs post-DCE (option a). | plan 22 §2.9 OPT7 (cross-CU MIR merge); cited in `src/mir/merge/mir_merge.cpp`; pairs with `D-OPT7-CROSSCU-MIR-MERGE` |

| `D-LK10-ENTRY-MACHO-SECTIONVA-COMPUTED` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (deferral loop, branch feature/0-0-2-p16).** Both Mach-O exec walkers (`encodeExec` + `encodeExecDynamic`) now FAIL LOUD via a shared chokepoint helper `textSegmentVaMatchesFileOff` (`macho.cpp`) when the schema's __text.virtualAddress-derived offset (`sectionVa - pageZeroSize`) does NOT equal this module's computed `textFileOff = alignUp(headerAndCmds, segmentPageSize)` — the stronger equality the existing congruence guard (`>= pageZeroSize`, `% segmentPageSize`) omits. **FORK resolved (fail-loud over compute-from-textFileOff):** the compute form would need reordering BOTH walkers' layout computation (`headerAndCmds` is computed AFTER `sectionVa`'s first use at lines 859/864) AND speculatively supports a non-existent huge-load-command darwin-exec (§A.2/§D no-speculative-build), whereas fail-loud closes the SILENT inconsistency minimally and matches the existing multi-page-unsupported acknowledgment (macho.cpp ~2057). Byte-identical for the shipped corpus (arm64/x86_64-darwin `headerAndCmds` ~840 ≪ `segmentPageSize` ⇒ `impliedOffset == textFileOff`, check passes silently — 394 ctest green, no corpus change). Pinned RED-on-disable by `MachOExecWriter.SchemaTextVaInconsistentWithTextFileOffFailsLoud` — a synthetic exec format (`loadFromText`, VA = `pageZeroSize + 2×segmentPageSize` = 0x100002000, congruent so validate ACCEPTS it but the textFileOff inequality fires) + trivial module ⇒ encode aborts (`bytes.empty()`); toggle-verified (revert ⇒ test fails). The two walker call sites route through the ONE tested chokepoint helper (§A.5 option-a by-construction coverage; static path end-to-end pinned, dynamic path's identical call verified by independent review). — *Original row (audit trail):* The Mach-O exec walker reads `__text.virtualAddress` from the format JSON and trusts it equals `pageZeroSize + alignUp(headerAndCmds, segmentPageSize)` (i.e. `textSecOffsetInSeg == textFileOff`). For the arm64-darwin corpus this holds because headerAndCmds (~864 bytes) ≪ segmentPageSize (16384), so textFileOff = 0x4000 and the shipped VA is 0x100004000. The walker's congruence guard (`macho.cpp` `(sectionVa - pageZeroSize) % segmentPageSize`) + validate() catch a NON-congruent VA loud, but NEITHER enforces the stronger `== textFileOff` equality (which is module-dependent — headerAndCmds depends on the load commands of the module being linked, not the schema alone). So a future format whose load commands exceed `segmentPageSize` (many dylibs / a huge symtab) would push textFileOff past the hardcoded VA and emit an inconsistent section addr. Strictly BETTER than the pre-16K state (headroom grew from 4096 → 16384 bytes). Priority: **trigger-gated / low** (first darwin-exec whose header+load-commands exceed segmentPageSize). | Compute `sectionVa = pageZeroSize + textFileOff` in the walker (making the schema `virtualAddress` advisory / a cross-check) OR add an encode-time `textSecOffsetInSeg == textFileOff` fail-loud assertion. Pin with a synthetic darwin-exec carrying enough load commands to exceed one segmentPageSize. | `D-LK10-ENTRY-MACHO-EXIT`; `src/link/format/macho.cpp` (the sectionVa derivation); future-work pin (do NOT cite in src) |

One RESIDUAL truly-unreachable overflow remains: `codeSlotCount`'s `(codeLimit + pageSize - 1u)` could wrap in u32 only if `codeLimit` is within `pageSize` of `UINT32_MAX` (a >~4 GiB signed binary) — and `macho.cpp` already fails loud if `codeSigFileOff > u32 max` before the builder runs, AND the `sig.size() != reservation` guard fails loud on any size disagreement, so it is fail-loud-protected + outside this compiler's operating range. Priority: **trigger-gated** (a >~4 GiB output binary reaches the codesign builder). | Widen `codeSlotCount` + `codeDirectoryLength` to compute in `std::uint64_t` with an explicit `> u32 max` fail-loud on the final blob length; OR raise the pageSize floor further. Pin with a synthetic ~4 GiB codeLimit. | `D-LK7-ADHOC-CODESIGN-MACHO` (the builder); cited in `src/link/object_format_schema_json.cpp` (the pageSize range validate) |
| `D-LK7-ADHOC-CODESIGN-MACHO` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-09 — runtime-ACCEPTED on Apple Silicon.** The closure-consistency witness: `D-LK10-ENTRY-MACHO-EXIT` is ✅ CLOSED runtime-confirmed (the `macho_arm64_exit` ad-hoc-signed arm64 binary RUNS on the `macos-latest` leg, CI run 27182845048 #194; the `global_int` + `shipped_include_puts` arm64-darwin corpora likewise execute) — and Apple Silicon's kernel/AMFI SIGKILLs an unsigned or bad-signature arm64 binary, so a green run is direct proof AMFI ACCEPTED DSS's ad-hoc `CodeDirectory`+`SuperBlob`. So a DSS macho64-arm64-darwin-exec could be byte-pinned but **could not RUN** on `macos-latest` → the §A.5 runtime closure is unreachable (exactly the FFI-ISEL over-claim trap). User chose **AD-HOC CODESIGN FIRST** (a self-contained prerequisite — NO CMS/cert, just a CodeDirectory + cdhash) over a byte-pin-only macOS cycle; macOS-ARM64 exec is the cycle AFTER this. **The locked plan** (from a Step-3 understand): (1) **SHA-256 is ABSENT** in `src/` — add a self-contained FIPS-180-4 `src/core/crypto/sha256.{hpp,cpp}` (`dss::crypto`, ~180 lines, FIPS-vector-tested; NOT the heavyweight Plan-16 BearSSL vendoring — ad-hoc needs only SHA-256). (2) **Big-endian** emit helpers (`byte_emit.hpp` is LE-only today; codesign blob headers are BE — the universal gotcha). (3) A `codeSignature` config block on `MachOImage` (`object_format_schema.{hpp,cpp,_json.cpp}`: `{kind:"adhoc", hashAlgorithm:"sha256", pageSize:4096, identifier}` — closed-enum + power-of-two + non-empty + MH_OBJECT-reject fail-loud). (4) **The fill** in a unit-testable `src/link/format/macho_codesign.{hpp,cpp}` (peer of `macho_chained_fixups`): compute `codeLimit=codeSigFileOff`, `nCodeSlots=ceil(codeLimit/pageSize)`, SHA-256 each page, assemble `CS_CodeDirectory`(magic 0xFADE0C02, version 0x20400 w/ execSeg fields [execSegFlags=CS_EXECSEG_MAIN_BINARY], flags=CS_ADHOC 0x2, hashType=2 SHA-256, pageSize=12) + `CS_SuperBlob`(0xFADE0CC0, 1 index), write into the reserved region. **AGNOSTIC**: all in the `macho.cpp` per-format walker (the Mach-O realization layer, like `elf.cpp`) DRIVEN by the format-JSON `codeSignature` block — ZERO shared-substrate `if(format==macho)`. **Verification**: host-independent byte-pins (`tests/link/test_macho_codesign.cpp` — BE magics, CS_ADHOC, hashType, nCodeSlots, codeLimit, page-hashes recomputed via `dss::crypto::sha256` byte-match the embedded slots [the CD-structure + page-slot hashes are pinned; a distinct cdhash=SHA256(whole-CD) pin is deferred — the kernel recomputes cdhash, so a literal would be layout-brittle]) + FIPS-vector pin for SHA-256 + an OPTIONAL `__APPLE__`-gated `codesign --verify` acceptance test on the macos leg (the system tool accepts our bytes; **hermetic-invariant judgment call — plan 16 §0 forbids shelling to codesign to PRODUCE, but read-only verify-our-own-bytes in a test is different; gate behind `__APPLE__`, byte-pins stand alone if deferred**). Proven on the existing x86_64-darwin DYNAMIC path (signature structure is arch-independent; the dynamic path synthesizes `__LINKEDIT`, the static path does not = D-LK7-1, left deferred). **No circularity** (signature is tail-appended; `codeLimit` is fixed before the signature is built — derive the exact reservation size up-front, replacing the hand-typed `codeSignatureSize`). Priority: **high** (the active prerequisite; runs next cycle with fresh context). DEFERS (pin later): cert-based CMS fill (Plan 16 CS2), SHA-256↔BearSSL convergence, the static-path `__LINKEDIT` (D-LK7-1), the macho64-arm64-darwin-exec format JSON + the runnable Apple-Silicon corpus (the cycle-after). | (closed 2026-06-09 — runtime-accepted) | plan 16 (codesign/publish — the ad-hoc subset pulled forward; CMS/cert = CS2, future); plan 14 LK7 (the LC_CODE_SIGNATURE reservation, now filled + runtime-accepted); `D-LK10-ENTRY-MACHO-EXIT` ✅ (the macOS-ARM64 exec arc this unblocked — the runtime witness); `D-LK6-14` (arm64 chained-fixups, the sibling Apple-Silicon mechanism) |
| `D-MERGE-LOWER-EMPTY-NAME-GUARD` | Cycle 25 Stage C (2026-06-05): the lower-half refactor into the shared `lowerMirModuleToAssembly` body (`src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp`) routes symbol names through a `nameOf(SymbolId)→string` seam. A naive restoration of the guard keyed on `nameOf().empty()` is WRONG: `nameOf` returns "" for BOTH "record-with-empty-name" AND "no-record", and a real `extern puts(...)` produces an externally-visible symbol with NO record (→ "" legitimately, MUST be skipped) — so the naive guard regresses real FFI programs (proven: `hello_puts`/`hello_printf`/`printf_int`/`string_literal_arg` → `K_SymbolUndefined`). The guarded state (a record WITH an empty name for an externally-visible symbol) is never produced by the analyzer today, so this is defense-in-depth, NOT a current behavior change (the guard never fired pre-refactor → single-CU byte-identity holds). Priority: **normal**. | Thread a record-EXISTENCE predicate through the shared body (single-CU passes `model.recordFor(sym) != nullptr`; the guard fires on `hasRecord && name.empty() && bind != Local`; the merged path passes a predicate that still skips synthesized externally-visible empty-named symbols). Pin with a (synthetic) externally-visible symbol carrying a record but an empty name → `K_SymbolUndefined`. | cycle-25 review (silent-failure reviewer); `src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp` (`lowerMirModuleToAssembly`); future-work pin (do NOT cite in src) |

| `D-PARSE-INFIX-ASSOCIATIVITY-STRUCTURAL` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-12 (created + closed same cycle — the audit-trail row for a SILENT-MISCOMPILE class).** Same-precedence infix chains parsed RIGHT-associative in every Pratt-walked grammar (c-subset + toy; tsql IMMUNE — its `flatBinaryOpRule` path already left-folded in `lowerFlatExpr`; that path is table-blind for PRECEDENCE too, a sibling latent class pinned as `D-HR10-FLAT-EXPR-TABLE-BLIND`, trigger-gated): `10 - 3 + 1` → 6 (correct 8), `a/b/c` (100,5,2) → 50 (correct 10), the dss-state `ptr_swap_through` probe's `return x - y + 4;` → 34 (correct 42) — target-independent (PE + ELF-x86_64 + ELF-arm64 all wrong identically). ROOT CAUSE: `DefaultPrattWalker::parseExpressionAt` parsed the infix RHS at `op.precedence` unconditionally (right-recursive trees) while its class-doc claimed "a downstream semantic pass reads associativity from the operator table" — **no such consumer ever existed**; the declared `associativity` was a knob-that-lies, and every tree consumer (semantic const-eval, HIR combineBinary, MIR ConstFold) inherited the wrong grouping. Survived 263 green tests because NO corpus example contained a result-changing same-prec chain. FIX (engine, table-driven, zero identity): infix + ternary arms switched to **wrap-in-place** (`wrapLastChildExprFrame` — the mechanism the postfix arm already used) with **RHS minPrec = (assoc==Right ? prec : prec+1)** (Left AND None → structurally left); the climb-level WalkerSnapshot rollback-replay machinery DELETED (caller-less; diag-snapshot + sketch-truncate equivalence argument recorded at the site); **trivia hold-then-place** at the climb top (held until the wrap decision, placed inside the wrapper before the operator) — which ALSO fixed the pre-existing spaced-postfix bug (`f (40)` previously wrapped WHITESPACE → internal H0001, red-on-baseline demonstrated). Left chains now build ITERATIVELY (a 2000-op chain parses clean; previously the depth guard aborted). Pins: exact-tree-shape tests c-subset + synthetic (left/right/None-omitted/ternary-chain/postfix-mix/mixed-prec/spaced/comment-trivia/2000-op) with red-on-disable DEMONSTRATED (rhsMin reverted → right-nested shapes captured → restored hash-identical); semantic const-eval pin (`int g = 10-3+1;` == 8); MIR ConstFold pin; corpus `ptr_swap_through` + `assoc_chains` (LEFT→42, old-RIGHT→26/34 documented in manifests; one deliberately SPACED call) — exit-42 witnessed PE + WSL x86_64-ELF + qemu arm64-ELF; Mach-O = macos leg. dss-state battery: MISCOMPILES section EMPTY, `--strict` exit 0. 265/265. | (closed in the same cycle that created it — recorded for the audit trail; the closing work IS the row above) | `src/analysis/syntactic/parser.cpp` DefaultPrattWalker; dss-state probe `ptr_swap_through`; plan-05 (walker design history — superseded note added); plan-00 §0.2 D15 (the cycle-sequencing decision) |

| `D-MIR-UNREACHABLE-PRUNE-NORMALIZE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-13 (MIR unreachable-prune cycle).** The §B-decided (Design 1) MANDATORY, unconditional MIR normalize that drops every block unreachable-from-entry, REUSING the shared `MirFunctionRebuilder`/`MirRebuildPolicy` substrate (policy: `selectBlocks` = RPO-reachable from entry, `acceptPhiIncoming` = pred-in-blockMap — DCE's exact shape) + a new `cloneGlobalsVerbatim` (NO carve-out — it must process every module; func-ids are stable since the prune drops BLOCKS only, so `initFunc` is tag-re-stamped `MirFuncId{old.v, newTag}`, never ordinal-remapped) + `rederiveStructCfMarkers`. Wired at the TOP of `optimizeModule` (compile_pipeline.cpp ~74), the universal post-lowering chokepoint that runs on every CU BEFORE `mergeCuMirs` AND on the merged module, BEFORE `opt::optimize`'s verify-after-every-pass — NOT a `PassId` (a pipeline config must never omit it); idempotent. This is the single BY-CONSTRUCTION chokepoint that owns the orphan-block class for ALL constructs (if / loop / switch / &&-\|\| / dead-code) and the FUTURE goto (FC5). §B alternatives REJECTED: Design 2 (lazy per-construct) = multi-site (the do-while-break sibling was already missed once that way); Design 3 (verifier exempts `Unreachable`-terminated blocks) = weakens a load-bearing invariant. Closes `D-HIR-SEALED-JOIN-UNREACHABLE-BLOCK`; unblocks plan-23 FC5. 272/272 ctest; 8 strict pins (incl. globals-not-carved-out + phi-pred-drop + red-on-disable) + 2 runnable 4-target corpus examples. | (closed — the prune IS the closing mechanism) | src/opt/passes/prune_unreachable.{hpp,cpp}; `cloneGlobalsVerbatim` in mir_rebuild_helper.{hpp,cpp}; wired in src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp `optimizeModule`; closes `D-HIR-SEALED-JOIN-UNREACHABLE-BLOCK`; unblocks plan-23 FC5 |
| `D-HIR-LOOP-BODY-ONLY-RETURN-DOUBLE-ATTACH` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-13 (pre-FC5 deferral mini-arc).** Root cause was NOT loop-specific: `maybeAppendImplicitReturnZero` (src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp) re-wrapped the body's ALREADY-PARENTED children via `makeBlock(oldKids+ret)`, and `makeBlock`→`addParent` aborts on a re-parented child (HIR is immutable) — so ANY non-empty non-terminating `main` crashed (the infinite-loop shapes AND `int main(){int x;x=1;}` are instances; the empty `int main(){}` survived only because oldKids was empty). FIX: immutability-safe nest — wrap the (unparented) body block + the synthetic return in a fresh outer block (`makeBlock({body,ret})`), never re-parenting children; the `addParent` guard is untouched. Infinite-loop mains now compile + run (the dead `return 0` is dropped by the MIR unreachable-prune); 274/274 ctest; corpus `infinite_loop_return_main` (exit 5) + `for_inf_return_main` (exit 9) + 2 HIR pins; red-on-disable demonstrated. The SEPARATE non-`main` infinite-loop over-rejection is `D-HIR-INFINITE-LOOP-NOT-TERMINATING` (next in the mini-arc). *(Original status, preserved:)* **OPEN (surfaced 2026-06-13 by the MIR unreachable-prune cycle's test-shape exploration — PRE-EXISTING; the prune diff touches NO src/hir/ or src/mir/lowering loop builder). Priority: normal.** A loop whose body is ONLY a return/seal (`while(1){return X;}`, `do{return X;}while(c);`, `for(;;){return X;}`) trips a HirBuilder fatal `addParent: HirNodeId=N already has a parent (double-attach)` during HIR construction — the tree-shaped-HIR structural guard fires on a VALID-C shape. Orthogonal to the MIR prune (upstream, in HIR build); the prune's loop-orphan coverage is instead via `for(i=0;i<10;i=i+1){return 11;}` (update-block orphan). | Root-cause the double-attach in HirBuilder for a loop body that seals immediately (a node re-parented between the body slot and a synthesized continue/exit edge); pin with the three loop-only-return shapes compiling + running. | hir_to_mir.cpp loop lowering; HirBuilder `addParent` guard; surfaced by the prune cycle; future-work pin (do NOT cite in src) |
| `D-HIR-DEAD-CODE-AFTER-RETURN-REJECTED` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-13 (pre-FC5 deferral mini-arc).** Relaxed the HIR verifier's dead-code-after-terminator rejection: `checkBlockTermination` now emits a NEW `H_UnreachableCode` (0xF00D) WARNING instead of the `H_VerifierFailure` ERROR, so `verify()` passes and `return X; <dead>;` COMPILES — the dead statement reaches MIR, lowers via the Block-lowering fresh-dead-block, and is dropped by the unreachable-prune (runtime = the reachable `return X`). ISO-C-aligned (warn, don't reject). Scope: ONLY `checkBlockTermination`; the non-void-fall-through `H_VerifierFailure` ERROR (`checkReturnCompleteness`) is UNTOUCHED (still rejects). The new code is suppressible (not in the unsuppressable set). 281/281 ctest; corpus `dead_code_after_return` (exit 42) + 5 HIR warning pins; red-on-disable pinned. *(Original status, preserved:)* **OPEN (surfaced 2026-06-13 by the MIR unreachable-prune cycle — PRE-EXISTING). Priority: normal (matters for the full-C / FC arc).** Statements after a `return` in the same block (`return 42; return 7;`) are REJECTED by the HIR verifier (`H0003`, "statement is unreachable") BEFORE MIR — so the MIR prune is not their guard. This is stricter than ISO C (which permits unreachable code, typically with a warning); a real full-C program with dead-code-after-terminator would be rejected. | For the FC arc: relax the HIR verifier to ACCEPT (optionally warn on) unreachable-after-terminator statements, letting the MIR prune drop the resulting dead blocks; pin with `return X; <dead>;` compiling + running X. | HIR verifier `H0003`; `D-MIR-UNREACHABLE-PRUNE-NORMALIZE` is the downstream dead-block owner once HIR admits it; plan-23 full-C arc; future-work pin (do NOT cite in src) |
| `D-HIR-INFINITE-LOOP-NOT-TERMINATING` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-13 (pre-FC5 deferral mini-arc).** Implemented the documented design: `lowerWhile`/`lowerFor` (cst_to_hir.cpp) now wrap a PROVABLY-INFINITE loop (const-truthy/absent condition via the shared `evalCstConstInt` const-eval, AND no `break` reachable in this loop's own frame — a frame-respecting body scan that does NOT descend into nested loops/switches) as `Block{ loop, Unreachable }`, so `pathTerminates` (recursing to the Block's last child) recognizes the function terminates. `int f(){while(1){return 5;}}` now compiles + runs (exit 5); `for(;;)`/`do-while(1)` likewise. NEGATIVE-pinned (no false positive = no miscompile): a breakable `while(1){if(x)break;}`, `while(0)`, `while(x)`, and a nested-inner-break are NOT wrapped. A statement-position `Unreachable` gained MIR lowering (`addUnreachable`), and MIR Block-lowering opens a fresh dead block for any sibling after a mid-block seal (dropped by the MIR unreachable-prune) — which INCIDENTALLY fixed a pre-existing latent crash of `<both-arms-return-if> <sibling>` (`int f(){if(x){return 1;}else{return 2;} return 99;}`), now pinned (corpus `nonmain_if_both_ret_dead_tail`). `pathTerminates`/the verifier/H0003/the inliner UNTOUCHED. 280/280 ctest; red-on-disable pinned. *(Original status, preserved:)* **OPEN (surfaced 2026-06-13 by the pre-FC5 deferral mini-arc — PRE-EXISTING). Priority: normal (next in the mini-arc, before FC5).** `pathTerminates` (the HIR verifier-substrate SSOT, src/hir/hir_verifier.hpp) is deliberately CONSERVATIVE — it does NOT count a loop body as terminating; the doc says lowering should append an `Unreachable` after a PROVABLY-INFINITE loop to make the structural answer match the dynamic one, but that step was NEVER implemented (no Unreachable-synthesis in cst_to_hir.cpp). Consequence: a non-`main` function whose only exit is inside a provably-infinite loop (`int f(){ while(1){ return 5; } }`, `for(;;){...}`) is OVER-REJECTED by the non-void-return-completeness check (pathTerminates(body)==false). Valid ISO C. (`main` is unaffected as of 2026-06-13: the implicit-return-0 nest gives it a terminating return; this anchor is the NON-`main` case.) Sibling of `D-HIR-DEAD-CODE-AFTER-RETURN-REJECTED` (both are pathTerminates/termination over-rejections of valid C). | Implement the documented design: detect a provably-infinite loop (constant-truthy or absent condition AND no `break`/`goto` that can exit this loop's frame) in cst_to_hir lowering and append a synthetic `Unreachable` after it, so `pathTerminates` recognizes the function terminates (the MIR unreachable-prune drops the resulting dead block). Pin: `int f(){while(1){return 5;}}` called from main → exit 5; a breakable loop still requires a real return after it. | src/hir/hir_verifier.hpp `pathTerminates` doc (the unimplemented append-`Unreachable` contract); src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp loop lowering; the non-void-completeness check in hir_verifier.cpp; sibling `D-HIR-DEAD-CODE-AFTER-RETURN-REJECTED`; downstream owner `D-MIR-UNREACHABLE-PRUNE-NORMALIZE`; future-work pin (do NOT cite in src) |
| `D-PP-FUNCTION-LIKE-MACRO` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-19 (FC13 cycle 2 — function-like macros).** The config-selected C preprocessor now implements function-like `#define F(a,b) ...` definitions AND invocations over the common direct-invocation subset of §6.10.3 (full hide-set deferred → `D-PP-MACRO-HIDESET-PRECISE`). `MacroExpander::handleDefine` parses the parameter list (comma-separated `Word` params, adjacency `(`-after-name detection on raw spans, empty `()` ⇒ zero params); the redefinition-compatibility check compares kind + params + replacement text (`sameDefinition`). The cycle-1 token-by-token `expandInto` was reworked into a STREAM expander `expand(std::span<Token const>, painting, depth)` (object- AND function-like uniformly): a function-like name is an invocation ONLY if the next significant token is the configured `(` (else emitted verbatim, §6.10.3p10); `collectArgs` collects paren-balance-tracked arguments (depth via the configured open/close paren; a depth-1 comma separates, the depth-0 close ends; trivia-trimmed); each argument is PRE-EXPANDED (`expand` in the current paint context, the invoked macro not yet painted, §6.10.3.1); `substitute` maps param-name tokens → pre-expanded arg tokens; the result is RESCANNED with the macro painted blue (§6.10.3.4 self-reference freeze). Zero-PARAM `M()` is normalized to zero args at the call site. FAIL-LOUD: arity mismatch + unterminated invocation (`P_PreprocessorMacroArgument`, new 0x0017); duplicate parameter + malformed param list (`P_PreprocessorDirective`); VARIADIC `#define V(...)` (`P_PreprocessorUnsupported` → `D-PP-VARIADIC-MACRO`, deferred). AGNOSTIC: the close-paren is config (`preprocess.functionLikeCloseToken`, mirroring the opener — `)` lexes as core `Punctuation`, so it MUST come from config). The cycle-1 `FunctionLikeMacroDefinitionFailsLoud` test was FLIPPED to `FunctionLikeMacroSimpleInvocationExpands`; 12 new function-like unit tests (nested, arg-is-macro pre-expansion, arity, verbatim-name, mixing, recursive-freeze, empty/zero-paren, variadic, dup-param, unterminated) + runnable 4-target corpus `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_function_macros` (exit 42). 331 ctest; object-macro tests + corpus unregressed; RED-on-disable demonstrated (neutering `substitute`). *(Original status, preserved:)* FC13 cycle 1 (2026-06-19): the config-selected C preprocessor (`src/analysis/preprocess/`) implements OBJECT-like `#define`/`#undef` only. A FUNCTION-like macro DEFINITION (`#define F(x) ...` - a `(` immediately adjacent to the macro name) is recognized and FAILS LOUD (`P_PreprocessorUnsupported`, `MacroExpander::handleDefine`), never silently passed through as object-like (which would mis-expand). Priority: **normal (cycle 2)** - the function-like macro feature (parameter capture, argument substitution; the `#`/`##` operators are SEPARATE = FC15) is the next preprocessor cycle. | Build function-like macro definition + invocation: parse the parameter list, capture call-site arguments (paren-balanced), substitute parameters into the replacement, then rescan. Pin: `#define F(x) ((x)+1)` + `F(2)` expands to `((2)+1)`; arity mismatch fails loud. Closing test in `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` (today `FunctionLikeMacroDefinitionFailsLoud` pins the fail-loud guard - flip it to expand-correctly when the feature lands). | plan 23 FC13; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp`; successor cycle 2 |
| `D-PP-MACRO-HIDESET-PRECISE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-19 (FC13 cycle 4 — the precise per-token hide set).** The recursion-scoped blue-PAINT set was REPLACED by Prosser's precise per-token hide set (the standard realization of C §6.10.3.4) — within FC13's no-`#`/`##` scope this now realizes the FULL §6.10.3.4, not merely the cycle-2/3-softened "common direct-invocation subset of §6.10.3". `Token` is a 16B trivially-copyable POD that cannot carry a set, so the expansion WORKING set is a wrapper `struct ExpToken { Token tok; HideSet hide; }` where `HideSet = shared_ptr<set<string> const>` (IMMUTABLE-once-built → copies are a refcount bump, every token of one replacement shares the SAME set object; a null pointer is the canonical EMPTY hide set, so the parser-bound body lifts in with `nullptr` and pays no allocation; `std::set` makes the hot intersection/union a linear merge). `run` lifts `vector<Token>` → `vector<ExpToken>` (empty hide sets), runs `expand`, drops hide sets back to `vector<Token>`. `expand` is now a SPLICE-RESCAN cursor (no longer isolated recursion): on each expansion it splices the produced tokens — each carrying its computed hide set — back over the consumed `[i,past)` region of the working set and rescans from the splice point, so a function-like name and a `(` that become adjacent only ACROSS the replacement/parent boundary RE-PAIR. A token (name M) expands iff `M ∉ hideset(T)`. OBJECT-like M: replacement tokens carry `hideset(T) ∪ {M}`. FUNCTION-like M (next significant token is the configured `(`, matching `)` exists): `HS' = (hideset(name) ∩ hideset(closeParen)) ∪ {M}` (the Prosser intersection — `collectArgs` surfaces the close paren's hide set; an argument's own accreted hide set is UNIONED with `HS'`, never dropped). The cycle-2/3 cross-boundary witnesses were FLIPPED: `#define A(x) x`+`#define F(x) ((x)+100)`+`A(F)(3)` → `((3)+100)` (was the literal `F(3)`); `#define NAME SQ`+`#define SQ(x) ((x)*(x))`+`NAME(4)` → `((4)*(4))` (was a frozen bare `SQ`). PRESERVED: direct object/function self-reference freeze (`#define X X`→`X`; `#define F(x) F(x)`+`F(1)`→`F(1)`), mutual recursion termination (`P↔Q`+`P(1)`), nested/argument-pre-expansion, variadic, object — all unchanged. The `depth` backstop is RETAINED as defense-in-depth but now bounds NESTING (argument pre-expansion) rather than chain length: a finite object CHAIN (`M0`→…→`M300`→0) now expands ITERATIVELY in one frame to `0` (the cycle-2/3 `DeepMacroExpansionFailsLoudNotSilent` premise was a recursive-engine artifact — FLIPPED to `DeepFiniteMacroChainExpandsToValue`), while a pathological 256-deep-nested argument (`F(F(…F(0)…))`) still FAILS LOUD (`DeepNestedMacroArgumentFailsLoudNotSilent`). AGNOSTIC: the new machinery operates on `Token`/`SchemaTokenId` + macro NAMES only; `(`/`)`/`,`/`...`/`__VA_ARGS__` remain config-driven (no new hard-coded lexeme/arch/format string). +6 net preprocessor unit tests (2 cross-boundary FLIPs, 4 preserved-freeze) + reframed deep-chain (1→2) + runnable 4-target corpus `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_hideset` (object-macro-names-function-macro + function-returns-function-name, runtime-seeded via `ident`, exit 42). 333 ctest; all object/function/variadic preprocessor tests + corpora unregressed; RED-on-disable demonstrated (reverting object-like to isolated recursion mis-freezes `NAME(4)` → bare `SQ`, `test_preprocessor.cpp` `HideSetObjectMacroNamingFunctionMacroExpands` fails). *(Original status, preserved:)* FC13 cycle 2 (2026-06-19): the function-like macro rescan uses a recursive blue-PAINT set (`expand`'s `painting` keyed on the macro NAME) — an APPROXIMATION of the precise per-token §6.10.3.4 hide-set. It is correct for the common DIRECT-invocation forms (a name immediately or near-adjacently invoked, self-reference freeze, nested/argument-as-macro pre-expansion — all pinned), but does NOT expand cases where a function-like name and its `(` are produced across SEPARATE expansion streams: `#define A(x) x` + `#define F(x) ((x)+100)` + `A(F)(3)` → the `A(F)` yields a bare `F`, and the trailing `(3)` is NOT re-paired with it, so the result is the literal `F(3)`; likewise `#define NAME SQ` + `#define SQ(x) ((x)*(x))` + `NAME(4)` freezes to bare `SQ` (the object expansion of `NAME` does not re-scan to collect `(4)`). These residual cases are a COMPLETENESS gap, NOT a silent miscompile: each FAILS LOUD downstream at the PARSER (verified: `got F` / `got SQ` — a bare function-like name with a stray `(…)` is a syntax error), never producing wrong code. `A(F)(3)` is the constructible loud-failure witness. Co-designed with FC15's `#`/`##` token-marker cluster (the precise per-token hide-set is the same machinery that tracks `#`/`##` placemarkers). Priority: **normal / trigger-gated** — closes with FC15, or sooner if a real corpus needs cross-stream re-pairing. | Replace the name-keyed blue-paint with a precise per-token hide-set (each replacement token carries the set of macro names disabled for it, per §6.10.3.4): a token's hide-set is the intersection of the invoked-macro's hide-set with the next-token's, plus the invoked macro; re-pairing of a function-like name with a `(` produced by a SEPARATE expansion then follows. Pin: `A(F)(3)` → `((3)+100)`; `NAME(4)` → `((4)*(4))`. Implement alongside the FC15 `#`/`##` placemarker tokens. | plan 23 FC13/FC15; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` `expand`/`painting`; witness `A(F)(3)` (today fails loud `got F` at the parser); FC15-area cluster (precise hide-set co-designed with `#`/`##`) |
| `D-PP-VARIADIC-MACRO` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-19 (FC13 cycle 3 — variadic `__VA_ARGS__` macros).** The config-selected C preprocessor now implements PLAIN variadic function-like macros over the C99/C23 `__VA_ARGS__` subset (NO `#`/`##`, so NO GNU `,##__VA_ARGS__` comma-elision — that stays FC15). `MacroDef` gained `bool isVariadic`; `parseParamList` ACCEPTS a trailing `...` (the configured `variadicMarkerToken` KIND) as the LAST parameter-list element (`(...)` or `(a, b, ...)`) — setting `isVariadic`, replacing the cycle-2 `P_PreprocessorUnsupported` fail-loud — and FAILS LOUD (`P_PreprocessorDirective`) when `...` is NOT last (`(a, ..., b)` / a stray token after it). The catch-all identifier is CONFIG-DRIVEN: new `PreprocessConfig::variadicArgsName` (c-subset `"__VA_ARGS__"`, matched by TEXT in the replacement like the directive words; loader OPTIONAL + non-empty-string-validated, `C_InvalidPreprocess`). Invocation of `F(named..., ...)`: the first `params.size()` args bind to the named params; the REST form `__VA_ARGS__` as ONE comma-joined run that PRESERVES the ORIGINAL source separator commas (captured by `collectArgs` into a `separators` vector), each trailing arg PRE-EXPANDED like a named arg (C 6.10.3.1). ARITY floor = the NAMED count (`args.size() < params.size()` → too-few fail-loud, `P_PreprocessorMacroArgument`); the variadic portion MAY be EMPTY (C23 — `__VA_ARGS__` then substitutes to nothing). `substitute` replaces a replacement `Word` whose text == `variadicArgsName` IN A VARIADIC macro with the `__VA_ARGS__` run; `__VA_ARGS__` in a NON-variadic (object- or function-like) macro is REJECTED at DEFINITION (`P_PreprocessorDirective`, C 6.10.3p5). AGNOSTIC: `__VA_ARGS__` appears as a STRING nowhere in `src/*.{cpp,hpp}` (only in comments + the c-subset config JSON) — the engine reads `cfg().variadicArgsName`; a red-on-disable rebind test (`variadicArgsName` → `__REST__`) proves it. The cycle-2 `VariadicMacroDefinitionFailsLoud` test was FLIPPED to `VariadicMacroNamedPlusVaArgsExpands`; +7 new variadic unit tests (named+__VA_ARGS__, zero-named, empty-C23, trailing-arg-pre-expansion, `...`-not-last, too-few-args, `__VA_ARGS__`-in-non-variadic) + a config-driven `VaArgsNameIsConfigDrivenNotHardcoded` pin + runnable 4-target corpus `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_variadic_macros` (runtime-seeded via `ident`, exit 42). All function/object preprocessor corpora + tests unregressed; RED-on-disable demonstrated (neutering the `__VA_ARGS__` arm in `substitute`). *(Original status, preserved:)* FC13 cycle 2 (2026-06-19): the function-like macro engine (`src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp`, `MacroExpander::parseParamList`) RECOGNISES a VARIADIC parameter list (`#define V(...) ...` — a `...` ellipsis lexeme in parameter position) and FAILS LOUD (`P_PreprocessorUnsupported`), never silently accepting it as a named parameter (which would mis-substitute) and never dropping it. Variadic macros need the `__VA_ARGS__` substitution mechanism + the `,##__VA_ARGS__` GNU comma-elision, which pair naturally with the `#`/`##` operators — both are FC15-area, so this is parked with that cluster. Priority: **trigger-gated** — closes when FC15 (`#`/`##`/predefined + variadic) lands, or sooner if a real corpus needs `__VA_ARGS__`. | Implement `...` in the parameter list + the `__VA_ARGS__` identifier in the replacement (collect the trailing arguments incl. their separating commas into one token run; substitute at `__VA_ARGS__`), plus the GNU `,##__VA_ARGS__` empty-elision once `##` exists. Pin: `#define LOG(fmt, ...) printf(fmt, __VA_ARGS__)` expands a call; the zero-trailing-arg case elides the comma. The closing test flips `VariadicMacroDefinitionFailsLoud` in `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp`. | plan 23 FC13/FC15; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` `parseParamList`; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp::VariadicMacroDefinitionFailsLoud`; FC15-area cluster |

| `D-PP-INCLUDE-SPLICE-POSITION-ATTRIBUTION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-19 (FC13 cycle-1 review fold — Fix 2).** Resolved via option (a): `makeRemap` now redirects BOTH header- AND main-origin diagnostics off the synth buffer onto their real origin buffer (the main-origin early-return was removed), and the line-map's per-segment `originStart` (already built across splice boundaries by `copyVerbatim`/`appendWithContinuationSplice`) shifts a post-`#include` main-file synth offset back to its ORIGINAL main.c offset -- so a main-file error after a leading quote-`#include` now reports the real main.c line, not a splice-shifted synth line. The missing pieces were: (1) `PreprocessResult::originBuffers` (the distinct origin buffers the line-map references, ex-synth) surfaced as `CompilationUnit::auxiliaryBuffers()`, and (2) registering them with the diagnostic `BufferRegistry` at every render site (`program.cpp runCusToTargets` + the `test_diagnostic_corpus` harness) so a remapped diagnostic resolves instead of rendering `--> <unknown-buffer:N>`. No residual: header AND main both attribute to the correct file:line. *(Original status, preserved:)* FC13 cycle 1 (2026-06-19): the preprocessor line-map remaps a HEADER-origin diagnostic to the header file, but a MAIN-file-origin diagnostic is intentionally LEFT on the synthesized buffer (`PreprocessResult::makeRemap` skips the main-source origin). For a no-include TU the synth buffer is byte-identical to the main file so line:col matches; for a TU with a LEADING quote-`#include` the spliced header text SHIFTS subsequent main-file content, so a main-file diagnostic renders at the SYNTH line:col, not the original main.c line:col. | (closed by Fix 2 — option (a) implemented) | plan 23 FC13; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` `makeRemap`/`LineMap`/`originBuffers`; `CompilationUnit::auxiliaryBuffers`; `program.cpp` registry; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |

Short-circuit `&&`/`||` + ternary fold only the live arm (C 6.6 unevaluated operands not folded → `0 && (1/0)` is safe); div0/shift/overflow refuse via `EvalOptions` → `P_Preprocessor*`. 20 strict unit pins (precedence `1+2*3==7` vs `==9`, `defined` paren+no-paren, dead-`#elif` operand-not-evaluated, `#elif`/`#else`-after-`#else`, config-rebind agnosticism) + runnable `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_conditionals` (exit 42, 4 targets + release arm). | `D-PP-CONDITIONAL-INCLUDE-ORDERING` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-26 (p18 Cluster G c17).** Both symptoms fixed: a quote-`#include` guarded by a dead `#if`/`#ifdef` is no longer resolved (P0016), and an illegal char (`$`/`@`/`` ` ``) in a dead branch no longer reports (P000E) while a LIVE one STILL fails loud. **★ The originally-planned Option A (a pre-scan dead-region oracle) was IMPLEMENTED then CAUGHT by the in-cycle review as a CRITICAL SILENT MISCOMPILE: the SynthBuilder pre-scan cannot see predefined (or header-supplied) macros, so a predefined-macro-guarded LIVE branch (`#if __STDC__`) was folded to 0 -> recorded DEAD -> a genuinely-live illegal char consumed before the parser (e.g. on a `#define` line) was SILENTLY suppressed (reproduced end-to-end via the CLI: `#if __STDC__\n#define M $\n#endif` compiled with zero diagnostics).** §B (user, 2026-06-26): **Option 1 — authoritative dead-regions.** The P000E suppression is now driven by the AUTHORITATIVE `MacroExpander` pass's liveness (`MacroExpander::deadRanges()`, recorded in `run()` from the full `table_`+`predefined_` state) — the SAME liveness the compiler emits/skips tokens on — so the illegal-char oracle can NEVER diverge from the real branch decision (the silent class is gone by construction). The P0016 include gate stays in the conditional-aware `SynthBuilder` (it must precede the macro pass), gated on `includeResolvable()`; its pre-scan dead-region machinery was REMOVED (now dead code). Plan-locked GO-WITH-FIXES (3 blocking folded: byte-interval recorder spec, per-directive re-sync, the proven-RED predefined test + false-green guard). Pins: the c17 `Preprocessor.*` block in `test_preprocessor.cpp` (`DeadBranch*`, `LiveBranch*`, `ActiveIllegalChar*` incl. `#define`-line/`#`-stringize/uninvoked-body, `PredefinedMacroGuardedLiveIllegalCharStillErrors` + false-green guard, `LiveOuterDeadInnerNest*`, `UnterminatedDeadBranch*`, `DeadRegionCloseUsesConfigEndifWord*`, FIX-3 function-like + `UnevaluableGuard*`) + runnable corpus `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_dead_branch_include` (predefined-guard live arm; exit 42, 4 targets + release). A residual conservative LOUD edge (predefined-VALUE-position guards in the include gate) is pinned separately as `D-PP-PRESCAN-PREDEFINED-VALUE-INCLUDE-GATE`. — ORIGINAL TRIGGER-FIRED note (2026-06-26): SQLite is the first real input to hit it: `#if SQLITE_OS_WIN`→`#include "windows.h"` resolves on linux/macos (P0016 ×5) AND a TCL gen-script in `#if 0` (sqlite3.c:32562) trips the illegal-char `$` (P000E ×10). §B DECISION (user, 2026-06-26): **Option A — a conditional pre-scan** (evaluate the `#if`/`#ifdef`/`#endif` structure right after tokenization + STRIP dead-group tokens BEFORE SynthBuilder splices quote-includes AND before illegal-char diagnostics finalize; share the conditional eval with the existing MacroExpander pass — DRY). Chosen over Option B (fold include-splice into the MacroExpander + separately defer the tokenizer illegal-char) for the single-mechanism cleanliness. — ORIGINAL deferral below. FC14 named exclusion (2026-06-22): a quote-`#include` GUARDED by a conditional is spliced by `SynthBuilder` BEFORE the `MacroExpander` conditional pass runs, so `#if 0\n#include "x.h"\n#endif` still RESOLVES x.h (its spliced CONTENT is then correctly elided by the conditional pass, but x.h's resolution side-effects — recursive includes, a missing-file `P_PreprocessorIncludeError` — already happened). NOT a silent miscompile: the elision is correct, and a missing dead-branch include errors LOUDLY (over-approximation, never a silent mis-include). The same class covers an illegal char (`@`) inside a dead branch (the single global tokenize precedes elision → a `P_IllegalChar`, loud). Priority: **normal** (rare — conditionally-guarded includes; the common case works). | Reorder so conditional elision precedes quote-include splicing — i.e. run a conditional/`#if` pre-scan that strips dead groups from the token stream BEFORE `SynthBuilder` resolves quote includes (or fold include resolution INTO the conditional-aware macro pass). Pin: `#if 0\n#include "does_not_exist.h"\n#endif\nint x;` compiles clean (no include error). | plan 23 FC14; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`SynthBuilder` splice vs the conditional pass ordering) |
| `D-PP-PRESCAN-PREDEFINED-VALUE-INCLUDE-GATE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-18 (SQLite testfixture arc C21).** The include-gating pre-scan (`SynthBuilder`) now materializes command-line `--define` AND object-like predefined macro VALUES (not just definedness), so a `#if <macro>` VALUE guard gating a quote-`#include` evaluates correctly. MECHANISM (Option 2, span-safe per the independent design-audit): `preprocess()` builds a `#define NAME VALUE\n` PREFIX — every `--define` (parsed EXACTLY like the `<command-line>` prologue, VALUE defaults to 1) PLUS every OBJECT-like predefined available on the active format — and each `SynthBuilder::build()` PREPENDS it as a NON-EMITTED span header of that build's scan buffer, so a seeded macro's replacement tokens slice the SAME `scanBuf` `sbExpand` reads (foreign-buffer spans were REJECTED as unsound in C19; this keeps every span native). The prefix is STRUCTURALLY un-emittable: `copyVerbatim` is SEGMENT-DRIVEN and the prefix has NO localMap segment (it is prepended as raw bytes, NOT via `appendWithContinuationSplice`), plus `copiedUpTo` starts at `prefixLen` — two stated load-bearing invariants, so NO pre-pass is needed. FINDING-A: FUNCTION-like predefines are EXCLUDED from the prefix (`isFunctionLike`) — value-seeding a bare `#if NAME` (no call) would make the pre-scan MORE-live than the authoritative pass → a silent P0016 re-open. FINDING-B: the predefined per-format filter is now the SINGLE shared helper `predefinedMacroAvailableOnActiveFormat`, which `sbNameDefined` + the `MacroExpander` `predefined_` seed + the `<built-in>` prologue ALL call, so the seed set cannot drift from the authoritative set. Option 2 SUBSUMES + REMOVES the C19 `seededDefines` NAME set (definedness now via `localMacros`) AND composes with a source `#undef` (which erases the seeded value). The one-directional-divergence invariant holds (the seeded values EXACTLY match the authoritative prologues → the pre-scan is more-live only IN LOCKSTEP → P0016 stays closed; a 0-valued define stays dead). RESULT: the 4 P9006 `test*.c` TUs (test6/test_devsym/test_journal/test_vfs — `#if SQLITE_TEST` → `#include "sqlite3.h"` → the `sqlite3_file`/`u8` typedef → the `(TYPE*)&p[1]` cast) go CLEAN. 8 red-on-disable pins in `test_preprocessor.cpp` (value core, value-vs-definedness parity, P0016 negative, FINDING-C not-emit synthText-count, predefined-value + FALSE converse, FINDING-A function-like exclusion, `#undef`-compose) + Pin-3 comment refreshed. It does NOT materialize a predefined macro used in VALUE position (`#if __STDC_VERSION__ >= 201112L`), folding it to 0. So a LIVE such branch's quote-`#include` is conservatively SKIPPED (→ loud missing-symbol downstream) and an angle-`#include`'s descriptor MACRO surface is not spliced (→ loud undefined-macro). **NEVER silent** (the c17 illegal-char oracle is authoritative via `MacroExpander::deadRanges()`; only the include gate is affected, whose every failure mode is loud). The user-approved "seed predefined macros into the pre-scan" deferred here per the c17 plan-lock (loud-only + rare → disproportionate to build the predefined value-materialization machinery now).
