Pin: `#if __STDC_VERSION__ >= 201112L\n#include "missing_live.h"\n#endif` → P_PreprocessorIncludeError (the live include IS resolved) + converse `#if __STDC_VERSION__ < 0` skips. | plan 25 c17; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`SynthBuilder::sbEvalIfOperand`/`sbExpand`/`includeResolvable`); `src/analysis/preprocess/pp_if_eval.hpp` (`PpProductText`) |
| `D-PP-PRESCAN-DEFINEDNESS-PARITY` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-18 (SQLite testfixture arc C19).** The `SynthBuilder` include-gating pre-scan (the quote-`#include` inliner; `D-PP-CONDITIONAL-INCLUDE-ORDERING` lineage) was BLIND to command-line `--define`s: they are materialized as a `<command-line>` `#define` PROLOGUE spliced straight into `synthText` (preprocess() ~L3313), but `builder.build(mainSource,…)` scans only `mainSource`, so `localMacros` (populated by `sbTrackDefine` during the scan) never learned them. Plus an ASYMMETRY: `#ifdef`/`#ifndef` (`isDefinedTok`) checked ONLY `localMacros` while `#if defined()` (`definedCb`) also checked `predefinedMacros`. NET: a `#ifdef <cmdline-define>`-gated quote-`#include` was read DEAD → left un-inlined → its `#define`s silently DROPPED → surfaced downstream as a spurious P0009 at the macro's use site (the real `#ifdef SQLITE_TEST` [test_intarray.c:282] → `#include "tclsqlite.h"` → the empty `#define SQLITE_TCLAPI` [tclsqlite.h:29] drop → `static int SQLITE_TCLAPI test_fn(` P0009 across the 7 dense `src/test*.c` TUs: test_intarray/multiplex/osinst/quota/schema/superlock/window). FIX (definedness-only, span-SAFE per the independent design-audit — a VALUE seed carrying foreign-buffer spans was REJECTED as unsound: `SourceSpan` is bare `[start,end)` offsets + `sbExpand` slices every token against the single `scanBuf`, so a foreign span → garbage bytes + a P0016 re-open risk): a shared `std::unordered_set<std::string> seededDefines` (command-line `--define` NAMES, built in preprocess() from the SAME `userDefines`), held by const-ref on EVERY SynthBuilder + threaded into children; a NEW single oracle `sbNameDefined(n)` = localMacros ∨ seededDefines ∨ predefinedMacros(format-filtered) used by BOTH `definedCb` and `isDefinedTok` (removing the asymmetry). The one-directional-divergence invariant is PRESERVED (the seed/predefined names are exactly those the authoritative `MacroExpander` sees defined → the pre-scan can only be MORE-live IN LOCKSTEP, never resolving a branch the real pass reads dead → P0016 stays closed). VALUE-context command-line/predefined guards (`#if NAME==k`) stay conservative → LOUD (never silent) — deferred as the SAME class the sibling `D-PP-PRESCAN-PREDEFINED-VALUE-INCLUDE-GATE` owns for predefined (the command-line value case is identical). RESULT: the 44 `test*.c` CLEAN count 24→31 (all 7 P0009 gone; test_intarray CLEAN); sqlite smoke unregressed (x86_64 → 3.54.0 + CRUD 42). 4 red-on-disable pins in `test_preprocessor.cpp` (`CommandLineDefineMakesIfdefIncludeLive` core, `…ViaDefinedOperatorIncludeLive` symmetry, `…SeedThreadsIntoChildBuilders` depth≥1, `…SeedDoesNotContaminateOutput`); independent design-audit GO-WITH-CHANGES (pivoted the value seed → definedness SeedTable). | (closed — feature delivered) | plan 25 / SQLite testfixture arc C19; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`SynthBuilder::seededDefines`/`sbNameDefined`, `definedCb`, `isDefinedTok`, child ctor, preprocess() seed build); sibling `D-PP-PRESCAN-PREDEFINED-VALUE-INCLUDE-GATE` (value-position residual); `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` (`CommandLineDefine*` pins) |
| `D-PP-PRESCAN-ANGLE-MACRO-SPLICE-AUTHORITATIVE-LIVENESS` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-20 (SQLite testfixture arc — errno / test_syscall).** The include-gating PRE-SCAN (`SynthBuilder`) is BLIND to a `#define` from a QUOTE-included header — each quote include is processed by a CHILD `SynthBuilder` whose `localMacros` is DISCARDED after `build()` returns (it is never merged up), so the parent never learns a macro defined there. Consequence: a `#if <that-macro>` VALUE guard (`#if SQLITE_OS_UNIX`) or a `#if defined(<that-macro>)` guard folds the macro to 0 **CONFIDENTLY** in the pre-scan (an undefined identifier → 0, C 6.10.1p4 — `pp_if_eval.cpp` "identifier that survived expansion → 0"; NOT flagged uncertain, which `sbEvalIfOperand` sets only on a nullopt/malformed eval), so the pre-scan mis-marks the branch DEAD even where the AUTHORITATIVE `MacroExpander` (which sees the quote-included header's spliced text) reads it LIVE. Previously the SynthBuilder's SHARED `includeResolvable()` gate then suppressed the ANGLE shipped-macro splice under such a guard → the shipped object-macro (`errno` → `(*__errno_location())`) was never injected → the errno-using code (KEPT by the authoritative pass) reached the parser as a bare identifier → `error[S0001]: got errno` — VALID C wrongly rejected (sqlite `src/test_syscall.c`: `#if SQLITE_OS_UNIX` [defined in the transitively-quote-included `os_setup.h`] gating `#include <errno.h>` AND `#include <sys/mman.h>`). **FIX (Option B / splice-always, angle arm only):** the ANGLE-macro-splice arm no longer consults `includeResolvable()` — it splices the shipped `#define`s unconditionally. This is span-safe (no `SbMacro` change) and correct because the injected `#define`s are emitted into the OUTPUT buffer (`out.append(defs)`) INSIDE the include's own conditional region, and the AUTHORITATIVE pass — which ELIDES dead-branch `#define`s (`handleDirective` early-returns on `!stackActive()`) — is the proper arbiter of their liveness. The QUOTE-include INLINE arm KEEPS its `includeResolvable()` gate (P0016 — it EAGERLY resolves a file, unlike the angle emit). The malformed-shipped-descriptor DIAGNOSTIC (`P_PreprocessorIncludeError` on an exists-but-fails-macro-decode descriptor) stays gated on `includeResolvable()` (threaded through `spliceSystemDescriptorMacros` as a `reportMalformed` flag) — the SPLICE is ungated but the diagnostic fires only on a confidently-LIVE include, restoring the pre-change dead-branch inertness of that error (C 6.10p1; the authoritative pass never reads the descriptor, so the pre-scan is its sole emitter — a code-audit Item-2 finding). **P0016 one-directional-divergence PRESERVED (adversarially verified):** the angle splice is EMIT-ONLY (into `out`), NEVER tracked into the pre-scan's `localMacros` (it does NOT call `sbTrackDefine`), so a dead-branch spliced define does NOT leak into the pre-scan's LATER `#if` verdicts — a dead-branch `errno.h` followed by `#if defined(errno)` gating a MISSING include compiles CLEAN (vs a DIRECT `#define errno` which makes the pre-scan resolve that guard LIVE → the missing-include error), proving no leak; AND the authoritative pass elides the injected define in a truly-dead branch (a `#if 0`-gated `errno.h` then `return errno` still fails S0001 — the final token stream is byte-identical). RESULT: test_syscall `errno` + `MAP_FAILED` both cleared (both were angle-macros suppressed by the same `#if SQLITE_OS_UNIX` gate); the residual `ENOMEM`/`EDEADLK` are missing `errno.json` CONSTANTS (a trivial descriptor follow-up, separate). Full preprocessor unit + example suites, ffi/shipped (88), and the sqlite3.c compile→run (exit 42) all unregressed. Witness `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_gated_shipped_macro` (a quote-include flag gates `#include <errno.h>`; EINVAL/EACCES read+written through the real `(*__errno_location())` → 42; RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the gate → the pre-scan skips the splice → `errno` undeclared → S0001 → no binary). Pins in `test_preprocessor.cpp` (POSITIVE: a quote-include-gated shipped OBJECT-macro injects+expands; NEGATIVE, final-output layer: a `#if 0`-gated shipped include yields NO usable macro in the final token stream — RED if the authoritative pass ever stops eliding). | (closed — feature delivered) | SQLite testfixture arc (errno/test_syscall); `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`SynthBuilder::build` angle-macro-splice arm — the removed `includeResolvable()` gate; the QUOTE arm's gate is retained); `src/analysis/preprocess/pp_if_eval.cpp` (identifier→0 confident fold); siblings `D-PP-DESCRIPTOR-MACRO-INJECT`, `D-PP-CONDITIONAL-INCLUDE-ORDERING`, `D-PP-PRESCAN-DEFINEDNESS-PARITY`, `D-PP-PRESCAN-PREDEFINED-VALUE-INCLUDE-GATE`; `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_gated_shipped_macro`; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |
| `D-PP-LINE-COMMENT-BEFORE-DIRECTIVE` | ✅ CLOSED 2026-06-26 (c22) — ROOT CAUSE: the `//` token's `stringStyle.endsAt:"\n"` CONSUMED the terminating newline as a comment-body token, so a `code // comment` line's only newline vanished → a following `#` directive lost its line boundary → `firstOnLine` judged it not-first-on-line → leaked to the parser (P0001). FIX: a new GENERIC `StringStyle.endsAtExclusive` (config-driven, agnostic) — the `//` mode ends AT but EXCLUDES the `\n`, which survives as a Newline token; handled at BOTH endsAt-consume sites (per-codepoint + coalesced). The c19 `firstOnLine` hypothesis was correctly DISPROVEN — the real gate was the TOKENIZER, not firstOnLine. Indep review+audit VERIFIED-CLEAN (adversarial: corpus exit 42 WITH the fix, directive-leak + NO binary WITHOUT); 445/445. (EOF line comment w/o trailing newline still fails loud P_UnterminatedComment — pre-existing, intended, C-conforming.) [orig NEW c18, refined c19] A `//` line comment AFTER code on a line, IMMEDIATELY followed by a `#` directive on the next line, makes the preprocessor FAIL to recognize the directive: it leaks to the parser → `D0004 <malformed include>` + `P0001 expected 'IncludeKeyword' got 'define'`, then a full cascade. Precise repro (CLI-confirmed): `int a; // c\n#define Z 1\nint b=Z;` → P0001. A `//` comment ALONE on its line before a directive (`// c\n#define Z 1`) WORKS; a trailing `//` comment ON the directive line (`#define Z 1 // c`) WORKS; a `/* */` comment before a directive WORKS — only `<code> // comment` then a directive breaks. NOT silent (loud parse error). Likely root (confirm at fix time): `MacroExpander::firstOnLine` + the `//`-comment token swallowing its trailing newline, so the `#` after `code // comment` scans back through the comment (trivia, not-newline) to the code `;` and is judged not-first-on-line → not stripped as a directive. PRE-EXISTING (FC14-era `firstOnLine`; NOT introduced by c17/c18 — neither touched directive recognition). NOT on the SQLite path (sqlite3.c uses `/* */`, so the c18 re-probe did not hit it — IncludeKeyword count 0). Priority: **normal** (rare pattern; loud, not silent; NOT on the SQLite path). ★ **c19 UPDATE (2026-06-26): the `firstOnLine` hypothesis was TESTED and DISPROVEN** — adding a LineComment line-boundary check to `MacroExpander::firstOnLine` did NOT fix it (a clean rebuild still failed `int a; // c\n#define Z 1`). The `#` IS first-on-line, so `firstOnLine` is not the gate. Variant probes narrowed it: `int a; // c\n#define` FAILS, but `int a;\n// c\n#define` (comment on its own line) and `int a;\n\n#define` (blank line) both WORK — so the trigger is specifically a `//` comment SHARING A LINE WITH CODE, before a directive. The real mechanism is a preprocess-tier `//`-comment interaction not yet root-caused (`ppLexemes` at the preprocess tier shows `//` lexed as `/`+chars, but the full CLI pipeline handles `//` elsewhere — so the dump is misleading; needs a parse-tier token dump of the failing vs passing case). DEEPER than a one-liner — its own focused investigation cycle. | Root-cause the `code // comment`-then-directive desync (dump the synth + parse token streams for `int a; // c\n#define Z 1` vs `int a;\n// c\n#define Z 1`); fix at the real gate. Pin: `int a; // c\n#define Z 1\nint b=Z;` compiles clean AND `b` expands to `1`. | plan 25 c18/c19; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` + the `//`-comment tokenizer mode |
| `D-CSUBSET-ADJACENT-STRING-CONCAT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-26 (p18 Cluster G c20, 441/441).** Adjacent string-literal concatenation (C 5.1.1.2 phase 6) implemented. Grammar `stringLiteralExpr` repeats the `StringStart StringLiteral` pair; a SINGLE chokepoint `decodeAdjacentStringBodies` (new `src/core/types/string_literal_decode.hpp`) decodes EACH body separately then byte-joins (phase-5-before-6 — `"\x41" "1"`→"A1", NOT a raw merge `\x411`); fail-loud `nullopt` on a bad escape. ★ The MULTI-SITE contract was honored: the EXACT consumer set is 4 (independently re-grep-confirmed, no 5th) — A `lowerStringLiteral` (cst_to_hir.cpp:1764, routed), B attribute-arg (fail-loud `H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier` on a 2nd opener, not silent-truncate), C `extractLibraryOverride` (routed), D the SEMANTIC typer (semantic_analyzer.cpp:2936 — restructured from per-body-TOKEN typing to typing the stringLiteralExpr RULE node via the SAME chokepoint, so `subtreeType` short-circuits on the rule node's `Array<Char,N+1>`; per-token fallback gated `!parentIsStringLiteralExprRule` keeps toy/tsql green). HIR + semantic provably agree on N (same chokepoint). Pins: parser (2/4/6 flat children), semantic (`Array<Char,12>` for `"hello" " world"` + `typedBodyTokens==0`), HIR (2-way/3-way/escape-boundary), corpus `adjacent_string_{concat,escape_boundary,macro}` (4 targets + release arm; escape-boundary runtime-confirmed "A1"). Plan-locked (architect blueprint) + independent review VERIFIED-CLEAN + independent audit VERIFIED-CLEAN. Single-string byte-identical (repeat fires 0×). — ORIGINAL: NEW (2026-06-26, p18 Cluster G c19 — a sqlite3.c re-probe blocker). Adjacent string literals (C 5.1.1.2 phase 6: `"a" "b"` ≡ `"ab"`) are NOT concatenated. The grammar `stringLiteralExpr` (`{ "sequence": ["StringStart","StringLiteral"] }`) matches exactly ONE string; a second adjacent string fails loud (`P0002 expected ')' — got '"'`, ×50 in sqlite3.c at line ~129544). NOT silent. ★ MULTI-SITE CONTRACT: the fix is grammar (repeat the `StringStart StringLiteral` pair) PLUS concatenating ALL body children in EVERY consumer of a string-literal body — `lowerStringLiteral` (cst_to_hir.cpp:1743, the primary path), the attribute-arg path (cst_to_hir.cpp:~776), `bodyLiteralNodeOf` + the extern asm-label path (~5914), and `hir_text.cpp` (~1137/1185). A consumer that reads only the FIRST body silently drops the rest → silent miscompile. Must funnel through ONE concat chokepoint OR fix+test every consumer (incl. forms no shipped lang hits yet). Priority: **high** (re-probe blocker). | Grammar: `stringLiteralExpr` → `StringStart StringLiteral { repeat StringStart StringLiteral }`. Lowering: a shared `decodeAdjacentStringBodies(node)` chokepoint that decodes+concatenates all `StringLiteral` children; route every body consumer through it. Tests: `"a" "b"` → "ab" (primary), in an attribute arg, in an asm label; + a runnable corpus (exit reflects the concatenated bytes). Pin: `char s[]="a" "b"; return s[1];` → 'b'. | plan 25 c19; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`stringLiteralExpr`); `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`lowerStringLiteral` + all body consumers); `src/hir/hir_text.cpp` |
| `D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-QUALIFIER` | ✅ CLOSED 2026-06-26 (c21, model B) — shipped: per-symbol/member `SymbolRecord.isVolatile` → `HirVolatileMap` (access-node keyed) → `MirInstFlags::Volatile` on EVERY user load/store — objects, struct/union MEMBERS (incl. sqlite's `volatile int isInterrupted`), `int * volatile p` pointer-objects, whole-volatile aggregate copies, volatile params. Pointer-to-volatile-POINTEE fail-loud-deferred → `D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-POINTEE`. Indep plan-lock + review + audit CLEAN; §D negative pin genuine (disassembly-verified RED-on-disable); 444/444. [orig NEW c19, §B own cycle] — `volatile` is DELIBERATELY fail-loud-gated (`S_VolatileNotSupported` at every decl site; the struct-member position doesn't even parse it → `P0009`). sqlite3.c uses `volatile int isInterrupted;` (line 18852) → blocks the compile. ★ GOOD NEWS: the OPTIMIZER already fully respects a `MirInstFlags::Volatile` flag — DCE treats it as a side-effect root (dce.cpp:53), CSE/LICM/Mem2Reg all SKIP volatile loads/stores (cse.cpp:207, licm.cpp:283, mem2reg.cpp:255/284). So the backend is volatile-correct. The FRONTEND has NO volatile support: the type system doesn't track volatile-qualification, the flag is NEVER set, hence the gate. ★ CORRECTNESS-CRITICAL MULTI-SITE: supporting it = grammar (accept `volatile` everywhere incl. struct members; remove the `S_VolatileNotSupported` gates) + TYPE-QUALIFIER tracking + HIR→MIR threading to set `MirInstFlags::Volatile` on EVERY volatile access. A single missed access = a silent no-op = the optimizer eliminates a volatile read → silent miscompile (the exact bar violation). Funnel load/store lowering through ONE chokepoint that sets the flag from the lvalue's volatile-ness, or test every access form. NOT a no-op-accept (silent risk) and NOT keep-gated (blocks SQLite). Priority: **high** (re-probe blocker; own cycle — feature-dev + plan-lock + multi-site threading tests + a negative miscompile-pin: a volatile read the optimizer would fold iff the flag is missing). | Grammar accept + remove gates; thread volatile through the type system to the MIR `Volatile` flag at the load/store chokepoint; negative pin + per-form tests. Pin: `volatile int x; ... x; x;` keeps BOTH reads (not CSE'd/DCE'd). | plan 25 c19; `c-subset.lang.json` (gates + struct-member grammar); the type system; `src/hir/lowering` (load/store → `MirInstFlags::Volatile`); optimizer infra already done (`src/mir/mir_node.hpp:45`, `src/opt/passes/*`) |
| `D-LK-MACHO-BSS-ONLY-LINKEDIT-STRUCTURAL-PIN` | NEW (2026-06-26, p18 Cluster G c19). The bar wants ALSO a HOST-INDEPENDENT structural pin (red-on-disable on EVERY leg): a low-level test that emits a `__bss`-only exec via `macho::encodeExecDynamic` (build an `AssembledModule` with a single unused tentative-def global, no `__data`) and asserts `__LINKEDIT.fileoff != 0` (== the GOT-following offset). | `D-PP-IF-UNSIGNED-INTMAX` | FC14 named exclusion (2026-06-22): a `#if`/`#elif` controlling expression is evaluated in SIGNED int64 (the shared `applyBinaryInt` core), NOT C's `intmax_t`/`uintmax_t` (C 6.10.1p1). An unsigned literal whose value exceeds `INT64_MAX` (e.g. `#if 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFu == -1`, true under C unsigned semantics) FAILS LOUD (`asInt64` → "not an integer constant") instead of evaluating with unsigned wraparound. NOT silently wrong — fail-loud. Priority: **normal** (rare in real `#if` usage). | Carry an unsigned/`uintmax` arm through the `#if` Pratt + the `const_eval_arith` fold (the arith core already has uint64 — thread the literal's signedness from the C 6.4.4.1 suffix/value ladder, mirroring the semantic literal typer). Pin: `#if 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFu > 0` takes the branch. | plan 23 FC14; `src/analysis/preprocess/pp_if_eval.cpp` (the I32/int64 literal decode + fold) |
| `D-PP-PASTE-OBJECT-LIKE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (cycle d — FC15 paste residuals).** Object-like `##` now routes the object-like replacement through the SHARED `collapsePastes` chokepoint the function-like arm already uses (no duplicated paste logic); a genuine dangling `##` (`#define OBJ a ##`) still fails loud (`FC15ObjectLikeDanglingPasteFailsLoud`). Pin `FC15ObjectLikePasteYieldsOneToken`/`…Chain`/`…ProductRescanned` + corpus `preprocessor_paste_residuals` (MK3→add3, exit 42). Historical: FC15a named exclusion (2026-06-24): the `##` (token-paste) operator is implemented ONLY in FUNCTION-like macros (the `substitute` chokepoint, per the locked design). A `##` in an OBJECT-like macro replacement (`#define HW a ## b`, valid C 6.10.3.3 — paste applies to object- AND function-like) is NOT collapsed: the object-like expand path copies the replacement verbatim, so `a`, `##`, `b` pass through. NOT a silent miscompile — it FAILS LOUD at the parser (`##`=HashHashOp is not a valid expression operator → P0001), verified. Priority: **normal / trigger-gated** (rare — object-like paste; the common `CONCAT(a,b) a##b` is function-like and works). | Route the OBJECT-like replacement through `collapsePastes` too (object-like has no params/stringize, so only `##` between literal replacement tokens needs handling). Pin: `#define HW a ## b` + `HW` → the single token `ab`. | plan 23 FC15b; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (object-like arm of `expand`); `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |
| `D-PP-PASTE-PLACEMARKER` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (cycle d — FC15 paste residuals).** An EMPTY `##`-operand argument now becomes a PLACEMARKER (`bool placemarker` on `ExpToken`, stamped in Phase A by `stampArgOrPM`): `collapsePastes` resolves `pm ## X`→X, `X ## pm`→X, `pm ## pm`→pm, and drops survivors at its return (`run()`/`expandTokens` backstop). The genuine-dangling-`##` boundary check is UNCHANGED + fires first, so `J(x,)`→`x` while `#define B(a) a ##` still fails loud. Pins `FC15Placemarker{RightEmpty,LeftEmpty,BothEmpty,MidChain}` + `FC15PasteEmptyVaArgsIsPlacemarkerNotDangling`. Historical: FC15a named exclusion (2026-06-24): pasting with an EMPTY operand (C 6.10.3.3p2 placemarker: `a ## b` where `b`'s arg is empty yields `a`) is NOT supported. An empty `##` operand leaves the `##` dangling (no token on that side) → it is reported as a start/end paste error OR the `##` pastes against the wrong neighbor and re-tokenizes to >1 token → P_PreprocessorPaste. NOT a silent miscompile — fail-loud. Priority: **normal** (rare — empty macro args with `##`). | Model placemarker tokens: an empty `#`/`##` operand collapses to nothing, and `x ## <empty>` → `x` (and `<empty> ## <empty>` → nothing). Pin: `#define J(a,b) a ## b` + `J(x,)` → `x`. | plan 23 FC15b; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` `substitute`/`collapsePastes` |
| `D-PP-VARIADIC-GNU-COMMA-ELISION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (cycle d — FC15 paste residuals).** The GNU `,##__VA_ARGS__` idiom is now CONFIG-GATED (`preprocess.variadicCommaElision`, c-subset opts in): in Phase A's `isVaArgsName` arm, when the `##` immediately precedes `__VA_ARGS__` AND the prior `items` token is the config-declared arg-separator, an EMPTY `__VA_ARGS__` drops both the separator and the `##` (`LOG("x")`→`f("x")`), a NON-empty one keeps the separator and emits the pre-expanded args with no paste (`LOG("x",1)`→`f("x",1)`). Separator matched by `isArgSeparator` (config KIND), `__VA_ARGS__` by `isVaArgsName` — never a hardcoded `,`/name. AGNOSTIC: flag default-false → standard placemarker (separator survives). Pins `FC15GnuCommaElision{EmptyVaArgs,NonEmptyVaArgs,IsConfigDriven}` + corpus. Historical: FC15a named exclusion (2026-06-24): the GNU `,##__VA_ARGS__` comma-elision extension is NOT implemented. FC15a delivers the standard `#` (stringize, C 6.10.3.2) + `##` (token-paste, C 6.10.3.3) operators, but a `, ## __VA_ARGS__` paste where `__VA_ARGS__` is EMPTY does NOT drop the preceding comma (GNU/C++20 `__VA_OPT__`-adjacent behavior): the literal comma stays (`f(a,)`), and the `##` against an empty operand pastes the comma with nothing (a no-op paste → the lone comma survives, NOT a fail-loud — the comma `,` is a single valid token). NOT a silent miscompile (a stray trailing comma is a parse-visible artifact, not wrong code). Priority: **trigger-gated** (FC15b — a real corpus needing variadic logging `LOG(fmt, ...)` with zero trailing args, or `__VA_OPT__`). | Implement the GNU comma-elision special case: in `substitute`, when a `##` has a `__VA_ARGS__` right operand that expands to EMPTY and a `,` left operand, drop the comma (and the `##`) entirely. Consider also C23 `__VA_OPT__(...)`. Pin: `#define LOG(fmt, ...) f(fmt, ## __VA_ARGS__)` + `LOG("x")` → `f("x")` (no trailing comma); `LOG("x", 1)` → `f("x", 1)`. | plan 23 FC15b; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` `substitute`/`collapsePastes`; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |
| `D-PP-PRAGMA-OPERATOR-FORM` | FC15c named exclusion (2026-06-24, `#pragma` + `__has_include` + `__has_c_attribute`): the `_Pragma("...")` OPERATOR form (C 6.10.9) is NOT implemented. FC15c delivers the `#pragma` DIRECTIVE form (C 6.10.6 — consumed-and-dropped in `handleDirective`); `_Pragma` is a DISTINCT, EXPANSION-TIME operator (`_Pragma(string-literal)` destringizes its argument and processes it as if it were a `#pragma` line), so it can appear in a macro replacement and is resolved during macro expansion, not at directive scan. NOT a silent miscompile — a `_Pragma(...)` token sequence today expands as an ordinary identifier + call shape (or fails loud at the parser), never altering codegen. Priority: **normal / trigger-gated** (rare in hand-written C; common in library headers that wrap pragmas in macros). | Add a `_Pragma` operator (config `pragmaOperator` word, matched by TEXT like `defined`/`__has_include`): in the expander, recognize `_Pragma ( string-literal )`, destringize the literal (strip the `"`, un-escape `\"`/`\\`, C 6.10.9p1), and treat the result as a `#pragma` line — i.e. consume-and-drop it (DSS recognizes no pragma), so `_Pragma("once")` expands to nothing. Pin: `_Pragma("GCC optimize(\"O2\")")` in a replacement list expands to nothing (no error, no stray tokens). | plan 23 FC15c; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`expand`); `src/core/types/preprocess_config.hpp`; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |
| `D-PP-PRAGMA-RECOGNIZED-SEMANTICS` | FC15c named exclusion (2026-06-24, `#pragma`): specific `#pragma` EFFECTS beyond consume-ignore are NOT implemented. FC15c consumes-and-DROPS every `#pragma` line (C 6.10.6p2 explicitly licenses an implementation to ignore a pragma it does not recognize, so consume-drop is CONFORMING). The standard pragmas `#pragma STDC FP_CONTRACT`/`FENV_ACCESS`/`CX_LIMITED_RANGE` (C 6.10.6p2) and the ubiquitous `#pragma once` (header-guard idiom) currently have NO effect — `#pragma once` does not dedup a doubly-included header (the `#include` machinery's existing canonical-path cycle guard catches a CYCLE, but a non-cyclic double-include is re-spliced). NOT a silent miscompile — dropping a pragma is standard-permitted, and the FP/FENV pragmas only relax codegen latitude DSS does not currently take. Priority: **normal / trigger-gated** (closes when a real corpus needs `#pragma once` dedup or an `STDC` pragma's codegen effect). | Give the recognized pragmas effect: parse the pragma payload (after the consume scan), dispatch on a config-declared recognized-pragma set. `#pragma once` records the current file's canonical path in a once-set the `SynthBuilder` consults to SKIP a second splice; the `STDC` pragmas set a per-region float-eval/fenv flag the lowering honors. Pin: a header with `#pragma once` included twice is spliced ONCE; `#pragma STDC FP_CONTRACT OFF` disables FMA contraction in the following region. | plan 23 FC15c; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`SynthBuilder` splice + `handleDirective` pragma arm); `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |
| `D-PP-LINE-DIRECTIVE` | FC15b named exclusion (2026-06-24, predefined macros): the `#line N` / `#line N "file"` directive (C 6.10.5) is NOT implemented. FC15b delivers the PREDEFINED macros (`__LINE__`/`__FILE__`/`__STDC__`/`__STDC_VERSION__`/`__STDC_HOSTED__`/`__DATE__`/`__TIME__`, C 6.10.8) — `__LINE__`/`__FILE__` resolve against the INVOCATION offset through the line-map to the PHYSICAL line / origin file name. A `#line` directive would OVERRIDE that presumed line number + file name for the tokens that follow, but until it lands `__LINE__`/`__FILE__` always report the PHYSICAL source line/name. NOT a silent miscompile — a `#line` directive currently falls through to the generic `P_PreprocessorUnsupported` fail-loud arm in `handleDirective` (an unsupported directive, never silently ignored); and `__LINE__`/`__FILE__` reporting the physical line/name (when no `#line` is in play, the universal case) is CORRECT, so this is a fail-SOFT gap that only bites a TU that actually uses `#line` (rare in hand-written C; common in generated code / `yacc`/`lex` output). Priority: **normal / trigger-gated** (closes when a real corpus needs `#line` — e.g. a generated lexer, or `__FILE__`/`__LINE__` accuracy under a `#line`-rewritten preamble). | Add a `#line` directive handler (config `lineDirective` word, mirroring `defineDirective` &c.): parse `#line <digit-sequence> [ "<filename>" ]`, then maintain a PRESUMED-position override (a per-region (line-delta, file-name) shift) that `materializePredefined`'s Line/File arms consult AHEAD of the raw line-map resolve. The line-map already gives the physical origin; `#line` layers a logical override on top. Pin: `#line 100 "gen.c"` then `__LINE__` on the next physical line → 100 (not the physical line), and `__FILE__` → `"gen.c"`. | plan 23 FC15b; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`handleDirective`, `materializePredefined`); `src/core/types/preprocess_config.hpp`; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |
| `D-CSUBSET-INCDEC-CONST-LVALUE` | **OPEN — pre-existing gap, fail-SOFT, normal (Cluster F item F1, 2026-06-24).** A `const`-qualified lvalue used as the operand of `++`/`--` (`const int x; x++;`) is NOT rejected — `classifyLvalue` / `simpleLvalue` do not model `const` today, so an `x++` on a `const` object lowers as an ordinary modifiable-lvalue read-modify-write. NOT introduced by F1: the SAME gap exists on plain assignment (`const int x; x = 5;`) and compound-assign (`x += 1;`), all of which route through the same `const`-unaware lvalue classification. F1's ++/-- lvalue guard DOES reject a manifest RVALUE operand (`5++`/`++5` → `S_IncDecNeedsModifiableLvalue`), but a `const` *lvalue* is addressable, so the shape guard admits it. Not a silent miscompile of VALUE (the increment is arithmetically correct); it is a missing CONSTRAINT diagnostic (C 6.5.2.4p1 / 6.5.16p2 require a MODIFIABLE lvalue). Priority: **normal**. | Add a modifiable-lvalue / `const`-qualifier check SHARED with assignment + compound-assign (the single lvalue-classification chokepoint), emitting `S_ConstViolation` (or `S_IncDecNeedsModifiableLvalue` for the ++/-- arm) when the lvalue's type carries the language's `const` qualifier. Requires the type lattice to carry a const-qualified marker the classifier can read. Pin: `const int x = 0; x++;` and `const int y = 0; y = 1;` both fail loud. | plan 23 Cluster F (F1); `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`classifyLvalue`/`classifyIncDecLvalue`/`simpleLvalue` + the assignment path); `tests/hir/test_hir_lowering_c_subset.cpp` |
| `D-PP-VA-OPT` | FC15-paste-residuals deferral (2026-06-24, cycle d): the C23 `__VA_OPT__(content)` construct (C23 6.10.3.1) is NOT implemented. It is a DISTINCT, more general mechanism than the GNU `,##__VA_ARGS__` comma-elision THIS cycle delivered: `__VA_OPT__(content)` emits `content` (an arbitrary balanced-paren token sequence) iff `__VA_ARGS__` expands to a non-empty list, and elides the whole `__VA_OPT__(...)` otherwise — it requires recognizing `__VA_OPT__` + balanced-paren body extraction + conditional emission, none of which the placemarker/comma-elision machinery does. PRE-EXISTING gap (not introduced this cycle): a `__VA_OPT__` token in a replacement is left as an ORDINARY identifier (so a user writing it gets the literal `__VA_OPT__ ( … )` tokens through, a parse-visible wrong result, NOT a silent miscompile and NOT a fail-loud). Priority: **normal / trigger-gated** (closes when a real corpus / SQLite needs `__VA_OPT__`; the GNU `,##__VA_ARGS__` idiom — far more common in existing C — is already covered). | Add a config `vaOptOperator` word (matched by TEXT like `defined`/`__has_include`); in `substitute`, recognize `__VA_OPT__ ( <balanced> )`, and emit the `<balanced>` content (pre-expanded) iff `rawVaArgs` is non-empty, else nothing. Pin: `#define F(...) g(__VA_OPT__(0,) __VA_ARGS__)` + `F()` → `g()`, `F(1)` → `g(0, 1)`. | plan 23 FC15; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`substitute`); `src/core/types/preprocess_config.hpp`; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |
| `D-CSUBSET-FLOAT-NEG-ENCODING` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c78; §B USER folded into c78) — fneg realized capability-dispatched: arm64 native FNEG (0x1E614000/0x1E214000); x86 `xorpd/xorps xmm,[rip+16B-signmask]` (a minted 16-byte rodata sign-mask + the RipRelDisp32 slot). Objdump-confirmed 16-aligned masks on all 3 legs INCL. pe64 (4096 PE section + per-item 16-offset; the USER's PE-align concern refuted); signed-zero IEEE-correct (-（+0.0)=-0.0). Witness `examples/c-subset/float_negate`=42 (RED-ON-DISABLE → A_NoEncodingDeclared). Was a Cluster-F F3 deferral (2026-06-24, cycle 7): float unary minus `-x` AND a negative float LITERAL (`-42.0`) lower to the MIR `fneg` op, which has NO encoding declared — `A_NoEncodingDeclared` (x86) / `L_RequiredLirOpcodeMissing` (arm64). FAIL-LOUD (the function is dropped with a positioned diagnostic; NEVER a silent miscompile). NOT a simple mirror of fadd/fmul/fsub: x86 float negate is not a 2-reg ALU op — it is `XORPS xmm, signmask` (needs a `0x8000…0` sign-bit mask constant in rodata) OR `0.0 - x` (an FSUB from a materialized zero); arm64 FNEG IS a single 1-source FP instruction (`FNEG Dd, Dn` = 0x1E614000 / S-form 0x1E214000). Surfaced when the F3 `float_mul_sub` corpus used `-42.0` literals (the corpus was rewritten to avoid it via `(a-b)-(b-a)`). Priority: **normal / trigger-gated** (closes when a real corpus / SQLite needs float negation — common in real C). | x86: add an `fneg` encoding (an XORPS-with-rodata-signmask sequence, OR lower MIR FNeg → `0.0 - x` FSUB at MIR→LIR — the simpler, arguably-better long-term path since it reuses the now-encoded fsub). arm64: add an `fneg` opcode row (FNEG single-source, mirror the `fmov`/`fpcvt` 1-source fixed32 shape). Pin: `double f(double x){return -x;}` compiles + `-x` is runtime-correct on both targets; byte-pin the encoding. | plan 23 Cluster F; `src/dss-config/targets/{x86_64,arm64}.target.json`; possibly `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.cpp` (FNeg→FSub lowering) |
| `D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-20 (type-identity model fix) — type IDENTITY comes from the language VOCABULARY entry, representation from the target CORE; the two axes are INDEPENDENT and identity is NEVER derived from representation.** ROOT: the language config declared a vocabulary of NAMED entries (`int`, `long`, `long long`, `double`, `long double`) but `TypeInterner::primitive()` interned every primitive on its `TypeKind` ALONE, so any target axis that gave two DISTINCT named C types the same core COLLAPSED them to ONE TypeId. Confirmed instances: `_Generic((x), float:, double:, long double:)` → S002B ambiguous on pe64 + macho-arm64 (f64 axis) but clean on elf64 (x87) — same source, different answer by TARGET FORMAT; `_Generic(int:, long:)` → S002B on LLP64; `_Generic(long:, long long:)` → S002B on LP64 (UNIVERSAL, not target-gated); and ★ `int *p; long *q = p;` on LLP64 was **SILENTLY ACCEPTED** — the collapse was not merely fail-loud, it accepted invalid C with no constraint diagnostic. FIX (general — integers AND floats, one mechanism): the interner's EXISTING `name` content slot (already the nominal-identity key for Struct/Union/Enum/Extension) is populated for primitives via a new `primitive(TypeKind, std::string_view vocabularyName)` overload; the 1-arg overload delegates with `{}` so ~200 existing call sites are untouched and every anonymous primitive interns bit-identically (the empty name short-circuits to the literal `TypeNameId{}` sentinel — `Interner::intern("")` would mint a REAL entry and break dedup). `kind()` is UNCHANGED, so every representation consumer (rank tables, layout, ABI, codegen) is unaffected BY CONSTRUCTION. Config: optional `name` + `rank` on `typeSpecifiers` rows (declared ONLY on the 4 `long` / 2 `unsigned long` / 4 `long long` / 2 `unsigned long long` / 2 `long double` rows — `int`/`short`/`float`/`double`/`bool`/`void`/plain `char` stay UNNAMED so they remain the ANONYMOUS representative of their core, which integer promotion and enum-underlying synthesis independently re-mint); loader rejects an empty name, rejects `rank` without `name`, and cross-checks that all rows sharing a name resolve to byte-identical core/coreByDataModel/coreByLongDoubleFormat/rank (NOT `complex` — plain and `_Complex` `long double` legitimately share a name); `BuiltinTypeMapping`'s tag is loader-DERIVED from the matching single-token row so the text-keyed and keyword-multiset tables can never disagree. RANK: C 6.3.1.1 defines conversion rank by type NAME, not width — an explicit config `rank` breaks the tie in `commonType` / `usualArithmeticCommonType` / `unify` when two operands share a core but not an identity, and the winner's vocabulary name is PRESERVED instead of re-synthesizing an anonymous primitive. ★ THE THREE SILENT BREAKS, each fixed: (i) `coerce()` (cst_to_hir.cpp) would have emitted `Cast(I32→I32)` → a REAL `MirOpcode::Bitcast` for `long l = someInt;` under LLP64 — now a zero-node RETAG gated on `sameRepresentation` AND equal qualifier bits (a qualifier-changing coerce keeps the historic Cast so a `volatile`/`_Atomic` deref never demotes to a plain Load); (ii) `type_reintern.cpp` dropped the tag on EVERY primitive reintern, silently re-collapsing `long` onto `int` across CU / static-link-merge / text-round-trip boundaries — now carries `src.name(srcId)`; (iii) `shippedLibs/stdbit.json`'s 14 generic `stdc_<op>` macros DELIBERATELY DROPPED `unsigned long` from their `_Generic` (an in-file comment blamed this exact bug) — now full C23 5-way, the `unsigned long` arm routing through the already-shipped per-format `stdc_<op>_ul` variant. WITNESS: 20 red-on-disable pins (`test_type_identity_vocabulary.cpp`) covering every confirmed instance on both data models + both long-double axes, same-name-still-dedups, anonymous-primitives-unaffected, the 4 both-direction implicit conversions, the pointer-incompatibility TIGHTENING, loader validation, reintern survival, a 2-CU merge, and a STRUCTURAL codegen-unchanged proof (`long l = someInt;` on LLP64 emits ZERO Cast/Bitcast); plus the rewritten 5-way stdbit pins. 675→676 ctest, 100% green. ★ **FOLLOW-UP PASS (2026-07-20, same branch — four REAL regressions the first pass left, found by adversarial review; all four were GREEN-but-broken because no test covered the combination).** ROOT CAUSE OF ALL FOUR: the shipped/synthesized vocabulary still spoke REPRESENTATION ONLY, so `size_t`, `uint64_t`, `sizeof`'s result and most FFI descriptor params minted ANONYMOUS 64-bit primitives — a THIRD thing matching NEITHER `unsigned long` NOR `unsigned long long`, where C says these ARE those named types. UNIFYING FIX: wherever the config/analyzer produces a type C defines as an ALIAS of a standard named entry, it now resolves to that NAMED entry per data model, declared in config, never branched on a spelling. (1) ENGINE-SYNTHESIZED types — new `semantics.synthesizedTypes` maps a CLOSED engine ROLE (`sizeof`/`alignof`/`pointerDifference`) to a per-data-model `typeSpecifiers` vocabulary NAME, resolved at LOAD through the same `DataModelTypeRef` machinery `integerLiteralTyping` uses; the 9 hardcoded `primitive(U64)`/`primitive(I64)` sites now mint through ONE `synthesizedType` seam per tier (an undeclared role keeps the historic anonymous core, so toy/tsql are byte-identical; a DECLARED role must cover EVERY data model or the load fails). (2) SHIPPED DESCRIPTORS — `when` gained a THIRD target axis `dataModel` alongside {arch,format} (validated against the closed vocabulary), so `size_t`/`ptrdiff_t`/`time_t` and the ten 64-bit `<stdint.h>` aliases select `u64 "unsigned long"` on LP64 and `u64 "unsigned long long"` on LLP64 — one general mechanism serving typedefs, structs, constants and symbol versions rather than a typedef-only key. (3) FFI DESCRIPTOR SWEEP — windows.json's LONG/ULONG/DWORD/LPDWORD/SIZE_T/ULONG_PTR/LONGLONG/ULONGLONG/ULONG64 typedefs, every LPDWORD out-param, and every DWORD/LONG/ULONG_PTR struct FIELD (kept in lockstep with the embedded `struct "N" {…}` typedef texts, which must intern to the SAME TypeId); the glibc/Darwin `long`-bearing POSIX struct fields (sys/stat, time, sys/time, dirent, fcntl); `ssize_t` + a per-format `off_t`; Darwin `tss_t`; and `_InterlockedCompareExchange` via a new `signatureByDataModel` on `builtinFunctions` (the shipped-lib key name + anti-lurking eager decode). (4) CODEGEN-NEUTRALITY — the FLOAT branch of `usualArithmeticCommonType` (and `TypeInterner::commonType`) returned the winning operand VERBATIM on a rank tie, so a `volatile`/`_Atomic` skin rode into the common type and, because a qualifier CHANGE is the one thing the same-representation re-tag refuses, `double r = d + vld;` on an f64 axis emitted a NEW Bitcast; both now route through the integer branch's `pick()` rebuild (C 6.3.2.1p2 — the usual arithmetic conversions yield the UNQUALIFIED type). Plus: `HirBuilder::retagType` now CHECKS its own precondition (same representation + same qualifier mask + arithmetic-core + unparented, fail-loud `abort` like `addParent`'s double-attach guard) instead of trusting a caller comment, and the MIR structural pin's circular part (c) — a MIR-text compare, which cannot witness identity because the text codec drops the vocabulary tag on emit — was replaced by a direct TypeId assertion. WITNESS: +16 pins, every one verified RED with its fix reverted; 676/676 ctest green; the five original repros re-verified through the CLI on pe64 / macho64-arm64 / elf64. ★★ **ROUND 3 (2026-07-20, same branch — TWO MORE REAL REGRESSIONS the round-2 sweep INTRODUCED, both invisible to 676/676 green, plus the convergence lever that ends the class.** (R1) `struct timeval` interned as TWO TypeIds: the sweep retagged sys/time.json's `tv_sec`/`tv_usec` to `i64 "long"` but left its cross-descriptor twin sys/resource.json (the `timeval` `structs` entry AND the two INLINE `struct "timeval" {…}` texts inside `rusage.ru_utime`/`ru_stime`) on bare `i64`. Tag injection is FIRST-WINS BY NAME and only the winner gets a `compositeScopeByType` field scope, so `#include <sys/time.h>` BEFORE `<sys/resource.h>` made `r.ru_utime.tv_sec` fail `S000D` on elf64 AND macho64-arm64 while the reverse order compiled — an INCLUDE-ORDER-DEPENDENT compile failure, and a direct violation of the invariant sys/resource.json's own `$comment` states verbatim. FIXED per format (macho's `tv_usec` stays `i32`). (R2) `ssize_t` was a PHANTOM on pe: a FLAT `i64 "long"` on a descriptor that ships on `[elf,macho,pe]`, and LLP64 mints `long` as I32, so the pair (I64,"long") is UNPRODUCIBLE — `_Generic(aSsizeT, long long:, long:)` took `default` and BOTH `long long *p = q;` and `long *p = q;` emitted `S0003`. That was a REGRESSION (pre-sweep it was an anonymous `i64`, which on LLP64 IS `long long`, the correct C answer — MSVC `SSIZE_T` is `__int64`); fixed with per-format arms like its `off_t` sibling. ★ THE CONVERGENCE LEVER — the class is now MACHINE-CHECKED, not re-asserted in a comment: new `ffi::ShippedTypeConsistency` (src/ffi/shipped_type_consistency.{hpp,cpp}) accumulates every NAMED type REACHABLE from every descriptor a CU resolves for one target and fails LOUD (`F_ShippedTypeIdentityConflict`, unsuppressable) on (A) two declarations of one struct/union TAG or one typedef NAME interning to different TypeIds — covering the `structs`, INLINE-text and cross-file forms through ONE walk — and (B) a vocabulary tag whose width the ACTIVE LANGUAGE cannot produce under the active data model. Wired into semantic_analyzer.cpp right before descriptor injection (so the ROOT CAUSE is reported instead of the downstream member-access failure), with the language vocabulary passed in as opaque (name→core) rows — no spelling or data-model branch in the checker. Backed by an EXHAUSTIVE test (tests/ffi/test_shipped_type_consistency.cpp) that ENUMERATES every `*.json` under shippedLibs/** and every target axis declared by object-formats/*.format.json × targets/*.target.json — catching pairs no corpus program co-includes yet. Both were verified RED on the reverted R1 and R2 states. Also this round: `TypeInterner::commonType`'s `a==b` short-circuit no longer returns the operand VERBATIM for arithmetic kinds (C 6.3.2.1p2 — `commonType(volatile double, volatile double)` was `volatile double`); the `_Complex` branch of `usualArithmeticCommonType`, which runs BEFORE the float branch and so kept BOTH defects the float branch had fixed, now routes its ELEMENT through the shared `pickFrom` rebuild (skin dropped, equal-core `long double` tie broken by declared rank); `builtinFunctions` gained a CLOSED key vocabulary and rejects `signatureByDataModel` alongside `params`/`result` (it was read only inside the `signature` branch, so on the scalar form it loaded clean and SILENTLY DID NOTHING). WITNESS: +21 pins — the exhaustive descriptor sweep, `retagType`'s five abort clauses (death tests), the hir_text vocabulary-tag emit→parse→emit stability pin (the EMIT side was entirely unexercised), literal-suffix identity (`1L`/`1LL`/`1UL`/`1ULL`/`1.0L` on both models and all three long-double axes), the four `signatureByDataModel` loader rejections plus the eager anti-lurking decode, and the `_Complex`/`commonType` qualifier pins. ★ Five pins that were WEAK were rewritten to be red-on-disable rather than no-error-only: the MIR `VolatileLongDoubleArithmeticEmitsNoExtraCast` pin was VACUOUS (`d + vld` put the PLAIN operand on the LEFT, and the pre-fix branch returned the left operand on an equal float rank, so it PASSED with the fix reverted — verified) and now uses `vld + d`; `TypeRules...OnTheFloatBranch`'s unnamed control gained the reversed order; `Fc3Stdbit.GenericUnsignedLongRoutesByDataModel` now observes the winner via `SemanticModel::selectedGenericExpr` instead of `kindOf(r)` (which reported the CONTROLLING type — [[D-CSUBSET-GENERIC-RESULT-TYPE-DEDUCTION]]); and the three shipped-descriptor pins (`ShippedStdbitGenericAcceptsUint64AndSizeof`, `InterlockedCompareExchangeTakesALongPointer`, `WindowsDwordPointerIsUnsignedLongPointer`, `SameRepresentationConversionsStayClean`) now assert the actual selected arm / parameter identity / TypeId distinctness plus a NEGATIVE control, each verified RED with its descriptor tag removed. The `shipped_getrusage` corpus now includes BOTH headers in the order that used to break and cross-assigns `struct timeval tv = r.ru_utime`, making it a runtime witness. | (closed — the vocabulary tag on the existing `name` slot IS the fix) | supersedes the `long`≡`int` LLP64 identity-collapse precedent cited across [[D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-GENERIC-DISTINCT]] / [[D-FULLC-STDBIT]]; `src/core/types/type_lattice/type_interner.hpp` + `type_lattice.cpp` + `type_reintern.cpp`; `src/core/types/semantic_config.hpp` + `grammar_schema_json.cpp` + `integer_literal_ladder.hpp`; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` + `type_rules.hpp`; `src/hir/hir.hpp` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp`; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` + `shippedLibs/stdbit.json`; defers [[D-LANG-UAC-UNSIGNED-COUNTERPART-OF-SIGNED]]; plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-LANG-UAC-UNSIGNED-COUNTERPART-OF-SIGNED` | ⏳ **DEFERRED (surfaced by [[D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY]], 2026-07-20; NOT a miscompile — a NAME-only divergence at identical representation).** C 6.3.1.8's FOURTH mixed-signedness branch ("otherwise, both operands are converted to the unsigned integer type CORRESPONDING TO the type of the operand with signed integer type") is unreachable in DSS: `usualArithmeticCommonType` picks the winning KIND from the width-rank lattice, so `aLong + anUnsignedInt` under LLP64 (I32 vs U32, but rank(long) > rank(unsigned int)) yields `unsigned int` where C says `unsigned long`. Both are U32 — the VALUE, width, and codegen are identical; only the resulting type's NAME differs, observable solely through `_Generic` on such a sum. The type-identity fix scoped it out deliberately: its tie-break fires only when the two operands share a KIND, and "when kinds differ, existing behavior is unchanged" was the stated boundary (pre-fix, `long`≡`int` and `unsigned long`≡`unsigned int` made the two answers the SAME TypeId, so the gap was invisible). NOT on the SQLite path. **Trigger:** a consumer whose `_Generic` (or `typeof`) dispatches on a mixed-signedness sum of a named and an anonymous integer entry at the same width. **Closing work:** declare each signed vocabulary entry's UNSIGNED COUNTERPART in the language config (a `unsignedCounterpart` key on the `typeSpecifiers` row, resolved at load like every other name), then implement branch 4 in `usualArithmeticCommonType`'s `RankPreferUnsigned` arm: when the unsigned operand's declared rank is LOWER than the signed operand's and the signed type cannot represent the unsigned range, return the signed entry's counterpart. Priority: **low**. Pin (BOTH data models — the gap is not LLP64-specific, and citing only one example understated it): (i) LLP64 — `_Generic((aLong + anUnsignedInt), unsigned long: 1, default: 0)` → 1; (ii) ★ LP64 — `_Generic((aLongLong + anUnsignedLong), unsigned long long: 1)` currently FAILS TO COMPILE (`S002A` controlling type matches no association, CLI-confirmed on `x86_64:elf64-x86_64-linux-exec`): `long long` and `unsigned long` are both 64-bit on LP64, so the width lattice picks the unsigned operand's own entry and yields `unsigned long`, where C 6.3.1.8 branch 4 requires the UNSIGNED COUNTERPART of the higher-ranked SIGNED type, i.e. `unsigned long long` (a 64-bit `long long` cannot represent every `unsigned long`). Same VALUE and width either way — only the resulting type's NAME differs. | (open) | [[D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY]] (the fix that made this observable); `src/analysis/semantic/type_rules.hpp` (`usualArithmeticCommonType`); plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-GENERIC-RESULT-TYPE-DEDUCTION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-20 (shared generic-selection chokepoint) — a generic selection now types from its SELECTED association in BOTH tiers (C23 6.5.1.1p3).** AS-BUILT: the association SELECTION (controlling-type resolve + typed/`default` matching → the winning result-expr) is extracted into ONE NET-diagnostic-free chokepoint `selectGenericAssociation(EngineState const&, SemanticConfig const&, Tree const&, NodeId, ScopeId, TypeId controllingType) -> GenericSelection{selected, matchCount, defaultExpr, anyBadType, controllingResolved, typedAssocTypeNodes}` — it takes the ALREADY-COMPUTED controlling type (never re-types it — see F1 below) and resolves each association type-name to match, then rolls the reporter back (`snapshotForRollback`/`truncateTo`, see F2) so those resolves are net-silent; the resolved TypeIds + benign interner/`nodeToType` side effects (e.g. a valid `_BitInt` mint) PERSIST (it is net-diagnostic-free, NOT strictly stamp-free), and it RETURNS the winner + the typed-association type-nodes for pass2Post to re-resolve LOUD + stamp. `subtreeType` (Pass 1.5 — `auto` inference / array-dim `sizeof`) gained a `_Generic` case (BEFORE the transparent-wrapper fallback) that types the selection on subtreeType's OWN work-stack via a multi-phase Generic frame (phase 0 enters the controlling child, phase 1 picks the winner from that type via the helper + enters it, phase 2 yields the winner's type) — BOTH a controlling-nested AND a winner-nested `_Generic` stay on the stack, ZERO host recursion — so Pass 1.5 no longer takes the node's FIRST visible child = the CONTROLLING expression. `pass2Post` (Pass 2) calls the SAME helper and keeps EXACTLY its prior side effects ONLY THERE — the ambiguous/no-match diagnostics (from `matchCount`/`controllingResolved`/`anyBadType`), the `nodeToType`/`nodeToSelectedExpr` node stamps, the assoc-`typeNode` stamps, and the loud S_UnknownType for a value-in-type-position (the bad association re-resolved with the default emitOnMiss, so Pass 1.5 never double-reports). The bug was SILENT because pass2Post ALREADY stamped the node with the winner's type — only Pass 1.5, running BEFORE that stamp existed, fell through to the controlling type. WITNESS: 7 pins `Fc3GenericResultType.*` (test_fc3_width_semantics.cpp) — the `sizeof`-fold mechanism-proof (winner `char`=1 not controlling `long`=8), `auto`-binds-winner, winner-≠-controlling-and-≠-any-arm-name (F64), nested `_Generic`-in-controlling (the work-stack path), the `_Generic`-result-feeds-`sizeof`-in-outer-controlling shape, + 2 diagnostic-refactor guards; verified RED with the subtreeType case reverted (5 RED, the 2 diagnostic guards stay GREEN); plus an end-to-end CLI witness `examples/c-subset/generic_result_type` (exit 42 on every 64-bit target, empirically RED=48 on LLP64 with the case reverted). 678/678 ctest green; the five type-identity repros + `Fc3Stdbit.*` hold. ★ FOLLOW-UP (adversarial review — 2 real edge regressions from the new subtreeType↔helper mutual recursion, both fixed, neither a miscompile): (F1) the helper originally host-called `subtreeType(controlling)`, so a controlling-nested `_Generic` HOST-RECURSED (regressing D-PARSE-DEEP-NEST-RECURSION-MEMORY) — now the helper takes the controlling type as a PARAMETER (never calls subtreeType) and subtreeType types the selection on its own work-stack via the multi-phase Generic frame above; pass2Post types the controlling expr once at top level and passes it in. A controlling-nest CRASH pin is PARSER-co-limited in the unit harness: the recursive-descent parser (buildShippedUnit, 1 MiB main-thread stack — the CLI builds CUs on a 64 MiB worker, D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK) overflows controlling-nested `_Generic` at ~250 levels (no-paren), and the reverted host-recursive frame was MEASURED to survive to that SAME ~250 floor (it does NOT crash below the parser) — so the two forms are co-limited and no crash window exists there; the axis is guarded by a depth-100 CORRECTNESS pin `ControllingNestedGenericDeepStaysFlat`, the fix being a structural O(1)-host-stack guarantee verified by the frame shape. (F2) association type-names resolve through resolveTypeNode, whose `_BitInt`/typeof-bitfield constraint diagnostics fire UNCONDITIONALLY and BYPASS the reporter dedup window, so the helper's resolve + pass2Post's stamp-loop re-resolve (+ Pass 1.5's auto call) DOUBLE/TRIPLE-emitted them — the helper now wraps its resolves in a reporter `snapshotForRollback`/`truncateTo` so they are FULLY silent (the resolved TypeIds + benign interner side effects persist), leaving pass2Post's stamp-loop the SOLE emitter. Pins `BitIntAssocDiagnosesExactlyOnce{NonAuto,Auto}` (RED-on-disable: 2/3 emits without the rollback) + `Auto{NoMatch,Ambiguous}DiagnosesExactlyOnce` + `WinnerNestedGenericTypesAsInnerWinner` (the winner-nested axis: the SELECTED arm is itself a `_Generic`, typed via phase 2 on the same work-stack; red-on-disable through the same frame). ★ ALSO F4 (adversarial review, PRE-EXISTING, SAME CLASS as F2 but OUTSIDE `_Generic`): subtreeType's CAST and COMPOUND-LITERAL arms resolved a `_BitInt(0)` / typeof-bitfield type-name in Pass 1.5 (emitOnMiss=false) while Pass 2 re-resolved, and the unsuppressable codes bypass dedup → S_BitIntWidthNotPositive emitted TWICE (`sizeof((_BitInt(0))x)`, `(_BitInt(0)){0}`) — FIXED by wrapping those two Pass-1.5 resolves in the SAME `snapshotForRollback`/`truncateTo` rollback (the Pass-2 arm re-resolves loud as the SOLE emitter; a failed resolve returns InvalidType WITHOUT stamping the node, so the re-resolve runs fully — safety-checked). Pins `Fc3ReTypingBitInt.{Cast,CompoundLiteral}BitIntZeroDiagnosesExactlyOnce` (RED-on-disable: 2 without the rollback). The two stale `resolveTypeNodeImpl` comments that claimed the dedup window collapses the Pass-1.5+Pass-2 double-visit (FALSE for unsuppressable codes) are corrected. 13 `Fc3GenericResultType.*` + 2 `Fc3ReTypingBitInt.*`; 678/678 ctest green. HISTORICAL (found while independently verifying [[D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY]], 2026-07-20; PRE-EXISTING and unrelated to that fix — a SILENT WRONG-TYPE bug): C23 6.5.1.1p3: a generic selection's type and value are those of its SELECTED association. DSS instead types the whole `_Generic` expression from the CONTROLLING expression. RUNTIME-CONFIRMED on elf64-x86_64 (LP64): `long x; auto r = _Generic((x), long: (char)1, default: (double)2); return (int)sizeof(r);` exits **8** (the controlling `long`) where C requires **1** (the selected `char`). The VALUE is correct, so it is invisible to any test that only checks the value — but everything TYPE-directed downstream is wrong: `sizeof`, the storage width of an `auto`/`typeof` binding, a nested `_Generic`/`typeof` dispatch on the result, and arithmetic promotion. It also means the pre-existing `Fc3Stdbit.GenericUnsignedLongRoutesByDataModel` pin was weaker than it read (it observed the controlling type, not the winner); the type-identity cycle re-pointed its own pins at `SemanticModel::selectedGenericExpr`, which observes the winner directly. **Trigger:** any consumer binding a `_Generic` result to `auto`/`typeof`, or nesting a `_Generic` in another's controlling expression. **Closing work:** type the generic-selection node from the selected association's expression (the analyzer ALREADY computes the winner — `selectedGenericExpr`) rather than from the controlling conversion; pin with the `sizeof` witness above plus a nested-`_Generic` case. Priority: **medium** (silent + runtime-observable, but needs `auto`/`typeof`/`sizeof` to manifest). | (closed — shared `selectGenericAssociation` chokepoint + the `subtreeType` `_Generic` case) | found by [[D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`selectGenericAssociation` chokepoint + the `subtreeType` `_Generic` case + pass2Post's arm); `tests/analysis/semantic/test_fc3_width_semantics.cpp` (`Fc3GenericResultType.*`); `examples/c-subset/generic_result_type`; plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VOCABULARY-TAG-RESIDUALS` | ⏳ **DEFERRED (opened by the [[D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY]] follow-up sweep, 2026-07-20).** The sweep tagged every shipped descriptor entry whose C type is UNAMBIGUOUSLY a named vocabulary entry on the formats that descriptor ships on. These were DELIBERATELY LEFT ANONYMOUS, each for a stated reason, and each is a *name-only* gap (the width/ABI is unaffected; it bites only on a POINTER to the entry or a `_Generic` over it): (a) `sys/types.json`'s `dev_t`/`ino_t`/`nlink_t`/`blksize_t`/`blkcnt_t`/`suseconds_t` — glibc spells them `unsigned long`/`long`, but the shipped u64/i64 already mis-models the Darwin widths (`dev_t` is `__int32_t`, `nlink_t` is `__uint16_t`, …), so a name-only tag would add a SECOND lie on top of a width lie; closing them means fixing the macho representations first. (b) `time.json`'s `struct timespec` — `tv_sec` is `long` on glibc/Darwin but `__int64` (`long long`) on the MSVC CRT, and `tv_nsec` is a 32-bit `long` there while the descriptor models both as i64; the struct is FLAT (all formats), so tagging needs the same per-format width fix. (c) `pthread.json`'s `pthread_t` and `threads.json`'s `thrd_t` — glibc `unsigned long`, Darwin an opaque POINTER modelled as u64. (d) `stdlib.json`'s `atol`/`strtol` (result), `strtoul` (result) and `labs` (arg + result) — the C `long`/`unsigned long` type, declared per-data-model via `signatureByDataModel` so the WIDTH is right on both models, but both arms are still ANONYMOUS (`i64`/`i32`, `u64`/`u32`); a caller doing `long *p = &r; ... strtol(...)` into a `long` is unaffected (by-value converts), but `_Generic((strtol(s,e,10)), long: ...)` takes no arm. (e) `sys/resource.json`'s 14 `ru_*` counters (`ru_maxrss` … `ru_nivcsw`) — glibc/Darwin spell every one `long int`, all still bare `i64`; the embedded `timeval` fields WERE tagged (c-round-3, the include-order fix), so this descriptor is now internally mixed — harmless (the counters are read by value) but worth closing together. (f) `time.json`'s `struct timespec` (`tv_sec`/`tv_nsec`, both bare `i64`) — same blocker as the `<time.h>` row above: the struct is FLAT across all three formats while the MSVC CRT spells `tv_sec` as `__int64` (`long long`) and `tv_nsec` as a 32-bit `long`, so tagging needs the per-format width fix FIRST. (g) `sys/types.json`'s `off_t` **pe** arm — the elf (`i64 "long"`) and macho (`i64 "long long"`) arms are tagged but the pe arm is a bare `i64`; MSVC's `_off_t` is a 32-bit `long` and `_off64_t` is `__int64` (`long long`), so the pe arm needs a WIDTH decision (which of the two `<sys/types.h>` spellings DSS models) before it can be tagged — unlike its `ssize_t` sibling, whose LLP64 answer is unambiguously `long long`. (h) windows.json's BY-VALUE `DWORD`/`LONG` scalar parameters and returns (~60 signatures) — left anonymous because a by-value position converts implicitly in BOTH directions (so the tag is cosmetic there) while a mis-tag from a guessed SDK type would be a real false error; the POINTER and STRUCT-FIELD positions, which are strict TypeId identity, were all tagged. **Trigger:** a consumer that takes the ADDRESS of one of these, assigns it to the correspondingly-spelled pointer, or `_Generic`s over it. **Closing work:** per group above — (a)/(b)/(f)/(g) need the per-format representation (or width) decision FIRST, then a `when:{format}`/`when:{dataModel}` tag; (c) needs a decision on modelling Darwin's opaque handle; (d)/(e) are mechanical tag sweeps (the widths are already right, only the identity name is missing); (h) is a mechanical sweep once each parameter's SDK type is verified rather than inferred. ★ Every one of these is now MACHINE-GUARDED against becoming INCONSISTENT while it stays open: `ffi::ShippedTypeConsistency` (compile-time, `F_ShippedTypeIdentityConflict`) plus the exhaustive `tests/ffi/test_shipped_type_consistency.cpp` sweep reject any two declarations of one tag/typedef that disagree for a target, and any tag whose width the active data model cannot produce — so a PARTIAL tag sweep can no longer silently split a type in two. Priority: **low** (name-only, no width/ABI/codegen effect). | (open) | opened by [[D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY]]; `src/dss-config/shippedLibs/` (sys/types.json, time.json, pthread.json, threads.json, windows.json); plan 11 FFI / plan 25 per-target structs |
| `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-COMPAT-ADDRESSOF-INITIALIZER` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-20 — the address-of INITIALIZER now runs the SAME C 6.5.16.1 `isAssignable` check as the assignment / pointer-variable-init / call-arg / return sites; the RULE is UNCHANGED, only the initializer now REACHES it (no over-tighten).** ROOT CAUSE: c-subset's `varDecl` is declarator-mode, so `pass2Post`'s scalar-init check derived the init type via a stamped-`typeAt` walk down the single-child wrapper chain — an address-of expression node carries NO stamped type there (the walk halts at the multi-child `&obj` unary), so `initTy` stayed Invalid and the `isAssignable` gate (`initTy.valid()`) was SILENTLY skipped. The sibling forms already diagnosed because a pointer-VARIABLE leaf (`char *cp = lp`) carries a pre-stamped type and the ASSIGNMENT form (`cp = &a`) re-derives via `subtreeType` WITH scope. AS-BUILT (`src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp`, PATH 1 declarator-mode block): after the stamped walk yields Invalid, when the walk's terminal is NOT a `braceInitList` (a SCALAR init), re-derive via `subtreeType(s, tree, initNode, here)` WITH the declaration's scope (the sibling at :2417 already passed `here`; the address-of operand needs scope to resolve, then `combineUnary` yields `Ptr<pointee>` → the existing `isAssignable` decides). The brace-init-list form is EXCLUDED because its per-element checks live in the HIR brace-init lowering; a `subtreeType` DFS into the braces would surface an element literal's type and false-fire S_TypeMismatch against the aggregate. AGNOSTIC: drives off `pointerConversions`/`isAssignable`, NO hardcoded AddressOf identity; the void*/qualifier/null-constant/string-literal admissions are byte-identical (verified clean). PINS — 5 red-on-disable in `tests/analysis/semantic/test_semantic_analyzer_c_subset.cpp`: incompatible `char* <- &long` and `int* <- &long` → 1× S0003 each (were 0); legal `void*`/same-pointee `char*`/`long*`/`int*` address-of inits + a `char* = "str"` string-literal init stay CLEAN; a parity pin proves the address-of-init AND pointer-var-init reject the same incompatible pair identically (→ 2). Boundary re-verified via CLI on elf64 AND pe64 (target-agnostic). Corpus: all 148 diagnostic-corpus files pass UNCHANGED, 0 goldens touched (`constexpr_pointer_non_null.c`'s `int* = &int` is same-pointee → stays clean; `auto_derived_declarator.c`'s literal-0 init is stamped → unaffected). LATENT SIBLING (NOT this bug, left as-is): the positional-init path (:8026, toy's `varDecl`) also omits scope, but toy has no address-of/pointers so the gap is unreachable and untestable there; c-subset's other positional-decl (`externDecl`) has no initializer. ORIGINAL DEFERRAL (audit trail): found while independently verifying [[D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY]], 2026-07-20; PRE-EXISTING, silently accepted invalid C — the gap was precisely the `T *p = &otherTypedObject;` INITIALIZER form (the pointer-VARIABLE and ASSIGNMENT siblings already emitted `S0003`). | ✅ (CLOSED) 5 pins red-on-disable + CLI elf64/pe64 boundary + 148-file corpus unchanged | found by [[D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (pass2Post declarator-mode scalar-init — subtreeType-with-scope fallback, brace-init excluded); `tests/analysis/semantic/test_semantic_analyzer_c_subset.cpp` (5 `PtrInit*` pins); plan 23 full-C arc |

---

## Allowlist (code-internal pins, NOT deferred work)

Anchor-shaped strings in `src/` that are **not** deferred-work markers and
should be **ignored** by the CI guard. Add a row when an in-code constant
or diagnostic-message identifier matches the `D-*` regex but isn't a
deferred-work anchor.

| Pattern | Reason |
|---|---|
| `D-MERGE-GLOBAL-INITFUNC` | Cycle 25 Stage B code-internal INVARIANT pin (`src/mir/merge/mir_merge.cpp`), NOT deferred work. Cited in the fail-loud abort that fires if a merged global's `initFunc` MirFuncId was not cloned into the merged module (`plan.funcMerged` miss). The invariant always holds for valid input — a global's module-init function is a defined function in the same CU, which the merge always clones (an init function is never a cross-CU weak loser). The abort is defense-in-depth: a breach means the planning pass dropped a function a global still references (a structural contradiction), so failing loud beats emitting a global whose initializer points at a stale/absent func. No closing work — it is a permanent guard, like the other `mergeFatal` invariant aborts. |
| `D-CSUBSET-COMPUTED-GOTO` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-23 (FC-deferral close-out c9, branch p14 — USER OPTED IN, overriding the named-exclusion).** GNU computed goto (`&&label` label-address + `goto *p` indirect branch) implemented END-TO-END, correct under the full release pipeline. §B-signed-off (full-mechanism-correct-under-opt + synthetic-per-block-symbol reloc); independently plan-locked (MF-1 CFG-correctness verified by construction; 8 opt passes cleared) + independently pr-reviewed. AS-BUILT: grammar (a dedicated `labelAddressExpr` operand rule `[AndAndOp, Identifier]` — NOT a prefix-group `&&`, because the parser rejects `&<undeclared-id>`; a 2-alt `gotoStmt`) + HIR `LabelAddressOf`/`IndirectGotoStmt` + MIR `IndirectBr` terminator (variadic successors = ALL address-taken blocks, like Switch) + `BlockAddress` value op (side-effecting → DCE-stable; `isBlockAddressTaken` DERIVED from BlockAddress ops, drift-proof) + the clone arms at mir_merge + mir_rebuild_helper + a SimplifyCfg guard (don't fold/merge an address-taken TARGET block) + Inlining refusal + LIR/asm (x86 NEW `jmp FF /4` / arm64 NEW `BR Xn` 0xD61F0000; BlockAddress→lea/adrp+add to a synthetic per-block symbol) + a NEW format-neutral linker interior-block-symbol VA (`interior_block_symbol_va.hpp`, ELF/PE/Mach-O, `SymbolKind::BlockLocal`). Witnessed: `examples/c-subset/computed_goto` (threaded interpreter exit 17) + `computed_goto_inline_host` (exit 11) on x86-PE + WSL arm64-qemu, under BOTH baseline AND release pipelines; red-on-disable pins (IndirectBr-successors-truncated, SimplifyCfg-address-taken-fold, inline-into-host-routing). 369→370 ctest. **The pr-review caught a reachable silent miscompile the plan-lock + build BOTH missed**: the `MultiBlockInliner`'s hand-rolled caller-host emit (a 3rd clone family) mis-copied a BlockAddress payload + aborted on IndirectBr when inlining INTO a computed-goto host — folded by routing computed-goto hosts to the single-block rebuilder (correct remap), red-on-disable demonstrated. **NAMED FOLLOW-UP (open):** `D-CG-INLINE-MULTIBLOCK-INTO-COMPUTED-GOTO-HOST`. | (closed — c9 branch p14) | grammar + HIR + MIR + opt + lir + asm + link (interior_block_symbol_va.hpp) / plan-23 full-C arc |
| `D-CG-INLINE-MULTIBLOCK-INTO-COMPUTED-GOTO-HOST` | **OPEN, OPT, trigger-gated (FC-deferral close-out c9, 2026-06-23).** A function that ITSELF contains a computed goto (`BlockAddress`/`IndirectBr`) is currently routed through the SINGLE-block `MirFunctionRebuilder` inliner (which remaps both correctly), NEVER the `MultiBlockInliner` — so a MULTI-block callee is NOT inlined into a computed-goto host (single-block callees still are). Correct + fail-safe (no miscompile); the only cost is a missed optimization. Closing work (only if a real perf consumer needs it): add `BlockAddress` (block-id payload remap via `blockMap_`) + `IndirectBr` (successor remap) arms to `MultiBlockInliner::emitCallerInst`/`emitTerminator` (mirroring the rebuild-helper arms), + a test inlining a multi-block helper INTO a computed-goto host under the release pipeline. Trigger: a benchmark showing the missed inline matters. | Add the 2 caller-host emit arms to MultiBlockInliner + a red-on-disable test. Priority: **opt (trigger-gated)**. | `src/opt/passes/inlining.cpp` MultiBlockInliner (the `functionHasComputedGoto` routing guard) + `D-CSUBSET-COMPUTED-GOTO` |
| `D-CSUBSET-LABEL-BEFORE-CASE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-23 (FC-deferral close-out c10, branch p14, 371 ctest + corpus `label_before_case` exit 25 PE/ELF/qemu baseline+release).** AS-BUILT = Option A (user-chosen — label NESTS over case, C-faithful): a new `caseStmt = [caseLabel, statement]` grammar form added to the `statement` alt + `switchBodyItem` made **speculative** (plan-lock CRITICAL catch: a non-speculative alt would trip the loader's `C_AmbiguousAlternatives` gate at CONFIG LOAD once {Case,Default} enters FIRST(statement) — c-subset would not load AT ALL) + a `caseStmtRule` config pointer (AGNOSTIC; toy/tsql don't declare it → `.valid()`==false → the new arms no-op, exactly like `caseLabelRule`). `lowerSwitch` peels a (multi-)label-wrapped `caseStmt` into arms via an iterative chain (handles `foo: case 1: case 2: stmt`), producing HIR **identical** to the long-supported `case 1: foo: stmt` — the label STAYS a real `LabelStmt` node, so the CST pre-scan + `goto`-INTO-switch resolution + duplicate/undefined-label checks all work UNCHANGED. A `caseStmt` reached as a non-direct-switch-item fails loud via NEW `S_CaseLabelNotInSwitch` (0xE023). Pins (all red-on-disable): HIR-equivalence (`foo: case 1: S` ≡ `case 1: foo: S`), bare-case-stays-flat (Finding 2), outside-switch guard; corpus `goto`s INTO the switch at the label → exit 25 baseline+release. Residual: [[D-CSUBSET-CASE-LABEL-IN-NESTED-BLOCK]]. ORIGINALLY: NAMED LIMITATION (FC5, 2026-06-15) — a goto-label immediately preceding a `case`/`default` label (`switch(x){ foo: case 1: … }`) is legal-but-rare ISO C that FC5 did NOT parse: `labelStmt`'s body is a `statement`, while `caseLabel` is reachable only via `switchBodyItem`, so `foo: case 1:` is a clean fail-loud parse error (P0001 "expected EndStatement", no binary — NEVER a silent mis-parse). All OTHER label positions (before any ordinary statement, forward/backward goto, goto into/out of loops, label on an empty statement, goto-over-initialization) are fully supported. Priority: **normal** (rare form; fails loud). | The clean fix is the case-labels-as-statements switch-grammar refactor — model `case N : stmt` / `default : stmt` as `statement` forms (so labels nest uniformly: `foo: case 1: stmt` = `label(foo, case(1, stmt))`) and re-point `lowerSwitch` at the nested shape. That is its own piece of work (touches the switch lowering's flat case-item consumption), disproportionate to bolt onto FC5 — hence the named blocker. Pin: `switch(x){ foo: case 1: return 1; }` parses + runs. | plan-23 full-C arc (FC5); c-subset `labelStmt`/`switchBodyItem`/`caseLabel`/`caseStmt`/`caseStmtRule`; `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` lowerSwitch + the lowerStmt CaseStmt guard |
| `D-CSUBSET-CASE-LABEL-IN-NESTED-BLOCK` | **OPEN — NAMED LIMITATION, fail-loud, rare (FC-deferral close-out c10, 2026-06-23).** A `case`/`default` label nested inside an INNER block of a switch arm (`switch(x){ case 1: { foo: case 2: y(); } }`) is not associated with the switch by the dss flat-switch model — `lowerSwitch` groups only DIRECT switch-body items, so a case in a nested block reaches `lowerStmt` and fails loud with `S_CaseLabelNotInSwitch` (C 6.8.1). This is a PRE-EXISTING limit of the flat model (it was P0001 before c10 too — `case` was not a `statement`), surfaced (not introduced) by the [[D-CSUBSET-LABEL-BEFORE-CASE]] grammar work; correct + fail-loud, never a miscompile. Priority: **normal** (rare; fails loud). | Closing it = lowerSwitch recursing into nested blocks to harvest their case labels (Duff's-device territory) — a switch-lowering generalization, only if a real consumer needs it. Pin: `switch(x){case 1:{foo: case 2: return 2;}}` runs. | `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` lowerSwitch (direct-switch-body-item grouping) + the `S_CaseLabelNotInSwitch` guard |
| `D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-POINTEE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-27 (p19 Cluster G c27) — model A: `volatile` as a TYPE QUALIFIER.** ★ §B (user, 2026-06-27): **Design B** — a qualified `TypeId` (`TypeKind::VolatileQual`, the registry's anticipated "model A") — chosen OVER a Ptr-pointee bit. TRANSPARENT wrapper: `kind()`/`operands()`/`scalars()` route through `materialId_` so the ~128 kind-dispatch sites are correct BY CONSTRUCTION (the wrapper is observable only via `isVolatileQualified`/`get()`); idempotent; `const` gets NO wrapper (stays ignored for type identity). Accesses set `MirInstFlags::Volatile` via a new `volatileFlagForType` ORed with the c21 `volatileFlagFor` at EVERY access form. c21's per-symbol `SymbolRecord.isVolatile` RETIRED → migrated to the type (fixed a c21 double-count: `volatile int *p` wrongly marked the pointer OBJECT volatile). The two `S_VolatilePointeeNotSupported` (0xE025) reject sites DELETED + retired from kUnsuppressable (enum kept for ordinal stability). ★★ The independent post-build AUDIT CAUGHT a silent-miscompile the local Windows suite missed: member/index/by-value access of a volatile CONTAINER (`volatile struct S *p; p->a` — the sqlite Kahan-summation `volatile SumCtx*` + WAL `volatile WalCkptInfo*` shapes) dropped the flag — a REGRESSION from c21's loud reject to silently-wrong. FIXED by type-propagation: a member/index access of a volatile container yields a volatile-qualified RESULT type, covering nested chains + complex-lvalue compound-assign (`pSum->rErr += r`) by-construction, + explicit by-value-arg/struct-return/brace-init threading; 10 per-form red-on-disable container pins. A reintern silent-drop (transparent `kind()` would reintern `volatile T` as `T` cross-CU) also caught+fixed. WITNESS: 29 MIR volatile pins (red-on-disable, exact-count) + runtime `volatile_pointee_cse` (CSE folds iff the flag is missing: exit 3 vs 2, WSL-verified) + `volatile_pointee`/`volatile_pointee_member` (the c21 reject examples INVERTED to positives); 449→450. ★ LESSON: a multi-site volatile contract needs EVERY container-access form tested — the c21 lesson recurred (the local Windows suite was green-but-wrong; only the WSL runtime pin + the audit's per-form MIR probes caught the container hole). [orig:] **OPEN — NAMED LIMITATION, fail-loud, trigger-gated (c21, D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-QUALIFIER, 2026-06-26).** c21 shipped `volatile` via **model B** (per-symbol/member `SymbolRecord.isVolatile` → `HirVolatileMap` on the access node → `MirInstFlags::Volatile` on every user Load/Store; the optimizer's DCE/CSE/Mem2Reg/LICM already honor the flag). Model B handles the OBJECT / struct-MEMBER / pointer-OBJECT (`int * volatile p`) forms — everything the SQLite frontier (`volatile int isInterrupted;`) needs. It CANNOT express a pointer-to-volatile **POINTEE** (`volatile int *p; *p`): there the volatility rides the DEREFERENCED access, which requires type-level cv-qualification (model A — a cv-qualified `TypeId` threaded through the interner + type-equality + every TypeId consumer + the optimizer's type inspection, a large core change). Building model A NOW, before a re-probe proves SQLite needs pointer-to-volatile, would be a speculative over-build, so the pointee form is REJECTED LOUD (`S_VolatilePointeeNotSupported`, 0xE025, unsuppressable) at BOTH type-name sites — the per-declarator typing arm (head `volatile` + a declarator star) AND the co-located pointer arm (head `volatile` before the first star) — making the fail-loud COMPLETE BY CONSTRUCTION (no pointer-to-volatile-pointee TYPE can be built ⇒ no `Deref` can silently drop the flag, the silent miscompile this gate forbids). | **Trigger:** a SQLite re-probe (or any real consumer) shows a genuine need for pointer-to-volatile-pointee. **Closing work (model A):** add cv-qualification to the type lattice (a const/volatile-qualified `TypeId`), thread it through type-equality + every TypeId consumer + the optimizer's type-inspection, and lower a volatile-pointee `Deref` to a flagged Load; then DELETE the two `S_VolatilePointeeNotSupported` reject sites (and the diagnostic + its kUnsuppressable membership). Pin: `volatile int *p; int a = *p; int b = *p;` keeps BOTH loads under the release pipeline (red-on-disable, exit-code witness mirroring `volatile_member`). | `D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-QUALIFIER` (the c21 model-B parent); `src/core/types/parse_diagnostic.hpp` `S_VolatilePointeeNotSupported`; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the two reject arms); plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-26 (p18 Cluster G c23).** A struct/union body admitted only ONE declarator per member (`int *a, *b;` → P0009 "got ';'"; sqlite3.c:15516 `struct HashElem { HashElem *next, *prev; ... };` the frontier). C 6.7.2.1 allows a comma-separated declarator LIST sharing one base type. FIX = REUSE the existing `declaratorList` mechanism (the engine's `collectDeclarators`/`declaratorNameNode` role dispatch that top-level/local decls already use): new grammar rules `structMemberDeclarator` = `{declarator?}{bitfieldDeclSuffix?}` (the suffix moved INSIDE each slot so `int a:3,b:5;` resolves per-slot) + `structMemberDeclaratorList`; `structField`/`unionField` head + `{structMemberDeclaratorList?}` + `EndStatement`; rows `declarator:1` → `declaratorList:1`; two OPTIONAL `DeclaratorConfig` roles `memberDeclaratorRule`/`memberListRule` (nullopt for toy/tsql → zero change).
