AGNOSTIC (config rows + the generic declarator engine; no lang identity branch). | ✅ THE CRUX (silent layout miscompile, handled by-construction): each declarator gets its OWN pointer/array/bitfield suffix; only the HEAD base type is shared — `int *a, b;` = Ptr<int> (a) + int (b). 3 mandatory plan-lock fixes folded: **FIX 1** (the feature is DEAD without it — breaks single-declarator too) — added the `memberDeclaratorRule` arm to `declaratorDeclaredType` (`semantic_analyzer.cpp`), mirroring the initDeclaratorRule arm, else every field type → InvalidType → struct never composes; the head base TypeId is taken BY VALUE into the append-only interner so no per-slot star leaks. **FIX 2** — added `memberDeclaratorRule`/`memberListRule` to the CLOSED `kDeclaratorKeys` array + OPTIONAL parse in `grammar_schema_json.cpp` (else c-subset config fails to load). **FIX 3** — the `int *a, b;` test asserts per-field TYPES distinctly (a=Ptr, b=I32) not just sizeof (==16 is ambiguous). THE ONE SEMANTIC CHANGE — the bitfield resolve search root moved `node`→`dNode` (per-slot) so widths resolve independently; the anonymous path stays from `node`. WITNESS: corpus `struct_multi_member` (`struct P{int *a,b;}; struct Q{int x,y[2],*z;}` write+read each field → exit 42; pe-native + elf/macho/arm64 release arm) + the per-slot/regression/sqlite-repro semantic tests + 445→446 ctest (the new corpus). MINOR pin: `D-CSUBSET-MIXED-NAMED-ANON-BITFIELD`. ALSO surfaced a PRE-EXISTING orthogonal limitation `D-CSUBSET-SELF-REFERENTIAL-STRUCT` (an inline `struct N { struct N *next; }` self-reference fails loud S_UnknownType — the tag binds after its body's fields type-resolve; reproduces single-declarator too, so NOT caused by c23). The grammar change (member stars moved out of the head into per-declarator) also re-routed the pre-existing pointer-to-volatile-POINTEE struct-member reject from the co-located head arm to the per-declarator arm (now positioned on `*p`, no spurious S_UnknownType cascade — a cleaner diagnostic; reject stays loud); updated `volatile_member_error` golden + the MIR `StructMemberPointeeVolatileFailsLoudViaPerDeclaratorArm` test. | p18 Cluster G c23; cited in `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (structField/unionField/structMemberDeclarator*/declarators block) + `src/core/types/semantic_config.hpp` (DeclaratorConfig roles) + `src/core/types/declarator_walk.hpp` (walk arms) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (FIX 1 + bitfield root) + `src/core/types/grammar_schema_json.cpp` (FIX 2); supersedes the single-declarator `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MEMBER-DECLARATOR` (c10). |
| `D-CSUBSET-MIXED-NAMED-ANON-BITFIELD` | **OPEN — NAMED LIMITATION, fail-aware (not silent), rare, off the SQLite frontier (p18 Cluster G c23, 2026-06-26).** A struct/union member list that MIXES a NAMED bit-field with a TRAILING ANONYMOUS bit-field in the SAME declaration (`struct S { int a : 3, : 0; };` — `a` named, `: 0` anonymous) drops the trailing anonymous slot: the named slot sets `resolvedNamed = true`, which SKIPS the post-loop anonymous-field path (`semantic_analyzer.cpp` ~2435, gated on `!resolvedNamed`). This is a PRE-EXISTING single-anonymous assumption (the c10 anonymous path was written for a field whose SOLE slot is anonymous), surfaced — not introduced — by c23's multi-slot member list. NOT a silent miscompile in practice: the anonymous zero-width bit-field is a layout PADDING/alignment-break hint; dropping it can only UNDER-pad (a layout difference), and the common single-anonymous and all-named forms are correct. Off the SQLite frontier (sqlite3.c does not use mixed named+trailing-anon bit-fields in one declaration). Priority: **minor (trigger-gated).** | **Trigger:** a real consumer needs a mixed named + trailing-anonymous bit-field declaration to lay out correctly. **Closing work:** make the anonymous-field path per-SLOT (iterate the member-list slots whose inner declarator is absent but whose own `bitfieldDeclSuffix` is present, minting an `<anon:…>` symbol for EACH, instead of the single post-loop `!resolvedNamed` block) — i.e. fold the anonymous handling INTO the per-`dNode` loop keyed on the slot's own absent-declarator+present-suffix shape. Pin: `struct S { int a : 3, : 0; int b; };` lays `b` out at the post-zero-width-break alignment (red-on-disable vs the dropped-slot layout). | `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR` (the c23 parent); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the `!resolvedNamed` anonymous-field block); plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-SELF-REFERENTIAL-STRUCT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-27 (p18 Cluster G c24) via NOMINAL composite typing.** ★ §B DECISION (user, 2026-06-26): nominal typing for composites — "100% config driven, no workaround, best long term" — chosen OVER an incomplete-sentinel workaround. FIX: struct/union types are now NOMINAL — identity = (kind, name, DECL-SITE/scope-id), NOT structural field-shape; fields live in an IMMUTABLE side-table (`compositeFields_`) that `operands()`/`scalars()` REDIRECT to for composites (so ~18 consumers unchanged); Pass-1 forward-mints ALL tags pre-order → a self-ref field resolves to the nominal TypeId (incomplete), completed in Pass-1.5. The minimal `struct N { struct N *next; }` compiles (next=Ptr<N>, sizeof 16) + the full `struct sqlite3` parses. ★ The plan-lock REJECTED the FIRST blueprint (name-only identity = silent miscompile for block-scoped same-name structs + breaks `test_type_interner.cpp` StructIsNominalAndStructural; record back-patch violates the append-only interner `arena_container.hpp:62`; frame-open parse-fix infeasible) — shipped the CORRECTED design: decl-site identity + side-table + MID-FRAME tag record (the REAL P0009 fix — `binder_sketch` recorded tags only at frame-CLOSE, so mid-body the decl-vs-expr oracle saw `sqlite3` as Unknown → multiply mis-parse; NOT lookahead) + explicit incomplete-FLAG (not operands-empty: `struct E{}` is a legal COMPLETE zero-field struct) + cycle-safe reintern (memo-before-recursion). Fail-loud: a DIRECT non-pointer incomplete member → `S_IncompleteTypeMember` (0xE026, in kUnsuppressableCodes); sizeof-incomplete → no layout. Indep plan-lock + review + audit all VERIFIED-CLEAN (both rebuilt + empirically tested every silent-miscompile surface incl. the descriptor/extern identity surface = one TypeId). WITNESS: corpus `struct_self_ref` (linked-list build+traverse → exit 42) + `struct_block_scope_distinct` (two different-layout same-name BLOCK-SCOPED structs stay DISTINCT → exit 11, the identity-safety pin) + self-ref/typedef/mutually-recursive/incomplete-member tests; 446→448. Mutually-recursive via implicit `struct B*` works (Pass-1 pre-order mints both tags — no forward decl needed); a BARE `struct B;` statement is the separate deferral `D-CSUBSET-FORWARD-STRUCT-DECLARATION`. [orig:] An INLINE self-referential struct/union-tag pointer member — `struct N { struct N *next; ... };` (and the SQLite `struct HashElem { struct HashElem *next, *prev; ... };`) — fails loud with `S_UnknownType` on the inner `struct N`. ROOT: the composite TAG (`N`) is minted/bound in Pass 1 at the struct-declaration node (`semantic_analyzer.cpp` ~2007, `floatToNamespaceScope`), but the post-order semantic walk visits the BODY's field declarations BEFORE the parent struct node, so when the field `struct N *next` resolves its head tag, `N` is not yet bound → unknown type. INDEPENDENT of declarator mode: it reproduces identically for a SINGLE declarator (`struct N { struct N *next; }`) AND a multi-declarator list, so c23 (multi-declarator members) neither caused it nor is blocked by it — a multi-declarator pointer pair to a SEPARATELY-DEFINED tag (`struct T{...}; struct S { struct T *a, *b; };`) composes correctly (proven by `MultiMemberSqliteHashElemRepro`). Correct + fail-loud (a clean `S_UnknownType`, never a silent miscompile). Off the immediate c23 scope; on the SQLite path (HashElem is self-referential), so a re-probe will need it. Priority: **normal (re-probe blocker for the linked-list/tree shapes).** | **Trigger:** a SQLite re-probe (or any real consumer) needs an inline self-referential record. **Closing work:** a Pass-1 FORWARD-BIND tag pre-pass — bind every composite TAG into its namespace scope BEFORE descending its body (or a two-phase tag resolution: declare-tag-then-resolve-fields), so a field's reference to the enclosing (or a mutually-recursive) tag resolves to the incomplete type. An incomplete struct pointer is legal C (a pointer to an incomplete type has known size); the field type is `Ptr<incomplete N>`, completed when the body finishes. Pin: `struct N { struct N *next; int v; };` composes (next = Ptr<struct N>) — flip `SelfReferentialStructStillFailsLoudPreExisting` (currently expects `S_UnknownType`). | `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR` (surfaced it); `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MEMBER-DECLARATOR` (c10, the field substrate); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (Pass-1 tag bind order); plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-27 (p18 Cluster G c25) — the D1 grammar-unify + dual-mode binder.** ROOT (the c25 frontier): `struct sqlite3` (and any large struct/union/enum BODY) failed P0009 because the body parsed INSIDE the SPECULATIVE probe that disambiguated body-vs-ref (the `XxxSpecifierBody | XxxTypeRef` pair in 4 declaration-head alts, `lookahead 256` ⇒ a 4096-token budget) — a large member list exhausted the budget → the body-alt failed → orphan `};` → P0009. NOT a member COUNT limit; a TOKEN-budget cliff (a 130-member control parsed; a mixed `struct sqlite3` hit it at ~80 members). ★ §B-approved **D1** (the rejected D2 was a FOLLOW-prune — empirically unsound: `BlockOpen ∈ FOLLOW(structTypeRef)` via a struct-returning fn def, so no prefix prune can drop the ref candidate). FIX: UNIFY each pair into ONE rule `XxxSpec = Kw {opt Identifier} {opt XxxBody}` (mirrors C's `struct-or-union-specifier`) in the 4 body-bearing alts (topLevelCompositeSpec / typeSpecifierForDecl / typedefHead / typeBaseAllowingStruct); now the SOLE candidate keyed on its lead keyword → the parser's EXISTING LL(k) unique-production DIRECT DESCENT (`parser.cpp` ~1804-1844) parses it NON-speculatively, so the member body has NO budget. Body-vs-ref becomes an internal 1-token (`BlockOpen`) decision via `{optional XxxBody}`. The BINDER is DUAL-MODE on the same node via a NEW generic facet `definesWhenChild: "<bodyRule>"` (`DeclarationRule.definesWhenChildRule`, child-RULE-keyed — agnostic, mirrors `kindByChild`): body PRESENT ⇒ the EXACT former define path (mint type / bind tag in the Tag namespace / open scope / compose fields from the XxxBody subtree — Pass-1.5 walks the spec's scope, not direct children, so the extra nesting is transparent); body ABSENT ⇒ the EXACT former `XxxTypeRef` tag-reference path (a PAIRED `references[]` row, isTagReference; the loader cross-validates the paired row exists). Gated at every define site (Pass-1 mint+scope-open, Pass-1.5 type-resolve, the type-position inline-type arm → falls through to the tag-ref arm, the Pass-2 reference walker skips a body-present definition, and `cst_to_hir.findCompositeSpecifierIn` requires the body child). `structTypeRef`/`unionTypeRef`/`enumTypeRef` KEPT AS-IS for the ref-only param/cast positions (declHeadForParam / castTypeBase) — minimal blast radius. The 3 `XxxSpecifierBody` rules RETIRED (folded). FULL struct+union+enum this cycle. **NOTE (behavior delta, intentional + still-loud):** a bare `struct S;` (no body, no object) PREVIOUSLY did not parse (a parse error); it now PARSES as a tag REFERENCE and fails loud with `S_UnknownType` (undefined tag) + `S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing` at HIR lowering — still loud, never silent; minting an incomplete tag from `struct S;` stays the separate deferral `D-CSUBSET-FORWARD-STRUCT-DECLARATION`. WITNESS: `LargeStructBody`/`LargeUnionBody`/`LargeEnumBody` (1500-member, GREEN) + `MixedLargeStructBody` (nested anon struct/union + fn-ptr + bitfield + array + multi-declarator past budget) + the parser-shape define-vs-ref pin + 10 semantic dual-mode pins (struct/union/enum define-mints / ref-resolves / member-access-types / undefined-tag-S_UnknownType / redefinition-S_RedeclaredSymbol / anonymous-typedef / nested-inline-body) + the HIR forward-decl + with-object pins; the retired-rule-absence + new-rule presence grammar pins. | (closed — the unify IS the fix) | p18 Cluster G c25; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`structSpec`/`unionSpec`/`enumSpec` + `structBody`/`unionBody`/`enumBody` + the 4 alts + the dual-mode declarations/references/scopes rows); `src/core/types/semantic_config.hpp` (`DeclarationRule.definesWhenChildRule`); `src/core/types/grammar_schema_json.cpp` (load + paired-reference cross-validation); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`isDefinitionAtNode`/`nodeHasVisibleChildOfRule` + the 4 gated sites); `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`compositeSpecifierIsDefinition`); `D-CSUBSET-SELF-REFERENTIAL-STRUCT` (the c24 nominal substrate it builds on); plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-TYPE-DEFINITION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-27 (p19 Cluster G c28) — the LOCAL twin of c25's no-declarator top-level path.** ROOT (the c28 frontier): a BLOCK-SCOPED struct/union/enum DEFINITION with NO declarator — `struct Sublist { int nList; ht_slot *aList; };` as a STATEMENT inside a function (sqlite3.c:68508, `walMergesort`) — failed P0009 at the `};`. `topLevelDecl` made `initDeclaratorList` OPTIONAL (so a bare top-level `struct S { … };` defines a type with no declarator) but the LOCAL `varDecl` REQUIRED the list, so a bare local type-definition statement had no grammar production. FIX (GRAMMAR-mostly, REUSE c25): make `varDecl`'s `initDeclaratorList` OPTIONAL in BOTH branches (the storage-class-led `localDeclSpecifiers declHead …` and the keyword/qualifier-led `kwDeclHead …`) — a local `struct S { … };` routes through `kwDeclHead` (Struct/Union/EnumKeyword-led → the unified c25 `structSpec`/`unionSpec`/`enumSpec` DEFINES the type via `definesWhenChild`, minting the tag into the ENCLOSING BLOCK scope), takes NO declarator, ends at `EndStatement`. `identVarDecl`'s list is DELIBERATELY left REQUIRED (a bare typedef-name `T;` must stay an expression statement, NOT a no-declarator decl — the struct/union/enum definition is keyword-led so only `varDecl` needs the optional list). BLOCK-SCOPING is automatic: the `structSpec` define machinery binds the tag into `current` (the enclosing scope = the BLOCK scope during a body walk) via the c24 nominal decl-site identity, so a local `struct S { … }` does NOT leak to an outer/sibling scope and a same-name outer `struct S` with a different layout stays DISTINCT. SEMANTIC: NO new code needed — the existing Pass-1 `structSpec` define path mints + interns the type in the block scope. CST→HIR: the ONE C++ change — `lowerVarLikeInto` gains the LOCAL twin of `lowerTopLevelInto`'s no-object branch: a LIST-mode (non-single) declaration with an EMPTY declarator list + `requireNamedDeclarators` either (a) DEFINES a composite (`findCompositeSpecifierIn` hits a body-present spec) ⇒ emit nothing (the type was already minted/interned semantically — a later `struct S v;` resolves through it; a body TypeDecl carries no MIR effect), or (b) introduces NO composite (`int;`) ⇒ FAIL LOUD `S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing` (C 6.7p2) — the SAME diagnostic + tier as the top-level `int ;`. The guard is gated on an EMPTY list so an ABSTRACT declarator (`int *;`, list non-empty) is NOT double-reported (the semantic `requireNamedDeclarators` arm already rejects it once). FAIL-LOUD preserved: `int;` loud (declares nothing); a ref to an undefined tag still `S_UnknownType`; no silent accept. WITNESS: corpus `local_struct_def_stmt` (the walMergesort shape — a block-scoped `struct Sublist { int nList; int aList; };` no-declarator definition used to compute a return value across a non-inlined call → exit 42, with a file-scope same-name 1-field `struct Sublist` proving decl-site distinctness; pe-native + elf/macho/arm64 + a `{shippedPipeline:release}` arm) + 7 semantic pins (`C28LocalStructDefineNodeShapeAndType` asserting the varDecl/structSpec/structBody node shape + NO initDeclaratorList + the 2-field Struct type + the in-block ref resolving to the SAME TypeId; union+enum local defines; `C28LocalStructDoesNotLeakToOuterScope` block-scope non-leak → outer ref S_UnknownType; `C28InnerStructShadowsOuterDistinctType` nominal distinctness; `int;` semantically-clean; `int *;` single-diagnostic; ordinary-local-decls-unaffected) + 3 HIR pins (`LocalStructDefinitionLowersClean`; define+declare+ref lower clean; `LocalDeclaresNothingFailsLoud`); 451→454 ctest (1 new corpus + the new pins). | (closed — the grammar tweak + the lowerVarLikeInto twin IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c28; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`varDecl` optional `initDeclaratorList` in both branches); `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`lowerVarLikeInto` no-object local twin); `D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER` (c25 — the `definesWhenChild` machinery it reuses); `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION` (the top-level no-declarator path this mirrors); `D-CSUBSET-SELF-REFERENTIAL-STRUCT` (c24 nominal decl-site identity → the block-scope distinctness); plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-DECLARATOR-TYPE-NAME` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-27 (p18 Cluster G c26).** ROOT (the c26 frontier): `((uid_t(*)(void))aSyscall[21].pCurrent)` (sqlite3.c:40989, the `osGeteuid` define) failed P9006/P0009 — a cast to an ABSTRACT FUNCTION-POINTER type `(T (*)(params))expr` was unsupported: `castTypeRef` admitted only `{repeat StarOp}` after the base, not the parenthesized abstract declarator `(*)(params)`. FIX = REUSE the existing declarator machinery, no new mechanism: `castTypeRef` gains an OPTIONAL `abstractDirectDeclarator` tail (the abstract twin of `directDeclarator`, sharing `parenDeclarator`/`fnSuffix`/`arrayDeclSuffix` verbatim); the semantic resolver folds it via the SHARED `directDeclaredType` engine — factored out of `declaratorDeclaredType` (the part after its pointer-layer loop) so the abstract type-name fold and the declaration-position fold can NEVER drift — yielding `Ptr<Fn(params)->base>` (the SAME type params get). Covers cast + sizeof + compound-literal + va_arg (all share `castTypeRef`). The abstract twin's base EXCLUDES `Identifier` (C 6.7.7 direct-abstract-declarator has no identifier production) — THE CRUX: a bare-identifier base would make `(c * c)` (a multiplication, sqlite/zext `(c*c)*(c*c)`) speculatively COMMIT as a bogus cast (the type child gains >1 leaf → commit-triage rule-1 'not a lone identifier' fires). AGNOSTIC: config grammar + the generic declarator engine; the optional `DeclaratorConfig.directAbstractRule` is nullopt for toy/tsql (zero change). FAIL-LOUD: a NAMED type-name declarator (a name nested in parens, `(int (x))expr`) is rejected with `S_TypeNameDeclaratorNotAbstract` (0xE027) — never silently parsed as the bare base; an unknown base type still `S_UnknownType` (the declarator fold never masks a missing base). ★ §B FORK (reported + resolved empirically): approach B (reuse the full `directDeclarator`, Identifier included) was tried FIRST and REGRESSED `(c * c)` → bogus cast → S_UnknownType (caught by the zext_u32_to_u64 corpus); approach A (the dedicated Identifier-excluding twin) is FORCED + correct. WITNESS: corpus `abstract_fnptr_cast` (a `void*` slot cast to `(int(*)(int))` and CALLED — the osGeteuid shape — exit 42, pe-native + elf/macho/arm64 + a `{shippedPipeline:release}` arm) + 8 parser pins (exact `abstractDirectDeclarator` nesting for cast/sizeof/compound/va_arg + `(c*c)`-stays-multiplication regression + base+stars no-tail regression) + 5 semantic pins (cast types `Ptr<Fn(void)->int>` / `Ptr<Fn(int)->int>` exact shape, named-cast fail-loud, unknown-base S_UnknownType, sizeof-resolves-clean); 448→449 ctest. NOTE: `sizeof(fn-ptr-type)` as an ARRAY DIMENSION does not fold (stays `S_NonConstantArrayLength`) — a PRE-EXISTING limit that equally affects `sizeof(int*)` as an array dim (orthogonal to c26, fails loud not silent). | (closed — the reuse IS the fix) | p18 Cluster G c26; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`castTypeRef` tail + `abstractDirectDeclarator` rule + `declarators.directAbstractRule`); `src/core/types/semantic_config.hpp` (`DeclaratorConfig.directAbstractRule`); `src/core/types/grammar_schema_json.cpp` (load the optional role + closed-key); `src/core/types/declarator_walk.hpp` (`declaratorNameNode` abstract-twin arm); `src/core/types/parse_diagnostic.{hpp,cpp}` (`S_TypeNameDeclaratorNotAbstract` 0xE027); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`directDeclaredType` extraction + the type-name fold + named-reject); `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MEMBER-DECLARATOR`/`D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR` (the declarator engine it reuses); plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-ARRAY-TYPE-NAME` | **OPEN — NAMED LIMITATION, fail-loud, off the SQLite frontier (surfaced p18 Cluster G c26, 2026-06-27).** The BARE-array abstract type-name `(int[10])` / `sizeof(int[10])` (a type-name whose abstract declarator is a top-level `[N]` array suffix) is NOT supported: c26's `abstractDirectDeclarator` deliberately EXCLUDES `arrayDeclSuffix` from its BASE alt because `(b[0])` — a pervasive array-INDEX value expression — would otherwise speculatively COMMIT as a type-name `b[0]` (base `b` + array suffix; the type child gains >1 leaf → commit-triage rule-1 'not a lone identifier' fires before the binder-sketch value check), regressing `sizeof(b) / sizeof(b[0])` (the element-count idiom — the `sizeof_value_array_dim` corpus). C's direct-abstract-declarator DOES allow a bare `[N]` base, but it collides with the index expression under the speculative type-vs-value triage. The `(*)[N]` array-OF-pointer form DOES work (its base is `parenDeclarator`; the `[N]` rides the suffix repeat). FAIL-LOUD: `(int[10])x` is a parse error / `sizeof(int[10])` resolves the bare `int` (no array) — never a silent miscompile. OFF the c26 fn-ptr frontier (sqlite3.c:40989 is the `(uid_t(*)(void))` fn-ptr cast); `sizeof` of a known array OBJECT (`sizeof(arr)`) is the value form and works. Pin: `sizeof(int[4])` folds to 16 AND `sizeof(b)/sizeof(b[0])` stays the value form (both green). | `D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-DECLARATOR-TYPE-NAME` (the c26 parent — its grammar comment carries the exclusion rationale); `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`abstractDirectDeclarator` base alt); `src/analysis/syntactic/parser.cpp` (`typeNameCommitApproved_` rule-1); plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF` | ✅ **LANDED 2026-07-09 (FC17) — C23 §6.7.2.5 `typeof`/`typeof_unqual`, the type-query type-specifiers.** `typeof(x)`=x's type (quals kept); `typeof_unqual(x)`=x's type top-level-qual-stripped; `typeof(T)`/`typeof_unqual(T)`=type-name forms. A PURE semantic-tier type resolver — **ZERO HIR/MIR/LIR/codegen change** (resolves to a TypeId inside `resolveTypeNodeImpl`, reusing the sizeof/_Alignof machinery; consumed downstream exactly like a typedef). Config-driven grammar: `typeof`/`typeof_unqual` keywords + `typeofSpecifier={alt:[typeofType,typeofValue],speculative}` (`typeofType` uses `castTypeRef` + `commitRequiresTypeName{requireKnownType}` [mirrors `alignasTypeName` — so `typeof(ENUM_CONST)` resolves as a VALUE, not mis-committed as a type], `typeofValue` uses `expression`) appended to the 7 type-specifier alt-lists (brand-new disjoint tokens → no FIRST-set collision, existing dispatch byte-identical); config fields `typeofTypeRule`/`typeofValueRule`/`typeofOperandChild`/`typeofStripQualifiersToken`. `typeof_unqual`=`stripVolatile` (only VolatileQual is interned; const is not). ★ **The design-audit caught a CRITICAL silent-miscompile the design MISSED: `resolveTypeNodeImpl`'s coarse volatile/const decl-head scans (`subtreeContainsToken`/`declaratorObjectIsConst`) descended INTO the typeof operand and re-qualified the result AFTER the arm stripped it → `typeof_unqual(volatile T)` was silently INERT (strip is its ONLY observable effect). FIX: a config-driven `opaqueRules` set (the typeof rule ids) makes the operand OPAQUE to both scans — code-audit CONFIRMED provably inert for every NON-typeof declaration on this shared decl-head path.** Fail-loud: `S_TypeofBitfieldOperand`=0xE036 (bit-field operand, C 6.7.3.6p3, unsuppressable); reuses `S_UnknownType` (bad type-name) / existing expr codes / `S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing` (bare `typeof(x);`). ⚠ KNOWN GAP (LOW, code-audit): a `typeof(const int) v;` scalar LOSES its const-flag (the const-scan opacity hides the operand → `v=5;` not rejected) — the SAFE direction of the const-model tradeoff (the alternative leaks const onto pointer objects → spurious `S_ConstViolation`), and const isn't interned at all → belongs to the deferred [[D-CSUBSET-POINTEE-CONST-ENFORCEMENT]] class; a missing DIAGNOSTIC (correct runtime value), NOT a miscompile. Independent design-audit (GO-WITH-FIXES → the scan-opacity fix) + code-audit (VERIFIED-CLEAN). 3-leg ctest **609/609** + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 × 3 legs. WITNESSES: `examples/c-subset/typeof_basic` (RUNTIME wrap-at-256 via a `typeof(base)` u8 object → 42, release arm) + `TypeofUnqualStripsVolatile` (red-on-disable for the scan-opacity fix — preservation passes either way, only strip catches the bug) + `TypeofEnumConstantOperandResolvesAsValue` (polarity pin) + 7 semantic tests + a bare-`typeof(x);` HIR test + 3 diagnostic goldens. | (closed) | plan-23 FC17; plan-00 stepper T3.2; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp}` + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the arm + the `opaqueRules` shared-scan fix); opens [[D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF-GNU-SPELLING]]; const gap → [[D-CSUBSET-POINTEE-CONST-ENFORCEMENT]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF-GNU-SPELLING` | ⏳ **TRIGGER-GATED (opened 2026-07-09 with the FC17 `typeof`/`typeof_unqual` feature, `D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF`).** The GNU pre-standard spellings `__typeof__` / `__typeof` (and `__typeof_unqual__`) are NOT recognized — only the C23 `typeof` / `typeof_unqual` (§6.7.2.5) keywords are. `__typeof__` behaves EXACTLY like the qualifier-preserving `typeof` (no strip); it is common in glibc system headers but has ZERO uses in the vendored SQLite amalgamation (which is C89-portable). FAIL-LOUD, never a silent miscompile: an `__typeof__(x)` today lexes `__typeof__` as an ordinary identifier → a parse/semantic error, never a wrong type. **Trigger:** a real consumer (a glibc-style header, or a user program) uses `__typeof__`/`__typeof`. | **Closing work:** add 2–3 more keyword rows to `c-subset.lang.json` (`__typeof__`/`__typeof` → `TypeofKeyword`; optionally `__typeof_unqual__` → `TypeofUnqualKeyword`) — the grammar's inline keyword-alt at `typeofType`/`typeofValue` child 0 already accepts a set, so the ONLY change is more `{ "word": …, "kind": "TypeofKeyword" }` rows (they route through the identical arm — qualifier-preserving). No semantic/config change. Pin: `__typeof__(x)` resolves identically to `typeof(x)`. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated). | `D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF` (the parent FC17 feature — the C23 `typeof`/`typeof_unqual` type-specifier, shipped 2026-07-09); `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (keyword table + `typeofType`/`typeofValue`); plan 23 full-C arc (FC17) |
| `D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR` | ✅ **LANDED 2026-07-09 (FC17) — C23 §6.7.1 `constexpr`, the OBJECT storage-class (scalar arithmetic + null-pointer objects; NOT functions — C23 has no constexpr functions).** THE EMPIRICAL DELTA vs `const`: `const int x = argc;` compiles clean (const-ness is initializer-blind; the fold is lazy) while `constexpr int x = argc;` fails AT ITS OWN DECLARATION (S_ConstexprNonConstantInitializer). **ZERO codegen** — a validated constexpr object lowers byte-identically to a const object with a foldable init (`hir_to_mir` folds global inits via `evaluateConstant` regardless of const-ness). Config-driven: `constexpr` keyword + `singleDeclSpecifier`/`localDeclSpecifier` alt appends (forDecl reuses the local prefix → `for (constexpr int i = 0;;)` parses, VALID C23 6.8.5p3) + `semantics.constexpr{keywordToken}` → `SemanticConfig::constexprKeywordToken`; linkage rides config too — C23 6.2.2p3 file-scope INTERNAL linkage via `topLevelDecl.linkageSpecifiers["constexpr"]={binding:local}` (composes idempotently with `static`; NOT the noreturn no-op-clobber hazard) + `varDecl.linkageSpecifierIgnoredKinds += ConstexprKeyword` (block scope carries no linkage). Pass 1 mints `SymbolRecord::isConstexpr` (implies isConst) via `specifierPrefixHasConstexpr` (the noreturn-scan mirror); Pass 2's `validateConstexprDeclarator` enforces 6.7.1 — ★ **F1 (audit): the hook is HOISTED ABOVE the per-declarator loop's `initDeclaratorRule` gate** (collectDeclarators admits BARE declarator carriers — a post-gate hook would silently skip the missing-init AND accept `constexpr int f(void){}` with wrong internal linkage). Initializer folding via the NEW float-capable `constExprValue` (allowFloat + `floatLiteralTokens`) sharing ONE extracted `buildConstEvalEnv` (6 resolvers, 3 audit-pinned invariants: the scope-gate, the dynamic/static scope-capture asymmetry, caller-owned token sets) with `constIntExpr` — broad semantic/hir/mir/preprocess/parser/integrated suites prove the extraction behavior-preserving. ★ **F2 (audit; also closed 2 PRE-EXISTING static_assert gaps): config-driven char/bool leaf arms in the SHARED CST evaluator** — a fixed-value keyword arm (`literalTypes` `value:` rows via `CstEvalContext::fixedValueTokens`, integer-valued cores ONLY so the NullptrT `nullptr` row NEVER folds as an integer) + a narrow-char arm ([charStart, charBody] shape via the SHARED `decodeCharLiteralBody`; wide/UTF openers stay loud — their element core is format-keyed/semantic-stamped) → `_Static_assert('a'==97,"")` and `_Static_assert(true,"")` FAILED S0029 at HEAD (empirically confirmed) and now PASS. F3 no-leak walls pinned (`_Static_assert(1.5>1.0)` still S0029; `int a[1.5+1.5]` still S000B; `int a[nullptr]` still S000B). 5 unsuppressable diagnostics: S_ConstexprNonConstantInitializer=0xE037 / S_ConstexprMissingInitializer=0xE038 (per-declarator) / S_ConstexprUnsupportedType=0xE039 / S_ConstexprFunctionNotSupported=0xE03A (proto + definition) / S_ConstexprInvalidQualifier=0xE03B (volatile OBJECT; volatile POINTEE + east-volatile pinned both ways). Enum-typed constexpr ADMITTED (F5, enumerators fold via resolveSymbolValue). WITNESSES: `examples/c-subset/constexpr_array_dim` (exit 42, 4 targets + release arm; sizeof(data)==20 proves N sized the TYPE; float/nullptr/char/bool arms + argc-seeded runtime fill) + the delta pin (`ConstexprDeltaVsConst`) + 13 semantic tests + the MIR linkage pin (`FileScopeConstexprGetsInternalLinkage`, bare/static/const contrast) + 6 diagnostic goldens (zero drift in existing goldens). Deferrals opened: [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR-AGGREGATE-TYPE]], [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR-POINTER-CAST-NULL]]. F7 nit (doc): a no-declarator `constexpr struct S{int x;};` silently ignores the specifier (GCC-warning parity, no declarator to validate). | (closed) | plan-23 FC17; plan-00 stepper T3.2; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp}` + `src/analysis/semantic/{semantic_model.hpp,semantic_analyzer.cpp}` (mint + validate + buildConstEvalEnv/constExprValue) + `src/hir/cst_const_eval.{hpp,cpp}` (fixed-value/float/char leaf arms); [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR]] (the null-pointer-constant machinery it rides); [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-CONSTEXPR-FOLD]] (nullptr==nullptr ICE folding — still open, unchanged) |
| `D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR-AGGREGATE-TYPE` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR]]).** A `constexpr` object of ARRAY / STRUCT / UNION type (`constexpr int a[3] = {1,2,3};`, `constexpr struct S s = …;`, **incl. `constexpr char s[] = "hi";` — a UNIFORM boundary, the char-array-from-string-literal form is deliberately NOT carved out** [Fork C]) is rejected loud with S_ConstexprUnsupportedType (0xE039), NEVER silently degraded to plain const. No CST-tier AGGREGATE evaluator exists (verified: the shared CST const-eval engine folds scalars; brace-init element-wise compile-time validation is a distinct engine) — validating a guessed subset would be a tight-slice. Pinned by `ConstexprAggregateTypesFailLoud` (array + string-char-array + struct) + the `constexpr_unsupported_type` golden. **Trigger:** a real TU needs a constexpr aggregate. | Element-wise compile-time validation of brace-init lists (+ the string-literal char-array form) against the shared evaluator, then admit the kinds in `validateConstexprDeclarator` and lower as the already-working const-global-with-folded-init. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`validateConstexprDeclarator` aggregate arm); the HIR brace-init lowering (the element engine it would reuse) |
| `D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR-POINTER-CAST-NULL` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR]]).** The CAST-form null pointer constant `constexpr int *p = (int*)0;` (C 6.3.2.3p3 admits `(void*)0`; GCC/Clang admit `(T*)0` too) is NOT admitted — `admitsNullPointerConstant`'s folded path requires an INTEGER-typed subexpression (`subtreeType((int*)0)` = Ptr, and the shared cast arm at its 6 call sites was deliberately not widened this cycle), so it falls to S_ConstexprNonConstantInitializer (0xE037) — LOUD, never a silent wrong admit. The uncast forms (`nullptr`, `0`, folded `1-1`) all work. **Trigger:** a real TU initializes a constexpr pointer with a cast-null. | Widen `admitsNullPointerConstant` (or the constexpr Ptr arm only) to admit a pointer-typed CAST whose operand folds to integer 0 — audit all 6 shared call sites for the widening's blast radius first. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`admitsNullPointerConstant` + the `validateConstexprDeclarator` Ptr arm) |
| `D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR-EXACT-REPRESENTABILITY` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR]], code-audit MEDIUM-1).** C23 6.7.1p10 requires a constexpr initializer's value be EXACTLY representable in the declared type — `constexpr int x = 1.5;`, `constexpr unsigned u = -1;`, `constexpr short s = 100000;` are constraint violations (GCC/Clang: “initializer not representable”). DSS currently ACCEPTS them (the fold succeeds; the stored value is byte-identical to the plain-`const` equivalent — a silent ACCEPT of invalid C23, NOT a miscompile). Pinned by the accepting-pin `ConstexprExactRepresentabilityCurrentlyUnenforced` (flips red when this closes → update deliberately). **Trigger:** conformance sweep (FC18) or a real TU depends on the reject. | Add an exact-representability check to `validateConstexprDeclarator`'s arithmetic arm: compare the folded `HirLiteralValue` against the declared type's range/kind (fractional-to-int, out-of-range, negative-to-unsigned → a new S_Constexpr code or 0xE037 with a distinct message). Priority: **normal**. | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR]] (code-audit); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`validateConstexprDeclarator`) |
| `D-CSUBSET-CHAR-HIGHBYTE-ICE-SIGNEDNESS` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by the [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR]] code-audit, MEDIUM-2; the underlying class is PRE-EXISTING from the wide/UTF cycle B).** The new shared-evaluator char leaf arm folds a high-byte narrow char constant `'ÿ'` to +255 in ICE contexts (faithful to the cycle-B value tier — the SAME shared `decodeCharLiteralBody`), but (a) all four DSS reference targets’ compilers use SIGNED char (`'ÿ' == -1`) → `int a['ÿ' < 0 ? 1 : 2]` sizes differently under DSS than GCC/Clang (char signedness is implementation-defined — a LEGAL choice, but a silent parity divergence where pre-fold behavior was a LOUD non-fold), and (b) DSS’s own runtime materializes Char→int as SExt → a POSSIBLE fold(+255)-vs-runtime(−1) disagreement on the same comparison — UNPROBED (the audit was read-only). 0–127 bodies (all real-world usage incl. sqlite) have ZERO divergence. **Trigger:** a runtime-vs-fold probe confirms a disagreement, or a real program hits the boundary. | Probe `'ÿ'` fold-vs-runtime; then either pin the unsigned-code-unit model end-to-end (incl. the SExt path) or reject high-byte narrow char constants in ICE contexts loud (the narrow sibling of [[D-CSUBSET-WIDE-HEX-OCTAL-ESCAPE-VALUE]]). Priority: **normal**. | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR]] (code-audit MEDIUM-2); `src/hir/cst_const_eval.cpp` (the char leaf arm) + `src/core/types/char_decode.hpp` (the shared decoder) + the Char→int SExt lowering |
| `D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS` | ✅ **LANDED 2026-07-09 (FC17, the LAST FC17 item) — C23 §6.7.13 standard-attribute SEMANTICS: `[[maybe_unused]]`/GNU `unused` (suppress S_UnusedVariable), `[[deprecated["msg"]]]`/GNU (warn per use site), `[[nodiscard["msg"]]]`/GNU `warn_unused_result` (warn on a discarded call result), + the unknown-`[[...]]` policy.** ZERO codegen — pure semantic-tier warnings + one grammar statement rule (see [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-STATEMENT]]). Config = a TABLE with a CLOSED VERB SET (`semantics.attributeSemantics{attrSpecRule, stdAttrRule, bareStatementRule, effects:[{names, effect}]}`, effect ∈ {suppressUnused, warnOnUse, warnOnDiscard, none}; loader validates the verb closed-set → C_InvalidSemantics on a typo) + `semantics.nodiscard{discardStatementRule, expressionRule}`. Scan: `extractOneAttrClause` (last-::-segment name, dunder-normalized via the shared `stripDunder`; optional string message via the SHARED `decodeAdjacentStringBodies` chokepoint) + `scanAttributeSemantics` (bounded descent that STOPS AT each attrSpec/stdAttr node; a stdAttr's Internal children are one clause each). Facts fold ONCE per declaration (the alignas/noreturn shared-prefix precedent, Pass-1.5 ~semantic_analyzer.cpp:4260) → applied to EVERY named declarator (`SymbolRecord.isMaybeUnused/isDeprecated+deprecatedMessage/isNodiscard+nodiscardMessage`); deprecated/nodiscard OR-merge across the proto/def pair in the post-1.5 `mergedFnDecls` sweep (messages first-non-empty-wins; isMaybeUnused deliberately NOT merged — consulted only at the declarator's own D8 check). CONSUMERS: D8 skips `isMaybeUnused`; the Pass-2 reference-resolution chokepoint warns S_DeprecatedSymbolUsed per use (incl. a call's callee — the operand-rule row); checkCall's direct-symbol tail warns S_NodiscardResultDiscarded. ★ **F1 (design-audit): the nodiscard discard check is TWO-hop** — the expression engine MATERIALIZES an `expression` node between the call and its statement, so fire iff `rule(parent(call))==expressionRule && rule(grandparent)==exprStmtRule` (both config rule ids; a one-hop parent==exprStmt check would NEVER fire — the audit caught exactly this). By construction `(void)f();` suppresses (castExpr interposes) and `x=f()`/`g(f())`/`return f()` no-fire — pinned red-on-disable (`NodiscardVoidCastSuppresses`). ★ **F4: the effects table carries `none` rows for KNOWN vocabulary** (fallthrough/likely/unlikely/reproducible/unsequenced + noreturn [the FC16 scan owns it] + packed [the composite scan owns it]) so `[[noreturn]] int f(void);` NEVER false-fires the unknown warning (pinned: `KnownC23NoOpAttributesDontWarn`). UNKNOWN-name policy: the C23 `[[...]]` form warns **S_UnknownAttribute=0xE03C, SUPPRESSIBLE** (C23 forbids fatal unknown standard attrs — the WG21 P2552 posture; hasErrors stays false, pinned + `--suppress=0xE03C` probe-verified); the GNU form keeps its pre-existing loud gates (file-scope H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier pinned STILL firing for `__attribute__((frobnicate))`; block scope = [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-GNU-BLOCK-SCOPE-UNKNOWN-NAME]]). All 3 new codes (0xE03C/S_DeprecatedSymbolUsed=0xE03D/S_NodiscardResultDiscarded=0xE03E) are WARNINGS, deliberately NOT in unsuppressable_codes (the S_UnknownTypeAttribute posture — comment added there). REQUIRED linkage seam: `topLevelDecl.linkageSpecifierIgnoredNames` ["noreturn"] += ["deprecated","maybe_unused","unused","nodiscard","warn_unused_result"] — WITHOUT it every file-scope GNU spelling hard-fails H000C (probe-confirmed at the pre-change HEAD); by-name skip has NO linkage effect so a co-present `static` keeps internal linkage (MIR pin `GnuDeprecatedDoesNotClobberStaticLinkage`); string args (`deprecated("m")`) independently skipped by the string-token guard. WITNESSES: 17 semantic tests (suppress+control/GNU-unused/multi-declarator/per-site×2-spans/message/proto-OR-merge red-on-disable/object-use/discard/void-cast F1 pin/consumed-positions/message/GNU-warn_unused_result+merge/unknown-suppressible/F4/fallthrough×2-spellings/bare-stmt-unknown) + 3 parser wrapper pins + 3 HIR tests (7 GNU spellings clean / the frobnicate H000C boundary / fallthrough-lowers-to-Skip) + the MIR static pin + 4 diagnostic goldens (attr_maybe_unused_suppressed / attr_deprecated_two_sites / attr_nodiscard_discard_pair / attr_unknown_std) + `examples/c-subset/switch_fallthrough_attribute` (exit 11 = 1+10 through the marked fall-through, 4 targets + full-release-like arm; empirically 11 on pe64 debug AND release). Deferrals opened: [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-DEPRECATED-TYPES]], [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-PARAM-POSITION]], [[D-CSUBSET-NODISCARD-INDIRECT-DISCARD-CONTEXT]], [[D-CSUBSET-NODISCARD-INDIRECT-CALLEE]], [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-GNU-BLOCK-SCOPE-UNKNOWN-NAME]], [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-TYPE-POSITION]]. | (closed) | plan-23 FC17 (LAST item); plan-00 stepper T3.2; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (attributeSemantics + nodiscard blocks + linkage names + grammar) + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp}` + `src/analysis/semantic/{semantic_model.hpp,semantic_analyzer.cpp}` (scan + mint + merge + D8 + deprecated + nodiscard + bare-stmt arm); [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-STATEMENT]] (the grammar sibling); [[D-CSUBSET-NORETURN]] (the scan/merge precedents) |
| `D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-STATEMENT` | ✅ **LANDED 2026-07-09 (FC17, with [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]) — the C23 §6.8.1 bare attribute-declaration STATEMENT (`[[fallthrough]];` / `__attribute__((fallthrough));`), pre-FC17 a P0009 parse error.** Grammar: `attributeDeclaration = [compositeAttrList, EndStatement]` (REUSES packed's `compositeAttrList` verbatim — audit-verified side-effect-free: `scanCompositePacked` runs only from structSpec/unionSpec) + `declOrAttrStmt = {alt:[varDecl, attributeDeclaration], speculative, lookahead 256}` REPLACING `varDecl` in the `statement` alt (the ONLY varDecl-as-alt-branch site; a direct statement-alt addition trips C_AmbiguousAlternatives — varDecl's FIRST already carries BracketOpen/AttributeKeyword via the localDeclSpecifiers attr alts). HOT PATH (design-audit confirmed + suite-timed): every non-attribute statement lead has varDecl as the SOLE FIRST-survivor → the parser's unique-production DIRECT DESCENT (no probe, no checkpoint); only `[[`/`__attribute__`-led statements speculate (varDecl probes first, rolls back on the immediate `;`). ★ **F2 (design-audit): the wrapper MATERIALIZES a CST node** (named alt rules do — the declOrExprStmt golden precedent) — OWNED deliberately: the `tests/corpus/c-subset/mini_calc.c.tree` golden regenerated (NET delta = exactly 14 `rule:declOrAttrStmt` insertions, one per statement-position varDecl — verified by a diff-counter script; the design's "2 goldens" hint was stale, exactly ONE c-subset corpus .tree exists), NO other golden drifted (hir-text/diag-corpus/toy/tsql all green untouched), + the wrapper-shape pin `StatementVarDeclRidesTheDeclOrAttrStmtWrapper` (statement>declOrAttrStmt>varDecl). Consumers transparent BY MECHANISM: HIR's unmapped-statement `soleMeaningfulChild` PassThrough peels the wrapper (both branches); semantic passes are rule-KEYED full-tree walks. Lowering: `attributeDeclaration → hirKind Skip` (the emptyStmt precedent — an empty Block, ZERO C++); Pass-2's `pass2Post` arm runs the attribute scan on the bare statement for the unknown-name warning only (facts discarded — no symbol). DSS emits no fallthrough-placement diagnostic to suppress, so `[[fallthrough]]` is semantics-neutral by construction (design F7); file-scope `[[x]];` stays a parse error (F7). WITNESSES: the runnable example `switch_fallthrough_attribute` (exit 11 iff the fall-through happens; debug + full-release-like) + parser pins (attributeDeclaration under declOrAttrStmt; attributed decl still commits varDecl) + `FallthroughStatementLowersToSkip` (HIR) + `FallthroughStatementParsesInSwitch`/`GnuFallthroughSpellingParses` (semantic) + `UnknownBareStatementAttrWarnsSuppressibly`. | (closed) | plan-23 FC17; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (attributeDeclaration + declOrAttrStmt + the statement alt swap + the hirLowering Skip row — config-only for the lowering); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the pass2Post bare-statement arm); `tests/corpus/c-subset/mini_calc.c.tree` (the F2 regen); [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-DEPRECATED-TYPES` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]], design F3).** `[[deprecated]]` on a TYPE — a struct/union/enum TAG (`[[deprecated]] struct S {…};`) or a typedef — is silently INERT: tags mint via `definesWhenChild` and their uses resolve via TYPE resolution (resolveTypeNodeImpl / the isTagReference arm), NOT the Pass-2 ordinary-namespace reference chokepoint the use-warning lives at; typedefDecl carries no specifier prefix at all. The declaration PARSES clean and the program compiles — a missing WARNING on advice-tier semantics, never wrong bytes (honest-named silent-inert, the audit-sanctioned boundary). ADJACENT (code-audit M1): the unknown-attr warning ALSO misses DECLARATOR-LESS declarations (`[[frobnicate]] struct S {…};` — the scan mint site sits inside the declarator-carrier branch, so a no-init-declarator-list decl is never scanned; GCC warns) — the same conservative-miss class, closes with this row. A `[[deprecated]] struct S {…} v;` (WITH a declarator) DOES warn on uses of `v` (the object carries the flag per C23 6.7.13 appertainment). **Trigger:** a real TU deprecates a type and needs the use-sites flagged. | Thread the facts onto the TAG/typedef symbol (the fieldChildren/definesWhenChild mint path + typedefDecl) and emit at the TYPE-resolution use sites (resolveTypeNodeImpl's tag/alias arms) — a distinct chokepoint from the ordinary-namespace one this cycle shipped. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the tag mint + type-resolution arms) |
| `D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-PARAM-POSITION` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]], design F5).** A PARAMETER-position attribute (`void f([[maybe_unused]] int p)`) is a PARSE ERROR today — the `param`/`declHeadForParam` grammar has no attribute surface. LOUD, never silent; and there is NO semantic hole behind it: params never warn unused in DSS (the `param` row does not opt into warnIfUnused — pinned by `UnusedParamDoesNotWarn`), so the attribute would be a no-op even if parsed. **Trigger:** a real TU writes param-position attributes (common in C++-leaning codebases; zero uses in sqlite). | Add an optional attribute prefix to `declHeadForParam` (FIRST {AttributeKeyword, BracketOpen} disjoint from every param head lead → non-speculative) + strip it from the positional count (the specifierPrefix precedent); the semantics stay a no-op until params gain an unused warning. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (param/declHeadForParam) |
| `D-CSUBSET-NODISCARD-INDIRECT-DISCARD-CONTEXT` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]], the F1 boundary).** The nodiscard discard check is TWO-hop-EXACT (parent==expression, grandparent==exprStmt), so a discard reached through an INTERPOSED wrapper is MISSED: the parenthesized `(f());`, the comma operand `f(), g();`, the FOR-clause expression `for(;;f())` (code-audit M2) — each discards the result without warning (GCC/Clang warn on both). A WARNING-only miss on advice-tier semantics — never wrong bytes; the dominant bare `f();` form (the idiom) fires correctly, and the miss direction is conservative (no false positives). **Trigger:** conformance sweep (FC18) or a real TU depends on the wrapped-discard warning. | Generalize the discard context to a bounded parent-chain walk that skips discard-TRANSPARENT wrappers (parenExpr, the comma binaryExpr's LHS operand position) before the exprStmt test — each skipped rule config-declared (a `discardTransparentRules` list), never engine-hardcoded. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the checkCall two-hop check) |
| `D-CSUBSET-NODISCARD-INDIRECT-CALLEE` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]).** The nodiscard check lives in checkCall's DIRECT-symbol tail — a call through a FUNCTION POINTER (`fp();` where `int (*fp)(void)` points at a nodiscard function) or an expression callee (`(c ? f : g)();`) never consults the callee symbol's flag (the pointer OBJECT carries no nodiscard; the ternary has no single symbol). The isDirectNoreturnCall scope precedent — same boundary, same conservative (no-false-positive) direction; a WARNING-only miss. C23 attaches nodiscard to the FUNCTION (and in C++ to types — nodiscard TYPES are a distinct, larger feature). **Trigger:** a real TU discards through a fn-pointer and needs the warning. | Either thread nodiscard onto the function TYPE (an interner attribute — heavy) or flag the fn-pointer OBJECT when its initializer is a nodiscard function (a symbol-level approximation). Priority: **low** (advice-tier, rare form). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (checkCall) |

File scope stays loud (H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier — pinned). A missing WARNING (GCC warns `unknown attribute`), never wrong bytes. **Trigger:** conformance sweep or a user hits a silent block-scope GNU typo. | Emit a suppressible S_UnknownAttribute for an unmatched GNU clause when `emitUnknown` — needs a decision on the vocabulary boundary first (GNU attribute space is vast: aligned/cleanup/format/… would all need `none` rows or a GNU-specific known-list before the warning is signal, not noise). | `D-CSUBSET-FORWARD-STRUCT-DECLARATION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c35) — OPAQUE / forward-declared (incomplete) struct types, via REUSE of the c24 incomplete-composite machinery (no new type state).** ON the SQLite path — opaque handles `sqlite3_stmt` / `sqlite3_blob` / … are exactly this pattern (`typedef struct sqlite3_stmt sqlite3_stmt;` at sqlite3.c:4649, used ONLY as `sqlite3_stmt *`); the prior "off the SQLite path" framing was wrong (sqlite3_stmt is a direct counterexample). ROOT (the c25-noted gap): a body-ABSENT named composite specifier never MINTED a tag — c24 forward-mints only when a body opens an inner scope (`rec.structScope.valid()`), so an opaque-tag reference MISSED → `S_UnknownType`, and a bare `struct S;` was body-absent → `S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing` at HIR. FIX (3 edits, config-driven, agnostic): (1) the `resolveTypeNodeImpl` isTagReference arm, on a tag MISS, FORWARD-MINTS an INCOMPLETE composite (`forwardComposite(kind,name,declSiteKey)`) and binds it into the Tag namespace of the nearest enclosing namespace scope (`floatToNamespaceScope`) — covers the opaque typedef base (`typedef struct S S;`), the opaque pointer base (`struct S *p`), and the bare `struct S;` head; the Tag binding deduplicates so repeat references resolve to ONE TypeId; driven by a NEW config field `references[].compositeKind` (struct/union mint, ENUM keeps the fail-loud miss — value-typed, no incomplete representation). (2) the HIR declares-nothing sites (top-level + local) gain a `findForwardCompositeSpecifierIn` sibling: a body-absent NAMED composite specifier (a forward declaration) emits NOTHING instead of `S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing` (the tag was minted semantically); `int ;` / `int;` (no tag) stays loud. ★ EDIT 2 (tag-merge relaxation for forward-then-define) proved UNNECESSARY — Pass-1's whole-tree PRE-ORDER definition-binding guarantees a same-TU definition's COMPLETE tag is bound BEFORE any reference resolves, so the isTagReference forward-mint only fires for a GENUINELY-never-defined tag; a forward-then-define (`struct S; struct S {…};`) resolves every reference to the definition's complete TypeId (no two-TypeId split, no collision) — verified by a type-identity round-trip pin (opaque `S*` from one fn, dereferenced as the complete type elsewhere → 42). The `completeComposite` conflicting-re-completion fatal remains the backstop. ★★ THE FAIL-LOUD AXIS IS PRESERVED (the no-silent-zero-size backstop): the c24 `computeLayout` incomplete guard (`type_layout.cpp` ~279) and its 5 loud consumers are UNTOUCHED — a VALUE / by-value-member / sizeof of an incomplete still fails loud (a local `struct S v;` → MIR `allocaForLocal` H0009; a member `p->x` → S_NotAComposite; sizeof → no layout; a global `struct S g;` → the .bss data-producer fails loud; a by-value param/return → H0009). WITNESS: 6 semantic pins (the INVERTED `C35BareStructForwardDeclMintsIncompleteTag` [was `C25BareStructForwardDeclFailsLoud`, which asserted the pre-c35 S_UnknownType] + opaque-typedef-via-ptr + forward-then-define-completes-no-collision + member/sizeof-of-incomplete-fail-loud + two-definitions-still-collide) + 2 HIR forward-decl-lowers-to-nothing pins (top-level + local) + 4 MIR end-to-end pins (value/member/sizeof-of-incomplete fail loud + opaque-handle-via-ptr lowers clean) + runtime corpus `opaque_handle_ptr` (an opaque `struct Stmt;` never defined, passed by pointer through a non-inlined call, the real object a `struct Vdbe` reached via `void*` — the Vdbe-behind-sqlite3_stmt* pattern → exit 42, 4 targets + release arm). 456→457 ctest. | (closed — the 3 edits ARE the fix; EDIT 2 was a no-op given Pass-1 ordering) | p19 Cluster G c35; `D-CSUBSET-SELF-REFERENTIAL-STRUCT` (c24 — the incomplete-composite substrate REUSED: forwardComposite/completeComposite/isIncompleteComposite + the computeLayout guard); `D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER` (c25 — made `struct S;` parse as a body-absent ref); `src/core/types/semantic_config.hpp` (`ReferenceRule.compositeKind`); `src/core/types/grammar_schema_json.cpp` (load `compositeKind` on tag-ref rows); `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (6 tag-ref rows + compositeKind); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the isTagReference forward-mint arm); `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`findForwardCompositeSpecifierIn` + the 2 declares-nothing sites); plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-MUTABLE-POINTER-TO-CONST` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c36) — object-const computed from the OUTERMOST pointer layer, not a coarse whole-decl scan.** ROOT (sqlite3.c, ~50× e.g. `zFormat += 4` / `zDate++`): DSS wrongly rejected `const char *p; p += n` with `S_ConstViolation` — `const char *p` is a MUTABLE pointer to const (C 6.7.3: const qualifies the POINTEE; only `char * const p` qualifies the pointer OBJECT). `rec.isConst` was set by `subtreeContainsToken(const)` over the WHOLE declaration, which saw the head/pointee const and wrongly marked the pointer object const. SAME bug c27 fixed for `volatile` — the code comment even said "`isConst` keeps its scan", a deliberate c27 deferral now forced. FIX (single file, agnostic, NOT §B): a new `declaratorObjectIsConst` helper (semantic_analyzer.cpp) — descend the per-slot declarator to its inner declaratorRule, and if it has ≥1 pointer layer, the object is const iff the LAST (outermost, source-order) pointer layer carries the const marker; with NO pointer layer the legacy whole-decl scan applies (scalar / typedef'd-pointer head const). Mirrors the c27 per-layer volatile walk in `declaratorDeclaredType`. Const stays a SYMBOL flag (NOT a type qualifier like c27's `VolatileQual`) because — UNLIKE volatile — const has no per-access codegen effect; the architect's Option-2 (a `ConstQual` lattice type) was rejected as a ~100-site surface buying nothing the helper doesn't. ★ CODEGEN NUANCE (the architect's "no codegen effect" claim was INCOMPLETE — caught + verified): `isConst` DOES thread to `MirGlobal.isConst` → `.rodata`/`.data` global placement (asm.cpp). The fix only ever REMOVES false-const (a `const char *g` global moves .rodata→.data, the SAFE/writable direction — placing a mutable pointer in read-only memory was a latent fault), NEVER the reverse; the string-literal `.rodata` override is unaffected. Sites B (anonymous field — never an assignment LHS) + C (legacy positional — no c-subset constMarker row reaches it) left coarse by design (documented in-code). WITNESS: 12 semantic pins (pointer-to-const param/east/multi-level/multi-declarator/const+volatile CLEAN; const-pointer / const-ptr-to-const / outer-const VIOLATE; east-const scalar still violates) + runtime corpus `const_ptr_walk` (a `const int *p` walks an array via `p++` → 42; RED-ON-DISABLE: pre-c36 `p++` was a spurious S_ConstViolation → would not compile; int ops dodge the deferred sub-32-bit ALU gap, p++ dodges the flagged p+n stride). 457→458 ctest. | (closed — the helper + the Site-A swap IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c36; builds on c27 `D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-POINTEE` (the volatile twin + the per-layer walk) + c29 `D-CSUBSET-POST-STAR-CAST-QUALIFIER` (pointerLayer/ptrQualifier); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`declaratorObjectIsConst` + Site A); defers `D-CSUBSET-POINTEE-CONST-ENFORCEMENT` + `D-CSUBSET-GROUPED-DECLARATOR-CONST`; a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-POINTEE-CONST-ENFORCEMENT` | ⏳ **DEFERRED (surfaced p19 Cluster G c36; the c36 audit broadened it).** The const-violation check (semantic_analyzer.cpp ~4404) only examines a PLAIN-IDENTIFIER LHS, so const enforcement is MISSING for every NON-plain-identifier const lvalue: (i) deref `const char *p; *p='x';` (pointee-const), (ii) member `struct S{const int v;}; s.v=2;`, (iii) array-subscript `const int a[2]; a[0]=5;`. All compile CLEAN today (a const GLOBAL-array element write would be a `.rodata` fault at runtime; a const local is silently wrong). c36 made the pointer OBJECT's const correct + did NOT touch this check (the const NAME `a`/`p`/member is correctly flagged — `const int a[2]; a=b;`→S0007 — only the check's LHS coverage is narrow); these gaps are PRE-EXISTING, an independent axis, never a silent miscompile of c36's fix. NOT on the SQLite compile path (sqlite is const-correct). **Trigger:** a consumer that writes through a const pointer/member/element, or a desire for full const-correctness diagnostics. **Closing work:** (a) widen the const-violation check's LHS to deref/member/subscript lvalues; (b) for pointee-const specifically, model const in the type lattice (a transparent `ConstQual` mirroring/generalizing c27's `VolatileQual`) to know a deref target's const-ness — a §B type-system decision (separate `ConstQual` vs a unified cv-qualifier). Pins: `const char *p; *p=0;` / `const int a[2]; a[0]=5;` / `struct S{const int v;}; s.v=2;` → each emits S_ConstViolation. | (open) | p19 Cluster G c36; `D-CSUBSET-MUTABLE-POINTER-TO-CONST` (the object-const fix that scoped this out); `D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-POINTEE` (c27 — the VolatileQual machinery a ConstQual would mirror); plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-GROUPED-DECLARATOR-CONST` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-22 (Tier-2 correctness cluster) — closed together with `D-MIR-ELEMENT-CONST-ARRAY-GLOBAL-CLASSIFICATION` by the SAME group-descent fix (the closing work below, done verbatim).** `declaratorObjectIsConst` now DESCENDS the parenthesized group — group → inner declaratorRule → outermost source-order pointer layer, the same by-ROLE recursion `directDeclaredType`/`declaratorNameNode` use — so a group-hidden `* const` is found. `char (* const p)` is now correctly a const object (`p += 1` → S_ConstViolation) and `const char (*p)` correctly MUTABLE (`p += 1` clean — the over-approximation is GONE), pinned red-on-disable by `GroupedConstPointerParamEmitsConstViolation` + `GroupedPointerToConstParamIsClean` (tests/analysis/semantic; the exact `const char (*p); p=0;` → clean pin) alongside the array sibling's `ElementConstFnPtrArrayGlobalIsConst`. — ORIGINAL (audit trail): ⏳ DEFERRED (surfaced p19 Cluster G c36). c36's `declaratorObjectIsConst` reads the outermost pointer layer that is a DIRECT child of the declarator; a GROUPED pointer declarator `char (* const p)` hides its layer inside the parenthesized group, so it falls through to the legacy whole-decl scan (STATUS QUO — no regression; the coarse scan was used for ALL forms before c36). Consequence: a grouped-with-head-const form like `const char (*p)` stays an over-approximation (the head const is seen by the fallback → p wrongly marked const) — IDENTICAL to pre-c36 behavior, never a silent miscompile (a LOUD false-positive S_ConstViolation on legal code, or a const global redundantly in .rodata). The common grouped case — a function pointer `void (*fp)(int)` with NO const — is correct (the fallback finds no const). NOT observed on the SQLite path. **Trigger:** a consumer using a grouped pointer declarator whose object-const matters. **Closing work:** descend the group (parenDeclarator) following the same recursion `directDeclaredType` uses, to find the TRUE outermost pointer layer. Pin: `const char (*p); p = 0;` → clean. | (closed — the group descent in `declaratorObjectIsConst`, `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp`) | p19 Cluster G c36; closed with `D-MIR-ELEMENT-CONST-ARRAY-GLOBAL-CLASSIFICATION` (same fix); `D-CSUBSET-MUTABLE-POINTER-TO-CONST` (the c36 helper this extends); plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-FUNCTION-DESIGNATOR-CAST` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c37) — a function DESIGNATOR cast to a fn-ptr type.** ROOT (sqlite3.c, 29× — `#define SQLITE_DYNAMIC ((sqlite3_destructor_type)sqlite3RowSetClear)`, `(sqlite3_syscall_ptr)posixOpen`): casting a bare function name to a function-pointer type failed error[S0010] S_InvalidCast. C 6.3.2.1p4 + 6.3.2.3p8: a function designator decays to a pointer-to-function, and a fn-ptr may be cast to ANY fn-ptr type (cross-signature included — calling THROUGH an incompatible type is UB, but the CAST is legal + value-preserving = the function's code address). ROOT: `isExplicitCastable` (type_rules.hpp) had NO arm for an `FnSig` OPERAND → `isExplicitCastable(Ptr<FnSig>, FnSig)` returned false (a function symbol's type is the raw `FnSig`; DSS decays per-consumption-site, not globally, and the CAST site lacked it — the assignment/init/arg path ALREADY decays via `isAssignable`'s Ptr/FnSig arm, so `int(*p)(int)=g;` already worked). FIX (NOT §B, ONE semantic arm, NO grammar/config/HIR/MIR change): add `if (tk==Ptr && ok==FnSig) return true;` to `isExplicitCastable`. The HIR `combineCast` already emits a Cast node for an FnSig operand + `mapCast(FnSig,Ptr)`→Bitcast over the GlobalAddr (present from c12 brace-init) → the cast lowers as a value-preserving NO-OP (the function's address); a call THROUGH the cast pointer reaches the function. ★ OVER-ADMISSION BOUNDARY (the audit surface, verified red-on-disable): the arm is tk==Ptr + ok==FnSig ONLY → struct/union/void OPERANDS (not FnSig) stay rejected, and a function→INTEGER target (`(long)g`, tk not Ptr) stays rejected. WITNESS: 4 semantic pins (same-sig + cross-sig fn-ptr cast ACCEPT; struct→fnptr + fn→int still REJECT) + runtime corpus `fnptr_designator_cast` (`(fp_t)helper` then call-through → exit 42; red-on-disable: pre-c37 it would not compile). 458→459 ctest. | (closed — the one cast arm IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c37; builds on c12 (the FnSig→Ptr mapCast Bitcast + the isAssignable Ptr/FnSig decay arm) + c26 `D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-DECLARATOR-TYPE-NAME` (the abstract fn-ptr cast TYPE); `src/analysis/semantic/type_rules.hpp` (`isExplicitCastable`); a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-NESTED-TAG-SCOPE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c38) — a struct/union/enum TAG nested in a struct body has ENCLOSING (block/file) scope, not the inner struct's member scope (C 6.2.1p4).** ROOT (the single largest sqlite S000D class, ~50× — `struct WalIterator { … struct WalSegment {…} aSegment[FLEXARRAY]; }` then `walIteratorNext` refs `struct WalSegment *pSegment`; also sColMap/ExprList_item/IdList_item): a tag defined NESTED inside a composite body bound into the INNER struct's MEMBER scope → a by-name reference `struct Inner *p` from the enclosing scope MISSED → c35 forward-minted an INCOMPLETE tag → `p->x` failed S_NotAComposite / `struct Inner v;` failed S_IncompleteTypeObject. (Member access `o->m.x` ALREADY worked — the member TYPE resolves via structScope, independent of the tag BIND scope; only by-NAME lookup failed.) ROOT SITE: `floatToNamespaceScope` (semantic_analyzer.cpp) STOPPED (`break`) at a composite-body scope (a `fieldChildren` scope), treating it as a namespace — but C 6.2.1p4: a struct-body is a MEMBER namespace, NOT a tag namespace; a nested tag belongs to the nearest ENCLOSING block/file scope. FIX (NOT §B; ONE line + comment; NO scope-model/HIR/MIR change): change the composite-body `break` to `scope = parent` (float a nested tag PAST a composite body, exactly as it already floats past a declarator-dominator scope) — only a BLOCK or the file/CU root now stops the float. ★ INVARIANTS PRESERVED (architect-verified, plan-lock-confirmed): nominal identity (the c24 decl-site key is independent of the BIND scope → the by-name `struct Inner *` and the member `Outer.m` are the SAME TypeId); member composition (c25 `structScope = here`, not bindScope → `o->m.x` unbroken); pre-order forward-mint (Pass-1 binds the COMPLETE nested tag at file scope BEFORE any function-body reference resolves → no c35 forward-mint, no two-TypeId split); BLOCK-scope correctness (a block-LOCAL nested tag floats only to its BLOCK scope via the not-in-declByRule break → never leaks to file); SHADOWING (a nested tag same-name as a file-scope tag → both at file scope → S_RedeclaredSymbol, correct per C 6.7p3). WITNESS: 7 semantic pins (nested ref/value/union/deeply-nested ACCEPT; member-access REGRESSION guard; block-non-leak OVER-FLOAT guard; same-name shadowing COLLISION) + runtime corpus `nested_tag_scope_ptr` (the WalSegment shape — a nested tag by-name pointer + arrow reads → exit 7; red-on-disable: pre-c38 it would not compile). 459→460 ctest. | (closed — the one-line float fix IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c38; builds on c24 `D-CSUBSET-SELF-REFERENTIAL-STRUCT` (nominal decl-site identity) + c25 `D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER` (the structScope/define path) + c35 `D-CSUBSET-FORWARD-STRUCT-DECLARATION` (the forward-mint that masked the miss); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`floatToNamespaceScope`); a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-FFI-STDINT-CONSTANT-MACROS` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c39) — the C99 7.18.4 `*_C` integer-constant macros shipped from <stdint.h>.** ROOT (the dominant sqlite S0001/S_UndeclaredIdentifier class, 48× — sqlite uses `UINT64_C(0x…)` for 64-bit bitmask constants): DSS's shipped <stdint.h> descriptor (c1) shipped the TYPEDEFs (uint64_t…) but NOT the `*_C` constant macros → `UINT64_C` undeclared. WITNESS: runtime corpus `stdint_constant_macros` (`UINT64_C(0x100000000)>>32` == 1 ONLY if full 64-bit, + INT64_C/UINT32_C → exit 42; RED-ON-DISABLE: pre-c39 UINT64_C undeclared → would not compile; a 32-bit truncation → exit 13) + the existing descriptor macros-surface parse test covers the load. 460→461 ctest. | `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-SUBTRACTION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c40) — `p - q` is ptrdiff_t (C 6.5.6p9); a COMPILE+RUN-green fix.** ROOT (the biggest remaining sqlite S0003/S_TypeMismatch class, ~50× — `sqlite3StrAppend64(pAccum, bufpt, fmt - bufpt)`, `length = &zOut[nOut-1] - bufpt`): `combineBinary` typed `Ptr<T> - Ptr<T>` as `Ptr<T>` (the "first valid type wins" fallback, since commonArithType returns invalid for non-arith operands) — WRONG. C 6.5.6p9: `p - q` (same pointer type) yields a SIGNED ptrdiff_t (i64) whose VALUE is the ELEMENT count (byte_difference / sizeof(*p)). ★ The ASYMMETRY that pinpointed it: `long n = a - b;` WORKED (the var-init check's single-child DFS skips the binary node + coerce passes the Ptr straight into the I64 slot — numerically OK for char) but `g(a - b)` FAILED S0003 (the call-arg check uses full `subtreeType` → Ptr<T> → `isAssignable(I64param, Ptr)` = false). FIX (NOT §B; 3 sites, p-q ONLY): (1) cst_to_hir `combineBinary` + (2) semantic `subtreeType`'s combineBinary lambda — both detect Sub + both-Ptr + SAME-pointee → result type I64 (operands stay Ptr, `common` was invalid so no coerce); (3) hir_to_mir `combineBinaryOp` — when op=Sub, operand kind=Ptr, result kind=I64 → PtrToInt both sides + Sub (byte diff) + (unless sizeof(pointee)==1) SDiv by `elementStride(pointee)` → the SIGNED element count. ★★ DOUBLE VALUE: fixes the COMPILE error (arg type matches) AND the flagged RUN-time silent bug's p-q half (an `int*` subtraction now gives the element count, NOT the 1-byte-unscaled byte count). ★ SCOPE: p-q ONLY; `p ± n` (pointer ± integer → Ptr result — the OTHER half of the flagged combineBinary bug, keeps the Ptr type, only the VALUE needs sizeof scaling) is architecturally distinct → DEFERRED `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-INTEGER-ARITH-SCALING` (c41). Mismatched-pointee `char* - int*` falls through to a Ptr-typed result — caught ONLY in numeric-ARG context (a non-arg `(int)(a-b)` is a deferred silent gap, `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-DIFF-EDGE-CASES`, which ALSO covers `p - arrayName` where the array operand never decays); incomplete/void pointee → fail loud (no element stride). ★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT VERIFIED the CORE fix CLEAN (scaling correct for int/long/double/struct12/char incl. NEGATIVES + the SDiv-not-UDiv proof, debug+release; result type ptrdiff_t in 9 contexts; NO regression to p++/p[i]/int-int/compare/deferred-p±n; void*/incomplete fail loud) — the 2 silent gaps it surfaced are PRE-EXISTING + outside the same-pointee scope. WITNESS: 4 semantic pins (char*/int* p-q as call-arg CLEAN; return+assign CLEAN; mismatched-pointee STILL fails) + runtime corpus `ptr_subtraction` (int* → 3 ELEMENTS not 12 bytes, char* → 5, SIGNED p1-p4 == -3, all passed through a `long` arg → exit 42; red-on-disable: pre-c40 won't compile / an unscaled int* = 12). 461→462 ctest. | (closed — the 3-site ptrdiff result-type + the MIR sizeof-division IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c40; the run-green half of the flagged p-q silent bug (combineBinary); DEFERS `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-INTEGER-ARITH-SCALING` (p±n); `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (combineBinary) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (subtreeType combineBinary) + `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` (combineBinaryOp); a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-DIFF-EDGE-CASES` | ⏳ **PARTIALLY CLOSED — ✅ c65 (p19 Cluster G, 2026-06-30) closed part (1) `p - arrayName`; part (2) mismatched-pointee diagnostic REMAINS DEFERRED.** ★ c65: by the c64 era part (1) had become a COMPILER CRASH (not just garbage) — the un-decayed Array operand made the HIR type `p - array` as pointer-INTEGER arith → the MIR `combineBinaryOp` `p±n` branch tried to widen the Array index via `mapCast(Array,I64)` → `MirOpcode::Invalid` → `addInst(Invalid)` std::abort (exit 134). UN-MASKED (not caused) by c64 clearing the ternary so the sqlite compile reached vdbeSorter `pSorter->iPrev = (u8)(pTask - pSorter->aTask)` (sqlite3.c:107252). FIX (NOT §B; C 6.3.2.1p3 + 6.5.6): (HIR cst_to_hir.cpp combineBinary) an ARRAY operand of pointer `Sub` decays to Ptr<elem> [covers `p-arr`, `arr-p`, `arr-arr`; `arr - scalarIndex` stays the c59 array-index Ptr form], and `ptrSub` now reads the decayed `lc/rc` → result I64 → the existing c40 ptrSub path (PtrToInt+Sub+SDiv by sizeof(elem)); (MIR hir_to_mir.cpp:1208) a fail-loud guard so a remaining non-int index emits a clean diagnostic instead of the addInst abort. ★ THE INDEPENDENT AUDIT caught a FAIL-LOUD REGRESSION: an UNCONDITIONAL decay made `int* - char[]` (mismatched pointee, gcc rejects) a SILENT miscompile (exit 8 — decayed to int*/char*, non-ptrSub → slips into the c41 p±n path treating the array address as an index) where c64 had ABORTED loud → FIXED: the decay fires ONLY when element/pointee types MATCH, so a mismatched array pairing stays un-decayed → fails LOUD (S0008 in a typed context, or the H0009 MIR guard in a pointer context). All valid-C paths verified regression-free (304/304 on all 3 legs, arm64 `sdiv`-by-stride proven). WITNESS corpus `pointer_minus_array` (16-byte element so the stride DIVISION is load-bearing; ptr-member-array + bare-array + the c40 `&arr[i]-&arr[j]` control → 42; RED-ON-DISABLE → the guard's clean H0009 'index TypeKind 21 has no widening cast', or the addInst abort if the guard is also reverted). 3-leg RUN green (x86_64-elf SysV + arm64-qemu AAPCS64 + Win-pe → 42, gcc-cross-checked -O0/-O2/arm64). The c59 `array - scalarIndex` + c40 `p - q` + c41 `p ± n` paths UNCHANGED. The enum-index sibling at the same MIR site → [[D-CSUBSET-POINTER-ARITH-ENUM-INDEX]] (guarded fail-loud, deferred). — *Original deferral (audit trail):* ⏳ DEFERRED (surfaced by the c40 audit, 2026-06-28). Two PRE-EXISTING silent-miscompile gaps in pointer SUBTRACTION that fall OUTSIDE c40's same-pointee-both-Ptr scope (c40 fixed `p - q` correctly; these were untouched, NOT regressions): (1) **`p - arrayName`** (`int *p=&arr[1]; (int)(p - arr)`) compiles clean + returns GARBAGE — the array operand never decays to Ptr (combineBinary: a pointer-pair makes `common` invalid → no coerce → the array stays `Array` → c40's `kind(rhs)==Ptr` guard misses → the fallback types it `Ptr<char>` → MIR raw unscaled Sub). `p - arr` is IDIOMATIC C (`&buf[i] - buf`) → a real RUN-green hazard (flagged via spawn_task). (2) **mismatched-pointee `char* - int*`** in a NON-arg context (`(int)(a-b)`, `long n=a-b`) compiles silently with a raw unscaled Ptr Sub — caught ONLY when the result coerces into a numeric param (an incidental `isAssignable`), never at the subtraction itself. **Trigger:** a consumer (or sqlite re-probe RUN) that hits `p - arrayName` or a mismatched/non-arg pointer difference. **Closing work:** (a) decay an Array operand to Ptr<element> in the subtraction (then c40's same-pointee path handles `p - arr` correctly); (b) when op==Sub and both operands are pointer/array but pointees DIFFER, emit a loud diagnostic at the binary node (not just incidentally in arg context). Pins: `int a[4]; int*p=&a[1]; (int)(p - a)` → 1 (not garbage); `(int)(charptr - intptr)` → a loud diagnostic. | (open) | p19 c40 audit; `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-SUBTRACTION` (c40 — the same-pointee fix that scoped these out); `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-INTEGER-ARITH-SCALING` (the related p±n value-scaling deferral, NOW CLOSED as `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-INT-ARITHMETIC` c41); plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-DIFF-ARRAY-DECAY` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-22 (full-SQLite-feature-set front-end arc) — the SEMANTIC-tier mirror of the c65 HIR array-decay-in-pointer-subtraction fix; closes the 1 FTS5 S0003 for the full-feature build.** ROOT: FTS5 `fts5TriTokenize` computes `zOut - aBuf` (`char aBuf[32]; char *zOut=aBuf;`) and passes it DIRECTLY as an `int` token length — UNCAST. The HIR combineBinary (c65 [[D-CSUBSET-POINTER-DIFF-EDGE-CASES]]) already decays an Array operand of pointer `Sub` to Ptr<elem> and lowers it as `p - q` (PtrToInt+Sub+SDiv), but the SEMANTIC type-oracle (subtreeType/combineBinary, semantic_analyzer.cpp:~9710) required BOTH operands already `Ptr` and never decayed an Array → `zOut - aBuf` typed `Ptr<char>` → the int-context isAssignable failed → S0003. The existing `pointer_minus_array` example only ever CAST the result `(int)(t - arr)`, and the explicit pointer→int cast ABSORBED the mistyped Ptr — why that example never caught this semantic asymmetry. FIX (NOT §B; C 6.3.2.1p3 + 6.5.6p9; ONE arm + a hoisted helper, NO HIR/MIR/codegen change): the semantic pointer-subtraction arm decays an `Array` operand to `Ptr<elem>` FIRST via a hoisted `decayArray` chokepoint (shared with the ternary path, whose local duplicate was removed), same-pointee guard IDENTICAL to c65, so `p - arr` / `arr - p` / `arr - arr` type as the ptrdiff int; `p - n` / `arr - n` keep their behavior (the decay leaves the int rhs non-Ptr → the arm does not fire). WITNESS: semantic pin `PointerMinusArrayTypesAsPointerDifferenceInt` (uncast `z - a` / `a - z` in an int context → 0 S_TypeMismatch; red-on-disable → 2) + runtime corpus `ptr_sub_array_decay` (UNCAST `p - buf` / `buf - p` / `buf - buf` + an int-array stride-4 element-count proof → 42; red-on-disable → revert the semantic decay → S0003). | (closed — the semantic pointer-sub Array-decay + the hoisted decayArray helper) | full-SQLite-feature-set front-end arc 2026-07-22; the SEMANTIC mirror of c65 [[D-CSUBSET-POINTER-DIFF-EDGE-CASES]] (HIR tier, already done); builds on c40 [[D-CSUBSET-POINTER-SUBTRACTION]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (subtreeType combineBinary); the FTS5 full-feature re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-ARITH-ENUM-INDEX` | ⏳ **DEFERRED (surfaced by the c65 root-cause + audit, 2026-06-30; guarded fail-loud, NOT a crash).** An ENUM-typed index in pointer arithmetic (`enum E{B=2}; int *p; … *(p + e)` / `p - e`) reaches the MIR `combineBinaryOp` `p±n` index-widen site (`hir_to_mir.cpp:1208`) with TypeKind Enum; `mapCast(Enum, I64)` returns Invalid (its `isInt` excludes Enum) — the SAME unguarded site as the c65 `p - arrayName` crash. c65 added a fail-loud guard there, so this now emits a CLEAN diagnostic ("index TypeKind … has no widening cast … D-CSUBSET-POINTER-ARITH-ENUM-INDEX"), NOT the addInst std::abort. NOT confirmed in sqlite3.c (the c65 root-cause found the array case as the actual construct; the enum case was a reproducer probe). DEFERRED to its OWN cycle because the fix — resolve the enum index to its underlying integer kind before the widen (the `combineCast` `enumUnderlying` precedent at `hir_to_mir.cpp:1270`) — is a SIGNED/UNSIGNED widen (SExt vs ZExt by the underlying's signedness) that warrants an arm64-focused audit (the c57 SCVTF lesson: a numeric conversion can silently miscompile on arm64 while green on the host). FIX (when needed): at `hir_to_mir.cpp:1207-1208` resolve `indexTy`'s enum → underlying kind, then `mapCast(underlying, I64)`; corpus an enum-indexed `p + enumConst`, value-correct on all 3 legs. | (open) | p19 c65 root-cause/audit; the same MIR site as [[D-CSUBSET-POINTER-DIFF-EDGE-CASES]] (c65); the `combineCast` enumUnderlying precedent; [[D-CSUBSET-ENUM-INT-CONVERSION]] (enum-as-underlying); plan 23 full-C arc |
