"%s" : 0`) decays the string literal to char* and treats 0 as a null pointer → the POINTER type, not an aggregate.** ROOT (the c65 re-probe's FIRST error — c65 cleared the p-arrayName crash so the compile reached this; H0009): sqlite `(sParse.zErrMsg ? "%s" : 0)` (sqlite3.c:161910, a printf-family format arg). combineTernary (BOTH tiers): the c49 null-ptr arm needs the non-0 arm already a Ptr (the string literal is an un-decayed Array); the c56 FnSig arm misses; the c64 array-decay arm needs BOTH arms arrays (the 0 is an int) → all miss → the fallback typed it Array<char,3> → the by-address aggregate-ternary lowering tried to string-materialize the literal-0 arm into a char[3] slot → the Literal pool entry is an INTEGER, not a string → H0009 ([[D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER-NONSTRING-ARRAY-LITERAL-DECAY]] producer guard, hir_to_mir.cpp:2335). FIX (NOT §B; C 6.3.2.1p3 + 6.5.15p6; 2 symmetric edits, the c49/c56/c64 sibling): both combineTernary null-pointer blocks (semantic_analyzer.cpp + cst_to_hir.cpp) gain an Array-arm + literal-0 arm → the array decays to Ptr<elem> (interner.operands(arr)[0] → interner.pointer(elem)); at the HIR tier `common`=Ptr<elem> drives the coerce → the Array arm hits the Array→Ptr decay Cast (a GlobalAddr to the rodata string), the literal-0 the null-ptr Cast. "%s" : 1`) is not literal-0 → not caught; a genuine struct arm is not an Array → c66 skips it (the by-address lowering). "%s" : 1`** (an array arm + a NON-zero int) — a C 6.5.15 constraint violation (gcc warns / `-pedantic-errors` rejects); dss accepts it WITHOUT a diagnostic but emits sound codegen MATCHING gcc-default error-recovery runtime (both exit the same → NO miscompile, only a diagnostic-completeness gap). (2) **`cond ? | `D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-GLOBAL-SYMBOL-ADDRESS` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c67; §B Option A USER-chosen) — a file-scope aggregate global with LINK-TIME-CONSTANT members (function address / &global / string literal) is emitted as STATIC DATA with abs64 RELOCATIONS at the member offsets (the F5 [[D-CSUBSET-SYMBOL-ADDRESS-GLOBAL]] scalar mechanism generalized to aggregate MEMBERS).** ROOT (the c66 re-probe's FIRST error, H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind ×64 — UN-MASKED by c66, NOT a regression: a gdb/diagnostic agent proved the raise line predates Cluster G + c66 cleared the last FUNCTION-body blocker so emitGlobals_ runs for the 1st time): sqlite `aSyscall[]={{"open",(sqlite3_syscall_ptr)posixOpen,0},…}` (sqlite3.c:40875) + ~64 global tables (Sqlite3Config, sqlite3_io_methods, FuncDef arrays…). evaluateConstant folds link-time constants (&x, fn addresses) only for TOP-LEVEL scalars (F5 tryClassifyAsSymbolAddr), NOT aggregate members → the brace-init fell to runtimeInit → emitGlobals_'s lowerExpr(aggregate) tripped the FC8 anti-resurrection guard (hir_to_mir.cpp:1067, [[D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME]] — routed AROUND, not re-opened). §B RESOLVED by the USER → Option A (static data + relocs; NOT runtime-init, which would segfault a const aggregate writing to .rodata). FIX (NOT a workaround; C-correct, gcc-matching; 4 edits): (hir_to_mir.cpp) a recursive `tryClassifyAggregateConst` builds a MirAggregateValue resolving each member via tryClassifyAsSymbolAddr / nested recurse / a new `tryClassifyNullPointerConst` (the `0`/`(void*)0` member) / evaluateConstant [any unresolved → whole-aggregate fallback], + `tryClassifyAsSymbolAddr` extended (a bare Ref to a FUNCTION symbol = decayed fn-designator → its address; a Ptr-result Cast wrapping a symbol-address → peel+recurse, Ptr-gated so `(long)&x` can't reach it), wired into classifyGlobals; (asm.cpp) `encodeAggregateValue` threads a relocs out-param + on a MirSymbolAddrValue leaf pushes a Relocation{base+memberOffset, target, abs64, addend} over the pre-zeroed 8-byte slot, + the aggregate data arm forces `.data` when relocs present (a reloc-bearing aggregate is load-writable even if const — the F5 .data rationale; Mach-O PIE can't rebase __TEXT,__const); (dce.cpp) the F5 liveness fixpoint now recursively descends MirAggregateValue (`collectSymbolAddrTargets`) so a function referenced ONLY via an aggregate-member reloc isn't DCE'd → K_SymbolUndefined in release. WITNESS: corpus `aggregate_global_symbol_addr` (struct + array-of-struct with fn-address + &global + string + null-fn-ptr members; runtime-verified — calls the fn-ptr, derefs &target through a nested struct, reads the rodata string, checks the null fn-ptr → 42; RED-ON-DISABLE → H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind). ★★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT SOUND, all 3 RUN legs (x86_64-elf SysV + arm64-qemu AAPCS64 + Win-pe → 42, gcc-checked): objdump both arches = .data + abs64 relocs with correct nested struct-in-struct offsets + array-of-struct stride 24 + string addends + null=zero-slot; const-aggregate-with-fn-ptr → .data; the c65-style OVER-FIRE hunt CLEAN [`(long)&x` fails loud; `struct{int v;}g2={n}` folds the VALUE not a spurious reloc to n]; DCE keeps the reloc-only fn, removes the dead one; 307/307 WSL+Win. Residual (pre-existing, fail-loud, NOT c67): [[D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-GLOBAL-NONSYMBOL-PTR-MEMBER]]. | (closed — tryClassifyAggregateConst + the encoder reloc-threading + the DCE aggregate-awareness) | p19 Cluster G c67; §B Option A USER; the c66 re-probe's H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind ×64 (un-masked); generalizes F5 [[D-CSUBSET-SYMBOL-ADDRESS-GLOBAL]]; routes around [[D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME]] (FC8 guard); `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` + `src/asm/asm.cpp` + `src/opt/passes/dce.cpp`; a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |

The const flag ALONE was insufficient: materializeStringLiteralGlobal minted its synthetic string global isConst=FALSE (default) yet its bytes must stay rodata. FIX (2 edits): (hir_to_mir.cpp materializeStringLiteralGlobal) mint the synthetic string-pool global isConst=TRUE (the bytes are immutable — matching tryClassifyAsSymbolAddr's string arm which already did) → routes to .rodata via the const selection; (asm.cpp) DROP the unconditional .rodata override → `isConst` ALONE selects the section: a SYNTHETIC pool global (now const) → .rodata, a NAMED mutable `char arr[N]="str"` (non-const declaration) → writable .data. The array-element sibling of c43's offsetof MEMBER folding [[D-CSUBSET-ADDRESS-CONSTANT-FOLD]] (which the CST const-eval punted to "the HIR/global-init engine"). **Class B — zName = `name##Name`** (a bare Ref to a static const char[] global → array-decay to &arr[0]): tryClassifyAsSymbolAddr's bare-Ref arm extended from functionSymbols to ALSO accept a globalSymbols Ref whose type is Array → {sym,0} (scalar-global Ref stays an rvalue load, rejected); c67's reloc encoder + DCE-liveness handle the rest. An aggregate-global member that is a SYMBOL-base address constant with a (possibly non-zero) offset — `&realArray[N]` (AddressOf(Index) on a REAL array global) or `&s.field` (AddressOf(MemberAccess)) — fails LOUD (H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind) where gcc emits a reloc with the element/field-offset addend. c68's tryClassifyNullBaseIndexConst handles only a NULL base (→ a bare integer, no reloc); a symbol base returns nullopt BY DESIGN (its value is symbol+N*stride, a reloc — NOT the bare integer; must not be mis-folded, the c68 audit confirmed `&realArray[2]`→fail-loud). | `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-INT-ARITHMETIC` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c41) — `p ± n` (pointer ± integer) stride-scaling; a RUN-green MISCOMPILE FIX** (user: include miscompile fixes in the cycles; this CLOSES the c40-deferred p±n a.k.a. `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-INTEGER-ARITH-SCALING`). ROOT (the most pervasive flagged silent miscompile — sqlite indexes pointers everywhere): binary `p + n` / `n + p` / `p - n` in `combineBinary` emitted a RAW Add/Sub with NO sizeof scaling (a 1-BYTE stride for ALL element types) — commonArithType returns invalid for a Ptr+Int pair → the fallback typed it `Ptr<T>` (correct type) but MIR `mapBinaryOp(Add, Ptr)` emitted a raw byte add. char* (stride 1) correct; int*/struct* WRONG (off by sizeof). COMPILES (re-probe can't catch it) but miscompiles at RUN time. C 6.5.6p8: `p + n` = the n-th element address = p + n*sizeof(*p). (`n + p` was even mis-TYPED as Int.) FIX (NOT §B; 3 sites; REUSES F1's scaleIndexToBytes/Gep — the SAME stride machinery p[i]/p++ use): (1) cst_to_hir `combineBinary` — CANONICALIZE `n + p` (swap so the Ptr operand is always kids[0]) + type the result Ptr; (2) hir_to_mir `combineBinaryOp` — when op∈{Add,Sub} + result-kind Ptr + kids[0]-kind Ptr → scaleIndexToBytes(n, pointee) then Gep(p, byteOff); for Sub Neg(n) first → Gep(p, -(n*stride)); (3) semantic `subtreeType` combineBinary — type `n + p` as Ptr (not Int). ★ MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE: c40's p-q fires on result-kind I64 (both-Ptr); c41 on result-kind Ptr (one-Ptr-one-int); F1's p[i]/p++ never reach combineBinaryOp (the Index path) → NO double-scaling. `n - p` (no C meaning) + void*/fn-ptr pointee → unhandled / fail-loud. WITNESS: 4 semantic pins (p+n/n+p/p-n typed Ptr; int+int unaffected) + runtime corpus `pointer_int_arith` (int* stride-4 + struct* stride-8, p+n/n+p/p-n/NEGATIVE-n → exit 42; red-on-disable: revert the Gep intercept → 1-byte stride → wrong slot → 1). The existing F1 p++/p[i] + c40 p-q MIR pins stay GREEN. 462→463 ctest. | (closed — canonicalize + Gep stride scaling IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c41; the run-green pointer-arith fix family (with c40 `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-SUBTRACTION`); reuses F1 scaleIndexToBytes/Gep; `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` + `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp`; a flagged miscompile fixed IN-loop (user); plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-RUN-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-INDEX-NEGATIVE-WIDEN` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c42) — a NEGATIVE array subscript `p[-n]` no longer segfaults; a RUN-green MISCOMPILE FIX** (user: include miscompile fixes in the cycles; surfaced by the c41 audit as a PRE-EXISTING crash one path over from c41's p±n). ROOT (run-green-critical — sqlite uses `p[-1]` lookback): the F1 Index path's Gep runtime index is a byte offset (a 32-bit `int` subscript, optionally scaled by a stride `Mul`) that reached the 64-bit `lea` / arm64-`ADD` address computation UN-widened — a NEGATIVE 32-bit value (high bit set, e.g. -1*12 = 0xFFFFFFF4) was consumed with garbage upper-32-bits → a huge positive address → SIGSEGV. The SAME 32-bit-negative-zero-extend bug c41 fixed for p±n, but in the Index path (`combineIndexAddr` / `scaleIndexToBytes` feed). ★ DEBUG-ONLY crash: in release ConstFold collapses a constant `Mul(-1,stride)` to a Const that takes the `lowerGep` constant-disp32 path (hardware sign-extends disp32) → masks it; the BASELINE/DEBUG pipeline is the witness. (`*(p-1)` worked — a pointer-arith Deref, a DISTINCT path; only `p[-1]` crashed.) FIX (NOT §B; ONE function `lowerGep`, LIR tier; NO HIR/MIR/grammar/config change): in the runtime-index branch (AFTER the constant-disp guard), inspect `interner.kind(mir.instType(operands[1]))` and widen a sub-64-bit index to a full 64-bit register BEFORE the lea — SExt for a signed source (Char/I8/I16/I32), ZExt for unsigned (U8/U16/U32), NO widen for I64/U64/Ptr (c41's pre-widened p±n offset → no double-extend) or enum/other (unchanged behaviour). The kind lists MIRROR the SExt/ZExt source-width dispatch gates in the same file → every synthesized widen is one those gates accept; the width flag is `widthFlagsForType(idxTy)` (the SOURCE width). ★★ WHY LIR not MIR (the architect's re-architecture): a FIRST attempt widened in `scaleIndexToBytes` (MIR) and REGRESSED register pressure — `deep_lvalue_chain` (150-deep, all-Const(0) indices) blew the regalloc scratch pool because a MIR SExt added an address-spanning vreg per index. The LIR fix sits AFTER the constant-disp guard, so deep_lvalue_chain's Const(0) indices take the disp path and NEVER reach the widen (no added vregs); a runtime-index widen vreg is short-lived (consumed by the next lea) + coalesceable. WITNESS: runtime corpus `index_negative` (strides 1/4/12 — char*/int*/struct-Trip*; CONSTANT `p[-1]`/`p[-4]` [debug: a runtime stride Mul → the crash] AND RUNTIME `int k=-3; p[k]`; 8 sub-cases → exit 42; RED-ON-DISABLE [PROVEN]: revert the lowerGep widen + rebuild → 0xC0000005 ACCESS_VIOLATION on the first runtime-Mul subscript in debug). deep_lvalue_chain + c40 ptr_subtraction + c41 pointer_int_arith stay GREEN. 463→464 ctest. ★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT (87 probes, debug+release, x86): the CORE widen VERIFIED CORRECT + regression-free — every stride/sign/width/expression-form/enum index reads the right element, no-regression paths (positive p[i], multi-dim, `*(p-1)`, member access) intact, and the register-pressure hypothesis CONFIRMED by a causation bisect (the debug n=11 regalloc cliff is PRE-EXISTING — identical for `int` [widened] vs `long` [not] indices → the widen does not shift it). ★★ THE AUDIT CAUGHT AN OVER-ATTESTATION (fixed before commit): the corpus claimed all-4-targets/release but arm64-RELEASE does NOT compile — ConstFold folds the negative subscript to a NEGATIVE-displacement lea, which arm64 cannot encode (its `lea` has only non-negative ADD-imm12 variants — a SEPARATE PRE-EXISTING gap, proven c42-independent: the constant-disp path that c42 never touches fails identically). FIXED HONESTLY: the corpus now runs BASELINE-only on all 4 targets (the bug is debug-only; the runtime variable-subscript cases exercise the widen via the register path incl. arm64 SXTW under qemu) with NO optimizedPipelines arm (a global ConstFold arm would emit the un-encodable arm64 negative-disp lea on a qemu-CI runner); the arm64 negative-disp lea is DEFERRED `D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA` (c43 — run-green-critical for sqlite arm64-release). Comments corrected (the audit's minor flags): a subscript integer-PROMOTES (C 6.3.1.1) so only the I32-SExt / U32-ZExt / 64-bit-noop arms fire — the narrow Char/I8/I16/U8/U16 arms are DEFENSIVE (mirror the dispatch gates), and an enum index → I32 → SExt (NOT the `default` arm the old comment claimed). | (closed — the one-function LIR index widen + the honest baseline-only corpus IS the fix; arm64-release neg-disp lea split to c43) | p19 Cluster G c42; the run-green pointer-arith fix family (c40 p-q `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-SUBTRACTION` + c41 p±n `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-INT-ARITHMETIC` — c42 is the Index-path sibling of c41's I64 widen); reuses the SExt/ZExt LIR dispatch gates; `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.cpp` (`lowerGep` runtime-index branch); a flagged miscompile fixed IN-loop (user); plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-RUN-green |
| `D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-03 (p19 Cluster G c93; §B — user chose Option 1 [B-gated LIR fold] NOW to unblock the qemu-arm64 sqlite RUN leg; Option A [native SUB variant] registered as the follow-up [[D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA-NATIVE-SUB]]).** arm64's `lea` has NO negative-displacement variant (all frame forms ADD-imm12 / shifted / movz-movk guarded NON-NEGATIVE via immMin/immMax; the base+index form requires `memoffset.zero`), so a NEGATIVE constant displacement matched NO variant → `A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant opcode 'lea' width 64` + the function dropped (17 lea errors / 9 funcs on the sqlite arm64 2-TU compile). x86 unaffected (signed disp32). STEP-0 ROOT (the DEBUG-trigger anomaly): `scaleIndexToBytes` returns a 1-byte-stride index UNCHANGED, so a negative constant CHAR subscript (`p[-1]`/`z[-1]` — sqlite's pervasive byte-lookback idiom, 17 sites ALL disp=-1 across 9 funcs) is a bare negative Const → `combineIndexAddr` → `Gep[base, Const(-1)]` → the `lowerGep` const-disp arm → negative MemOffset, NO ConstFold needed (why the DEBUG compile hit it; the ConstFold/RELEASE path folds wider-stride `p[-N]` the same way). FIX (ONE LIR site + one capability probe, NO HIR/MIR/grammar change): a CONFIG-DRIVEN capability `targetHasSignedDispLea()` (true iff `lea` declares a `[reg,membase,memoffset]` variant with NEITHER immMin NOR immMax = an unbounded signed disp slot — the sanctioned vocabulary-probe pattern like `targetHasMovImm64`, NEVER `if arch`); in `lowerGep`'s const-disp arm, when `disp < 0 && !targetHasSignedDispLea()`, materialize the negative disp into a FRESH GPR via `emitBareConstToFresh` (the c70 helper — arm64 MOVZ + 0xFFFF-MOVK ladder fills the sign-extended 64-bit value) and emit the 4-op base+index `lea [base + idxReg*1 + 0]` = arm64 `ADD Xd,Xn,Xm` (Xm = sign-extended -N → base − N, the correct byte address) — the SAME mechanism the c42 runtime-subscript register path encodes. A POSITIVE disp (≤4095) still takes the single-word ADD-imm12 form (guard is `< 0` ONLY); x86 keeps its single-instruction `lea [base+disp32]` (gate true there → no fold; objdump-verified). Base survives into Rn; the materialized value is a DISTINCT fresh Rm → no clobber. ★★ THE DECISIVE CLEAR: sqlite arm64 2-TU 17 lea errors → 0, 9 aborted funcs → 0, ELF PRODUCED, no new frontier → ★★★ SQLITE EXECUTES SQL ON ARM64: the qemu-aarch64 smoke = 42 == aarch64-gcc, `--version` 3.54.0 byte-identical, JOIN/CTE/typeof all == gcc. WITNESS: `index_negative` regained its `release` + `full-release-like` arms (omitted since c42 precisely to dodge this gap) + gained constant-negative-index STORE round-trips; GREEN all 4 targets both pipeline levels; RED-ON-DISABLE proven (revert the fold → the exact 8× `A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant opcode 'lea' width 64` on the first constant `p[-N]`). 3-leg gate GREEN 549/549 (Win -C Debug + WSL + qemu). ★ c94 (2026-07-03) SUPERSEDED the fold with a NATIVE `SUB` encoding ([[D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA-NATIVE-SUB]]) — the fold + `targetHasSignedDispLea` deleted; the corpus RED-on-disable now witnesses the native SUB. | (closed — the config-gated `targetHasSignedDispLea()` probe + the lowerGep negative-const-disp base+index fold; superseded by the c94 native SUB) | p19 Cluster G c93; §B user Option-1-now; closes the c42-audit deferral; sibling [[D-CSUBSET-INDEX-NEGATIVE-WIDEN]] (c42 register/DEBUG path); the native-SUB follow-up [[D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA-NATIVE-SUB]]; `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.cpp`; the qemu-arm64 sqlite RUN leg; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-RUN-green |
| `D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA-NATIVE-SUB` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-03 (p19 Cluster G c94) — the arm64 negative-const-disp `lea` now encodes as a NATIVE single-instruction `SUB Xd,Xn,#|disp|` (config + shared matcher + encoder), the c93 base+index FOLD is REMOVED, and the residual DEAD const materialization is GONE (sign-agnostically, for the positive member-offset `ADD` too).** c93 unblocked `p[-1]`/`z[-1]` via a B-gated LIR fold that materialized the negative disp into a fresh GPR (MOVZ/MOVK ladder) + emitted a 4-op base+index `ADD Xd,Xn,Xm` — CORRECT but 5-7 arm64 instructions incl. a 2-insn DEAD const. c94 replaces it with the native SUB, mirroring the positive ADD-imm12 / shifted / MOVZ-MOVK `lea` ladder with the op-bit SET. **(1) The matcher (shared substrate, AGNOSTIC):** a NEW config-driven SIGN axis `negMemoffset` (bool, `guard.negMemoffset`) on `TargetEncodingVariant` — a THIRD routing axis beside width + imm-range. `walker_util.hpp` gains `variantNegMagnitude` (the |value| of a STRICTLY-NEGATIVE value-bearing operand, `-(int64)v` so INT32_MIN widens cleanly); `variantMatchesInst` reads the negative half when `negMemoffset` is set, the non-negative half (existing `variantImmMagnitude`) otherwise — the two axes PARTITION the signed value line. x86's disp32 lea slot has no immMin/immMax AND no negMemoffset ⇒ its match-any slot swallows BOTH signs in ONE `lea [base±disp32]` — COMPLETELY UNCHANGED (byte-pinned, see witnesses). `validate()` gains negMemoffset as a disambiguator (variants differing in negMemoffset route on disjoint sign domains, never a shadow) + a coherence reject (negMemoffset needs a `memoffset` operand). **(2) The config:** 3 arm64 `lea` negMemoffset variants — single-word `SUB Xd,Xn,#|disp|` (0xD1000000, |disp|≤4095), shifted word-pair `[0xD1000000, 0xD1400000]` (|disp| 4096..0xFFFFFF), and MOVZ/MOVK + EXTENDED-register `SUB Xd,Xn,Xd` 3-word `[0xD2800000, 0xF2A00000, 0xCB206000]` (|disp| >16MiB, Rn WIRED to the base not baked-sp). All SUB encodings re-derived from the ARM ARM (add/subtract-immediate + extended-register) + verified vs the existing `sub` opcode's fixedWords + aarch64-gcc objdump. **(3) The encoder (fixed32.cpp):** the MemOffset arm computes `effectiveDisp` = |disp| when `selected->negMemoffset` (fail-loud on a non-negative disp on a SUB variant — the sign matcher should have excluded it) and feeds the SAME Imm12 / Imm12HiLo24 / Imm32MovzMovk slots (no new EncodingSlotKind — the bit-windows are identical; the SUB base word makes it a subtract). **(4) The c93 fold REMOVED:** `lowerGep`'s const-disp arm emits the plain 3-op `lea [base + MemOffset(disp)]` for EVERY disp on EVERY target; `targetHasSignedDispLea()` DELETED (unused). x86 takes its signed disp32, arm64 the native SUB — target-blind. **(5) The DEAD const eliminated (sign-agnostic):** a NEW `foldedConstDisps_` set + `constDispFoldsIntoGep` predicate (mirroring the `foldedGlobalAddrs_` fold-skip precedent) — a single-use integer Const whose sole user is a 2-operand const-disp Gep (the disp operand, fits int32) is NOT materialized by `lowerConst` (`lowerGep` reads it via `constIntegerValue`, never `regForValue`; a stray `regForValue` fails loud on the undefined vreg). This removed the pre-existing dead-const on BOTH the negative `SUB` AND the positive member-offset `ADD` (objdump-confirmed: `mov #0xFFF..` count 0 in the arm64 release binary; `mov #0x4` gone before the positive `add x28,x28,#0x4`). WITNESSES: 4 NEW arm64 byte-pins (`Arm64Encoder.NegativeDispLea{EncodesNativeSubImm12, AtImm12BoundaryEncodesSub, WiderThanImm12SplitsToShiftedSub, Beyond16MiBSplitsToMovzMovkSub}` — exact bytes 22 60 00 D1 / 60 FC 3F D1 / 22 70 32 D1 + 42 20 40 D1 / 02 00 88 D2 + 62 24 A0 F2 + 22 60 22 CB) + 1 NEW x86 agnosticism byte-pin (`X86VariableEncoder.NegativeDispLeaSingleInstructionUnchanged` — 48 8D 83 F8 FF FF FF, the c93 baseline byte-for-byte) + the `index_negative` corpus (12 native SUB sites objdumped on arm64 release, ZERO dead const, exit 42 on x86_64-linux + qemu-arm64 across debug + full-release-like + release). RED-ON-DISABLE (witnessed): strip the 3 negMemoffset variants → the negative-disp `lea` fails `A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant opcode 'lea' width 64` + `A_FunctionEncodeAborted` (the c93 fold is GONE, so no fallback — native SUB or bust). 549/549 Win Debug + 549/549 WSL ctest (QEMU_LD_PREFIX). AGNOSTICISM: the matcher change is 100% config-driven (a bool guard axis + a magnitude helper), zero `if(arch)`; x86 negative disp32 is BYTE-UNCHANGED (pinned). F1 dead-const: GONE. ★ 2 LOW audit notes (aaddb688, no fix required): (a) `foldedConstDisps_` is WRITE-ONLY dead state — the fail-loud is actually provided by `regForValue`s existing undefined-vreg check, not the set (unlike the consumed `foldedGlobalAddrs_`); remove the set or correct its comment; (b) the anchor NAME understates scope — the const-disp fold-skip is GENERAL (positive member-offsets, ALL targets, ALL opt levels incl. debug), not just arm64 negative lea. | (closed) | p19 Cluster G c94; replaces [[D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA]] (c93 — the fold) natively; siblings [[D-CSUBSET-INDEX-NEGATIVE-WIDEN]] (c42) + [[D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-WIDE-IMMEDIATE]] (shifted-imm precedent) + [[D-LIR-GLOBALADDR-LOAD-RIPREL-FOLD]] (the foldedGlobalAddrs_ fold-skip precedent constDispFoldsIntoGep mirrors); `src/asm/format/walker_util.hpp` (variantNegMagnitude + the sign axis) + `src/asm/format/fixed32.cpp` (effectiveDisp) + `src/core/types/target_schema.{hpp,cpp,_json.cpp}` (negMemoffset field/parse/validate) + `src/dss-config/targets/arm64.target.json` (3 lea SUB variants) + `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.cpp` (fold removed, targetHasSignedDispLea deleted, foldedConstDisps_) + `tests/asm/test_asm_{arm64,x86_variable}.cpp` + `examples/c-subset/index_negative`; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green RUN phase |

The harness stays on master. | `D-CSUBSET-TYPEDEF-CAST-SCOPE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-03 (p19 Cluster G c99) — RESOLVED-BY-NON-REPRODUCTION + a regression pin.** The c94 audit aaddb688 saw **98× `error[P9006]` in a transient sqlite-master shell.c** at `span64` (`return (*(sqlite3_uint64*)&a) - …`) — the parser read the pointer-cast as a multiply. c99 was scoped to fix it, so I INSTRUMENTED the root cause on the CURRENT master (sqlite `a20de6d683`, span64 present at `shell.c:8048`): **it does NOT reproduce.** Empirical (both x86_64 + arm64, the c98 binaries): a LOCAL-typedef `*(myu64*)&a`, a same-TU `sqlite3_int64/uint64` span64 body, a typedef-in-a-`#include`d-header cast (== the shell.c shape), real shell.c ALONE (0 parse errors; 148 K_SymbolUndefined = the sqlite3.c API, expected), AND the real 2-TU compile all parse the cast CLEAN. WHERE the typedef-ness lives: the parser's `BinderSketch` (`src/analysis/syntactic/binder_sketch.cpp`) records `sqlite3_uint64` as a **Type** binding when it parses `typedef sqlite_uint64 sqlite3_uint64;` (sqlite3.h:308, reached via shell.c:160 `#include "sqlite3.h"`); at the cast site (`typeNameCommitApproved_`, parser.cpp:900-901) `sketch.lookup("sqlite3_uint64")==Type` COMMITS the cast — the `#include`d typedef reaches the disambiguation table correctly, ~7900 lines later. The 98× was a use-BEFORE-visible-typedef ORDERING ARTIFACT of a since-superseded master amalgamation (the current master puts the typedef before span64); NOT a DSS parser gap. **REGRESSION PIN:** `examples/c-subset/cast_deref_through_typedef_pointer` — the exact `*(myu64*)&a` cast-deref (a type-pun round-trip → 42) on x86_64-linux + qemu-arm64; a future master ordering that regresses the in-scope cast-deref goes red. The genuine sqlite-green blocker c99 actually cleared was a SIBLING (a FAM-in-union, [[D-CSUBSET-FAM-IN-UNION-MEMBER]]), not this. | (closed — non-reproducing + pinned) | p19 Cluster G c99 (instrumented, non-reproducing on `a20de6d683`); sibling that DID block: [[D-CSUBSET-FAM-IN-UNION-MEMBER]]; `examples/c-subset/cast_deref_through_typedef_pointer`; `src/analysis/syntactic/binder_sketch.cpp` + `parser.cpp:836` typeNameCommitApproved_; plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-FAM-IN-UNION-MEMBER` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-03 (p19 Cluster G c99; NOT §B — C99 + gcc/clang give one answer) — a flexible-array-member-bearing struct is a legal DIRECT member of a UNION.** ROOT (the c99 re-probe's live blocker, the 3× `error[S001D]` = `S_FlexibleArrayInAggregate` in **sqlite3.c**, NOT shell.c): sqlite's `union { SrcList sSrc; u8 srcSpace[SZ_SRCLIST_1]; } uSrc;` (sqlite3.c:111815/158387/165978 — a stack-allocated slab that backs a "fake" one-entry SrcList; `SrcList` = `struct{int nSrc; u32 nAlloc; SrcItem a[FLEXARRAY];}`, a true C99 FAM struct). The FC6 embedded-FAM check (`semantic_analyzer.cpp:3600`, the composite-member composition loop) rejected a FAM-struct member of a struct OR a union — but C99 §6.7.2.1p18 forbids it only as a member of a STRUCTURE or an ELEMENT OF AN ARRAY, saying nothing about a union; gcc/clang ACCEPT the union form CLEANLY (verified: `-std=c11 -pedantic -Wall -Wextra` → 0 warnings). FIX (NOT §B; narrow, target-agnostic): the `typeContainsFlexibleArray` branch (semantic_analyzer.cpp:3600) gates the S001D emit on `permittedAsUnionMember = (ck==Union)` — a FAM-struct member of a UNION is permitted; a FAM-struct as a STRUCT member (ck Struct) STAYS fail-loud AT this branch. (The independent audit + review established that `ft` reaching this branch is ALWAYS a FAM-struct — `typeContainsFlexibleArray` doesn't recurse into unions, and an array-of-FAM member is InvalidType'd UPSTREAM at applyArraySuffix ~1630/1972 — so the once-proposed `&& in.kind(ft)==Struct` was a TAUTOLOGY that would additionally OVER-REJECT the p18-legal `union{union V v}` under any future recursion change; DROPPED. The gate is purely union-vs-struct.) An array-of-FAM-struct union member STAYS fail-loud upstream (still S001D — p18 "element of an array"); the nested `union{union V v}` form is ACCEPTED (gcc-parity, audit-verified). Matches gcc's pedantic-warn/reject posture + DSS's pre-existing FC6 stance. LAYOUT (no change needed — the real risk, verified clean): `computeLayout`'s Union arm already sizes to `max(member sizes)` and `computeLayout(FAM-struct)` already returns the non-flexible PREFIX size with `hasFlexibleArrayMember=true` (the FAM tail is 0-length for sizeof), so `sizeof(union{SrcList; u8[N]})==max(prefix,N)` — matches gcc (`sizeof(SrcList)==4`, `sizeof(union)==16`). ★ THE RE-PROBE (2-TU sqlite, current master `a20de6d683`): the 3× S001D CLEARED → **x86_64:elf64 + arm64:elf64 compile the `sqlite3` binary CLEAN (0 errors), and RUN SQL: `SELECT sum→42` + `--version→3.54.0` on x86_64-native + qemu-arm64** — the chase-master run-green legs are restored. (pe64 surfaces the NEXT frontier — os_win kernel32 `localtime`/`InterlockedCompareExchange` S0001×50 + `time.h` F001D + cascade, the [[D-FFI-WINDOWS-KERNEL32-FUNCTIONS]] continuation, classified not fixed = c100.) WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/fam_struct_in_union_member` (the `union{struct{int n; T a[];}; char[K];}` written+read through both members → 42 on x86_64-linux + qemu-arm64; RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the carve-out → the exact `S001D got struct Slab s;`) + 4 semantic pins (union-member accepted [genuine red-on-disable for the ck==Union gate] / struct-member rejected [genuine red-on-disable, emitted AT the branch] / array-of-FAM-in-union rejected [UPSTREAM posture guard, not a carve-out guard] / nested union-of-union accepted [gcc-parity boundary lock]) + a TypeLayout companion pin (union sizes to max-of-prefix — layout unchanged by the diff, guards a silent union-sizing miscompile of the newly-reachable shape) + the runnable examples given the `release`/shippedPipeline optimizer arm. | (closed — union direct-member carve-out) | p19 Cluster G c99; the sibling the c99 task was mis-scoped to ([[D-CSUBSET-TYPEDEF-CAST-SCOPE]], non-reproducing); `semantic_analyzer.cpp:3600` + `type_layout.cpp` Union arm; `examples/c-subset/fam_struct_in_union_member`; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-TEST-CONFIG-DISCOVERY-OUT-OF-TREE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-04 (p19, config-discovery hardening; user-directed after the c99 gate exposed it) — shipped-config discovery now honours a `DSS_CONFIG_ROOT` env override so OUT-OF-TREE / CI builds resolve `src/dss-config/` regardless of launch cwd.** ROOT: `findShippedConfig` (config_path_walk.cpp) resolved config by walking UP from `fs::current_path()` for `<ancestor>/src/dss-config/`; fine for an IN-TREE build (ctest's test cwd is a build subdir inside the repo) but an OUT-OF-TREE build's test cwd has NO `src/dss-config` in its ancestry → loadShipped returns null → tests null-deref ("segfault at 0x48") / abort. This is what made a fresh out-of-tree WSL ctest red (~40 backend tests) — MISDIAGNOSED as heap corruption until ASan/gdb/isolation/serial-loop ALL passed + memory was 30GB-free; the real cause was config-discovery (symlink + in-tree both prove it). FIX (agnostic, production-unchanged-when-unset): `findShippedConfig` consults `$DSS_CONFIG_ROOT` (a dir CONTAINING `src/dss-config`, checked BEFORE the walk, falls THROUGH on miss; the path-like-name rejection still gates `loc.name` FIRST so it is not a `../` vector) + `dss_add_test` sets `ENVIRONMENT DSS_CONFIG_ROOT=${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}` on every test. Unset (production) = EXACTLY the historical cwd-walk. WITNESS: `tests/core/test_config_path_walk.cpp` — EnvRootResolvesConfigOutsideCwdAncestry (RED-ON-DISABLE, verified out-of-tree: a synth target under a TEMP dir the walk can never reach resolves only via the env) + traversal-still-rejected + set-but-miss-falls-through. AUDIT SOUND (a22c1ed7 + threading sub-agent a8ac1da0: agnostic, traversal-safe, portable MSVC+GCC, no runtime env writes so getenv is race-free, regression-free). | (closed — config-discovery env override) | p19 config-discovery hardening (exposed by the c99 out-of-tree gate); `src/core/types/config_path_walk.{cpp,hpp}` + `tests/CMakeLists.txt`; broader residual = [[D-TEST-OUT-OF-TREE-CWD-DEPENDENCIES]]; plan 00 test-infra |

| `D-BUILD-GNU-WINDOWS-BIGOBJ-SCOPE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-22 (TF-C51 adjunct build-infra fix) — the GNU-on-Windows COFF big-obj remedy (`-Wa,-mbig-obj`) is now applied PROJECT-WIDE (mirroring the project-wide MSVC `/bigobj`), not just per-file to `grammar_schema_json.cpp`.** SURFACED during the TF-C51 fat-archive gate: a full local build under the GNU-on-Windows toolchain (MinGW / Strawberry-g++, `build-dbg`/`build-mingw`) FAILED to assemble `tests/lir/test_mir_to_lir.cpp` (~6.7k lines) with binutils `as`: "can't write … 'file too big'" (the WINDOWS COFF object-section-count cap). ROOT: the MSVC `/bigobj` in `CMakeLists.txt` is PROJECT-WIDE (reaches every src/ + tests/ target, added below it), but the GNU-Windows `-Wa,-mbig-obj` was scoped per-FILE to ONE TU (`src/core/CMakeLists.txt` `grammar_schema_json.cpp`) — so every OTHER large TU (notably large TEST TUs) under GNU-Windows was EXPOSED, an ASYMMETRY (**NOT a DSS compiler defect, NOT caused by TF-C51**: ELF/Mach-O have no such cap; MSVC + Linux/macOS GCC all build the TU fine — the WSL x86_64 leg is 724/724 INCLUDING this TU). FIX: a project-wide `add_compile_options("$<$<AND:$<CXX_COMPILER_ID:GNU>,$<PLATFORM_ID:Windows>>:-Wa,-mbig-obj>")` beside the `/bigobj` block, gated GNU AND Windows (the Linux/macOS GNU `as` REJECTS `-mbig-obj` — the SAME three-way scoping the src/core comment documents; the per-file scoping is retained there as a defensive belt for that one generated TU). AGNOSTIC: a COFF-object-size accommodation via a generator expression, NO language/CPU/format branch. WITNESSED: `build-dbg` (GNU-Windows) now assembles `test_mir_to_lir.cpp` + the full Windows Debug ctest builds + passes (previously the TU was excluded from the Windows leg + deferred to the WSL leg). | (closed — project-wide GNU-Windows big-obj) | pairs with the project-wide MSVC `/bigobj` (`CMakeLists.txt`) + the per-file src/core scoping (`157f074` CI hotfix); surfaced by the TF-C51 fat-archive Windows gate; `CMakeLists.txt` |
| `D-FFI-WINDOWS-OS-WIN-FLIP` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-03 (p19 Cluster G c95) — the Windows-host FLIP-AND-UNBLOCK: predefine `_WIN32` so sqlite selects its native os_win.c VFS (Fork B, user §B), erase `__stdcall`, resolve `#include "windows.h"`, and ship a windows.json descriptor SKELETON → os_win.c PARSES for pe64.** Probe a1a476db found DSS predefined NO OS macro → sqlite picked os_unix on pe → the c94 F001D POSIX wall; user chose Fork B (native os_win). FIX (8 files, config/format-driven, ZERO if(arch/os/format) in engine): (1) **per-format predefined macros** — `PredefinedMacroDef` gains an optional `availableObjectFormats` filter (preprocess_config.hpp + grammar_schema_json decode/fail-loud-validate via objectFormatKindFromName + the preprocessor per-format SEED), the SAME `ffi::objectFormatInAvailabilitySet` set-membership the shipped-header gate uses; `_WIN32`/`_WIN64`=1 pe-gated in c-subset.lang.json. (2) **★ PREREQUISITE FIX** — `MacroExpander::isDefined()` + the SynthBuilder pre-scan `definedCb` consulted ONLY the `#define` table not `predefined_`, so `#if defined(X)` was FALSE for EVERY predefined macro (value-context worked, defined()-context didn't → they DISAGREED; without this, `_WIN32` seeds but sqlite's `#if defined(_WIN32)` still picks os_unix). Now both consult `predefined_` with the same per-format filter. (3) **§B(i) `__stdcall`/`__cdecl`/`__fastcall`/`WINAPI` ERASE-on-x64** — pe-gated empty predefined macros (correct: all collapse to MS x64, PE64 imports un-decorated; applies to sqlite3.c:35898's direct `__stdcall` declarator too). (4) **§B(iii) QUOTE→ANGLE include fallback** (C 6.10.2p3) — a shared `spliceSystemDescriptorMacros()` helper; a `#include "h"` NOT found on disk RETRIES the `<stem>.json` descriptor path + rewrites the quote bytes to `#include <h>`. ★ Does NOT shadow on-disk quote headers — fires ONLY inside the `if(!resolved)` arm AFTER resolveQuote misses (witnessed: local stdio.h beats stdio.json → exit 123); off-pe windows.json's `availableObjectFormats:["pe"]` → NotAvailable → elf `#include "windows.h"` stays the same P0016. (5) NEW **windows.json** skeleton — 13 core typedefs (HANDLE→ptr, DWORD→u32, BOOL→int, LPCWSTR→ptr<u16>…) + 5 field-bearing structs (OVERLAPPED/LARGE_INTEGER[struct{i64 QuadPart}, not union — the only member os_win reads]/SYSTEM_INFO/WIN32_FILE_ATTRIBUTE_DATA/FILETIME, real layouts) + 56 constants + INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE, `library:{"pe":"kernel32.dll"}`, `availableObjectFormats:["pe"]`. ★ THE FLIP re-probe (2-TU sqlite pe64): os_win.c (sqlite3.c 49112-54458) = **0 errors, PARSES end-to-end**; windows.h F001A gone, __stdcall/WINAPI erased, all types/structs/constants resolve; the parse wave collapsed 109-in-sqlite3.c → 1 (F001A process.h). WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/windows_predefine_macro` (pe RUN=42, differential arm; RED-ON-DISABLE: drop the 6 macros → t_defined pe 42→98 / t_quote_winh → S0001) + test_preprocessor opt-out 7→13 + a load-error pin. Win ctest 550/550, WSL no-regression (the isDefined/quote-angle shared changes don't perturb elf/macho). | `D-CE-HOST-SIGNED-OVERFLOW-UB` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-04 (p19, the user-directed CI-FIX cycle after c103) — the const-eval engine's Neg/Add/Sub/Mul folds were HOST-UB at the INT64 boundaries; now 2's-complement WRAP over uint64.** ROOT (PR#34's linux-clang UBSan CI leg): `examples/c-subset/stdint_limit_macros` aborted — `const_eval_arith.hpp:113` `applyUnaryInt` `case Neg: folded.value = -iv;` with iv=INT64_MIN = signed-overflow UB inside the COMPILER (reached from the preprocessor's `#if` const-eval, pp_if_eval.cpp:510); UBSan's own message names the fix ("cast to an unsigned type"). Local/normal builds pass because hosts happen to wrap — ONLY a sanitizer sees it (why the 3-leg gate stayed green while CI reddened). AUDIT of the whole shared header found exactly 4 sites (Neg:113 + Add/Sub/Mul:149-151); everything else already safe (Div/Rem REFUSE INT64_MIN/-1 explicitly citing this UBSan leg; Shl computes via uint64; Shr/BitNot defined; casts range-guarded). FIX: `wrapNegI64/wrapAddI64/wrapSubI64/wrapMulI64` helpers — evaluate over uint64 (defined for every input), value-cast back (modular, defined since C++20; the `wrapToIntTarget` precedent). POLICY (from the file's own Div-contrast rationale + gcc parity): the WRAPPED value is the correct fold for Neg/Add/Sub/Mul — it is the runtime outcome on every shipped target (x86_64+arm64 wrap) AND gcc/clang's `#if` fold, so folding is behavior-preserving; Div/Rem INT64_MIN/-1 STAY refused (x86 idiv #DE TRAPS at runtime — folding would hide it). SINGLE CHOKEPOINT by construction: all three walkers (const_eval.cpp, cst_const_eval.cpp, pp_if_eval.cpp) route Neg/Add/Sub/Mul through these shared helpers — grep-verified zero direct signed folds outside them. WITNESS: `tests/hir/test_const_eval.cpp::IntegerFoldsWrapAtInt64BoundariesWithoutHostUb` (exact wrap pins: -INT64_MIN→INT64_MIN [the reported site], INT64_MAX+1→INT64_MIN, INT64_MIN-1→INT64_MAX, 0-INT64_MIN→INT64_MIN, INT64_MAX*2→-2, INT64_MIN*-1→INT64_MIN) + `DivRemAtInt64MinByMinusOneRefuseWithOverflow` (the contrast-policy guard) + a WSL clang UBSan standalone repro of the header (trap-gone proof; red-on-disable = revert a helper to the direct signed form → the UBSan abort returns). | (closed — 4 wrap helpers, one chokepoint) | p19 CI-fix (user-directed, PR#34 run 28694482000); `src/hir/const_eval_arith.hpp` + `tests/hir/test_const_eval.cpp`; consumers const_eval/cst_const_eval/pp_if_eval; plan 12.5 const-eval |

NO self-referential-macro blue-paint dependency); (4) `MirOpcode::AtomicCas` {3,3,Value,hasSideEffects=TRUE,"atomic_cas"} — [ptr,comparand,newval]→the ORIGINAL value; side-effecting pins it vs DCE/CSE/LICM (the c103-audit-verified consumers); (5) `lowerAtomicCas` (mir_to_lir) capability-probed: Rule 1 x86 `lock_cmpxchg` (LOCK 0F B1 /r, mandatoryPrefix [240] BEFORE auto-REX; store-shaped mem-dest operands [newval→modrm.reg, ptr→modrm.rm.mem]; implicitRegisters inputs/outputs/clobbered=[rax], inputRoles{comparand}, outputRoles{old} — CMPXCHG loads the observed value into RAX on BOTH outcomes; pin-mov/core/capture-mov, the div/umulh pattern) / Rule 2 arm64 LDAXR/STLXR acquire-release RETRY LOOP as REAL CFG blocks created mid-lowering (retry:[ldaxr old,[ptr]; cmp old,comp @value-width; b.ne→done] store:[stlxr status,new,[ptr]; cmp status,#0 @64 (STLXR's W-write zero-extends → the 64-bit compare is exact; audit: a width-32 reg-imm cmp also exists, the 64 default is strength not necessity); b.ne→retry] done:[result=old, lowering resumes]— the Switch mid-lowering precedent; `old`/`status` vregs created ONCE, loop-spanning live ranges; retry dominates store+done so `old` defined on every path); fail-loud: no realization / half-declared pair / missing roles; (6) arm64 `ldaxr` (W 0x885FFC00/X 0xC85FFC00; result=Rt via the rd slot) + `stlxr` (W 0x8800FC00/X 0xC800FC00; the STATUS result lives in the Rs bit-field 20:16 = the generic `rm` slot — resultSlot:"rm", the slot vocabulary is bit-positional; stored value wires to the rd POSITION, the MOVK dest-wire precedent); width axis: i32→W forms + the 32-bit no-REX.W lock_cmpxchg (the LLP64 LONG CAS — a 64-bit encoding would be a silent miscompile). | `D-PP-USER-DEFINE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-04 (p19 Cluster G c105) — the universal `-D`: `--define NAME[=VALUE]` (repeatable; VALUE defaults 1).** Needed by the MSVC-profile flip (★ USER §B 2026-07-04: pe presents `_MSC_VER=1943` — sqlite's dirent features REQUIRE its MSVC windirent shim [kernel32 FindFirstFileW, importable]; the MinGW opendir is a static-lib shim, IMPOSSIBLE for import-only FFI; REVERSES c100's no-_MSC_VER direction) — sqlite's sanctioned knobs (SQLITE_OMIT_SEH + the TEMPORARY SQLITE_DISABLE_INTRINSIC, ★ USER: staged, c106 removes it) are BUILD configuration and belong on the harness invocation, NEVER in the lang config (purpose-agnosticism). MECHANISM (the design-audit's F1 shape, abd50aa8 GO-WITH-CHANGES): each entry lowers to a `#define NAME VALUE` line in a synthetic **"<command-line>" PROLOGUE** prepended to the synth stream (the gcc model: "as if #define appeared before the first source line"; its own SourceBuffer origin so diagnostics name it) — the ORDINARY directive handler then owns EVERYTHING: tokenizer-true name validation, the C 6.10.8.1 predefined-collision guard (a -D may NOT silently flip `_MSC_VER`/`_WIN32` — loud), the 6.10.3p2 duplicate policy (identical dup idempotent; conflicting dup loud), and #undef-ability (gcc -D parity; predefined_-seeding would have broken sqlite-adjacent `#undef` patterns — the audit's HIGH catch). Plumb: CliArgs.defines (+`InvalidDefine` on empty NAME / '(' [function-like -D unsupported — use a config predefine]) → Program::setUserDefines (the setOutputDir pattern) → both UnitBuilder sites → preprocess(userDefines) → the prologue. A non-preprocess language with pending defines fails loud (`D_DefineRequiresPreprocess` 0xD012). WITNESS: 5 pp pins (ordinary+#undef-able / default-1 / 6.10.3p2 dup policy both arms / predefine-collision loud). | (closed — the -D feature) | p19 c105; `cli_args.{hpp,cpp}` + `program.{hpp,cpp}` + `compilation_unit.{hpp,cpp}` + `preprocessor.{hpp,cpp}` + `parse_diagnostic.{hpp,cpp}`; harness `build-and-test.sh` pe64 knobs; plan 23 Windows leg |

MECHANISM (the audit's F2 shape): a params-bearing predefine does NOT join `predefined_` — it lowers to a `#define name(params) value` line in a **"<built-in>" PROLOGUE** (before "<command-line>"), so the ordinary directive handler owns param parsing (parseParamList), C 6.10.3p6 dup rejection, 6.10.3p2 redefinition, and the function-like ARG-EATING expansion (nested parens balanced — `align(128)` is the hard case) — ZERO new expander machinery; the predefined_-seed loop SKIPS isFunctionLike entries (else the prologue #define would trip the 6.10.8.1 guard against itself). Consequence (documented): a function-like predefine is an ORDINARY macro (#undef-able) — the modeled construct is a compiler keyword by convention. Sibling pe predefines: `__int64`→`long long` (the MinGW-faithful TEXT map; re-tokenizes to 2 real keywords → the `unsigned long long` multiset row; audit-verified sound in every position sqlite uses) + `__forceinline`→"". | `D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-BARRIER` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-05 (p19 Cluster G c113, user-directed harness cleanup) — `<intrin.h>` resolves on pe64 + `_ReadWriteBarrier` as a lowered COMPILER-barrier builtin: sqlite compiles+RUNS without `SQLITE_DISABLE_INTRINSIC`.** ROOT (empirical probe with a minimal include-resolving descriptor): dropping the define leaves EXACTLY ONE error — `S0001 got _ReadWriteBarrier` (sqlite3MemoryBarrier, sqlite3.c:31355, the `MSVC_VERSION>=1400` branch). The `_byteswap_*` fast path is `SQLITE_BYTEORDER==1234`-gated and DSS's config takes the portable byte-by-byte `#else` (objdump: the c112 exe imports NO byteswap/umulh/barrier) → the design-audit's planned bswap machinery had NO consumer (speculative build, correctly NOT built — see [[D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-BSWAP]]); `#pragma intrinsic(...)` was already consumed-and-dropped (preprocessor.cpp:1489, C 6.10.6p2); `__umulh` already resolves (c103 builtin). MECHANISM (reuses the c103 seam verbatim): `BuiltinLowering::Barrier` (`"barrier"`) + c-subset.lang.json `{_ReadWriteBarrier, fn() -> void, lowering:barrier}` (always-on builtin) → hir_to_mir `Barrier` arm → a NEW zero-operand `MirOpcode::CompilerBarrier` `{0,0,0,0,R::None,false,hasSideEffects=TRUE,false,"compiler_barrier"}` (the BlockAddress declarative shape; R::None ⇒ addInst takes InvalidType, the Store convention) → mir_to_lir emits NOTHING (a pure compile-time ordering fence — no ISA instruction, no target-opcode entry, identical on every target/format). ★ THE AUDIT-F1 LOAD-BEARING FIX (the read-motion hole): the Load-motion clobber scan had TWO sites — `mir_alias.hpp mirAnyMayAliasingStoreInRegion` (CSE cross-block + LICM's loop wrapper) AND cse.cpp's inline `storesClobber` same-block slices — BOTH scanned ONLY `Store`, so a may-aliasing Load could be CSE-reused/LICM-hoisted ACROSS the fence (and across a Call — a LATENT pre-existing miscompile class, since an opaque callee may write the loaded memory). FIX (multi-site rule: funnel through ONE chokepoint): NEW shared per-inst predicate `mirInstClobbersLoadPtr` (mir_alias.hpp) used by the region walker AND the cse.cpp slices — Store stays PRECISE (mirMayAlias), any other op in the NEW `opcodeClobbersMemory` positive list (mir_opcode.hpp, the isCommutative convention: Store/Call/IntrinsicCall/AtomicCas/CompilerBarrier) is an OPAQUE clobber. ★ REVIEW-CAUGHT CORRECTION (the fold's own critical): the first draft keyed the arm on `hasSideEffects` — but that is a DCE-LIVENESS flag, TRUE for every TERMINATOR (Br/CondBr/Return/…) + Alloca + the Va* leaves; since `blockInstAt` iteration INCLUDES the terminator and every loop body ends in one, that draft disabled ALL LICM Load-hoisting (the pre-existing `Licm.LoadHoistedAcrossDistinctPrimitiveStore…` pin reds) and blocked all cross-block Load CSE. The corrected key = the dedicated memory-clobber classification, with the negative-control pin asserting a Return terminator is NOT a clobber. DESCRIPTOR: `shippedLibs/intrin.json` `availableObjectFormats:["pe"]` (a Microsoft compiler-intrinsic header; elf/macho reject fail-loud), NON-empty via the honest `size_t`→u64 typedef (MSVC's real intrin.h makes size_t visible; string/stdio.json convention). ★ EMPIRICAL TRAP RE-CONFIRMED: a draft declared `_byteswap_*` as msvcrt `symbols` → **msvcrt.dll exports NO _byteswap_*** (objdump -p, positive control memcpy/_msize/puts) → the eager-import crashed the loader 0xC0000139 STATUS_ENTRYPOINT_NOT_FOUND end-to-end (the windows.json InterlockedCompareExchange lesson, now twice-proven). WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/intrin_barrier` (pe64-only corpus: include + 2 barriers straddling global stores → 42, release/shippedPipeline arm — witnesses the compile+run chain; single-threaded reordering is unobservable so the ORDERING contract is pinned structurally) + `Cse.LoadNotCsedAcrossCompilerBarrier` (the consumer pin, same-block slice: instructionsCsed==0, both Loads survive, the barrier survives; RED-on-disable: drop CompilerBarrier from opcodeClobbersMemory → CSE folds → fails) + `MirAlias.RegionWalkTreatsMemoryClobberOpsAsClobber` (the chokepoint pin: positive fenced-region=true + the DOUBLY-load-bearing negative control [non-aliasing Store + a Return TERMINATOR]=false — keying on hasSideEffects instead reds the negative half). Re-probe: sqlite pe64 with ONLY `SQLITE_OMIT_SEH` → 0 errors + sqlite3.exe RUNS (--version 3.54.0 + CRUD→62); `SQLITE_DISABLE_INTRINSIC` DROPPED from real-examples/c/sqlite/build-and-test.sh (comment updated; SQLITE_OMIT_SEH stays — the SEH cycle is next). | (closed — intrin.h + the compiler barrier; the define dropped) | p19 Cluster G c113; reuses the [[D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-UMULH]] c103 seam; audit-F1 hardened the shared clobber walk for [[D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-ATOMIC-CAS]]'s Call/AtomicCas class too; `semantic_config.hpp`/`c-subset.lang.json`/`hir_to_mir.cpp`/`mir_opcode.hpp`/`mir_to_lir.cpp`/`opt/analysis/mir_alias.hpp`/`shippedLibs/intrin.json`/`real-examples/c/sqlite/build-and-test.sh`; plan 23 SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-BSWAP` | ⏳ **DEFERRED (opened c113, 2026-07-05; trigger-gated) — the MSVC `_byteswap_ushort/_byteswap_ulong/_byteswap_uint64` byte-reverse intrinsics are NOT declared anywhere (not builtins, not descriptor symbols).** WHY: sqlite's only references sit under `SQLITE_BYTEORDER==1234 && MSVC_VERSION>=1300` fast paths that DSS's config `#if`'s OUT (the portable byte-by-byte `#else` is taken — objdump-confirmed: the running sqlite3.exe contains no byteswap), so a builtin lowering would be a consumer-less speculative build (the §A.2 violation in the other direction); and they CANNOT ship as msvcrt descriptor `symbols` — msvcrt.dll exports NO `_byteswap_*` (objdump-verified c113; a draft that declared them crashed the pe loader 0xC0000139, the eager-import-of-a-non-export trap, twice-proven). The audit adjudicated this (B) over msvcrt-externs (not exports; not agnostic) and pure-expansion (MIR bloat): 1 MIR op, native where the ISA has it, fallback where it doesn't. Corpus must execute the x86 width-16 fallback AND arm64 REV16 (the audit-F2 completeness rule). Priority: trigger-gated. | (open) | p19 c113 (opened by [[D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-BARRIER]]'s probe); the c103 [[D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-UMULH]] seam is the template; `shippedLibs/intrin.json` (the header the calls would come through); plan 23 |
| `D-CSUBSET-BITCOUNT-INTRINSICS` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-15 (FC17.9(b) walking skeleton) — the 3 hardware bit-count primitives (Popcount/Clz/Ctz) as 6 GCC-compat builtins: the C23 `<stdbit.h>` substrate the 14 `stdc_*` ops layer on.** MECHANISM (mirrors the c103 `__umulh` seam exactly, config-driven + agnostic — NO arch/name branch): (1) 3 leaf `BuiltinLowering` enumerators (`Popcount`/`Clz`/`Ctz`, semantic_config.hpp) resolved from config `lowering` strings `"popcount"`/`"clz"`/`"ctz"`; (2) 6 always-on `builtinFunctions` in c-subset.lang.json — `__builtin_popcount(U32)->I32` / `__builtin_popcountll(U64)->I32` + `_clz`/`_clzll` + `_ctz`/`_ctzll` (the width lives in the param core, read by the arm); (3) 3 pure-unary `MirOpcode` Popcount/Clz/Ctz `{1,1,R::Value}` (mir_opcode.hpp — like UMulH: non-terminator, no side effect, NOT commutative/mem-clobbering/const-folded); (4) 3 hir_to_mir arms map BuiltinCall→the MIR op (result I32; the count ≤P≤64 has zero upper bits so it reads narrow with NO Trunc); (5) `lowerPopcount`/`lowerClz`/`lowerCtz` (mir_to_lir) capability-probed at the OPERAND width P∈{32,64}: NATIVE where the target declares the mnemonic (x86 POPCNT/LZCNT/TZCNT; arm64 CLZ + RBIT-then-CLZ for ctz) else a branchless SWAR bit-trick over the universal ALU verbs (Hacker's-Delight popcount; clz = P − popcount(smear); ctz = popcount(~x & (x−1))) — the choice reads `opcode(MnemonicSlot::…Native)`, NEVER `if(arch==)`; (6) new MnemonicSlots PopcountNative/ClzNative/CtzNative/Rbit + x86 `popcount`/`clz`/`ctz` (F3 0F B8/BD/BC, fpcvt-shaped, width64→rexW) + arm64 `clz`(0xDAC01000/0x5AC01000)/`rbit`(0xDAC00000/0x5AC00000, data-proc-1-source rd/rn) target-JSON encodings; (7) 2 agnostic helpers — `emitWideConstToFresh` (correct 64-bit masks: x86 imm64 LiteralPool / arm64 MOVK ladder; emitBareConstToFresh truncates >imm32 on x86) + `emitShiftConst` (constant-amount shift mirroring lowerShift's imm/CL/reg-reg capability rules, since shifts are the one ALU family not shape-uniform across ISAs). Clz/Ctz DEFINED at 0 = width (LZCNT/CLZ/TZCNT semantics) — a SAFE SUPERSET of GCC's UB-at-0. WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/builtin_bitcount` (all 6 builtins through fold-resistant wrapper fns, width-discriminating inputs → exit 158; release/shippedPipeline arm proves the ops survive inlining — like UMulH they are NOT ConstFold-folded; the arm64-elf/qemu arm is the RUNTIME closure for BOTH the SWAR popcount AND native CLZ / RBIT+CLZ ctz) + x86 byte-pins {POPCNT F3 [48] 0F B8 C0, LZCNT …BD, TZCNT …BC × 32/64} + arm64 byte-pins {CLZ 83 10 C0 DA/5A, RBIT 83 00 C0 DA} + a MIR pin (each builtin → its op, not a Call) + a mir_to_lir native pin + the ★ SWAR RED-ON-DISABLE pin (drop the `popcount` mnemonic → Popcount lowers to the SWAR mul+and+sub, NOT fail-loud, NOT a popcount op — proves the fallback is real). The 4 semantic symbol-count pins +6'd (the 6 always-on builtins). | (closed — the 3 primitives; the 14 stdc_* ops are the next layer) | FC17.9(b); mirrors the [[D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-UMULH]] c103 seam; `semantic_config.hpp`/`c-subset.lang.json`/`hir_to_mir.cpp`/`mir_opcode.hpp`/`mir_to_lir.cpp`/`{x86_64,arm64}.target.json`; plan 23 FC17.9(b) → full-C23 `<stdbit.h>` |

| `D-FULLC-STDBIT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-15 (FC17.9(b)) — the WHOLE C23 `<stdbit.h>` (N3096 §7.18): all 14 type-generic bit operations × 5 concrete widths + the 4-way generic form + the endian/version macros, composed over the proven [[D-CSUBSET-BITCOUNT-INTRINSICS]] substrate (NO new MIR op, NO arch/format branch).** MECHANISM (config-driven + agnostic): (1) 14 leaf `BuiltinLowering` enumerators `StdcLeadingZeros…StdcBitCeil` (semantic_config.hpp) resolved from the config `lowering` strings `stdc_*`; (2) 56 always-on `builtinFunctions` in c-subset.lang.json — `__builtin_stdc_<op>_<T>` for the 14 ops × {uc→U8,us→U16,ui→U32,ull→U64}, result cores U32 (the 11 count/index/width ops) / Bool (has_single_bit) / the operand core (bit_floor+bit_ceil); (3) ONE shared hir_to_mir composer `emitStdbitOp` the 14 arms route through — reads the operand's EXACT width W∈{8,16,32,64} from its param core (mir.instType), zero-extends to the promotion width P∈{32,64}, and emits the exact §7.18 formula from Popcount/Clz/Ctz + universal ALU verbs (ZExt/Sub/Add/And/Or/Not/Shl/ICmp/Mul), binding the operand ONCE (single-eval) and BRANCHLESS (the 8 `?:` ops select via `mask = 0 − (uP)cond`); ★ every `Shl(1,amt)` is SHIFT-UB-SAFE — bit_floor `amt = bitWidth − (x≠0)`, bit_ceil `amt = bitWidth(x−1) & (W−1)` + the ==W overflow→0 select (audit I3); (4) `shippedLibs/stdbit.json` (the ONLY user-facing surface — `stdc_*` appear on include only): 56 concrete `stdc_<op>_uc/us/ui/ull(x)` macros + 14 per-FORMAT `stdc_<op>_ul(x)` variants (pe→`_ui` / elf+macho→`_ull`, the LP64/LLP64 divergence) + 14 4-WAY `stdc_<op>(x)` `_Generic` (unsigned long DROPPED — audit C1: it width-collapses onto `_ui`(LLP64)/`_ull`(LP64), so a 5-way is S_GenericSelectionAmbiguous, but the 4-way routes an `unsigned long` operand CORRECTLY by that same collapse; each assoc carries the FULL builtin call — the firstNameToken Area-5 mitigation) + 4 object macros (`__STDC_VERSION_STDBIT_H__ 202311L` + `__STDC_ENDIAN_{LITTLE,BIG,NATIVE}__` = 1234/4321/1234). WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/c23_stdbit` (all 14 ops through fold-resistant wrapper fns across uc/us/ui/ull + the generic form + the per-format `_ul` + edge inputs 0/1/all-ones/single-high-bit → exit **168** HAND-DERIVED from §7.18, NOT from DSS; release/shippedPipeline arm proves the ops survive inlining; the arm64-elf/qemu arm is the differential vs native x86 that catches a width/clz-ctz bug) + 7 MIR-shape pins (leading_zeros→Clz, count_ones→Popcount, bit_width→Clz+Sub, has_single_bit→Popcount+ICmpEq/Bool, first_leading_one→Clz+select, bit_ceil→Clz+Shl+And, ★ bit_floor SHIFT-CLAMP red-on-disable: the Shl amount MUST be `Sub(bitWidth, ZExt(ICmpNe))` not a bare const-1) + the `_Generic` dispatch pins on BOTH LP64 & LLP64 (no ambiguity for uc/us/ui/ul/ull + `unsigned long`→U64/U32 routing). The 4 semantic symbol-count pins +56'd (17u→73u / 18u→74u). | (closed — all 14 ops × widths + generic + macros) | FC17.9(b); layers on [[D-CSUBSET-BITCOUNT-INTRINSICS]]; `semantic_config.hpp`/`c-subset.lang.json`/`hir_to_mir.cpp`/`shippedLibs/stdbit.json` + `examples/c-subset/c23_stdbit`; opens [[D-FULLC-STDBIT-BIG-ENDIAN-NATIVE]] + [[D-FULLC-STDBIT-ADDRESSABLE-FN]]; plan 23 FC17.9(b) |
| `D-FULLC-STDBIT-BIG-ENDIAN-NATIVE` | ⏳ **DEFERRED (opened FC17.9(b), 2026-07-15; trigger-gated) — `__STDC_ENDIAN_NATIVE__` is hardcoded to `__STDC_ENDIAN_LITTLE__` (1234) in stdbit.json, since every DSS target (x86_64, arm64 in its LE configuration) is little-endian → NATIVE == LITTLE.** A C program observes ONLY the equality relation (`#if __STDC_ENDIAN_NATIVE__ == __STDC_ENDIAN_BIG__`), which is correct today. **Trigger:** a big-endian target lands (e.g. a BE MIPS/PowerPC/s390x port). CLOSING WORK: endianness is per-TARGET, not per-object-format, so it is NOT expressible via stdbit.json's format-keyed `when` variants today — it needs a per-target macro channel (a `.target.json` predefined-macro surface, or an endianness knob threaded into the shipped-macro splice). Until then a BE target would report the wrong NATIVE. Priority: trigger-gated (no BE target on the roadmap). | (open) | FC17.9(b) (opened by [[D-FULLC-STDBIT]]); `shippedLibs/stdbit.json` endian macros; plan 23 |
| `D-FULLC-STDBIT-ADDRESSABLE-FN` | ⏳ **DEFERRED (opened FC17.9(b), 2026-07-15; trigger-gated) — the concrete `stdc_<op>_T` are function-like MACROS (→ `__builtin_stdc_*` intrinsics), so `&stdc_leading_zeros_ui` (taking the address of a stdbit function) does not yield a function pointer.** C23 §7.18.1p4 permits an implementation to define these as macros OR functions; the mainstream-observable surface (calling them) is fully covered, and no real consumer takes their address (the intrinsics are not linkable symbols). **Trigger:** a program takes `&stdc_*` or passes one as a function-pointer argument. CLOSING WORK: emit a real addressable thunk function per `stdc_*_T` (a thin wrapper calling the builtin) into the shipped surface, OR expose the builtins as addressable. Priority: trigger-gated. | (open) | FC17.9(b) (opened by [[D-FULLC-STDBIT]]); `shippedLibs/stdbit.json`; plan 23 |

MECHANISM (the plan-lock design-audit's PLAN-SOUND-WITH-CORRECTIONS shape): `ShippedField.offset` (all-or-none per struct, decode fail-loud) → the offsets ride a PARALLEL side-table channel `CompositeFields.fieldOffsets` (NOT the scalar pool — that IS the bitfield-presence signal `!scalars().empty()` at 3 sites + reintern decodes it as widths [audit F1]) → enter `contentDeclSiteKey` (guarded on non-empty → pre-c107 structs keep byte-identical declSiteKeys, ZERO churn) so the shipped `structs` entry AND the bare typedef's inline `struct "X" { T @off }` canonicalize to ONE TypeId (the tag/typedef collapse .member scope keys on — audit item 8 VERIFIED same TypeId) → `computeLayout` Struct arm places offsets verbatim (may overlap; size=max extent, align=max field align) + rejects bitfield+offset fail-loud → member access already reads `layout->fieldOffsets[i]` (a PURE layout change, no new codegen path) → type-text `@off` PARSE (field-local loop, all-or-none) + EMIT + reintern carry (round-trip identity) → 2 fail-loud guards (brace-init hir_to_mir + static-encode asm — the overlap silent-miscompile trap sqlite never hits; by-value COPY is exempt, re-writes coherent bytes). ★ PROBE: the 2 pe64 QuadPart errors CLEARED, lower-mir runs both TUs (a NEW previously-masked blocker surfaced = H0009 shell.c:160 `__iob_func()[N]` striding zero-size opaque FILE = c109). WITNESS: 4 unit pins — TypeLayout.ExplicitOffsetsOverlapAndSizeIsMaxExtent (size 8, offsets {0,0,4}; red-on-disable: drop @4 → derive → size 16) + ExplicitOffsetsWithBitfieldsFailsLoud (the guard, GnuPacked-realized so only c107 rejects) + ShippedLibDescriptor.RealWindowsUlargeOverlayLayout (the REAL windows.json through the layout engine) + ParseTypeFromText.ExplicitFieldOffsetsParseAndForkIdentity + HirText.ExplicitFieldOffsetsRoundTrip (emit→parse→emit byte-identical). ★ NO RUNNABLE CORPUS (deliberate, audit item 10 confirmed SUFFICIENT): the mechanism is a pure LAYOUT-value change with no new runtime codegen path (member access = the same Gep(base,off)+Load/Store every struct uses); an overlap struct is only descriptor-reachable (ULARGE is pe-only, pe can't link/run until the link walls); a cross-target corpus would need a test-only descriptor polluting shipped configs for zero added coverage over the layout+identity+roundtrip pins — the skill's MIR-tier-no-runtime-difference carve-out; the runtime witness rides sqlite3.exe at the link cycle. 3-leg gate GREEN 556³ (the core type-system change affects ALL targets; no TypeId churn — AllShippedDescriptorsDecode + every layout/semantic test green). AUDIT SOUND-WITH-FIXES (a3415905b, 0 CRITICAL/HIGH/MED — the FIRST clean audit of the arc; 1 LOW folded = the no-churn comment made literally true via the non-empty guard; 1 INFO folded = the lowerAggregateCopy exemption documented). USER §B chose this over a full `unions` block + anon-member flattening. | (closed — the descriptor offset field) | p19 Cluster G c107; `type_interner.hpp`/`type_lattice.cpp`/`type_layout.cpp`/`type_reintern.cpp`/`hir_text.cpp`/`shipped_lib_descriptor.{hpp,cpp}`/`hir_to_mir.cpp`/`asm.cpp`/`windows.json`; plan 23 SQLite-green Windows leg | ORIGINAL DEFERRAL: ⏳ **DEFERRED → c107 (trigger FIRED by the c106 probe) — shipped-descriptor UNION/OVERLAY records: ULARGE_INTEGER's real windows.h shape is `union { struct { DWORD LowPart; DWORD HighPart; }; ULONGLONG QuadPart; }` — shell.c's fileTimeToUnixTime WRITES both parts then READS .QuadPart (the FILETIME→u64 idiom, sqlite shell.c ~24555-24565). The descriptor `structs` block derives offsets positionally (no union kind, no explicit offsets); DSS's TypeKind::Union layout puts EVERY member at offset 0 (type_layout.cpp:368) so a flat union cannot place HighPart@4 — the real shape needs the anonymous-struct-in-union + C11 6.7.2.1p13 member flattening, or descriptor-side explicit per-field offsets (the c95 OVERLAPPED flatten precedent GENERALIZED — c95 flattened because os_win's accesses didn't overlap; here they DO). ★ USER §B DECISION (2026-07-04): chose candidate (a) — the descriptor `offset` field (FFI-boundary-honest, minimal, agnostic; member access already flows through computeLayout's fieldOffsets so overlapping offsets 'just work'; the offsets ride the type-text `struct "X" { T @off, ... }` so the bare typedef + the structs-block entry canonicalize to the SAME offset-bearing TypeId — else the tag/typedef TypeId forks and .member scope breaks). Plan-lock design-audit IN PROGRESS. Candidates were: (a) descriptor field `offset` key → an overlay STRUCT with explicit offsets (FFI-boundary-honest — a descriptor documents a foreign record's MEMORY LAYOUT; small, agnostic; the bitfield scalars-payload precedent shows per-field metadata can drive layout); (b) full descriptor `unions` block + language anonymous-member flattening (max fidelity; a big language feature); (c) reject — config-only modeling cannot express overlapping members (proven: {LowPart,HighPart} covers the 4 writes, QuadPart reads fail loud S0001 ×2 — the DELIBERATE c106 residual). CURRENT STATE: windows.json ULARGE_INTEGER = struct {LowPart u32, HighPart u32} (writes compile; QuadPart loud). **Trigger:** FIRED — the pe64 2-TU compile's LAST 2 errors. | `D-PERF-RESOLVER-SIBLING-WALK` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-03 (p19 Cluster G c97) — the ~102× compile-time win: `TreeCursor::gotoNextSibling`/`gotoPrevSibling` O(n²) linear self-search → O(log K) VERIFIED binary search.** USER perf pivot (2026-07-03): sqlite compile ~15min/target vs gcc seconds. c97 added per-phase `--time` timers (permanent, pipeline-verb names, agnostic) → MEASURED (WSL Release, sqlite 2-TU x86_64:elf64): total **15m29s, of which `resolve-imports` = 15m18.7s = 99.0%**; 291/294 stack samples in `TreeCursor::gotoNextSibling` under `ConfigDrivenImportResolver::resolveIncludeFollowing` → the resolver's `walkPreOrder` over sqlite's ~10⁵-direct-child amalgamation root did an O(K) linear self-search per sibling = O(K²). **★ The 2026-06-29 `.temp/sqlite-build-perf-root-cause.md` PARSER attribution (no-memo speculative PEG, unordered_map lookups) is REFUTED for this tree: parse=0.72s + oracle-reparse=0.91s ≈ 1.6s, not minutes; the parser was never the wall** (the doc was INFERRED, no phase timer existed). FIX: `siblingIndexOf` locates self via `lower_bound` on the ascending-by-construction NodeId order, **VERIFIED (`kids[lo]==self`) with a LINEAR FALLBACK** on a miss (a hypothetical future unsorted producer) → byte-identical, O(K²)→O(K log K). Secondary O(1)/load-time transforms (no identity branch): dense `vector<CompiledRule>` rule table (was unordered_map), FIRST/expected/predictive-prefix BITSETS (was linear ranges::find), precomputed altRuleBranches, per-depth scratch buffers, ScopeTree heterogeneous lookup — measured-minor on sqlite (parse already fast) but correct O(1). **MEASURED after: total 15m29s→9.09s (102×; audit a66365ce independently reproduced 824s→7.84s=105×); resolve-imports→0.42s.** WITNESS: behavior BYTE-IDENTICAL (impl: 3 corpus artifacts + sqlite diagnostics; audit: 18 diverse examples byte-identical + 98 sqlite diagnostics byte-identical + all-3-langs 550/550) + the permanent `--time` phase report (RED-on-disable pin `PhaseTimersRecordEveryPipelinePhase`) + 2 NEW fallback pins (`TreeCursor.SiblingSearchFallbackOn{Descending,Scrambled}ChildIds` — a RawTreeBuilder tree with non-ascending children span → navigation is correct ONLY via the linear fallback; RED-on-disable). 3-leg gate GREEN 550/550. AUDIT SOUND (binary-search edge-correct at first/last/only/absent + fallback fires for unsorted; agnostic; scope complete — both next+prev, no other O(K) cursor primitive). | (closed — the verified-binary-search + fallback in `siblingIndexOf`) | p19 Cluster G c97; USER perf pivot; refutes `.temp/sqlite-build-perf-root-cause.md`; `src/core/types/tree_cursor.cpp` + `src/core/substrate/phase_timers.{hpp,cpp}` + the grammar_schema dense-table/bitset transforms; the re-priced big-ticket options = [[D-PERF-BIG-TICKET-OPTIONS]]; detail in the c97 perf topic; plan 00 perf |
| `D-PERF-1-PREPROCESSOR` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-17 — macro-expander O(n²)→~linear; was DEFERRED (perf backlog #1, user-ordered 2026-07-03; do AFTER sqlite run-green all legs, per the user sequence run-green→preprocessor→CI→merge).** After c97 ([[D-PERF-RESOLVER-SIBLING-WALK]]) removed the 99% resolver wall, the sqlite 2-TU compile is 9.09s of which **preprocess = 6.21s (68%) = the new per-target FLOOR** — but UN-PROFILED. Closing work: MEASURE first (per the user's rule — add finer preprocess sub-timers or a sampling profile) to find whether it hides another O(n²)/O(K²) wall like the resolver, then fix it O(1)/O(n) (candidates: the c95 per-include descriptor-macro splice, `__has_include`/predefine re-eval, the 269k-line macro expansion). HIGHEST measured leverage for gcc-compatible per-target compile time. ★ **MEASURE-STEP DONE + ROOT-CAUSED 2026-07-17 (this cycle):** added agnostic preprocess sub-timers (splice/tokenize/expand `CompilePhase` sub-seams; red-on-disable pin `PhaseTimersRecordEveryPipelinePhase`). RE-MEASURED (`--time`, current HEAD): preprocess is **33% of the DEBUG compile (NOT the stale 68%)**, and within it the **macro-expand stage is ~75% (4.3s of ~5.7s)** — splice 807ms + tokenize 626ms are minor. Scaling probe CONFIRMS super-linear (67k→135k lines = 2× input → 3.9× expand time; ~O(n^1.6–2)). **ROOT CAUSE: `MacroExpander::expand` holds the whole token stream in a `std::vector in` and `spliceOver(in,i,i+1,repl)` does an O(n) mid-vector splice PER macro expansion → O(n²)** (the macro table is already a hash map — not the culprit). **REMAINING FIX (next cycle, design-audited per Step 3.5 — correctness-critical, byte-preserving): restructure the expander core away from mid-vector splicing** (a work-list / token-source-stack so each expansion is O(repl)), preserving hideset propagation + rescan-across-boundary + the ahead-reading `collectArgs`/`nextSignificant`. ★ **DESIGN DETERMINED + INDEPENDENT DESIGN-AUDIT (GO-WITH-FIXES) 2026-07-17:** the fix = a **front-consumed `std::deque<ExpToken>`** (the loop already consumes `in` front-to-back + splices only AT the cursor `i`; the audit VERIFIED in-code the "cursor never reads behind itself, i≡front at splice" invariant) — pop_front non-macro→out, expansion = pop_front the consumed range + push_front the replacement REVERSED; `collectArgs`/`nextSignificant` stay FORWARD-indexed (deque[j] vs span[j]) so their logic is byte-identical. Chosen over (a) reversed-vector-as-stack [error-prone reverse-indexing in a correctness-critical spot] + (b) source-span-stack [biggest rewrite] — deque is the MOST AUDITABLE. **4 audit fixes folded into the build plan:** (1) MUST enumerate the arityBad `i=past` path (:2994) as emit-name-then-discard-to-`past` [a silent-miscompile seam if left a no-op]; (2) MUST copy/move `front()`→out BEFORE pop_front + read all of `t` before any splice [UAF ordering]; (3) keep `expand`'s by-value `vector` param, convert to a local deque internally [callers + the `expand(arg,depth+1)` recursion untouched]; (4) STRICT TEST = a deterministic token-move COUNTER (`≤ k·N`, red-on-disable by reverting to the vector splice — NOT a flaky wall-clock ratio) + an arityBad-tail correctness pin + the existing byte-identical macro/sqlite suite green. ★ **✅ FIXED 2026-07-17 (this cycle):** the front-consumed `std::deque<ExpToken>` restructure of `MacroExpander::expand` LANDED — pop_front non-macro→out; expansion = pop_front the consumed range + push_front the replacement REVERSED; `collectArgs`/`nextSignificant` deque-forward-indexed (logic byte-identical). The 4 audit fixes folded: arityBad emit-name-then-discard-to-`past` (pin `ArityMismatchDropsMalformedCallKeepsTrailingTokens`), UAF front-copy `ExpToken t = work.front()`, by-value `vector` param → local deque (callers + recursion untouched), `tokenMoves_` effectiveness counter (pin `DPerf1MacroPassTokenMovesStayLinear` = exactly 2·N). INDEPENDENT Step-8.5 code-audit **VERIFIED-CLEAN** (all 6 seams traced in-code: push_front-REVERSAL correct, arityBad pops exactly `past`, UAF copy, depth>256 backstop returns the vector BEFORE conversion, front-relative indexing, old vector overload fully removed). Gate: full ctest **679/679 (Win + WSL** — the byte-identical macro suite is the correctness proof) + **preprocess-expand 4.29s→3.26s (~24%, the O(n²)→~linear win)** + sqlite compiles clean + anchor guard. | (✅ CLOSED — the macro-expander O(n²) killed; residual preprocess = linear substitution/re-tokenize) | p19 c97 measured; #1 of the 4 user-ordered perf levers; [[D-PERF-RESOLVER-SIBLING-WALK]]; c97 perf topic; plan 00 perf |
| `D-PERF-2-TYPEDEF-SEED-DISAMBIGUATION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-21 — the first-parse typedef seed kills the finish() FULL-FILE oracle reparse (MEASURED gone on sqlite: `phase reparse 0ms / 0 runs`).** Seed the FIRST parse's binder sketch with the LIVE shipped-descriptor typedef NAMES so `(size_t)(expr)` commits as a CAST on parse 1 (no AmbiguousTypeNameCandidate → no reparse). Opt 3 (additive, EMIT-ONLY `PreprocessResult::resolvedShippedDescriptors` → `parseAndAdd_` harvests typedef names into `ParserConfig::seedGlobalTypeNames`; the finish() reparse RETAINED as the residual net for in-buffer FORWARD refs). ★ **§B RESOLVED (user 2026-07-21) = the seed set == the finish() oracle's authoritatively-live `shippedLibDescriptors`** so it is a PURE behavior-preserving "resolve-earlier" optimization (never resolves a name the reparse would not): record EVERY angle-`#include` splice UN-GATED (`D-PP-PRESCAN-ANGLE-MACRO-SPLICE-AUTHORITATIVE-LIVENESS`) + its synth-buffer offset, then DROP any whose offset lies in an authoritative `MacroExpander::deadRanges()` region; `__has_include` probes do NOT seed. | `D-CSUBSET-ADDRESS-CONSTANT-FOLD` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-29 (p19 Cluster G c43) — compile-time `offsetof` folding via a general ADDRESS-CONSTANT value kind (Option A).** ROOT (sqlite `struct uKey { u8 keyinfoSpace[SZ_KEYINFO_0]; }` @sqlite3.c:24984, SZ_KEYINFO_0=offsetof(KeyInfo,aColl) → S001C): DSS's CST const-evaluator (folds ARRAY DIMENSIONS at Pass 1.5) was integer-only and could not fold the portable offsetof idiom `((size_t)((char*)&((T*)0)->M - (char*)0))`, so an array sized by offsetof degraded to an incomplete-array FAM → S000B (plain) / S001C (sole member). §B RESOLVED by the user → Option A (a GENERAL address constant, NOT a null-only special-case): add `HirAddressValue{base(0=NULL),byteOffset,pointeeType}` to the HirLiteralValue variant (mirrors F5's MirSymbolAddrValue so toMirLiteral is a field copy) + teach the CST evaluator the address arms — `(T*)0`→{NULL,0}; `->M` adds the field's computeLayout offset; `&` passes the lvalue address through; `(char*)` retypes; `- (char*)0` collapses to the integer byte offset; `(size_t)`/`(unsigned long)` casts it. Two interner-backed closures (resolveCastTarget = classify the cast type incl. integer width/signedness for C-correct narrowing; resolveFieldOffset = compositeScopeByType→memberScope→fieldIndex + computeLayout.fieldOffsets[idx] + a bit-field guard) keep the engine interner-free; cast/postfix rules added to the wrapperChild isDispatched set so they reach the arms. ★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT (36 min, 120 probes, a PRE-c43-binary diff): the fold is CORRECT + REGRESSION-FREE — value-correct across 11 shapes incl. alignment + nested members + CROSS-ABI REAL RUNS (PE/ELF-WSL/arm64-qemu exit 42 on the exact sqlite KeyInfo-aColl@24 shape); 12 fail-loud cases (`(T*)5` non-null base / bit-field member / runtime-var dim / `(int)&g` symbol-addr / null deref / index → S000B/S000C); narrowing C-correct ((char)300→44); no silent miscompiles; no regression (casts didn't fold pre-c43). ★ The audit's lone over-attestation (`(size_t)` "parses as a call, doesn't fold") was a PROBE ARTIFACT (size_t UNDEFINED in the probe) — VERIFIED `(size_t)` FOLDS when size_t is a typedef (as sqlite has via <stddef.h>; sizet_form→exit 4); RESOLVED by STRENGTHENING the corpus with the canonical `(size_t)` form. ★ SCOPE: the value kind is the GENERAL Option-A kind (symbol-or-null base) but the CST engine PRODUCES only null-base (offsetof) this cycle; the &global/string-addr folding + the F5 unification (the symbol-base USE) → DEFERRED `D-CSUBSET-ADDRESS-CONSTANT-GLOBAL-FOLD` (c44). WITNESS: corpus `offsetof_const` (sole-member structs sized by offsetof — alignment {char;double}→8, multi-field, the sqlite uKey shape, BOTH `(unsigned long)` + `(size_t)` cast forms → exit 42; RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the address arms → S001C). 464→465 ctest. | `D-CSUBSET-TERNARY-NULL-POINTER` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-29 (p19 Cluster G c49) — a conditional `cond ? 0 : ptr` (or `cond ? ptr : 0`) has the POINTER type (C 6.5.15p6).** ROOT (the c48 re-probe linux FIRST error, after S0003 Bool→char cleared): sqlite `return n<=0 ? 0 : sqlite3Malloc(n);` (sqlite3.c:31769) fired error[S0008] S_ReturnTypeMismatch. `combineTernary` computes the result via `commonArithType(thenT,elseT)` which returns Invalid for `0(int) : Ptr` (a pointer has no int rank), then FALLS THROUGH to the FIRST arm (I32) → the leading `0` SHADOWS the pointer arm → `return <ternary>` (typed I32) mismatches the fn's pointer return. The asymmetry localized it: `n>0 ? gp : 0` does NOT fail (then-arm=Ptr → fallthrough returns Ptr). Same root cause at BOTH tiers (semantic subtreeType:5508 + HIR cst_to_hir:2679) and across contexts (return→S0008; assign/call-arg→S0003). FIX (NOT §B; REUSES the existing null-ptr-const model — `isLiteralIntegerZero` + flag `nullPointerConstantFromIntegerZero` + the HIR coerce null-ptr arm cst_to_hir.cpp:465): a coordinated TWO-TIER rule — BEFORE the first-arm fallthrough, if one arm is `TypeKind::Ptr` and the OTHER arm is a literal-0, the conditional type = the POINTER arm's type. WITNESS: corpus `ternary_null_pointer` (`void *f(int n){return n<=0?0:gp;}` + reverse `n>0?gp:0`, n runtime via pick(), f(5)==&obj + f(-1)==0 → 42; RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the arms → S0008). 470→471 ctest, 3-LEG gate. ★ SCOPE: ternary/null-ptr ONLY; the sibling re-probe S0003s (`buf`/`"-Inf`/`"unistr('`/`x` — string-literal/identifier pointer mismatches; root-cause verified string-literal ternaries do NOT S0003; a `const char*`-arg case → H0009) are SEPARATE next frontiers. | (closed — the two-tier combineTernary null-ptr arm IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c49; the sqlite frontier after c48 (S0003→S0008); REUSES `D-LANG-NULL-POINTER-CONSTANT` (isLiteralIntegerZero / admitsNullPointerConstant / the HIR coerce arm) extended to the C 6.5.15p6 ternary type-balance; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (combineTernary :5508) + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (combineTernary :2679); a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |

NOT the static-forward-prototype (a RED HERRING — that compiles clean; the agent's H1-H4 all rejected). FIX (NOT §B; the c26 function-call sibling of the `(c*c)`/`(b[0])` abstract-declarator exclusions): the parser cast-vs-value triage `typeNameCommitApproved_` (parser.cpp Rule 1 ~864) committed on ANY >1-leaf type-child; an IDENTIFIER base with an abstract-declarator tail (`f(p)`, `name*`) is still type-vs-value AMBIGUOUS — so Rule 1 now keys on the BASE (leftmost) leaf: an identifier base falls through to rules 2-4 (sketch lookup on the base name + the follower-operator test, which sees the trailing `+`/`&`/`*` and rolls back to the call reading); a keyword/struct/star/const base keeps the unconditional commit. A grammar-only fnSuffix removal is INSUFFICIENT (falls to parenDeclarator → S0027); the triage is the locus. OVER-ADMISSION GUARD: a genuinely-undeclared callee still errors (rollback re-reads the call → checkCall reports S_UndeclaredIdentifier). WITNESS: corpus `paren_call_binop` (r=(((idn(35))+7)&~7)=40 the EXACT ROUND8 shape + s=((idn(20))+22)=42; red-on-disable → S0006; all 4 targets, pe RAN to 42 on Windows + the c26 cast tests stay green [11/11]). | (closed — the Rule 1 base-leaf discriminator) | p19 Cluster G c55; the c54 re-probe's S0006 frontier; the c26 cast-ambiguity sibling; `src/analysis/syntactic/parser.cpp` (`typeNameCommitApproved_`); a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |

FIX (NOT §B; a localized scan-scope correction, C 6.7.3 — an object's const derives from its own declaration, never a cast inside its initializer): added a `headNode` param; the scalar fallback now scans the type-specifier HEAD (`kids[*decl.headChild]`, base type, no pointer stars) instead of declNode — mirrors the legacy positional path's `kids[*decl.typeChild]` scoping.
