OVER-REJECTION GUARD (verified with the rebuilt compiler): a genuine `const int c=5; c=6;` STILL errors S0007 (its const lives in the HEAD); `char * const p` stays a const object (the c36 lastLayer path untouched); `const char *p` stays a mutable pointer. WITNESS: corpus `local_init_const_leak` (`int r=firstByte((const char*)buf)`=5 then `r=37+r`=42 → 42; red-on-disable → S0007; the const/volatile/mutable suite 16/16 stays green). | (closed — the headNode-scoped scalar fallback) | p19 Cluster G c58; the c57 re-probe's LAST compile error; the c29/c36 const-handling neighborhood; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (declaratorObjectIsConst); a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |

| `D-CSUBSET-SWITCH-NESTED-CASE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c60; §B approach I = flatten-to-one-CFG, USER-chosen) — `case`/`default` nested inside an inner block of a switch (C 6.8.1 jump-into-block).** ROOT (the c59 re-probe's FIRST error, S0023 S_CaseLabelNotInSwitch ×7): sqlite's "shared-tail with local declarations" idiom — a `case`/`default` nested INSIDE the brace-block opened by a PRECEDING case (braces forced by local decls): VDBE `case OP_SeekRowid: { VdbeCursor*pC; …; goto notExistsWithKey; } /*fall through*/ case OP_NotExists: …; notExistsWithKey: …` (sqlite3.c:101365) + the JSON parser `jsonTranslateTextToBlob` (`case '+': { …; case '.': …; case '-': …; case '0'..'9': …; parse_number: … }`, 11 cases + 3 labels in ONE block). DSS's FLAT-SWITCH model (lowerSwitch + the pumpSwitch driver grouped only TOP-LEVEL switchBodyItems into disjoint CaseArms; MIR minted one block per arm) could not represent a jump target inside another arm's body → the nested CaseStmt reached the general statement dispatch (cst_to_hir.cpp:3297) and failed loud. §B RESOLVED by the USER → approach (I): flatten the switch body to ONE CFG, case/default = labels at any depth. FIX (Design I-A): the HIR `SwitchStmt` becomes `[disc, body Block, dispatch arms]` — `CaseArm` repurposed as a dispatch entry (value? + a synthetic LABEL ORDINAL, no body); a per-switch prescan (`switchPrescanDispatch`, NOT descending into a nested switch) assigns every case/default a synthetic ordinal from the SHARED per-function `nextLabelOrdinal_` counter (named-label + case-label ordinals never collide; MIR's getOrCreateLabelBlock keys both); the body lowers as ONE flat Block where each case/default → a synthetic `LabelStmt` marker (reusing makeLabelStmt + the MIR label-block + goto jump-into-block machinery); both MIR Switch forms target those label-blocks via `addSwitch` (the EXISTING cascading-compare dispatch — NOT a jump-table, which DSS never had: [[D-OPT-SWITCH-JUMP-TABLE]]) + lower the flat body. PRESERVED: label-before-case ([[D-CSUBSET-LABEL-BEFORE-CASE]]), adjacent cases, fall-through (now straight-line), break→join, default-anywhere; S0023 STILL fires for a case genuinely OUTSIDE any switch (switchBodyDepth_ gating). `pathTerminates(SwitchStmt)` is now strictly more accurate (models fall-through). WITNESS: corpus `switch_nested_case` (the VDBE shared-tail `case 1:{int t;…goto shared;} case 2:…; shared:…` + a case inside a deeper if-block `case 3:{ if(k>0){ case 4:… } }`; classify(2)+classify(3)+classify(1)-8 = 32+6+12-8 = 42; RED-ON-DISABLE → S0023 on the nested case 2/case 4). ★★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT (the c57 lesson, all 3 RUN legs): x86_64-elf native + arm64-qemu both RAN to 42, every value gcc-cross-checked; the nested-case jump-into-block (dispatch DIRECTLY to a case past a decl inside an if-block → correct value), fall-through into/out of nested blocks, 8 edge cases (default-only/no-default/single/default-not-last/enum+char values/empty-body/Duff's-device/inner-loop-break-vs-switch-break), ordinal-collision + nested-switch-isolation + discriminant-once — ALL correct on both legs; no dead blocks in the final binary. ★ c60 clears the 7 S0023 ONLY — H0002 (continue-in-switch) is PRE-EXISTING and NOT cleared (a separate later cycle; the audit proved c59 has the identical H0002). | (closed — the flat-CFG switch lowering: HIR shape + oracle + driver + 2 MIR forms) | p19 Cluster G c60; §B approach-I USER-chosen; the c59 re-probe's S0023 frontier; reuses the goto/label infra (makeLabelStmt + getOrCreateLabelBlock) + addSwitch; `src/hir/hir_node.hpp` + `hir.{cpp,hpp}` + `hir_text.cpp` + `hir_verifier.hpp` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` + `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp`; a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-OPT-SWITCH-JUMP-TABLE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c70; §B USER-chosen jump-table over a minimal immediate-compare fix) — a dense `switch` lowers to an O(1) JUMP TABLE (was: a linear cascading-compare chain that, for a >255-case switch, ALSO exhausted the regalloc).** ROOT (the post-c69 re-probe compiled sqlite3.c END-TO-END → I_VerifierFailure operand count 349; with the verifier fix [[D-MIR-VERIFIER-UNBOUNDED-OPERAND-SENTINEL]] the switch reached LIR and EXHAUSTED the regalloc, L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc): sqlite's ~348-case VDBE opcode dispatch (sqlite3VdbeExec) — lowerSwitch's O(n) chain materialized EVERY case constant into a live register → ~348 vregs live from the switch header. FIX (opus impl + opus audit, LIR-tier, ZERO new opcodes): dense (caseCount>=8 && span<=3*caseCount && span<=2^20) → widen discrim (SExt/ZExt) + idx=discrim-minCase + ONE unsigned `cmp idx,span-1`+`jcc(Ugt) default` + off=idx*8 + lea table + indexed load + jmp_indirect; the table = an AssembledData in .data (Mach-O rejects reloc-bearing .rodata — the c67 placement), span*8, one abs64 reloc per slot to the target block's synthetic symbol (gaps->default), block symbols bound POST-assemble() via a new AssembledFunction::blockByteOffsets (emitted in compile_pipeline.cpp — NO dead per-target lea). Sparse/small → the compare chain but each small non-negative case is an IMMEDIATE `cmp discrim,imm` (window [0,4095]=arm64 imm12; wide/negative → register fallback) — this alone cures the pressure. WITNESS: 6 corpora (switch_vdbe_dispatch 350-dense=sqlite's shape [.data=2800=350*8], switch_jump_table_boundaries [first/last/GAP->default/below/above/negative-min], switch_wide_bound, switch_over_255_cases 300, switch_sparse_immediate, switch_unsigned_dense) → 42 all 4 targets gcc-cross-checked. ★★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT SOUND (the WRONG-CASE hunt, the control-flow analog of c69's wrong-slot): every discrim below-min->above-max for dense/gappy/negative/unsigned CHECKSUMMED vs gcc across 6 legs → ALL AGREE; objdump both arches = slot j->case(min+j), gaps->default, idx=discrim-min, NO off-by-one (VDBE slot0->case0 slot349->case349); Win 500/500 + WSL/qemu 315/315; red-on-disable reproduces L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc. | `D-CSUBSET-NARROW-SWITCH-DISCRIMINANT-CMP` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c78) — the switch controlling expression integer-PROMOTES to int at HIR (switchPrologue, reusing c71's integerPromotedType+coerce), so the dispatch cmp runs at ≥32-bit width with a SExt/ZExt'd operand. Was the 313× DOMINANT of the post-c77 sqlite encoder frontier (a LOWERING fix — neither x86 NOR arm64 has a sub-32 cmp). Witness `examples/c-subset/switch_subint_discriminant`=154 (RED-ON-DISABLE → A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant cmp width 8). Surfaced + confirmed PRE-EXISTING by the c70 audit; fail-loud, NOT c70.** A `switch` on a `char`/`short` (sub-32-bit) discriminant fails to compile on x86 + arm64: the dispatch emits `cmp` at the narrow discriminant width → `A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant: opcode 'cmp': no encoding variant matches at width 8` (the D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS family — no native narrow-width ALU form). PROVEN pre-existing: fails identically with c70's immediate-compare change reverted AND the jump table disabled (the old `cmp reg8,reg8` chain has no width-8 cmp either). Fail-loud (a compile error, never a silent miscompile). No corpus exercises a narrow-width switch discriminant. NOT confirmed in sqlite (sqlite switches on int opcodes/tokens). FIX (when needed): integer-PROMOTE the switch discriminant to int (C 6.8.4.2 — integer promotions apply to the controlling expression) before the dispatch cmp, so it runs at >=32-bit width. | (open) | discovered p19 c70 audit; the D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS family; `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.cpp` (lowerSwitch cmp width) OR the HIR switch-discriminant promotion; plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-MIR-VERIFIER-UNBOUNDED-OPERAND-SENTINEL` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c70) — the MIR verifier honors the variadic-operand sentinel (`maxOperands == kMirUnboundedOperands == 0xFF`) instead of reading it as a literal max of 255.** ROOT (the c69 re-probe's FIRST error, after sqlite3.c finally compiled end-to-end): `I_VerifierFailure` "opcode switch operand count 349 outside [1, 255]" — the c60 switch-flatten produces ONE MIR `Switch` opcode whose operands are `[discriminant, caseValue*N]`; sqlite's ~348-case VDBE dispatch (`sqlite3VdbeExec`) = 349 operands. `MirOpcode::Switch` (and `IndirectBr`) declare `maxOperands == kMirUnboundedOperands` (0xFF) — genuinely variadic — and the BUILDER (`mir.cpp` addInst) has always exempted that sentinel from the upper-bound check (`maxOperands != kMirUnboundedOperands && count > maxOperands`), so it accepted the 349-operand Switch. But `MirVerifier::checkStructuralInvariants` did a plain `operands.size() > info.maxOperands`, reading the 0xFF sentinel as a literal 255 → it REJECTED the Switch the builder had already built. FIX (1 edit, `src/mir/mir_verifier.cpp`): mirror the builder's exemption — `bool const overMax = info.maxOperands != kMirUnboundedOperands && operands.size() > info.maxOperands;` — so a variadic-operand opcode is not upper-bounded. WITNESS: corpus `switch_over_255_cases` (a 300-case switch → 301 operands > 255; classify(k)=1000+k for k in [0,299] else 7 → 42; RED-ON-DISABLE → I_VerifierFailure "operand count 301 outside [1, 255]"). This UN-MASKED the register-pressure exhaustion the jump table + immediate-compare chain fix ([[D-OPT-SWITCH-JUMP-TABLE]]). | (closed — the verifier's variadic-sentinel exemption, matching the builder) | p19 Cluster G c70; the c69 re-probe's FIRST error (sqlite finally compiled end-to-end); `src/mir/mir_verifier.cpp`; paired with the jump-table lowering; a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-CONTINUE-SKIPS-SWITCH` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c61) — `continue` inside a `switch` inside a loop targets the enclosing LOOP, not the switch (C 6.8.6.2).** ROOT (the c60 re-probe's FIRST error, H0002 H_InvalidBreak ×2): sqlite `sqlite3VXPrintf` (sqlite3.c:33119/33311) — a format-char loop whose body `switch`es on the conversion type, with `continue` inside the switch to advance the format string → "continue resolves to a switch; continue can only target a loop". `isBranchTargetKind` (hir_node.hpp:163) counts a SwitchStmt as a branch target, and a `continue`'s depth-0 resolution (makeContinue(0) — C has no labeled continue) landed on the innermost switch → the verifier (hir_verifier.cpp:193) rejected it. PRE-EXISTING (the c60 audit proved c59 has the identical H0002; c60's switch-flatten did NOT touch the continue model — it only un-masked the H0002 by clearing the S0023 wall). FIX (NOT §B; C 6.8.6.2 — a switch is a `break` target but TRANSPARENT to `continue`; 2 symmetric edits): (a) the verifier `checkBreakContinueScoping` drops SwitchStmt frames from a continue's `enclosingBranchTargets` list (`std::erase_if`) before the depth/range check — the loops-only index space, depth 0 = innermost loop; the old "resolves to a switch" rejection is gone (a continue with NO enclosing loop now fails the range check → "no enclosing loop"); (b) the MIR ContinueStmt lowering (hir_to_mir.cpp ~6211) walks the branchStack SKIPPING switch frames (a switch frame has continueBB invalid) to the depth-th loop frame. `break` is UNCHANGED (keeps the full loops+switches stack — a switch IS a break target). The verifier's loops-only view and the MIR's skip-invalid-continueBB walk drop the IDENTICAL frames (a SwitchStmt HIR node ⟺ a MIR frame with invalid continueBB), both depth 0 = innermost loop → consistent by construction. WITNESS: corpus `continue_in_switch` (a for-loop with `case 2: continue;` inside a switch; the continue must skip the post-switch `acc += 5` → 42, a wrong target [break out of switch] would yield 47; RED-ON-DISABLE → H0002). ★★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT all 3 RUN legs (x86_64-elf native + arm64-qemu + windows-pe, every value gcc-cross-checked): the corpus (42) + adversarial nesting [continue in while/do-while; nested switch-in-inner-loop-in-outer-loop → advances the INNER loop only; loop-in-switch-in-loop → targets the INNERMOST loop] + break-unchanged [break-in-switch=47, break-in-loop=15] + no-over-admission [continue-no-loop / bare-continue / cross-function still rejected] + arm64 objdump [the continue `b`-branches to the correct loop LATCH, not the post-switch code] — ALL correct; a faithful sqlite3VXPrintf miniature ran identically on gcc/DSS-x64/DSS-arm64. The audit ALSO caught a stale c60-committed unit test (`ContinueSkippingSwitchToOuterLoopIsValid` used makeContinue(1), the old depth-1-skips-switch model) → updated to makeContinue(0) (the real-C loops-only form — a better regression test). | (closed — the verifier erase-switches + the MIR skip-switch-frames continue resolution) | p19 Cluster G c61; the c60 re-probe's H0002 frontier (PRE-EXISTING, un-masked by the c60 switch-flatten clearing the S0023 wall); `src/hir/hir_verifier.cpp` + `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` + `tests/hir/test_hir_verifier.cpp`; a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-STRING-LITERAL-ARRAY-ZERO-FILL` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c62; §B Option A pad-at-producer, USER-chosen) — a string literal initializing a `char[N]` array zero-fills the trailing N−len bytes (C 6.7.9p14).** ROOT (the c61 re-probe's FIRST error, H_VerifierFailure ×4): sqlite's date/time `aXformType[]` table (sqlite3.c:26283) has a `char zName[7]` field initialized by string literals SHORTER than 7 (`"hour"`=Array<char,5>, `"day"`=4, `"month"`=6, `"year"`=5) → ConstructAggregate child[1] type Array<char,M> ≠ field Array<char,7> (the 6-char names fit [7] exactly via sameType + pass). DSS did not implement the zero-fill (the short string's Array<char,M> never widened to Array<char,N>). The SAME gap rejected the scalar `char x[7]="hi"` with S0003. §B RESOLVED by the USER → Option A (pad at the producer). FIX (3 tiers, admit⟺realize parity): (semantic) isAssignable gains a `charArrayFromStringLiteralInit`-gated arm admitting `char[N]<-char[M]` (N≥M, char both, string-literal init only) + `initIsStringLiteral` threaded through the scalar VarDecl sites; (HIR) `coerce` re-types the string Literal to Array<char,N> by minting a fresh Literal SHARING the literal-pool index (no double-decode); (MIR/asm) the global is recorded at the target Array<char,N> type + the asm string-literal arm ZERO-PADS the global's bytes to the type size N (the Option-A producer step — avoids the latent OOB the design was chosen to prevent: a short M-byte global + an N-byte aggregate-copy would read adjacent rodata as padding), + `lowerAggregateCopy` gains an Array arm + `encodeAggregateValue` an Array string-char arm (const-folded aggregate). EXACT-FIT (N==M) stays via sameType; OVER-LONG (N<M, `char[3]="hello"`) stays a loud S0003; `char *p="hi"` (POINTER) UNCHANGED (3-byte rodata). WITNESS: corpus `string_array_zero_fill` (the aXformType shape + a scalar `char s[7]="hi"`; byte-checks the padding == 0 AND the string bytes → 42; RED-ON-DISABLE → H_VerifierFailure / S0003). ★★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT all 3 RUN legs (the c57 lesson — the impl could only RUN pe; the audit closed arm64): the rodata global is BYTE-IDENTICAL N-byte zero-padded on x86_64 AND arm64 (`68 69 00 00 00 00 00`, NOT a short global + OOB), the corpus + repro RAN to 42/4 on x86_64-elf + arm64-qemu (gcc-cross-checked all 4), a 0x7f-neighbor probe confirmed the padding is genuinely ZERO at runtime on arm64, admit⟺realize parity + over-long-S0003 + char*-unchanged + scalar+aggregate ALL correct; the pre-existing mutable-char[N]-in-rodata bug confirmed NOT sqlite-critical (all 17 sqlite file-scope char[]=string globals are const). KNOWN-LIMITATION (fails LOUD, not silent; sqlite uses the bare form): brace-wrapped `char x[7]={"hi"}` → H_VerifierFailure [[D-CSUBSET-STRING-ARRAY-BRACED-INIT]]. ★ EXTENDED (C14, TF arc, 2026-07-18): the arm now admits a NARROW string literal into an array of ANY of the THREE C 6.2.5p15 character types — `char` / `signed char` (I8) / `unsigned char` (U8), not char-only. ROOT (sqlite testfixture re-probe): test_hexio `const unsigned char zHex[]="0123456789ABCDEF"` + test_func's `unsigned char` charmaps hit S0003 (scalar) / H_VerifierFailure (struct field) because the admit/realize/producer arms keyed on `TypeKind::Char` on BOTH sides. FIX (shared `isCharacterType` + `stringLiteralArrayInitCompatible` in type_rules.hpp, applied in LOCKSTEP): (admit) isAssignable; (realize) the cst_to_hir `coerce` arm retypes the `char[M]` literal to the lhs `<c>[N]` — identical bytes (all 3 char types are 1 byte); (producer) asm.cpp's AGGREGATE-member arm widened `Char`→`{Char,I8,U8}` (the STANDALONE-global producer was already element-agnostic via `scalarByteSize`). The struct-field form is covered BY-CONSTRUCTION through the same `coerce` chokepoint (no 4th site — INDEPENDENT code-audit VERIFIED-CLEAN, incl. no missed `TypeKind::Char` producer site + exact admit⟺realize⟺verify parity). Over-long (`uchar[3]="hello"`, N<M) + non-character (`int[]="abc"`) stay LOUD; the shared predicate's `lhs==rhs` disjunct also generalizes `wchar_t[N>M]=L"…"` zero-fill at the semantic tier (audit-confirmed byte-correct + GCC-consistent, no silent miscompile). WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/string_init_char_types` (local uchar/schar + a struct uchar field → byte + zero-fill checks → 42, release arm) + `test_type_rules` static_asserts + runtime pins (red-on-disable: pre-fix S0003/H_VerifierFailure → post-fix CLEAN, empirically captured). Gate: Win 682/682 + WSL x86_64 682/682 + qemu arm64 + sqlite amalgamation compiles clean (rc=0). CLEAN test TUs 29/44 (test_func S0003→S0001, test_hexio zHex-S0003 cleared). `src/analysis/semantic/type_rules.hpp` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` + `src/asm/asm.cpp`. | (closed — the 3-tier pad-at-producer: isAssignable arm + coerce retype + asm zero-pad; C14 widened all 3 tiers to every C 6.2.5p15 character type) | p19 Cluster G c62; §B Option A USER-chosen; the c61 re-probe's H_VerifierFailure frontier; `src/analysis/semantic/type_rules.hpp` + `semantic_analyzer.cpp` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` + `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` + `src/asm/asm.cpp`; a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |

| `D-CSUBSET-VA-LIST-PARAM-SLOT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c63; NOT §B) — a `va_list` PARAMETER consumed by `va_arg` needs a storage slot.** ROOT (the c62 re-probe's FIRST + ONLY error — sqlite's LAST compile error, H0009): sqlite `sqlite3_str_vappendf(sqlite3_str*, const char*, va_list ap)` (the vprintf/vappendf family) at `pArgList = va_arg(ap, PrintfArguments*)` → "symbol N has no storage slot (non-addressable param or unbound) — required by lvalue use" (hir_to_mir.cpp:2359). va_arg READS + ADVANCES the va_list in place (lvalue use), so `ap` needs a slot; a va_list PARAM was received as a pure-SSA Arg (a LOCAL va_list is slot-backed by its VarDecl — only the PARAM form failed). PER-TARGET va_list ABI (agnosticism bar — config/strategy-driven, NO arch branch): SysVRegisterSave x86_64 = Array `__va_list_tag[1]`; HomogeneousPointer Win64/Apple = `char*` (Ptr<I8>); Aapcs64DualCursor arm64 = `__va_list` struct (already slot-backed by receiveByValueParam — unchanged). FIX (2 edits, hir_to_mir.cpp): (a) collectAddressTakenSymbols marks a va_arg/va_start/va_end operand address-taken → routes the Win64/Apple `char*` param onto the addressTaken-scalar arm (alloca + store) BY USAGE (its Ptr<I8> type is indistinguishable from a plain char*); (b) a dedicated reception arm — a SysV `va_list` param's incoming GPR is a POINTER to the CALLER's tag (C 6.7.6.3p7 array-param adjustment), so register that pointer AS the symbol's address (NO alloca, NO 24-byte copy — a copy reads a stale tag = a silent miscompile; mirrors receiveByValueParam's ByReference precedent). ★★ the FORWARDING half is [[D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-AT-CALL-ARG]] (needed TOGETHER for the vappendf family). WITNESS: corpus `va_list_param_forward` (a va_list-PARAM callee + a `...`-forwarder that va_start's + forwards; 10+11+12+9 = 42; red→H0009). ★★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT all 3 RUN legs (the c57 lesson + this cycle's near-miss): the FIRST audit CAUGHT a silent SysV SIGSEGV (the forwarding gap, INVISIBLE on Windows which skips the elf RUN) → fixed; the fix RAN 42/15/24 on x86_64-elf + arm64-qemu + Win64-pe, gcc-cross-checked, objdump = pointer-reception (no 24-byte copy). | (closed — the va-operand address-taken mark + the SysV-va_list-param reception arm) | p19 Cluster G c63; the c62 re-probe's LAST compile error; `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp`; the vprintf/vappendf shape; a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-AT-CALL-ARG` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c63; NOT §B) — an ARRAY used as an rvalue / call argument decays to a pointer to its first element (C 6.3.2.1p3).** ROOT (surfaced by c63's first audit as a SILENT SysV SIGSEGV): a SysV `va_list` (`__va_list_tag[1]`, an Array) FORWARDED as a call arg (`vsum(n, ap)`) must decay to `&ap[0]`, but `lowerExpr(Ref(array))`'s addressableLocal arm emitted `Load(slot, Array)` = the tag's FIRST WORD (the gp_offset/fp_offset int) → the callee dereferenced garbage → SIGSEGV. The HIR inserts a `Cast<Array→Ptr>` (→ lowerLvalueAddress) ONLY when the arg type DIFFERS from the param; a va_list arg (Array) forwarded to a va_list param (Array) has MATCHING types → no Cast → the bare Load. (AAPCS64 struct + Win64 char* va_lists escaped; only SysV Array crashed — INVISIBLE on Windows, which skips the elf RUN.) FIX (hir_to_mir.cpp lowerExprNode Ref arm, Option A): a Ref to an Array-typed addressableLocal symbol returns `Gep(slot, 0)` typed Ptr<elem> instead of the invalid Load — byte-identical to the proven Cast<Array→Ptr> decay arm, purely TYPE-driven (TypeKind::Array gate, no arch branch), reusing the scalar call-arg GPR accounting. Cannot mis-fire: every other array-rvalue context carries an explicit Cast (→ lowerLvalueAddress); only a bare same-type array call arg reaches this arm. WITNESS: the [[D-CSUBSET-VA-LIST-PARAM-SLOT]] corpus + the audit's probe_fwd(15)/probe_passthru(24) RAN green on SysV (was 139 SIGSEGV) + arm64 + Win64, gcc-cross-checked; SysV objdump now `lea`/address-of; the array-decay family (array_decay / array_decay_additive[c59] / array_storage_index / memcpy_array_arg / string_array_zero_fill) stays green. | (closed — the Array-typed Ref-rvalue decay-to-Gep arm) | p19 Cluster G c63; surfaced by c63's audit (the forwarding half of the va_list-param fix); `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` (lowerExprNode Ref arm); general C 6.3.2.1p3 array decay at call sites; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-TERNARY-ARRAY-DECAY` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c64; NOT §B) — an ARRAY arm of a conditional (`?:`) decays to a pointer-to-element (C 6.3.2.1p3 + 6.5.15), so a string-literal/array ternary has the common POINTER type, never an aggregate.** ROOT (the c63 re-probe's FIRST + ONLY error — c63 un-masked it; H0009 D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-VALUED-CONTROL-EXPR): sqlite `sqlite3BtreeIntegrityCheck`/`checkAppendMsg` (sqlite3.c:83149) `checkAppendMsg(pCheck, "%s is %u but should be %u", isFreeList ? | `D-CSUBSET-INCOMPATIBLE-ELEMENT-ARRAY-TERNARY` | ⏳ **DEFERRED (pre-existing fail-SOFT, NOT a c64 regression; surfaced by c64's audit, 2026-06-30).** A conditional whose two ARRAY arms have DIFFERENT element types (`cond ? "ab" : intArr` — char[] vs int[]) compiles to a type-confused byte-copy into the first arm's aggregate slot with only a WARNING (S000A), instead of a hard error. The c64 array-decay arm ([[D-CSUBSET-TERNARY-ARRAY-DECAY]]) correctly SKIPS it (the decayed pointers differ → `thenD != elseD` → falls through to the pre-existing FC8 aggregate-ternary by-address path [[D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-VALUED-CONTROL-EXPR]]) — the audit VERIFIED identical behavior with the c64 arm reverted, so NOT a c64 defect. NOT run-green-critical: sqlite's ternaries are all same-element string literals (the c64 arm handles them), and an incompatible-element array conditional is a C constraint violation no real code relies on. FIX (when desired): make the aggregate-ternary lowering reject mismatched-element array arms LOUD (or apply the C 6.5.15 composite-type / constraint rule). | (open) | p19 c64 audit; [[D-CSUBSET-TERNARY-ARRAY-DECAY]] (the c64 arm that correctly skips it); [[D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-VALUED-CONTROL-EXPR]] (the FC8 path it falls to); plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-MIR-ENTRY-BLOCK-ALLOCA-HOIST` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c69; NOT §B) — every body-local's storage `Alloca` is pre-emitted into the function ENTRY block (which dominates all uses), not at its decl site.** ROOT: a local DECLARED in an entry-unreachable block (the canonical case: a decl before the first `case` of a `switch`, which the c60 switch-flatten places in a predecessor-less pre-case block) had its `Alloca` emitted into that dead block; the mandatory unreachable-prune (`D-MIR-UNREACHABLE-PRUNE-NORMALIZE`) then dropped the block while a reachable case body still Load/Stored the slot → the `MirFunctionRebuilder` rewrite-map completeness abort (`D-OPT2-REWRITE-MAP-COMPLETENESS`), a SIGABRT compiler crash. FIX (`src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp`): `lowerFunction` runs a `collectLocalDecls` pre-pass that emits each local's slot into the (still-open) entry block before lowering the body; the VarDecl site reuses the pre-emitted slot. The jumped-over initializer's Store stays at the (dead) decl site. Corpus `examples/c-subset/switch_decl_before_case` (→42; RED-ON-DISABLE → SIGABRT exit 134). Companion of `D-CSUBSET-ALLOCA-ADDRESS-REMATERIALIZE` (below — the hoist makes alloca addresses live from entry, which that anchor's remat keeps off the register-pressure cliff). |
| `D-AS4-ARM64-LARGE-DISP-LEA-BASE-CLOBBER` | ⏳ **DEFERRED (surfaced + confirmed PRE-EXISTING by the c69 audit, 2026-06-30; NOT introduced by c69).** An arm64 `lea result,[base+disp]` with a displacement > 16 MiB (> 0xFFFFFF) lowers to a `MOVZ Xd; MOVK Xd; ADD Xd, base, Xd` macro that CLOBBERS `base` when regalloc coalesces the base vreg into the result (base == Xd == result). c69's alloca-address rematerialization ([[D-CSUBSET-ALLOCA-ADDRESS-REMATERIALIZE]]) EXPOSED it on `large_frame_beyond_16mib` (a > 16 MiB frame slot) → SIGSEGV on arm64-qemu under the optimized pipeline; FIXED for the alloca-base case by folding a constant-offset alloca Gep into one `lea [sp + slotOff + disp]` (base = sp, the reserved frame register, never the clobberable result). RESIDUAL: a NON-frame `p + hugeConst` (> 16 MiB constant added to a DERIVED pointer whose vreg coalesces with the result) still hits the general Gep lea path, which c69 does NOT touch (audit-verified: no makeMemOffset/emitInst(Lea) added there; alloca bases are routed away first). PRE-EXISTING, c69-INDEPENDENT, astronomically rare, NO sqlite relevance. FIX (when needed): a scratch register for the arm64 large-disp `lea` multi-word variant, OR a regalloc base≠result constraint for it. | (open) | discovered p19 c69 (impl + audit); [[D-CSUBSET-ALLOCA-ADDRESS-REMATERIALIZE]] (c69, whose remat exposed it) + sibling [[D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA]] (c42); `src/dss-config/targets/arm64.target.json`; plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-ALLOCA-ADDRESS-REMATERIALIZE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c69; NOT §B) — a body-local alloca's ADDRESS is rematerialized at each use (a fresh `lea_frame_slot` per use) instead of held in one entry-spanning vreg.** ROOT (un-masked by the c69 entry-block alloca-hoist above): hoisting every alloca to entry makes its address-value live from entry; reusing ONE address vreg across that range tips a register-pressure cliff (`examples/c-subset/index_negative`'s `main` takes addresses of locals → `R_SpilledDueToPressure` + `L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc` scratch-pool exhaustion at the rewrite stage). FIX (the conventional "rematerialize frame addresses" treatment; `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.cpp` + `src/lir/lir_callconv.cpp` + both target JSONs): MIR→LIR emits the `alloca` op ONCE (it reserves the slot, in scan order) and, at each use of the address (`regForValue` chokepoint), a fresh `lea_frame_slot k` (k = the alloca's 0-based scan-order index, on the payload) whose result has a tiny def→use range; lir_callconv materializes each `lea_frame_slot k` into `lea [sp + offsetOf(k)]` — byte-identical to the original alloca's `lea` (offset from the alloca-index→offset map it builds in scan order, asserted congruent with the alloca arm's running offset). The new `lea_frame_slot` virtual op is DISTINCT from `alloca` so the frame-layout slot-count scan is unchanged (exactly ONE slot per original alloca). A CONSTANT-offset Gep whose base IS an alloca (`&local[constIdx]`/`&s.field`) FOLDS its displacement into ONE sp-based `lea [sp + slotOff + disp]` (via an optional ImmInt operand on `lea_frame_slot`) — cheaper (one lea) AND load-bearing on arm64: without the fold, a >16 MiB `disp` in the un-folded `lea_frame_slot` base + `lea [base+disp]` pair lowers to the arm64 MOVZ/MOVK macro `ADD Xd,base,Xd` which clobbers `base` when regalloc coalesces the tiny-range remat base into the Gep result (base==Xd → SIGSEGV, `examples/c-subset/large_frame_beyond_16mib` under the full-release-like pipeline); folding makes the base `sp` (never the result), so the macro is safe. Corpora `examples/c-subset/index_negative` (→42; RED-ON-DISABLE → the scratch-pool exhaustion compile error) + `large_frame_beyond_16mib` (→42 on arm64-qemu; the const-disp fold is what keeps it green under remat). Verified RUN-green on all 3 legs (Win pe compile + WSL x86_64-elf + arm64-qemu). |
| `D-CSUBSET-CHAR-ALU-INTEGER-PROMOTION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c71; NOT §B) — a SUB-INT (`char`/`signed char`/`short`) operand of a truth-value condition OR of `++`/`--` integer-PROMOTES to `int` (C 6.3.1.1) before the ICmp/Sub.** ROOT (the c70 re-probe compiled sqlite3.c END-TO-END past the VDBE switch, 12m41s → 902× `error[L_UnsupportedLoweringForOpcode]` "ICmp/Sub operand: integer TypeKind ordinal 15 [Char] has no native-width ALU forms" = the [[D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS]] gate correctly refusing an 8-bit ALU op): a text library scans/classifies/steps `char` on nearly every line. Root-caused (delegated agent) to TWO HIR-lowering sites that build the op DIRECTLY, bypassing the C 6.3.1.1 integer promotion the ORDINARY binary combine site already applies (agent-verified: `a<b`/`c-'0'`/`*z==t` widen fine): (A) `coerceCondition` (cst_to_hir.cpp ~641) minted a SAME-TYPE (Char) zero → `Ne(char,char)` (a Char-typed ICmp) for a bare char truth value (`while(*z)`, `if(c)`, `for(;*z;)`, `?:`/`&&`/`||`); (B) `incDecArithValue` (~3975) → `incDecArithType(Char)=Char` (only Enum was remapped) → `BinaryOp Add/Sub` at opType=Char for `c++`/`c--`; (A') `combineUnaryOp`'s logical `!` (~2722) built a Char-typed `ICmpEq(operand,0)` (the pervasive `if(!*z)` scan idiom) — this 3rd site was FOUND by the independent audit (my initial fix + claims covered only A+B) and FOLDED IN this cycle. FIX (NOT §B — the tier that owns promotion [HIR combine sites] + the correct primitive [`integerPromotedType`→`coerce`→SExt/ZExt] already exist and work; the sites simply skipped them): all THREE sites promote the sub-int operand to `int` via the SAME `integerPromotedType(interner, t, *arith_)` + `coerce` chokepoint the array-index path (~3093) uses — Char/signed→SExt, unsigned(U8/U16)→ZExt via `mapCast`, automatic per kind; (A) mints the zero at the promoted type → `Ne(i32,i32)`; (B)'s EXISTING trailing `coerce(sum, lv.type)` narrows the i32 result back (Trunc) for the store, preserving 8-bit wraparound EXACTLY (C's `(char)((int)c±1)`; signed char 127++→−128); (A') `!` promotes its operand then keeps its Bool result (Bool operand EXCLUDED — its ICmpEq-0 form is already native). `arith_`-gated (block-less toy/tsql unchanged); float conds pass through (`integerPromotedType` no-ops floats → the `Ne` stays an FCmp). WITNESS: corpus `char_alu_integer_promotion` (`while(*z)` scan + plain/signed-char `c++` + signed-char 127→−128 wrap + `if(hi)`/`hi<0` SExt assertion → 42; EVERY observable independent of plain-char signedness [DSS types char SIGNED uniformly; gcc uses UNSIGNED char on arm64] so it matches gcc on all legs, the 2 signedness asserts use `signed char` explicitly; CONSOLIDATED into ONE function of SEQUENTIAL loops so it stays under the pre-existing x86_64 register-pressure cliff that many small inlinable static helpers would tip under -O — the cliff is [[D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS]]/regalloc, NOT a c71 defect). Golden test `CharIfConditionLowersAsICmpNeTypedZero`→`CharIfConditionIntegerPromotesToI32BeforeICmpNe` (asserts I32 operands, not Char). RED-ON-DISABLE: the pre-fix compiler emits the exact ordinal-15 (Char) + ordinal-1 (signed char) ICmp/Sub errors on this corpus (WITNESSED on the WSL clone — 315/316, ONLY this corpus red pre-fix). 3-leg gate: Win 501/501; WSL x86_64-elf + qemu arm64 316/316. ★★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT (worktree, all-3-legs gcc-cross-check + the wrong-value/wrong-signedness/double-eval hunt): **SOUND** for A+B (no miscompile — 31 adversarial programs, 23 gcc-cross-checked ×8 legs [gcc x86 signed-char + aarch64 UNSIGNED-char, O0/O2]; objdump confirms SExt(signed)/ZExt(unsigned) both arches; single-eval confirmed; red-on-disable = the ordinal-15/-1 wall; 316/316 + 212/212 no regression; float/int/ptr/enum conds unchanged). The audit's ONE material finding = the un-covered `!` site (A' above) → FOLDED IN this cycle + re-gated (Win 501/501 + WSL/qemu 316/316, gcc-cross-check 42 both arches; `!char` is signedness-agnostic — 0/1 regardless of SExt/ZExt). SIBLINGS NOT fixed (their own sites, deferred/latent): `switch(char)` discriminant ([[D-CSUBSET-NARROW-SWITCH-DISCRIMINANT-CMP]], `A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant` cmp width 8) + unary `-c`/`~c` (Neg/BitNot on char — a result-type change; handle if a re-probe hits them). | (closed — the coerceCondition + incDecArithValue + combineUnaryOp[`!`] integer-promotion, reusing `integerPromotedType`+`coerce`) | p19 Cluster G c71; the c70 re-probe's 902-error frontier; the [[D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS]] family (the two bypass sites of C 6.3.1.1 promotion); reuses the array-index-promotion chokepoint; `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` + `tests/mir/test_mir_lowering_c_subset.cpp`; a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-UNARY-SUBINT-PROMOTION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c72; NOT §B) — unary `~` (BitNot) and `-` (Neg) on a SUB-INT operand integer-PROMOTE the operand to `int` (C 6.5.3.3p3/p4) and carry the PROMOTED result type.** ROOT (the post-c71 re-probe CLEARED the 902 Char [[D-CSUBSET-CHAR-ALU-INTEGER-PROMOTION]] errors → 8 REMAINED on U8/U16 [TypeKind ordinal 6/7 = sqlite's `u8`/`u16`] at a `<deferred>` opcode): sqlite's `~u8`/`-u8` flag math. c71 generalized `combineUnaryOp`'s promotion ONLY to `!` (logical Not); `~`/`-` still built a sub-int-typed `UnaryOp` (`result=operand.type`) → the MIR Not/Neg walled at the LIR sub-native ALU gate. Binary `&`/`|`/`^`/`<<` already promote via `combineBinary` (only the UNARY operators bypassed it). This is EXACTLY the sibling the c71 independent audit flagged as latent. Probe confirmed: `~u8`→ord6, `-u8`→ord6, `~u16`→ord7 all error; `u8 & u8`/`| ^ <<` all OK. FIX (`src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` combineUnaryOp): widen the promotion condition from `Not` to `{Not,BitNot,Neg}` + coerce the operand to `integerPromotedType` (u8/u16→ZExt, signed char/i8→SExt via `mapCast`); the KEY NEW behavior vs c71 — `~`/`-` carry the PROMOTED (int) RESULT type (C 6.5.3.3 — "the result has the promoted type"; threaded via a `promotedTy` local into BOTH the operand coerce and the UnaryOp result), so an assignment back to a narrow lvalue truncates via the normal coerce; `!` keeps its Bool result. Bool EXCLUDED (not gated — keeps `~bool`/`!bool`); float/pointer/≥int operands pass through (`integerPromotedType` no-ops → `-1.5`/`~intConst` unchanged). ZERO combineBinary change. WITNESS: corpus `unary_bitnot_neg_promotion` (`(u8)~(u8)0xF0`=15 + `(~(u16)0xFFF0 & 0xFF)`=15 + `-(u8)1==-1`→+5 + `~(signed char)0==-1`→+7 => 42; observables masked-to-low-byte [signedness-agnostic — u8/u16 ZExt to positive int everywhere] or explicit `signed char`, so gcc agrees all 4 legs; sequential stmts dodge the -O regalloc cliff; RED-ON-DISABLE → `<deferred>: integer TypeKind ordinal 6/7 has no native-width ALU forms`). 3-leg gate Win 502/502 + WSL/qemu 317/317; gcc-cross-check 42 x86(O0/O2)+aarch64 (the result-type change COMPOSES — the `(u8)~m` truncate-back verified). ★★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT (worktree, focused on the c72 delta): **SOUND** — ~4300 observables gcc-cross-checked (x86 signed-char + aarch64 UNSIGNED-char, O0/O2), incl. ALL 256 u8 × 9 contexts + ALL 256 signed-char × 7 contexts; objdump ZExt(u8/u16)/SExt(i8/char) both arches; the result-type change proven GENUINELY-int (unsigned-wide reads + narrow-store truncation catch a stay-as-u8 bug); red-on-disable = the ordinal 6/7/1 wall; 317/317 no regression; float/int/`!`/Bool unchanged. | `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-SUBINT-PROMOTION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c73; NOT §B) — a bit-field whose allocation UNIT is SUB-INT (u8/u16/signed char) computes its extract/insert RMW arithmetic at the PROMOTED int width, then Truncs back.** ROOT (the post-c72 re-probe cleared the 8 unary [[D-CSUBSET-UNARY-SUBINT-PROMOTION]] errors → 2 REMAINED on U8 [ordinal 6] at a `<deferred>` opcode): sub-int BIT-FIELDS (`struct{ u8 a:3; u8 b:5; }`). c71+c72 promoted the char/sub-int ALU at the SOURCE-OPERATOR sites (condition/++--/`!`/`~`/`-`); but a bit-field read/write is SYNTHESIZED at `emitBitfieldExtract` (hir_to_mir.cpp:2233) + `emitBitfieldInsert` (:2288) — `(unit>>off)&mask` / `(unit&~fieldMask)|((rhs&mask)<<off)` — with EVERY op at the field's allocation-UNIT type; for a u8 unit those shift/mask/and/or are u8-typed → the LIR `requireNativeIntWidth` gate walls them. sqlite uses `u8` flag bit-fields pervasively. Probe: `u8 a:3` read/write/`++` all error; `unsigned a:3` (int unit) OK; binary `&|^<<` OK (combineBinary promotes). FIX: both functions — when `unitBytes*8 < 32` — set computeTy=I32 + B=32, cast the loaded unit to i32 (`mapCast(fieldTy,I32)` ZExt/SExt; SExt-vs-ZExt irrelevant, the shift/mask re-derive the field value+sign at width B), run the ops at i32, and Trunc the extract RESULT / merged-unit BACK to the field type for the caller-node contract / Store (identical value, off the gate). A NATIVE unit (>=32-bit: u32/i32/u64 incl. the wide `u64:40` case the :2236 comment protects) keeps computeTy==fieldTy = the ORIGINAL path, byte-identical. The insert rhs is FIELD-TYPED by the caller contract (assignment RHS HIR-coerced; the aggregate-init caller at :2979 now coerces the init value to the field type first — MirBuilder can't query an inst's type mid-build, so the fix casts the rhs FROM the field type). WITNESS: corpus `bitfield_subint_promotion` (u8 read+write+RMW NEIGHBOUR-preserve + 3-bit OVERFLOW-trunc 9&7=1 + signed-char SIGN-EXTEND −3 + AGGREGATE-INIT + u16 unit → 42; observables signedness-agnostic or explicit `signed char` so gcc agrees x86+aarch64; RED-ON-DISABLE → `<deferred>: ordinal 6/7`). 3-leg gate Win 503/503 + WSL/qemu 318/318 (ZERO regression in existing bit-field + golden-MIR tests — the native/wide-unit + init paths); gcc-cross-check 42 x86(signed char)+aarch64(unsigned char). ★★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT (worktree, exhaustive bit-field hunt): **SOUND** — no miscompile on any layout/value/offset/width/signedness/arch. Generated sweeps (EVERY u8 two-field split × values + over-width + neighbour-preserve; signed char at offset 0 AND non-zero offset [exercises `Shl(32-off-W)`]; u16; the adversarial ZExt-vs-SExt edges `u8:8=0x80`→+128 [NOT sign-extended] / `signed char:8=200`→−56 / 1-bit signed=−1) all == gcc across 6 dss legs (pe/x86-elf/arm64-qemu × dbg/rel) + 4 gcc legs (x86+aarch64, O0/O2). objdump: u8 read = movzx+movzx+`and r,0x7`(32-bit)+movzx-trunc, signed char = movsx+shl+sar, `~fieldMask` clear-mask at 32-bit (neighbours preserved); WIDE `u64:40` = 64-bit load + `movabs 0xffffffffff` mask, NO promote/trunc = BYTE-IDENTICAL to pre-c73. Red-on-disable = the ordinal-6 wall. | `D-CSUBSET-COMPOUND-ASSIGN-VALUE-PROMOTION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c74; NOT §B) — a compound assignment on a SUB-INT lvalue used in VALUE/expression position integer-PROMOTES the base op to int (C99 `a OP= b` == `a = (T)((a) OP (b))`).** ROOT (the post-c73 re-probe STILL showed the SAME 2 U8 [ordinal 6] `<deferred>` ALU errors — c73 [[D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-SUBINT-PROMOTION]] fixed u8 BIT-FIELDS, a REAL gap, but NOT sqlite's actual construct; ★ PROCESS MISS — inferred bit-fields from a probe instead of confirming sqlite's site, the re-probe is ground truth): I INSTRUMENTED the LIR ALU gate (mir_to_lir.cpp ~949, print `op#{int(op)} func# operand-ordinals`), compiled sqlite3.c once → **op#18 [=And] operands (U8,I32) → U8** = a compound bitwise-assign on a sub-int lvalue used in VALUE position (`if( (p->flags &= ~MASK) )`, `while((c|=b))`, `return (x&=y)`). c71/c72/c73 promoted the condition/++--/unary/bit-field sites, and the STATEMENT-position compound assign (`x &= y;`) already promoted via `lowerCompoundAssign` (my earlier probes used the statement form — which is why I missed the value form). But a VALUE-position compound assign lowers through TWO OTHER paths — the `lowerExpr` work-stack driver's `finishAssign` (cst_to_hir.cpp:1721, PRIMARY) + the `lowerBinary` Assign arm (~2600, recursive fallback) — both of which built `compoundLhsRead OP rhs` at the LVALUE type with UN-promoted operands → the sub-int ALU op → the gate. FIX (both paths, mirroring `lowerCompoundAssign`): `common = commonArithType(lhs,rhs)` (i32 for sub-int); coerce BOTH operands to `common` + build the BinaryOp at `common`; then `stored = (common != lv.type) ? coerce(opResult, lv.type) : opResult` (narrow back; the store+re-read yield the TRUNCATED value = C's compound-assign value). A NON-arithmetic common (Ptr compound `p += n`, common Invalid) keeps opType==lv.type → the c41 stride-Gep, BYTE-IDENTICAL. Promote-casts are pure conversions → the read-before-rhs value-position order holds. WITNESS: corpus `compound_assign_value_promotion` (`(x&=0x0F)`=15 + `(y<<=2)`=20 + `((z&=0x0F)==0)`+3 + `(w|=2)`=3 + `((s+=9)==1)`+1 [signed char] → 42; the yielded value is the STORED/TRUNCATED value so observables are low-byte [signedness-agnostic or explicit signed char], gcc-agreeing x86+aarch64; RED-ON-DISABLE → the ordinal-6/1 wall). 3-leg gate Win 504/504 + WSL/qemu 319/319 (ZERO regression — this value-position path change is GLOBAL but int/pointer/float/statement compound assigns all pass); gcc-cross-check 42 x86(signed char)+aarch64(unsigned char). ★★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT (worktree — truncated-yield property + no-regression on int/pointer/float compound + single-eval + red-on-disable): **SOUND** — 52 single-observable per-case programs (every op `&= |= ^= <<= >>= += -= *= /= %=` × u8/u16/s8/s16/char, operands chosen so the TRUNCATED stored value ≠ the promoted i32: `(u8 0x40)<<=2`→0 not 256, `(u8 0x10)*=0x11`→16 not 272, `(s8 100)+=100`→−56, `(u16 0x4000)<<=2`→0, plus mixed-sub-int + wider-rhs [common i64]) ALL match gcc on 8 legs (x86 signed-char + aarch64 UNSIGNED-char, O0/O2) — ZERO mismatch; STRUCTURAL: the yield is a fresh `lvRead` of the sub-int slot AFTER the store → by construction the truncated value, NEVER the promoted i32; the conditional narrow ≡ the untouched path's unconditional `coerce` (identity no-op to same type, cst_to_hir.cpp:331; `TypeId::==` on `.v`); the pointer path BYTE-IDENTICAL (`commonArithType` Invalid for Ptr → opType==lv.type → c41 stride-Gep, objdump-confirmed `int* p+=2` scales ×4 both arches); single-eval preserved (`a[i++]+=1`/`*p++&=m`/rhs-once = 204 everywhere). Red-on-disable = the exact ordinal-6 (`<deferred>` U8) + ordinal-1 (Add I8) wall. 319/319 WSL c-subset + HIR/MIR golden/lowering units (toy/tsql/generic/multi_lang byte-neutral). ★ PRE-EXISTING NON-c74 (deferred [[D-CSUBSET-BOOL-COMPOUND-ASSIGN]]): `_Bool b; b|=5`/`b+=3` fails S0003 in BOTH the untouched statement path (`lowerCompoundAssign`) AND value position → NOT a c74 regression (fails identically before/after); unlikely a sqlite blocker (sqlite uses u8/int not `_Bool`) → re-probe is ground truth. | (closed — the finishAssign + lowerBinary value-position compound-assign promotion, mirroring lowerCompoundAssign) | p19 Cluster G c74; the TRUE post-c73 blocker; the value-position member of the [[D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS]] family (siblings c71/c72/c73); `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (finishAssign + lowerBinary Assign arm); a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-AS-REGALLOC-SPILL-RELOAD-SCRATCH` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c75; §B USER — reserve-K, cycle 1 of a 2-cycle plan [[project_v0_0_2_p19_clusterg_c75_c76_regalloc_scratch_plan_2026_07_01]]; c76 = per-point scratch SUPERSEDES this reservation) — a function under GENERAL register pressure (peak simultaneous live values > allocatable GPRs) reserves K caller-saved registers per class as guaranteed spill-reload scratch.** ROOT: the post-c74 MILESTONE re-probe compiled sqlite3.c 12m57s through the WHOLE 8 MB amalgamation → `error[L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc]` (lir_rewrite.cpp:411): function 1033 (sym 9794), INSTRUMENT-confirmed `op=1 [=arg] isCall=0 nOps=0 nSpilled=0` = a spilled incoming-PARAMETER's result with an EMPTY global scratch pool (general pressure, NOT a wide call). The linear-scan allocator (lir_regalloc.cpp, Poletto-Sarkar) reserved NOTHING for reloads + `pickScratchRegs` (lir_rewrite.cpp:82) built the scratch pool GLOBALLY (allocatable − regs-assigned-to-ANY-range) → a huge func assigns all 15 GPRs → empty pool → a spilled vreg's reload has no register. FIX (`buildFreeLists` + a new `computeReloadReserve`, lir_regalloc.cpp): reserve K caller-saved regs per class (held back from the free lists → never assigned → `pickScratchRegs` picks them up as scratch AUTOMATICALLY, unchanged). ★ K is DERIVED per-function from the max single-instruction register-reload demand (count of same-class VIRTUAL reg operands + result over NON-CALL instructions — calls are the deferred [[D-AS-REGALLOC-WIDE-CALL-OPERAND-COUNT]]) — NEVER a hardcoded count; x86_64 and arm64 compute their own. Caller-saved so a transient reload needs no callee-save; held back from the caller-saved END (allocator's last-choice partition) minimizing codegen impact; the existing L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc check is the LOUD backstop if K is ever under-provisioned. WITNESS: corpus `regalloc_spill_reload_scratch` (f has 15 params each in TWO inline sums → the first sum's result coexists with all 15 live params → MAXLIVE 16 > 15 GPRs → spill; main calls f with 15 args = a call that FITS → 42; arm64 ~30 GPRs never spills → trivially green + value-checked). RED-ON-DISABLE (WITNESSED — git stash the fix + rebuild): `error[L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc] function 1 exhausted the per-class scratch pool` on x86_64. 3-leg gate Win 505/505 + WSL/qemu 320/320. Rewrite units re-pinned (the fix intentionally changes allocation): `ExhaustedClass...` → a 20-arg WIDE CALL (the one exhaustion reserve-K genuinely can't fix); `Arm64HighFpr...` precondition (d31 is now reserved SCRATCH not assigned). Knife-edges index_negative + deep_lvalue_chain green. ★★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT found NOT SOUND — a SILENT MISCOMPILE (reserve-K reserved ARG registers [x86_64 SysV r9 = the 6th int arg] as scratch → the rewriter clobbered an incoming param; a 15-param fn returned 149 not 156, the 6th param read as the 5th's value; + a sibling PRE-EXISTING allocator bug assigning arg regs [xmm7] to other vregs → 54 not 42; pre-c75 both were LOUD failures → c75 turned them silent) → FIXED by the companion [[D-AS-REGALLOC-ARG-REGISTER-OCCUPIED]] (arg-exclude the reservation + model incoming arg registers as occupied [entry, argOp) so neither allocation nor scratch reuses them while a param is live); a 3rd fix followed (idiv implicit-reg scratch, part 4 of [[D-AS-REGALLOC-ARG-REGISTER-OCCUPIED]]); the 2nd COMPLETE RE-AUDIT is SOUND. | (closed with the arg-register companion fix — buildFreeLists reserve-K [arg-excluded] + computeReloadReserve; pickScratchRegs unchanged) | p19 Cluster G c75; the post-c74 MILESTONE re-probe frontier (whole-amalgamation compile); §B USER 2-cycle plan [[project_v0_0_2_p19_clusterg_c75_c76_regalloc_scratch_plan_2026_07_01]]; `src/lir/lir_regalloc.cpp`; a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 12 MIR/LIR / SQLite-green |
| `D-AS-REGALLOC-WIDE-CALL-OPERAND-COUNT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c76; §B USER option E) — FIXED by a new PRE-REGALLOC pass `lowerWideCallArgs` (`src/lir/lir_wide_call_args.{cpp,hpp}`, invoked in compile_pipeline.cpp before liveness): it splits each Call's SCALAR args beyond the cc's register-passed count (argGprs/argFprs, config-driven, no if(arch)) into `store_outgoing_arg` stack carriers before regalloc, so no Call holds more register-operands than the machine passes in registers → func 2088 clears; computeReloadReserve dropped its isCall exclusion (a call's operands are now bounded). ops[0]=callee (FC4-c2) + ops[1]=sret (FC7-C3) never split. INDEPENDENT AUDIT SOUND (22 probes 3 legs vs gcc O0/O2; the loud L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc guard stays exact for a genuinely-too-wide callee — NO eviction); Win 515/515 + WSL/qemu 515/515. Value-witnessed by examples/c-subset/wide_call_{int,variadic,indirect,mixed}; the OPERAND-EXPLOSION red-on-disable is the unit `LirRewrite.ExhaustedClassEmitsLoudFailureNotSilentClobber` (small pure-C examples don't reach the explosion). Was: a single CALL instruction whose live register-operand count exceeds the register file exhausts the spill-reload scratch pool, and reserve-K ([[D-AS-REGALLOC-SPILL-RELOAD-SCRATCH]]) CANNOT fix it** (raising K reduces allocatable registers 1:1, so the call's spilled operands always exceed K by one; only reshaping wide outgoing arg lists to incremental stack stores BEFORE regalloc — the root-cause agent's option E — or a rewriter-side evict-a-live-reg escape hatch would fix it). WITNESSED by the c75 corpus's earlier 16-param variant (main's 16-arg call, func 2 exhausted) + the re-pinned `LirRewrite.ExhaustedClassEmitsLoudFailureNotSilentClobber` unit (a 20-arg wide call → loud `L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc`). ★ NOW a SQLITE BLOCKER (falsifying the earlier 'not a blocker'): the post-c75 re-probe (c75's compiler) CLEARED func 1033 but hit **func 2088 (sym 18765), INSTRUMENT-confirmed `op=26 isCall=1 nOps=9 nSpilled=4`** — a sqlite call with 4 spilled register-operands exceeding K. Per the standing directive [[feedback_address_any_sqlite_green_blocker_2026_06_30]] this is IN-SCOPE, to be handled in the loop AFTER c75 is made sound (the arg-register fix [[D-AS-REGALLOC-ARG-REGISTER-OCCUPIED]] lands first). The loud failure is CORRECT (never a silent clobber) but it BLOCKS sqlite-green → needs option E (reshape wide outgoing arg lists into incremental stack stores BEFORE regalloc) or a rewriter-side evict-a-live-reg escape hatch — a §B fork to bring to the user. Sibling of c75 [[D-AS-REGALLOC-SPILL-RELOAD-SCRATCH]]. | (IN-SCOPE — needs option-E arg-list reshape or rewriter eviction, NOT reserve-K) | p19 Cluster G c75; discovered building the c75 corpus, CONFIRMED as sqlite's func 2088 by the post-c75 re-probe; `src/lir/lir_regalloc.cpp` (computeReloadReserve excludes calls); plan 12 MIR/LIR / SQLite-green |
| `D-AS-REGALLOC-DIRECT-ARG-RELOAD` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c77; USER-chosen) — func 2088 CONFIRMED CLEARED on real sqlite3.c (independent verifier a1b4246e compiled the whole 9.5MB amalgamation for x86_64:elf64: `L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc` count 0, the ENTIRE regalloc/rewrite phase clean — closing the c75→c77 register-allocation arc; correctness SOUND, 7 probes 3 legs vs gcc O0/O2; the sqlite frontier ADVANCED past regalloc to the x86_64 encoder = c78). A SPILLED register-passed CALL ARG loads DIRECTLY into its ABI arg register, not via a scratch register that callconv then moves.** ROOT (post-c76 re-probe, INSTRUMENTED ground truth, agent a217cf96): sqlite func 2088 = a DIRECT CALL with 6 GPR args, ≥4 SPILL under pressure; the rewriter reloaded each spilled call-arg into a distinct SCRATCH GPR, but SysV has only 3 non-arg caller-saved GPR scratch (rax/r10/r11) vs up to 6 spilling args → exhaust at the 4th → `error[L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc]`. c76's [[D-AS-REGALLOC-WIDE-CALL-OPERAND-COUNT]] (stacks args BEYOND the register pool) was NECESSARY-NOT-SUFFICIENT — it never touches the IN-POOL args that spill (func 2088 was MISDIAGNOSED as a wide-operand-count problem; the real cause is call-arg reload-scratch SUPPLY). USER chose direct-arg-reload OVER a rewriter eviction escape-hatch (keeps the no-eviction stance). FIX (agent aa22ff13, config-driven no if(arch)): a new POST-REGALLOC operand kind `SpillSlotRef` (slot v + reg class; repurposes a LirOperand padding byte, still 8B; passes verifyLirPostRegalloc since not a Reg; consumed by callconv before assembly) — the rewriter's `classifyCallRegArgs` (mirrors callconv's NSAA arg-cursor EXACTLY, fail-loud on divergence) marks register-passed scalar args; a spilled one becomes a SpillSlotRef instead of a scratch reload (the callee ops[0]/FC4-c2 + sret ops[1]/FC7-C3 + by-value carriers KEEP the scratch path); callconv's emitParallelRegMoves gains a MEM-SOURCE arm — a mem-src move is a pure SINK (never a source → never a cycle member; the cycle-break picks a reg-src victim) emitting `frame_load argReg,[slot]`, composing with the c76 move-cycle machinery UNCHANGED → demand==supply (1 arg reg/arg) → ZERO scratch for call args; REVERTS c76's isCall-exclusion removal in computeReloadReserve. KEEPS the loud L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc guard (NO eviction) + 2 new fail-loud guards (classifier divergence, SpillSlotRef-in-overflow). WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/spilled_call_arg_reload` (non-inlinable recursive 6-GPR-arg callee, args live across a high-pressure loop → spill; distinct decimal weights → wrong-reload changes the exit; =227; RED-ON-DISABLE under the RELEASE arm → L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc, the exhaustion being a Mem2Reg-SSA-spill phenomenon). Impl-agent-validated 3 legs vs gcc. ★ CONFIRMED via ground truth (the verifier's real-sqlite compile — the c76 lesson applied: NOT committed until func 2088 actually cleared, unlike c76). | (CLOSED c77; the SpillSlotRef direct-arg-reload) | p19 c77; the post-c76 re-probe frontier; COMPLETES the necessary-not-sufficient c76 [[D-AS-REGALLOC-WIDE-CALL-OPERAND-COUNT]]; root-cause a217cf96 + impl aa22ff13; `src/lir/lir_node.hpp`+`lir_rewrite.cpp`+`lir_callconv.cpp`+`lir_regalloc.cpp`; plan 12 MIR/LIR / SQLite-green |
| `D-AS-REGALLOC-ARG-REGISTER-OCCUPIED` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c75 correctness fix; NOT §B — a clean bug fix) — the allocator + rewriter model an incoming ABI ARGUMENT REGISTER as OCCUPIED over [entry, argOp.late), so neither allocation nor spill-reload scratch reuses it while the incoming parameter is still live in it.** ROOT (the c75 [[D-AS-REGALLOC-SPILL-RELOAD-SCRATCH]] independent AUDIT caught a SILENT MISCOMPILE): a param vreg's live range starts at its `arg` op's late slot, and the `mov home, argreg` materialization is inserted POST-regalloc by callconv — so [entry, argOp) has the incoming arg register appear FREE, and c75's added pressure/reservation reused it. TWO variants (both pre-c75 LOUD `L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc` failures → c75 turned silent): (V1) c75's reserve-K reservation reserved an arg register (x86_64 SysV's last caller-saved GPR r9 = the 6th int arg) as scratch → the rewriter staged a spill-reload through r9 at entry → clobbered the 6th incoming param (15-param fn → 149 not 156); (V2, PRE-EXISTING, unmasked by c75's pressure) the allocator assigned an FP arg register (xmm7 = 8th FP arg) to a non-param vreg's home → clobbered the incoming FP param (→ 54 not 42). FIX (4 parts, all cc-config-driven, no arch identity, reusing existing machinery): (1) `buildFreeLists` reserves K caller-saved NON-ARG registers — excludes argGprs∪argFprs∪indirectResultRegister; under-reserves (SAFE — loud backstop) when a class has <K non-arg caller-saved (ms_x64 FPR has only xmm4/xmm5=2); (2) new `collectArgRegisterOccupied` + a covered-window `excludedScratch` augment (mirrors implicit-clobber/cross-call) — a range live before an arg register's release (`r.start < releasePos`, not the arg's own vreg) excludes that ordinal from allocation + spill-evict → fixes V2; (3) `rewriteOneFunc` forbids the cc's arg registers (the FC4-c2 `forbiddenBase`) as scratch for a SPILLED `arg`-op result → fixes V1's rewriter side (arg ops are contiguous entry-block-head + operand-less, so the arg-op result store is the sole entry-region reload that can clobber a still-live incoming arg reg). §B: NONE (a clean correctness fix of the ABI invariant 'incoming arg reg occupied [entry, argOp)'), orthogonal to + survives the c76 per-point supersession. (4) `rewriteOneFunc` ALSO forbids the instruction's OWN implicit registers (inputOrdinals∪clobberedOrdinals — e.g. x86 idiv rax+rdx) as reload scratch for its operands+result — the 1st RE-AUDIT caught a spilled idiv DIVISOR reloading into rax (reserve-K reserves the non-arg caller-saved rax), clobbering the dividend the idiv still needs → 121 not 160 (a SILENT miscompile; the mod/remainder path → rdx → 120); driven by the SAME per-opcode schema union collectImplicitClobberPositions already builds for the allocator's crossing-range exclusion, applied at the op where the transient reload happens (no idiv special-case). VALIDATED: the 4 arg repros + 3 corpora (arg_reg_gpr 156 / arg_reg_fp 42 / div_reload_scratch 160) RUN-correct on x86_64-elf(WSL)+pe(Win)+arm64(qemu) vs gcc O0/O2; 3-leg gate Win 508/508 + WSL/qemu 323/323. A 2nd COMPLETE RE-AUDIT (worktree agent a69337) = **SOUND** — all 6 focus areas complete with RUN evidence vs gcc on 3 legs (O0/O2): prior bugs stay fixed; NO next unsafe-scratch class (variable shift `x>>cl` = rcx is COVERED by the SAME generic `info->implicitRegisters` forbidden path — the shift opcodes declare rcx implicit; x86's 7 implicit-reg opcodes ALL handled, arm64 has 0; `mul` has no rdx:rax implicit; return-rax is materialized POST-rewrite; 2-address is post-rewrite; scratch is confined to one instruction's window); all 3 fix-parts RED-ON-DISABLE load-bearing (idiv off → div=121, arg off → 149/54, reservation off → loud L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc); full ctest Win 508/508 + WSL/qemu 323/323 (qemu needs QEMU_LD_PREFIX+QEMU_STACK_SIZE) + knife-edges 42; under-reserve fails LOUD never silent (Win64 FP exhaustion → L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc, no artifact). Independently RE-FOUND the pre-existing orthogonal SysV mixed-class stack-arg miscompile [[D-FF3-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-STACK-OVERFLOW-OFFSET]] (reproduces on c74, NOT caused by c75). | (closed — buildFreeLists arg-exclusion + collectArgRegisterOccupied excludedScratch + rewriter arg-op-result forbidden-scratch) | p19 Cluster G c75 correctness fix; the c75 audit's silent-miscompile finding; `src/lir/lir_regalloc.cpp` + `src/lir/lir_rewrite.cpp`; a foundational fix under BOTH reserve-K and c76 per-point; plan 12 MIR/LIR / SQLite-green |
| `D-AS-REGALLOC-FPR-SPILL-RELOAD-SCRATCH` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c76) / RE-CHARACTERIZED (agent aa0c00e5, 2026-07-01) — the 'FPR spill-reload scratch collapse' was a MISDIAGNOSIS; the REAL defect is a SOUNDNESS BUG in the c76 move-cycle fix ([[D-ML7-2.3]] `L_MoveCycleUnsupported`), fixed as its correctness completion (ships WITH it, same commit).** The c76 move-cycle agent's DESIGN §5 mis-read an 8-FP callee's `movaps %xmmN,%xmm14` body as an FPR scratch-collapse (the FPR analog of c75's GPR reserve-K). The deeper root-cause (agent aa0c00e5, 2069s, 3-leg) DISPROVED it: (a) c75's reserve-K / computeReloadReserve / collectArgRegisterOccupied are ALREADY class-generic and cover FPR by construction (they select cc.argFprs for an FPR-class result) — NO FPR spill-reload gap exists; (b) the `xmm14` body is HARMLESS dead-param coalescing (a LIVE param always gets its own home; `direct8.c` reading all 8 FP params compiles CORRECTLY on the pristine c75 base). The REAL bug is CALLER-side, inside the move-cycle patch: at a cycle-break `emitParallelRegMoves` hands `pickScratchReg` the SHRUNKEN `moves` vector, so a register whose clean move was already EMITTED (now holding its committed argument) OR a dropped IDENTITY-move dst (`dst==src`, an incoming arg already in place) falls OUT of the exclusion → the picker returns it (e.g. xmm0) → the break `movaps scratch,cycSrc` CLOBBERS a committed argument → a SILENT SysV miscompile on a wide FP rotation whose caller sources overlap the low arg registers (rot8=1 not 42). ★ Without this fix the move-cycle patch converts `L_MoveCycleUnsupported` REJECTIONS into silent miscompiles — the two are ONE fix, MUST ship together. FIX (`src/lir/lir_callconv.cpp` `emitParallelRegMoves`, FPRFIX_isolated.diff, APPLIED to c76; config-driven, NO arch/reg names): capture the FULL move-destination footprint ONCE, BEFORE the identity-erase, and fold it into the scratch picker's `reserved` span at the cycle-break (the scratch avoids EVERY committed/identity destination, not just pending-move regs; a caller-saved scratch of the class always remains — SysV xmm8-15 vs the xmm0-7 dests; the `L_MoveCycleUnsupported` fail-loud backstop stays exact). §B: NONE (one agnostic fix; the anticipated reserve-K/arg-occupancy/per-point forks all target the wrong layer — the allocator/reserve layer c75 governs is fine, the bug is a missing exclusion in the post-regalloc move resolver). WITNESS: unit `CycleBreakScratchAvoidsCommittedArgDestination` (replays the emitted moves as a register machine, asserts each arg-dest ends holding its correct source token — name-agnostic; red-on-disable = xmm0 clobbered) + examples `fpr_arg_cycle_scratch` / `mixed_arg_cycle_scratch` (rot8 / simultaneous-GPR+FPR-cycle = 42 on 3 legs vs gcc O0/O2; red-on-disable → 1 on SysV); arg_reg_fp + all c75 witnesses green; Win 510/510. | (IN-SCOPE c76 → CLOSED at commit; the move-cycle-fix scratch-picker soundness completion, NOT an FPR spill-reload bug) | p19 c76; RE-CHARACTERIZED from the move-cycle agent's §5 misdiagnosis by agent aa0c00e5; the correctness completion of the move-cycle fix [[D-ML7-2.3]]; `src/lir/lir_callconv.cpp` (emitParallelRegMoves); plan 12 MIR/LIR / SQLite-green |
| `D-FF3-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-STACK-OVERFLOW-OFFSET` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c76; USER Option 2) — FIXED: the CALLEE-side incoming overflow-arg offset now follows a monotonic class-SHARED byte cursor (`incomingStackArgByteOffset` in lir_callconv materializeOneFunc) mirroring the caller's overflowIdx, so a GPR-overflow and an FP-overflow arg occupy DISTINCT source-order slots (repro=73 not 64); slot-aligned CCs (Win64) get a congruence assertion. Audit SOUND (515/515 3 legs, gcc-cross-checked; red-on-disable → 64). ★ AUDIT CORRECTION: NOT SysV-only — arm64/AAPCS64 ALSO collides when BOTH classes overflow deeply (>8 GPR + >8 FPR args; the original 7int+9double repro only overflowed FP on arm64 so it masked this); the class-agnostic fix handles both (p05 red-on-disable → arm64=52). Was a SILENT MISCOMPILE (user-reported 2026-07-01; PRE-EXISTING — reproduces on c74 440b74a AND current build-rel [CONFIRMED: repro → 64 not 73], independent of the c75 regalloc arc) — x86-64 SysV (elf64): when a function has BOTH integer/GPR-class params overflowing past the 6 GPR arg registers AND float/FP-class params overflowing past the 8 FP arg registers, the CALLEE reads the overflowed GPR stack arg and the overflowed FP stack arg from the SAME stack offset instead of consecutive slots.** ROOT (disasm-confirmed by the reporter): `int probe(int i1..i7, double d0..d8)` (7 ints + 9 doubles) — the callee emits `mov 0x88(%rsp),%r9` (reads i7) AND `movsd 0x88(%rsp),%xmm4` (reads d8), BOTH at 0x88(%rsp); the caller wrote i7→[rsp+0] and d8→[rsp+8], so the FP overflow arg reads the INT overflow arg's slot → garbage double. The independent per-class overflow counters (GPR-overflow-index, FP-overflow-index) must EACH advance the SHARED incoming-stack cursor, not each restart at 0. WITNESS (CONFIRMED on build-rel): the repro → **DSS x86-elf SysV = 64; gcc -O0/-O2 = 73; arm64 AAPCS64 = 73; Win64 ms_x64 = 73** (only SysV wrong — AAPCS64 + ms_x64 slot-align, so no collision). FIX (config-driven per the SysV ABI's declared stack-overflow discipline — a per-class→shared-cursor rule, NOT an `if (arch==…)` identity branch): the incoming-stack-arg OFFSET assignment — callee-side param stack-slot layout (`src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` ArgOrdinalCounter / stack-overflow handling + `src/lir/lowering/lir_callconv.cpp` recv_by_value_stack_param) and/or caller-side computeMaxOutgoingStackArgs. | (OPEN — a full /dss-cycle: root-cause, config-driven fix, gcc-cross-check 3 legs, a c-subset example witness [mixed int+FP overflow, red-on-disable → SysV≠gcc]) | user-reported + added-as-a-cycle 2026-07-01 (loop's current item NOT stopped); a real silent miscompile but NOT a sqlite-green COMPILE blocker (mixed 7+GPR & 9+FP overflow is rare — sqlite is int/pointer-heavy with few doubles); a potential sqlite RUN-green concern IF a sqlite fn has such a signature (in-scope the moment sqlite RUN hits it per [[feedback_address_any_sqlite_green_blocker_2026_06_30]]); priority HIGH, queued as a dedicated cycle AFTER the current c75-regalloc + func-2088-wide-call arc; `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` + `src/lir/lowering/lir_callconv.cpp`; the FF3 per-target ABI arc / plan 12 MIR-LIR |
| `D-OPT-LIR-ARG-REGISTER-CLASS-MISMATCH-FAILLOUD` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by the [[D-OPT-RELEASE-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-REG-ARG-DROP]] fix; audit F8).** The mixed-class miscompile's disassembly was `cvttss2si %xmm15` — the x86 encoder SILENTLY accepted a GPR register ordinal (r15) placed into an XMM operand field, so a wrong-register-class operand assembled to nonsense instead of failing loud. A LIR/encoder register-CLASS consistency check (an FPR-class LIR operand must encode through an XMM field, a GPR-class through a GP field, else fail loud) would have made THIS bug — and every future wrong-class-operand bug — loud at first occurrence rather than a silent runtime miscompile. Deferred (not on the critical path now the root is fixed): it is a broad encoder-tier backstop touching the x86/arm64 encoders + regalloc's class tracking, its own cycle. TRIGGER: the next wrong-register-class operand bug (a class-confusion in regalloc/lowering) OR a general encoder-hardening pass. | (gated — a defense-in-depth backstop; the arg-position fix removed the one known trigger) | opened by the T2 mixed-class fix (2026-07-07); `src/lir`/`src/asm` x86_64+arm64 encoders + regalloc register-class tracking |
| `D-OPT-RELEASE-FP-CONSTANT-UNLOADED-XMM` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (T2 miscompile-hunt) — SAME ROOT as [[D-OPT-RELEASE-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-REG-ARG-DROP]], fixed by that cycle.** The "un-loaded xmm / FP-constant" symptom was the mixed-class inline arg-DROP: a callee with many mixed int+FP args (`probe(int i1..i7, double d0..d8)`) mapped its FP params to INTEGER actuals after inlining (the per-class Arg ordinal collided across GPR/FPR), so the FP-sum referenced registers that were never loaded with the FP values → garbage. The Arg now carries a flat call-operand POSITION and the inliner maps FP actuals correctly (`core/types/arg_payload.hpp`). WITNESS: `mixed_class_stack_overflow` (the exact repro that forced this anchor's carve-out) RE-ARMED with its `release` optimizedPipelines arm → returns 73 on all 3 run legs (was 33/31). See the sibling anchor's row for the full fix + all witnesses. | ✅ (CLOSED) | T2 miscompile-hunt 2026-07-07; closed by [[D-OPT-RELEASE-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-REG-ARG-DROP]] (one root); ORIGINAL discovery p19 c76 D-FF3 agent a2848ce5. **PRE-FIX detail below (audit trail):** 🔴 **OPEN / SILENT MISCOMPILE (release pipeline only; discovered p19 c76 D-FF3 agent a2848ce5, 2026-07-01; PRE-EXISTING, NOT a c76 defect).** A function with MANY distinct FP CONSTANT args, under the `release` shipped pipeline, miscompiles: after INLINING, the FP-sum references UN-LOADED xmm registers (agent saw xmm3/5/9/13/14/15 holding garbage) → a wrong FP result (the D-FF3 repro's release build = 32 not 73). ROOT (agent-confirmed, disassembly): NOT D-FF3 (under release the callee stack-arg reads ARE at distinct offsets — the D-FF3 fix is correctly applied); the wrong value comes from the FP-CONSTANT materialization / inlining+regalloc path failing to LOAD the constant xmm registers after inlining. PRE-EXISTING: the BASE compiler (D-FF3 reverted) yields the SAME 32 under release; the BASELINE (non-optimized/debug) path is CORRECT (73/142). Only surfaces on the WSL/qemu gate (there the SysV/arm64 legs RUN the optimized differential-verify; Windows ctest is compile-only for non-pe → not exercised). IMPACT on c76's "no-miscompiles" hardening: FP corpus examples with FP-CONSTANT args must OMIT `optimizedPipelines` (documented, per the corpus carve-out) OR use RUNTIME FP args (non-constant — sidesteps the FP-constant materialization bug AND still witnesses the optimizer×feature composition). A potential sqlite RUN-green concern (sqlite uses doubles under the release pipeline) — in-scope the moment sqlite RUN hits it [[feedback_address_any_sqlite_green_blocker_2026_06_30]]. FIX (its own cycle): root-cause why the FP-constant xmm loads are dropped/un-scheduled after inlining (FP-constant materialization ↔ inlining ↔ regalloc interaction). WITNESS (base repro): the D-FF3 examples `mixed_class_stack_overflow`/`_interleaved` with an `optimizedPipelines:[{shippedPipeline:release}]` arm added → RED (32/garbage) on the WSL/qemu run legs; debug arm GREEN (73/142). | (OPEN — a full /dss-cycle: root-cause the FP-constant materialization under inlining+regalloc, config-driven fix, gcc-cross-check 3 legs incl. the optimized run legs, a release-arm corpus witness) | discovered p19 c76 D-FF3 agent a2848ce5 (DESIGN §6, disassembly-confirmed); a DIFFERENT class from the c76 calling-convention fixes; `src/mir`/`src/lir` FP-constant materialization + inlining/regalloc; plan 12 MIR/LIR (or an optimizer plan) / SQLite-green RUN phase |
| `D-RUNTIME-MAIN-ARGC-ARGV` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-02 (p19 Cluster G c88) — the ELF program entry materializes argc/argv for `int main(int argc, char** argv)` via a NEW config-driven `processArgs` format block; the pe64 leg split off as [[D-RUNTIME-PE-MAIN-ARGS]] (a genuinely different mechanism).** ROOT (registered by c76 corpus agent a62343ea, CONFIRMED all 3 targets: no-args argc read pe64=1801535488 / x86-elf=224 / arm64-qemu=80, all garbage; became THE sqlite RUN frontier at c87 — the 2-TU sqlite3 loaded clean [188/188 binds] then crashed INSIDE shell.c's `main(argc,argv)` on garbage argc=846361312: SIGSEGV(139) / glibc malloc.c:2599 sysmalloc assert+SIGABRT(134), no output, while gcc on the SAME bld-dss sources answered 42/3.54.0): the entry trampoline called main WITHOUT setting the argument registers — on SysV x86_64 / AAPCS64 Linux the kernel leaves argc + the in-place NULL-terminated argv vector ON THE INITIAL STACK ([sp]=argc, sp+8=argv[0]…) and the registers hold loader garbage. FIX (config + one emitter arm, agnostic): (a) NEW closed-enum `ArgsMechanism`/`ProcessArgs` vocabulary (target_schema.hpp, the ProcessExit discipline: loader rejects "none"/unknown, offsets explicit u32 ≤ int32-max, validate() pairs it with `processExit` + exec-flavor only, wasm/spirv reject it as dead data); (b) format JSON `processArgs { mechanism:"stack-vector", argcStackOffset:0, argvStackOffset:8 }` on BOTH shipped elf64-*-linux-exec formats; (c) `injectEntryTrampoline` emits the arm FIRST — before the ABI-prologue SP adjust (offsets are process-entry-SP-relative) and before `call main`: `load argGprs[0], [sp+argcOffset]` + `lea argGprs[1], [sp+argvOffset]` (the vector's ADDRESS, never dereferenced) — x86_64 `mov rdi,[rsp]` + `lea rsi,[rsp+8]`; arm64 `LDUR x0,[sp]` + `ADD x1,sp,#8` (the target's frame-relative lea form). Registers come from `entryCallingConvention.argGprs[0..1]` (single source of truth); ZERO if(arch)/if(os) in the emitter — both ISAs ride the SAME 3-op LIR shapes through `assemble()`. SIGNATURE-INDEPENDENT: `main(void)` ignores the two registers (C-legal) — the whole corpus is unaffected (Win 545→546 green incl. every main(void) example). Mach-O declares NO block — LC_MAIN entry is CALLED by dyld with argc/argv ALREADY in the argument registers (pass-through is the correct mechanism; untestable locally, no darwin run leg); pe64 declares NO block — the Windows OS entry receives NO C argument vector at all (msvcrt `__getmainargs` out-parameter route = [[D-RUNTIME-PE-MAIN-ARGS]], the Windows-leg cycle; pe main(void) unchanged-green meanwhile). envp NOT materialized (c-subset main is `(int,char**)`; libc `environ` serves programs that need it). | `D-RUNTIME-PE-MAIN-ARGS` | ★ USER §B DECISION (2026-07-04, c111): chose the **synthesized pre-main INIT FUNCTION** hosting (option B — a compiler-generated function in ordinary MIR/LIR where stack locals + calls are FREE via the normal callconv pass, NOT hand-built trampoline RSP math) with the **msvcrt `__wgetmainargs`** CRT route (dumpbin-verified export ord 168; the WIDE form — shell.c's MSVC entry is `wmain(int,wchar_t**)`; already importing msvcrt, no new import library). CLOSURE GATES: (a) wide/narrow from the entry sig or fail-loud on mismatch [never silent wchar**→char**]; (b) RSP 16-aligned at the __wgetmainargs call [B gets this free from callconv]; (c) _startupinfo real zeroed non-NULL local; (d) every main(void) pe example still 42; (e) a host-independent structural pin (the synthesized fn / trampoline emits the __wgetmainargs call + arg-reg loads, red-on-disable every leg); (f) all-CI-green before CLOSED. WITNESS: flip `main_argc_argv` pe leg to runOn windows + sqlite3.exe RUNS (--version→3.54.0, SELECT→42) = the 3rd leg. Gates a–f met: (a) wide/narrow from the sig, fail-loud else ✓; (b) RSP-16 free from callconv — the calls execute ✓; (c) `_startupinfo` zeroed non-NULL local ✓; (d) main(void) pe still 42 (windows_predefine_macro + shipped_windows_crt_batch) ✓; (e) the structural pins ✓; (f) 3-leg gate ✓. ★ SCOPE: this closes the ARGS WALL ONLY — sqlite3.exe now BUILDS+LINKS (4.7MB PE) and the synth delivers a VALID argc/wargv (proven in isolation), but `sqlite3 --version`/SQL still 0xC0000005 in shell.c `wmain`'s kernel32 CONSOLE prologue (`WideCharToMultiByte` loop / `GetStdHandle` / `GetConsoleMode` / `SetConsoleMode` — all IMPORTED — or `utf8_main`), a SEPARATE deeper blocker PAST this wall (args + malloc proven working in isolation) = the next sqlite Windows-RUN cycle. [SUPERSEDED OPEN TEXT — split from [[D-RUNTIME-MAIN-ARGC-ARGV]] at c88, 2026-07-02: pe64 `main(argc,argv)` read entry-register garbage; the Windows OS entry receives NO C argument vector, so the ELF stack-vector mechanism cannot apply.]** The Windows loader calls the PE entry via `RtlUserThreadStart` with NO argc/argv anywhere in registers or on the stack — the C runtime must ASK for them. §B FORK (≥2 defensible designs, deliberately not half-picked): **(A) msvcrt `__getmainargs(&argc,&argv,&env,0,&startinfo)`** — the classic msvcrt CRT-init call; DSS already imports msvcrt.dll for the whole shipped-lib surface and the trampoline already calls through an import (kernel32!ExitProcess via `call_indirect_via_extern`), BUT the call needs FOUR out-POINTERS to writable stack locals + a 5th STACK argument (beyond the 4 ms_x64 register args) + reloading argc/argv from the locals into rcx/rdx after it returns — the hand-built trampoline LIR would grow stack-slot addressing (lea rcx/rdx/r8 off rsp, a [rsp+32] store for arg 5) it has never needed; feasible with existing opcodes (`lea`/`store`/`load` all encode [rsp+disp]) but a real mechanism step-up. **(B) ucrtbase route** (`_configure_narrow_argv` + `__p___argc()`/`__p___argv()`) — the modern CRT's accessors — the same out-parameter shape, different import library (ucrtbase.dll — a NEW DT-equivalent import DSS doesn't carry today). **(C) kernel32 `GetCommandLineA` + trampoline-side parsing** — rejected: quoting/escaping rules make the parser a small program, absurd as hand-built LIR. Design question for the §B ask: extend the trampoline (A) vs synthesize a tiny compiler-generated pre-main INIT FUNCTION in ordinary MIR/LIR (where stack locals + calls are free) that the trampoline calls — the latter generalizes to future per-OS runtime init (atexit wiring, environ).
