| `D-OPT-RELEASE-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-REG-ARG-DROP` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (T2 miscompile-hunt) — ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED; this single root ALSO closed [[D-OPT-RELEASE-FP-CONSTANT-UNLOADED-XMM]].** ROOT: the MIR `Arg` payload carried ONLY a per-ABI-class register ORDINAL. On a SysV-style CC the GPR/FPR pools count INDEPENDENTLY, so `int a, double x` gives a=gpr#0 AND x=fpr#0 — BOTH ordinal 0. The INLINER (`emitCalleeInst`) mapped a callee Arg → the caller's actual BY that ordinal, so a mixed int+FP callee spliced the WRONG actuals (`(int)x` read the int arg → the release build returned 0 for `g(1,47.0)`; ms_x64 immune — its slot-aligned CC makes ordinal==position). Bisect: `[Identity,Inlining]` reproduces, `[Identity]` correct, the verifier did NOT flag it (disasm: `cvttss2si %xmm15` — a GPR ordinal encoded through the XMM field). FIX (independent design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES, F1-F8 folded): the Arg payload now packs `(position<<16)|ordinal` (`core/types/arg_payload.hpp`); `argIndex()`=ordinal (lir_callconv unchanged), NEW `argPosition()`=the FLAT call-operand position; `hir_to_mir` threads a flat position counter through EVERY receive site (scalar/piece/byref/va_list/sret-hidden + advancing for the two no-Arg operand shapes: x8-sret ReadIndirectResult + straddle RecvByValueStackParam); the inliner's gate + splice map by POSITION (+ a gate type-consistency refusal backstop); ★ F1 (audit CRITICAL): `addInst` REFUSES Arg (dedicated builder) so the 3 `addArg` re-encode sites (`mir_rebuild_helper.cpp` — EVERY pass; `mir_merge.cpp` — the 2-TU path; `inlining.cpp` MultiBlockInliner host) thread the position or it dies before Inlining runs; F5 a MirVerifier duplicate-position check (`I_ArgPositionDuplicate=0xA012`); F2 mir_text round-trip. WITNESSES: repro `g(1,47.0)` release 0→47 + `isum(2,10,3,20,43)` 78 on x86_64 AND arm64 (both = gcc); corpus `inline_mixed_class_args` (release arm, all legs) + `inline_mixed_class_args_crosscu` (the 2-TU merge witness) + `mixed_class_stack_overflow` RE-ARMED with its release arm (was omitted for the FP-constant bug, now fixed); MIR-tier `Inlining.{MixedClassCalleeSplicesFpArgByPositionNotOrdinal (operand-identity), ArgPositionSurvivesRebuildRoundTrip}`; 3-leg gate. ★ F8 opened: [[D-OPT-LIR-ARG-REGISTER-CLASS-MISMATCH-FAILLOUD]] (the encoder silently accepted a GPR ordinal through an XMM field — the missing backstop). | ✅ (CLOSED) | T2 miscompile-hunt 2026-07-07; `core/types/arg_payload.hpp` + `mir/mir.{hpp,cpp}` + `mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` + `opt/passes/{inlining,mir_rebuild_helper}.cpp` + `mir/merge/mir_merge.cpp` + `mir/mir_verifier.cpp` + `mir/mir_text.cpp` + `core/types/parse_diagnostic.{hpp,cpp}` + examples + `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp`; ORIGINAL discovery p19 c77 verifier a1b4246e (proven pre-existing on c76). **PRE-FIX detail below (kept for the audit trail):** 🔴 **OPEN / SILENT MISCOMPILE (release pipeline; discovered p19 c77 verifier a1b4246e, 2026-07-01; PRE-EXISTING, NOT a c77 defect).** On x86-64 SysV (elf64) under the `release` shipped pipeline, a function with a leading GPR (integer/pointer) arg FOLLOWED by an FP arg DROPS the FP arg. Minimal repro `int g(int a, double x){ return (int)x; }` → release SysV returns 0, gcc returns x's value (47); DEBUG is CORRECT; Windows ms_x64 CORRECT — only SysV-release wrong. PROOF pre-existing (NOT c77): the repro has ZERO spilled call args so c77's SpillSlotRef path is never taken; stashing c77 + rebuilding the c76 baseline yields BYTE-IDENTICAL wrong values (p4a=6/p4b=197/z=0 on both). Likely the REGISTER-arg facet of the SysV mixed-class family — DISTINCT from the CLOSED overflow-offset [[D-FF3-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-STACK-OVERFLOW-OFFSET]] (this is IN-REGISTER args, release-ONLY → an OPTIMIZER×mixed-class-arg interaction, possibly kin to [[D-OPT-RELEASE-FP-CONSTANT-UNLOADED-XMM]]). Impact on sqlite-green: sqlite is int/pointer-heavy (few mixed int+FP fns) → a RUN-green concern IF such a fn runs under release, in-scope then [[feedback_address_any_sqlite_green_blocker_2026_06_30]]; NOT the compile blocker (c78 = the x86_64 encoder gap). FIX (its own cycle): root-cause the release mixed-class register-arg placement/read (why an FP arg after a GPR arg is dropped under the optimizer). | (OPEN — a full /dss-cycle: root-cause the release mixed int+FP register-arg drop, gcc-cross-check 3 legs RUN, a red-on-disable witness) | discovered p19 c77 verifier a1b4246e (proven pre-existing on c76); a release-optimizer × mixed-class-arg concern; `src/mir`/`src/lir` arg placement/read + optimizer; plan 12 or an optimizer plan / SQLite-green RUN phase |

| `D-CSUBSET-SIZEOF-VALUE-OPERAND-TYPE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-02 (p19 Cluster G c89) — `sizeof <value-expr>` with an OPERATOR in the operand sizes the expression's RESULT type (C 6.5.3.4), not the first stamped leaf below it.** ROOT (the c88 smoke wall, INSTRUMENT+BISECT hunted by agent ad0a71b4 — a redzone-shim tracer + multi-TU driver bisection of sqlite's init chain): semantic Pass 2 stamped refs/members/calls/casts/literals but NOT operator nodes; `lowerSizeof` (cst_to_hir.cpp:4454-74) recovered the sized type via `resolveStampedTypeBelow`, a blind first-stamped-descendant DFS that sailed past the unstamped `*p` into the leaf `p` → `sizeof(*p)`=8 (the POINTER), `sizeof(tab[0])`=sizeof(the WHOLE array), `ArraySize(X)≡sizeof(X)/sizeof(X[0])`=**1**. THE DETONATION (gcc-gdb witnessed): shell.c main → sqlite3_initialize → `pthreadMutexAlloc` (sqlite3.c:30994) `p = sqlite3MallocZero( sizeof(*p) )` → 8 instead of 40 → glibc's own pthread_mutex_init/lock legitimately wrote the 40-byte recursive mutex → the malloc top chunk clobbered → sysmalloc SIGABRT on EVERY invocation (argv/input-independent — the c88 wall). FIX (20 lines, semantic_analyzer.cpp Pass-2 `sizeofValueRule` arm ~4264; NO §B): stamp the operand child with its full expression type via `subtreeType` (the exact deriver the Pass-1.5 array-dimension sizeof closure uses: Deref→pointee, Index→element, AddressOf→pointer); value-identical for already-stamped operand shapes; the TYPE form + Pass-1.5 untouched. WITNESS: examples/c-subset/sizeof_value_expression (15 rows, distinct exits; =42; RED-ON-DISABLE audit-witnessed → rows 1/2/4/5/6/11 mis-size [8/13 coincide pre-fix]) + the unit pin `HirLoweringCSubset.SizeofValueOperandCarriesExpressionType` (audit-written; fails pre-fix all 4 asserts) + codegen proof malloc($0x8)→($0x30) + repro2 SIGABRT→green. ★★ INDEPENDENT AUDIT (a7b96782, the FULL bar): **SOUND for the claimed scope** — a 38-row form-space grid on 3 legs × debug+release, every value == gcc; Pass-1.5/array-dims/static-inits untouched; fail-loud parity (sizeof(fn)/incomplete → H0009). 4 adjacent-family findings registered (NONE in sqlite3.c/shell.c — 1634 sizeof sites grepped): [[D-CSUBSET-SIZEOF-COMPARISON-INT-TYPE]] (F1/F2) + [[D-CSUBSET-SUBTREETYPE-UNARY-PROMOTION-DRIFT]] (F3) + [[D-CSUBSET-SIZEOF-DEREF-ARRAY-SILENT-FALLBACK]] (F4). Post-fix sqlite3: `--version` → 3.54.0 BYTE-IDENTICAL to gcc rc=0 (the binary's FIRST correct output); the sysmalloc assert GONE from every path. Eliminated suspects: pthread.json CORRECT (48=deliberate max-size); ROUND8 compiles correctly; [[D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-POINTER-IDENTITY]] NOT implicated (stays open). AUDIT NOTE: the fix touches the VALUE form only; audit leads = stamp-ABSENCE consumers on wrapper nodes, the silent `lowerSizeof` fallback residual (a fail-loud candidate), the sizeof(&s)/sizeof(f)/nested-sizeof grid. | `D-CSUBSET-ASSIGN-VALUE-RHS-COERCE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-02 (p19 Cluster G c90) — a plain assignment used as a VALUE (a comma operand, a chained assign, a condition, an operand of any operator) coerces the RHS to the LVALUE's type before the store (C 6.5.16p3).** ROOT (the post-c89 SQL SIGSEGV at sym_14500+523, INSTRUMENT+BISECT, gcc-gdb + shim forensics): BOTH HIR value-position arms in cst_to_hir.cpp — `finishAssign` (~1775) and `lowerBinary`'s Assign arm (~2670) — never coerced; the adjacent COMPOUND (OP=) arms DO (c74), statement assigns + initializers DO — exactly plain-`=`-as-value was missed (the c74 sibling). THE DETONATION: any SQL → first prepare → schema init → `estimateTableWidth` (sqlite3.c:128176) `for(i=pTab->nCol, pTabCol=pTab->aCol; …)` — the i16→int COMMA-operand assign stored 16 bits into i's 32-bit slot → i = 0x008c0005 instead of 5 → pTabCol walked 3822×16B past the 80-byte aCol → the `movzbq` SIGSEGV (arithmetic verified end-to-end). Manifestations (grid-proven): sub-int→wider = STALE upper bits; wider→sub-int = a full-width store CLOBBERING neighbors (real corruption); i32→i64 partial store (maskable by pre-zeroed slots — a dirty-stack harness defeats zero-luck); `if((i=v)>0)` passed on stack luck while carrying the same bad store (objdump-proven — why 546 tests stayed green). FIX (+11/−2, NO §B): one `coerce(…, lv.type)` per arm, mirroring the statement path; the lvWrite-producer sweep found no third gap. WITNESS: examples/c-subset/assign_value_coerce (7 distinct-exit checks: the exact sqlite struct-walk, neighbor-integrity BOTH directions, chains, double→int via a volatile source, the raw stale-upper witness; dirty() stack-junk defeats zero-luck; =42; RED-ON-DISABLE → checks 1/2/3/5/6/7 fail) + 23 grid rows == gcc. Post-fix: the SQL SIGSEGV GONE from every input; --version stays byte-identical. ★ The next frontier UNMASKED (c91): `free(): invalid pointer` in sqlite3ParserFinalize's `if(pParser->yystack != pParser->yystk0)` — the compare LOADS the array's first 8 bytes as a value instead of taking its ADDRESS = [[D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-POINTER-IDENTITY]], the TOP registered run-green deferral, its trigger NOW FIRED on the run path (single-TU red witness c90r4.c: member + GLOBAL compares wrong; plain-local correct). | (closed — coerce in finishAssign + the lowerBinary Assign arm) | p19 Cluster G c90; the post-c89 SQL frontier; the c74 [[D-CSUBSET-COMPOUND-ASSIGN-VALUE-PROMOTION]] sibling; independent convergence by 2 hunt agents; `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp`; plan 23 / SQLite-green RUN phase |
| `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ASSIGN-VALUE-POSITION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-20 (Option B — the single-chokepoint no-drift design; closed the NAMED-member `.`/`->` bit-field mutation class [value =/+=/++/-- AND statement +=/++/--], not just value-position plain-`=`; the anon-member + array-arrow residual is now FAIL-LOUD, `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ANON-ARROW-MUTATION-RESIDUAL`).** ROOT (audit-traced): `classifyLvalue` + `classifyIncDecLvalue` bound `&(bf.a)` (a sub-unit address, via `combineMemberAddr`'s UNIT byte offset — zero bit-field awareness) into a temp pointer and emitted a plain `*p` Deref target; MIR's bit-field RMW keys on a **MemberAccess** target (`bitfieldPlacementOf`, hir_to_mir.cpp) → the Deref silently took the plain full-unit `emitScalarStore` arm → clobbered packed neighbours + skipped truncation, and the value-position read-back (`*p` Deref Load) yielded the un-truncated unit. Statement plain-`=` (`lowerAssign`) was correct because it passes the raw MemberAccess as target. NAMED-member `.`/`->` mutation class fixed (all shared the classifyLvalue/classifyIncDecLvalue → lvWrite Deref seam): value-position `(bf.a=V)`/`(bf.a+=1)`/`(bf.a++)`/`(++bf.a)` AND statement compound/inc-dec `bf.a+=1;`/`bf.a++;`/`--bf.a;`. RESIDUAL (now FAIL-LOUD, NOT silent — `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ANON-ARROW-MUTATION-RESIDUAL`): a bit-field mutation through an ANONYMOUS-member hop chain or an ARRAY-arrow decay base (the single-`memberFieldIdx` Lvalue can't synthesize the multi-hop chain) → `S_BitfieldMutationUnsupportedBase` (unsuppressable); statement plain-`=` on such a member stays correct via `lowerAssign`; non-bit-field members through those bases stay on the correct generic path. FIX (Option B, HIR-lowering restructure, agnostic — no lang/arch/format branch, NO new store/mask logic): a new `classifyMemberLvalue` binds the CONTAINING AGGREGATE's address in `prep` (`p = &base` for `.`, the base pointer itself for `->`; a struct IS addressable, a bit-field sub-unit is NOT) and marks the `Lvalue` so the new `lvNode`/`lvRead`/`lvWrite` RECONSTRUCT `MemberAccess(Deref(p), fieldIdx)` — routing BOTH statement and value forms through the ONE existing RMW chokepoint (`emitBitfieldInsert` on write / `emitBitfieldExtract` on read). A NON-bit-field member reconstructs to the SAME MemberAccess a plain `s.x = v` uses → a plain scalar store (unaffected — the #1 blast-radius guard, example-verified). The base is lowered EXACTLY once (side-effect-once for `arr[i++].a`/`f()->a`). HIR is a strict single-parent tree (double-attach aborts loud), so the base is re-lowered from the postfix's `baseN` (not the committed child); field resolution is the SHARED, non-emitting `resolveMemberField` (refactored out of `combineMember`, so the rvalue + lvalue paths cannot disagree on which field/index a member names) and the container-volatility qualification mirrors `combineMember`'s `volatileQualifiedAccess` exactly → the reconstructed node is byte-identical to the rvalue/statement member access. Anonymous-member fields + array-arrow bases return nullopt → the UNCHANGED generic path (no regression). WITNESS: `tests/mir/test_mir_lowering_c_subset.cpp` BitFieldMutationValueAndCompoundFormsAreReadModifyWrite (7 fns × value+statement forms assert Or+Shl+And RMW, NOT a bare Store; value `=` also asserts the LShr extract read-back; RED-ON-DISABLE = revert the aggregate-address reconstruction → Or/Shl vanish) + the existing BitFieldReadExtractsAndWriteIsReadModifyWrite stays green + `examples/c-subset/bitfield_mutation_forms` (value =/+=/++/-- + statement +=/++/-- on `unsigned a:4,b:4`, neighbour-survival, a non-bit-field member row, a `ga[i++].a` single-eval row → 42; release-pipeline arm). PROBE scratchpad/miscompile_probes/p1_bitfield_assign_value.c: pre-fix mis-stored → now exit 42 (p2-p5 stay clean). PRE-FIX audit trail (the c90 audit a68ff1d3, 2026-07-02): a BITFIELD lvalue assigned in VALUE position (`x = (bi.a = 300)`) took a plain FULL-UNIT store of the UN-truncated RHS, clobbering neighbours + yielding the raw unit read (x=300 where C 6.5.16p3/gcc say 4); same on u8-unit + SIGNED int:4, all 3 legs. | ✅ (CLOSED 2026-07-20 — classifyMemberLvalue aggregate-address bind + lvNode MemberAccess reconstruction; shared resolveMemberField) | the c90 audit (a68ff1d3); CLOSED 2026-07-20; `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (classifyMemberLvalue / lvNode / resolveMemberField / classifyLvalue / classifyIncDecLvalue) + `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` bitfieldPlacementOf (chokepoint, unchanged); the c73 bit-field-RMW sibling; plan 23 / SQLite-green RUN phase |

| `D-CSUBSET-COMPARISON-SEMANTIC-INT-HIR-I1-DIVERGENCE` | ✅ **DOCUMENTED-DIVERGENCE (opened + closed 2026-07-20 by the subtreeType comparison/unary correctness cycle) — a DELIBERATE, NOT-a-bug tier divergence, anchored to stop a future reader "aligning the tiers".** The SEMANTIC type (subtreeType, the type-oracle) of a comparison/logical result (`< > <= >= == != && || !`) is C's LANGUAGE `int` ([[D-CSUBSET-SIZEOF-COMPARISON-INT-TYPE]]); the CST→HIR SSA CARRIER (cst_to_hir combineBinary comparison/`&&`/`||` + unary `!`) stays i1/Bool — a MACHINE detail, widened to int on any arithmetic use. Different PROPERTIES by design, EXACTLY like char→i32: the semantic tier answers "what C type is this" (sizeof/auto/_Generic/call-arg checking), the HIR tier answers "what SSA value carries it". Flipping the i1 carrier to a full int would reintroduce the REJECTED global-flip codegen cost (int-width materialization on every branch/compare) with ZERO language-visible gain — the §B the user ratified as subtreeType-ONLY. ORIGINAL defect (pre-TF-C28): `derefResultType(Array)` = Invalid (no decay arm) → the operand stayed UNSTAMPED → lowerSizeof's `resolveStampedTypeBelow` silently DFS-fell to the LEAF: sizeof(*arr10)=40 (gcc 4), sizeof(*m[3][4])=48 (gcc 16), sizeof(*"hello")=6 (gcc 1). ★ TF-C28 (`632ac8d1`) had since MASKED the wrong VALUES by adding a MANUAL Array→Ptr pre-decay at BOTH tiers' Deref CALL sites — but left the shared law itself Ptr-only (a 2-site DUPLICATION / drift surface) and the DFS silent-guess wall STILL MISSING (the class stayed open; any future unstamped value-form operand would mis-size). This cycle closes BOTH residuals (per the audit "no silent guess may survive"): **(a)** the shared `derefResultType` law gained an Array arm (`Array<T,N>→T`, the sibling of `indexResultType` which already decays an Array base for `[]`) — the semantic tier's `subtreeType` combineUnary Deref now RELIES on it and its duplicated manual pre-decay was dropped → ONE source, no tier drift (cst_to_hir's Deref keeps its call-site decay because it also emits the materializing Cast MIR loads through). **(b)** lowerSizeof's VALUE form reads the operand's DIRECT stamp (`model.typeAt`) and FAILS LOUD (H0009) when it is missing — it NEVER descends into a sub-expression, so the silent-guess can never mis-size again (the TYPE form keeps the legitimate leaf-descent). FIX (cst_to_hir.cpp, NO §B — reuses the existing decay arm, no new mechanism): a new `coerceCallArg(E, paramType)` — a valid paramType coerces byte-identically; an INVALID paramType (variadic tail / unknown sig) with an Array-typed arg decays via the EXISTING coerce decay arm to pointer-to-elem; swapped into all FOUR call-arg lowering sites (work-stack phase-2 [the sqlite path], pumpCallArgs, the recursive Call arm, the SQL flat-call). | `D-CSUBSET-VARIADIC-DEFAULT-ARG-PROMOTION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-22 (the variadic default-argument-promotion miscompile cycle) — C 6.5.2.2p6 default ARGUMENT promotions are applied to a SCALAR variadic-tail / K&R-unprototyped arg in `coerceCallArg`: `float`→`double` (FPExt) and integer-narrower-than-`int`→`int` (SExt for a signed source, ZExt for unsigned).** ROOT: `coerceCallArg` (cst_to_hir.cpp) previously did ONLY the c79 Array→Ptr decay for an invalid-paramType (variadic) arg and returned the scalar UNCHANGED; a narrow signed int then sat in a 32-bit reg with the default ZERO-fill → a signed `short -1` arrived as 65535, a `signed char -1` as 255 (a SILENT miscompile — the correct assignment / declared-`int`-param path funnels coerce→makeCast→mapCast `isSignedInt(from)?SExt:ZExt`, but the variadic tail never invoked it). Broke sqlite's `sqlite3_expert_new` (nArg=-1 → 65535 > SQLITE_MAX_FUNCTION_ARG → rejected); a `float` variadic arg was pushed 32-bit where `va_arg(ap,double)` reads 64 → garbage. This was the explicit "out of scope" deferral of [[D-CSUBSET-VARIADIC-ARG-ARRAY-DECAY]] (c79). FIX (HIR tier — the default promotions ARE a typing operation, so the tier owning the coerce/makeCast machinery is their natural home; NO §B, NO new mechanism): in `coerceCallArg`, when paramType is invalid and the arg is scalar, `float`(F32)→`coerce`(F64) and every other arithmetic arg → the shared `promoteSubIntArith` (`integerPromotedType`+`coerce`) — the SAME chokepoint `coerceCondition` / the switch-discriminant path use, NO hand-rolled rank logic. Config-gated (a language with no `arithmeticConversions` block has no `arith_` → inert; only c-subset opts in — no lang/arch/format branch). ABI class UNCHANGED (sub-int→int stays GPR, float→double stays FPR). WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/varargs_default_arg_promotion` (release-arm; signed short/char -1 → -1, unsigned short 65535 control stays 65535 [the SExt-vs-ZExt discriminator: identical low-16 bits, different 32-bit result], float -1.5f → -1.5; runtime volatile-derived operands the optimizer can't fold → 42) + the HIR pin `tests/hir/test_hir_lowering_c_subset.cpp::VariadicTailScalarArgGetsDefaultArgPromotion`. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the promotion → the signed cases become 65535/255 → exit 1 not 42; the HIR pin's Cast/type asserts flip. | (closed — `coerceCallArg` scalar default-arg promotion via `promoteSubIntArith` + the F32→F64 widen) | 2026-07-22 the variadic default-arg-promotion cycle; the successor to [[D-CSUBSET-VARIADIC-ARG-ARRAY-DECAY]]'s explicit int/float out-of-scope note; `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` `coerceCallArg`; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |

| `D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-IN-CONDITION` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-02 (p19 Cluster G c91, with [[D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-POINTER-IDENTITY]] — the same funnel, one cycle as this row planned).** A string literal / ANY array in a CONDITION position (`if ("x")`, `if (arr)`, `ok && arr`, `arr ? a : b`, and `!arr` via combineUnaryOp) decays to Ptr<elem> (C 6.3.2.1p3 + 6.8.4.1p1): coerceCondition gains the Array arm — decay through the ONE coerce funnel, then RE-ENTER coerceCondition so the decayed pointer takes the EXISTING null-pointer `Ne` arm (an object's address is never null → C's always-true truth value; gcc -Waddress warns, compiles). `!array` decays in combineUnaryOp's Not arm to the same effect (constant false). Neg/BitNot on arrays deliberately NOT decayed (C requires arithmetic operands there; stays loud). WITNESS: corpus `array_decay_pointer_identity` (the condition/`!`/ternary checks) + the HIR unit pin (condition + `!` asserts; red-on-disable witnessed: I_ ×3 loud / pin red). | `D-LK-ELF-SYMBOL-VERSIONING` | ✅ **CLOSED c156 (2026-07-16, roadmap 0a — the FIRED realpath bug; §B version-source fork RESOLVED → option (a) descriptor-pinned) — DSS-emitted ELF dynamic images now emit the full symbol-version REQUIREMENT trio (`.gnu.version` Versym + `.gnu.version_r` Verneed/Vernaux + DT_VERSYM/VERNEED/VERNEEDNUM), so a versioned glibc import binds its DECLARED version instead of misbinding an unversioned reference to the library's OLDEST compat instance.** REPRODUCED (WSL glibc 2.39): a DSS `realpath(".", NULL)` exec printed `REALPATH_NULL` + exit 3 (`readelf -V` = "No version information found"; `--dyn-syms` = `realpath` UNVERSIONED UND — the unversioned ref bound `realpath@GLIBC_2.2.5`, whose pre-2.3 form EINVALs a NULL resolved buffer); gcc's binary exits 42 with `.gnu.version_r` requiring `realpath@GLIBC_2.3`. FIX (config + `src/link/format/elf.cpp`, AGNOSTIC): a NEW optional per-symbol `version` descriptor field — resolved PER-TARGET via the EXISTING `variants` (`when:{arch?,format?}`) mechanism (the version STRING is arch-specific: glibc realpath = `GLIBC_2.3` on x86_64 but the single baseline `GLIBC_2.17` on aarch64) to a plain string that threads the `mangledName`/`libraryPath` rail (ShippedSymbol→ShippedExternSymbol→HirExternRecord→ExternDeclRef→FfiMetadata→ExternImport) to the ELF writer, which emits the trio EXACTLY as it emits DT_NEEDED from `libraryPath` — NO arch/format/symbol-name branch in the walker. OPT-IN + gated: `emitVersioning` fires only when ≥1 import declares a version, so a versionless image (every existing corpus/test) is BYTE-IDENTICAL to pre-c156 (the trio + 2 section headers + `.shstrtab` names + the layout offsets are all conditional; the nested 2|4|8 alignUps collapse to the original `alignUp(hashOff+hashSz,8)` when off); an unversioned import stamps Versym `VER_NDX_GLOBAL` (1) + no verneed. `stdlib.json` pins realpath's x86_64/elf requirement to `GLIBC_2.3` (arm64 stays unversioned — its 2.17 baseline binds fine). §B RESOLUTION — **option (a) descriptor-pinned** over (b) read-libc-verdef-live: the default version of a glibc base symbol is STABLE + well-known (realpath@@GLIBC_2.3 since glibc 2.3 / ~2002), config fits the agnostic model, needs no libc-location logic, is deterministic + target-environment-independent; (b) is heavier + target-env-dependent + overlaps the unfired consumer-wiring work. The version hash is the classic SysV `elf_hash` (`elf_hash("GLIBC_2.3")` == 0x0d696913, byte-verified against gcc's verneed). WITNESSES (WSL glibc 2.39): **W1** — the reproduced case RUNS: `realpath(".", NULL)` prints `/tmp` + exit 42; `readelf -V` shows `.gnu.version_r` File libc.so.6 / Cnt 1 / Name GLIBC_2.3 / Version 2 + Versym slot 2 = `2 (GLIBC_2.3)` (all 79 other imports `1 (*global*)`); `--dyn-syms` = `realpath@GLIBC_2.3 (2)`; `LD_DEBUG=bindings` = "normal symbol `realpath' [GLIBC_2.3]"; LD_BIND_NOW eager load = 42. **W2** — corpus `examples/c-subset/shipped_realpath_versioned` (realpath NULL form + free + unversioned-sibling strtoll → exit 42; runOn linux; x86_64 native 42 + arm64 qemu 42 + release-pipeline arm 42). **W3** (opt-in regression) — the SAME source on arm64 emits NO version machinery ("No version information found"; realpath plain UND) + runs 42; the existing `posix_shell_symbols` realpath consumer (buffer form) still exits 42 on BOTH arches. TESTS: `ElfExecWriter.{VersionedImportEmitsVerneedVersymAndDynamicEntries, UnversionedImportEmitsNoVersionMachinery, MixedVersionedAndUnversionedImportsVersymDiscriminates}` (byte-pin the Verneed/Vernaux/Versym + DT_VER* + the independently-computed elf_hash; red-on-disable) + `ShippedLibDescriptor.{SymbolVersionFlatStringDecodes, SymbolVersionVariantSelectsPerTarget, SymbolVersionMalformedShapesFailLoud}`. ★ SILENT-HOLE FOLD (independent review, same cycle): the goal-2 SUPPRESSED-shipped synthesis path dropped the version — a TU that both `#include <stdlib.h>` AND redeclares `realpath` (the common feature-test-macro pattern) suppresses the descriptor's versioned injection and synthesizes the import through the bare-proto path (cst_to_hir.cpp), which passed no version, silently reintroducing the SAME realpath@GLIBC_2.2.5 misbind. FIX: `SemanticModel.suppressedShippedLibraries_` now stores `SuppressedShippedSymbol{library, version}` (was library-map-only), populated with `sym.version` at semantic injection (semantic_analyzer.cpp) + threaded to the synthesized `HirExternRecord` (cst_to_hir.cpp); the dormant FF1 `ingest.cpp` path + the `hir_text` FfiMetadata serializer (`fmtFfi`/`parseFfi`, conditional like `soname`) gained version parity too. WITNESS: NEW corpus `shipped_realpath_redecl_versioned` (the redeclaration shape → x86_64 emits `realpath@GLIBC_2.3` + exit 42, arm64 unversioned + 42; RED-ON-DISABLE WITNESSED — neuter the synthesized version → x86_64 emits no `.gnu.version_r` → misbinds @GLIBC_2.2.5 → exit 3). Win ctest **662/662** (660 + 2 examples). — ORIGINAL: 🔴 **OPEN (discovered p19 c82, 2026-07-02; a RUN-degradation class, witnessed live).** DSS's ELF dynamic imports carry NO symbol-version info (no `.gnu.version` / `.gnu.version_r` sections), and the dynamic linker binds an UNVERSIONED reference to the OLDEST version of a multi-versioned glibc symbol (compat-first semantics for pre-versioning binaries). WITNESSED: `realpath(".", NULL)` through the c82-shipped stdlib.json symbol returned NULL/errno=EINVAL on native x86_64-elf — the binding landed `realpath@GLIBC_2.2.5` (the pre-2.3 compat version that REJECTS a NULL resolved buffer) instead of the `realpath@@GLIBC_2.3` default; qemu-aarch64 was UNAFFECTED (aarch64 glibc baseline 2.17 has a single version). EXPOSURE (enumerated against the current shipped surface): exactly TWO dual-versioned intersections — `realpath` (REAL: the NULL-buffer form degrades; sqlite shell.c:25343 calls exactly `realpath(zPath, NULL)` on its .open canonicalization path → a run-green concern the moment that path executes) and `memcpy` (2.14 vs 2.2.5 — LATENT-SAFE: the old binding is semantically correct for conforming non-overlapping callers; sqlite is clean). libc carries ~217 dual-versioned names total, so the class GROWS with every shipped symbol. **c87 UPDATE (libm joined the import surface via D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED):** libm.so.6 adds FOUR dual-versioned intersections — `pow`/`log`/`exp` (GLIBC_2.2.5 compat vs the 2.29 defaults) + `fmod` (2.2.5 vs 2.38) — ALL VALUE-SAFE empirically: a 29-probe bit-exact comparison (DSS's unversioned binding vs gcc's default binding, glibc 2.39; pow×6/log×5/exp×4/fmod + the single-versioned rest; scratchpad c87libm/vercheck-verdict.txt) came back BYTE-IDENTICAL — glibc's compat wrappers route to the SAME __ieee754_* cores, so the residual divergence is SVID errno/matherr handling on DOMAIN-ERROR inputs only (e.g. the errno shape of pow(-1,0.5)), plus any future glibc that changes the cores without re-versioning the compat aliases. The anchor stays OPEN on the realpath-class REAL degradations; the libm additions raise the surface, not the severity. FIX (its own cycle, linker feature work): emit `.gnu.version` (one u16 per dynsym) + `.gnu.version_r` (a VERNEED block per DT_NEEDED lib naming the required version per import) so every import binds its DEFAULT version — the standard toolchain behavior; needs the per-symbol version STRING (readable from the build machine's libc at descriptor-verification time, or pinned in the descriptor). WORKAROUND until then: shipped-lib consumers avoid version-divergent argument forms (the c82 posix_shell_symbols example uses the buffer-form realpath, version-portable on every binding). | (closed c156 — the `.gnu.version`/`.gnu.version_r`/DT_VER* emission gated on the opt-in per-symbol `version` descriptor field, option (a) descriptor-pinned) | discovered p19 c82 (the posix_shell_symbols x86_64 leg, errno=EINVAL bisect); `src/link/format/elf.cpp` (dynsym/verneed); the shipped-lib surface (`src/dss-config/shippedLibs/stdlib.json` realpath caveat); plan 14 linker / SQLite-green RUN phase (shell.c realpath-NULL) |

CONFIG-DRIVEN: `prototypeSynthesizesExtern` per-declaration flag (c-subset `topLevelDecl` + `varDecl` — a BLOCK-scope proto, re-homed per D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-PROTOTYPE, routes via the static-local `moduleDecls_` accumulator); `static`/weak protos NEVER synthesize (C 6.2.2p3 — internal/weak must not bind a sibling's public symbol; they keep the loud H0009); flag-off = the pre-c86 H0009 (RED-ON-DISABLE witnessed: flag off → the exact H0009 at bare_proto_crosscu's call). | `D-MIR-SYNTHETIC-GLOBAL-SYMBOL-ALIAS` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-02 (p19 Cluster G c86; discovered BY the c86 2-TU probe; PRE-EXISTING, merge-tier-visible only) — a synthetic literal-promoted MIR global's SymbolId could ALIAS an unrelated SEMANTIC record, making the LK11 merge fabricate a NAMED strong definition from an anonymous string/float literal.** ROOT: `mintSyntheticGlobalSymbol` (hir_to_mir) seeded `1 + max(functionSymbols ∪ globalSymbols)` — the MIR-VISIBLE ids only — but the semantic table also holds typedefs/tags/fields/locals/injected constants, and `mergeCuMirs`' `nameOf` maps EVERY MIR symbol to a name via `model.recordFor`. WITNESSED: the sqlite3.c+shell.c 2-TU probe reported five bogus `K_SymbolRedefinedAcrossUnits` on OPAQUE TAG names (`sqlite3_stmt`/`sqlite3_blob`/`sqlite3_pcache`/`Fts5Context`/`Fts5Tokenizer` — both TUs' string-literal globals aliased their own `typedef struct X X;` records; instrumented merge-def dump showed shell.c 'defs' named RUSAGE_SELF/ru_utime/rusage/sqlite3_context/… = descriptor constants + struct fields + tags, all init-carrying literal globals). WORSE-THAN-LOUD risk: an alias onto a record whose NAME a sibling CU genuinely defines would MIS-MERGE calls/loads onto a string literal (silent miscompile class) — the fix closes both. FIX: `MirLoweringConfig.syntheticSymbolFloor` (default 0 = pre-fix, golden-MIR fixture stability); compile_pipeline passes `model.symbols().size()`; the minter seeds `max(scan+1, floor)`. Single-CU emitted images are byte-equal except synthetic symbol NUMBERING (no golden asserts ids). | `D-LK-OBJECT-RELOC-ADDEND-CROSSTOOLCHAIN` | ✅ **CLOSED c140 (2026-07-14, ELF) — the ELF `.rela.text` producer now BAKES the target schema's `addendBias` into the emitted `r_addend` (`rel.addend + targetSchema.relocationInfo(kind)->addendBias`), so a FOREIGN linker receives the full psABI implicit addend.** FIX (`src/link/format/elf.cpp`, the `!isExec` `.rela.text` loop): a `call`/`lea` rel32 (R_X86_64_PC32, addendBias -4) now emits `r_addend = -4` (byte-matching gcc); abs64/abs32 (addendBias 0, loader-guaranteed by the `addendBias != 0 ⇒ pcRelative` invariant) unchanged; + a defensive `K_RelocationKindMismatch` fail-loud if the target schema lacks the kind's `TargetRelocationInfo`. DSS's OWN in-place exec applier (`applyExecRelocations`) is UNTOUCHED (it keeps `addend + addendBias` separate and emits no `.rela`); DSS never re-reads its own `.o` (the FFI reader parses only a FOREIGN lib's `.dynsym`/`.dynstr`), so the baked addend is consumed exclusively by external linkers — no double-count. PE/Mach-O objects are STRUCTURALLY unaffected: they store addends IN-FIELD (REL32/BRANCH relative-to-instruction-END conventions) and fail-loud on a non-zero `rel.addend`, so a call (addend 0) is already correct; the RELA explicit-addend model is ELF-only. WITNESSES: `ElfWriter.RelaRecordsNativeRelocTypeAndSymtabIndex` (rel.addend 0 → r_addend -4; red-on-disable — reverting to `rel.addend` verbatim emits 0 → fails) + independent silent-failure review (VERIFIED-CLEAN) + ctest 639/639. ★ END-TO-END on WSL gcc 13.3: `dss_part.o` (`dss_add` calls the static `dss_helper`) → `readelf -r` now shows `R_X86_64_PC32 sym_8 - 4` ×2, and the gcc-linked program RUNS → **exit 42** (was SIGSEGV/139 before the fix). Unblocks running ANY DSS object with intra-object calls under a foreign linker — the next step toward the SQLite testfixture route. — ORIGINAL (discovered 2026-07-14): 🔴 **OPEN (discovered 2026-07-14 by [[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-SYMBOL-NAMES]]'s end-to-end witness).** DSS relocatable-object `.rela.text` emits a `call rel32` R_X86_64_PC32 reloc with `r_addend = 0`, but the x86-64 psABI (and gcc's `ld`) require `r_addend = -4` for a rel32 field — the field is relative to the instruction END, 4 bytes past the reloc offset (a gcc reference object of the same source shows `R_X86_64_PLT32 helper - 4`). DSS's OWN linker applies its relocs with a compensating convention (the whole corpus runs green), and the EXEC path applies relocations IN-PLACE (never emitting `.rela`), so this is INVISIBLE to intra-DSS linking + every CI leg. It only bites cross-toolchain: the instant [[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-SYMBOL-NAMES]] made `gcc main.c dss.o` LINK, a DSS object with ANY intra-object CALL SIGSEGVs at runtime — the call lands 4 bytes into the callee (mid-instruction). VERIFIED (WSL gcc 13.3, 2026-07-14): `dss_part.o` (`dss_add` calls static `dss_helper`) → `readelf -r` shows `R_X86_64_PC32 sym_8 + 0` ×2 → the gcc-linked program SIGSEGVs (exit 139); the LEAF variant (no intra-object calls → zero `.rela` entries) runs → exit 42. **Impact:** blocks running ANY realistic DSS object (sqlite3.c has thousands of intra-object calls) under a foreign linker — a hard prerequisite for the definitive testfixture/TCL-suite validation. **Trigger:** FIRED (the witness above). | Emit the psABI-correct `r_addend` for PC-relative relocs in the `.rela.text` producer (`-4` for a rel32 call/jmp field; verify per reloc kind AND per arch — ARM64 branch/ADRP relocs use different addend conventions), OR normalize DSS's own reloc-application convention to the ELF standard so DSS's linker and gcc's `ld` agree on one `.rela`. Verify: `gcc main.c dss_part.o` with an intra-object call runs → 42; a gcc-vs-DSS `.rela` addend diff is byte-clean. Priority: normal (trigger-gated on the cross-toolchain route; no in-tree consumer until testfixture). | [[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-SYMBOL-NAMES]] (exposed by its link-success — the EXPORT-naming dual); `src/link/format/elf.cpp` (`.rela.text` `appendI64LE(relaText, rel.addend)`); `src/asm/` (the reloc `addend` origin in codegen); plan 14 linker / SQLite testfixture route |

Deferred (fail-loud, not silent): TLS `tdata`/`tbss` (the linker `acceptsDataSection` gate + the arm's TLS reject) + data-INTERNAL relocations `.rela.data` (`buildExecDataSection` allowItemRelocations=false). | `D-LK-OBJECT-NOLIB-EXTERN-RELOCATABLE` | ✅ **CLOSED c143 (2026-07-14) — a REFERENCED bare-prototype no-library extern (the `SQLITE_API` shape: `int sqlite3_foo(...);` — no `extern`, no import library) is now KEPT as an SHN_UNDEF symbol in a relocatable object instead of rejected; the FINAL (foreign) linker resolves it.** FIX (`src/link/linker.cpp` `rejectOrDropUnboundExterns`): the referenced-no-library-extern reject is now IMAGE-scoped via a new `outputIsImage` param (from the caller's `objectFormatSchema.isImageFlavor()` — the existing cross-format exec-vs-relocatable predicate). For an IMAGE (ELF ET_EXEC / PE Exec/Dll / Mach-O MH_EXECUTE) an unresolved no-library extern is a load-time silent-failure → K_SymbolUndefined (UNCHANGED). For a RELOCATABLE object it is a LEGAL undefined symbol the final linker binds against a sibling object or a library → KEPT (the ET_REL writer's undefined-symbol loop emits it by `mangledName` via `externName`, c141). Unreferenced no-library externs are still dropped (dead surface) for both. WITNESSES: `Linker.RelocatableKeepsReferencedNoLibraryExternAsUndefined` (no reject + `image.ok()` for a `.o`; red-on-disable) + `Linker.NoLibraryExternWithoutDefinitionIsUndefinedSymbol` (REALIGNED to an IMAGE format — the reject stays, exactly-once + names the symbol + blocks emission; the pre-existing test used a relocatable synthetic format, a flavor-mismatch this anchor exposes); ctest 639/639. ★ END-TO-END on WSL gcc 13.3: `int puts(const char*); static const char msg[] = "hi from dss"; int greet(void){ return puts(msg); }` (bare-proto extern + rodata global + extern call — exercises THIS + c141 + c142 together) → `.o` with `nm` = `T greet` + `U puts` + `R sym_<id>`; `gcc greetmain.c externdata.o` links (puts→libc) + runs → **prints "hi from dss"**. This is exactly the SQLITE_API extern shape — the next step toward compiling the sqlite3.c amalgamation. **LATENT NOTE (unreachable today):** `isImageFlavor()` returns FALSE for ELF `ET_DYN` / Mach-O `Dylib` (which ARE load-time images, incl. a PIE executable); wiring it into this reject means that IF a `dyn`/`dylib`/PIE output format were ever shipped + routed here, a referenced no-library extern in it would be KEPT (a deferred load-time failure) rather than rejected. No shipped format in `src/dss-config/object-formats/` declares `dyn`/`dll`/`dylib` today (only `rel`/`obj`/`object` + `exec`), so it cannot fire — revisit `isImageFlavor()`'s `ET_DYN` coverage when a shared-library / PIE output format lands (a PRE-EXISTING imprecision in the predicate, surfaced by this cycle's code-review). **RESOLVED c150 (2026-07-16, D-LK1-4 shared-library half):** `isImageFlavor()` now returns TRUE for ELF ET_DYN, and the undefined-extern policy moved off that predicate onto the schema-driven THREE-flavor `allowsUndefinedImports()` (relocatable → keep for the final linker; ELF ET_DYN → keep, ld.so resolves from the global scope — standard `ld -shared`; exec image → reject loud, this row's behavior unchanged). The "deferred load-time failure" this note feared is the CORRECT `.so` semantic, now deliberate + pinned (`ElfDynLinker.ReferencedNoLibraryExternKeptForDynRejectedForExec`). | (closed) | [[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-CALL-RELOCATABLE]] (the library-bound extern-call sibling this extends to no-library externs) + D-CSUBSET-BARE-PROTO-EXTERN-SYNTHESIS (the frontend that synthesizes the no-library extern) + the cross-toolchain object arc ([[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-SYMBOL-NAMES]] / [[D-LK-OBJECT-DATA-SECTION-RELOCATABLE]]); `src/link/linker.cpp` (`rejectOrDropUnboundExterns` + the `isImageFlavor` gate); plan 14 linker / SQLite testfixture route |
| `D-LK-OBJECT-DATA-EXTERN-RELOCATABLE` | ✅ **CLOSED c144 (2026-07-14) — a libc DATA extern (the `stdout` shape: `extern FILE *stdout;`, shipped `kind:object`) now lowers into an ELF relocatable object as an SHN_UNDEF NOTYPE symbol + a plain PC32 reloc that a FOREIGN linker resolves by copy-relocation; `gcc main.c dss.o` links + binds `stdout` identically to the gcc side.** The last blocker before the real `sqlite3.c` amalgamation reached the DATA-item-reloc stage (see next-blocker note). FIX — **(1)** `src/link/linker.cpp`: the `K_FormatLacksImportSupport` data-import reject (a format that declares no `dataImportBinding`) is now IMAGE-scoped via `objectFormatSchema.isImageFlavor()` — a relocatable object does NOT bind imports (no import walker), so a surviving data extern is emitted as SHN_UNDEF for the final linker; only an IMAGE (load-time-bound, no later linker) with no declared binding rejects (mirrors [[D-LK-OBJECT-NOLIB-EXTERN-RELOCATABLE]] for functions). **(2)** `src/link/format/elf.cpp` — the ET_REL writer's OWN blanket `isData` reject (the second guard, which claimed a plain UNDEF+reloc would "mis-bind shared-library data") is REMOVED: both extern kinds fall through to the normal ET_REL writer, which emits the referenced data extern as a NOTYPE UND symbol (the reloc-driven symtab loop, c141 `externName`) and drops the unreferenced ones. **(3)** `src/link/format/elf.cpp` — the PLT32 selection (`externTargets`→`externCallTargets`) now EXCLUDES `isData` externs: a DATA reference emits `nativeId`/PC32 (type 2), a function CALL keeps `pltNativeId`/PLT32 (type 4). This is the correctness core — a data extern routed through PLT32 would bind `stdout` to a linker-built PLT stub and the code would read jump-stub bytes as the `FILE*` value (the exact silent miscompile the image-path reject guarded, here prevented in the relocatable writer). EMPIRICAL CONTRACT: gcc emits `R_X86_64_PC32` (`-4`) + a `NOTYPE GLOBAL UND` symbol for `extern FILE *stdout` in a `.o` even under `enable-default-pie`/`-fPIE` (the `.o` targets an EXECUTABLE, where copy-relocations are allowed) — DSS now emits byte-identically. WITNESSES: `ElfWriter.ObjectDataExternRefEmitsUndefNameAndPc32NotPlt32` (a module referencing BOTH `stdout`→PC32 and `fputs`→PLT32; asserts the two reloc types + both NOTYPE UND — the discriminator pin; **red-on-disable DEMONSTRATED**: revert the `isData` exclusion → the data ref becomes PLT32 (type 4) → the assertion fails `Which is: 4` vs `2`) + `ElfRelWriter.ExternDataImportNoLongerFailsLoudOnEtRel` (the writer reject is lifted; unreferenced data extern dropped, no `K_FormatLacksImportSupport`) + `Linker.RelocatableKeepsReferencedDataExternAsUndefined` (the linker keeps a referenced data extern for a `.o`; red-on-disable on the `isImageFlavor` gate); ctest **639/639**. ★ END-TO-END on WSL gcc 13.3: `FILE *getout(void){return stdout;} int logline(const char*s){return fputs(s,stderr);}` → `.o` whose `readelf -r` = `R_X86_64_PC32 stdout - 4` + `R_X86_64_PC32 stderr - 4` + `R_X86_64_PLT32 fputs - 4` and `readelf -s` = `NOTYPE GLOBAL UND stdout/stderr/fputs` (byte-identical to a gcc `-c` reference of the same source); `gcc bindmain.c usestdout.o` links + runs, writes via both `getout()`→stdout and `logline()`→stderr, and `getout() == stdout` (pointer-identical — copy-relocation bound the extern to the same libc object the gcc-compiled `main` sees). **NEXT BLOCKER (the real amalgamation, exposed the instant this landed):** compiling the full 269k-line `sqlite3.c` → `.o` now advances PAST `stdout` and fails loud at the FIRST data→data relocation (`elf::encode (ET_REL): data item #1474 carries 36 relocation(s)` — a const pointer table) = the pre-existing [[D-LK1-ELF-RODATA-DATAITEM-RELOC]] (`.rela.data`/`.rela.rodata` emission), the next SQLite-testfixture cycle. **PINNED FUTURE TRIGGER (not a silent miscompile):** a data extern in a `.o` linked into a SHARED LIBRARY (`-fPIC`/`-shared`) would need a GOT-indirect binding (`R_X86_64_GOTPCREL`) — but that path is a LOUD ld error (`relocation R_X86_64_PC32 against undefined symbol ... recompile with -fPIC`), never a silent miscompile; a `.o`→`.so` consumer + a got-indirect `dataImportBinding` for the relocatable ELF format is the trigger (the GotIndirect LIR lowering already exists for macho — see `mir_to_lir.cpp` `externDataGotSymbols_`). | (closed for the executable-link data extern; shared-library GOTPCREL pinned; the next amalgamation blocker is [[D-LK1-ELF-RODATA-DATAITEM-RELOC]]) | [[D-LK-OBJECT-NOLIB-EXTERN-RELOCATABLE]] / [[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-CALL-RELOCATABLE]] / [[D-LK-OBJECT-DATA-SECTION-RELOCATABLE]] (the cross-toolchain object arc); [[D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT]] (the exec-side copy-relocation/GOT binding this is the relocatable dual of); `src/link/linker.cpp` (the `isImageFlavor` data-import gate) + `src/link/format/elf.cpp` (the writer reject-removal + `externCallTargets` isData exclusion); plan 14 linker / SQLite testfixture route |
| `D-PP-ELIFDEF-ELIFNDEF` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-09 — the C23 §6.10.1 `#elifdef`/`#elifndef` directives are implemented; the silent miscompile is FIXED.** Config-driven: 2 OPTIONAL `PreprocessConfig` directive words (`elifdefDirective`/`elifndefDirective`) routed through the existing conditional-group state machine `sbHandleElif` with `SbIfKind::Ifdef`/`Ifndef` + the shared `sbEvalDefinedName` — the SAME direct definedness test `#ifdef`/`#ifndef` use, NOT the `#if` expression evaluator; ZERO engine identity branch. TWO dispatch arms sit BEFORE the `stackActive()` gate so BOTH sub-paths close: the dead-enclosing silent-skip (a false `#if`/`#ifdef` arm no longer falls through a true `#elifdef` to `#else`) AND the live-after-taken spurious-unsupported path; fail-loud NAMES the right directive on a missing/malformed identifier (never a silent skip). Negative miscompile-pins (double-traced red-on-disable): `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_conditionals_elifdef` (42→2) + `_elifndef` (42→3). Independent design-audit + code-audit both GO; 3-leg gate ctest **606/606** (Win Debug + WSL x86_64 + arm64 qemu) + sqlite 3.54.0 + CRUD 42 on all 3 legs. Opened [[D-PP-SYNTHBUILDER-PREDEFINED-DEFINEDNESS]] (the pre-scan `isDefinedTok` predefined-macro gap the mirror INHERITED — fail-loud, not silent). ORIGINAL: 🔴 **OPEN (discovered 2026-07-08, hardened dss-state C23 battery).** The C23 preprocessor directives `#elifdef`/`#elifndef` (C23 §6.10.1 — shorthand for `#elif defined(X)` / `#elif !defined(X)`) are NOT recognized by the FC14 conditional-compilation substrate (which shipped `#if`/`#ifdef`/`#ifndef`/`#elif`/`#else`/`#endif` + `defined()`; `D-PP-CONDITIONAL-COMPILATION` ✅). ⚠ **SILENT MISCOMPILE, not fail-loud:** an unrecognized `#elifdef` is not treated as a conditional arm, so when the preceding `#if`/`#ifdef` arm is FALSE the true `#elifdef` group is SKIPPED and control falls through to `#else` → the wrong branch compiles, rc=0. VERIFIED by the `c23_elifdef` battery probe (`#define FEATURE_B` then `#ifdef FEATURE_A / #elifdef FEATURE_B / #else` → selects `#else`, exit **2** not 42, 2026-07-08). Witness is `#elifdef`; `#elifndef` is its unshipped C23 pair (same substrate gap). **Trigger:** FIRED — any C23 TU using `#elifdef`/`#elifndef` mis-selects today; the probe is in-tree. | Extend the FC14 condition-stack dispatcher (`MacroExpander`) so `#elifdef X` is `#elif defined(X)` and `#elifndef X` is `#elif !defined(X)` — SAME balance-tracking + `stackActive()` gating as `#elif`, operand resolved via the existing `defined` path BEFORE expansion; a real branch, FAIL-LOUD on a missing/malformed identifier (NOT a silent skip). Verify: `c23_elifdef` probe returns 42 + a fail-loud red on `#elifdef` with no identifier. Tracked as plan-23 **FC17.5** (feature) + plan-00 stepper **T2.3** (miscompile tier). Priority: normal — silent-miscompile class; close before any C23-conformance claim. | `D-PP-CONDITIONAL-COMPILATION` (✅ FC14 — the substrate this extends); `D-PP-VA-OPT` (sibling C23-preprocessor addition); plan-23 FC17.5; plan-00 stepper T2.3; `src/analysis/preprocess/` MacroExpander condition stack; dss-state probe `c23_elifdef` |
| `D-PP-SYNTHBUILDER-PREDEFINED-DEFINEDNESS` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-PP-ELIFDEF-ELIFNDEF]]).** The SynthBuilder PRE-SCAN's bare-name definedness check (`isDefinedTok`) consults only `localMacros`, NOT the predefined macros the `#if defined()` path (`definedCb`) is aware of — so a `#ifdef`/`#ifndef`/`#elifdef`/`#elifndef` guarding a quote-`#include` and testing a PREDEFINED macro can diverge from the authoritative pass IN THE PRE-SCAN. ⚠ **PRE-EXISTING — inherited verbatim from `#ifdef`** (the elifdef fix MIRRORED it, did not introduce it); worst-case symptom is a LOUD parse error (an unspliced quote-include → stray tokens), NEVER a silent miscompile. **Trigger:** a real consumer hits the pre-scan divergence for a predefined-macro-guarded quote-`#include`. | Unify `isDefinedTok` with `definedCb` so all four bare-name forms (`#ifdef`/`#ifndef`/`#elifdef`/`#elifndef`) agree with `#if defined()` in the pre-scan. Priority: normal (tracked limitation, not a blocker). | opened-by [[D-PP-ELIFDEF-ELIFNDEF]] (the elifdef cycle that surfaced it); [[D-PP-CONDITIONAL-COMPILATION]] (the FC14 substrate); `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`isDefinedTok` vs `definedCb`); dss-state preprocessor surface |
| `D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-09 (FC17 cycle 1) — C23 §6.4.4.6/§6.2.5 `nullptr`/nullptr_t: the predefined null-pointer constant + its distinct type + one-way conversions + comparison rules + a fail-loud operator gate.** NEW closed-enum `TypeKind::NullptrT` (append-only after VolatileQual), **SEMANTIC-TIER-ONLY**: the KEYSTONE (Fix 1(a)) lowers the `nullptr` literal DIRECTLY to the target-agnostic integer-0 null constant at the HIR tier (`cst_to_hir` `lowerLiteral`, keyed on the core kind), so NullptrT NEVER reaches MIR — reusing ALL existing null-pointer-constant lowering (coerce→Ptr, null comparison, ternary null-materialization, `if(nullptr)`→false). Config-driven: `nullptr` keyword + operand.alt + `literalTypes {NullptrKeyword,NullptrT,0}` + `coreTypeFromName` + a NEW `pointerConversions.nullPointerConstantFromNullptrT` flag (default false → toy/tsql inert). Conversions via ONE `isAssignable` chokepoint (Ptr←NullptrT, ONE-WAY — nothing converts TO nullptr_t) covering init/positional-init/assign/call-arg/return; `isExplicitCastable` (T*)nullptr only. Fail-loud: an EXPLICIT `S_NullptrInvalidOperand` (0xE033) SE-phase gate in `pass2Post` rejects nullptr as an arithmetic/bitwise/shift/relational/compound-assign operand, unary -/~, and ==/!= against a non-ptr/non-nullptr peer (WITHOUT it Fix 1(a) would silently compile `nullptr+1` as `0+1`); the gate ADMITS ==/!= vs ptr/nullptr, plain-assign, comma, logical &&/||. `combineTernary` NullptrT arm (Ptr-vs-nullptr → Ptr, both orders). MIR backstop `I_NullptrTypeInMir` (0xA014) — a never-fires tripwire (red-on-disable unit test). Runtime witness `examples/c-subset/nullptr_basic` exit 15 (opaque volatile pointer source so `p != nullptr` is a genuine runtime compare, release arm) + 2 diagnostic-corpus goldens (`nullptr_int_assign`→S_TypeMismatch, `nullptr_arithmetic`→S_NullptrInvalidOperand) + `test_type_rules`/`test_semantic_analyzer_c_subset`/`test_mir_verifier` unit tests + red-on-disable (flip the flag → S_TypeMismatch). Deferrals opened: [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-BOOL-CONVERSION]], [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-NONLITERAL-OPERAND]], [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-T-DECLARABLE]], [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-CONSTEXPR-FOLD]]. Independent design-audit (Fix 1(a)) + code-audit (VERIFIED-CLEAN; 2 MEDIUM folded — the FnSig/Array Eq/Ne peer-decay false-positive + a config-load cross-check requiring `nullPointerConstantFromIntegerZero` when the nullptr flag is set) + 3-leg gate ctest **607/607** (Win Debug + WSL x86_64 + arm64 qemu) + sqlite 3.54.0 + CRUD 42 on all 3 legs. | (closed) | plan-23 FC17; plan-00 stepper T3.2; `src/core/types/type_lattice/core_type.hpp` (NullptrT) + `type_layout.cpp` + `type_reintern.cpp`; `type_rules.hpp` (isAssignable/isExplicitCastable arms); `cst_to_hir.cpp` lowerLiteral (Fix 1(a)); `semantic_analyzer.cpp` (pass2Post gate + combineTernary + variadic reject); `mir_verifier.cpp` (tripwire); `c-subset.lang.json` + `grammar_schema_json.cpp` + `semantic_config.hpp`; `D-LANG-NULL-POINTER-CONSTANT` (the integer-0 sibling) |

FIX (C 6.3.1.2, config-driven, the MIRROR of `boolWidensToArith`): a NEW `scalarConvertsToBool` gate — (a) SEMANTIC `type_rules.hpp` `isAssignable` admits arithmetic (int rank/float/Char/Enum) OR pointer OR nullptr INTO a `_Bool` lhs; (b) CODEGEN `cst_to_hir.cpp` `coerce()` realizes it as the `!= 0` truthiness test via the ONE `coerceCondition` chokepoint (the `if(x)` shape) — NOT a low-bit-truncating Cast (`_Bool b=2` is TRUE), an Enum bridged to its underlying int first, a nullptr already an I32 0 so `Ne(0,0)`->false; (c) `(bool)nullptr` explicit cast admitted in `isExplicitCastable` (ungated, nullptr->0->false). Genuinely-incompatible sources (struct/union/void/FnSig) stay LOUD. Threaded through all 5 isAssignable call sites; wired via `semantic_config.hpp` + `grammar_schema_json.cpp` + c-subset.lang.json `scalarConvertsToBool:true` (toy/tsql keep `_Bool` strict). RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the isAssignable arm -> the corpus fails S0003. [PRE-FIX GATED TEXT — audit trail]: GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR]]). C23 §6.3.2.3.2 says `nullptr` converts to `bool` (yielding false), but the c-subset had NO scalar→bool conversion at all (`bool b = 0;` was itself S_TypeMismatch), so admitting ONLY nullptr→bool would be inconsistent AND hit the unrealized `Trunc→Bool` codegen form. Trigger was: general scalar→bool conversion lands. | ✅ (CLOSED 2026-07-20 — the `scalarConvertsToBool` gate: isAssignable scalar->Bool arm + coerce() `!= 0` truthiness + `(bool)nullptr` cast) | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR]]; CLOSED 2026-07-20 by the scalar->_Bool cycle (the code-audit follow-on to D-CSUBSET-SIZEOF-COMPARISON-INT-TYPE); `src/analysis/semantic/type_rules.hpp` (isAssignable scalar->Bool + isExplicitCastable nullptr arm) + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (coerce truthiness) + `semantic_config.hpp` + `grammar_schema_json.cpp` + `c-subset.lang.json`; plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-NONLITERAL-OPERAND` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR]]).** The `pass2Post` operator gate detects a nullptr operand via a BOUNDED transparent-descent (O(1): thin single-Internal-child wrappers/parens to a nullptr token, or an already-stamped NullptrT node) — chosen over a per-node `subtreeType` call to avoid re-typing every binary/unary operand (O(N²) on the sqlite hot path). It therefore does NOT catch a NullptrT value arising from a NON-literal subexpression used as an arithmetic/relational operand — `(c ? nullptr : nullptr) + 1` or `(x, nullptr) < p` (astronomically rare; no real code writes it). ⚠ Under the Fix 1(a) lowering these degrade to DEFINED 0-arithmetic (`0 + 1`), never a crash or wrong-value miscompile — a silent-ACCEPT of ill-formed code, not a silent miscompile. **Trigger:** a real program hits it. | Extend the detector to cover NullptrT-typed non-literal operands (stamp NullptrT expression results so the O(1) already-stamped check catches them, or a bounded typed check). Priority: normal. | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`pass2Post` nullptr operator gate `isNullptrOperand`) |
| `D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-T-DECLARABLE` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR]]).** `nullptr_t` as a SPELLABLE/declarable/storable type name (`nullptr_t x;`, the `<stddef.h>` typedef) is NOT supported this cycle — `nullptr_t` as a type name fails loud (unknown type), never silent. Making NullptrT a REAL storable value BREAKS the never-reaches-MIR keystone invariant → needs genuine MIR/LIR/regalloc/asm surface for the type. A materially larger cycle. **FC17.5 fold (2026-07-10, [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE]]):** `auto p = nullptr;` — an auto object whose INFERRED type would be nullptr_t — is the SAME deferral surface and rejects loud `S_AutoInferenceInvalid` (0xE044, unsuppressable; golden + unit pin) at the inference arm's NullptrT reject, protecting the keystone from the inference side too. **Trigger:** a real TU declares a `nullptr_t` object / needs the `<stddef.h>` typedef. | Give NullptrT a full value representation (pointer-width, all-zero) through MIR→codegen, retire/scope the `I_NullptrTypeInMir` tripwire, add the `nullptr_t` type-name, and admit the auto-inferred form. Priority: normal. | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR]]; the `I_NullptrTypeInMir` tripwire (`src/mir/mir_verifier.cpp`) it would retire; the FC17.5 auto NullptrT reject (`resolveAutoInferredDeclaration`) |
