Priority: normal (low-leverage). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR]]; the CST const-int evaluator (`src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp`) |
| `D-PARSE-SPECULATIVE-OPTIONAL` | ✅ **LANDED 2026-07-09 (FC17) — a GENERIC speculative/backtracking capability for `{"optional"}` grammar clauses.** A `{"optional": X, "speculative": true}` clause is TRIED after the shared prefix and ROLLED BACK to the sibling continuation when X does not match — the reusable parser improvement the enum underlying-type feature rides (its `enum <tag> :` prefix collides with the anonymous enum bit-field `enum C : 4;`). Engine (Fork B, audit-revised): (1) `PositionBuilder::build` extracts `applySpeculativeFlags(body,posId)` shared by the `"alt"` AND `"optional"` builders (a plain optional stays byte-identical — the bit is set only when `speculative` is present); the optional records its skip/continuation branch (`Position::setSkipBranch`, the 2nd branch == `cont`). (2) THE AUDIT FIX — `collectAltBranchRules` EXCLUDES a SPECULATIVE optional's skip branch from its OWN candidate enumeration (gated `posId==startPos`, so an enclosing non-speculative optional still reaches those rules; the non-speculative `altRuleBranches` route stays byte-identical), so a peek matching only the continuation yields `candidates=[]` → the nullable skip fires → the body parses NON-speculatively (avoids the D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER c25 cliff on the common bodied enum). (3) `parser.cpp stepOnce` speculative arm adds a nullable-fallback after the candidate loop (KEEP; the naive edit-(i) direct-descent guard was DROPPED per the audit). CRUX safety (verified empirically — 30/30 broad ctest): all shipped speculative alts are non-nullable-tailed, so the parser edit is inert for them. Keys ONLY on generic `Position` properties (`speculative()`/`nullableTail()`/`skipBranch()`) — never a rule/language identity. WITNESSES: synthetic `kSpecOptionalSchema` in `test_parser_speculation.cpp` (clause-matches, backtrack-to-sibling [red-on-disable for the nullable-fallback], body-over-budget). | (closed) | plan-23 FC17; `src/core/types/{compiled_shape.hpp,grammar_schema.cpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp}` + `src/analysis/syntactic/parser.cpp` + `tests/analysis/syntactic/test_parser_speculation.cpp`; consumed by [[D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE` | ✅ **LANDED 2026-07-09 (FC17) — C23 §6.7.2.2 enum with an EXPLICIT underlying integer type (`enum E : unsigned char { A, B }`).** The underlying type fixes the enum's size/representation (scalars[0] on the interned Enum type — `sizeof(enum E)==1`/align 1, not the default int 4). Grammar: new `enumTypeSpecifier = [Colon, castTypeBase]` with `commitRequiresTypeName{requireKnownType}` (MIRRORS `alignasTypeName`), wired into `enumSpec` as a SPECULATIVE optional ([[D-PARSE-SPECULATIVE-OPTIONAL]]) so the anonymous enum bit-field `enum C : 4;` still parses (the clause rolls back on a non-type). Semantic: config-driven `DeclarationRule.enumUnderlyingType {rule,typeChild}` (sibling to `bitfieldSuffix`); `semantic_analyzer` resolves the clause via the shared `resolveTypeNode`, gates on `isIntegerKind` → threads the kind into `enumType(name, underlying)` (the type substrate was ALREADY generic on scalars[0] — layout/reprKind/enumUnderlyingOrSelf/reintern), and range-checks each enumerator (`enumeratorValueFitsUnderlying`) ONLY when explicit (default-int enums UNCHANGED). Fail-loud: `S_InvalidEnumUnderlyingType` (0xE034, non-integer) + `S_EnumeratorValueOutOfRange` (0xE035, overflow), both UNSUPPRESSABLE. ★ **CODEGEN FIX (beyond the design's edits — the design's "mir_to_lir already generic" claim was STALE):** `requireNativeIntWidth` in `mir_to_lir.cpp` gated int↔enum CONVERSIONS on the RAW `interner.kind` (→ Enum ordinal 23, "no encoded conversion form") instead of `reprKind` (→ underlying) — so a NARROW-underlying enum was RUNTIME-UNUSABLE (even `(int)v` fail-loud). Fixed to read `reprKind` at every kind test in that gate (identity for all non-enum kinds → zero effect elsewhere), so a `enum E : unsigned char` routes through the realized U8 conversion forms. WITNESSES: 12 `test_semantic_analyzer_c_subset` tests (scalars/distinct/anonymous/non-integer/struct/OOB-256/boundary-255/default-never-fires/bitfield-width/the fork pin/bare-id rollback), `TypeLayout.EnumFollowsExplicitU8Underlying`, `examples/c-subset/enum_underlying_type` (RUNTIME wrap-at-256 → 42, release arm all 4 targets), 4 diagnostic-corpus goldens. | (closed) | plan-23 FC17; plan-00 stepper T3; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp}` + `src/analysis/semantic/{semantic_analyzer.cpp,type_rules.hpp}` + `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.cpp` (reprKind conversion-gate fix) + tests + example + goldens; rides [[D-PARSE-SPECULATIVE-OPTIONAL]]; opens [[D-CSUBSET-ENUM-OPAQUE-FORWARD-DECL]] + [[D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE-QUALIFIERS]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-OPAQUE-FORWARD-DECL` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE]]).** A BODYLESS enum with an explicit underlying type used as an OPAQUE forward declaration — `enum E : unsigned char;` (C23: an enum with a fixed underlying type is a COMPLETE type even without a body, so it can be forward-declared). Today it PARSES (the underlying clause + no `{…}`) but falls to the existing enum tag-miss fail-loud (Enum lacks the two-phase forwardComposite/completeComposite that Struct/Union have), so a bodyless `enum E : T;` is a LOUD rejection, never a silent wrong type. **Trigger:** real source needs an opaque fixed-underlying enum forward declaration. Resolution: give Enum the forwardComposite/completeComposite two-phase mint (mirroring struct forward-decl, D-CSUBSET-FORWARD-STRUCT-DECLARATION), keyed on the fixed underlying making the tag complete. | (gated — no shipped consumer; a loud rejection today) | opened by the enum-underlying cycle; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (Enum forward/complete two-phase); cross-ref `D-CSUBSET-FORWARD-STRUCT-DECLARATION` |
| `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE-QUALIFIERS` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE]]).** A `const`/`volatile`-qualified underlying type on the clause — `enum E : const unsigned char { … }`. The clause is `[Colon, castTypeBase]` (the qualifier-free base type-name), so a qualifier before the base is NOT admitted → the speculative clause does not match and the parse rejects it (LOUD, never a silent drop of the qualifier). C23 permits any integer type-specifier-qualifier sequence; the qualifier is semantically irrelevant to the underlying (an enum's underlying is an rvalue integer). **Trigger:** real source writes a qualified underlying type. Resolution: widen `enumTypeSpecifier`'s base from `castTypeBase` to a qualifier-admitting form (e.g. the head-qualifier `{repeat headQualifier}` prefix castTypeRef uses) and strip the (ignored) qualifier at resolve. | (gated — trivial widen; no shipped consumer writes a qualified enum underlying) | opened by the enum-underlying cycle; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`enumTypeSpecifier` base) |
| `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-CHAR-BOOL` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE]], code-audit LOW finding).** `char` / `_Bool`(`bool`) as the enum underlying type — `enum E : char { … }` / `enum E : bool { … }`. C23 §6.7.2.2 permits both (`char` and `bool` ARE integer types), but DSS's `isIntegerKind` (the underlying-type gate) is `I8..U128` only — it EXCLUDES `Char`/`Bool`/`Byte`, consistent with DSS's deliberate "`Char`/`Bool` are NOT arithmetic-integer ranks" posture (they reach the int ranks only via the `charConvertsToArith`/`boolWidensToArith` config gates, not natively). So `enum E : char` is REJECTED with `S_InvalidEnumUnderlyingType` (LOUD, never a silent wrong-width enum). **Trigger:** real source needs a char/bool-underlying enum, OR native Char/Bool-as-integer-rank support lands. Resolution: admit `Char`/`Bool` in the enum-underlying gate + map to their byte width in `enumeratorValueFitsUnderlying`, once they participate in the integer ranks. | (gated — a loud rejection today; consistent with the Char/Bool non-arithmetic-rank posture) | opened by the enum-underlying cycle (code-audit); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`isIntegerKind` underlying gate) + `type_rules.hpp` (`enumeratorValueFitsUnderlying`) |
| `D-CSUBSET-EMPTY-INITIALIZER` | ✅ **LANDED 2026-07-10 (FC17.5) — C23 §6.7.10 `{}` empty initializer + the SCALAR brace initializer.** `int z = {};` zero-initializes (6.7.10p11), `int v = {42};` / the scalar compound literal `(int){42}` initialize with the single expression (6.7.10p12), `int *p = {};` zero-fills to null — all via a NEW scalar arm at the TOP of the ONE `lowerBraceInit` funnel (cst_to_hir.cpp `lowerScalarBraceInit`; all 4 routes — decl init / call-arg ×2 / compound literal — inherit it). **F4 CLOSED ALLOWLIST** (`isScalarBraceInitKind`: Bool/I8..U128/F16..F128/Char/Byte/Enum/Ptr — exactly what `synthZeroOrError` can zero-mint); Void/FnSig/etc. keep the aggregate gate's fail-loud reject (`(void){}` stays H0009-loud). 0 elements → the SHARED `synthZeroOrError`; 1 undesignated NON-brace element → `lowerExprOrBraceInit` (byte-identical to `= expr`); >1 / designator / **N2 nested `{{42}}`** → `S_InvalidScalarInitializer` (0xE03F, plain Error — the lowering returns an Error node either way, no silent-accept path). **F3**: `validateConstexprDeclarator` gains an EMPTY-brace arm (`constexpr int x = {};` / `constexpr int *p = {};` = zero = valid; `{5}` folds via the normal descent — the now-green delta pin). Aggregate `{}` zero-fill was ALREADY green (regression-pinned). Tests: the FLIPPED `ScalarCompoundLiteralLowersViaScalarBraceInit` (was the aggregate-only fail-loud pin) + 3 malformed-shape 0xE03F pins + the `(void){}` F4 pin + `empty_init_scalar` runtime example (exit 42, 4 targets + release) + 2 constexpr semantic pins. Empirical: 8/8 pe64 runtime probes + elf64 emits. |
| `D-CSUBSET-FUNC-PREDEFINED-IDENTIFIER` | ✅ **LANDED 2026-07-10 (FC17.5) — C99 §6.4.2.2 `__func__` (+ the GNU `__FUNCTION__` alias, config vocabulary `semantics.predefinedFunctionNames.identifiers`).** Pass 1 binds per spelling a SYNTHETIC SymbolRecord into a function DEFINITION's own scope BEFORE the params (**N4**: a param named `__func__` collides → S_RedeclaredSymbol at its own span): kind=Variable, **F1 `isConst=true`** (SE4's const check catches `__func__ = x` / `+=` → S_ConstViolation — isConst is the ONLY guard, both pinned + corpus-goldened), type=`Array<narrow-string-core, len+1>` (**N5**: the element core from the language's config string-literal core — `SchemaIndexes::stringLiteralElementCore`, never hardcoded Char). HIR FOLDS a read at the identifier-operand path to a string-literal-shaped `HirLiteralValue` (BYTE-IDENTICAL to `lowerStringLiteral`'s narrow arm) → rodata/decay/`__func__[i]`/sizeof/`&__func__` (LEGAL, the Literal-lvalue arm) all ride unchanged. **F1 guard**: `++__func__`/`--`/compound-assign classifiers → `S_PredefinedIdentifierNotAddressable` (0xE040) at the ONE `simpleLvalue` chokepoint (returns {sym, InvalidType} so both classifiers bail with no second engine error; previously an engine-level MIR "no storage slot"). **F2 (C REQUIRES `__func__ == __func__`)**: `materializeStringLiteralGlobal` MEMOIZES by (array TypeId, byte content) — identical string literals now share ONE rodata global module-wide (C 6.4.5p7 permits; also a size win — sqlite's repeated literals collapse); red-on-disable MIR pins (1-global counts) + the runtime identity term in `func_name_basic` (exit 42, 4 targets + release). File-scope `__func__` stays S_UndeclaredIdentifier (the binding is per-function-definition, pinned). NOTE — `__func__[0] = 'x'` (the rodata-WRITE class) rides the PRE-EXISTING [[D-CSUBSET-INCDEC-CONST-LVALUE]] const-lvalue family gap: not newly opened here, closes with that class. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA` | 🟢 **C1a + C1b + C2 + C3 + C4a + C4b + C4c + C5 ✅ ARC COMPLETE (C1a–C4a 2026-07-13, C4b–C5 2026-07-14) — `int a[n]` + MULTI-DIM RUN + `sizeof <vla>` runtime + ptr-to-VLA `int (*p)[n]` (LOCAL + PARAMETER) + VLA typedef `typedef int R[n]; R a;` + array-param `static`/cv-qualifiers/`[*]` (`int f(int a[static 3])`/`[const n]`/`int a[*]`, all decay to a pointer) on all 3 legs (LEAF scope); `__STDC_NO_VLA__` REMOVED, `c99_vla`+`c99_vla_multidim`+`c99_vla_ptr`+`c99_vla_ptr_param`+`c99_vla_typedef`+`c99_array_param_static` GREEN. ★ C4c ALSO landed a GENERALIZABLE parser capability (the schema compiler honors `speculative` on inline repeat-alts) via 2 user §B decisions. C5 (block-scope teardown) ✅ LANDED 2026-07-14 — the FULL VLA arc is COMPLETE. — arc OPENED, §B forks DECIDED. ★ The substrate probe CORRECTED the stale premise: `int a[n]` PARSES clean + is rejected ONLY at semantic `S_NonConstantArrayLength` (0xE00B — the `S000B` render is the 0xE high-nibble stripped by `parse_diagnostic.cpp:409`, NOT parse `P_UnmatchedClose` 0x000B; verified via `--suppress=0xE00B` compiling to an object) ⇒ the arc is TYPE+SEMANTIC+CODEGEN, ZERO grammar for the core `int a[n]`. §B DECIDED (user 2026-07-13): FORK A → `TypeKind::Array` + a `kVlaLength=-2` sentinel + an out-of-band size side-table (the incomplete-array `-1`-sentinel precedent; low blast radius over the 83 Array dispatch sites; `computeLayout` already nullopts on `sc[0]<0`); FORK B → TRUE dynamic stack (runtime-size `Alloca` operand → aligned `sub rsp,<size>` + a frame pointer [RBP] + SP save/restore — best-long-term, + it UNLOCKS the `alignas` over-aligned-local frame-model the `L_OverAlignedStackLocal` fail-loud waits on; NO dynamic-SP precedent exists today). Cycle split: **C1a** (type-rep + the 2 semantic accept-arms `semantic_analyzer.cpp:2313`+`:3298` + CST→HIR size-expr un-skip `cst_to_hir.cpp:170` + MIR runtime-operand `Alloca` + verifier) / **C1b** (LIR/asm dynamic-alloca: frame pointer + `sub rsp,reg` + SP save/restore, agnostic ×x86_64/arm64×PE/ELF/Mach-O) → C2 sizeof-on-VLA → C3 multi-dim `a[n][m]` (runtime stride) → C4 ptr-to-VLA+VLA-typedef+`[*]`/`[static n]` → C5 block-scope teardown. Full design [[project_vla_arc_design_2026_07_13]]. ★ **C1a ✅ LANDED 2026-07-13:** the `kVlaLength=-2` sentinel + `vlaArray`/`isVlaArray`; both accept-arms + the Pass-2 `validateVlaDeclarator` owning file-scope→`S_NonConstantArrayLength`, non-integer→`S_VlaSizeNotInteger` 0xE053, static/extern→`S_VlaWithStaticStorage` 0xE051, multi-dim/array-of-VLA→`S_VlaMultiDimUnsupported` 0xE052; CST→HIR size-expr un-skip + a SymbolId-keyed side-table; MIR runtime-operand `Alloca` (total-byte-size, decl-point-emitted via the hoist-skip) + the verifier invariant `I_VlaAllocaOperandInvalid` 0xA016; the named C1b boundary = MIR→LIR fail-loud `L_VlaDynamicAllocaUnsupported` 0xB00D. Design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (scope gate = the existing agnostic `fileScopeOf`; 3 fail-loud gaps folded pre-code) + code-audit (caught+fixed a CRITICAL non-integer-length SILENT-truncation — `int a[1.5]`/`int a[nullptr]` were `FPToSI`/`SExt`-truncating to 1-element/0-byte past a dead `mapCast` guard → now rejected at the SEMANTIC tier by `S_VlaSizeNotInteger` + block-scope goldens; the fix also cleared a pre-existing `H0009` on `_BitInt` bounds via `resolveScalarIntKind`; 2nd-order self-review CLEAN). 628/628 ×3 legs + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ×3; `__STDC_NO_VLA__` STAYS =1 (`FC175StdcNoVlaDefined` green). ★ **C1b ✅ LANDED 2026-07-13** — the dynamic-stack RUN: a `framePointer` CC field + 2 OPTIONAL LIR ops (`sub_sp_reg` [arm64 EXTENDED-register `0xCB2063FF`, reg31=SP — the naive reg-reg form targets XZR] + `sp_copy`) emitted by ONE config-resolved handle; a per-function VLA-ONLY conditional frame pointer (§B — regalloc pool + rewriter-scratch reserve + force-saved); a SITE-SPECIFIC SP→FP base-switch at every fixed-frame ref + a completeness verifier; `lowerVlaAlloca` (alignUp→`sub_sp_reg`→base←SP). LEAF scope: fail-loud `L_VlaNonLeafFrameUnsupported` (0xB00E) on VLA+call/`va_start`/over-align/own-`__try`(SEH). Independent DESIGN-audit folded 4 CRITICAL silent-miscompiles PRE-code (SP-relative spills; unsaved caller-FP; prologue-order clobber; the non-leaf outgoing-args collision → the LEAF scope-cut); the mandated SPILLING test caught a rewriter-scratch FP-leak the spec itself missed; the CODE-audit caught 1 missed base-switch site (`recv_by_value_stack_param`) → folded (a leaf by-value-struct-param VLA now RUNS). `c99_vla`/`_spill`/`_structparam` RUN → 42 on x86_64-elf + arm64-elf(qemu, debug+release — the extended-register SP encoding proven by EXECUTION) + pe64; 631/631 ×3 legs + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ×3; a non-VLA function BYTE-IDENTICAL; agnostic (no arch/format identity in `src/lir/`). New anchors `-NONLEAF-CALL-FRAME`/`-WIN64-UNWIND`/`-WIN64-STACK-PROBE`. ★ **C2 ✅ LANDED 2026-07-13** — `sizeof <vla-object>` is a RUNTIME `size_t`: `lowerSizeof` records (SizeOf HIR node → the VLA operand SymbolId) via `vlaObjectOperandSymbol` (descend transparent grouping wrappers to the bare identifier; a COMPOSITE operand — `sizeof(b,a)`/`sizeof(0,a)`, ≥2 sub-exprs — is REJECTED so it FAILS LOUD, since C decays a composite value to a pointer → never a silent wrong size); `vlaAllocaForLocal` FREEZES `count*stride` (= the exact C `sizeof`; the 16-align is LIR-only) into a hidden 8-byte U64 slot ONCE at the decl (§6.7.6.2p2, side-effect-safe for `int a[f()]`); the MIR SizeOf case Loads it (+a desync fail-loud). ★ THE INVARIANT: const-eval keeps DECLINING a VLA sizeof (the SizeOf node's `vlaArray` TypeRef is LEFT UNCHANGED + NO const-eval path touched) → `_Static_assert(sizeof a==K)`→`S_StaticAssertFailed` "not an integer constant expression" (`S0029`); never a wrong constant. Object-form ONLY; the type-form `sizeof(int[n])` isn't even constructible in the c-subset (parse `P0009`) → honest deferral `-SIZEOF-TYPEFORM`. Design-audit GO (invariant CONFIRMED-SAFE by construction; the mid-function fixed size-slot IS frame-summed + FP-relative in the VLA frame; `bytes`==exact sizeof). Code-audit caught+fixed a CRITICAL silent-miscompile (a broad-DFS operand match mis-keyed `sizeof(0,a)`→`a`'s frozen size; the wrapper-peel now rejects composites → fail loud; red-on-disable pin `SizeofOfCompositeVlaOperandFailsLoud`). 632/632 ×3 legs + `c99_vla_sizeof` RUNS→42 (x86_64-elf + arm64-qemu[debug+release] + pe64) + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ×3; pins `SizeofOfVlaLoadsDeclFrozenSizeSlot` + `StaticAssertSizeofVlaIsNotConstantFailsLoud`. New anchor `-SIZEOF-TYPEFORM`. ★ **C3 ✅ LANDED 2026-07-13 — multi-dim VLAs RUN with a RUNTIME inner stride** (`int a[n][m]`, `a[n][m][k]`, `a[n][5]`, `a[5][n]` — all block-scope multi-dim shapes; LEAF scope). §B DECIDED (user 2026-07-13): **storage = Option 1** (store cumulative row-strides ONCE at the decl, Load at each index) + **`a[5][n]` INCLUDED (no-compromise)** via a transitive `typeContainsVla`. Single-tier (semantic + `cst_to_hir` + `hir_to_mir` + interner + verifier; NO LIR/asm/target/format — a runtime stride is just more MIR `Mul`/`Load`). Mechanism: nested `vlaArray(vlaArray(int))` type (the fixed `Index(Index(..))` + `indexResultType`-peel + byte-`Gep` machinery composes for free); lift the 4 `S_VlaMultiDimUnsupported` rejects; `interner.typeContainsVla` (recursive `ops[0]`/`isVlaArray` walk, does NOT over-fire on `int[5][5]`) threads 8 `isVlaArray` gates (the 4 rejects + `validateVlaDeclarator` + `captureVlaSize` call + sizeof-recording + the MIR verifier alloca rule) so top-fixed `a[5][n]` works; `captureVlaSize` collects EVERY suffix (outer→inner vector); `vlaAllocaForLocal` evaluates all dims ONCE (§6.7.6.2p2, side-effect-safe), folds cumulative row-strides bottom-up, freezes one slot per runtime level keyed by **`(baseSymbol.v, elemTy TypeId.v)`** (the interned peel → store/load keys MATCH by construction); ★ CRITICAL-1 CONVENTION (design-audit caught a self-contradictory level map — `a[1][0]`→`a[0][1]`): scale a subscript / size a row by the runtime `sizeof` of the SUBSCRIPT-RESULT type — a VLA-row elemTy Loads its `(sym, elemTy)` slot, a FIXED elemTy uses compile-time `elementStride`; this UNIFIES index + row-`sizeof a[0]` + whole-`sizeof a` (the C2 total slot IS the whole-object entry). Design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (3 CRITICAL folded pre-code: the level-map, the verifier `a[5][n]` reject, the 4-gate a[5][n] leak) + code-audit SHIP (stride arithmetic hand-verified per shape; keys match; evaluate-once holds; typeContainsVla complete+non-over-firing; 3 MINOR — the misleading "internal desync" message for the C4-deferred typedef-VLA/ptr-to-VLA shapes REWORDED to honest fail-loud; `S_VlaMultiDimUnsupported` 0xE052 now UNEMITTED but kept-reserved for C4 `[*]`). Orchestrator-verified INDEPENDENTLY: the off-diagonal `a[1][0]`==77 (NOT the transposed 88) on all 3 legs; per-dim non-integer→`S0053`, file-scope→`S000B`, wide-`_BitInt`→`H0009`, struct-member-VLA→`S_FlexibleArraySoleMember`, `_Static_assert(sizeof a==K)`→`S0029` (C2 invariant preserved). 633/633 ×3 legs + `c99_vla_multidim`→42 (x86_64-elf + arm64-qemu[debug+release] + pe64) + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ×3; new MIR pins `MultiDimVlaLowersWithRuntimeStrideSlots`/`FixedMultiDimArrayDoesNotRouteToVlaPath`/`RowSizeofOfMultiDimVlaLoadsStrideSlot`. **C4 (ptr-to-VLA `int(*p)[n]` + VLA typedef + `[*]`/`[static n]`) NEXT.** ★ **C4 §B DECIDED (user 2026-07-13): cover ALL 4 shapes across as many cycles as needed** (explorer-confirmed clean tier seam — codegen core vs grammar surface). Split: **C4a** ptr-to-VLA (the substantial codegen core; FORK-1 = a per-pointer runtime-row stride slot FROZEN at the decl, reusing the C3 `vlaStrideSlot`/`scaleIndexToBytes` substrate — the type `Ptr<vlaArray>` already builds; the only gap is `p[j]`/`p±j` scaling by the runtime pointee size; **source scope = LOCAL + PARAMETER** [a local slot at the decl, a param slot in the callee prologue; globals/struct/cast/ternary bases fail loud]) · **C4b** VLA typedef (`typedef int R[n]; R a;` — a NEW freeze-at-typedef-declaration size capture; the only silent-miscompile surface) · **C4c** `[*]`+`[static n]` (grammar-only prototype-param syntax, decays to a pointer, ZERO codegen). Full substrate map + FORK-1 options in the C4 explorer output (2026-07-13). C4a is the substantial one + carries FORK-1. ★ **C4a-LOCAL ✅ LANDED 2026-07-13** — a LOCAL `int (*p)[n]` RUNS: `p[i][j]` scales the outer subscript by the RUNTIME pointee size via a per-pointer stride slot FROZEN at the decl (reuses C3's `scaleIndexToBytes`/`vlaStrideSlot` verbatim). GAP 2 (the slot): the `captureVlaSize` gate widened for `kind==Ptr && typeContainsVla(pointee)`; the stride store at the VarDecl DECL SITE (program order — design-audit CRITICAL-2: `allocaForLocal` is HOISTED → `n` read before its store = silent miscompile) via shared helpers `widenVlaDim`/`computeVlaByteSize`/`storePtrToVlaStride` (refactored from `vlaAllocaForLocal`, behavior-preserving, keeps all belts); keyed `vlaSlotKey(sym, pointeeTy)` == the subscript's `hir.typeId(Index)` (matches verbatim). Design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (2 CRITICAL folded pre-code: the decl-site placement + NOT swapping the init-derivation wholesale [brace-init mass-regression]) + code-audit SHIP (refactor line-by-line behavior-preserving; decl-site correct; key matches; + a deferral-boundary pin). Witness = the ASSIGNMENT form `int(*p)[n]; p=b; p[i][j]`; the off-diagonal `p[1][0]`==77 (not transposed) on all 3 legs; `c99_vla_ptr`→42 (x86_64-elf/arm64-qemu[release]/pe64); 635/635 ×3 + sqlite ×3. **DEFERRED (both fail-loud):** the INIT form `int(*p)[n]=b` (`D-CSUBSET-VLA-PTR-INIT-FORM-TYPING` — the init node is pre-stamped decayed, defeating the init-compat derivation; pinned red-on-disable) + `p+j` pointer-arith (a non-single-root base → `:4746`). ★ **C4a-PARAM ✅ LANDED 2026-07-13** — a PARAMETER `int (*p)[n]` (+ the adjusted `int a[][n]`) RUNS: the callee `p[i][j]` scales by the RUNTIME pointee row size frozen ONCE in the callee prologue from the incoming `n`; a ptr-to-VLA param emits only a FIXED 8-byte stride slot (NO `sub_sp_reg`) so the callee is NOT leaf-restricted (CALLS freely) and a genuine VLA arg `int b[K][n]` decays + type-checks. GAP 1b = **Option B**: a DISTINCT `paramDecay` bool threaded through `applyDeclaratorSuffix`/`directDeclaredType`/`declaratorDeclaredType` (checked FIRST at the nullopt decision site) builds a present-length PARAM `[n]` as `vlaArray(-2)` [then `adjustArrayToPointer` top-strips a plain `int a[n]` param to `int*` unchanged], while the struct-field FAM path still passes `allowFlexibleArray` (NOT paramDecay) → BYTE-IDENTICAL (`StructFieldVlaSoleMemberStillFailsLoud` pin). GAP 2 param = the stride store in the prologue param-loop scalar arm (`n` is param 0, placed before `p` → no hoist hazard). FIX-3 = the `int a[][n]` `captureVlaSize` leading-decayed-suffix skip (`skip = numSuffixes − pointeeArrayDepth`, Ptr-only). Design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (config rows DISJOINT; paramDecay-first ordering the crux) + code-audit SHIP (VERIFIED-CLEAN — paramDecay only true via the single `param` config row; struct-FAM byte-identical; plain-decay preserved; tests red-on-disable). `c99_vla_ptr_param`→42 (x86_64-elf/arm64-qemu[release]/pe64); 636/636 ×3 + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ×3; pins `ParamPtrToVlaAcceptsAndVlaArgDecays`+5 + new anchor `-FIXED-ARRAY-ARG-COMPAT` (a fixed `int b[2][2]` arg → `ptr(array)`≠`ptr(vlaArray)` → S0003 fail-loud, stricter-than-C leniency deferred). ★ **C4b ✅ LANDED 2026-07-14 — VLA typedef `typedef int R[n]; R a;` RUNS** (+ `R a; R b;` shared freeze · multi-dim `R[n][m]`/`R[5][n]`/`R[n][5]` · multi-declarator `R a,b;` · `const R a;`). C99 §6.7.7p2: `n` evaluated ONCE at the typedef + FROZEN; every use allocates that frozen size (a probe mutating `n=100` AFTER the typedef keeps `sizeof b==12`, not 400). Mechanism (reuses C1b/C2/C3/C4a substrate — semantic tier had ZERO core gaps): **(A)** a NEW `SymbolRecord::vlaTypedefOrigin` (a→R) set in `resolveDeclTypesPost` under the design-audit I1 gate `declTy==headTy && (isVlaArray||typeContainsVla)(headTy)` — this gate EXCLUDES `R a[m]` (extra dim) / `R *p` (Ptr) for free; the head-descent resolves the alias via the SAME `scopes.lookup(here,name)` the type resolver used. **(B)** HIR — `lowerTypeDecl` captures R's OWN `[n]` (`captureVlaSize(R)`), `lowerVarLikeInto` records a→R into a NEW `typedefVlaOriginBySymbol` side-table + SKIPS `captureVlaSize(a)` (avoids H0009). **(C)** MIR — the `HirKind::TypeDecl` no-op → `computeVlaByteSize(R, freeze=true)` freezes R's per-level slots ONCE in program order (evaluates `n` once). **(D)** `vlaAllocaFromTypedef` COPIES only R's FROZEN level-slots (`vlaStrideSlot.find`, skip misses — I3) into a's own `(a.v,levelTy.v)` keys + sizes a's alloca from level-0's whole-object total. ★ The #1 hoist hazard (C4a-local CRITICAL-2 class) does NOT apply — the entry-alloca hoist SKIPS VLA locals (`aggregateByteSize` nullopts) so a's copy-down emits at the VarDecl site AFTER R's freeze (design-audit independently VERIFIED at `hir_to_mir.cpp:9190`). Design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (★ I1 crux: my original token-keyed capture WOULDN'T FIRE [the `identDeclHead` is Internal] → moved to `resolveDeclTypesPost`; + I2 multi-dim runtime-inner test, I3 enumerate-frozen-only, I4 own-suffix fail-loud, I5 jumped-over→loud) + code-audit SHIP-WITH-FIXES (NO CRITICAL, no silent seam — freeze-once + the copy-down key-match hand-verified by simulation; folded: the CHAINED VLA typedef `typedef R S;` now fails loud CLEANLY via the `vlaTypedefOrigin` discriminator [`D-CSUBSET-VLA-TYPEDEF-CHAINED`] instead of a C4b-introduced confusing generic `internal:` H0009). Orchestrator-verified INDEPENDENTLY on pe64: basic→42, freeze-once (n=100 after typedef → `sizeof b==12`), two-use, multi-dim off-diagonal `c[1][0]`≠`c[0][1]`, all deferred shapes loud. `c99_vla_typedef`→42 (x86_64-elf/arm64-qemu[release]/pe64); 637/637 ×3 + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ×3; pins `VlaTypedefFreezesSizeAtTypeDeclStatement`/`VlaTypedefObjectAllocaLoadsFrozenSizeNotReLoweredN` + 4 fail-loud (stacked/own-suffix/ptr/chained). DEFERRED fail-loud: stacked-suffix (`typedef int R[5]; R a[n]` / `typedef int R[n]; R a[m]`) + ptr-to-VLA typedef (`typedef int (*P)[n]; P p`) + chained (`typedef R S`). ★ **C4c ✅ LANDED 2026-07-14 — array-parameter `static`/cv-qualifier/`[*]` syntax RUNS** (`int f(int a[static 3])`, `[const n]`, `[restrict]`, `[const static N]`, `int f(int a[*])` — ALL decay to a bare pointer, ZERO new codegen). Parse-permissive + a `paramDecay`-gated `S_ArrayParamQualifierNonParameter` (0xE054) fail-loud for a NON-param use (the `typeSpecifierSeq`→`S_InvalidTypeSpecifierCombination` pattern; §B Option A over forking a param-only sub-grammar). Shared `arraySuffixBoundNode` skips the leading markers to locate the bound (4 sites). ★ The `[*]` form (`D-CSUBSET-VLA-PARAM-STAR`, CLOSED) needed TWO user §B decisions → a CORE parser-engine capability (schema compiler honors `speculative` on inline repeat-alts) + `arrayStarSuffix` + a guard-less `fnSuffixTail` (the fnSuffix commit-guard would else fire spuriously in the speculative suffix). Deferred: const/volatile pointer-qualifier ENFORCEMENT (`-PARAM-QUAL-ENFORCEMENT`, lenient), outermost-only static (`-PARAM-STATIC-OUTERMOST`, lenient), unnamed abstract `int f(int[*])` (`-PARAM-STAR-ABSTRACT`) — each fail-loud/lenient, never a miscompile. Design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES + code-audit SHIP (engine fix opt-in, all 6 fnSuffix sites, no silent seam); all 3 languages (toy/tsql/c-subset) ctest-verified byte-identical for the engine change; `c99_array_param_static`→42 (x86_64-elf/arm64-qemu[release]/pe64); 63x/63x ×3 + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ×3. **C5 ✅ LANDED 2026-07-14 — block-scope teardown RUNS on all 3 legs: NEW MIR ops StackSave/StackRestore → the existing `sp_copy` + a NEW `sp_restore` LIR op (result:none, [SP,saved], both target JSONs); a `vlaScopeStack` of per-VLA-decl watermarks; teardown on natural-exit/back-edge + break + continue + goto (FULL general: forward/outward/backward-over-a-decl) + for-init-VLA at loop-exit (not the back-edge — it persists across iterations); `H_VlaJumpIntoScope` 0xF018 entry-ban (C99 6.8.6.1p1 — the dominance guarantor for legal gotos); computed-`goto *expr` crossing a VLA scope + multi-declarator for-init (`D-CSUBSET-VLA-FOR-INIT-MULTIDECL`) fail-loud; StackSave/Restore join opcodeClobbersMemory (no load sunk into freed stack); inliner bails on teardown funcs; MIR-verifier pairing+dominance check; design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES + code-audit SHIP (zero silent leak/UAF), empirical battery incl. the back-edge-no-UAF clobber witness; 639/639 ×3 legs + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ×3. ★ THE FULL VLA ARC (C1a–C5) IS COMPLETE.**  (Prior: C1b handoff: the `_BitInt`-bound widening arithmetic + Mem2Reg-must-not-drop a side-effecting size (`int a[f()]`) are C1b-verified. — a FULL VLA IMPLEMENTATION arc (user directive 2026-07-11: after the `_BitInt` arc completes, before FC18; SUPERSEDES the prior no-VLA decision recorded below).** Until that arc lands, DSS has NO variable-length-array support (C99 §6.7.6.2 VLAs: a runtime array bound fails LOUD — `S_NonConstantArrayLength`; no silent decay, no guessed length) and now DECLARES it conformantly: **`__STDC_NO_VLA__ = 1`** rides the c-subset `predefinedMacros` config row (C11 6.10.8.3 / C23 6.10.9.3 REQUIRE a VLA-less implementation to define it — the conformance-HONEST line given `__STDC_VERSION__ = 202311L`). A conforming program's `#ifdef __STDC_NO_VLA__` now selects its fixed-size fallback (preprocessor pin `FC175StdcNoVlaDefined`, red-on-disable). The `c99_vla` battery probe stays RED by design. TRIGGER: **FIRED (user directive 2026-07-11)** — a full VLA arc (`int a[n]` runtime length + `int a[expr]` + the C99 §6.7.6.2 variably-modified-type substrate: runtime stack allocation + runtime `sizeof`) is SCHEDULED after the `_BitInt` arc completes, before FC18; its own designed multi-cycle work (TLS/`_BitInt`-scale), never a drive-by. ★ WHEN IT LANDS `__STDC_NO_VLA__` MUST be REMOVED from the c-subset `predefinedMacros` (a VLA-supporting implementation must NOT define it) + the `FC175StdcNoVlaDefined` preprocessor pin flipped/retired + the `c99_vla` battery probe flips GREEN. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-FIXED-ARRAY-ARG-COMPAT` | ⏳ **GATED — normal (opened 2026-07-13, VLA C4a-param; NOT src-cited — test/example-cited only).** A FIXED multi-dim array argument (`int b[2][2]`) decays to `ptr(array(int,2))`, which is a DISTINCT interned type from the ptr-to-VLA param's `ptr(vlaArray(int))` (the `-2` VLA sentinel vs a constant length), so the arg-decay compare (`type_rules.hpp:371`, exact `lhsElem[0]==rhsElem[0]`) REJECTS it — `S_TypeMismatch` (`S0003`), a CLEAN fail-loud, NEVER a silent miscompile. C 6.7.6.2p6 makes a VLA-typed pointer and a compatible fixed-array pointer assignment-compatible at runtime, so DSS is STRICTER than C here (rejects valid C), but the -2 sentinel keeps every accepted call genuinely VLA-shaped (no wrong-stride risk). A genuine VLA arg `int b[K][n]` (rows `int[n]`) DOES decay + type-check (`c99_vla_ptr_param` + the `ParamPtrToVlaAcceptsAndVlaArgDecays` pin); this anchor is only the fixed-arg leniency. Closing work: admit a fixed `array(int,K)` element against a `vlaArray` element in the decay compare when C-compatible (runtime bound check deferred to the callee). Trigger: a real need. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-TYPEDEF-CHAINED` | ⏳ **GATED — normal (opened 2026-07-14, VLA C4b; src-cited `cst_to_hir.cpp` `lowerTypeDecl`).** A CHAINED VLA typedef — `typedef int R[n]; typedef R S;` — where S aliases a VLA typedef with NO own `[n]` suffix (its VLA-ness comes from the head alias R, so the semantic I1 gate stamps `S.vlaTypedefOrigin`). C4b freezes the ORIGINAL typedef's size from its own captured `[n]`; a chained alias has no `[n]` to capture. `lowerTypeDecl` recognizes the chained form by its OWN array-suffix ABSENCE (`declaratorHasArraySuffix` — a self-contained HIR-tier check; the `vlaTypedefOrigin` field is stamped OBJECTS-ONLY, `DeclarationKind::Variable`, so it is never written on a typedef symbol) and FAILS LOUD CLEANLY ("a typedef that aliases a variable-length-array typedef is not yet supported", H at HIR) — NEVER a silent miscompile. Pre-C4b `typedef R S;` was a no-op that compiled but any USE (`S a;`) failed loud; C4b moved the (clean) error to the chained typedef itself. Closing work: freeze S's slots by copying R's frozen slots at S's own TypeDecl statement (reuse the C4b copy-down slot-copy, minus the object alloca), then S-objects copy S's slots — a modest extension of injection (C). Trigger: a real need. Pinned `ChainedVlaTypedefFailsLoud` (red-on-disable). |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-NONLEAF-CALL-FRAME` | ⏳ **GATED — normal (opened 2026-07-13, VLA C1b).** A VLA function that ALSO makes a call (`hasVla && hasCalls`) or uses `va_start` FAILS LOUD `L_VlaNonLeafFrameUnsupported` (0xB00E, unsuppressable) — NOT a miscompile. Root cause (design-audit CRITICAL-4): the fixed outgoing-args area is reserved at a compile-time `[sp+off]`, but a VLA's `sub sp,<size>` moves SP → outgoing args would land INSIDE the VLA (clobber), and switching them to the frame pointer breaks the ABI (the `call` runs at the moved SP). NO base choice is correct in the fixed-reservation model. Closing work: the NON-LEAF VLA frame model — a §B-worthy design (outgoing-args placed BELOW where the VLA goes, or a separate outgoing-args pointer; or over-allocate + a two-region frame). Trigger: a real need / a dedicated cycle. Cited `lir_callconv.cpp` + tests (red-on-disable). |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-WIN64-UNWIND` | ⏳ **GATED — normal (opened 2026-07-13, VLA C1b; design-audit IMPORTANT-4; src-cited `lir_callconv.cpp`).** A pe64 VLA function's `.pdata`/`.xdata` reproduces the FIXED prologue (`sub sp,F` + pushes; `rsp = established + F`) — it does NOT reflect the VLA's dynamic `sub sp,<size>` nor the FP-setup, so an exception/stack-walk THROUGH a pe64 VLA frame mis-unwinds. ★ C1b code-audit fold (IMPORTANT-3): a VLA function that OWNS a `__try` (`hasVla && isSehParent` — its symbol is a `sehFuncletParents` parent, PE-only-populated so agnostic) now FAILS LOUD `L_VlaNonLeafFrameUnsupported` (closes the `hasCalls`-isn't-a-sufficient-proxy gap — a `__try` around a trapping NON-call op like `#DE` has `hasCalls=false`; pinned `VlaSehParentFunctionFailsLoud`). RESIDUAL (narrow, still open): a leaf VLA function that TRAPS (no own `__try`) whose CALLER has a `__try` → the OS unwinds through the imprecise `.pdata`; needs a trapping op + a caller handler (the examples are safe — normal return). Also `isSehParent` assumes every `.pdata`-emitting `__try` produces a funclet-parent entry (true for x64 `__except`/`__finally`). Closing work: emit VLA-aware Win64 unwind (UWOP_SET_FPREG + dynamic-alloca codes). Trigger: the residual SEH case or a real need. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-WIN64-STACK-PROBE` | ⏳ **GATED — normal (opened 2026-07-13, VLA C1b code-audit MINOR-1; NOT src-cited — tracked-only).** On Windows, the prologue `stack_probe` guard-page walk covers only the FIXED frame `F`; a VLA's runtime `sub_sp_reg` descends further with NO page-walk, so a LARGE VLA (past the guard page) → a stack-overflow access violation. SAFE-ish: a FAIL-STOP (a clean AV / `#SS`), NEVER a miscompile; the `n=6` examples are far under a page. Closing work: emit a `stack_probe`-style page-walk on the dynamic `sub_sp_reg` for the PE profile (`cc.stackProbePageBytes>0`). Trigger: a large-VLA Windows need. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-PARAM-STAR` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-14** (opened 2026-07-14, VLA C4c; landed SAME-CYCLE via the §B core parser-engine change; src-cited `grammar_schema_json.cpp` + `c-subset.lang.json` arrayStarSuffix + `examples/c-subset/c99_array_param_static`). The bare unspecified-size `*` array-PARAMETER form `int a[*]` (C99 §6.7.6.3, a prototype VLA-parameter size marker) now PARSES, decays to a bare pointer, and RUNS. LANDED via (1) a CORE schema-compiler fix — `grammar_schema_json.cpp`'s `"repeat"` arm now propagates `speculative`/`lookahead` from an inline `{repeat:{alt:…}}` body onto the loopEntry AltChoice the parser dispatches at + `setSkipBranch(cont)` (reusing the D-PARSE-SPECULATIVE-OPTIONAL skip-branch machinery; opt-in, byte-identical for every non-speculative repeat) — and (2) a DISTINCT `arrayStarSuffix` = `[BracketOpen,StarOp,BracketClose]` grammar rule listed BEFORE `arrayDeclSuffix` in the now-speculative suffix repeat (directDeclarator/abstractDirectDeclarator), so `[*]` matches it while a real bound (`[N]` / the deref-VLA `[*p]`, both still compile) rolls back CLEANLY to `arrayDeclSuffix`. Semantic: `applyDeclaratorSuffix`'s `arrayStarSuffixRule` arm (new OPTIONAL `declarators.arrayStarSuffixRule` config role) builds an unspecified-size array that `adjustArrayToPointer` decays like a bare `[]` for a param; a NON-param `[*]` fails loud S_ArrayParamQualifierNonParameter (0xE054, the SAME paramDecay gate). Pinned: `SpeculativeInlineRepeatAltBacktracksToSiblingBranch` + `SpeculativeInlineRepeatSeqAltComposes` (engine, red-on-disable), `ArrayParamStarFormDecaysCleanNonParamFailsLoud` (semantic), `DerefSizedVlaStillCompiles` (the `[*p]` regression guard), + `gstar(int a[*])` in `c99_array_param_static` (runtime witness, exit 42 ×3 legs). ★ CODE-AUDIT WRINKLE (folded, not in the original agent draft): making the suffix repeat SPECULATIVE dragged `fnSuffix`'s `requireKnownType` commit guard into the probe → it fired spuriously at the suffix position, breaking `int f()` / casts / every function declarator. FIX = a guard-less `fnSuffixTail` clone (grammar-identical to `fnSuffix`, no `commitRequiresTypeName`) for the suffix position, recognized via a new OPTIONAL `fnSuffixTailRule` config role at the 6 fn-suffix sites (`isFnSuffixRule`); the base-position `fnSuffix` (fnSuffix-vs-parenDeclarator triage + the cross-file-typedef ambiguous-record) is UNCHANGED. Corpus golden `mini_calc.c.tree` + the `test_c_subset` hand-built pin regenerated (named-fn suffix → `fnSuffixTail`). SCOPE: only `directDeclarator` (the NAMED `int f(int a[*])`) is speculative; **`abstractDirectDeclarator` stays NON-speculative** — the UNNAMED abstract `int f(int[*])` is DEFERRED (`D-CSUBSET-VLA-PARAM-STAR-ABSTRACT`, fail-loud parse; the abstract cast-triage interaction is off-scope). EMERGENT multi-dim `[*]` combos are SAFE (code-audit): `int a[*][3]` decays like `int a[][3]` (accepted, fixed inner stride), `int a[n][*]`/`[*][*]` → S0003 fail-loud (NEVER a silent stride; pinned `ArrayStarOuterDecaysInnerStarFailsLoud`). Independent code-audit VERDICT SHIP (engine fix opt-in; all 6 fnSuffix sites covered; no silent seam). |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-PARAM-STAR-ABSTRACT` | ⏳ **GATED — normal (opened 2026-07-14, VLA C4c).** The UNNAMED abstract form of `[*]` — `int f(int[*])` (a star-modifier on an ABSTRACT param declarator, no name) — is DEFERRED: `abstractDirectDeclarator`'s suffix repeat is kept NON-speculative (only `directDeclarator`'s, for the NAMED `int f(int a[*])`, was made speculative), so an abstract `[*]` FAILS LOUD at parse (P-error), NEVER a miscompile. Reason: making `abstractDirectDeclarator` speculative interacts with the cast-vs-value type-name triage (the abstract declarator is the cast/sizeof type-name tail) — a separate, riskier change than the named-param path; the named `int f(int a[*])` is the common form. Closing work: extend the `fnSuffixTail`/arrayStarSuffix speculative-suffix treatment to `abstractDirectDeclarator` with a cast-triage regression sweep. Trigger: a real need / a dedicated cycle. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-SIZEOF-TYPEFORM` | ⏳ **GATED — normal (opened 2026-07-13, VLA C2; NOT src-cited — tracked-only).** VLA C2 makes `sizeof <vla-OBJECT>` (`sizeof a`, the value form) a runtime value. Two other composite/type sizeof shapes are FAIL-LOUD (never a silent wrong size), each deferred: **(1) the TYPE-NAME form `sizeof(int[n])`** — a VLA type-name whose `n` C 6.5.3.4p2 evaluates FRESH at the sizeof (a DISTINCT mechanism from the object's decl-frozen slot). Not even constructible in the c-subset today: the `sizeof(TYPE)` grammar rejects an abstract array declarator `int[n]` at PARSE (`P0009`), so it can't reach a `vlaArray`. **(2) a COMPOSITE OPERAND** whose value is a VLA — `sizeof(b, a)` (comma), `sizeof(c ? a : b)` (ternary) — where C applies lvalue conversion (array→pointer decay) so the answer is the POINTER size, NOT the object's frozen size. The c-subset does not model comma/ternary array decay (a PRE-EXISTING gap affecting even fixed arrays), so `vlaObjectOperandSymbol` rejects composites → they FAIL LOUD `H0009` (pin `SizeofOfCompositeVlaOperandFailsLoud`). Closing work: (1) accept `int[n]` in the sizeof type-name grammar + a fresh-evaluate `n*stride` MIR arm; (2) model composite-operand array→pointer decay (a broader semantic fix). Trigger: a real need for either. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-PTR-INIT-FORM-TYPING` | ⏳ **GATED — normal (opened 2026-07-13, VLA C4a-local; src-cited `semantic_analyzer.cpp`).** The NATURAL init form `int (*p)[n] = b` (array-to-pointer-decay INITIALIZER of a pointer-to-VLA local) does NOT compile — it FAILS LOUD `S_TypeMismatch` (`S0003`), a CLEAN rejection, NEVER a silent miscompile. ROOT CAUSE: the Pass-2 init-type derivation (`semantic_analyzer.cpp:7564`, the single-child `typeAt` walk) yields `b`'s DECAYED pointer type, not the raw `array(vlaArray(int),2)` the `Ptr<vlaArray> ← array(array)` decay compare (`type_rules.hpp:371`) needs. A guarded `subtreeType` override (design CRITICAL-1) was ATTEMPTED but proved INERT: `subtreeType` short-circuits on the SAME stamped node (`:8874`), and the initializer identifier `b` is pre-stamped with its decayed type by an earlier pass (the same stamp that types `p`'s body subscript non-VLA — the "body-typing wrinkle"). Verified empirically: the guarded override left `int(*p)[n]=b` still `S0003`, while brace/struct/nested/scalar/decay/string inits stayed clean, the `int(*p)[5]=b_vla` mismatch still rejected, and `int(*p)[5]=b_fixed` still accepted — so it was reverted (no half-working form shipped). The C4a-local WITNESS uses the ASSIGNMENT form `int (*p)[n]; p = b; p[i][j]` (passes assignability + RUNS the runtime-stride path — `c99_vla_ptr` example + a semantic accept pin). Closing work: stop pre-decaying the ptr-to-VLA initializer's stamp (or a dedicated array-decay-init derivation that recovers the raw array shape) so `= b` matches the same decay compare the assignment form uses. Trigger: a real need for the init form / a dedicated cycle. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-FOR-INIT-SCOPE` | ✅ **LANDED 2026-07-14 (VLA C5) — a for-INIT VLA (`for (int a[n]; …; )`) now gets FULL for-scope block teardown.** (This anchor was briefly a fail-loud DEFERRAL earlier in this SAME cycle; the code-audit correctly flagged that gate as a REGRESSION — a top-level for-init VLA compiled + RAN pre-C5, function-scoped + epilogue-freed — and it contradicted the user OPTION-C full-general-teardown directive, so it was replaced with real teardown before the cycle closed. The prior "leaks per-iteration" rationale was factually wrong for a top-level for-init.) MECHANISM: a for-init VLA is declared ONCE at loop entry and PERSISTS across all iterations; C5 frees it ONLY on the loop-EXIT edges, NEVER on the back-edge (freeing it there = a use-after-free of `a[]` in iteration 2+). `emitStackSaveForVla` accepts a `ForStmt` scope node (single-declarator for-init: `hir.parent(declNode) == ForStmt`); the For driver captures the VLA watermark BEFORE the init (`f.vlaBase`), so the for-init frame sits at exactly `vlaBase` and the BODY block's per-iteration teardown (whose watermark is DEEPER, captured after the init) can never reach it (the depth bookkeeping keeps them distinct). The For driver emits ONE StackRestore at the loop EXIT block (the merge of the cond-false edge + every `break` — a break already restored the body VLAs to the post-init watermark, so both predecessors arrive with SP at the post-init level); `continue` frees only body VLAs (post-init watermark), keeping the for-init live; a `goto` OUT of the loop restores the for-init via the ancestry walk (`vlaFrameEnclosesLabel` treats a ForStmt scope as whole-for). Pins: `ForInitVlaEmitsForScopeTeardownAtLoopExitOnly` (for-init-only → 1 save + 1 restore, NONE on the back-edge) + `ForInitAndBodyVlaTornDownAtDistinctPoints` (for-init + body VLA → 2 restores in DIFFERENT blocks) + the `c99_vla_loop` phase-6 runtime witness (a for-init VLA in a 400000-iteration outer loop, written at k=0 + read at k>0 — catches BOTH a back-edge-free [use-after-free → wrong total] AND a leak [overflow]). Multi-declarator for-init VLAs remain deferred → `D-CSUBSET-VLA-FOR-INIT-MULTIDECL`. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-FOR-INIT-MULTIDECL` | ⏳ **GATED — normal (opened 2026-07-14, VLA C5; src-cited `hir_to_mir.cpp` `emitStackSaveForVla`).** A VLA in a MULTI-declarator for-init clause (`for (int a[n], b[m]; …)`) is FAILED LOUD at HIR→MIR (H at the VarDecl), NEVER a silent early-free. WHY: cst_to_hir wraps a multi-declarator for-init in a `Block` of VarDecls (whose parent is the ForStmt); that Block is lowered via `lowerForClauseNode` during For phase 0 and drains fully (its own Block-scope teardown fires) BEFORE the loop header/body — so a VLA there would be freed at the END OF THE INIT, before the first iteration (a use-after-free for the whole loop). The single-declarator for-init (`for (int a[n]; …)`, the common form, `hir.parent == ForStmt`) gets full for-scope teardown (D-CSUBSET-VLA-FOR-INIT-SCOPE). Detection: `emitStackSaveForVla` recognizes the wrapper by `hir.kind(scopeNode)==Block && hir.parent(scopeNode)==ForStmt && hir.forInit(that)==scopeNode`. Closing work: give the for-init wrapper Block for-scope (not block-scope) teardown semantics — push its VLAs onto the vlaScopeStack without the Block driver's per-exit restore, and free them at the For driver's loop-exit. Trigger: a real multi-declarator-for-init-VLA need. |
| `D-CSUBSET-FOR-INIT-SCOPE` | ✅ **LANDED 2026-07-13 (user-reported bug).** C99 6.8.5.3: each `for`-statement opens its OWN scope, so SIBLING/nested `for (int i = ...)` loops re-declaring the same loop name are VALID (the 2nd `i` is a distinct object in a distinct scope). ROOT CAUSE: `forStmt` was ABSENT from the `c-subset.lang.json` `scopes` list → the for-init `int i` LEAKED into the enclosing BLOCK scope → two sibling `for(int i)` collided (2nd loop: uses reported undeclared `S0002` + decl reported unused `S000A`; braced or braceless, no binary emitted). Slipped past the empirical battery (declare-then-assign convention) + sqlite (C89-style loop vars). ★ FIX = add `"forStmt"` to the config `scopes` list — config-driven + AGNOSTIC, the ROOT-CAUSE fix (not a name-reuse special-case): the ONE shared `nodeOpensChildScope` predicate (`semantic_analyzer.cpp:533`, `scopeByRule` from `cfg.scopes:926`) drives all 3 passes (Pass1 mint `:4046`, Pass1.5 `:6406`, Pass2 `:8074`) → the new for-scope is re-derived CONSISTENTLY. Also correctly fixes shadow-then-use-outer (`int i=5; for(int i…){} use(i)` — was a false collision). Independent design-audit + code-audit SHIP (per-pass consistency + cond/incr/body see `i` in braced+braceless + out-of-scope-after-for FAILS LOUD `S0001` + NO leak-reliance regression across examples/tests/sqlite; while/do/switch deliberately NOT added — no init-declaring clause + their braced bodies already open a `block` scope). Pins `SiblingForInitSameNameHaveDistinctScopes` + `ForInitVariableOutOfScopeAfterForRejects` (red-on-disable) + runnable `for_init_sibling_scope`→42 (x86_64-elf/arm64-qemu[release]/pe64), 634/634 ×3 legs + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ×3. |
| `D-CSUBSET-BITINT` | ✅ **ARC COMPLETE 2026-07-13 — C1 ✅ (N≤64) + C2 ✅ (N>64 easy ops) + C3 ✅ (N>64 mul/div) + C4a ✅ (`_BitInt` bit-field members) + C4b ✅ (2026-07-13, wb/uwb arbitrary-magnitude literals + wrap-aware bignum const-fold — `-WIDE-LITERAL`+`-CONSTFOLD-LARGE`; a from-scratch HOST bignum `bit_int_value.hpp` + 2 forks resolved at §B [the integer ladder owns bit-precise typing; the type-blind CST evaluator gained data-model widths → computes the TRUE C23 UAC result width]; independent design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES + code-audit [9 highest-risk surfaces SAFE; I1 const-ref-declared-width silent-wrong-value + I2 message-pin folds re-verified RESOLVED]) LANDED — all N RUN on all 3 legs → 42 (Win pe64 Debug 628/628 + WSL x86_64 628/628 + arm64 qemu `c23_bitint_wide_literal`/`_static_assert`; sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ×3). ★ C4 SPLIT (user §B 2026-07-12: C4 as posed doesn't fit one cycle): C4a = bit-fields (done); **C4b = wb/uwb arbitrary-magnitude literals + wrap-aware bignum const-fold (`-WIDE-LITERAL` + `-CONSTFOLD-LARGE`) — a C2/C3-scale sub-arc [a from-scratch compile-time bignum + 2 architectural forks: the integer-typing ladder can't express "N from the literal's magnitude"; the CST const-evaluator is type-blind by design], its own §B — CLOSES this parent anchor.** ★ USER §B (2026-07-11, fresh post-squash branch): FULL ARBITRARY-WIDTH `_BitInt(N)`** (N up to a large BITINT_MAXWIDTH — multi-word arithmetic for any width — chosen over the conformant-N≤64 minimum and the N≤128 option; the user directed 'implement 100% of FC17.5' and picked the no-compromise scope; the plan's §3.2 'arbitrary-width beyond standard widths' deferral is thereby PROMOTED into scope, not deferred). Crux facts probed: TypeKind is a FIXED enum (I8..I128, one member/width) → `_BitInt(N)` needs a WIDTH-CARRYING representation; general >64-bit integer arithmetic is NOT built today (I128/U128 are type-level only — rank ladder/layout/width-classification — no runnable i128 example, no multi-word add/sub/mul/div lowering; only the `__umulh` 64×64→hi intrinsic); the battery probe is `_BitInt(32)`. This is a MULTI-CYCLE ARC (TLS-scale): whole-arc design + INDEPENDENT design-audit + §B split-approval, THEN per-cycle implementation. **GATED (recorded 2026-07-10, FC17.5 majors triage).** C23 §6.2.5/§6.7.2 `_BitInt(N)` bit-precise integer types are NOT implemented (the keyword does not parse — fail-loud, red `c23_bitint` battery probe). A MAJOR: N-bit arithmetic semantics (promotion rules DIFFER from standard integers — no integer promotion above int for _BitInt), storage/ABI layout per target, and codegen for non-native widths. Trigger: FC18+ full-C23 arc prioritization; requires its own designed cycle with the type-lattice substrate (a new TypeKind axis or a width-parameterized integer core) decided at a §B gate. |
| `D-CSUBSET-BITINT-C2-WIDE` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-12 — `_BitInt(N>64)` RUNS multi-limb on all 3 legs** (Win pe64 + WSL elf64 623/623 ctest, arm64 elf64 direct qemu `c23_bitint_wide`→42; sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 re-probed x86_64-elf + arm64). MULTI-LIMB storage/ABI = ceil(N/64) little-endian i64 limbs, alloca-backed, memory-resident like a struct, reached BY ADDRESS ("Model A") + the EASY ops (add/sub/and/or/xor/not/shift[const+variable]/compare/negate/convert) as inline bounded LIMB-LOOP MIR (scales to BITINT_MAXWIDTH=8388608). The `n>64` semantic gate deleted; `bitIntContainerKind(N>64)`→`latticeFatal` backstop; the by-value ABI rides the UNCHANGED `classifyAggregate` eightbyte classifier (probe+audit-confirmed identical to a same-size all-integer struct on all 4 targets — NO BitInt fork). Two type-shape predicates `isMemoryResidentType`/`isByValueClass` funnel the ~20 aggregate dispatch/guard sites (§A.5 by-construction). Independent design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (2 CRITICALs folded: Model-A by-address dispatch; wide-member-struct byte-copy) + independent code-audit (core VERIFIED; caught float↔wide silent-miscompile → `S_BitIntWideFloatConvUnsupported` 0xE050). `* / %` on wide fail-loud `S_BitIntWideMulDivUnsupported` 0xE04F (C3). Opened `-C2-UNROLL-FASTPATH` (perf). |
| `D-CSUBSET-BITINT-C3-MULDIV` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-12 — wide `_BitInt(N>64)` `* / %` RUN on all 3 legs** (Win pe64 + WSL elf64 624/624 ctest, arm64 elf64 qemu `c23_bitint_wide_muldiv`→42; sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ×3 dormant). Multiply = schoolbook via the shipped `UMulH`, SIGN-AGNOSTIC low-N + one `maskTopLimb` (★ column carry via `Add(Add(hi,c1),c2)` — a carry-out can be 2, so `Or` would silently drop 2^64). Divide/remainder = binary bit-by-bit long-division on UNSIGNED-typed magnitude/rem/quotient temps (so the reused `emitWideNeg`/`emitWideOrder` don't corrupt the most-negative magnitude 2^(N-1)) + C99 signed fixup (`q=(sa^sb)?-uq:uq`, `r=sa?-ur:ur`); div-by-zero → `Unreachable` trap (ud2/BRK, narrow `idiv` #DE parity, user §B). Built on the C2 substrate (`emitLimbLoop`/`maskTopLimb`) + a new runtime-count `emitLimbLoopN`; the 0xE04F fail-loud RETIRED (kept append-only, pinned unreachable). Independent design-audit (math re-derived correct) + code-audit (VERIFIED-CLEAN — arithmetic validated 3 ways; folded F1 = removed a provably-dead `ovf` capture the implementer added, the design-audit's original sizing was right). Runnable `c23_bitint_wide_muldiv` (`_BitInt(128/200/256)`; crafted carry-2 + `INT128_MIN/INT128_MIN` red-on-disable). |

BLR; ZERO new encoder). ★ Mach-O is a THIRD TLS model — it stores a REAL descriptorVA in symbolVa (ordinary reloc kinds 2/3), so it OPTS OUT of the tlsSymbols/XOR backstop [CRIT-1] and runs only the decoupled arm-(a) data-item belt on a macho-local tlsDataSymbols set (`&t`→S0048 + K_RelocationKindMismatch; plain thread_local links clean); word2 reuses the addTlsTemplateOffsets ARITHMETIC only (0-based, no TCB bias) [CRIT-2]; one `__tlv_bootstrap` bind per descriptor at word0 [CRIT-3, exact two-underscore name verified vs Apple's threadLocalVariables.c]; over-aligned macho TLS >16 → fail-loud K_ThreadLocalOveralignedForFormat 0x8016 [M-3, `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-MACHO-OVERALIGN`]. Independent code-audit VERIFIED-CLEAN (the x29-FP-clobber-across-blr seam re-derived SAFE: epilogue reloads x29/x30 from fixed SP slots + cross-call live values in callee-saved x19-x28; all 8 cruxes re-derived from the produced binary; HIGH confidence it runs on a Mac). The 3 arm64 Mach-O binaries (thread_local_basic + thread_local_pthread [the true per-thread discriminator via libSystem] + release) produced + structurally parsed. Opened `-MACHO-OVERALIGN`, `-MACHO-TDATA-RELOC`. ★★ ALL 4 LEGS RUNTIME-WITNESSED GREEN — C1 (WSL x86_64-ELF) · C2 (qemu arm64-ELF) · C3 (native Windows pe64) · C4 (native Apple-Silicon Mach-O, 2026-07-11 → 42) all executed to their asserted exits; `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL` ✅ CLOSED via the user's-Mac witness path (§A.5); the macos-latest CI leg re-confirms post-push (PR#38 label). ★ **C3 (PE/COFF x86_64) ✅ LANDED 2026-07-11**: thread_local RUNS as a native pe64 exe on the Windows host (local ctest, no emulator) — the PeIndexed 4-instr access (mov rax,gs:[0x58] TEB → 32-bit `mov ecx,[__dss_tls_index]` [CRIT-3] → scale-8 SIB block base → lea +secrel32); ONE `.tls` section = the Tdata template, Tbss embedded as raw zeros [DEV-1, SizeOfZeroFill=0 — the loader-doesn't-zero-early-main-thread-fill case, MSVC-consistent, code-audit VERIFIED sound]; IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64 + DataDirectory[9] + a writer-minted LINK-tier singleton `__dss_tls_index` (reserved SymbolId 0xFFFFFF01, fail-loud on multi-CU remap) + AddressOfIndex; the reloc kind = the SHARED target kind 4 (Linear, tls:true) with the PE↔ELF split at the FORMAT tier (a new `IMAGE_REL_AMD64_SECREL` nativeId row — R3/CRIT-1, since x86_64.target is shared by both) + a distinct positive-secrel walker helper `addTlsTemplateOffsets` (NOT the variant-keyed negative-tpoff one); the CRIT-1 walker backstop REPLICATED in pe.cpp (both XOR arms) with the directory VAs written as section-span (never tls-symbol relocs → no false-reject); 3 DIR64 base relocs (Start/End/AddressOfIndex, NOT callbacks=0). ★ A kernel32 `CreateThread` 2-thread witness DISCRIMINATES (workers 105/105 from the template, main 6 → 42) — PE's true per-thread proof (pthread's PE analog). | `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-CROSS-TU-MISMATCH` | ⏳ **GATED (recorded 2026-07-10, TLS C1 code-audit LOW-2 / design-audit M-2).** C11 6.7.1p3 requires `thread_local` on EVERY declaration of the same object; DSS diagnoses the per-TU redeclaration mismatch (0xE047) but a CROSS-TU mismatch (CU1 `thread_local int g=5;` + CU2 `extern int g;`) compiles with NO diagnostic. Codegen is CORRECT (audit-probed exit-42: the LK11 merge precedes the ONE shared MIR→LIR lowering, so every access is TLS-consistent — a missed constraint diagnostic, never a miscompile). Closing work: a K-tier mismatch check at the merge name-unification point, which needs an extern-decl storage-attribute channel that exists for NO attribute today. Trigger: the FC18 conformance sweep or the first cross-TU attribute-mismatch substrate need. |
| `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-DYNAMIC-TLS` | ⏳ **GATED (recorded 2026-07-10 — the §B-accepted arc caveat).** The arc is LOCAL-EXEC only: the exec's PT_TLS block joins glibc's static-TLS set allocated for EVERY thread at pthread_create, so worker threads stay correct absent dlopen (the C1 pthread witness proves it). dlopen'd modules carrying TLS need dynamic TLS (`__tls_get_addr`/TLSDESC). Trigger: a dlopen/shared-library artifact profile ([[D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-INITIAL-EXEC]] is the nearer step of the same family). |
| `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-PE-CALLBACKS` | ⏳ **GATED (recorded 2026-07-10, arc design).** TLS C3 (PE) will emit IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64 with `AddressOfCallBacks = 0` — TLS callbacks matter for DLL thread-attach/detach semantics, not for a self-contained exe. Trigger: a DLL artifact profile. |

Trigger: the block-scope-extern grammar surface (FC18 conformance sweep). |

A future freestanding/no-libc profile would need DSS-side main-thread TLS setup (arch_prctl/TPIDR_EL0 init) since no loader runs. Trigger: a freestanding/no-libc artifact profile. |
| `D-CSUBSET-DECL-QUALIFIER-BEFORE-STORAGE-CLASS` | ⏳ **GATED (recorded 2026-07-10, surfaced by TLS C1 witnesses).** C 6.7p2 lets declaration specifiers appear in ANY order, but the c-subset grammar requires storage-class-first: `const static int x;` / `const thread_local int y;` (qualifier BEFORE the storage-class) are LOUD parse errors (P-tier), while `static const` / `thread_local const` work. Pre-existing class (predates TLS — `const static` fails at pre-arc HEAD identically). Loud, never silent. Trigger: the FC18 conformance sweep or real-code need. |
