WITNESSED: pe64 mutex+cnd+tss example → exit 42 (SYNTHESIZED, ran natively); elf x86_64 + arm64-elf compile clean (plain FFI). ★ MULTI-CU WORKS (code-audit SHIP-WITH-FIXES fold): a referenced-only shim symbol is skipped from import per CU, so the merge's def/extern planning never gave it a merged id → a cloned caller's `GlobalAddr(shim)` std::abort()'d `mergedSymbolOf` BEFORE the post-merge reconstruction ran (a hard crash on valid input). FIX = merge step-3c (mir_merge.cpp): thread each CU's `synthRecipeBySymbol` via `MergeCuInput.synthRecipes` + pre-register each referenced-only shim a merged id (unified by NAME across CUs, collapsing onto a genuine user def if one exists), so the clone remaps + the merged id reaches `symbolNames` + the reconstruction synthesizes it. WITNESSED: a 2-file pe64 build (a.c uses mtx_*, b.c cross-CU-calls into it) compiles (no abort) + runs → 42. `__STDC_NO_THREADS__` is now macho-ONLY (Cycle 2 moved it off the ungated set → the pre-FC18 threads config gate CLOSED; the header is complete on elf/pe64). Sub-anchors: -TRAMPOLINES ✅ CLOSED (Cycle 2 — thrd_create DIRECT-PASS + call_once __dss_once_tramp + thrd_join multi-block) · -MACHO ✅ CLOSED (macho pthread-synth vehicle, runtime-green on a Mac) · -TIMED · -MTX-PLAIN-RECURSIVE · -MACHO-MTX-PLAIN-RECURSIVE · -TSS-DTOR (Cycle-2 residual: tss_dtor_t stayed ptr<void>, non-NULL destructor → S0003) (below). Was: 🟡 SCHEDULED before FC18 — trigger FIRED (user 2026-07-11). (recorded 2026-07-10 — user asked about a real threading include during TLS C1; `#include <pthread.h>` ALREADY ships [shippedLibs/pthread.json: create/join/self/equal + the mutex API + per-format PTHREAD_MUTEX_INITIALIZER] and the `thread_local_pthread_header` example exercises it).** C11 `<threads.h>` (`thrd_create`/`thrd_join`/`mtx_*`/`cnd_*`/`tss_*`) is NOT shipped — DSS conformantly declares `__STDC_NO_THREADS__=1` (landed TLS C1). Building it = a new shippedLibs/threads.json (glibc ≥2.28 ships `thrd_*` IN libc.so.6 → the ELF leg is a descriptor + example; macho libSystem likewise) **+ FLIPPING `__STDC_NO_THREADS__` off** (shipping a working header while defining the macro misleads portable code) + a pe64 posture decision (MSVC/ucrt ship NO C11 threads → pe64 stays loud or maps to a shim — its own §B). A clean small cycle, SCHEDULED before FC18 (user 2026-07-11) alongside the VLA arc. ★ Removing `__STDC_NO_THREADS__` (`c-subset.lang.json:62`) is GATED on shipping a working `<threads.h>` — a conforming impl must not define it once the header works. Trigger: **FIRED (user 2026-07-11)**. |
| `D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MACHO` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-14 (macho C11-threads, runtime-green on a Mac).** The trigger FIRED (this cycle ran on a macOS Apple-Silicon host). Empirical verdict: macOS libSystem exports NO C11 `thrd_*`/`mtx_*`/`cnd_*`/`tss_*` (a link of `extern int thrd_create()` fails "Undefined symbols for architecture arm64: _thrd_create"; no `<threads.h>` in the SDK) — so the SECOND branch: a **pthread-backed synth shim**, the Darwin analog of pe's win32 shim. Landed: a new config-declared FORMAT property `LibrarySynthesis{vehicle: win32\|pthread, libraryPath}` (`object_format_kind.hpp` + a `.format.json` block, read at BOTH synth seams via `format.librarySynthesis()`/`(*formatR)->librarySynthesis()`, captured on `CuMirModule` beside tlsAccess) — pe64→win32/kernel32, macho→pthread/libSystem; ELF omits it (direct FFI). `synthesizeThreadsShim` is now vehicle-parameterized (the win32 kernel32 chain + a NEW 18-recipe pthread chain over pthread_mutex_*/pthread_cond_*/pthread_key_*/pthread_self/sched_yield/pthread_exit), dispatching on the DECLARED vehicle verb, never a format identity — and this DISSOLVED the pass's former hardcoded "kernel32.dll" (both vehicles' import library is now config-sourced). Helper import names are `applyCMangling`-mangled for the format (macho `_pthread_mutex_init` — the un-mangled name SIGABRT'd the first witness). threads.json += macho availableObjectFormats + 18 macho `synthesize` twins + macho typedef variants (mtx_t arr<i64,8>=64 / cnd_t arr<i64,6>=48 / once_flag arr<i64,2>=16 / **tss_t u64** [pthread_key_t=8, DIVERGES from elf/pe u32] / thrd_t u64 — all measured on arm64, == pthread.json). thrd_exit widens res via **SExt** i32→i64 (the only i32→64 form arm64 realizes — SXTW; ZExt admits no I32 source). Multi-CU reconstruction now `unapplyCMangling`s the merged symbol name before the vocab check (identity on pe/elf, strips `_` on macho). WITNESSED: the c11_threads corpus macho arm64 arm compiles → links libSystem pthread → RUNS NATIVELY on the Mac → exit 42 (real tss round-trip); +6 unit tests (per-recipe pthread coverage of all 18, missing-vehicle fail-loud, multi-CU pthread, format-schema load + malformed-vehicle, tss u64-width variant). Commit: this cycle. ★ MACHO TRAMPOLINES LANDED 2026-07-14 (a follow-up cycle) — thrd_create/call_once/thrd_join over libSystem pthread: thrd_create DIRECT-PASSes the C11 int(*)(void*) to pthread_create (ABI-validated on the Mac); call_once is a DIRECT pthread_once (same shape, NO adapter unlike pe); thrd_join is a multi-block pthread_join into an Alloca'd 8-byte void* slot + Trunc to the i32 res. macho once_flag ONCE_FLAG_INIT is a per-format macro variant seeding the macOS PTHREAD_ONCE_INIT magic sig 0x30B1BCBA (816954554), the PTHREAD_MUTEX_INITIALIZER-sig precedent. macho <threads.h> is now COMPLETE (21 recipes) → `__STDC_NO_THREADS__` REMOVED from c-subset.lang.json ENTIRELY (was momentarily macho-only after Cycle 2). WITNESSED: `examples/c-subset/c11_threads_create` macho arm RUNS natively on the Mac (a real pthread spawn + join + call_once-once) → exit 42 (debug + release); the pthread coverage test now spans all 21 recipes. Deferred: mtx_recursive → [[D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MACHO-MTX-PLAIN-RECURSIVE]]. | Config-declared synth vehicle + pthread shim; macho corpus runtime-green. | [[D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-HEADER]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-TRAMPOLINES` | 🟢 **CYCLE 2 LANDED 2026-07-14 (all 3 legs) — completes C11 `<threads.h>` on elf/pe64/arm64.** `thrd_create`/`call_once`/`thrd_join` shipped. pe64 synth (`src/mir/merge/synth_threads_shim.cpp`): **thrd_create = DIRECT-PASS** to CreateThread (audit Finding 1 collapsed the two-trampoline design to one — the C11 `int(*)(void*)` start routine has the SAME x64 ABI as Win32 `DWORD(*)(void*)`, so `func`+`arg` pass straight through; NO malloc closure, NO `__dss_thrd_tramp`; single block `h=CreateThread(0,0,func,arg,0,0); *thr=h; ret ZExt(h==0)*2`) · **call_once** = InitOnceExecuteOnce via ONE module-scoped address-taken `__dss_once_tramp(io,param,ctx)->i32` (`fn=param; fn(); ret 1` — an indirect Call through a ptr<void> callee, D-CSUBSET-FNPTR-INDIRECT-CALL; minted above the recipe max, emitted only when a call_once recipe is present; all 3 Args emitted so ordinals stay contiguous — DCE keeps Args as roots, D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-ARGINDEX) · **thrd_join = the first MULTI-block recipe** (entry `WaitForSingleObject(t,INFINITE); CondBr(res!=0)` → then `GetExitCodeThread(t,(LPDWORD)res via Bitcast)` → join `CloseHandle(t); ret thrd_success`, built with the plain MirBuilder createBlock/addCondBr/addBr API + `rederiveStructCfMarkers(mir)` module-wide after finish() — the merge-path per-pass MirVerifier enforces stored==derived, codegen ignores markers so the single-CU post-optimize path is idempotent). threads.json += thrd_create/call_once DUAL entries + thrd_join pe entry; `thrd_start_t` typed as the REAL `ptr<fn(ptr<void>)->i32>` so a conforming `thrd_create(&t,worker,&arg)` compiles CAST-FREE (fn→void* is NOT an implicit conversion in DSS → S0003). windows.json += InitOnceExecuteOnce (kernel32). elf/arm64 = plain libc.so.6 FFI (F4-verified `nm -D`: thrd_create/call_once/thrd_join are REAL dynamic exports `T @@GLIBC_2.34`+`@2.28` on BOTH x86_64 AND the aarch64 sysroot — NOT atexit-style static shims). ★ `__STDC_NO_THREADS__` moved from the ungated set to macho-ONLY (`availableObjectFormats:["macho"]`) — the pre-FC18 threads config gate CLOSED (threads.h complete on elf/pe64; macho stays carved, D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MACHO). WITNESSED: `examples/c-subset/c11_threads_create` → exit 42 on pe64 (a REAL spawned thread + call_once-exactly-once) + elf x86_64 + arm64-qemu. Was: ⏳ GATED — SCHEDULED Cycle 2 (opened by C11-threads Cycle 1, 2026-07-14). | (LANDED) | [[D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-HEADER]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-TIMED` | ⏳ **GATED — normal (opened by C11-threads Cycle 1, 2026-07-14).** The TIME-conversion pe recipes are elf-FFI-only this cycle: `mtx_timedlock`/`cnd_timedwait` (abstime→relative-ms) AND `thrd_sleep` (a `struct timespec` read — its Windows time_t/long field widths are UNVERIFIED from a Windows host, and a wrong offset is a SILENT MISCOMPILE, so thrd_sleep carries only an elf entry rather than risk a wrong struct read). A pe reference to any of these fails loud (undefined symbol — not injected on pe). Closing work: verify the pe `struct timespec` layout (via the shipped <time.h>), then a timespec-read + abstime→rel-ms pe recipe. Trigger: a real pe timed-wait need or the FC18 sweep. | Verify pe timespec layout; add the timed/sleep pe recipes. | [[D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-HEADER]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MTX-PLAIN-RECURSIVE` | ⏳ **GATED — best-effort, named (opened by C11-threads Cycle 1, 2026-07-14).** On pe64 EVERY mtx_t is a Win32 CRITICAL_SECTION, which is ALWAYS recursion-capable — so `mtx_init(&m, mtx_plain)` yields a mutex that a same-thread re-lock will NOT deadlock (C11 leaves a plain mutex's same-thread-relock behavior UNDEFINED, so a conforming program must not rely on the deadlock; a recursive-capable plain mutex is harmless for conforming use). The `type` argument is accepted + IGNORED (mtx_plain/mtx_recursive/mtx_timed all map to the same CRITICAL_SECTION). Not a miscompile — a deliberate, documented Win32 semantic. Closing work: if strict mtx_plain non-recursive semantics are needed, a manual owner-thread/recursion-count guard around the CRITICAL_SECTION. Trigger: a real program relying on plain-mutex same-thread-relock deadlock. | Manual non-recursive guard if strict semantics needed. | [[D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-HEADER]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-TSS-DTOR` | ⏳ **GATED — normal (opened by C11-threads Cycle 2 code-audit, 2026-07-14).** Cycle 2 upgraded `thrd_start_t` to the concrete `ptr<fn(ptr<void>)->i32>` (so a conforming `thrd_create(&t,worker,&arg)` accepts a bare function CAST-FREE — fn→void* is NOT an implicit conversion in DSS → S0003), but `tss_dtor_t` (the `tss_create` destructor param) stayed the Cycle-1 opaque `ptr<void>`. A NULL destructor coerces fine (the `c11_threads` Cycle-1 witness passes `0` — a null pointer constant coerces to any pointer), but a NON-NULL real destructor `void dtor(void*)` would hit S0003 at the call site. A pre-existing Cycle-1 residual (config/descriptor-only — cited nowhere in src), out of scope for Cycle 2. Closing work: type `tss_dtor_t` as `ptr<fn(ptr<void>)->void>` (the thrd_start_t mirror, threads.json) + a witness passing a real tss destructor that runs at thread exit. Trigger: a real non-NULL `tss_create` destructor, or the FC18 conformance sweep. | Type tss_dtor_t as a concrete fn-ptr + witness a real destructor. | [[D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-HEADER]] |
| `D-LK3-MACHO-ARM64-OBJECT` | ✅ **CLOSED 2026-07-15 (branch feature/macho-arm64-lib) — the arm64 Mach-O relocatable-OBJECT format shipped + cross-toolchain-witnessed on a Mac.** Authored `src/dss-config/object-formats/macho64-arm64-darwin.format.json` — the arm64 sibling of the x86_64 macho object: cputype CPU_TYPE_ARM64 (0x0100000C), filetype MH_OBJECT, one `__text` section, and the 4 ARM64_RELOC_* kinds (BRANCH26/PAGE21/PAGEOFF12/UNSIGNED, kinds 1-4) copied from the arm64 EXEC format (their MH_OBJECT nativeId packing was already declared). ZERO src/ change — the macho.cpp MH_OBJECT walker is target-blind (reads cputype/section-flags/reloc-nativeId from the schema; the only per-cputype dispatch is the EXEC-only `__stubs` substrate a .o never emits). WITNESSED: `dss --compile lib.c --target arm64:macho64-arm64-darwin` emits a valid arm64 MH_OBJECT (`file` → "Mach-O 64-bit object arm64", otool → cputype 16777228 / filetype 1), carrying the ld64-mangled `_forty_two`, and `/usr/bin/clang main.c lib.o -o exe && ./exe` → exit 42 (the cross-toolchain proof, the macho analog of c140-c142's `gcc dss.o` ELF work). Tests: 3 new test_macho_writer arm64 pins (schema-load identity, mach_header golden bytes, an arm64-DISTINCT PAGE21 reloc packing r_type=3 ≠ x86's 0) + a compile_pipeline arm64 `.o`-emission pin (cputype byte + `_forty_two`). SCOPE: leaf-only, matching the x86_64 macho object sibling — extern CALLS deferred to [[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-CALL-MACHO]] (empirically this cycle: declaring externCallDispatch=direct-plt emits the reloc but names the undefined extern `_sym_7` not the source `_base`, so a foreign linker can't resolve it — the macho analog of the ELF c141 extern-call-in-.o mechanism is unbuilt). Was: ⏳ GATED — high (opened 2026-07-14, user-flagged during the macho-threads cycle).** ASYMMETRY: `src/dss-config/object-formats/` ships a Mach-O relocatable-OBJECT (MH_OBJECT `.o`) format for x86_64-darwin (`macho64-x86_64-darwin.format.json`, plan 14 LK3) but NOT for arm64-darwin — only the arm64 EXEC (`macho64-arm64-darwin-exec`). So `arm64:macho64-arm64-darwin` (an object-emission target `test_target_spec.cpp` already PARSES) has no backing format → fail-loud "format not found". Mostly mechanical: the arm64 EXEC format ALREADY declares the full `ARM64_RELOC_*` vocabulary WITH the MH_OBJECT `nativeId` packing `(r_type<<28)|(r_length<<25)|(r_pcrel<<24)`, so the object format mirrors the x86_64 object's MH_OBJECT structure (header filetype=MH_OBJECT, section_64, nlist_64, relocation_info) + the arm64 cputype (0x0100000C) + those relocations. NOT a file-drop: MH_OBJECT emission is a DIFFERENT macho.cpp code path than MH_EXECUTE (writes real relocation_info records vs applies relocs in-place), UNVERIFIED for arm64 — closing MUST ship a witness that emits a correct arm64 `.o` (assert mach_header + relocation_info records; ideally the in-tree linker consumes it). Orthogonal to threads (threads uses the exec format). Closing work: author `macho64-arm64-darwin.format.json` + an arm64 MH_OBJECT emission test/corpus. Trigger: user-directed NEXT cycle (2026-07-14). | New arm64 macho object format + a `.o`-emission witness. | [[D-LK3-RODATA]] |
| `D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-CALL-MACHO` | ✅ **CLOSED c146 (2026-07-16, roadmap 0b first half) — extern FUNCTION calls now lower into an arm64 Mach-O relocatable object that a FOREIGN linker resolves.** FIX (mirrors ELF c141 exactly): **(1)** `macho.cpp` MH_OBJECT undefined-extern nlist loop emits `objNames.externName(e, "_sym_")` — the REAL pipeline-mangled import name (`_rand`, verbatim, the ld64 `_` already applied by applyCMangling) instead of the internal `_sym_<id>`; `_sym_<id>` stays the fallback for a target that is neither defined nor a known import. **(2)** `macho64-arm64-darwin.format.json` declares `externCallDispatch: "direct-plt"` (the lowering emits a plain BL + BRANCH26 reloc; ld64 synthesizes the stub — DSS never builds one in a `.o`). The x86_64-darwin object sibling stays leaf-only (NO runtime leg exists to witness it — the D-LK1-MACHO-X8664-DARWIN-DATA-SECTION untested-form discipline; the walker code is shared, so it lights up by declaring the field when a runner lands). WITNESSES: `MachOWriter.Arm64ObjectExternCallEmitsUndefImportRealName` (undefined nlist carries `_libc_fn` + N_UNDF|N_EXT + n_sect=0, BRANCH26 reloc r_extern=1/r_symbolnum=extern idx; **red-on-disable DEMONSTRATED** — reverting to the `_sym_` spelling fails `_sym_20 != _libc_fn`) + END-TO-END CLI witness: `extern int rand(void); int two_rands(void){return rand()+rand();}` → `arm64:macho64-arm64-darwin` `.o` (magic 0xfeedfacf, cputype 0x100000C) whose parsed nlist = `_two_rands` DEFINED (0xf, sect 1) + `_rand` UNDEF (0x1, sect 0) — exactly the clang-emitted shape ld64 resolves against libSystem. Runtime link+run validation = the user's Apple-Silicon mac + the macos-latest CI leg (per the roadmap's cross-target discipline). RESIDUAL (the 0b second half) ✅ CLOSED c147 (2026-07-16): the MH_OBJECT walker now emits DATA SECTIONS — `__TEXT,__const` (rodata) / `__DATA,__data` / `__DATA,__const` (relro, reloc-bearing const — per-section `relocation_info` with ARM64_RELOC_UNSIGNED r_extern=1 + the addend IN the 8-byte slot, clang's in-place convention) / `__DATA,__bss` (S_ZEROFILL last; vmsize covers, filesize does not) — one anonymous LC_SEGMENT_64, flat cumulative addrs, data nlist = N_SECT|N_EXT with n_sect = 1-based ordinal + n_value = FLAT address, undefined-extern scan extended over data-item reloc targets; synthetic per-BLOCK symbols (computed-goto / dense-switch jump tables) registered + emitted as N_SECT LOCALS at flat text addresses (the ELF STB_LOCAL leg's mirror — the c147 silent-failure review caught the writer fabricating N_UNDF `_sym_<id>` externs for them, an unlinkable-or-silently-misbound `.o`; folded pre-commit) + the data-reloc gate rejects non-Linear formula kinds (the `pcRelative` flag is Linear-only — same fold applied to the shared exec kernel); 11 strict red-on-disable pins + two CLI witnesses (a string-literal `puts(msg)` `.o` + a const fn-ptr table + mutable global `.o`, both python-parsed to clang-shaped layouts); reloc-bearing RODATA + TLS still fail loud; the data-free module stays byte-identical. The Mach-O analog of ELF c142+c145 — the arm64 Mach-O `.o` route (extern calls c146 + data sections c147) is now feature-complete pending the user's mac/CI link+run validation. — ORIGINAL: ⏳ GATED (opened 2026-07-15, surfaced by D-LK3-MACHO-ARM64-OBJECT). A relocatable Mach-O `.o` that CALLS an external function does not link cross-toolchain: declaring `externCallDispatch: direct-plt` on a macho object format DOES lower the call + emit the BRANCH26/PAGE21/PAGEOFF12 relocs with correct nativeId packing, BUT the UNDEFINED extern symbol lands in the symtab as an internal id (`_sym_7`) rather than the source-level C name (`_base`), so a foreign linker fails "undefined symbols: _sym_7". The DEFINED-function naming is correct (c139, [[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-SYMBOL-NAMES]]); the UNDEFINED-extern-reference naming for the macho object walker is unbuilt — the macho analog of the ELF-only c141 [[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-CALL-RELOCATABLE]]. Affects BOTH macho object arches (x86_64 + arm64), which is why NEITHER declares externCallDispatch (leaf-only). Closing work: emit the source-level name for undefined extern symbols referenced by relocations in the macho MH_OBJECT walker (object_symbol_names.hpp / macho.cpp), then declare `externCallDispatch: direct-plt` on both macho object formats + a cross-toolchain call-through-.o witness. Also gated: [[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-CALL-RELOCATABLE]] is elf-x86_64-only; elf-aarch64 is likewise leaf-only. Trigger: a real need for extern calls in a macho `.o`, or a cross-arch object-completeness sweep. | Emit undefined-extern source names in the macho object walker + externCallDispatch. | [[D-LK3-MACHO-ARM64-OBJECT]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MACHO-MTX-PLAIN-RECURSIVE` | ⏳ **GATED — best-effort, named (opened by C11-threads macho, 2026-07-14).** The macho pthread `mtx_init` recipe passes a NULL mutexattr → PTHREAD_MUTEX_NORMAL (non-recursive), which is CORRECT for `mtx_plain` (the tested + common mode) but means `mtx_init(&m, mtx_recursive)` on macho yields a NON-recursive mutex — a same-thread re-lock **DEADLOCKS at runtime**. This is a SILENT behavioral divergence (not a fail-loud, not a compile error): the mtx type is a runtime arg the single-block shim cannot statically reject. UNOBSERVABLE this cycle — Cycle 1 has no `thrd_create`, so the program is single-threaded and no recursive-relock path executes. (This is the MIRROR-IMAGE of the pe wart [[D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MTX-PLAIN-RECURSIVE]], but the OPPOSITE direction: pe's always-recursive is benign [recursive ⊇ plain], macho's always-plain deadlocks on a recursive request.) Chose NULL-attr over an always-recursive `pthread_mutexattr_init`/`settype`/`destroy` dance because the latter needs a stack `Alloca`, breaking the pass's single-block/alloca-free invariant. Closing work: honor `mtx_recursive` via a mutexattr (once multi-block shim recipes are in scope) OR a branchless attr-select. Trigger: `thrd_create` (Cycle 2, makes it observable) or a real program using a macho recursive mutex. | Honor mtx_recursive via mutexattr (needs multi-block shim). | [[D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MACHO]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-PE-OVERALIGN` | ⏳ **GATED — trigger-gated (opened by TLS C3, 2026-07-11).** A PE `thread_local` whose required alignment exceeds the x64 loader's guaranteed TLS block-base alignment (16 bytes = MEMORY_ALLOCATION_ALIGNMENT) is FAIL-LOUD this cycle (K_ThreadLocalOveralignedForFormat in pe.cpp — the IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64 has no field to request block over-alignment, unlike ELF's PT_TLS p_align which C1/C2 honor). ≤16-aligned TLS (every normal scalar/pointer/small-struct) is unaffected. Closing work: a block-base over-alignment mechanism (a padded-slot / loader-cooperative scheme) if a real >16-aligned PE TLS need (SIMD/atomics) fires. Trigger: that need. |
| `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-PE-LARGE-ZEROFILL` | ⏳ **GATED — normal (opened by TLS C3, 2026-07-11).** PE TLS embeds the WHOLE per-thread block (tdata template + tbss zeros) as raw `.tls` bytes with `SizeOfZeroFill=0` — correct on every thread incl. the main thread's early static TLS (the empirically-observed loader-doesn't-zero-early-main-thread-fill case; matches MSVC for small TLS), but costs file bloat for LARGE zero-init TLS. Closing work: emit a real `SizeOfZeroFill` tail once the loader-zeroing is verified safe across all thread-creation paths (incl. early main-thread). Trigger: a large zero-init PE thread_local where the bloat matters. |
| `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-MACHO-OVERALIGN` | ⏳ **GATED — trigger-gated (opened by TLS C4, 2026-07-11).** A Mach-O `thread_local` whose required alignment exceeds 16 (the malloc/TLV-block-base guarantee) is FAIL-LOUD this cycle (K_ThreadLocalOveralignedForFormat 0x8016 — the format-generic gate C3 introduced). macho CAN express section-align, but whether dyld over-aligns the per-thread TLV block base beyond 16 is UNVERIFIABLE without a Mac; conservative + consistent with the C3 PE gate, never a silent under-align. Closing work: verify dyld's TLV block-base over-alignment on real hardware, then align word2 + set section_64.align + lift the gate + a CI `_Alignas(32)` macho example. Trigger: that Mac-verified behavior. |
| `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-MACHO-TDATA-RELOC` | ⏳ **GATED — normal (opened by TLS C4, 2026-07-11).** A reloc-bearing macho TLS template (`thread_local char *msg = "hi";` — an initialized pointer into rodata inside __thread_data) FAILS LOUD via buildExecDataSection(allowItemRelocations=false). PIE-rebasing a per-thread template pointer (the loader copies the template per thread; a link-time abs pointer + a DIR64-analog rebase into a per-thread copy) is unverifiable without a Mac. Closing work: verify dyld's handling of a rebased pointer in a copied TLV template on real hardware, then allow item relocations in the __thread_data template + emit the rebase. Trigger: that Mac-verified behavior (or a real consumer needing a pointer-typed macho thread_local). |
| `D-PIPELINE-CU5-MULTIFILE-EXTERN-DATA` | ⏳ **GATED — TRIAGE (recorded 2026-07-10, surfaced by the TLS C1 cross-CU E2E pin).** The API-only `compileFiles`-with-N-sources surface (CU5: ONE multi-FILE unit — the CLI never routes there; every multi-source CLI build goes `routesToMultiUnit`→`compileUnits`/LK11) mints a SURVIVING extern-data import for a sibling-FILE definition instead of resolving it intra-unit. For a THREAD-LOCAL extern the TLS C1 linker gate correctly fail-louds (K_FormatLacksThreadLocalSupport citing [[D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-INITIAL-EXEC]]) — LOUD, never a silent mis-bind. Whether PLAIN (non-TLS) extern data on that same CU5 path silently becomes a copy-relocation library import (vs an intra-unit resolution) was NOT isolated. Closing work: probe the non-TLS shape; either resolve sibling-file externs intra-unit on CU5 or fail loud. Trigger: any real API consumer of multi-file `compileFiles`, or the FC18 sweep. |
| `D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE` | ✅ **LANDED 2026-07-10 (FC17.5 cycle 2) — C23 §6.7.9 `auto` type inference (`auto x = expr;`).** The audit-revised design (GO-WITH-FIXES, 30 probes): a HEAD-LESS `autoInferredVarDecl` grammar rule (`localDeclSpecifiers initDeclaratorList ;`) + the `autoInferredForDecl` for-init mirror; the type is INFERRED from the sole declarator's initializer at the declaration's own Pass-1.5 visit (`resolveAutoInferredDeclaration` → the inferred type plays the head's role and the ORDINARY declarator fold applies it — one proven write path). GENERIC engine capability: `DeclarationRule.inferTypeFromInitializer` (loader waives the declarator-mode head requirement for it; `head ∧ infer` → C_ConflictingField; the flag on a non-declarator row → loud) + `DeclarationRule.requiredSpecifierToken` (loader-resolved token-kind NAME — the constMarker idiom; never an engine keyword identity). ★C1 the presence gate: `static x=5;`/`register y=2;`/`alignas(4) z=9;`/`[[maybe_unused]] w=3;` parse into the headless rule and STAY errors (S_AutoInferenceInvalid) — C89 implicit-int never resurrects. ★C2 branch order `[autoInferredVarDecl, varDecl, attributeDeclaration]` (+ the forStmt inline speculative mirror `[autoInferredForDecl, forDecl, forInitAmbig]`, gatedMarkers copied — `for (static auto i=0;;)` stays S_StaticStorageInForInit): autoInferred fast-fails ≤3 tokens on real-typed decls; varDecl is the declared-LAST structural candidate so the all-fail REPLAY (parser.cpp ~:1984) re-parses it committed with NO budget — the >4096-token `static const int big[]={…}` pin compiles (EMPIRICALLY red under varDecl-first: P0009; sqlite's largest is 3918/4096 so the sqlite gate can't catch the cliff). ★C3 the full normalization: braced-single unwrap (`auto x={5};`; `{}`/`{1,2}`/designated/nested → shared S_InvalidScalarInitializer) → literal PRE-STAMP via the EXTRACTED shared `typeLiteralIfAny` chokepoint (pass2Post's literal-typing block, byte-identical — at Pass 1.5 the Pass-2 blanket stamps don't exist; probe-confirmed `typeof(42) a=7; int arr[sizeof(a)];` fails S000B at HEAD = the backfill false-green the audit's p1 hit) → `subtreeType` → DECAY Array→Ptr<elem> (`auto s="str"` is char*) + FnSig→Ptr (`auto f=fn` callable) → REJECT LOUD Void / NullptrT (folded into [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-T-DECLARABLE]]) / invalid incl. self-ref `auto x=x;` → stripVolatile (6.7.9p2 drops top-level qualifiers). Diagnostics 0xE041–0xE044 (S_AutoRequiresSingleDeclarator / S_AutoRequiresPlainIdentifier / S_AutoRequiresInitializer / S_AutoInferenceInvalid) ALL UNSUPPRESSABLE (the ★C3 backfill seam: Pass 2's decl arm backfills `rec.type = initTy` for any unresolved declarator-mode symbol — a suppressed reject would silently adopt it; closed-table 96→100 + named membership pins). hirLowering rows → VarDecl (zero HIR change — lowerVarLikeInto reads rec->type). Tests: 16 unit pins (incl. the Pass-1.5-VISIBILITY `typeof(x) y;` discriminator + the >4096 replay pin + C89 `auto int x;` + constexpr-auto + volatile-strip — 3 pin groups verified red-on-arm-disable) + 5 diagnostic goldens + the `auto_type_inference` 4-target runnable example (exit 42 debug AND release; width/deref/array-decay/fn-call/for/static/constexpr/braced/visibility all runtime-witnessed) + battery `c23_auto_type` GREEN (130/133). Deferrals opened: [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-QUALIFIED]], [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-DERIVED-DECLARATOR]], [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-FILE-SCOPE]]. | (closed) | plan-23 FC17.5; `c-subset.lang.json` (grammar + 2 decl rows + 2 hirLowering rows) + `semantic_config.hpp` (2 DeclarationRule fields) + `grammar_schema_json.cpp` (loader relax + 3 cross-checks) + `semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`resolveAutoInferredDeclaration` + the `typeLiteralIfAny` extraction + the resolveDeclTypes arm) + `parse_diagnostic.{hpp,cpp}` (0xE041–0xE044) + `unsuppressable_codes.cpp` (+4 → 100) |
| `D-CSUBSET-AUTO-QUALIFIED` | ⏳ **GATED (opened 2026-07-10 by [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE]]).** `const auto x = 5;` / `auto const x = 5;` / `volatile auto …` — a QUALIFIED inference declaration (C23 6.7.9 allows the type-qualifier composition) is a LOUD PARSE ERROR today (the headless rule has no head-qualifier slot; `const`/`volatile` are not localDeclSpecifiers) — pinned loud by `AutoFileScopeAndQualifiedStayLoudParseErrors`, never silent. **Trigger:** a real TU declares a qualified auto object. Needs a qualifier slot on the inference rule + the const/volatile symbol marking WITHOUT re-opening the c58 initializer-const-leak (the row deliberately declares NO constMarker/volatileMarker — the headless fallback scan spans the initializer). Priority: normal. | Admit head qualifiers on the inference form; mark the symbol const/volatile from the PREFIX slot only (never the whole-decl scan). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE]]; the c58 `D-CSUBSET-INITIALIZER-CONST-TOKEN-LEAK` scan scoping |

Needs per-layer inference (unify the declarator fold with the inferred base). | `D-LK-DYN-TEXT-ABS-RELOC` | ⏳ **OPEN (c150, opened with the D-LK1-4 shared-library half).** A loader-slid ELF ET_DYN image cannot carry an ABSOLUTE fixup in `.text`: the page is read-only at load (patching would need deprecated DT_TEXTREL, rejected by hardened loaders) and the link-time value is wrong under any nonzero slide. The dyn arm of `encodeElfExecDynamic` (src/link/format/elf.cpp, pre-flight loop) fails loud `K_RelocationKindMismatch` on any function relocation whose target row is `Linear && !pcRelative` (abs32/abs64/tpoff32-class); slide-safe kinds (Linear pc-relative rel32/riprel32 + every non-Linear ARM64 instruction formula) pass. DSS codegen reaches globals rip-relatively, so no shipped producer trips this — it is the belt for a future absolute-in-code producer. **Trigger:** a producer that legitimately needs an absolute in `.text` of a `.so` (none known; the correct fix is always pc-relative codegen or a GOT indirection). Priority: low (belt, not a feature gap). | Either keep rejecting forever (absolutes in slid text are wrong by construction) or add GOT-indirect materialization for the offending pattern. | [[D-LK1-4]] (the ET_DYN arm); `src/link/format/elf.cpp` pre-flight loop; D-LK-DYN-RODATA-ITEM-RELOC (the data-side sibling) |
| ~~`D-LK-DYN-RODATA-ITEM-RELOC`~~ | ✅ **CLOSED c154 (2026-07-17, with [[D-LK11-ELF-MACHO-CROSSCU-THUNK-EMISSION]] / roadmap A4) — the last rodata+relocs PRODUCER is fixed; the walker belt STAYS.** The LK11 assembled-tier merge (`mergeModules`, src/link/linker.cpp) was the only producer minting reloc-bearing `Rodata` (its cross-CU thunk slots). c154 keys the merge on the format's DECLARED `externCallDispatch`: under `direct-plt` (every shipped format) the reference binds DIRECTLY to the sibling definition — no slot at all (the old unconditional slot ALSO retargeted the direct call site into the slot's data bytes: linked clean, SIGSEGV'd at run — witnessed pre-fix on elf-exec + pe-exec); under `indirect-slot` the slot survives and mints as **`RelRoConst` via the shared c145 `relocBearingGlobalSection` chokepoint** (hoisted from asm.cpp's anonymous namespace to `core/types/section_kind.hpp` — producer + linker now share the ONE const/mutable/thread-local rule), riding the relro→`.data` merge with an `R_X86_64_RELATIVE` row on ET_DYN exactly like a c145 const fn-ptr table. Pinned: `CrossCuLinkFormats.IndirectSlotDynMintsRelRoThunkSlotWithRelativeRow` (RELATIVE r_offset/r_addend/slot-bytes/indirect-call-disp; re-minting `Rodata` trips this belt = red-on-disable) + `DirectPltLinksCleanOnAllFourShippedImageFormats`. The dyn walker's rodata-reloc reject (`K_RelocationKindMismatch`, elf.cpp dyn arm) STAYS as the belt for a hand-built module / future producer regression — `ElfDynWriter.RelocBearingRodataFailsLoudOnDynArm` keeps it pinned. | (closed — `mergeModules` is dispatch-keyed; the slot arm mints `RelRoConst` via the shared chokepoint) | [[D-LK1-4]]; [[D-LK-RELRO-CONST-DATA-RELOCATABLE]] (c145 — the adopted section kind + chokepoint); [[D-LK11-ELF-MACHO-CROSSCU-THUNK-EMISSION]] (c154); `src/link/format/elf.cpp` dyn rodata belt (kept) |
| `D-LK-DYN-TLS-MODEL` | ⏳ **OPEN (c150, opened with the D-LK1-4 shared-library half).** Thread-locals in a SHARED LIBRARY need the general-/local-dynamic (or initial-exec) TLS model: the library's TLS block gets a LOADER-assigned module offset, so the link-time local-exec tpoffs the dynamic walker computes (valid only for the executable's own PT_TLS block) would silently address another module's slots. Three-layer loud wall today: (1) the shipped `elf64-x86_64-linux-dyn.format.json` declares NO `tlsAccess` -> the MIR->LIR gate fails `K_FormatLacksThreadLocalSupport` on the first thread-local access; (2) it advertises NO tdata/tbss in `supportedDataSections` -> the linker's pre-walker section gate rejects; (3) the walker belt in `encodeElfExecDynamic` (isDyn && hasTls) fails loud for hand-built modules / prematurely-opted-in schemas. **Trigger:** a real `.so` source using `thread_local`. Closing work: `__tls_get_addr`-based general-dynamic lowering (TLSGD reloc pair + DTPMOD64/DTPOFF64 dynamic relocs + PT_TLS on the .so) or initial-exec (GOTTPOFF + TPOFF64 GOT slot), plus the c145-style relro handling for the template. Priority: normal. | General-dynamic (or initial-exec) TLS lowering + the dyn walker PT_TLS arm + DTPMOD/DTPOFF emission. | [[D-LK1-4]]; [[D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL]] (the exec-side local-exec model); [[D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-INITIAL-EXEC]] (the import-side sibling gap); `src/link/format/elf.cpp` dyn TLS belt |
| `D-LK2-DLL-DLLMAIN-ENTRY` | ⏳ **OPEN (c152, opened with the D-LK2-4 dll half).** The DSS `.dll` ships ENTRY-LESS: `AddressOfEntryPoint = 0`, so the Windows loader SKIPS the DllMain process/thread notification calls entirely (legal PE -- the `link /NOENTRY` shape; witnessed live: ctypes.CDLL loads + calls exports). A future DLL that NEEDS per-process/per-thread init (a `DllMain`, C++-style dynamic initializers, or the D-LK-DLL-TLS-MODEL callback path) requires the entry arm: a schema-declared dll entry cluster (the c151 PIE discriminator pattern -- validate() currently REJECTS `processExit`/`entryCallingConvention`/`processArgs`/`entryPoint` on `pe.type=dll`, `object_format_schema.cpp` Dll shape block), a `DllMain(hinst, reason, reserved)`-shaped trampoline (ms_x64, return TRUE), and `AddressOfEntryPoint` pointing at it. **Trigger:** the first DLL consumer needing load-time init. Priority: normal. | Add the dll entry-cluster discriminator to validate() (all-or-none, mirroring the ET_DYN PIE rule), synthesize the DllMain trampoline in the linker keyed on the schema (zero walker identity branches), point AddressOfEntryPoint at it. | [[D-LK2-4]] (the entry-less dll that shipped); `src/link/object_format_schema.cpp` Dll shape block + `src/link/format/pe.cpp` dll entry arm; c151 PIE cluster precedent |
| `D-LK-DLL-TLS-MODEL` | ⏳ **OPEN (c152, opened with the D-LK2-4 dll half).** Thread-locals in a Windows DLL are their own loader-interaction arc: the exec's static-TLS machinery (IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64 + gs:[0x58] pe-indexed access, TLS C3) assumes the module is present at process start; a LoadLibrary'd DLL's TLS directory is honored only by the Win 8.1+ dynamic-static-TLS path, threads created before load get their block lazily, and zero-init behavior differs from the exec's embed-the-block workaround -- an UNTESTED form. Loud wall today (mirrors the ELF D-LK-DYN-TLS-MODEL stance): the shipped `pe64-x86_64-windows-dll.format.json` declares NO `tlsAccess` and NO tdata/tbss in `supportedDataSections`, so the MIR->LIR gate + the linker's pre-walker section gate reject every `thread_local` loudly. **Trigger:** a real `.dll` source using `thread_local`. Priority: normal. | Witness the LoadLibrary static-TLS path on a real Windows loader (incl. pre-existing-thread access + zero-init), then declare tdata/tbss + tlsAccess on the dll schema; the walker machinery is shared with the exec arm already. | [[D-LK2-4]]; [[D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL]] (the exec-side model); [[D-LK-DYN-TLS-MODEL]] (the ELF sibling stance); `src/dss-config/object-formats/pe64-x86_64-windows-dll.format.json` |
| `D-LK3-DYLIB-TLS-MODEL` | ⏳ **OPEN (c153, opened with the D-LK3-3 dylib half).** Thread-locals in a dlopen'd Mach-O DYLIB are an UNTESTED form on this no-Mac host, even though Mach-O's TLV-descriptor model is structurally PER-IMAGE (each image carries its own `__thread_vars` descriptors dyld rewires at image load — unlike ELF, where a `.so` needs a different TLS model outright): whether dyld's TLV setup runs identically for a dlopen'd (vs launch-time) image, and how threads existing BEFORE the dlopen observe the new image's thread-locals, cannot be witnessed here. Priority: normal. | Witness the dlopen TLV path on real Apple Silicon (incl. pre-existing-thread access), then declare tdata/tbss + `tlsAccess {model: "macho-tlv"}` on the dylib schema; the walker's TLV machinery (descriptors + `__tlv_bootstrap` binds + MH_HAS_TLV_DESCRIPTORS) is shared with the exec arm already. | [[D-LK3-3]]; [[D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL]] (the exec-side macho-tlv model); [[D-LK-DYN-TLS-MODEL]] + [[D-LK-DLL-TLS-MODEL]] (the tri-mirror siblings); `src/dss-config/object-formats/macho64-arm64-darwin-dylib.format.json` |
| `D-LK3-DYLIB-WEAK-EXPORT` | ⏳ **OPEN (c153, opened with the D-LK3-3 dylib half).** A WEAK definition exported from an MH_DYLIB needs Mach-O weak-definition machinery this cycle does not ship: the trie terminal must carry `EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_WEAK_DEFINITION` (0x04), the mach header must advertise `MH_WEAK_DEFINES` (0x8000, plus `MH_BINDS_TO_WEAK` on consumers), and dyld's weak-coalescing pass must see matching weak-bind info — silently exporting a weak symbol STRONG (flags 0) would change cross-image coalescing semantics (two images' weak definitions would no longer collapse to one). The dylib export-set builder (`macho.cpp` `encodeExecDynamic`, the export-trie loop) FAILS LOUD on `SymbolBinding::Weak` citing this anchor (pinned: `MachoDylibWriter.WeakExportFailsLoud`). The ELF dyn arm exports Weak as STB_WEAK fine (c150) — this is a Mach-O-specific gap, not a producer one. **Trigger:** the first weak definition in a dylib-profile source. Priority: normal. | Emit `EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_WEAK_DEFINITION` on the trie terminal + `MH_WEAK_DEFINES` in the header flags (content-derived, like MH_HAS_TLV_DESCRIPTORS) + the weak-bind stream, then validate on real Apple Silicon. | [[D-LK3-3]]; `src/link/format/macho.cpp` export-trie loop; the c150 ELF STB_WEAK export precedent |
| `D-LK3-DYLIB-CHAINED-FIXUPS-EXPORT-TRIE` | ⏳ **OPEN (c153, opened with the D-LK3-3 dylib half).** The dylib's EXPORT TRIE is emitted through the legacy `LC_DYLD_INFO_ONLY.export_off` field; under chained fixups (`LC_DYLD_CHAINED_FIXUPS`, D-LK6-14) that command is absent and the trie must move to the separate `LC_DYLD_EXPORTS_TRIE` (0x80000033) linkedit_data_command — not implemented, so a chained-fixups dylib would ship exports dyld never finds (dlsym misses everything). validate() REJECTS `useChainedFixups: true` on a Dylib schema loud, citing this anchor (pinned: `MachoDylibFormatJsonValidate.ChainedFixupsRejected`). No shipped format enables chained fixups anyway (exec included — the emitter is unit-test-only, D-LK6-14). **Trigger:** the first dylib schema wanting chained fixups (macOS 13+ modernization arc). Priority: low (rides D-LK6-14's own integration backlog). | Emit LC_DYLD_EXPORTS_TRIE (16-byte linkedit_data_command pointing at the same trie blob) when useChainedFixups && filetype==Dylib, plus the DATA-fixup chained encodings D-LK6-14 already tracks. | [[D-LK3-3]]; [[D-LK6-14]] (the chained-fixups integration family); `src/link/object_format_schema.cpp` dylib shape block |
| `D-LK-PE-IMAGE-TEXT-ABS-RELOC` | ⏳ **OPEN (c152, opened with the D-LK2-4 dll half; live on the EXEC arm too -- the condition is schema-read; c152 review fold: the belt keys on `isDll || DYNAMIC_BASE`, because a DLL rebases on preferred-base COLLISION regardless of the ASLR flag).** A relocatable-at-load PE image may be rebased by the loader (a `.dll` routinely is; the ASLR exec at boot), which adjusts exactly the absolute slots listed in `.reloc`. The PE walker collects IMAGE_REL_BASED_DIR64 sites from DATA-ITEM fixups + the TLS directory VAs -- NOT from `.text` (`applyExecRelocations` patches in place without reporting sites), so an ABSOLUTE (non-pc-relative, non-tls) function relocation would bake a preferred-base VA into code that no `.reloc` row ever adjusts: silent wrong address under rebase. The walker belt (`pe.cpp` pre-flight loop, keyed on `oh.dllCharacteristics & DYNAMIC_BASE`) fails loud `K_RelocationKindMismatch`; DSS x86_64 codegen emits none (rip-relative lea, rel32 calls, jump tables as data items, FF 25 thunks), so this is the belt for a future absolute-in-code producer -- the exact PE mirror of [[D-LK-DYN-TEXT-ABS-RELOC]]. **Trigger:** a producer legitimately needing an absolute in image `.text` (none known). Priority: low (belt, not a feature gap). | Either keep rejecting (absolutes in rebased text are wrong by construction without collection) or extend the `.reloc` collector to report text fixup sites. | [[D-LK2-4]]; `src/link/format/pe.cpp` pre-flight loop; [[D-LK-DYN-TEXT-ABS-RELOC]] (the ELF sibling) |
| `D-CSUBSET-STATIC-INT-TO-PTR-ABSOLUTE` | **✅ CLOSED 2026-07-21 (TF-C38, SQLite testfixture-link sub-arc, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`/PR #46) — an explicit integer-constant→pointer cast in a STATIC/global initializer folds to an ABSOLUTE-address pointer constant.** `static void *p = (void*)0x5;`, an aggregate member `{…, (void*)0x5}`, an array `{(void*)1,(void*)2}`, and the real trigger `TCL_CHANNEL_VERSION_5 = ((Tcl_ChannelTypeVersion)0x5)` inside a `static Tcl_ChannelType {…}` initializer (tclsqlite-ex.c — the LAST error blocking the SQLite testfixture MAIN TU). C permits an integer-constant address in a STATIC-storage pointer initializer (6.6 / 6.3.2.3); its value IS the integer, an absolute address with NO symbol and NO relocation (gcc/clang emit the same bytes). `const_eval.cpp` DELIBERATELY refuses a cast-to-pointer (invariant: "pointer targets remain non-foldable" — UNCHANGED; this anchor does NOT make const-eval fold pointer casts), so the aggregate fell to `runtimeInit` → the `hir_to_mir.cpp` bitfield-rvalue fail-loud guard (`H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind`, `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME`). **Closing work:** a new `tryClassifyIntToPtrConst` classifier in `hir_to_mir.cpp` — the sibling of the c67/c80 null-pointer (`tryClassifyNullPointerConst`) and c68/c80 null-base-index (`tryClassifyNullBaseIndexConst`) static-pointer-constant family — peels the explicit pointer `Cast` and folds its INTEGER operand to a plain `uint64_t` leaf (`core=Ptr`); the asm scalar-leaf arm (`src/asm/asm.cpp` `decodeScalarLiteralBits`) writes 8 raw LE bytes, no reloc. Wired at BOTH global-init sites (the `tryClassifyAggregateConst` member loop + the `classifyGlobals` scalar cascade) AFTER the specific null-pointer / null-base-index handlers, BEFORE the generic `evaluateConstant` fallback (so `(void*)0` and the `SQLITE_INT_TO_PTR` AddressOf-Index shape stay with their own arms). CONSERVATIVE: fires only when the operand folds to a PLAIN integer (int64/uint64) — a symbol address, an aggregate, or a float → nullopt (the symbol-addr / whole-aggregate-bail / runtimeInit paths still govern). Block-scope `static void *p = (void*)0x5;` reaches the SAME `classifyGlobals` path (static-duration locals lower to module globals) — witnessed compiling clean. Rides the c67/c68/c80 static-pointer-constant classifier family. Tests: `examples/c-subset/static_int_to_ptr` (scalar + aggregate-member + symbol-in-aggregate mix + array + block-scope; baseline + `release` optimizedPipeline arms) + MIR-lowering pool pins (classifier fires, red-on-disable) + asm-substrate decode pins (raw 8-byte int→ptr leaf beside a symbol reloc). | (closed — `hir_to_mir.cpp` `tryClassifyIntToPtrConst` + 2 wiring sites; `const_eval.cpp` UNCHANGED) | [[D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-GLOBAL-SYMBOL-ADDRESS]] (c67 — null-pointer sibling); [[D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-GLOBAL-NONSYMBOL-PTR-MEMBER]] (c68 — null-base-index sibling); [[D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME]] (the fail-loud guard this bypasses); `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp`; `src/asm/asm.cpp` scalar-leaf arm |
| `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-TERMINATION-LAST-REACHABLE` | **✅ CLOSED 2026-07-21 (TF-C40, SQLite testfixture-link sub-arc, `feature/sqlite-full-units-probe-3565456`/PR #46) — the structural-termination predicate `pathTerminates` decides a block's termination by its LAST REACHABLE statement, not by its literal last child.** THE BUG: DSS emitted 13× H0003 (`H_VerifierFailure`, "non-void function may fall through without returning a value") on `ext/recover/sqlite3recover.c`, which gcc/clang compile CLEANLY — a genuine analysis gap (compiler bug, NOT a policy question). Root cause: a trailing null statement `;` (the `MACRO(...);` idiom where the macro body ends in `return`) lowers to an empty `Block` placed as the function body's LAST child, AFTER the `return`; the old Block/LabelStmt case of `pathTerminates` (`src/hir/hir_verifier.hpp`) inspected ONLY `kids.back()` — the empty Block → false → the body read as non-terminating → spurious H0003. Minimal repro `int m(void){ return 0; ; }` → H0003; `int ok(){ return 0; }` clean. **Closing work:** the Block/LabelStmt case now WALKS FORWARD tracking a `reachable` flag — a (recursively) terminating statement makes subsequent positions unreachable, so trailing dead code after a real terminator no longer reads as fall-through (exactly the dead code `checkBlockTermination` already WARNS unreachable → the two rules now AGREE). ★ SOUNDNESS (the audit's mandatory correction): a `goto` can re-enter a label ANYWHERE, incl. nested inside a dead-tail child, so a NEW template helper `subtreeContainsLabel` (declared immediately before `pathTerminates`, uses only kind()+children(); ★ ITERATIVE heap worklist, NOT recursion — per [[D-PARSE-DEEP-NEST-RECURSION-MEMORY]], because a `goto` label can nest at ANY depth and SQLite emits deeply-nested dead-code trees, so an unbounded recursive tree-walk here would be a latent stack overflow; gated by the `!reachable` caller so it only runs in the rare dead-tail region) re-establishes reachability at any dead-tail child whose SUBTREE contains a `LabelStmt` — else `{ if(x) goto L; return 0; if(x){ L: ; } }` (a genuine fall-through) would be wrongly accepted. The `!reachable` gate confines the subtree scan to the rare dead region. The new rule is a STRICT GENERALIZATION of the old (identical when the last statement is the reachable terminator). Both live callers benefit consistently: `hir_verifier.cpp checkReturnCompleteness` (the H0003 site) + `cst_to_hir.cpp maybeAppendImplicitReturnZero` (the `main` implicit-return-0; a dead-tail `main` now short-circuits the synthetic-return append cleanly). No other `pathTerminates` case touched; `checkReturnCompleteness`/`checkBlockTermination` UNCHANGED (the fix is purely the shared predicate). FC5 goto/label precedent: a `LabelStmt` is a goto re-entry point (the same insight as `checkBlockTermination`'s FC5 label carve-out + `pathTerminates`'s GotoStmt/LabelStmt terminator arms). Tests (`tests/hir/test_hir_lowering_c_subset.cpp`): 4 CLEAN pins (minimal `; `, the RECOVER_VFS_WRAPPER if/else shape, dead-call-after-return, if/else-both-return + dead `;`) + 4 STILL-ERROR miscompile-pins (genuine fall-through, if-no-else, direct-label fall-through, ★ the NESTED-LABEL soundness guard `int nested(int x){ if(x) goto L; return 0; if(x){ L: ; } }` → MUST still H0003; RED against the unsound direct-`LabelStmt`-only reset, GREEN against `subtreeContainsLabel`) + the de-recursion flat-witness `HirVerifier.DeepDeadTailLabelScanFlatOnNormalStack` (`tests/hir/test_hir_verifier.cpp`; a directly-built non-void `()->i32` with a 100000-deep LABEL-FREE dead-tail Cast spine after `return 0;` — a deep EXPRESSION so pathTerminates IGNORES the ExprStmt [its `default` arm] and the ONLY deep host-recursion this input drives is `subtreeContainsLabel`; verify runs on a bounded 2 MiB reserve; RED-on-disable = restore the recursive helper → ~kDepth frames overflow the reserve and hard-crash. Built directly via HirBuilder, NOT the front end, whose deep-expression typing is ~quadratic — too slow to reach an overflow depth; mirrors `DeepNestedSwitchAnalyzesFlatOnNormalStack`, per [[D-PARSE-DEEP-NEST-RECURSION-MEMORY]]). Example `examples/c-subset/dead_stmt_after_return` (`int f(void){ return 42; ; }` → exit 42, baseline + `release` arm). RED-ON-DISABLE demonstrated both ways (revert→CLEAN pins go red; unsound version→nested-label pin goes red). VERIFIED: sqlite3recover.c 13 H0003 → 0; the 44 test*.c sweep + zipfile.c/csv.c/normalize.c/test_recover.c stay clean. | (closed — `src/hir/hir_verifier.hpp` `subtreeContainsLabel` helper + the Block/LabelStmt last-reachable-statement case IS the realization; `checkReturnCompleteness`/`checkBlockTermination` UNCHANGED) | FC5 goto/label (`GotoStmt`/`LabelStmt` terminator arms + `checkBlockTermination`'s FC5 label carve-out); `D-HIR-DEAD-CODE-AFTER-RETURN-REJECTED` (the `checkBlockTermination` WARN-not-reject sibling the two-rules-now-agree note references); `D-LK10-ENTRY-MAIN-IMPLICIT-RETURN` (`maybeAppendImplicitReturnZero`, the second live caller); `src/hir/hir_verifier.{hpp,cpp}`; `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` |
